claim: SRU++ is a highly-efficient architecture that combines fast recurrence and attention for sequence modeling.
SRU++ achieves better bits-per-character and perplexity with reduced training cost compared to top-performing Transformer models on standard language modeling tasks.
SRU++ requires minimal attention to achieve near state-of-the-art performance.
DyERNIE learns evolving entity representations in a product of Riemannian manifolds, with the composed spaces estimated from the sectional curvatures of the data.
DyERNIE uses a velocity vector defined in the tangent space at each timestamp to capture the evolutionary dynamics of temporal knowledge graphs.
The dynamics of multi-relational graph data can be more properly modeled by the evolution of embeddings on Riemannian manifolds.
EMMA is a unified encoder-decoder model that reasons over images and trajectories and casts action prediction as multimodal text generation.
EMMA learns a language of actions by unifying tasks as text generation, which facilitates transfer across tasks.
EMMA is a single multitask model where each task contributes to goal completion.
EMMA sets a new state-of-the-art on the Dialog-guided Task Completion benchmark.
MultiLogicNMR is a multi-extension non-monotonic reasoning dataset that includes one variant with more extensions and one variant with text diversity.
MultiLogicNMRer is a neural-symbolic framework for multi-extension non-monotonic reasoning.
Large language models face significant challenges in non-monotonic reasoning with multiple extensions.
MultiLogicNMRer achieves higher accuracy for multi-extension non-monotonic reasoning than prompt-based methods and some fine-tuning methods.
Adversarial Semantic Decoupling is a robust and model-agnostic slot filling method that decouples local semantics inherent in open-vocabulary slot words from the global context.
Adversarial Semantic Decoupling outperforms other methods on open-vocabulary slots while maintaining performance on normal slots.
Human rationales do not necessarily perform well on automatic rationale evaluation metrics.
Improved rationale evaluation metrics account for model-dependent baseline performance.
Model retraining characterizes rationale quality.
Fidelity curves reveal properties of rationale quality such as irrelevance and redundancy.
MetaFill is a text-infilling-based approach for meta-path generation.
MetaFill formulates meta-path identification as a word sequence infilling problem solvable by pretrained language models.
MetaFill outperforms existing meta-path generation methods and graph embedding methods that do not leverage meta-paths in link prediction and node classification.
MetaFill accurately classifies edges in the zero-shot setting where existing approaches fail to generate meta-paths.
AfriSenti is a Twitter sentiment analysis benchmark for 14 African languages from four language families, annotated by native speakers.
NumeroLogic represents numbers by prefixing the digit count (e.g., '2:42').
NumeroLogic serves as a Chain of Thought for number generation by requiring the model to consider the digit count first.
NumeroLogic improves numerical reasoning on arithmetic tasks.
NumeroLogic improves language understanding on the MMLU benchmark.
Training regularization with propositional Boolean expressions maintains logical consistency between coarse- and fine-grained propaganda predictions.
Class representations derived from literal definitions of propaganda techniques in natural language regularize model parameters for propaganda detection.
Micro-Dialect Identification (MDI) is a task for predicting fine-grained dialect varieties at the city level from a single short message.
MARBERT predicts micro-dialects at the city level from a single short message.
Spatially and linguistically-motivated multi-task learning models identify micro-dialects.
A large-scale dataset of low-resource Arabic micro-varieties is suited for micro-dialect identification.
Leaderboards and NLP practitioners are consumers of models, and the benefit from a model is its utility to them.
Leaderboards in their current form can be poor proxies for the NLP community at large.
Leaderboards should report statistics of practical concern, such as model size, energy efficiency, and inference latency, to enable practitioners to estimate a model's utility.
Clause recommendation aids and accelerates contract authoring.
ClauseRec uses a two-staged pipeline that predicts clause type relevance and then recommends clauses based on contract context.
BERT is pre-trained on a library of clauses with two additional tasks for clause type relevance prediction and clause recommendation.
LIDDiA navigates the drug discovery process in silico.
LIDDiA generates molecules meeting key pharmaceutical criteria for the majority of tested clinically relevant targets.
LIDDiA balances exploration and exploitation in the chemical space.
LIDDiA identifies a promising novel candidate molecule for the AR/NR3C4 target.
VC4VG optimizes video captions for text-to-video generation.
VC4VG-Bench evaluates video captions for text-to-video generation with fine-grained, multi-dimensional, and necessity-graded metrics.
Improved caption quality correlates with better video generation performance for text-to-video generation.

Finetuned prompt-based models for sentence pair classification tasks adopt inference heuristics based on lexical overlap, such as incorrectly assuming a sentence pair has the same meaning because they consist of the same set of words.
The inference heuristic based on lexical overlap is less present in zero-shot prompt-based models for sentence pair classification tasks than in finetuned prompt-based models.
Finetuning prompt-based models for sentence pair classification tasks is destructive to useful knowledge learned during pretraining.
Adding a regularization that preserves pretraining weights mitigates the destructive tendency of few-shot finetuning in prompt-based models for sentence pair classification tasks.
MultiCochrane is the first sentence-aligned multilingual text simplification dataset for the medical domain in English, Spanish, French, and Farsi.
Fine-tuned and zero-shot models can generate viable simplified medical texts in multiple languages.
Multilingual medical text simplification faces several outstanding challenges.
Dynamic weighting by discriminator gradient magnitudes combines multiple style objectives in a reward function for multi-style controllable text generation.
Dynamic weighting by discriminator gradient magnitudes outperforms static weighting approaches for style control while maintaining linguistic quality.
An affinity scoring function predicts transfer gain between task pairs using their task embeddings.
Soft prompts serve as task embeddings that summarize task-specific information.
An affinity scoring function predicts the most transferable source tasks for a novel target task without brute-force search.
DiscoSense evaluates commonsense reasoning through understanding a wide variety of discourse connectives.
Conditional Adversarial Filtering extends Adversarial Filtering by employing conditional generation to create compelling distractors.
State-of-the-art pre-trained language models perform poorly on DiscoSense.
KOLD is a Korean offensive language dataset with hierarchical annotations of offensive language type and target, offensive span annotations, and context from article and video titles.
The target group distribution in KOLD differs drastically from that in existing English offensive language datasets.
Providing context information from article and video titles improves model performance for offensiveness detection, target classification, and target group classification.
MVQG is a dataset for generating engaging questions from multiple images.
Building stories behind an image sequence enables models to generate engaging questions.
A novel framework for Avalon gameplay features a multi-agent system that facilitates efficient communication and interaction among LLM-based agents.
Humans are capable of generating a substantial amount of adversarial examples using semantics-preserving word substitutions.
Human-generated adversarial examples are not more able than the best algorithms to generate natural-reading, sentiment-preserving examples.
Human generation of adversarial examples is much more computationally efficient than the best algorithms.
BLANC uses context prediction as an auxiliary task in multi-task learning.
BLANC uses a block attention method to learn the context prediction task.
BLANC outperforms state-of-the-art QA models in reading comprehension, and this performance advantage increases as the number of answer text occurrences in the passage increases.
BLANC outperforms baseline models in a zero-shot setting for supporting fact prediction on HotpotQA after training on SQuAD.

Dialogue-guided Chain-of-Thought (DialCoT) decomposes complex reasoning problems into simpler sub-questions and generates intermediate reasoning steps in a dialogue format for Smaller Language Models.
Proximal Policy Optimization optimizes Smaller Language Models to select the optimal reasoning path.
Visual text representations create continuous vocabularies for machine translation by processing visually rendered text with sliding windows.
Models using visual text representations achieve comparable performance to traditional text models on small and large machine translation datasets.
Models using visual text representations exhibit significant robustness to varied types of noise in machine translation.
The partition filter network decomposes feature encoding for joint entity and relation extraction into partition and filter steps.
The partition filter network uses entity and relation gates to segment neurons into two task-specific partitions and one shared partition.
The shared partition in the partition filter network represents inter-task information and is evenly shared between entity and relation tasks to ensure two-way interaction.

The task-specific partitions in the partition filter network represent intra-task information and are formed through concerted efforts of both gates to make entity feature encoding and relation feature encoding dependent on each other.
Relation prediction contributes non-negligibly to named entity prediction in joint entity and relation extraction.
Performance degradation in Multimodal Large Language Models from corrupted training data is largely reversible.
Disabling a small subset of parameters in corrupted Multimodal Large Language Models almost fully restores performance.
Corrupted Multimodal Large Language Models inherently differentiate between clean and corrupted samples, facilitating dataset cleaning without external intervention.
A corruption-robust training paradigm mitigates the effects of corrupted data in Visual Instruction Tuning.
DiCoRe is a divergent-convergent reasoning framework that decouples the task of event detection using Dreamer and Grounder.
Dreamer encourages divergent reasoning through open-ended event discovery to boost event coverage.
Grounder introduces convergent reasoning with finite-state machine guided constrained decoding to align free-form predictions with task-specific instructions.
LLM-Judge verifies final outputs to ensure high precision.
DiCoRe consistently outperforms prior zero-shot, transfer-learning, and reasoning baselines in event detection.
FLODIAL is a dataset of flowchart-grounded task-oriented dialogs for troubleshooting.

MSKE-Dialog leverages multiple heterogeneous knowledge sources including commonsense knowledge facts, text knowledge, and infobox knowledge to improve the knowledge coverage.
Multi-Reference Selection avoids topic conflict among context and different knowledge sources by better selecting context and knowledge.
Multi-Reference Generation generates informative responses by referring to multiple generation references simultaneously.
Multi-source heterogeneous knowledge is used in open-domain knowledge-enhanced dialogue generation for the first time.
Large language models exhibit high susceptibility of Agreeableness, Extraversion, and Conscientiousness to user influence, while Emotional Stability and Intellect remain relatively stable.

CrossFit standardizes seen/unseen task partitions, data access during different learning stages, and evaluation protocols for studying cross-task generalization ability.
NLP Few-shot Gym provides diverse few-shot NLP tasks created from open-access NLP datasets and converted to a unified text-to-text format.
Few-shot learning ability on unseen tasks can be improved by an upstream learning stage using a set of seen tasks.

Synchronous dual network with cross-type attention separately and interactively considers entity types and relation types for joint entity and relation extraction.
Synchronous dual network uses two isomorphic bi-directional type-attention LSTM to encode entity type enhanced representations and relation type enhanced representations.
Synchronous dual network explicitly models the interdependence between entity types and relation types via cross-type attention.
A new multi-task learning strategy models the interaction between entity type information and relation type information.
Synchronous dual network achieves state-of-the-art performance on the NYT and WebNLG datasets for joint entity and relation extraction.
SafeText is a benchmark dataset for commonsense physical safety consisting of real-life scenarios with paired safe and physically unsafe advice.
State-of-the-art large language models generate advice that violates commonsense physical safety.
State-of-the-art large language models have difficulty rejecting advice that violates commonsense physical safety.
Debate Speech Evaluation requires LLM judges to assess debate speeches for argument strength, relevance, coherence, organization, style, and tone.
Debate Speech Evaluation uses a dataset of meticulously annotated debate speeches.
Among state-of-the-art LLMs, larger models approximate individual human judgments in some respects but differ substantially in overall judgment behavior for debate speech evaluation.
Frontier LLMs may perform at a human level when generating persuasive, opinionated speeches.
MultiAgentESC does not require training.
MultiAgentESC emulates the human-like process of providing emotional support through dialogue analysis, strategy deliberation, and response generation.
MultiAgentESC uses a multi-agent system at each stage to iteratively enhance information understanding and reasoning through diverse interactions among expert agents.

MultiAgentESC uses a response-centered approach to handle the one-to-many problem in strategy selection by generating diverse responses with multiple valid strategies and selecting the optimal response through multi-agent collaboration.
MultiAgentESC diversifies support strategy selection.
MAVEN is a general-domain event detection dataset containing 4,480 Wikipedia documents, 118,732 event mention instances, and 168 event types.
Existing event detection methods achieve lower performance on MAVEN than on small datasets.
STATENet screens for suicidal risk in English tweets by incorporating temporal and emotional context from user history.
Emotional and temporal contextual cues from social media history improve suicide risk assessment.
Question models that use only the input question text approximate the answer confidence scores of state-of-the-art Question Answering systems.
Transformer-based question models are distilled from Transformer-based answering models.
Question models approximate the Precision/Recall curves of target Question Answering systems.
Question models used as filters trade off lower computation cost for lower Recall in Question Answering systems.
The Within-Between Relation model recognizes lexical-semantic relations between words.
The Within-Between Relation model integrates relational and distributional signals to form a sub-space representation for each lexical-semantic relation.
SumFC is a fine-grained two-stage framework for assessing factual consistency in abstractive summarization.
SumFC selects the top-K most relevant sentences from the document for a summary sentence to assess factual consistency.
SumFC performs fine-grained factual consistency reasoning at the sentence level and aggregates consistency scores to produce the final assessment.
SumFC uses data synthesis to generate training pairs and contrastive loss to identify subtle cues for factual consistency assessment.
SumFC distinguishes detailed differences in factual consistency assessment better than prior methods.
State-of-the-art large language models struggle to locate the first error step in student solutions even when given access to the reference solution.
Generating an intermediate corrected student solution that aligns more closely with the original student's solution improves performance in locating the first error step in student solutions.
ComplexTempQA is a large-scale dataset for complex temporal question answering.
ComplexTempQA covers questions spanning multiple decades.
A taxonomy categorizes temporal questions as attributes (revolving around events), comparisons (revolving around entities), and counting questions (revolving around time periods).
ComplexTempQA questions require reasoning capabilities including across-time comparison, temporal aggregation, and multi-hop reasoning involving temporal event ordering and entity recognition.
ComplexTempQA provides detailed metadata for each question, including specific time scopes, to enable comprehensive evaluation of temporal reasoning in large language models.
Redundancy in pretrained transformer models has two classes: General Redundancy and Task-specific Redundancy.
A large majority of neurons in pretrained transformer models are redundant.
A large majority of redundant neurons in pretrained transformer models can be removed during optimization for a downstream task.
A feature-based transfer learning procedure for pretrained transformer models maintains high performance while using a very small fraction of the original neurons.
The textual encoder facilitates cross-modal conversion in end-to-end speech translation, but noise in speech impedes the consistency between text and speech representations.
IMTL bridges the modal gap in end-to-end speech translation by mitigating the difference in length and representation.
With additional data, IMTL achieves state-of-the-art results on the MuST-C end-to-end English-to-Spanish speech translation task while requiring less training time than prior methods.
Date fragmentation ratio measures how faithfully tokenisers preserve multi-digit date components.
DateAugBench evaluates temporal reasoning on context-based date resolution, format-invariance puzzles, and date arithmetic across historical, contemporary, and future time periods.
Large language models use an emergent date-abstraction mechanism to stitch together fragments of month, day, and year components for temporal reasoning.
Excessive date fragmentation correlates with accuracy drops on uncommon dates such as historical and futuristic dates.
Larger models heal date fragments faster via the emergent date abstraction mechanism.
Large language models follow a reasoning path to assemble date fragments that differs from human interpretation (year → month → day).
PsyGUARD detects suicide ideation and assesses suicide risk in psychological counseling.
Foundational theories form the basis of a detailed taxonomy for detecting suicide ideation.
PsySUICIDE is a large-scale, high-quality dataset for suicide detection.

CompKBQA optimizes the fine-tuning process of large language models for generating logical forms by enabling progressive learning of skeleton generation, topic entity generation, and relevant relations generation.
R3 retrieves and incorporates knowledge base information into the process of logical form generation.
CompKBQA achieves state-of-the-art performance on the WebQSP and CWQ knowledge base question answering benchmarks.
CausalTalk is an annotated dataset of Reddit posts about public health for causal language understanding.

CausalTalk annotations combine domain expert labels with GPT-4o-generated labels that are human-verified.

CRoW is a manually-curated, multi-task benchmark that evaluates the ability of models to apply commonsense reasoning in six real-world NLP tasks.
CRoW is constructed using a multi-stage data collection pipeline that rewrites examples from existing datasets using commonsense-violating perturbations.

Segment+ enables language models with limited context windows to process long texts.
Segment+ manages information flow for long text processing using structured notes.
Segment+ manages information flow for long text processing using a filtering module.
Segment+ produces a controllable system for long text processing.
Segment+ produces an interpretable system for long text processing.
Multi-Word Expressions are translated by cross-lingual word embeddings when pre-tokenized as single tokens.
Bilingual word lists for evaluating Multi-Word Expression translation contain Multi-Word Expressions that correspond to single words in another language.
Pre-tokenization of Multi-Word Expressions as single tokens outperforms averaging the embeddings of individual tokens for Multi-Word Expression translation.
Pre-tokenization of Multi-Word Expressions as single tokens does not degrade single-word translation performance.
SDS-CLIP is a lightweight and sample-efficient distillation method that enhances CLIP's compositional visio-linguistic reasoning.
SDS-CLIP fine-tunes CLIP using a distillation objective from large text-to-image generative models.

The benchmark for solving linguistic puzzles is extended to include Linguistic Olympiad puzzles.

Data augmentation for active learning uses labeled guided outputs from a language generation model to train text classifiers.

Levenshtein Training uses the Levenshtein Transformer for word-level quality estimation.

Heuristics for word-level quality estimation construct reference labels compatible with subword-level finetuning and inference.
Levenshtein Training has superior data efficiency for word-level quality estimation in data-constrained settings.
Levenshtein Training achieves competitive performance for word-level quality estimation in unconstrained settings.
Imbalanced label distribution in question answering causes model collapse during test-time adaptation.
Anti-CF utilizes the source model's output to regularize the update of the adapted model during test time.
Anti-CF includes an efficient side block that reduces inference time.
Anti-CF achieves comparable or better results than previous test-time adaptation methods.
Anti-CF maintains inference speed close to that of vanilla forward propagation.
Fine-grained Category Discovery under Coarse-grained supervision (FCDC) discovers fine-grained categories using only coarse-grained labeled data.

Hierarchical weighted self-contrastive learning combines a weighted self-contrastive module with supervised learning in a hierarchical manner to discover fine-grained categories from coarse-grained labeled data.
Modern large language models can be effectively adapted for information reranking using minimal, high-quality supervision.
LIMRANK-SYNTHESIZER generates diverse, challenging, and realistic reranking examples.

LIMRANK generalizes to downstream tasks including scientific literature search and retrieval-augmented generation for knowledge-intensive problem solving.
The information gap between dialogue contexts and desired inferences renders the inductive inference process more challenging.

A new Logical Form grammar models a wide range of knowledge graph queries and is sufficiently simple for efficient supervision data generation.
A Transformer-based model incorporates knowledge graph and conversational contexts via a JSON-like structured input, which is transformed to embeddings and processed by standard attention layers.

RLET performs iterative single step reasoning for entailment tree generation using sentence selection and deduction generation modules.

CEMTM infers coherent and interpretable topic structures from short and long documents containing text and images.

Lifelong Explanation (LLE) continuously trains a student explainer under the supervision of a teacher explanation algorithm on different tasks in lifelong learning.
Lifelong Explanation (LLE) uses Experience Replay to prevent catastrophic forgetting in the student explainer.
Lifelong Explanation (LLE) enhances the stability of explanations for all seen tasks.
Lifelong Explanation (LLE) maintains the same level of faithfulness to the black-box model as the teacher explanation algorithm.
Lifelong Explanation (LLE) is faster at test time than existing explanation methods.
The Experience Replay mechanism in Lifelong Explanation (LLE) enhances the learning capabilities of the student explainer.
Scheduled sampling based on decoding steps increases the selection chance of predicted tokens as decoding steps increase.
Scheduled sampling based on decoding steps more realistically simulates the inference scene during training.

Training with gold AMR and machine-translated sentences improves multilingual AMR-to-text generation over training with generated silver AMR.
Training with a combination of gold AMR with machine-translated sentences and generated silver AMR improves multilingual AMR-to-text generation.

ConRPG uses a context regularizer for meaning in paraphrase generation.

A pre-trained encoder for entity relation extraction uses a span encoder and a span pair encoder to incorporate intra-span and inter-span information.

In entity relation extraction, pre-training with intra-span and inter-span information outperforms distantly supervised pre-training.
A collaborative learning framework for unsupervised text style transfer uses a pair of decoders with opposite decoding directions.

A collaborative learning framework for unsupervised text style transfer guarantees the difference in knowledge acquisition between decoders by opposite decoding directions and a distinguishability constraint.

A collaborative learning framework for unsupervised text style transfer employs mutual knowledge distillation to drive both decoders to a better optimum and alleviate the over-transfer and under-transfer problems.

Sentence ordering, adjacent sentence prediction, TF prediction, TF-IDF prediction, a FastSent variant, and a Quick Thoughts variant yield better performance than pure masked language modeling when used as auxiliary pre-training tasks.

FLUTE is a dataset of figurative language natural language inference instances with textual explanations for Sarcasm, Simile, Metaphor, and Idioms.

Measuring variance of word vector distributions detects semantic differences in words appearing in two corpora without requiring corpus or word alignments.

Plan-guided retrieval uses the planning capabilities of instruction-tuned LLMs to guide retrieval for grounded text generation.

Authorship Representation Regularization (ARR) creates authorship representation with reduced reliance on topic-specific information.

Few-shot hardness is intrinsic to datasets for a given pre-trained model.

Sequence ranking is the best training objective for in-domain pronoun resolution.

Jellyfish models are instruction-tuned local large language models that serve as universal data preprocessing task solvers on a local, single, low-priced GPU, ensuring data security and enabling customization.

Jellyfish models are competitive with GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 in data preprocessing tasks.

Three novel features in advanced semantics are used in text readability assessment.

KANGAROO captures the implicit graph structure among entities in closed domains.

Target-driven multi-modal alignment captures target-specific relationships in multimodal stance detection.

T-MAD achieves robust multimodal stance detection in both in-target and zero-shot settings.
Target-driven multi-modal alignment enhances multimodal stance detection effectiveness.
Dynamic weighting enhances multimodal stance detection effectiveness.
HiCore learns multi-level user interests by boosting multi-interest self-supervised learning with item-, entity-, and word-oriented multiple-channel hypergraphs.
An exhaustive overview defines the task and challenges of model editing for large language models.

LEAP incorporates explicit planning into the inference procedure for multi-step logical reasoning.

Structurally-diverse sampling selects structurally-diverse synthetic utterance-program pairs.

For English and Spanish, monolingual speech in monolingual discourse contexts is prosodically different from monolingual speech in multilingual discourse contexts.

MIO understands and generates speech, text, images, and videos in an end-to-end, autoregressive manner.
MIO is trained on a mixture of discrete tokens across four modalities using causal multimodal modeling.

MIO generates interleaved video-text sequences.
MIO performs chain-of-visual-thought reasoning.
MIO generates visual guidelines.

A new formulation for multilingual entity linking resolves language-specific mentions to a language-agnostic Knowledge Base.

An intent-slot co-occurrence graph captures global intent-slot correlation from the entire training corpus.

SCALE is a task-agnostic model that detects factual inconsistencies in long documents using a Natural Language Inference approach with large text chunks.

ToKen decomposes the few-shot hate speech detection task into constituent parts.

UL2R improves language models and their scaling curves with minimal extra compute and without new data sources.

A protocol constructs a relatedness-based evaluation dataset using adaptive pairwise comparisons, tailored to available resources and optimized for top-rank evaluation accuracy.

Weibo20 is a Chinese microblog dataset for rumor detection containing posts and associated comments collected from Sina Weibo.

Multi-modal, multi-label framing analysis of news at scale is achieved using large vision-language models.

Context-mixing measures for text models are adapted for spoken language models.

GOME uses a chain-of-thought prompting method rooted in rhetoric to specify metaphorical devices such as vehicles and groundings in systematic instructions for LLMs, ensuring accurate and faithful descriptions for text-to-image models.

Symbol tuning finetunes language models on in-context input-label pairs with natural language labels replaced by arbitrary symbols.

Language-clustered vocabularies combine separately trained vocabularies of automatically derived language clusters to balance cross-lingual subword sharing and language-specific vocabularies.

COPEN evaluates three aspects of conceptual knowledge in pre-trained language models: entity organization by conceptual similarities, conceptual property learning, and contextual conceptualization of entities.

CareCorpus+ is a large dataset of caregiver strategies for pediatric rehabilitation contexts.

TraveLER uses a Planner agent that updates instructions based on shared feedback from other agents.

Models trained on source language data become less calibrated when applied to target languages in zero-shot cross-lingual transfer.

An adaptive segmentation policy for simultaneous translation learns to segment the source text by considering possible translations produced by the translation model to maintain consistency between segmentation and translation.

Existing active learning strategies for Natural Language Generation exhibit inconsistent performance, outperforming random selection in some cases but not in others.
Active learning in text classification differs notably from active learning in Natural Language Generation.
NeuralNews contains 4 types of machine-generated news articles that include images and captions.
Cross-modal inconsistency detection defends against neural fake news.
IBKD maximizes mutual information between the final representations of the teacher model and student model in text representation models.
IBKD minimizes mutual information between the student model's representation and the input data in text representation models.
IBKD preserves important learned information and avoids unnecessary information in text representation models, reducing the risk of over-fitting.
OPENPI is the first dataset for tracking state changes in procedural text from arbitrary domains using an open vocabulary.

Tracking state changes in procedural text is formulated as generating state change tuples (entity, attribute, before-state, after-state) for each step with entity, attribute, and state values predicted from an open vocabulary given only the procedural text.
The field of NLP lacks agreement on the term 'hallucination'.
Perspectives from NLP and AI practitioners on hallucination in large language models provide a thematic understanding of its societal influence and ramifications.
Fine-grained Entity Typing can be trained without knowledge base access.
Pseudo data with fine-grained entity labels is automatically generated from a large unlabeled dataset for training Fine-grained Entity Typing models without knowledge base access.

A neural network model is trained on pseudo data in an unsupervised manner or with self-training guided by a coarse-grained Named Entity Recognition model for Fine-grained Entity Typing without knowledge base access.
Outline-conditioned story generation is the task of generating a coherent narrative consistent with an outline of key characters and events.
PlotMachines transforms an outline into a coherent story using dynamic plot state tracking.
PlotMachines uses high-level discourse structure to learn different writing styles for different parts of the narrative.
Large-scale language models such as GPT-2 and Grover are not sufficient for generating coherent narratives consistent with an outline.
Dynamic plot state tracking is important for composing narratives with tighter, more consistent plots.
Mamba-based Visual Language Models outperform Transformer-based Visual Language Models in image captioning, visual question answering, and reading comprehension.
Transformer-based Visual Language Models outperform Mamba-based Visual Language Models in visual grounding and the performance gap increases with model scale.
Task-aware visual encoding yields minimal performance gains for visual grounding in Visual Language Models.
Transformer-based Visual Language Models outperform Mamba-based Visual Language Models at in-context multimodal retrieval.
Mamba-based Visual Language Models perform well on tasks requiring image summarization but struggle on tasks requiring explicit information retrieval from the context.
ArtELingo is a benchmark and dataset for diversity across languages and cultures.
ArtELingo adds emotion annotations in Arabic, Chinese, and Spanish to evaluate cultural-transfer performance.
ArtELingo includes artworks with multiple annotations in three languages.
ArtELingo enables the study of similarities and differences across languages and cultures.
The language and culture diversity in ArtELingo improves the performance of baseline models on captioning tasks.
ChaosNLI is a dataset for studying collective human opinions in commonly used NLI evaluation sets.
High human disagreement exists in a noticeable number of examples in NLI evaluation sets.
State-of-the-art models cannot recover the distribution over human labels in NLI tasks.
State-of-the-art models achieve high accuracy on NLI examples with high human agreement but near-random performance on examples with low human agreement.
Examples with low human agreement account for most errors made by state-of-the-art models on NLI evaluation sets.
Cross-lingual cross-target stance detection requires transferring both language and target-oriented knowledge from source to target language.
A cross-lingual teacher for cross-lingual cross-target stance detection transfers language knowledge from source to target language.
A cross-target teacher for cross-lingual cross-target stance detection mines target category information and generalizes it to unseen targets in the target language via category-oriented learning.
The aspect-centric tree structure shortens the distance between aspects and corresponding opinion words in aspect-based sentiment analysis.
The aspect-centric tree structure adaptively correlates aspect and opinion words to enable better polarity identification in aspect-based sentiment analysis.
The average distance between aspect and opinion words is shortened on the SemEval Restaurant14 dataset.
A process-oriented evaluation framework with progressive interventions (Intrinsicality, Instruction, and Imitation) assesses variability and adaptation in LLM decision-making.
LLMs adopt stable, conservative strategies that diverge from observed human behavior in decision-making tasks by default.
Risk-framed instructions cause LLMs to behave more like humans in decision-making tasks.
Risk-framed instructions cause complex irregularities in LLM decision-making behavior.
Incorporating human decision trajectories via in-context learning narrows distributional gaps between LLM and human decision-making.
LLMs underexpress round-to-round variability relative to humans across all interventions, indicating a persistent alignment gap in behavioral fidelity.
A topic-informed discrete latent variable model learns a shared latent space for sentence-pair representation via vector quantization for semantic textual similarity.
A topic-informed discrete latent variable model explores richer semantic information via topic modeling for semantic textual similarity compared to models limited to local semantic contexts.
A semantic-driven attention mechanism injects quantized representations into a transformer-based language model for semantic textual similarity.
A framework trains a single multi-domain neural machine translation model that translates several domains without increasing inference time or memory usage.
A single multi-domain neural machine translation model improves translation on both high- and low-resource domains over strong multi-domain baselines.
A single multi-domain neural machine translation model is robust under noisy data conditions.
Unbalanced gradients are not the root cause of the instability of Transformer training.

Heavy dependency on the residual branch in each layer of a multi-layer Transformer model causes training instability by amplifying small parameter perturbations and resulting in significant disturbances in the model output.
Light dependency on the residual branch limits model potential and leads to inferior trained models.
Admin (Adaptive model initialization) stabilizes the early stage of Transformer training and unleashes full model potential in the late stage.
Admin (Adaptive model initialization) stabilizes Transformer training, accelerates convergence, and improves model performance.
Sparse and robust subnetworks exist for vision-language pre-trained models that simultaneously reduce model size and improve out-of-distribution robustness on visual question answering tasks.
Sparse and robust subnetworks for vision-language pre-trained models achieve performance competitive with debiased full models on out-of-distribution visual question answering.
Sparse and robust subnetworks for vision-language pre-trained models outperform state-of-the-art debiasing methods while using fewer parameters on out-of-distribution visual question answering.
SLOG is a semantic parsing dataset that extends COGS with 17 structural generalization cases.
Transformer models and structure-aware parsers exhibit a large discrepancy between lexical and structural generalization capacities.

Transformer-based language models appear to overfit to superficial patterns in the data from controlled fragments of natural language with satisfiability problems of increasing complexity rather than acquiring the logical principles governing reasoning in these fragments.
TaskDiff utilizes utterances, intents, and slots and their distributions to compute similarity for task-oriented conversations.
TaskDiff improves robustness for task-oriented conversation similarity compared to other related approaches.
A natively sparse language model trained and sampled with entmax avoids the mismatch between training and testing conditions in text generation.
A natively sparse language model using entmax reduces repetitions in generated text.
A natively sparse language model using entmax produces n-gram diversity closer to human text.
𝜖-perplexity is a metric for comparing sparse or truncated distributions.
sparsemax score is a metric for comparing sparse or truncated distributions.
Jensen-Shannon divergence is a metric for comparing sparse or truncated distributions.
Entmax sampling produces more engaging and coherent outputs in story completion and dialogue generation tasks.
Bottom-up information quality is defined as the mutual information between visual information and word identity.
A Bayesian update model predicts that reduced bottom-up information quality increases reading difficulty.
The upper half of words contains more information about word identity than the lower half in English and Chinese.
The asymmetry in information distribution between upper and lower halves of words is more pronounced in English than in Chinese.
Reading times reflect the greater asymmetry in information distribution for English compared to Chinese.
seqBench allows systematic variation of logical depth, backtracking steps, and noise ratio for probing sequential reasoning limits in Large Language Models.
Large Language Models exhibit a universal failure pattern in sequential reasoning where accuracy collapses exponentially beyond a model-specific logical depth.
Top-performing Large Language Models systematically fail on structured reasoning tasks with minimal search complexity, revealing limitations in their commonsense reasoning.
Self-supervised task distributions for meta-learning in NLP vary by diversity, difficulty, type, domain, and curriculum.
Varying task diversity, difficulty, type, domain, and curriculum meaningfully alters the task distribution for meta-learning in NLP.
Self-supervised task distributions improve downstream few-shot accuracy over previous unsupervised meta-learning methods.
Self-supervised task distributions achieve comparable downstream few-shot performance to supervised methods on the FewRel 2.0 benchmark.
Personal information leakage detection in conversations is formulated as an alignment optimization problem.
PERSONA-LEAKAGE is a new dataset for evaluating personal information leakage detection in conversations.
Two constrained alignment models detect personal information leakage in conversations.
Two constrained alignment models consistently outperform baseline methods for personal information leakage detection.
Personalized chit-chat dialogue systems suffer more from personal information disclosure than Seq2Seq models and language models.
COMET trains multilingual machine translation evaluation models that achieve high correlation with human judgments.
COMET leverages cross-lingual pretrained language modeling to train machine translation evaluation models.
COMET exploits information from both the source input and a target-language reference translation to predict machine translation quality.
COMET demonstrates robustness to high-performing machine translation systems.
A multifaceted framework evaluates authorship obfuscation techniques on evasion, content preservation, and misattribution.
A new information-theoretic measure characterizes misattribution harm caused by detection evasion.
State-of-the-art authorship obfuscation techniques exhibit key weaknesses in evasion, content preservation, and misattribution.
A back-translation baseline performs competitively for authorship obfuscation on evasion, content preservation, and misattribution.
Multi-Chain Reasoning (MCR) meta-reasons over multiple chains of thought to generate an explanation and predict the answer for multi-hop question answering.
Multi-Chain Reasoning (MCR) outperforms strong baselines on multi-hop question answering.
Multi-Chain Reasoning (MCR) explanations enable humans to verify answers.
DiscoDVT learns a discrete variable sequence that summarizes the global structure of the text.
DiscoDVT uses the discrete variable sequence to guide the generation process at each decoding step.
DiscoDVT uses an auxiliary objective to model discourse relations within the text.
The latent codes in DiscoDVT learn meaningful correspondence to discourse structures.
DiscoDVT generates long texts with better long-range coherence.
Query auto completion for voice queries is conditioned on intermediate transcriptions.
Conditioning on intermediate transcriptions improves query auto completion quality for voice queries.
Naive query auto completion methods fail for streaming voice search because intermediate transcriptions often are not prefixes or substrings of the final query.

A hand-generated and annotated benchmark of diverse english-language rebus puzzles, including simple pictographic substitutions and spatially-dependent cues, evaluates vision-language models on tasks requiring multimodal abstraction, symbolic reasoning, and cultural, phonetic, and linguistic puns.
Vision-language models exhibit capabilities in decoding simple visual clues.
Vision-language models struggle with tasks requiring abstract reasoning, lateral thinking, and understanding visual metaphors.
TADPOLE is a task adapted pre-training framework that uses data selection techniques adapted from Domain Adaptation.
TADPOLE formulates data selection as an anomaly detection problem that works well for limited downstream corpora.
TADPOLE is a scalable and efficient unsupervised technique that eliminates the need for manual data curation.
TADPOLE achieves performance gains on small datasets with minimal additional pre-training steps.
Combining TADPOLE with data augmentation achieves the highest performance for small downstream corpora.
Split-Merge consolidates specialized domain experts into a single large language model without further training.

Split-Merge uses relative task vectors, defined as difference representations encoding each expert's unique traits with respect to a shared base model, to guide a principled and efficient merging process.
Split-Merge dissects parameters into common dimensions averaged across experts and complementary dimensions unique to each expert to balance generalization and specialization.
Split-Merge employs a compression mechanism for complementary parameters that retains only principal components and scalar multipliers per expert to minimize overhead.
Split-Merge incorporates a dynamic router that selects the most relevant domain at inference to preserve domain-specific precision.
Split-Merge demonstrates versatility across code generation, mathematical reasoning, medical question answering, and instruction-following tasks.
Layer-wise minimal pair probing evaluates contextual syntactic and semantic features in speech representations.
Speech language models encode grammatical features more robustly than conceptual features.
Self-supervised speech models match or surpass automatic speech recognition encoders on every linguistic level without using text.

Self-supervised speech model representations peak in the middle layers and degrade in the final layers, while automatic speech recognition and auditory large language model encoders maintain or improve representations throughout the network.
Self-supervised speech models encode grammatical cues prior to word onset, while auditory large language models distribute evidence more evenly across time.
Random entity quantization achieves similar results to current entity quantization strategies for knowledge graph representation.
Random entity quantization produces entity codes with higher entropy at the code level.
Random entity quantization produces entity codes with higher Jaccard distance at the codeword level.
Random entity quantization makes different entities more easily distinguished, facilitating effective knowledge graph representation.
There is room for improvement in entity distinguishability beyond current entity quantization strategies.
Text-to-Format generates code for Microsoft Word to format document content from user instructions.
DocFormEval data is a high-quality dataset for evaluating automatic document formatting approaches.
The effect of prompting strategies for document formatting positively correlates with the amount of domain knowledge introduced.
RecBase is a domain-agnostic foundational model pretrained with a recommendation-oriented objective.
A unified item tokenizer encodes items into hierarchical concept identifiers, enabling structured representation and efficient vocabulary sharing.
RecBase matches or surpasses the performance of larger LLM baselines in zero-shot and cross-domain recommendation tasks.
NAST uses a unidirectional encoder and a non-autoregressive decoder with intra-chunk parallelism.
NAST generates blank tokens or repetitive tokens to adjust the READ/WRITE strategy flexibly.
NAST is trained to maximize non-monotonic latent alignment using an alignment-based latency loss.
NAST outperforms autoregressive simultaneous machine translation baselines on multiple benchmarks.
IGT-to-paradigms generates entire morphological paradigms from interlinear glossed text input.
Existing morphological reinflection models can solve the IGT-to-paradigms task, with accuracy varying by language.
Cleaning noisy interlinear glossed text data with minimal expert effort improves model performance on the IGT-to-paradigms task.
POS tags do not affect the accuracy of morphological reinflection models on the IGT-to-paradigms task.
ProReason decomposes visual reasoning into proactive visual perception and textual reasoning.
ProReason iterates proactive information collection and reasoning to conclude answers with necessary and sufficient visual descriptions.
ProReason integrates large language models to compensate for reasoning deficits in large vision-language models.
ProReason outperforms existing multi-step reasoning frameworks on various benchmarks for both open-source and closed-source models.
Hierarchical distance has a stronger effect than linear distance on BERT's susceptibility to NPI illusive effects.
ADePT is not differentially private.
The true sensitivity of ADePT's private mechanism is higher than claimed when the encoder's dimension is very small.
The proportion of utterances not privatized by ADePT can be very high.
Current methods for low-resource cross-lingual entity linking are limited by their reliance on Wikipedia's interlanguage links and suffer when the foreign language's Wikipedia is small.
Low-resource cross-lingual entity linking requires the use of outside-Wikipedia cross-lingual resources.
QuEL is a zero-shot system for low-resource cross-lingual entity linking that utilizes search engine query logs.
QuEL achieves higher gold candidate recall than state-of-the-art baselines in low-resource cross-lingual entity linking.
QuEL achieves higher end-to-end linking accuracy than state-of-the-art baselines in low-resource cross-lingual entity linking.
mAggretriever leverages semantic and lexical features from pre-trained multilingual transformers for dense retrieval.
mAggretriever uses approximate masked-language modeling prediction for computing lexical features, reducing GPU memory requirements during fine-tuning.
mAggretriever fine-tuned solely on English training data outperforms state-of-the-art multilingual dense retrieval models trained on large-scale multilingual data.
Temporal referential consistency evaluates large language models for consistency in temporal references across multiple languages.
Large language models exhibit insufficient temporal referent consistency.
UnTRaP uses reasoning path alignment to enhance temporal referential consistency in large language models.
Natural Asemantic Variation is a category of minor variations in nuance (non-typos) that preserve meaning with respect to the target language.
Existing machine translation models fail on Natural Asemantic Variation data.
Fine-tuning machine translation models with human-generated variations improves robustness to Natural Asemantic Variation.
Robustness to Natural Asemantic Variation can be transferred across languages.
Synthetic perturbations achieve some but not all of the benefits of organic Natural Asemantic Variation data for improving machine translation robustness.
A model for answering multi-span questions casts the task as a sequence tagging problem that predicts for each input token whether it is part of the answer.
A sequence tagging model for multi-span question answering improves performance on span extraction questions in the DROP and Quoref datasets.
DPDF-LQ processes multimodal inputs through two complementary paths: a global path that establishes cross-modal dependencies and a local path that captures fine-grained representations.
Dynamic Global Learnable Query Attention (DGLQA) dynamically allocates weights to each modality to capture relevant features and learn global representations.
DPDF-LQ improves fine-grained sentiment prediction by combining global and local features.
Prior optimal transport transfers knowledge between annotated and non-annotated data in semi-supervised Bilingual Lexicon Induction.
Bi-directional lexicon update transfers knowledge between annotated and non-annotated data in semi-supervised Bilingual Lexicon Induction.
Two-way interaction between supervised signal and unsupervised alignment accounts for performance gains in semi-supervised Bilingual Lexicon Induction.
Two-way interaction between supervised signal and unsupervised alignment in semi-supervised Bilingual Lexicon Induction demonstrates robustness on distant language pairs.
Embedding and Gradient Say Wrong models the distributional distance between the output generated with input text and the output generated without input text.
Embedding and Gradient Say Wrong uses Taylor Expansion in the output probability space to leverage embedding and first-order gradient information.
Embedding and Gradient Say Wrong is a plug-and-play method adaptable to any autoregressive LLM.

Semantic paths connecting concepts in Argumentative Discourse Units are identified using external resources (WordNet, ConceptNet, and Wikipedia) to obtain implicit chains of reasoning for argument relation prediction.
Attention-based Multi-Network architectures leverage semantic paths for argument relation prediction.
Argument relation prediction using Wikipedia as the external resource achieves higher performance than baselines across four diverse datasets.
A benchmark for text-based compositional multi-tasking comprises four practically relevant compositional tasks.
Learnable Calibration is an efficient method for text-based compositional multi-tasking in on-device large language models.
Discourse content and communicative function have varying influences on code-switching patterns, with communicative function having a stronger influence.
KRLS uses a faster generation procedure that samples from independent next-word distributions after supervised language model training in offline task-oriented dialog training.
KRLS uses a fine-grained reward function that measures the importance and semantic closeness of each generated token to focus on key information in dialogs during offline training.
KRLS achieves state-of-the-art performance for end-to-end response generation in offline task-oriented dialogs.
KRLS reduces training time in offline training compared to standard reinforcement learning algorithms using auto-regressive generation.
VerifyMatch uses LLM pseudo-labels to guide the training of a semi-supervised learning model for natural language inference.
VerifyMatch uses the semi-supervised learning model to verify LLM-generated natural language inference data.

VerifyMatch applies MixUp to pseudo-labeled samples when the semi-supervised learning model's predicted label disagrees with the LLM-generated label or when both labels are the same but the semi-supervised learning model's confidence is below an adaptive threshold.
TDEER decodes triples by translating the subject to objects using the relation.
TDEER handles the overlapping triple problem by recognizing all possible triples, including overlapping and non-overlapping triples.
TDEER uses negative samples to alleviate error accumulation at different stages.
TDEER is computationally more efficient than state-of-the-art baselines.
BABERT encodes unsupervised statistical boundary information into pre-trained language models.
BABERT induces features for Chinese sequence labeling tasks.
BABERT provides consistent improvements on Chinese sequence labeling tasks across multiple benchmarks.
Integrating BABERT with external lexicon information achieves further improvements on Chinese sequence labeling tasks.
Whisper-UT uses lightweight adapters for seamless adaptation across speech and text tasks.
Whisper-UT enables multi-modal machine translation by conditioning translation on both speech and source language text inputs.
Whisper-UT incorporates ASR hypotheses or ground-truth transcripts as prompts to process both speech and text modalities simultaneously.
Whisper-UT enhances speech translation performance with a 2-stage decoding strategy.
Cross-modal and cross-task fine-tuning improves multi-modal translation performance without requiring 3-way parallel data.
Differentiable Masking masks input subsets while maintaining differentiability.
Differentiable Masking uses a simple model based on intermediate hidden layers to decide token inclusion.
Differentiable Masking reveals what the network knows at intermediate layers.
Differentiable Masking enables analysis of how decisions are formed across network layers.
Longtriever splits long documents into short blocks and models local semantics within blocks and global context semantics across blocks in a tightly-coupled manner.
Longtriever uses a pre-training phase to model underlying semantic correlations.

Divergence-based calibration calibrates token probabilities for pretraining data detection by computing the cross-entropy between the token probability distribution and the token frequency distribution.
PatentMIA is a Chinese-language benchmark for assessing pretraining data detection approaches on Chinese text.
Divergence-based calibration outperforms existing pretraining data detection methods on English-language benchmarks and PatentMIA.
TheoremLlama uses an NL-FL bootstrapping method that integrates natural language proofs into Lean4 code to leverage large language models' natural language reasoning for formal reasoning.
TheoremLlama uses curriculum learning and block training techniques to train large language models for formal theorem proving.
TheoremLlama uses an iterative proof writing method to generate Lean4 proofs.
Open Bootstrapped Theorems is an aligned natural language and formal language dataset for theorem proving.
TheoremLlama outperforms GPT-4 in formal theorem proving on the MiniF2F benchmark.
An open-domain method automatically annotates modifier constituents in Wikipedia categories as disambiguated property-value pairs.
Annotating modifier constituents in Wikipedia categories as disambiguated property-value pairs achieves a better trade-off between precision and recall than treating them as mere strings.
An enriched dataset for POI type prediction includes images accompanying text messages.
A method for POI type prediction captures interactions between text and image by extracting relevant information from each modality.
Using both text and images for POI type prediction outperforms using text only.
SMRC2 extends representation learning to the multi-document level.
SMRC2 jointly learns latent semantic information from content and rich relatedness information from topological networks in a shared space using a language model and graph structure.
A new biomedical dataset supports scientific multi-literature representation learning.
Domain-slot related verbs and nouns are extracted from dialogue for dialogue state tracking.
Domain-slot related verbs and nouns are integrated into a description for slot identification.
A random sampling strategy enhances domain generalization in dialogue state tracking.
Few-shot cross-domain dialogue state tracking using domain-slot related description outperforms existing few-shot dialogue state tracking methods on MultiWOZ.
Few-shot cross-domain dialogue state tracking using domain-slot related description improves slot accuracy over existing slot description methods.
OpenAI models memorize a wide collection of copyrighted books.
The degree of memorization of books by OpenAI models is tied to the frequency of passages from those books on the web.
Memorization of books by OpenAI models contaminates test data for cultural analytics.
OpenAI models perform better on memorized books than on non-memorized books for downstream tasks.
MolTRES uses generator-discriminator training to learn from challenging examples that require structural understanding.
MolTRES enriches molecular representations by transferring knowledge from scientific literature through external materials embedding.
MolTRES outperforms state-of-the-art models on popular molecular property prediction tasks.
FedED trains a central medical relation extraction model without exchanging private local data.
FedED uses the predictions of an ensemble of local models to train the central medical relation extraction model without uploading local parameters.

A denoising training approach for cross-lingual spoken language understanding uses mutual supervision from multiple models trained on data from different augmentation methods to mitigate noise in augmented data.

A denoising training approach using mutual supervision from multiple models trained on data from different augmentation methods outperforms the state of the art on cross-lingual spoken language understanding benchmarks.

Separate domain adapters and modality specific adapters process noisy inputs from automatic speech recognition, optical character recognition, and user-generated content in Neural Machine Translation.
Dynamic routing combines domain adapters and modality specific adapters at runtime for known noise sources in Neural Machine Translation.
Fast Fusion and Multi Fusion are transfer learning mechanisms that combine domain adapters and modality specific adapters when the noise source is unknown in Neural Machine Translation.
Aspect Sentiment Quad Prediction jointly detects all four sentiment elements (aspect category, aspect term, opinion term, and sentiment polarity) in quads for an opinionated sentence.
Paraphrase modeling casts the Aspect Sentiment Quad Prediction task as a paraphrase generation process.
Paraphrase modeling solves Aspect Sentiment Quad Prediction in an end-to-end manner.
Paraphrase modeling fully exploits the semantics of sentiment elements by generating them in natural language form.
Paraphrase modeling enables cross-task transfer for aspect-based sentiment analysis tasks.
QADA adapts question answering models to unseen target domains via self-supervision.
QADA uses multi-hop synonyms to augment question representations.
QADA augments token embeddings for questions by sampling with Dirichlet distributions.
QADA drops context spans via an attentive sampling strategy for context augmentation.
QADA trains the model via attention-based contrastive adaptation using pseudo labels.
Span-based multimodal variational autoencoder fuses text and image features for semi-supervised multimodal named entity recognition.
Span-based multimodal variational autoencoder uses modal-specific variational autoencoders to model text and image latent features.
Span-based multimodal variational autoencoder uses product-of-experts to acquire multimodal features.
Span-based multimodal variational autoencoder models the implicit relations between labels and multimodal features.
Span-based multimodal variational autoencoder outperforms baseline methods in supervised multimodal named entity recognition.
Span-based multimodal variational autoencoder improves multimodal named entity recognition performance with less labeled data compared to existing semi-supervised methods.
HEAR warns users of potential errors in instructions and suggests corrections.
Communicating uncertainty prevents misguidance in human guidance tasks.
Communicating uncertainty reduces the search space for users in human guidance tasks.
HEAR motivates users to make more attempts at the task, which increases success rate.
Uncertainty communication provides practical benefits in long-horizon sequential decision-making problems.
BLA is an automatically constructed benchmark for evaluating multimodal models on active-passive voice, coordination, and relative clauses.
Transformer-based multimodal models including CLIP, ViLBERT, and BLIP2 struggle with BLA in a zero-shot setting.
Most tested multimodal models only marginally benefit from fine-tuning or construction-specific prompting on BLA.
BLIP2 shows promising trends in in-context learning on BLA.
Tree approximation of Wasserstein distance with word embedding selection handles high-dimensional word vectors.
Word embeddings with large Wasserstein distance are selected in tree approximation of Wasserstein distance by solving an entropic regularized maximization problem.
A training-free prompt-free approach for Chinese spelling correction uses large language models to generate output tokens sequentially.
A minimal distortion model utilizes pronunciation or shape similarities between original and replaced characters to maintain output faithfulness to the input in Chinese spelling correction.
Two reward strategies address practical challenges specific to Chinese spelling correction.
A training-free prompt-free approach for Chinese spelling correction enables large language models to compete with state-of-the-art domain-general Chinese spelling correction models.
Younger, female, and Asian-American personas trigger refusal guardrails more often for censored or illegal information requests in ChatGPT.
ChatGPT guardrails refuse to comply with requests for a political position the user is likely to disagree with.
Certain identity groups and sports fandom elicit changes in ChatGPT guardrail sensitivity similar to direct statements of political ideology.
ChatGPT infers political ideology from demographic categories and American football team fandom and modifies guardrail behavior accordingly.
RaTEScore assesses the quality of radiology reports generated by AI models.
RaTEScore emphasizes diagnostic outcomes and anatomical details in radiology reports.
RaTEScore is robust against complex medical synonyms and sensitive to negation expressions in radiology reports.
RaTEScore aligns more closely with human preference than existing metrics for radiology report evaluation.
RaTE-Eval is a benchmark for evaluating radiology report generation metrics.
Personal values in online discussions are estimated and aggregated into value profiles using state-of-the-art models.
The dissimilarity of value profiles correlates with disagreement in specific cases.
Including value information improves agreement prediction performance.
Role question generation requires producing a set of questions asking about all possible semantic roles of a predicate given a predicate mention and a passage.
A two-stage model for role question generation first produces context-independent question prototypes for each role and then revises them to be contextually appropriate for the passage.

A role question generation approach conditions on the type of information to inquire about without requiring existing answers in the text, regardless of whether the answer appears explicitly, could be inferred, or should be sought elsewhere.
A role question generation model produces diverse and well-formed questions for a large, broad-coverage ontology of predicates and roles.
Personalized target-oriented dialogue uses a <dialogue act, topic> pair as the conversation target and incorporates personalization during target accomplishment.
An automatic dataset curation framework for personalized target-oriented dialogue uses a role-playing approach.
TopDial is a large-scale personalized target-oriented dialogue dataset.
Accent adaptation in end-to-end ASR systems is achieved using cross-attention with a trainable set of codebooks.
A set of trainable codebooks captures accent-specific information and is integrated within the ASR encoder layers.
Generalization to unseen English accents in accented speech recognition is achieved using cross-attention with a trainable set of codebooks.
Zero-shot transfer for accented speech recognition on the L2Artic dataset is achieved using cross-attention with a trainable set of codebooks.

Evaluations of multi-hop explanations using gold explanations substantially underestimate explanation quality in terms of fact relevance and explanation completeness because models produce valid explanations that differ from gold explanations.
A corpus of domain-expert relevance ratings for facts in explanations to standardized science exam questions includes relevant facts not present in the gold explanations.
Expert-augmented ratings provide better estimates of explanation quality than original gold evaluations.
Both original gold evaluations and expert-augmented automatic evaluations substantially underestimate explanation quality compared to full manual expert judgements.
The underestimation of explanation quality by automatic evaluations varies across different models.
PRIDE uses BERT and Transformer to create conversation representations.
PRIDE utilizes dialogue structure and external knowledge about speaker features and conversation style.
PRIDE performs multi-label prediction of fine-grained interpersonal relationships.
Large-scale screenplay-based datasets contain directed interpersonal relationships of conversation participants.
PRIDE outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in predicting interpersonal relationships.
Attribution tie detection identifies factors held responsible for the 2019 Chennai water crisis in social media documents.
A neural baseline model identifies attribution ties in social media documents about the 2019 Chennai water crisis.
The first annotated dataset of social media comments about the 2019 Chennai water crisis is released for attribution tie detection.
Transformer language models conditioned on relevant information from an extractive step produce abstractive summaries of long documents exceeding several thousand words.
Transformer language models conditioned on relevant information from an extractive step produce more abstractive summaries than prior work using a copy mechanism.
Transformer based summarization methods produce summaries with fewer n-gram copies, resulting in n-gram copying statistics more similar to human generated abstracts.
Transformer based summarization models are ranked highly for coherence and fluency in human evaluation, but purely extractive methods score higher for informativeness and relevance.
Neural language models' prediction behavior for reference generally does not resemble human language users' prediction behavior.
DecoMT decomposes the machine translation process between related languages into a sequence of word chunk translations.
DecoMT surpasses multiple established few-shot baseline approaches for machine translation between related languages.
A weakly-supervised method for ad-hoc document retrieval uses generative and discriminative models trained from the corpus documents.
An end-to-end retrieval system combines traditional information retrieval with two deep learning re-rankers.
A weakly-supervised method for ad-hoc document retrieval achieves state-of-the-art results without manual labels.
Log-FGAER recognizes fine-grained address entities in multi-turn spoken dialogues by formulating address hierarchy relationships as logic rules and applying them probabilistically.
An ontology-based data augmentation methodology augments spoken dialogue datasets with labeled address entities using ChatGPT.
Fine-tuning restructures text embedding space into modular, non-convex regions aligned with model predictions for highly ambiguous instances.
In ambiguous text classification data, alignment with ground-truth labels drops despite high prediction purity, revealing a hidden tension between structural confidence and label uncertainty.
Mapper uncovers decision regions, boundary collapses, and overconfident clusters in text embedding geometry.
Pre-training on rephrased synthetic data alone does not accelerate convergence to a target validation loss compared to pre-training on natural web texts.
Pre-training on a mixture of one-third rephrased synthetic data and two-thirds natural web texts accelerates convergence to a target validation loss at larger data budgets.
Pre-training on textbook-style synthetic data alone results in higher loss on many downstream domains at small data budgets.
Training on rephrased synthetic data does not degrade model performance at foreseeable scales.
Training on mixtures of textbook-style pure-generated synthetic data exhibits patterns consistent with model collapse.
The Chinese Minority Headline Generation (CMHG) dataset provides headline generation data for Tibetan, Uyghur, and Mongolian.
The CMHG benchmark is a high-quality test set for headline generation in Chinese minority languages, annotated by native speakers.
A knowledge type ontology for why-questions in narratives defines categories of commonsense knowledge.
External knowledge injection improves performance for language models of various sizes when answering why-questions in narratives, but the improvement diminishes as model size increases.
The format of provided knowledge critically affects performance when answering why-questions in narratives.
Smaller language models benefit more from larger amounts of external knowledge for answering why-questions in narratives.
Cross-document attention enables structural comparisons at document-to-document and sentence-to-document levels.
Cross-document attention is weakly supervised from document pairs.

Cross-document attention outperforms hierarchical attention encoders that do not account for structural correspondence between documents in predicting document-to-document and sentence-to-document relationships for citation recommendation and plagiarism detection.
GeoLM uses geo-entity mentions as anchors to connect linguistic information with geospatial information from geographical databases.
GeoLM connects linguistic and geospatial context using contrastive learning and masked language modeling.
GeoLM incorporates a spatial coordinate embedding mechanism to encode distance and direction relations for capturing geospatial context.
GeoLM supports toponym recognition, toponym linking, relation extraction, and geo-entity typing.
Active instruction tuning identifies informative tasks for instruction tuning by measuring prompt uncertainty with model output disagreement over perturbed prompts.
Active instruction tuning achieves better out-of-distribution generalization with fewer training tasks than baseline task selection strategies.
A task map categorizes and diagnoses tasks based on prompt uncertainty and prediction probability.
Training on ambiguous (prompt-uncertain) tasks improves generalization for instruction tuning.
Training on difficult (prompt-certain and low-probability) tasks does not improve generalization for instruction tuning.
Competency-aware neural machine translation translates source sentences and estimates translation competency.
A self-estimator in neural machine translation estimates translation competency by determining if the source sentence semantics can be reconstructed from the decoding procedure information.

Competency-aware neural machine translation achieves higher correlation with human quality judgments than reference-based quality estimation methods without requiring reference translations or annotated data.
Competency awareness is robust in competency-aware neural machine translation.
Humans can decompose hard questions into simpler questions that are easier for language models to solve.
Human-in-the-loop Question Decomposition improves language model performance on reasoning tasks.
Human-in-the-loop Question Decomposition can provide an alternate path to building large language models.
A low-rank subspace in language model activations primarily encodes Binding IDs.
Principal component analysis identifies the low-rank Binding ID encoding subspace in language model activations.
Editing representations along directions in the low-rank Binding ID encoding subspace causes language models to bind entities to different attributes.
MacroIE constructs a fact graph in which nodes represent fact elements and edges connect nodes that belong to the same fact.
MacroIE reformulates Open Information Extraction as a non-parametric process of finding maximal cliques in the fact graph.
MacroIE outputs the final set of facts in one step, eliminating the need to predict fact order and avoiding error propagation between facts.
MacroIE significantly outperforms state-of-the-art Open Information Extraction methods.
Text2Mol retrieves molecules using natural language descriptions as queries.
A dataset of molecules paired with natural language descriptions supports cross-modal molecule retrieval.
An aligned common semantic embedding space enables retrieving molecules from natural language queries.
A cross-modal attention-based model provides explainability and reranking for molecule retrieval by interpreting attentions as association rules.
An ensemble approach integrates multiple cross-modal architectures to retrieve molecules from natural language queries.
DeLorean is an unsupervised decoding algorithm that incorporates both past and future contexts using off-the-shelf left-to-right language models without supervision.
DeLorean incorporates future context through back-propagation by updating only the internal representation of the output while keeping model parameters fixed.
DeLorean alternates between forward and backward propagation to decode output representations reflecting both left and right contexts.
DeLorean is applicable to abductive text generation and counterfactual story revision.
DeLorean achieves better performance than unsupervised and some supervised methods on abductive text generation and counterfactual story revision according to automatic and human evaluation.
A minor penalty for the disfavored style is sufficient for preference alignment.
ORPO performs preference optimization without requiring a reference model.
ORPO uses the odds ratio to contrast favored and disfavored styles during supervised fine-tuning.
The odds ratio is a sensible choice for contrasting favored and disfavored styles during supervised fine-tuning for language models of sizes from 125M to 7B.
ORPO fine-tuned models trained on UltraFeedback alone outperform state-of-the-art language models with more parameters.
Cross Copy Networks (CCN) explores the current dialogue context and similar dialogue instances' logical structure simultaneously.
Cross Copy Networks (CCN) outperforms existing state-of-the-art content generation models on court debate dialogue generation and customer service dialogue generation.
Fine-grained paraphrasing suggests paraphrases that achieve the intended level of politeness under a given communication circumstance.
