Fine-grained paraphrasing reduces the potential for misalignment between the speaker’s intentions and the listener’s perceptions.
Weakly-Supervised temporal Article Grounding localizes groundable sentences in an article to a relevant video, where sentences may be at different semantic scales.
YouwikiHow is a dataset for Weakly-Supervised temporal Article Grounding that uses multi-scale descriptions from wikiHow articles and YouTube videos.
DualMIL uses a two-level MIL loss and a single-/cross-sentence constraint loss for Weakly-Supervised temporal Article Grounding.
SynSetExpan enables entity set expansion and synonym discovery to mutually enhance each other.
SynSetExpan uses a synonym discovery model to include popular entities' infrequent synonyms into the entity set.
SynSetExpan uses the set expansion model to generate pseudo training data for fine-tuning the synonym discovery model.
The Synonym-Enhanced Set Expansion (SE2) dataset is the first large-scale crowdsourced dataset for studying the interplays of entity set expansion and synonym discovery.
Chunk-wise training divides reasoning rationales into semantically coherent chunks using heuristic search and focuses small language model learning on one chunk per iteration.
Chunk-wise training separates non-reasoning chunks from reasoning chunks during small language model training, increasing the fraction of core reasoning tokens in the training iteration.
Skip-thinking training enables small language models to skip non-reasoning medium chunks to reach the answer.
Skip-thinking training improves reasoning speed while maintaining accuracy for small language models.
KP-PLM can be flexibly combined with existing mainstream pre-trained language models.
A knowledge sub-graph is constructed from knowledge bases for each context.
Multiple continuous prompt rules transform a knowledge sub-graph into natural language prompts.
Prompt relevance inspection is a knowledge-aware self-supervised task.
Masked prompt modeling is a knowledge-aware self-supervised task.
SynGen recognizes biomedical concepts in input text using span-based predictions.
SynGen uses a synonym distance regularizer to minimize synonym generalization error.
SynGen uses a noise perturbation regularizer to minimize synonym generalization error.
SynGen has a theoretical bound on synonym generalization error.
SynGen outperforms previous dictionary-based models on biomedical named entity recognition benchmarks.
Hypergraph attention networks (HyperGAT) are highly expressive for inductive text classification.
Hypergraph attention networks (HyperGAT) are computationally efficient for inductive text classification.
ARSJoint jointly learns abstract retrieval, rationale selection, and stance prediction using a machine reading comprehension framework that incorporates claim information.
A regularization term enhances information exchanges and constraints between abstract retrieval and rationale selection by linking sentence attention scores and estimated outputs.

DIDS ensures intra-domain consistency by grouping training data based on learning effects using a gradient clustering algorithm that employs a proxy language model and dimensionality reduction to reduce computational overhead.

DIDS measures domain impact with a Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) guided metric that quantifies how domain-specific parameter updates affect model output distributions on downstream tasks, with theoretical guarantees.

DIDS determines optimal domain sampling ratios by combining FIM-guided domain impact assessment and loss learning trajectories indicating domain-specific potential while accounting for diminishing marginal returns.
DIDS improves average performance on downstream tasks while maintaining comparable training efficiency.
The existence of inactive training examples in neural machine translation depends on the data distribution.
An identification model distinguishes inactive training examples from active examples using sentence-level output probabilities.
A rejuvenation model re-labels inactive training examples using forward-translation.
Rejuvenated training examples and active training examples are combined to train the final neural machine translation model.
Data rejuvenation improves the performance of strong neural machine translation models.
Data rejuvenation stabilizes and accelerates the training process of neural machine translation models, resulting in models with better generalization capability.
Safety mechanisms in large language models are predominantly prevalent in the middle-to-late layers.
SABER bypasses safety alignment in large language models by connecting two intermediate layers s and e (with s < e) via a residual connection.
SABER causes only a marginal change in the perplexity of large language models.
TaskWeb is a large-scale benchmark of pairwise task transfers for NLP tasks using different model types, sizes, and adaptation methods.
TaskShop uses TaskWeb to estimate the benefit of using a source task for learning a new target task.
TaskShop uses TaskWeb to choose a subset of helpful training tasks for multi-task training.
TaskShop improves the overall rankings and top-k precision of source tasks.
TaskShop builds smaller multi-task training sets that improve zero-shot performance.
The high variance sensitivity of softmax is a primary cause of attention entropy collapse.
Attention methods that control or are insensitive to the variance of attention logits prevent attention entropy collapse and enable more stable training.
The concentration of attention probabilities increases the probability matrix norm, leading to gradient exploding.
A single Bradley-Terry model has an irreducible error when human preferences follow a mixture distribution of diverse subgroups.
MiCRo models diverse human preferences using a mixture of preferences.
MiCRo uses an online routing strategy to dynamically adapt mixture weights based on specific context to resolve ambiguity in preference modeling.
MiCRo improves personalized preference learning on downstream tasks.
MefMax uniquely assigns tokens to frequency classes to group tokens by similar frequencies and equalize frequency mass between classes.
Fˆ2-Softmax decomposes the target token probability distribution into the conditional probability of the frequency class and the conditional probability of the token given the frequency class.
Fˆ2-Softmax and MefMax improve the diversity and quality of generated texts.
MolCA enables language models to understand both text- and graph-based molecular contents via a cross-modal projector.
The cross-modal projector in MolCA is implemented as a Q-Former to connect a graph encoder's representation space and a language model's text space.
MolCA employs a uni-modal adapter (LoRA) for efficient adaptation of language models to downstream tasks.
MolCA retains language models' ability of open-ended text generation and augments them with 2D graph information.
MolCA outperforms baselines on molecule captioning, IUPAC name prediction, and molecule-text retrieval tasks.
RoBERTa, BERT and DistilBERT exhibit difficulties with basic arithmetic, compositional semantics, and lexical semantics on the CoQA task.
Ensembles of models enhanced with linguistic knowledge via multitask learning outperform baseline models on the CoQA task, with the largest gains on the hardest question classes.
RoBERTa, BERT and DistilBERT differ in their ability to represent compositional and lexical information on the CoQA task.
DuRecDial 2.0 contains bilingual parallel human-to-human recommendation dialogs.
DuRecDial 2.0 contains annotations for profile, goal, knowledge, context, and response in both English and Chinese.
Monolingual, multilingual, and cross-lingual conversational recommendation baselines are built for DuRecDial 2.0.
Additional English data improves Chinese conversational recommendation performance.
mT6 uses machine translation, translation pair span corruption, and translation span corruption as cross-lingual text-to-text pre-training tasks.
mT6 employs a partially non-autoregressive objective for text-to-text pre-training.
mT6 improves cross-lingual transferability over mT5.
Training data attribution retrieves influential training samples for test predictions in a given language from multilingual data.
Multilingual language models rely on data from multiple languages during fine-tuning.
The reliance of multilingual language models on data from multiple languages increases during fine-tuning.
Training samples from other languages reinforce and complement the knowledge from the test language's data in multilingual language models.
Perspective-taking prompting inspires large language models to integrate diverse human perspectives and self-regulate their responses.
Perspective-taking prompting reduces both toxicity and bias in large language model responses.

The Psychology of Falsehood survey explores the evolving interplay between traditional fact-checking approaches and psychological concepts including cognitive biases, social dynamics, and emotional responses.
The Psychology of Falsehood survey reveals critical limitations of current misinformation detection methods through analysis using human psychology and behavior.
The Psychology of Falsehood survey identifies opportunities for improvement in misinformation detection through analysis using human psychology and behavior.

The Psychology of Falsehood survey outlines future research directions for misinformation detection including neuro-behavioural models that integrate technological factors with the complexities of human cognition and social influence.
Transformers require at least log2(n+1) layers to handle entity tracking with n state changes.
Chain and Causal Attention tracks entity states with a single layer by modeling attention as an adjacency matrix.
Chain and Causal Attention achieves the same entity tracking performance with fewer layers.
Chain and Causal Attention maintains competitive performance on standard natural language modeling tasks.
Chain and Causal Attention reveals structured internal representations of attention.
OpenAsp evaluates multi-document open aspect-based summarization.
A cost-effective annotation protocol derives an open aspect dataset from existing generic multi-document summarization datasets.
OpenAsp contains high-quality content.
Realistic multi-document open aspect-based summarization is challenging for current state-of-the-art summarization models and large language models.
Open-domain Korean multi-turn response selection models make incorrect predictions based on superficial patterns without comprehensive context understanding.
An adversarial dataset evaluates open-domain Korean multi-turn response selection models for context-understanding weaknesses.
A strategy builds robust multi-turn response selection models for adversarial environments.
Cross-lingual Expert Language Models (X-ELM) mitigate inter-language competition for model parameters by independently training language models on subsets of the multilingual corpus.
X-ELM specializes to different languages while remaining effective as a multilingual ensemble.
X-ELM outperforms jointly trained multilingual models across all evaluated languages when given the same compute budget and these gains transfer to downstream tasks.
X-ELM can adapt to new languages by iteratively adding new experts without catastrophic forgetting.
Asynchronous training of X-ELM reduces hardware requirements for multilingual training.
A multi-aspect controllable text generation method searches for the intersection areas of multiple attribute distributions.
A multi-aspect controllable text generation method estimates the attribute space using an autoencoder structure.
A multi-aspect controllable text generation method iteratively minimizes distances to points representing different attributes to approach the intersections of multiple attribute distributions.
A multi-aspect controllable text generation method maps to attribute-relevant sentences using a prefix-tuning-based decoder.
A multi-aspect controllable text generation method achieves better attribute relevance and text quality than strong baselines.

ChatGPT outperforms many popular models in human evaluation on most metrics for text summarization, text simplification, and grammatical error correction but underperforms in classic automatic evaluation metrics.
Human reviewers rate the gold reference outputs worse than the best models' outputs on text summarization, text simplification, and grammatical error correction.

GPT-4 ranks models' outputs with reasonable alignment to human judgement on text summarization, text simplification, and grammatical error correction, with lower alignment in grammatical error correction.
ICL CIPHERS reformulates in-context learning tasks using bijective substitution ciphers.
LLMs consistently achieve better performance on in-context learning tasks reformulated with bijective substitution ciphers compared to non-bijective substitution ciphers.
LLMs internally decode ciphered inputs in in-context learning tasks.
ZEST evaluates NLP systems on unseen tasks defined by English task descriptions.
ZEST formulates task descriptions as questions that apply to many possible inputs.
ZEST tests specific types of systematic generalization.
Multi-modal Contrastive Learning for multimodal review helpfulness prediction uses mutual information between input modalities to elaborate cross-modal relations.
Adaptive Weighting increases flexibility in optimization for Multi-modal Contrastive Learning in multimodal review helpfulness prediction.
The Multimodal Interaction module addresses the unalignment of multimodal data to produce more reasonable multimodal representations for multimodal review helpfulness prediction.
Knowledge Enhanced Prompt Learning (KEPL) extracts hypernym-hyponym relations from Chinese text.
Knowledge Enhanced Prompt Learning uses Hearst-like patterns as prior knowledge.
Knowledge Enhanced Prompt Learning uses a Dynamic Adaptor Architecture to select matching patterns to form prompts for text.
A Chinese hypernym-hyponym relation extraction dataset contains three typical scenarios: baike, news, and We-media.
Revising a text with ChatGPT and measuring the similarity between the original text and the revised text achieves zero-shot black-box detection of LLM-generated texts.
Higher similarity between a text and its ChatGPT-revised version indicates that the text is LLM-generated.
Outward analysis in low-resource harmful meme detection retrieves relative memes with annotations to leverage label information as auxiliary signals for an LMM agent.
Inward analysis in low-resource harmful meme detection elicits knowledge-revising behavior within an LMM agent to derive well-generalized insights into meme harmfulness.
Combining outward and inward analysis in low-resource harmful meme detection enables dialectical reasoning over intricate and implicit harm-indicative patterns.
Continued pre-training from Pythia checkpoints with injected sequences creates a controlled setting for studying verbatim memorization.
Non-trivial amounts of repetition are necessary for verbatim memorization to occur.
Later and presumably better checkpoints are more likely to verbatim memorize sequences, including out-of-distribution sequences.
The generation of memorized sequences is triggered by distributed model states encoding high-level features and uses general language modeling capabilities.
Unlearning methods often fail to remove verbatim memorized information and degrade language model quality.
Current uncertainty quantification methods have limitations in handling long text generation.
Luq is a sampling-based uncertainty quantification approach for long text generation.
Luq correlates with generated text factuality scores more strongly than existing baseline methods.
Luq-Ensemble ensembles responses from multiple large language models and selects the response with the lowest uncertainty.
Luq-Ensemble improves response factuality compared to the best standalone large language model.
IndoNLI is the first human-elicited natural language inference dataset for Indonesian.

IndoNLI incorporates linguistic phenomena including numerical reasoning, structural changes, idioms, temporal reasoning, and spatial reasoning to provide a challenging test for Indonesian natural language inference.
IndoNLI uses expert-annotated data exclusively as a test set.
XLM-R outperforms other pre-trained models on IndoNLI.
The expert-annotated test set of IndoNLI is especially challenging as the best model performance is substantially below human performance.
The expert-annotated data in IndoNLI is more diverse and contains fewer annotation artifacts than the crowd-annotated data.
Representing an initial set of bilingual word alignments as a graph and predicting additional word alignment edges leverages multiparallelity for word alignment.
A graph algorithm for predicting additional word alignment edges is inspired by recommender systems.
A graph algorithm for predicting additional word alignment edges is based on network link prediction.
SpeechQE is a task for quality estimation of direct speech translation.
The SpeechQE benchmark evaluates quality estimation of direct speech translation.
A novel end-to-end system for quality estimation of direct speech translation leverages pre-trained text LLM.
End-to-end approaches are better suited for quality estimation of direct speech translation than cascaded approaches that use text-based quality estimation systems.
Quality estimation of direct speech translation needs to be studied as a separate problem from text quality estimation.
Self-ICL generates pseudo-input-label pairs for a test input by creating pseudo-inputs and predicting their labels via zero-shot prompting.
Self-ICL performs in-context learning for the test input using self-generated pseudo-input-label pairs as demonstrations.
Self-ICL outperforms zero-shot baselines on BIG-Bench Hard tasks.
Self-ICL with zero-shot chain-of-thought achieves results comparable to using real demonstrations.

PubMedVision is a dataset of medical visual question answering samples derived from PubMed image-text pairs that have been refined and processed using multimodal large language models for denoising and reformatting.
PubMedVision enhances the medical multimodal capabilities of multimodal large language models on the MMMU Health & Medicine benchmark.
PubMedVision has superior data quality compared to medical vision-text datasets constructed by other methods.
HuatuoGPT-Vision is a 34B-parameter medical multimodal large language model trained on PubMedVision.
HuatuoGPT-Vision achieves superior performance in medical multimodal scenarios among open-source multimodal large language models.
RE-Net is an autoregressive architecture for predicting future interactions in temporal knowledge graphs.
RE-Net models the occurrence of a fact in a temporal knowledge graph as a probability distribution conditioned on temporal sequences of past knowledge graphs.
RE-Net employs a recurrent event encoder to encode past facts in temporal knowledge graphs.
RE-Net uses a neighborhood aggregator to model the connection of facts at the same timestamp in temporal knowledge graphs.
Word equivalence classes define word-level information transfer pathways for multilingual machine translation.
Graph networks fuse word embeddings across languages for multilingual machine translation.
Word embedding semantics are better aligned across languages in multilingual machine translation.
Translation quality improves for high-resource and low-resource multilingual machine translation.

Word embedding fusion for multilingual machine translation requires minimal additional trainable parameters and a limited increase in computational costs while keeping inference time identical to baseline systems.
Existing representations of structured label spaces in large-scale multi-label text classification have a structural issue when applied to the ICD-9 ontology.
An alternative representation for structured label spaces in large-scale multi-label text classification uses the depth in the label hierarchy.
CoPHE is a set of count-preserving hierarchical evaluation metrics for large-scale multi-label text classification based on the depth of the label hierarchy.
Dynamic Blocking prevents the language model from generating the next token in the source sequence in the step immediately following the generation of a source token.
Unsupervised paraphrasing with pre-trained language models achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Quora Question Pair and ParaNMT datasets.
Unsupervised paraphrasing with pre-trained language models is robust to domain shift between the Quora Question Pair and ParaNMT datasets.
Unsupervised paraphrasing with pre-trained language models transfers to paraphrasing in other languages without additional finetuning.
A doubly sparse data-parallel sampler for the hierarchical Dirichlet process topic model uses a representation of certain conditional distributions within a hierarchical Dirichlet process.
A doubly sparse data-parallel sampler for the hierarchical Dirichlet process topic model utilizes all available sources of sparsity in natural language.
Many well-known results for the standard Transformer directly transfer to linear Transformers and Fast Weight Programmers.
Recurrent Fast Weight Programmers and self-referential weight matrices enable linear Transformers to generalize on the parity problem.
Analogy identification associates analogical math word problem pairs in a latent space by encoding analogical pairs close together and non-analogical pairs far apart.
A solution discriminator enhances the association between a math word problem and its true solution.
An analogical learning strategy for math word problem solving improves performance with fewer parameters.
Analogical learning from easy math word problems provides stronger generalization ability for solving difficult math word problems.
A multimodal video summarization task setting produces a predefined number of keyframe-caption pairs by simultaneously extracting keyframes and generating captions.
A dataset for multimodal video summarization with keyframe-caption pairs is constructed by expanding existing datasets.
An evaluation framework assesses multimodal video summarization systems that generate keyframe-caption pairs.
Two baseline systems generate keyframe-caption pairs for multimodal video summarization.
Attribute Collapse causes the fluency of generated text to rapidly decrease when the control strength exceeds a critical value, rendering the text unusable.
Air-Decoding reconstructs attribute distributions to balance the weights between attribute words and non-attribute words.
Air-Decoding avoids Attribute Collapse by using reconstructed attribute distributions to guide language model generation.
Air-Decoding achieves state-of-the-art control performance on multiple controllable text generation tasks.
Cross-Thought trains a Transformer-based sequence encoder on short sequences to automatically select the most useful information for predicting masked words.
Cross-Thought outperforms state-of-the-art encoders trained with continuous sentence signals and traditional masked language modeling baselines on question answering and textual entailment tasks.
Cross-Thought achieves state-of-the-art results on HotpotQA (full-wiki setting) by improving intermediate information retrieval performance.
A gating strategy smooths the decision making and question generation dialogue states in a single decoder to bridge the processes and provide a richer dialogue state reference.
A gating strategy achieves state-of-the-art results on the OR-ShARC dataset.
SeqMix performs mixup for both sequences and token-level labels of queried samples in each iteration of active sequence labeling.
SeqMix uses a discriminator to judge the plausibility of generated sequences during sequence mixup.
SeqMix improves the performance of active sequence labeling on Named Entity Recognition and Event Detection tasks.
Large Language Models fail to reason about physical attributes in event implications.
Existing approaches misrepresent the surprising abilities of Large Language Models due to improper task encodings.
Proper model prompting is especially useful for out-of-domain attributes or when data is limited.
APoLLo combines adapter learning and prompt learning for vision-language models.
APoLLo uses trainable cross-attention-based adapter layers to strengthen the alignment between vision and language modalities.
APoLLo enforces consistency between encoder branches receiving augmented inputs to prevent overfitting in downstream tasks.
APoLLo improves generalization to novel classes for diverse image recognition datasets.
DivEMT is the first publicly available dataset of post-editing effort for Neural Machine Translation across typologically diverse languages.
Post-editing Neural Machine Translation outputs is consistently faster than translating from scratch.
Post-editing effectiveness for Neural Machine Translation varies with the typological relatedness of the target language to English, independent of system architecture and training data size.
CRDR unifies query rewriting and context modelling in a single framework.
CRDR's query rewriting explicitly highlights relevant terms in the query context to improve the contextualized query embedding.
CRDR performs only necessary modifications on the original query for query rewriting, improving accuracy and efficiency.
CRDR outperforms baseline models in query rewriting quality and context-aware ranking quality.
DisLoRA decomposes pretrained weight matrices into orthogonal backbone and task-specific subspaces using singular value decomposition.
DisLoRA dynamically identifies task-specific directions.
DisLoRA employs adaptive soft orthogonal regularization with mean-normalization mechanism.
DisLoRA balances task-specific and orthogonal losses without manual tuning, ensuring robust training stability.
DisLoRA outperforms established parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods on GLUE and Commonsense Reasoning benchmarks.
ToTTo is an open-domain English controlled table-to-text generation dataset for producing one-sentence descriptions from Wikipedia tables and highlighted cells.
A table-to-text dataset construction process produces natural and faithful targets by having annotators directly revise existing candidate sentences from Wikipedia.
Existing table-to-text methods often hallucinate phrases that are not supported by the source table.
OpenASL is a large-scale American Sign Language (ASL) - English dataset collected from online video sites in multiple domains and is the largest publicly available ASL translation dataset.
Sign search serves as a pretext task for pre-training in sign language translation.
Mouthing and handshape features are fused for sign language translation.
Sign search as a pretext task for pre-training and fusion of mouthing and handshape features improve sign language translation quality over baseline models.
Stacking layers improves the representation ability of neural machine translation models.
Adjacent layers in deep Transformer systems for neural machine translation perform similarly.
Shallow-to-Deep Training learns deep neural machine translation models by stacking shallow models.
Shallow-to-Deep Training reduces training time for deep neural machine translation models compared to training from scratch.
Recognizing semantic differences is a token-level regression task.
An unsupervised approach for recognizing semantic differences uses word alignment and sentence-level contrastive learning.
An unsupervised approach for recognizing semantic differences based on word alignment and sentence-level contrastive learning has a robust correlation to gold labels.
All unsupervised approaches for recognizing semantic differences leave a large margin of improvement.
FastClass retrieves class-relevant documents from an external unlabeled corpus using dense text representation.
FastClass selects an optimal subset of class-relevant documents to train a classifier.
FastClass is less reliant on initial class descriptions than keyword-driven methods in weakly-supervised text classification.
FastClass outperforms keyword-driven models in classification accuracy on a wide range of classification tasks.
FastClass achieves faster training speed than keyword-driven models in weakly-supervised text classification.
AdapterDrop removes adapters from lower transformer layers during training and inference.
AdapterDrop dynamically reduces computational overhead in multi-task inference with minimal performance loss.
AdapterDrop prunes adapters from AdapterFusion, improving inference efficiency without performance loss.
Speaker Persona Detection (SPD) detects speaker personas from conversational text.
Persona Match on Persona-Chat (PMPC) is a dataset for detecting speaker personas from conversational text.
U2P matching networks detect speaker personas from conversational text through fine-grained matching of utterances to persona profiles.
U2P models outperform baseline models in detecting speaker personas from conversational text.
Multiple reward functions for dialog training incorporate implicit conversational cues that indicate positive human feedback, including language similarity, elicitation of laughter, and sentiment.
Offline RL algorithms for dialog training use KL-control to penalize divergence from a pre-trained prior language model.
Offline RL algorithms for dialog training use a pessimistic strategy to handle uncertainty.
A dialog model trained with the novel offline RL algorithms achieves higher user ratings than existing deep offline RL approaches in open-domain conversations.
The offline RL method is viable for improving any existing generative dialog model with a static dataset of human feedback.
VeriFact identifies and resolves incomplete and missing facts in long-form responses.
FactRBench evaluates both precision and recall of factuality in long-form responses.
FactRBench provides reference fact sets from advanced LLMs and human-written answers.
VeriFact enhances fact completeness in long-form factuality evaluation.
VeriFact preserves complex facts with critical relational information during factuality evaluation.
On FactRBench, larger language models in the same family achieve higher precision and recall.
On FactRBench, high precision in factuality evaluation does not necessarily imply high recall.
SALAM uses an auxiliary agent to assist the main LLM in learning from mistakes through interactive cooperation.
SALAM uses a student assistant agent that probes the main LLM, analyzes its errors, and collects the interaction in a mistake memory.
SALAM uses a study assistant that provides guidelines by retrieving relevant cases from a mistake memory to help the main LLM avoid similar errors.
SALAM customizes the study assistant to provide LLM-specific guidance through imitation learning from successful guidance experiences.
SALAM improves LLM accuracy on two challenging frameworks.
WhyAct is a dataset of visual actions in lifestyle vlogs manually annotated with action reasons.
A multimodal model infers the reasons for actions in lifestyle vlogs using visual and textual information.
Recurrent Attention (RAN) learns attention weights without token-to-token interaction.
Recurrent Attention (RAN) improves attention capacity through layer-to-layer interaction.
Recurrent Attention (RAN) models outperform Transformer models in certain machine translation scenarios while using fewer parameters and less inference time.
Applying Recurrent Attention (RAN) to the decoder of Transformer models consistently improves translation performance.
FANToM stress-tests theory of mind within information-asymmetric conversational contexts via question answering.
FANToM formulates multiple types of questions that demand the same underlying reasoning to identify illusory or false sense of theory of mind capabilities in large language models.
FANToM is challenging for state-of-the-art large language models, which perform worse than humans even with chain-of-thought reasoning or fine-tuning.

A multilingual expertly curated dataset of COVID-19 vaccine misinformation contains annotated tweets from Brazil, Indonesia, and Nigeria with annotations for relevance to COVID-19 vaccines, presence of misinformation, and misinformation themes.
Percentage changes in the number of new COVID-19 cases are positively associated with COVID-19 vaccine misinformation rates in a staggered manner in Brazil and Indonesia.
COVID-19 vaccine misinformation rates are significantly positively associated across Brazil, Indonesia, and Nigeria.
An unsupervised non-transferable learning method for text classification does not require annotated target domain data.
A secret key component enables recovery of target domain access through explicit and implicit methods.
Unlearning and obfuscation are formally distinct concepts.
A probing-based evaluation framework assesses whether approaches genuinely remove targeted information.
DF-MCQ removes knowledge about target individuals by flattening the model predictive distribution over automatically generated multiple-choice questions using KL-divergence.
DF-MCQ triggers appropriate refusal behaviour.
DF-MCQ exhibits higher random choice-level uncertainty than obfuscation on probing questions.
An interactive multi-round learning paradigm uses black-box large language models as reasoning teachers.
An interactive multi-round learning paradigm enables student models to expose deficiencies to black-box teacher models, which then provide customized training data.
Self-reflection learning motivates student models to learn from self-made mistakes.
Learning from self-reflection and large language models is tailored to the student model's learning status.
A context keyword selection model improves the language quality of generated math word problems.
An equation consistency constraint for math equations improves the mathematical validity of generated math word problems.
Moderated Twitch comments are annotated for norm violations.
Existing models perform poorly for norm violation detection in live-stream chats.
The informational context used by humans for live-stream moderation is identified.
Models that leverage contextual information for norm violation detection in live-stream chats are trained.
Appropriate contextual information boosts norm violation detection performance in live-stream chats.
Underpowered experiments are common in the NLP literature.
For several tasks in the GLUE benchmark, small test sets result in underpowered comparisons to state-of-the-art models.
The typical experimental design for human rating studies is underpowered to detect small model differences that are frequently studied.
Nearest neighbor local attention focuses each phoneme token only on adjacent phoneme tokens before and after it.
Phoneme-level local adaptive weights loss function focuses the model more on hard-to-synthesize parts of the mel-spectrogram.
VISaGE is an evaluation dataset for vision language models containing typical and exceptional images.
Conceptual understanding in vision language models degrades when incongruent images violate the pragmatic prior's congruency assumption.

The effect of incongruent images violating the pragmatic prior's congruency assumption is stronger than the effect of the semantic prior in vision language models when queried about individual instances.
CAFE uses a coarse-grained filtering method that leverages retrieval heads to identify and rank relevant documents.
CAFE uses a fine-grained steering method that guides attention to the most relevant content.
CAFE outperforms baseline methods in multi-document question answering.
HIT's Head-Tail Detector detects boundary tokens of nested named entities using multi-head self-attention and a bi-affine classifier.
HIT's Token Interaction Tagger characterizes internal token connections within entity boundaries using traditional sequence labeling approaches.
PropRAG uses context-rich propositions for knowledge representation in Retrieval Augmented Generation.
PropRAG uses an efficient, LLM-free online beam search over proposition paths to discover multi-step reasoning chains.
PropRAG achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on the 2Wiki, HotpotQA, and MuSiQue benchmarks.
Modularized multilingual neural machine translation can enjoy the benefits of multi-way training without the capacity bottleneck.
The interlingual space of modularized multilingual neural machine translation allows convenient model modification.
Incrementally added modules in modularized multilingual neural machine translation exhibit better performance than singly trained models.
The zero-shot performance of incrementally added modules in modularized multilingual neural machine translation is comparable to supervised models.
Temporal Message Passing (TeMP) combines graph neural networks, temporal dynamics models, data imputation, and frequency-based gating techniques for temporal knowledge graph completion.
Temporal Message Passing (TeMP) outperforms the state of the art on multiple standard temporal knowledge graph completion benchmarks.
Temporal knowledge graph datasets contain important sources of variability both within and across datasets.
Simple baselines for temporal knowledge graph completion outperform the state of the art in specific settings.
Outlier Suppression+ applies channel-wise shifting to remove outlier asymmetry in activations.
Outlier Suppression+ applies channel-wise scaling to balance quantization burden.
Channel-wise shifting and scaling in Outlier Suppression+ maintain equivalence when migrated into subsequent modules.
Outlier Suppression+ uses a fast and stable scheme to calculate effective shifting and scaling values.
Outlier Suppression+ achieves near-floating-point performance at 8-bit and 6-bit quantization for both small and large language models.
An attributed value encoder and knowledge graph subgraph partitioning model various types of attribute triples for entity alignment.
A hard experimental setting for entity alignment uses equivalent entity pairs with very different names as the test set.

An entity alignment method that uses an attributed value encoder and knowledge graph subgraph partitioning outperforms existing methods in cross-lingual and monolingual entity alignment under both regular and hard evaluation settings.
CouldAsk evaluates reformulation of unanswerable questions in document-grounded question answering.
State-of-the-art large language models have limited capabilities in reformulating unanswerable questions.
Unsuccessful reformulations by large language models often involve rephrasing the question or generating identical questions.
The internal attention structure of standard Transformer language models does not support the aggregation of text information into a single dense representation required by dense encoders.
Condenser is a pre-training architecture for dense retrieval that conditions language model prediction on a dense representation.
Condenser outperforms standard Transformer language models on text retrieval and similarity tasks.
The NLLB3.3B model consistently outperforms other multilingual large language models in English-Amharic bidirectional translation.
Mistranslation, omission, untranslated segments, and additions are common errors in English-Amharic machine translation by multilingual large language models.
Mistranslation is the most frequent error in English-Amharic machine translation by multilingual large language models.
Punctuation and spelling errors are rare in English-Amharic machine translation by multilingual large language models.
BabyBERTa trained with unmasking-removal policy is a stronger starting point for downstream tasks than RoBERTa masking policy when trained on a small amount of data.
The advantage of unmasking-removal policy over RoBERTa masking policy for downstream tasks decreases with increasing training data.
The Text-to-Query task paradigm unifies semantic parsing tasks across various query languages.
Query skeletons are a shared optimization target of Text-to-Query tasks.
A general dynamic data augmentation framework for Text-to-Query tasks diagnoses model-specific weaknesses in handling query skeletons to synthesize targeted training data.
Dynamic data augmentation for Text-to-Query tasks achieves state-of-the-art performance with minimal synthesized training data.
A Transformer-based headline generation method guarantees inclusion of a specified phrase in the headline.
A headline generation strategy for lexically constrained headline generation utilizes the controllable generation order of Transformer.
Headline generation methods that guarantee phrase inclusion achieve comparable quality to previous Transformer-based methods.
A headline generation strategy for lexically constrained headline generation achieves better performance than previous strategies.
ReFill retrieves diverse existing text, masks schema-specific tokens, and refills with tokens relevant to a new schema.
ReFill produces more diverse text queries than standard SQL-to-Text generation models.
ReFill outperforms prior data-augmentation methods in adapting Text-to-SQL parsers to new database schemas.
SentiLARE introduces word-level linguistic knowledge of part-of-speech tags and sentiment polarity inferred from SentiWordNet into pre-trained language models.
A context-aware sentiment attention mechanism acquires the sentiment polarity of each word using its part-of-speech tag by querying SentiWordNet.
The label-aware masked language model pre-training task constructs knowledge-aware language representations.
SentiLARE achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple sentiment analysis tasks.
IDEAW enables efficient watermark locating in neural audio watermarking through a dual-embedding approach.
IDEAW enhances model reasonableness and stability in neural audio watermarking by accounting for the impact of the attack layer on the invertible neural network during robustness training.
IDEAW provides increased capacity for embedded messages in neural audio watermarking.
MedCLIP decouples images and texts for multimodal contrastive learning to scale the usable training data combinatorially at low cost.
MedCLIP replaces the InfoNCE loss with a semantic matching loss based on medical knowledge to eliminate false negatives in contrastive learning.
MedCLIP outperforms state-of-the-art methods on zero-shot prediction, supervised classification, and image-text retrieval.
MedCLIP outperforms the state-of-the-art method with less pre-training data.

EchoMist is the first comprehensive benchmark for implicit misinformation that embeds false assumptions in queries to LLMs and targets misinformation from diverse sources including human-AI conversations and social media.
State-of-the-art LLMs often fail to detect false premises in queries containing implicit misinformation and generate counterfactual explanations.
A rule-based approach performs gender-neutral rewriting for English.
NeuTral Rewriter performs gender-neutral rewriting for English.
NeuTral Rewriter is trained on data generated by a rule-based approach.
WinoBias+ is a manually curated synthetic benchmark for gender-neutral rewriting.
A manually curated natural data benchmark for gender-neutral rewriting uses OpenSubtitles and Reddit.
Dialogue Act Script (DAS) is a structured framework for encoding, localizing, and generating multilingual dialogues from abstract intent representations.
Dialogue Act Script (DAS) generates culturally and contextually appropriate dialogues in the target language without direct translation of utterances.

Dialogue Act Script (DAS) uses structured dialogue act representations to support flexible localization across languages, mitigate translation artifacts, and enable fluent, naturalistic conversations.

Dialogue Act Script (DAS)-generated dialogues outperform machine-translated and human-translated dialogues on cultural relevance, coherence, and situational appropriateness in Italian, German, and Chinese.
MODE-LSTM disentangles the LSTM hidden state into independently updated small hidden states.
MODE-LSTM applies an orthogonal constraint on the recurrent matrices of the independently updated small hidden states.
MODE-LSTM uses sliding windows of different sizes to extract multi-scale n-gram features.
MODE-LSTM achieves better or competitive performance against state-of-the-art models on eight sentence classification benchmark datasets.
Combining MODE-LSTM with BERT improves generalization performance for sentence classification.
Structured pruning of large language models parameterizes weight matrices using low-rank factorization and adaptively removes rank-1 components during training.

Structured pruning of large language models outperforms unstructured and block-structured pruning at various compression levels and achieves speedups during training and inference on language modeling tasks.
Structured pruning of large language models applies to adaptive word embeddings in large language models and to BERT on downstream fine-tuning classification benchmarks.
A neural semantic parser converts natural language questions into intermediate question representations.
A non-trainable transpiler converts intermediate question representations to SPARQL queries.
Converting natural language questions to SPARQL queries via intermediate question representations achieves execution accuracy comparable to state-of-the-art text-to-SQL methods on the Spider dataset.
CURIOUS creates a graph of relevant influences for a defeasible reasoning question and then uses that graph as an additional input when answering the question.
Explicitly modeling the question scenario improves defeasible reasoning performance.
CURIOUS achieves state-of-the-art performance on three different defeasible reasoning datasets.
Abstractive Open Information Extraction includes both surface-form relations and inferred relations.
A new semantics-based metric is necessary for evaluating abstractive Open Information Extraction extractions.
META leverages metadata as an additional source of weak supervision for text classification.
META organizes text data and metadata into a text-rich network to capture appropriate combinations of metadata using network motifs.
META ranks and filters motif instances based on seed words to distill highly label-indicative seed motifs that provide additional weak supervision.
META trains the classifier and expands seed words and seed motifs iteratively in a bootstrapping manner.
OTSeq2Set is an autoregressive sequence-to-set model for extreme multi-label text classification.
OTSeq2Set uses a bipartite matching loss function to enable permutation-invariant label prediction.
OTSeq2Set generates predictions using a student-forcing scheme.
OTSeq2Set uses optimal transport distance to focus on the closest labels in semantic label space.
OTSeq2Set outperforms competitive baselines on benchmark datasets for extreme multi-label text classification.
Projecting the input representation in the latent space produces contrastive explanations that capture only features differentiating two potential decisions.
Contrastive explanations identify, for a given input feature, the label for which it is useful and the alternative label against which it is useful.
Label-contrastive explanations provide fine-grained interpretability of model decisions.
Dual-space knowledge distillation unifies the output spaces of teacher and student models for knowledge distillation.
A cross-model attention mechanism automatically aligns representations of teacher and student models with different vocabularies.
Dual-space knowledge distillation supports knowledge distillation between any two large language models regardless of their vocabularies.
Dual-space knowledge distillation outperforms the current white-box knowledge distillation framework with various distance functions on task-agnostic instruction-following benchmarks.
Dual-space knowledge distillation surpasses existing knowledge distillation methods for large language models with different vocabularies on task-agnostic instruction-following benchmarks.
ReasoningLM enables structural subgraph reasoning for Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs.
A subgraph-aware self-attention mechanism imitates graph neural networks to perform structured reasoning.
An adaptation tuning strategy adapts model parameters using subgraphs with synthesized questions.
ReasoningLM achieves state-of-the-art performance on Question Answering over Knowledge Graphs with fewer updated parameters and less training data.
AimMerging dynamically monitors a language model's training status using learning and forgetting signals from the training trajectory.
AimMerging adaptively determines the timing and frequency of iterative model fusion using a training trajectory-guided merge controller.
AimMerging computes merging weights and executes model fusion using a rehearsal-based knowledge fusion module.
AimMerging improves learning new knowledge and prevents forgetting in continual learning.
Structural sentence positional encodings explicitly represent sentence positions.
Inter-sentence attentions capture sentence interactions and enhance sentence representation.
Structural relative positional encoding of sentences is the most effective for identifying discourse elements.
Inter-sentence attention vectors are useful as sentence representations for identifying discourse elements.
Z-LaVI endows language models with visual imagination capabilities.
Z-LaVI leverages two complementary types of visual imaginations: recalling existing images through retrieval and synthesizing nonexistent images via text-to-image generation.
Z-LaVI jointly exploits language inputs and visual imagination to compose a zero-shot solution to language tasks using a pretrained vision-language model.
Fueling language models with visual imagination leverages visual knowledge to solve plain language tasks.
Z-LaVI consistently improves the zero-shot performance of language models on diverse language tasks.
eQASC provides multiple candidate explanations per answer for the QASC multihop question-answering dataset.
eQASC-perturbed contains crowd-sourced perturbations of explanations that preserve validity to test consistency and generalization of explanation prediction models.
eOBQA adds explanation annotations to the OBQA dataset to test generalization of explanation prediction models trained on eQASC.
A delexicalized chain representation replaces repeated noun phrases with variables to form generalized reasoning chains.
Generalized reasoning chains maintain explanation quality while being more robust to certain perturbations.
A span-level bidirectional network uses an aspect decoder and an opinion decoder to extract triplets in aspect-to-opinion and opinion-to-aspect directions.
A similar span separation loss expands the KL divergence of similar spans during training to facilitate distinguishing correct spans.
An inference strategy removes conflicting triplets based on confidence scores.
A span-level bidirectional network outperforms state-of-the-art methods in predicting triplets with multi-token entities.
A span-level bidirectional network outperforms state-of-the-art methods in extracting triplets in sentences containing multiple triplets.
A translation quality estimation model jointly learns to score translations and rank translation pairs.
A translation quality estimation model that jointly learns to score translations and rank translation pairs uses cross-lingual sentence embeddings from pre-trained multilingual language models.
A translation quality estimation model that jointly learns to score translations and rank translation pairs achieves state-of-the-art results on the WMT 2019 QE as a Metric task.
A translation quality estimation model that jointly learns to score translations and rank translation pairs outperforms sentBLEU on the WMT 2019 Metrics task.
SPE constructs symmetrical prompts for subject and object prediction for factual probing in pretrained language models.
SPE outperforms previous probing methods for factual probing in pretrained language models.
Tiny-NewsRec uses a self-supervised domain-specific post-training method to adapt pre-trained language models to the news domain via a contrastive matching task between news titles and news bodies.

Tiny-NewsRec uses a two-stage knowledge distillation method that transfers knowledge from multiple teacher models at different post-training time steps to a student model in both post-training and finetuning stages, improving efficiency while maintaining performance for pre-trained language model-based news recommendation.
X-FACTR is a multilingual benchmark of cloze-style probes for factual knowledge retrieval in language models across typologically diverse languages.
Probing for factual knowledge retrieval handles multi-word entities.
Decoding algorithms for factual knowledge retrieval generate multi-token predictions.
State-of-the-art language models exhibit varying performance on factual knowledge retrieval in languages with different resource availability.
A code-switching-based method improves the ability of multilingual language models to access factual knowledge.
TFD operates during prediction without additional training for emotion recognition in conversations.
TFD does not balance data or modify the model structure for emotion recognition in conversations.
TFD extracts biases from emotion recognition in conversations models by generating counterfactual utterances and contexts.
TFD mitigates biases in emotion recognition in conversations using element-wise subtraction operations.
TFD improves generalization ability and fairness across different emotion recognition in conversations models.
CLIRMatrix is a collection of bilingual and multilingual datasets for Cross-Lingual Information Retrieval extracted automatically from Wikipedia.
CLIRMatrix is the largest and most comprehensive Cross-Lingual Information Retrieval dataset to date.
BI-139 is a bilingual dataset of queries in one language matched with relevant documents in another language for a large number of language pairs.
MULTI-8 is a multilingual dataset of queries and documents jointly aligned in multiple languages.
CoTKR generates reasoning traces and corresponding knowledge in an interleaved manner.
PAQAF leverages feedback from question answering models to optimize knowledge rewriters.
CoTKR generates the most beneficial knowledge representation for question answering models among previous knowledge rewriting methods.
Pre-training data creation for a specific community uses synthetic and retrieval-based methods to incorporate language, cultural heritage, and cultural values.

NileChat is a large language model for Egyptian Arabic and Moroccan Arabic, including Arabizi variants, that incorporates the language, cultural heritage, and cultural values of Egyptian and Moroccan communities.
NileChat outperforms existing Arabic-aware LLMs of similar size on understanding, translation, and cultural and values alignment benchmarks.
NileChat performs on par with larger models on understanding, translation, and cultural and values alignment benchmarks.

Federated Interactive Distillation (FedID) for large-scale pretraining language models utilizes a small amount of labeled data retained by the server to rectify local models during knowledge transfer.
A benchmarking framework for federated distillation in natural language processing spans multiple tasks with diverse data distributions and is based on the GLUE benchmark.
Federated Interactive Distillation (FedID) for large-scale pretraining language models outperforms existing methods in homogeneous and heterogeneous federated learning scenarios.
A generative framework for end-to-end argument mining frames argument mining outputs as a simple target sequence.
A constrained pointer mechanism models clues for all argument mining subtasks.
A reconstructed positional encoding alleviates order biases induced by autoregressive generation in argument mining.
A generative framework for end-to-end argument mining achieves state-of-the-art performance on two argument mining benchmarks.

Collaborative Performance Prediction (CPP) predicts large language model performance on downstream tasks by leveraging historical performance of various models and design factors for both model and task.
A collaborative dataset contains historical performance and additional design factors for large language models and tasks, collected from online platforms.
Collaborative Performance Prediction (CPP) predicts the performance of scaled large language models on downstream tasks more accurately than traditional scaling laws.
Collaborative Performance Prediction (CPP) enables detailed analysis of the importance of design factors in large language model performance prediction.
A large dataset of summaries paired with user reviews for products enables supervised training for opinion summarization.
Opinion summarization is formulated as jointly learning to select informative review subsets and summarize the opinions in these subsets.
In opinion summarization, the review subset choice is predicted as a latent variable by a small and simple selector.
In opinion summarization, amortized variational inference and policy gradient methods are used for joint training of the selector and summarizer.
In opinion summarization, selecting informative reviews improves the quality of summaries and reduces hallucinations.
CodeAgent is a multi-agent Large Language Model system for code review automation.
CodeAgent incorporates a supervisory agent called QA-Checker that ensures all agents' contributions address the initial review question.

CodeAgent achieves state-of-the-art performance in code review automation for detecting inconsistencies between code changes and commit messages, identifying vulnerability introductions, validating code style adherence, and suggesting code revisions.
T-Modules enables zero-shot cross-modal transfer between speech and text for translation tasks.
T-Modules encodes multilingual speech and text in a joint fixed-size representation space.
T-Modules is trained without cross-modal labeled translation data.
T-Modules sets a new state-of-the-art for zero-shot speech translation on Must-C.
T-Modules achieves the first results for zero-shot direct speech-to-speech and text-to-speech translation.
MathTutorBench is an open-source benchmark containing datasets and metrics that cover tutor abilities as defined by learning sciences research in dialog-based teaching.
A reward model scores the pedagogical quality of open-ended teacher responses.
The reward model discriminates expert from novice teacher responses.
Pedagogy and subject expertise form a trade-off that is navigated by the degree of tutoring specialization of the model.
Simpler questioning strategies fail in longer dialogs.
RL-TRC uses topic- and rhetorical-coherence tasks to enhance the perception of coherence with the target utterance in multi-party dialogue generation.
RL-TRC employs an agent that learns a coherence policy to guide response generation towards topical and rhetorical alignment with the target utterance in multi-party dialogue generation.
RL-TRC uses three discourse-aware rewards to assess coherence between the generated response and the target utterance for policy optimization in multi-party dialogue generation.
RL-TRC generates responses that are more coherent with the target utterances than state-of-the-art baselines in multi-party dialogue generation.
Catastrophic forgetting causes training instability in dense retrieval, where models learn and forget different negative groups during training iterations.
ANCE-Tele accumulates momentum negatives from past training iterations.
ANCE-Tele uses lookahead negatives as teleportations along the time axis to approximate future training iterations.
ANCE-Tele eliminates the dependency on sparse retrieval negatives for dense retrieval.
Teleportation negatives reduce catastrophic forgetting in dense retrieval training.
The first manually translated news bitext corpus between Portuguese and Emakhuwa exists.
News topic classification datasets for Emakhuwa exist.
Monolingual data for Emakhuwa exists.
Incorporating more data improves machine translation and news topic classification for Emakhuwa.
Multimodal RAG systems exhibit a U-shaped accuracy curve with respect to evidence position.
The Position Sensitivity Index (PSIp) quantifies position bias in multimodal RAG systems.
A visualization framework traces attention allocation patterns across decoder layers in multimodal RAG systems.
Position bias is more intense in multimodal RAG systems than in unimodal RAG systems.
Position bias in multimodal RAG systems increases logarithmically with retrieval range.
Many conventions in NLP are poorly suited for online hate speech detection.
A BERT-based method ranks scalar adjectives by intensity.
Intensity of scalar adjectives is modeled by vectors derived from contextualised representations.
BERT encodes rich knowledge about the semantics of scalar adjectives.
BERT provides better quality intensity rankings than static embeddings and previous models that use dedicated resources.
Model compression techniques lead to poor performance in regression-based sentence-level quality estimation of machine translation.
A full model parameterization is required to achieve state-of-the-art results in regression-based sentence-level quality estimation of machine translation.
The level of expressiveness of a model in a continuous range is unnecessary for the downstream applications of sentence-level quality estimation of machine translation.
Reframing sentence-level quality estimation of machine translation as a classification problem and using classification metrics better reflects actual performance in real-world applications.
Optimizing an Information Bottleneck bound manages the trade-off between rationale conciseness and task accuracy.
An Information Bottleneck approach jointly learns a rationale explainer that predicts sparse binary masks without explicit supervision and an end-task predictor that uses only residual sentences.
An Information Bottleneck-based learning objective allows direct control of rationale mask sparsity levels through a tunable sparse prior.
An Information Bottleneck approach improves task performance and agreement with human rationales on the ERASER benchmark.
A modest amount of gold rationales closes the performance gap with full-input models in semi-supervised rationale extraction.
Jump Selection Strategy improves efficiency in probability calibration by selecting the most informative instances and filtering out less significant ones.
Multi-Binning Scaling models different confidence levels separately to increase capacity and flexibility in probability calibration.
Wasserstein distance-based calibration loss improves calibration performance.
Existing calibration methods are ill-suited for knowledge graph embedding models and degrade ranking performance.
KGE Calibrator outperforms existing calibration methods in both effectiveness and efficiency for knowledge graph embedding models.
Certain test instances for compositional generalization are consistently difficult across different sequence-to-sequence models.
In sequence-to-sequence models, a test instance for compositional generalization is hard if it contains a local structure not observed during training.
A decision rule based on unobserved local structures predicts instance-level generalization outcomes for compositional tasks in sequence-to-sequence models.

The decision rule based on unobserved local structures predicts instance-level generalization outcomes for compositional tasks in sequence-to-sequence models more accurately than alternative decision rules.
Local structures enable the creation of difficult adversarial compositional splits for sequence-to-sequence models.
Strategic selection of training examples based on local structures improves compositional generalization for sequence-to-sequence models when training data is limited.
GRADE uses coarse-grained utterance-level contextualized representations and fine-grained topic-level graph representations to evaluate dialogue coherence.

GRADE obtains graph representations by reasoning over topic-level dialogue graphs enhanced with evidence from a commonsense graph, including k-hop neighboring representations and hop-attention weights.
GRADE achieves higher Pearson and Spearman correlations with human judgments than state-of-the-art metrics for diverse dialogue models.
A new large-scale human evaluation benchmark for dialogue systems provides human judgments for evaluating automatic dialogue evaluation metrics.
Uni-Parser is a unified semantic parser for question answering on both knowledge bases and databases.
Uni-Parser uses primitives (relations and entities in knowledge bases, and table names, column names, and cell values in databases) as essential elements.
Uni-Parser ensures the number of primitives grows linearly with the number of retrieved relations in knowledge bases and databases, avoiding exponential growth of logical form candidates.
Uni-Parser uses a generator to predict final logical forms by altering and composing top-ranked primitives with operations such as select, where, and count.
Uni-Parser uses a contrastive primitive ranker to prune the search space, enabling the generator to capture the composition of primitives and enhance generalization.
Uni-Parser achieves competitive performance on multiple knowledge base and database question answering benchmarks with improved efficiency in compositional and zero-shot settings.
PORTIA splits answers into segments based on length and semantics and merges them into a single prompt for LLM evaluation.
PORTIA enhances the consistency rates of LLM-based evaluators for pairwise comparisons.
PORTIA-enhanced GPT-3.5 achieves human agreement rates comparable to GPT-4.
PORTIA-enhanced GPT-3.5 surpasses standalone GPT-4 in alignment with human evaluators.
Logically dependent multi-task learning follows a mediation assumption in causal inference.
Label transfer utilizes the labels of lower-level tasks for each task in multi-task learning.
Gumbel sampling deals with cascading errors in multi-task learning.
Gumbel sampling estimates the causal effect between tasks via counterfactual reasoning.
Label transfer and Gumbel sampling improve predictions' consistency in logically dependent multi-task learning.
Cross-register projection automatically annotates news headlines with POS tags by projecting predicted tags from corresponding news body sentences.
Joint training of a multi-domain POS tagger on long-form text and headline text improves performance compared to training on a single register or naïvely concatenating training sets.
Better headline POS tags improve the performance of a syntax-based open information extraction system.
POSH is a newly-annotated POS-tagged corpus of English news headlines from the Google sentence compression corpus.
RuCCoD is a benchmark dataset for ICD coding in Russian that includes diagnosis fields from electronic health records annotated with over 10,000 entities and more than 1,500 unique ICD codes.
For ICD coding in Russian, training with automated predicted codes improves accuracy compared to training with manually annotated data from physicians.
The shortcut exploited by backdoor attacks in prompt-based learning arises from the contrast between the trigger and the poisoned data.
Conventional negative data augmentation methods face challenges in balancing effectiveness and stealthiness for clean-label backdoor attacks in prompt-based learning.

Contrastive Shortcut Injection leverages activation values to integrate trigger design and data selection for crafting stronger shortcut features in clean-label backdoor attacks for prompt-based learning.
Contrastive Shortcut Injection achieves high effectiveness and high stealthiness at low poisoning rates for clean-label backdoor attacks in prompt-based text classification tasks.
R-TeaFor utilizes the pairwise relationship between original training data and modified training data for better regularization in abstractive summarization.
R-TeaFor surpasses previous state-of-the-art models in abstractive summarization.
R-TeaFor's improved performance in abstractive summarization generalizes to different pre-trained models.
Rejection learning rejects potentially noisy tokens in abstractive summarization.
Regularized decoding for abstractive summarization penalizes non-factual candidate summaries during inference using rejection probability.
Rejection learning improves the factuality of generated summaries.
Rejection learning increases the abstractiveness of generated summaries.
A multi-task data augmentation approach generates new sentence pairs by transforming target sentences (e.g., reversing target sentence order) to produce unfluent target sentences.

A multi-task data augmentation approach achieves consistent improvements over baseline and over data augmentation methods that extend the support of the empirical data distribution on low-resource translation tasks.
Systems trained with multi-task data augmentation rely more on source tokens.
Systems trained with multi-task data augmentation are more robust against domain shift.
Systems trained with multi-task data augmentation experience fewer hallucinations.
Spurious images that are highly relevant but inconsistent with answers cause multi-modal large language models to suffer from visual illusion.
CorrelationQA assesses the visual illusion level in multi-modal large language models given spurious images.
Multi-modal large language models universally suffer from instinctive bias to varying degrees when exposed to spurious images.
The sense hierarchy is used to select negative examples for contrastive learning in implicit discourse relation recognition.
Ensemble adversarial training reduces the hypothesis-only bias in Natural Language Inference sentence representations.
Ensemble adversarial training achieves better generalization to other Natural Language Inference datasets than previous de-biasing methods.
Higher-dimensional sentence representations in Natural Language Inference require more adversarial classifiers for de-biasing and are harder to de-bias.
Flipped-VQA predicts the answer from video-question pairs, the question from video-answer pairs, and the video from question-answer pairs.
Flipped-VQA consistently improves the performance of multiple large language models on Video Question Answering.
Flipped-VQA enhances the exploitation of linguistic shortcuts in Video Question Answering.
Flipped-VQA mitigates linguistic bias that causes incorrect answers by over-reliance on the question in Video Question Answering.
The Unseen Emotion Recognition in Conversation (UERC) task is for recognizing unseen emotions in conversations.
ProEmoTrans recognizes unseen emotions in conversations by transferring emotion knowledge from seen emotions.
An LLM-enhanced description approach handles implicit expressions in emotion definition.
A parameter-free mechanism efficiently encodes utterances in long conversations while preventing overfitting.
An improved Attention Viterbi Decoding method transfers emotion transitions from seen to unseen emotions.
Most documents in open-world multi-label text classification under extremely weak supervision have a dominant class covering the majority of content.
Long-tail labels in open-world multi-label text classification under extremely weak supervision appear in some documents as a dominant class.

X-MLClass constructs an initial label space for open-world multi-label text classification under extremely weak supervision by clustering dominant keyphrases obtained from large language models prompted with user descriptions, and iteratively refines the label space using a zero-shot multi-label classifier to discover long-tail labels.
X-MLClass increases ground-truth label space coverage compared to topic modeling and keyword extraction methods.
X-MLClass achieves the best end-to-end multi-label classification accuracy.
ATHENA mimics human thought expansion mechanisms for solving math word problems using neural network propagation.
Thought expansion recurrently generates candidates for math expressions from the previous step and selects valid pathways to the goal.
ATHENA achieves state-of-the-art performance on variant math word problems under restricted training informativeness.
Children's texts contain more and more typical commonsense assertions.
childBERT uses task-unspecific fine-tuning on small amounts of children's texts for commonsense knowledge extraction.
childBERT improves language-model-based commonsense knowledge extraction.
Correlational Copying Network (CoCoNet) enhances the copying mechanism by keeping track of the copying history.
Correlational Copying Network (CoCoNet) uses prior copying distributions to encourage copying of input words relevant to the previously copied word at each time step.
Correlational Copying Network (CoCoNet) uses pre-training with corpora that simulate copying behaviors.
Correlational Copying Network (CoCoNet) copies more accurately.
Correlational Copying Network (CoCoNet) achieves state-of-the-art performance on news and dialogue summarization benchmarks.
BeSimulator is a modular LLM-powered framework for behavior simulation in text-based environments.
Chain of Behavior Simulation analyzes action feasibility and state transitions for robot behavior simulation.
Code-driven reasoning enables arithmetic operations in behavior simulation.
BTSIMBENCH is a behavior-tree-based benchmark for behavior simulation.
BeSimulator outperforms baseline methods in behavior simulation.
INSPIRED is a dataset of 1,001 human-human dialogs for movie recommendation that includes measures for successful recommendations.
An annotation scheme for recommendation strategies based on social science theories is designed and applied to human-human movie recommendation dialogs.
Sociable recommendation strategies, such as sharing personal opinions or communicating with encouragement, more frequently lead to successful recommendations.
Recommendation dialog systems trained with strategy labels outperform systems trained without strategy labels in both automatic and human evaluation.
Label-enhanced hierarchical contextualized representation for sequential metaphor identification extracts information from both sentence-level and discourse-level contexts.
Label-enhanced contextualized representation leverages the metaphor information of non-target words in a sentence to strengthen reasoning for sequential metaphor identification.
Position-aware global memory network learns long-range dependencies among the same words within a discourse for sequential metaphor identification.

Label-enhanced hierarchical contextualized representation outperforms state-of-the-art methods without external knowledge beyond pre-trained language models on sequential metaphor identification tasks.
Robust Retrieval Augmented Generation extends dense passage retrieval with hard negatives for zero-shot slot filling.
Robust Retrieval Augmented Generation employs robust training procedures for zero-shot slot filling.
Robust Retrieval Augmented Generation improves passage retrieval for zero-shot slot filling.
Robust Retrieval Augmented Generation improves slot value generation for zero-shot slot filling.
Robust Retrieval Augmented Generation demonstrates domain adaptation capability for slot filling through zero/few-shot learning.
Entity typing models exhibit six types of spurious correlation biases: mention-context bias, lexical overlapping bias, named entity bias, pronoun bias, dependency bias, and overgeneralization bias.
Counterfactual data augmentation for entity typing uses debiased counterparts of training examples to mitigate spurious correlations.
Counterfactual data augmentation improves the generalization of entity typing models on both original and debiased test sets.
A fact-aware model assesses discrepancies between textual and factual entity graphs through graph comparison.
Hierarchical feature extraction with gating units enables adaptive fusion of multi-grained features from entity, sentence, and document levels.
On three public datasets, a fact-aware model for machine-generated text detection outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
A fact-aware model for machine-generated text detection captures differences in entity graphs between machine-generated and human-written texts.
PropBank-style semantic role labeling is reduced to syntactic dependency parsing.
Three syntactic patterns account for the vast majority of PropBank-style semantic role labeling annotations in both English and Chinese data.
A conversion scheme packs PropBank-style semantic role labeling annotations into dependency tree representations using joint labels and allows accurate recovery to the original format.
Syntactic dependency trees can encode semantic role relations within their syntactic domain of locality.
Self-adjusting arithmetic coding based on a neural language model encodes secret messages in linguistic steganography.
Neural linguistic steganography via self-adjusting arithmetic coding achieves higher bits per word and lower KL divergence than previous state-of-the-art methods.
Human evaluations demonstrate that generated cover texts can fool eavesdroppers.
Neural architectures outperform feature-engineering-based approaches for translationese classification.
BERT-based models achieve the best performance for translationese classification in both monolingual and multilingual settings.
Many hand-crafted translationese features correlate with neural model predictions for translationese classification.
The most important features for neural architectures and feature-engineering-based approaches differ in translationese classification.
Translationese universals exist across languages.
Major Entity Identification (MEI) assumes the target entities are specified in the input.
Major Entity Identification (MEI) limits the task to only frequent entities.
MEI models generalize well across domains on multiple datasets with supervised models and LLM-based few-shot prompting.
MEI fits the classification framework, enabling the use of robust and intuitive classification-based metrics.
MEI allows users to search for all mentions of a particular entity or a group of entities of interest.
Adaptive Ranking Transferability computes expected rank as transferability for text ranking model selection to reflect the model's ranking capability.

Adaptive Ranking Transferability adaptively scales isotropic sentence embeddings to mitigate anisotropy and incorporate training dynamics, yielding an accurate expected rank score for transferability estimation in text ranking.
Adaptive Ranking Transferability captures subtle differences between models for text ranking model selection.
Adaptive Ranking Transferability improves model selection for text ranking over classification-oriented transferability estimation methods, human intuition, and ChatGPT with low time consumption.
CI-ToD is a dataset for Consistency Identification in Task-oriented Dialogue.
CI-ToD provides a single label for contradiction and fine-grained labels for Dialogue History Inconsistency, User Query Inconsistency, and Knowledge Base Inconsistency.
