There is a large performance gap between state-of-the-art methods and human performance for consistency identification in task-oriented dialogue.
Model extraction from monolingual victim models is possible when the local copy is initialized from a pre-trained multilingual model.
The extracted model generalizes to multiple languages without being trained on well-formed inputs in those languages for the target task.
Adding a few real examples improves the extracted model's generalization to multiple languages.
A coarse-to-fine modeling framework for query focused multi-document summarization uses progressively more accurate modules to estimate text segment relevance, answer likelihood, and centrality.
Modules in a coarse-to-fine modeling framework for query focused multi-document summarization can be independently developed and leverage training data when available.

An instantiation of a coarse-to-fine modeling framework for query focused multi-document summarization uses a trained evidence estimator with distant supervision from question answering to identify text segments likely to answer the query for inclusion in the summary.
A coarse-to-fine modeling framework for query focused multi-document summarization is robust across domains and query types (long and short).
A coarse-to-fine modeling framework for query focused multi-document summarization outperforms strong comparison systems on benchmark datasets.
A data augmentation approach restricts the output of neural NLG systems to guarantee reliability.
Existing neural generation approaches tend to produce dull and repetitive text.
Neural NLG systems trained with a data augmentation approach that restricts output achieve semantic accuracy parity with template systems on the E2E NLG Challenge dataset.
Sentence Smith generates controllable text edits in English for evaluating text embeddings.
Sentence Smith induces hard negative text pairs in English that challenge text embedding models.
Sentence Smith enables fine-grained evaluation of text embedding models on English text by isolating different types of semantic shifts.
Sentence Smith is resource-efficient because it relies only on smaller neural networks.
Sentence Smith verifies the validity of text transformations in English via entailment check.
Universal Expression Tree (UET) represents equations of various math word problems uniformly.
SAU-Solver resolves multiple types of math word problems in a unified model.
SAU-Solver generates a universal expression tree by deciding symbols based on the semantic meanings of generated symbols.
SAU-Solver uses subtree-level semantically-aligned regularization to enforce semantic constraints and rationality of the generated expression tree by aligning with contextual information.
Hybrid Math Word Problems dataset (HMWP) consists of three types of math word problems.
DyLex uses word-agnostic tag embeddings for BERT-based sequence labeling to avoid re-training when updating the lexicon.
DyLex employs a supervised lexical knowledge denoising method for BERT-based sequence labeling to smooth matching noise.
DyLex uses a column-wise attention mechanism for fusing lexical knowledge in BERT-based sequence labeling to ensure pluggability.
DyLex achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple BERT-based sequence labeling tasks with large-scale dynamic lexicons.
MGToolBench is a training dataset containing statement and category-level instructions that reflect real-world scenarios.
ToolPlanner is a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that uses path planning and two feedback mechanisms to enhance task completion and instruction-following in tool-augmented LLMs.
ToolPlanner improves the Match Rate, Pass Rate, and Win Rate compared to the state-of-the-art model.
A genetic algorithm induces adversarial examples that fool almost all existing text classification models by finding an ensemble of models.
Adversarial examples that fool almost all existing text classification models reflect defects in the learning process and data bias in the training set.
Word replacement rules derived from adversarial examples that fool almost all existing text classification models are used for model diagnostics.
A new representation for CCG derivations decomposes CCG derivations into several independent pieces.
A new representation for CCG derivations prevents span-based parsing models from violating CCG rule schemata.
An off-the-shelf span-based parser using a new representation for CCG derivations achieves performance comparable to previous CCG parsers.
Learning a transformation through static anchors requires only another pre-trained model and no labeled data.
Learning a transformation through static anchors leads to consistent improvement on benchmark tasks for contextual variations of meaning.
Static embeddings provide greater gains than contextualized embeddings when used to improve contextual representations.
An objective counters bias using balanced training to achieve Equal Opportunity fairness.
A gated model incorporates protected attributes as input for bias mitigation.
A gated model reduces bias in predictions through demographic input perturbation.
Combining a gated model that incorporates protected attributes as input with balanced training outperforms other bias mitigation techniques.
Debiasing techniques do not incur performance disadvantages when applied to non-English languages.
SentenceDebias is the most effective debiasing technique for multilingual BERT in English, French, German, and Dutch.
Debiasing techniques with additional pretraining are more effective for cross-lingual debiasing, especially in lower-resource languages.
RAcQUEt evaluates referential ambiguity in image-based question answering.
RAcQUEt-BIAS evaluates whether unresolved referential ambiguity causes stereotypical, socially biased responses.
State-of-the-art large multimodal language models exhibit significant limitations and overconfidence when addressing referential ambiguity in image-based question answering.
TopWORDS-Poetry simultaneously segments text and discovers words in classical Chinese poetry using Bayesian inference.
TopWORDS-Poetry requires no pre-given vocabulary or training corpus for processing classical Chinese poetry.
TopWORDS-Poetry recognizes named entities and literary allusions in classical Chinese poetry.
TopWORDS-Poetry segments classical Chinese poetry lines into sequences of meaningful words.
Contextualized pointwise mutual information (CPMI) uses large pretrained language models to estimate statistical dependence between words.
CPMI dependencies achieve an unlabelled undirected attachment score that is above a non-contextualized PMI baseline but generally comparable to a simple adjacent-word baseline.
Certain types of linguistic dependencies are better captured by CPMI dependencies than others.
Different training schemes of large pretrained language models affect the types of dependencies they capture.
A dataset of real-world consumer product websites supports research on conversational search ranking manipulation.
Different LLMs vary significantly in prioritizing product name, document content, and context position for conversational search rankings without adversarial injections.
A tree-of-attacks-based jailbreaking technique reliably promotes low-ranked products in conversational search engines.
The tree-of-attacks-based jailbreaking technique transfers effectively to state-of-the-art conversational search engines.
Prefix injection in Standardized Arabic fails to provoke Large Language Models into generating unsafe content on the AdvBench benchmark.
Arabic transliteration and chatspeak (arabizi) provoke Large Language Models such as OpenAI GPT-4 and Anthropic Claude 3 Sonnet into generating unsafe content.
Importance sampling with n-gram features of multi-granular tokens balances sentence compression and representation capabilities.
Sampled data has high correlation with target downstream task performance and preserves effectiveness on other tasks.

Pretrained models trained on a small fraction of data achieve performance comparable to models trained on the full dataset and outperform models trained on randomly selected data across multiple benchmarks and model sizes.
Categorical Bias score quantifies ethnic bias in monolingual BERT.
A method using a multilingual model mitigates ethnic bias in monolingual BERT.
A method using contextual word alignment of two monolingual models mitigates ethnic bias in monolingual BERT.
The relative effectiveness of ethnic bias mitigation using a multilingual model versus using contextual word alignment depends on the amount of NLP resources available for the language.
Two ethnic bias mitigation methods work for Arabic and Greek in monolingual BERT.
The grounded minimal editing framework minimally edits existing responses to be grounded on a given concept.
Grounded Minimal Editor (GME) edits responses by disentangling and recombining persona-related and persona-agnostic parts.
PersonaMi-nEdit is a dataset for evaluating persona-grounded minimal editing.
GME outperforms competitive baselines in persona-grounded minimal editing.
GME improves persona consistency in dialogue responses while preserving knowledge and empathy.
Controlled Text Reduction requires generating a coherent text from a source text with highlighted spans of targeted content such that the output includes all and only the target information.
Development and test datasets for Controlled Text Reduction are crowdsourced.
A silver training dataset for Controlled Text Reduction is automatically generated from summarization benchmarks using a pretrained summary-source alignment model.
Model-tuning Via Prompts (MVP) adapts to downstream tasks by appending a prompt template to the input and making prediction via text infilling/completion.
Model-tuning Via Prompts (MVP) achieves higher adversarial robustness than standard methods for adapting NLP models to downstream tasks.
Model-tuning Via Prompts (MVP) achieves higher adversarial robustness than adversarial training-based defenses.
Combining Model-tuning Via Prompts with adversarial training achieves higher adversarial robustness than Model-tuning Via Prompts alone while maintaining performance on unperturbed examples.

The vulnerability of the standard method of adapting NLP models by appending a multilayer perceptron and fine-tuning is caused by the misalignment between pre-training and fine-tuning tasks and the randomly initialized multilayer perceptron parameters.

Impliret is a benchmark for evaluating retrieval systems on document-side reasoning where relevance depends on implicitly stated facts involving temporal, arithmetic, and world knowledge relationships.
Sparse and dense retrievers exhibit poor performance on document-side reasoning for implicit fact retrieval.
GPT-o4-mini exhibits poor performance on document-side reasoning for implicit fact retrieval even when provided with a short context containing the relevant document.
KGML bridges the gap between meta-training and meta-testing tasks in low-resource text classification by leveraging external knowledge bases.
KGML introduces additional representation for each sentence learned from an extracted sentence-specific knowledge graph.
Set-of-Vision prompting (SoV) uses spatial information such as bounding boxes and facial landmarks to mark targets for zero-shot emotion recognition in Vision Large Language Models.
Set-of-Vision prompting (SoV) improves accuracy in face count and emotion categorization while preserving enriched image context.
QuRe provides crowd-sourced human annotations of generalized quantifiers in Wikipedia sentences with percentage-equipped predicates.
PRESQUE combines natural language inference and the Rational Speech Acts framework for quantifier comprehension.
PRESQUE outperforms a literal listener baseline in predicting percentage scopes for quantifiers without additional training.
FL-GLM prevents embedding gradient attacks from the server by placing the input block and output block on the local client.
FL-GLM prevents reverse engineering attacks from peer-clients by employing key-encryption during client-server communication.
FL-GLM improves training efficiency by employing client-batching or server-hierarchical optimization methods that adapt to the server's computational capabilities.
FL-GLM achieves performance comparable to a centralized chatGLM model on natural language understanding and generation tasks.
Methods for automatic Matrix Language Identity determination in code-switched speech apply the Matrix Language Frame theory.
MLID predictors from audio show higher correlation with the textual principles of the Matrix Language Frame theory than acoustic language identity (LID) in all cases for code-switched speech.
MLID predictors from audio outperform acoustic language identity (LID) in Matrix Language Identity recognition tasks for code-switched speech.
Non-English languages (Mandarin and Spanish) are preferred over English as the Matrix Language in code-switched speech, which contrasts with language identification in monolingual utterances.
The modality gap is the pronounced performance difference between speech and text inputs in Large Speech Language Models after speech-text alignment training.
In Large Speech Language Models, speech and text representations in deeper layers become increasingly aligned in direction but diverge in magnitude.
Representation similarity in Large Speech Language Models is strongly correlated with the modality gap.
Large Speech Language Models exhibit a spontaneous token-level alignment pattern between text and speech representations.
The Alignment Path Score quantifies token-level alignment quality between speech and text representations in Large Speech Language Models.
The Alignment Path Score exhibits stronger correlation with the modality gap than representation similarity.
Angle projection and length normalization interventions can improve speech input correctness in Large Speech Language Models.
TempoSum evaluates the temporal generalization ability of abstractive summarization models using data from 2010 to 2022.
Parametric knowledge stored in summarization models significantly affects the faithfulness of generated summaries on future data.
Existing faithfulness enhancement methods do not reliably improve the faithfulness of summarization models on future data.
A geometric mapping in embedding space transforms linguistic properties in monolingual and cross-lingual settings without tuning the pre-trained sentence encoder or decoder.
TrojanStego embeds sensitive context information into natural-looking outputs via linguistic steganography without requiring explicit control over inference inputs.
A taxonomy outlines risk factors for compromised LLMs.
An encoding scheme based on vocabulary partitioning embeds secrets into LLM outputs via fine-tuning.
Compromised LLMs transmit 32-bit secrets reliably on unseen prompts.
Compromised LLMs maintain high utility and coherence while evading human detection.
A three-stage pipeline for knowledge graph reasoning uses large language models without fine-tuning and includes knowledge alignment, knowledge graph reasoning, and entity reranking.
Knowledge alignment uses three strategies to associate unconnected nodes with semantic relations for aligning large language model knowledge to the knowledge graph schema.
Entity reranking improves recall of correct answers by reranking top-scored entities using large language models.
A three-stage pipeline for knowledge graph reasoning using large language models without fine-tuning improves knowledge graph reasoning in incomplete and general situations.
PsFuture is a zero-shot adaptive read/write policy for Simultaneous Machine Translation that enables the translation model to independently determine read/write actions without additional training.
Prefix-to-Full (P2F) is a training strategy that adjusts offline translation models for Simultaneous Machine Translation applications by exploiting bidirectional attention mechanisms.
PsFuture achieves performance comparable to strong baselines in Simultaneous Machine Translation.
Prefix-to-Full (P2F) enhances Simultaneous Machine Translation performance by achieving a favorable trade-off between translation quality and latency.
UnifiedSKG unifies 21 structured knowledge grounding tasks into a text-to-text format.
T5 with necessary simple modifications achieves state-of-the-art performance on almost all of the 21 structured knowledge grounding tasks.
Multi-task prefix-tuning improves performance on most structured knowledge grounding tasks.
T0, GPT-3, and Codex struggle in zero-shot and few-shot learning for structured knowledge grounding.
UnifiedSKG is easily extensible to additional structured knowledge grounding tasks.
GreedyCAS segments scientific abstracts in an unsupervised manner by placing two segmentation boundaries through greedy optimization of the Normalized Mutual Information score between segments.
GreedyCAS achieves the best performance on non-structured abstracts for abstract segmentation.
GreedyCAS outperforms baseline methods on structured abstracts for abstract segmentation.
Normalized Mutual Information strongly correlates with evaluation metrics for abstract segmentation.
Meta Fine-Tuning (MFT) is a meta-learning procedure that enables neural language models to solve similar NLP tasks across domains.
Meta Fine-Tuning (MFT) learns from typical instances of various domains to acquire highly transferable knowledge.
Meta Fine-Tuning (MFT) uses novel domain corruption loss functions to encourage language models to encode domain-invariant representations.
PermuteFormer is a Performer-based model with relative position encoding that scales linearly for long sequences.
PermuteFormer applies position-dependent transformation on queries and keys to encode positional information into the attention module.
The position-dependent transformation in PermuteFormer ensures that the self-attention output is invariant to absolute token positions.
PermuteFormer has negligible computational overhead compared to Performer.
PermuteFormer uniformly improves the performance of Performer with negligible computational overhead.
PermuteFormer outperforms vanilla Transformer on most tasks.
COST-EFF integrates static model compression and dynamic inference acceleration for pre-trained language models.
Slenderizing pre-trained language models in width while maintaining depth and using layer-wise early exiting speeds up inference dynamically.
Joint training calibrates model slenderization and preserves contributive structures for each exit in multi-exit language models.
COST-EFF achieves Pareto optimality for spatial and temporal efficiency at high compression and acceleration rates.
Summarization models are often fine-tuned on datasets that contain factually inconsistent summaries.
A fact-aware filtering mechanism improves the factuality of summarization models.
A corrector module improves the factual consistency of generated summaries.
A re-ranking technique samples summary instances from a summarization model's output distribution and re-ranks them based on factuality.
Optimizing a summarization model for one factuality metric affects its factuality as measured by other factuality metrics.
Critique-to-preference transformation converts user critiques into positive preferences for conversational recommendation systems.
Critique-to-preference transformation uses a large language model in a few-shot setting.
A restaurant critique dataset supports evaluation of conversational recommendation systems.
Critique-to-preference transformation improves conversational recommendations.
Three general cases explain the improved conversational recommendation performance from critique-to-preference transformation.
Social Genome is the first benchmark for fine-grained, grounded social reasoning abilities of multimodal models.
Social Genome contains videos of interactions and human-annotated reasoning traces for inferences about the interactions.
The reasoning traces in Social Genome contain reasoning steps that reference evidence from visual cues, verbal cues, vocal cues, and external knowledge.
Social Genome is the first benchmark to study external knowledge in social reasoning.
Social Genome computes metrics for holistic evaluation of semantic and structural qualities of model-generated social reasoning traces.
Models trained on counterfactually augmented data for social computing constructs have lower in-domain performance but better out-of-domain generalization.
Counterfactually augmented data reduce model reliance on spurious features for social computing constructs.
A novel typology categorizes counterfactually augmented data for social computing constructs.
Counterfactually augmented data that act on the social computing construct directly or are diverse improve model performance for social computing constructs.
CAT-Gen generates adversarial texts using controllable attributes invariant to task labels.
CAT-Gen generates more diverse and fluent adversarial texts.
CAT-Gen generates adversarial texts that are more robust against model re-training and different model architectures.

Unsupervised multilingual embeddings can be learned by decoupling unsupervised word alignment between language pairs and mapping monolingual embeddings to a shared multilingual space, and solving these sub-problems separately using existing techniques.

When applied to distant languages, decoupling unsupervised word alignment between language pairs and mapping monolingual embeddings to a shared multilingual space shows robust behavior and outperforms existing unsupervised multilingual word embedding approaches.
Counterfactual text for classifiers is generated by optimizing in the latent space and using a language model to generate candidate modifications from optimized latent representations.
Shapley values estimate the combinatoric effect of multiple changes in counterfactual text generation.
Shapley value estimates guide a beam search for generating counterfactual text.
Latent optimization improves the success rate and quality of generated counterfactual text.
The use of Shapley values improves the success rate and quality of generated counterfactual text.
A dynamic memory stores user-supplied corrections to erroneous model beliefs identified during interaction.
Retrievals from a dynamic memory of user feedback provide additional context for question-answering to avoid previous mistakes in similar new situations.
A question-answering system with dynamic memory of user feedback continually improves over time without model retraining.
A question-answering system with dynamic memory of user feedback achieves performance near that of full user feedback with limited user feedback.
A question-answering system with dynamic memory of user feedback continually improves over time with real user feedback.
Prolog-MATH is a curated corpus that supports mathematical reasoning in large language models through logic programming.
A two-stage automated pipeline predicts relevant mathematical predicates for verbal math problems.
A two-stage automated pipeline uses predicted mathematical predicates and the expected answer type to generate complete Prolog programs for verbal math problems.
A predicate-aware reward function evaluates how well generated solutions incorporate suggested mathematical predicates.
GRPO training with the predicate-aware reward function enables base models to solve problems that Deepseek-V3 fails to solve.
A large-scale dataset of expressions of distress and corresponding offerings of condolence enables examination of the dynamics of condolence online.
Widespread disparity exists across types of distress in receiving supportive condolence versus non-supportive engagement.
A dataset quantifies empathy in condolence using appraisal theory.
The features of condolence that people find most helpful online differ substantially from those in interpersonal settings.
M3 employs a multi-view fusion and multi-decoding mechanism for multi-document reading comprehension.
M3 uses a question-centered fusion mechanism and cross-attention mechanism for fine-grained fusion of evidence clues from different documents in the encoder and decoder concurrently.
M3 simultaneously employs extractive decoding and generative decoding to guide the training process.
M3 can perform extractive decoding and generative decoding independently and optionally.
M3 achieves consistent improvements over previous state-of-the-art methods on the Natural Questions and TriviaQA datasets.
Targeted persuasion score (TPS) quantifies the persuasiveness of a context to a language model based on the context's ability to alter the model's answer to a question.
Targeted persuasion score (TPS) provides a fine-grained view of language model behavior by moving beyond the model's most likely answer.
Targeted persuasion score (TPS) measures the shift in a language model's answer distribution toward a target distribution using the Wasserstein distance.
Targeted persuasion score (TPS) captures a more nuanced notion of persuasiveness than previously proposed metrics.
A counterfactual-based analysis framework explores the causal effects of factual knowledge injection on language model performance in the pretrain-finetune paradigm.
The correctness of injected factual knowledge has only a very limited effect on language models' downstream performance.
ETC scales attention to longer inputs using a global-local attention mechanism between global tokens and regular input tokens.
ETC encodes structured inputs by combining global-local attention with relative position encodings and a Contrastive Predictive Coding pre-training objective.
ETC achieves state-of-the-art results on natural language datasets requiring long and/or structured inputs.
Low-resource automatic program repair generates program fixes for bugs with limited training samples.
Meta-APR is a meta-learning framework integrated with code pretrained language models for generating program fixes for low-resource bugs.
Meta-APR learns error-specific knowledge from high-resource bugs via first-order meta-learning optimization to enable faster adaptation to low-resource bugs.
Meta-APR is a model-agnostic framework that can be integrated with any neural models.
ARES is a semi-supervised approach that produces sense embeddings for lexical meanings in a lexical knowledge base in a space comparable to contextualized word vectors.

ARES embeddings, trained only on English sense-annotated data, enable a 1 Nearest-Neighbour algorithm to outperform state-of-the-art models in English and multilingual Word Sense Disambiguation tasks.
ARES embeddings, when used as an external source of knowledge, improve the performance of a neural model in the Word-in-Context task, enabling it to compete with more complex architectures.
A heuristic method for single-root dependency parsing finds the optimal dependency tree.
Reweighting the inference graph input to a dependency parser identifies the highest-scoring parse tree for single-root dependency parsing.
Reweighting the inference graph input to a dependency parser achieves optimal running time for single-root dependency parsing.

Simulated Multiple Reference Training (SMRT) approximates the full space of possible translations for machine translation by sampling paraphrases of reference sentences and training machine translation models to predict the paraphraser’s token distribution.
Simulated Multiple Reference Training (SMRT) improves machine translation performance in low-resource settings for translation to English.
Simulated Multiple Reference Training (SMRT) is complementary to back-translation in machine translation.
The Sentence-Incremental Neural Coreference Resolution system builds coreference clusters incrementally using a shift-reduce method after marking mention boundaries.
A simulation method constrains non-incremental coreference systems to form partial coreference chains before observing new sentences for incremental evaluation.
The Sentence-Incremental Neural Coreference Resolution system achieves higher performance than state-of-the-art methods in an incremental evaluation setting.
The Sentence-Incremental Neural Coreference Resolution system achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art baselines in a conventional coreference setup.
The degree of incrementality in the encoder has a large effect on coreference resolution performance.
dd-utt extends a span-based entity coreference model with task-specific characteristics for end-to-end discourse deixis resolution in dialogue.
dd-utt achieves state-of-the-art results on the CODI-CRAC 2021 shared task for discourse deixis resolution.
Masking correct tokens allows error correction models to correct original error tokens and predict masked tokens using context.
Masking correct tokens alleviates trivial copy in error correction training.
Masking correct tokens leverages effective training signals from correct tokens for error correction.
Masking correct tokens is a plug-and-play module applicable to different error correction models and tasks.
Masking correct tokens improves error correction accuracy on spelling, speech recognition, and grammar error correction tasks for both autoregressive and non-autoregressive models.
SODA is the first publicly available million-scale social dialogue dataset.
Social commonsense knowledge contextualization from a knowledge graph enables distillation of a broad spectrum of social interactions from a large language model.
Conversations in SODA are more consistent, specific, and natural than those in prior human-authored datasets.
COSMO is a generalizable conversation model that is more natural and consistent on unseen datasets than best-performing conversation models.
COSMO is sometimes preferred to human-written gold responses.
Knowledge-enriched conversations are distinct from natural social chitchats.

A sequential cross-document coreference resolution model for both entity and event coreference incrementally composes mentions into cluster representations and predicts links between a mention and already constructed clusters to approximate a higher-order model.
Various inputs and representation types are important in coreference resolution.
Mixture of Attention Heads combines multi-head attention with the Mixture-of-Experts mechanism.
Mixture of Attention Heads uses a router to dynamically select a subset of attention heads for each token.
Mixture of Attention Heads scales the number of attention heads and model parameters while preserving computational efficiency.
Mixture of Attention Heads automatically differentiates the utilities of attention heads, offering a new perspective on model interpretability.
A dataset of scientific findings annotated for sentence-level and aspect-level certainty.
Hedges alone provide only a partial explanation of certainty in scientific findings.
Pre-trained language models can predict overall certainty and individual aspects of certainty in scientific findings.
Modeling sentence-level and aspect-level certainty is meaningful for science communication.
TeleMelody generates melodies from lyrics using a two-stage process with a music template (tonality, chord progression, rhythm pattern, and cadence).
TeleMelody's template-to-melody module is trained in a self-supervised way without requiring lyric-melody paired data.
TeleMelody's lyric-to-template module uses rules and a lyric-to-rhythm model trained on lyric-rhythm data that is easier to obtain than lyric-melody data.
TeleMelody allows control of generated melodies by adjusting musical elements in the template.
TeleMelody generates melodies of higher quality than previous lyric-to-melody generation systems.
TeleMelody provides better controllability of generated melodies than previous lyric-to-melody generation systems.
A black-box jailbreak framework automates red teaming of large language models.
Malicious content concealing conceals malicious content in prompts to jailbreak large language models.
Memory reframing reframes the memory context to facilitate jailbreaking large language models.
An iterative optimization algorithm optimizes jailbreak attacks against large language models.
A black-box jailbreak framework is effective, scalable, and transferable for jailbreaking large language models.
Language models benefit from summarized versions of complicated programming questions.
Superfluous information in problem descriptions such as human characters, background stories, and names does not help language models in understanding programming tasks.
The meta-dataset provides human and synthesized summaries of long programming questions for program synthesis from the APPS and CodeContests datasets.
Summaries improve program synthesis performance on the APPS and CodeContests datasets.
Summaries improve program synthesis performance for introductory and interview programming questions but show minimal improvement for competitive programming questions.
Searching for fact-checking articles that address the content of original tweets containing potential misinformation uses both text and images.

Searching for fact-checking articles that address the content of original tweets containing potential misinformation warns users about misinformation, discourages spreading of fake news, and scales up verified content on social media.
Domain-Lifelong Learning for Dialogue State Tracking (DLL-DST) continually trains dialogue state tracking models on new domains while avoiding catastrophic forgetting of old domains.

Knowledge Preservation Networks (KPN) uses multi-prototype enhanced retrospection and multi-strategy knowledge distillation to solve expression diversity and combinatorial explosion in domain-lifelong dialogue state tracking.
Knowledge Preservation Networks (KPN) alleviates catastrophic forgetting in domain-lifelong dialogue state tracking.
Knowledge Preservation Networks (KPN) outperforms state-of-the-art methods for domain-lifelong dialogue state tracking.
EntityQuestions is a set of simple, entity-rich questions based on Wikidata facts.
Dense retrievers under-perform sparse methods on EntityQuestions.
Dense retrievers can only generalize to common entities unless the question pattern is explicitly observed during training.
Data augmentation is unable to fix the generalization problem in dense retrievers.
A more robust passage encoder facilitates better question adaptation using specialized question encoders for dense retrievers.
TinySQL is a synthetic text-to-SQL dataset that progresses from basic to advanced SQL operations.
A testbed for mechanistic interpretability research is established using text-to-SQL models of varying sizes.
Minimal circuits and components supporting SQL generation are identified using Edge Attribution Patching and Sparse Autoencoders.
Circuits for different SQL subskills are evaluated for minimality, reliability, and identifiability.
Models compose SQL queries across layers in a progression from intent recognition to schema resolution to structured generation.

The effective convolutional attention network uses a deep convolution-based encoder with squeeze-and-excitation networks and residual networks to aggregate document information and learn document representations covering different text ranges.
The effective convolutional attention network uses multi-layer and sum-pooling attention to extract the most informative features from multi-scale document representations.
The effective convolutional attention network combines binary cross entropy loss and focal loss to improve performance for rare labels.
The effective convolutional attention network achieves state-of-the-art performance for medical code prediction on MIMIC-III.
The effective convolutional attention network demonstrates language independence by outperforming prior models on non-English datasets.
Empirical multimodally-additive function projection (EMAP) isolates whether cross-modal interactions improve performance for a given model on a given task.
Empirical multimodally-additive function projection (EMAP) eliminates cross-modal interactions in model predictions to isolate additive unimodal structure.
Removing cross-modal interactions in multimodal models often results in little to no performance degradation on multiple image+text classification tasks.
When expressive multimodal models outperform less expressive models, the performance improvements often cannot be attributed to cross-modal feature interactions.
CAT uses attribute classifiers to generate semantically meaningful contrastive explanations for natural language text.
CAT employs a minimal perturbation approach regularized by a BERT language model and attribute classifiers to generate contrastive text with minimal edits and high fluency.
CAT conveys more insight about model behavior than alternative methods due to attribute-based explanations.
CAT generates contrastive text with higher fluency and fewer edits than alternative methods.
Increasing the size of the training dataset for broad-coverage natural language understanding systems often decreases performance on a small set of new symbols.
Multiple mainstream models often exhibit decreased performance on new symbols as training datasets grow for intent recognition and semantic parsing tasks.

The performance decrease on new symbols with growing training datasets is associated with source signal dilution, which is the dilution of strong lexical cues for the new symbol as the training dataset grows.
Selectively dropping training examples to prevent source signal dilution often reverses the performance decrease on new symbols.
Mainstream neural natural language understanding models over-rely on simple lexical cues.

A multi-task teacher-student framework enables few-shot rationalization of pre-trained language models through self-training.

CrowS-Pairs measures social bias in language models against protected demographic groups in the United States.

A fully differentiable end-to-end paradigm generates fluent and clinically accurate medical reports from chest X-ray images and clinical history.

Existing knowledge distillation techniques are unsuitable for deep neural machine translation engines.

The degree of paraphrase cannot be well characterized by a single binary label.

QA-Align aligns question-answer propositions across texts to represent cross-text content overlap.

Aligning question-answer propositions across texts captures content overlap beyond lexical similarity.

Text-to-visualisation benchmark datasets differ substantially from real-world visualisation code in the distribution of chart types, attributes, and actions.
One text-to-visualisation benchmark dataset is representative of real-world visualisation usage but requires extensive modification to serve as a practical end-to-end benchmark.
Cross-lingual data contamination, injected by overfitting large language models on translated benchmark test sets, inflates large language models' performance while evading current detection methods.

Multilingual HolisticBias is a multilingual template-based taxonomy of textual people references built from combinations of demographic descriptors and patterns, with gendered language alternatives for ambiguous English references.
For English-to-foreign-language translation, translation quality is higher when evaluated with masculine references than with feminine references.

The shift between training and test distributions is equivalent to virtual parameter corruption or perturbation.
GA-SAM finds flat minima that generalize better.

Event Graph Schema connects two event types through multiple paths involving entities that fill important roles in a coherent story.

Efficient-FedRec decomposes the news recommendation model into a large news model maintained on the server and a light-weight user model shared between the server and clients.
Efficient-FedRec has clients request the user model and news representations from the server and send locally computed gradients for aggregation.
Efficient-FedRec has the server update the global user model with aggregated gradients and update the news model to infer updated news representations.
Efficient-FedRec aggregates gradients using a secure method to preserve privacy.
Efficient-FedRec reduces computation and communication costs on clients while maintaining model performance.
A large-scale multi-domain dataset is available for persona-based empathetic conversations.

MetaASSIST adaptively learns the weighting parameter for robust dialogue state tracking.

A structured attention model aggregates multiple linear-chain CRFs to extract aspect-specific opinion spans for aspect sentiment classification.

A knowledge distillation framework for dialogue chain-of-thought reasoning leverages large language models as unreliable teachers and selectively distills consistent and helpful rationales using alignment filters.

Personalised Distillation has the student model attempt a task before receiving adaptive refinement from the teacher model.

Partial Label Model (PLM) enables taxonomy expansion in Named Entity Recognition using partially annotated datasets.

Adding Universal Dependency relations and Universal Part-of-Speech tags as model-agnostic features improves multiple semantic parsers for zero-shot cross-lingual semantic parsing.

DILBERT uses categorical information from source and target domains to create domain and category invariant representations for unsupervised domain adaptation in aspect extraction.

A BERT language model for Ancient Greek discovers previously unknown statistical properties relevant to literary, philosophical, and historical problems.

MovieChats is a large-scale high-quality corpus for movie-domain chat with fine-grained annotations.

Linear complexity language models exhibit scaling capabilities similar to conventional transformer-based models.

CTCC encodes contextual correlations, such as counterfactual correlations, across multiple dialogue turns for large language model fingerprinting.

Distress Identification and Cause Extraction (DICE) is a problem for multimodal online posts.

A multilingual generative language model trained on a diverse multilingual corpus achieves state-of-the-art few-shot learning performance in more than 20 languages.

Defend-And-Summarize (DAS) filters attack responses in social media rumor detection.

Ensemble distillation produces well-calibrated structured prediction models without requiring an ensemble at inference time.

mQG generates multiple questions from storybook contexts.
mQG generates diverse questions from storybook contexts.

SMART generates mnemonics for students based on feedback from real students learning new terms.
