Code-Switching Pre-training randomly replaces words in the source sentence with their translation words in the target language.
Code-Switching Pre-training induces translation lexicons using unsupervised word embedding mapping between source and target languages.
Code-Switching Pre-training uses an encoder-decoder framework that processes a code-mixed sentence and predicts replaced fragments.
Code-Switching Pre-training relieves the pretrain-finetune discrepancy by avoiding artificial symbols such as [mask].
A dataset of 6,492 resumes for Clinical Research Coordinator positions with four experience levels is manually annotated by experts with high inter-annotator agreement.
A transformer-based model classifies resumes to Clinical Research Coordinator experience levels using section encoding and multi-head attention decoding.
A transformer-based model predicts the suitability of a resume for a job description using section encoding and multi-head attention decoding.
Prediction errors in Clinical Research Coordinator experience level classification occur mostly among adjacent experience levels that are hard for experts to distinguish.
COGS is a semantic parsing dataset based on a fragment of English.

The evaluation portion of COGS contains systematic gaps that require compositional generalization, including new combinations of familiar syntactic structures and new combinations of familiar words with familiar structures.
Transformers and LSTMs achieve high in-distribution accuracy but low generalization accuracy on the COGS test set, with high sensitivity to random seed.
PUBHEALTH is a dataset of public health claims with journalist-crafted gold standard explanations justifying veracity judgments.
Three coherence properties of explanation quality are defined and evaluated with human and computational methods.
Training on in-domain data improves explainable automated fact-checking for public health claims.
URL signals enable the identification of cross-lingual document pairs at scale.
CCAligned is a web dataset of document pairs that are translations of each other, created from Common Crawl and covering a wide variety of language pairs including English.
Baseline methods for identifying aligned documents use cross-lingual representations and textual content.
The Hierarchical Variational Auto-Encoder model learns better distribution representation for public sentiment drift detection.
A new drift measure directly evaluates distribution changes between historical data and new data for public sentiment drift detection.
The Hierarchical Variational Auto-Encoder model outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in public sentiment drift detection.
PALM uses a joint autoencoding and autoregressive pre-training scheme on a large unlabeled corpus for context-conditioned text generation.
PALM alleviates the mismatch between pre-training and fine-tuning for language generation tasks that require producing new text beyond reconstructing original text.
PALM achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple language generation benchmarks.
Adversarial self-Supervised Data-Free Distillation (AS-DFD) compresses large transformer-based language models for text classification without requiring the original training dataset.
Plug & Play Embedding Guessing crafts pseudo embeddings from the teacher model's hidden knowledge to avoid text generation in discrete space.
A self-supervised module quantifies the student model's ability and adapts the difficulty of pseudo embeddings in an adversarial training manner.
BERT encodes semantic role and proto-role information with linguistically meaningful differences depending on the linguistic formalism.
Layer probing detects subtle differences between implementations of the same linguistic formalism.
Linguistic formalism is an important dimension in probing studies, alongside cross-task and cross-lingual experimental settings.
Structure-Aware Transformer injects table structural information into the mask of the self-attention layer.
A method combines symbolic and linguistic reasoning for table fact verification.
Explicit object region visual features yield higher translation quality than global visual features in simultaneous machine translation under low latency conditions.
Multimodal simultaneous machine translation systems correctly translate from English into gender-marked languages in cases where unimodal systems fail.
Multimodal simultaneous machine translation systems handle adjective-noun placement differences between English and French in cases where unimodal systems fail.
An automated framework extracts a first-pass grammatical specification of morphological agreement from raw text in a concise, human- and machine-readable format.

Using cross-lingual transfer without target-language expert annotations, an automated framework extracts a grammatical specification of morphological agreement that is nearly equivalent to specifications created from gold-standard annotated data.
A BERT-based approach for radiology report labeling exploits the scale of rule-based systems and the quality of expert annotations.
A biomedically pretrained BERT model is first trained on rule-based labeler annotations and then finetuned on a small set of expert annotations augmented with automated backtranslation.
CheXbert outperforms the previous best rule-based labeler for radiology report labeling with statistical significance.
Structure Aware Negative Sampling (SANS) utilizes knowledge graph structure by selecting negative samples from a node’s k-hop neighborhood.
Structure Aware Negative Sampling (SANS) finds semantically meaningful negative samples in knowledge graph embedding.
Structure Aware Negative Sampling (SANS) is competitive with state-of-the-art knowledge graph embedding approaches without requiring additional parameters or difficult adversarial optimization.
Captioning with A Purpose (CapWAP) uses question-answer pairs for training and post-inference evaluation.
Reinforcement learning optimizes caption generation for specific information needs by rewarding outputs that allow a question answering model to provide correct answers to user questions.
Several visual question answering datasets are converted into Captioning with A Purpose (CapWAP) datasets.
Purposeful captioning systems better anticipate and fulfill specific information needs than generic captioning systems.

ENT-DESC is a large-scale dataset for entity description generation that retrieves abundant knowledge of various entity types from a knowledge graph and requires selecting the most significant information.
A multi-graph structure comprehensively represents knowledge graph information for entity description generation from abundant knowledge.
Aggregation methods extract significant graph information for entity description generation from abundant knowledge.
MLSUM is the first large-scale multilingual summarization dataset.
MLSUM contains article/summary pairs in French, German, Spanish, Russian, and Turkish.
State-of-the-art summarization systems exhibit biases in cross-lingual evaluation.
A method quantifies semantic shift between word classes using contextualized word embeddings.
Word class flexibility is quantified in 37 languages.
Contextualized embeddings capture human judgment of class variation within words in English.
Contextualized embeddings uncover shared tendencies in class flexibility across languages.
Greater semantic variation occurs when flexible lemmas are used in their dominant word class.
Video-and-Language Event Prediction (VLEP) is a dataset of future event prediction examples with rationales from diverse TV Show and YouTube Lifestyle Vlog video clips.
An adversarial human-and-model-in-the-loop data collection procedure creates non-trivial challenging examples for video-and-language future event prediction.
A baseline model for video-and-language future event prediction incorporates video, dialogue, and commonsense knowledge.
Video, dialogue, and commonsense knowledge are each useful for video-and-language future event prediction.
A heuristic-based program that exploits spurious patterns in the ShARC dataset achieves performance comparable to neural models on the ShARC task.
The ShARC corpus contains four types of spurious patterns that neural models exploit.
A modified ShARC dataset with fewer spurious patterns is introduced.
Transfer learning techniques produce automatic AMR annotations across languages.
XL-AMR is a cross-lingual AMR parser that does not rely on AMR aligners or source-copy mechanisms.
XL-AMR outperforms previous methods for cross-lingual AMR parsing in Chinese, German, Italian, and Spanish.
A BERT-based model classifies the affective polarity of event phrases.
A BERT-based model for affective event classification outperforms a large affective event knowledge base.

Discourse-enhanced self-training iteratively labels event phrases for affective event classification by combining predictions from an affective event classifier and polarities of coreferent sentiment expressions.
Discourse-enhanced self-training improves both recall and precision for affective event classification.
Unsupervised grammar induction techniques are appropriate for analyzing emergent languages.
A certain message length and vocabulary size are required for syntactic structure to emerge in emergent languages.
More sophisticated game scenarios are required for emergent languages to obtain syntactic properties similar to those of human language.
A comprehensive library facilitates the analysis of emergent languages.
CoDIR distills knowledge from a teacher model's intermediate layers to a student model using a contrastive objective that distinguishes a positive sample from negative samples.
CoDIR compresses large-scale language models during both pre-training and fine-tuning stages.
A novel unifying theoretically-grounded framework characterizes the communication of empathy in text-based conversations.
A corpus of 10,000 (post, response) pairs is annotated for empathy with rationales.
A multi-task RoBERTa-based bi-encoder model identifies empathy in conversations and extracts rationales.
Users do not self-learn empathy over time in mental health interactions.
Randomization tests erasing name regularity, mention coverage, or context diversity from standard NER benchmarks explore the impact of these factors on model generalization.
A new open NER dataset focuses on entity types with weaker name regularity and lower mention coverage.
Name regularity is critical for models to generalize to unseen mentions.
High mention coverage undermines model generalization ability.
Context patterns do not require enormous data to capture when using pretrained encoders.
A new dataset contains Chinese-English bilingual text with code-switched sentences and their translations into Chinese.
Word surprisal is a significant predictor of code-switching beyond other known predictors, while word entropy is not.
Sentence length is a significant predictor of code-switching.
High cognitive effort causes code-switching by reducing resources for inhibiting the alternative language.
A filter-and-refine solution based on stacked-ensemble learning enables domain adaptation of Thai word segmentation models in black-box settings.
A filter-and-refine solution based on stacked-ensemble learning achieves performance comparable to transfer learning for domain adaptation of Thai word segmentation in black-box settings.
Self-contained summary highlights are understandable on their own to avoid confusion.
Combining determinantal point processes and deep contextualized representations identifies an optimal set of sub-sentence segments that are both important and non-redundant for summary highlights.
Representing discourse relations as question-and-answer pairs enables crowdsourcing of wide-coverage discourse relation annotations.
The QADiscourse dataset provides wide-coverage discourse relation annotations.
Baseline algorithms predict discourse relations in the QADiscourse dataset.

A zero-shot modeling framework for crosslingual sentence simplification transfers simplification knowledge from English to languages without parallel simplification corpora and generalizes across languages and tasks.
A shared transformer encoder constructs language-agnostic representations and task-specific encoder layers handle multiple tasks including sentence simplification and translation.
A zero-shot modeling framework for crosslingual sentence simplification produces better simplifications than unsupervised and pivot-based methods.
Parallel Interactive Networks model in-turn dependencies and cross-turn dependencies between system and user utterances for multidomain dialogue state tracking.
An interactive encoder jointly models in-turn dependencies and cross-turn dependencies for multidomain dialogue state tracking.
Slot-level context extracts more expressive features for different slots in multidomain dialogue state tracking.
A distributed copy mechanism selectively copies words from historical system utterances or historical user utterances in multidomain dialogue state tracking.

Transfer learning or distant supervision enables multilingual transformer models to achieve comparable performance to baselines with substantially more labeled data using only a small number of labeled sentences for African languages in named entity recognition and topic classification.

Transfer learning or distant supervision does not always enable multilingual transformer models to achieve comparable performance to baselines with substantially more labeled data using only a small number of labeled sentences for African languages in named entity recognition and topic classification.
The LAtent BElief State (LABES) model represents belief states as discrete latent variables and jointly models them with system responses given user inputs.
LABES enables semi-supervised learning under the variational learning framework.
LABES-S2S is a copy-augmented Seq2Seq model instantiation of LABES.
Semi-supervised LABES-S2S outperforms supervised-only and semi-supervised baselines when utilizing unlabeled dialog data.
LABES-S2S reduces belief state annotation demands without performance loss on MultiWOZ.

An extension of the HotFlip attack algorithm for universal trigger generation in fact checking systems jointly minimizes the target class loss of a fact checking model and the entailment class loss of an auxiliary natural language inference model.
A conditional language model generates semantically valid adversarial claims that include universal triggers for fact checking systems.
Adversarial claims for fact checking systems that preserve ground truth meaning and are semantically valid maintain directionality and semantic validity better than previous work.
Interactive Fiction game playing is a Multi-Passage Reading Comprehension task.
Context-query attention mechanisms and structured prediction generate and evaluate action outputs in Interactive Fiction game playing.
An object-centric historical observation retrieval strategy mitigates the partial observability of textual observations in Interactive Fiction game playing.
Multi-Passage Reading Comprehension for Interactive Fiction game playing achieves higher winning rates and lower data requirements than previous techniques.
TernaryBERT ternarizes the weights of fine-tuned BERT models.
TernaryBERT uses approximation-based and loss-aware ternarization methods.
TernaryBERT leverages knowledge distillation in the training process to reduce accuracy degradation from low-bit quantization.
TernaryBERT outperforms other BERT quantization methods on the GLUE benchmark and SQuAD.
TernaryBERT achieves performance comparable to full-precision BERT on the GLUE benchmark and SQuAD while being significantly smaller.
MEGA-DT is a large-scale discourse-annotated corpus generated using distant supervision from sentiment annotated datasets.
A scalable methodology generates discourse trees with structure and nuclearity for arbitrary-length documents using an efficient heuristic beam-search strategy extended with a stochastic component.
Discourse parsers trained on MEGA-DT achieve better inter-domain performance than those trained on human-annotated discourse corpora.
A knowledge-grounded generation model produces knowledge-enriched text.
A pre-training paradigm uses a massive knowledge-grounded text corpus crawled from the web.
A model trained with knowledge-grounded pre-training fine-tunes to various data-to-text generation tasks to produce task-specific text.
Knowledge-grounded pre-training enables zero-shot data-to-text generation on WebNLG where baselines fail.
Knowledge-grounded pre-training achieves comparable performance with substantially fewer labeled examples in few-shot data-to-text generation.
The widely used benchmark dataset for Split and Rephrase universally contains easily exploitable syntactic cues caused by its automatic generation process.
A simple rule-based model can perform on par with the state-of-the-art model on the widely used Split and Rephrase benchmark by exploiting syntactic cues.
Two crowdsourced benchmark datasets for Split and Rephrase contain more diverse syntax and are controlled for quality according to a well-defined set of criteria.
The two crowdsourced benchmark datasets for Split and Rephrase better represent the Split and Rephrase task and are more challenging for models.
A new natural language inference benchmark contains tasks where negation plays a critical role.
State-of-the-art transformers struggle with natural language inference tasks where negation plays a critical role.
HAHSum models different levels of information including words and sentences for extractive summarization.
HAHSum models redundancy dependencies between sentences.
HAHSum uses message passing to model label dependencies among sentences.
HAHSum outperforms previous extractive summarization methods.
A few-shot named entity recognition system combines nearest neighbor learning with structured inference.

A nearest neighbor classifier in the feature-space of a source-domain supervised named entity recognition model outperforms standard meta-learning approaches for few-shot named entity recognition across multiple test domains.
A method for capturing label dependencies between entity tags avoids conditional random field training.
Combining structured decoding with nearest neighbor learning achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard few-shot named entity recognition evaluation tasks.
MCTS-DDU performs decision-time planning for task-completion dialogue policy learning using dialogue state search trees built by Monte Carlo simulations.
MCTS-DDU is robust to simulation errors in task-completion dialogue policy learning.
An embedding model predicts the frequency and sentiment of legislator tweets.
Legislator attitudes towards a subject are represented as vector embeddings that interact with embeddings of the subject constructed from the subject's text using a neural network.
NwQM assesses Wikipedia article quality using article text, metadata, and images.
NwQM improves Wikipedia article quality assessment over state-of-the-art approaches.
Transformer rankers can be designed with separate modules for text representation and interaction.
Modular Transformer rankers achieve faster ranking by using offline pre-computed representations and light-weight online interactions.
Modular Transformer rankers are easier to interpret.
Modular Transformer rankers provide insight into the ranking process in Transformer rankers.
Soundex, MetaPhone, NYSIIS, and logogram are applied to Natural Language Processing for coding textual inputs.
Fixed-output-length coding with Huffman extension is developed for textual inputs in Natural Language Processing.
Integrating codewords with text provides more reliable inputs to neural network-based NLP systems through redundancy compared to text-alone inputs.
Integrating codewords with text achieves state-of-the-art performance on machine translation, language modeling, and part-of-speech tagging.
A multi-label dataset of movie synopses and a tagset for story attributes (including genre and event types) supports story characterization by theme and style.
A multi-view model encodes movie synopses and reviews using hierarchical attention for story characterization.
A multi-view approach to story characterization using both synopses and reviews outperforms single-view approaches that use only synopses.
Reviews provide a complementary set of story-attributes that are extractable without direct supervision.
CTC models latent alignments for non-autoregressive machine translation using dynamic programming.
Imputer models latent alignments for non-autoregressive machine translation using dynamic programming.
A simple CTC model achieves state-of-the-art performance for single-step non-autoregressive machine translation.
Imputer with 4 generation steps matches the performance of an autoregressive Transformer baseline for non-autoregressive machine translation.
CTC and Imputer for non-autoregressive machine translation do not require target length prediction or re-scoring with an autoregressive model.

Discriminative nearest neighbor classification with deep self-attention performs intent detection and out-of-scope intent detection by training a binary classifier with BERT-style pairwise encoding to estimate the best matched training example for a user input.
Natural language inference transfer boosts the discriminative ability of discriminative nearest neighbor classification for intent detection.

Discriminative nearest neighbor classification with natural language inference transfer achieves more stable and accurate in-domain and out-of-scope intent detection than RoBERTa-based classifiers and embedding-based nearest neighbor approaches.

Natural language inference transfer enables a discriminative nearest neighbor model trained with few examples to achieve competitive performance with models trained with more examples for intent detection while maintaining constant inference time.
Optimus is the first large-scale language Variational Autoencoder model for organizing sentences via pre-trained modeling of a universal latent space.
Optimus incorporates a universal latent embedding space for sentences pre-trained on a large text corpus and fine-tuned for language generation and understanding tasks.
Optimus enables guided language generation from an abstract level using latent vectors.
Optimus generalizes better on low-resource language understanding tasks due to its smooth latent space structure.
Optimus achieves state-of-the-art performance on VAE language modeling benchmarks.
State-of-the-art parsers fail to identify 'hers' and 'theirs' as pronouns but identify the masculine pronoun 'his'.
Recent language models like BERT exhibit bias against the pronouns 'hers' and 'theirs' relative to the masculine pronoun 'his'.
Language models amplify bias against the pronouns 'hers' and 'theirs' beyond the bias present in the training data.
Linguistic differences between English pronouns that are not inherently biased can become biases in some machine learning models.
Generating partially-synthetic data from a model using Bayesian approximations measures bias in the model.
A meta-learning approach for zero-shot cross-lingual transfer uses a model to select beneficial training instances for the source language model.
A meta-learning approach for zero-shot and few-shot cross-lingual transfer achieves state-of-the-art results for natural language inference and question answering.
The correlation of typological features between languages partly explains when parameter sharing in meta-learning is beneficial for cross-lingual transfer.
Nine human-human and multi-turn task-oriented dialogue datasets are unified for language modeling.
TOD-BERT incorporates user and system tokens into masked language modeling during pre-training.
TOD-BERT uses a contrastive objective function during pre-training to simulate the response selection task.
TOD-BERT outperforms strong baselines on intention recognition, dialogue state tracking, dialogue act prediction, and response selection.
TOD-BERT mitigates data scarcity in task-oriented dialogue through few-shot learning.

A large-scale human-annotated dataset for identifying consistency between dialogue responses and attribute profiles contains single-turn conversations, key-value attribute profiles, and manually labeled consistency relations.
A key-value structure information enriched BERT model identifies consistency between dialogue responses and attribute profiles.
Identifying consistency between dialogue responses and attribute profiles improves dialogue consistency in downstream tasks.
Contextual consistency of a quotation in online conversations is characterized by latent topics, interactions with dialogue history, and coherence to the query turn’s existing contents.
An encoder-decoder neural framework generates quotations that continue the conversation context.
An encoder-decoder neural framework for quotation generation outperforms state-of-the-art models on large-scale datasets in English and Chinese.
Topic consistency, interaction consistency, and query consistency are all helpful for quotation generation in online conversations.
BioMegatron is a larger biomedical domain language model trained on a larger domain corpus.
BioMegatron achieves state-of-the-art performance on biomedical question answering, named entity recognition, and relation extraction benchmarks.
Synthetic question-answer pairs alone achieve higher accuracy than the SQuAD1.1 training set on the SQuAD1.1 question answering task.
Synthetic question-answer pairs generated from a synthetic text corpus achieve high performance on the SQuAD1.1 dev set without real Wikipedia data.
Synthetic question-answer generation using large language models achieves higher Exact Match score on SQuAD2.0 than prior work using synthetic data.
Cross Segment Attention uses three transformer-based architectures for document and discourse segmentation.
Cross Segment Attention achieves state-of-the-art performance on three standard document and discourse segmentation datasets.
Cross Segment Attention models with reduced parameter counts maintain document and discourse segmentation performance.
SlotRefine is a non-autoregressive model for joint intent detection and slot filling.
A two-pass iteration mechanism handles the uncoordinated slots problem in non-autoregressive models for slot filling.
SlotRefine outperforms previous models in slot filling and speeds up decoding.
The two-pass iteration mechanism remedies the uncoordinated slots problem in non-autoregressive models for slot filling.
A bi-encoder independently embeds mention context and entity descriptions for dense retrieval in entity linking.
A cross-encoder concatenates mention and entity text for re-ranking candidates in entity linking.

A two-stage zero-shot linking algorithm achieves state-of-the-art results on zero-shot and non-zero-shot entity linking benchmarks without explicit entity embeddings or manually engineered mention tables.
Bi-encoder linking enables efficient large-scale entity linking with nearest neighbor search.
Knowledge distillation transfers cross-encoder accuracy gains to the bi-encoder in entity linking.
ConjNLI is a stress-test for natural language inference over conjunctive sentences where the premise and hypothesis differ by conjuncts being removed, added, or replaced.
Large-scale pre-trained language models do not understand conjunctive semantics well and resort to shallow heuristics for inferences over conjunctive sentences.
Iterative adversarial fine-tuning uses synthetically created training data based on boolean and non-boolean heuristics.
ConjNLI remains challenging for current natural language inference methods.
PAIR generates long text with controlled content using planning and iterative refinement.
Content plans are constructed by adapting BERT to assign keyphrases to sentence-level positions.
A refinement algorithm gradually enhances generation quality in sequence-to-sequence text generation.
Planning improves generation quality across multiple domains.
Text generated with planning is rated as more relevant and coherent by human judges.
GLUCOSE captures implicit commonsense causal knowledge through causal mini-theories grounded in narrative contexts, each pairing a story-specific causal statement with a generalized inference rule.
A crowdsourcing platform uses semi-structured templates to elicit story-based causal explanations at scale.
Existing knowledge resources and pretrained language models do not include or readily predict the rich inferential content of GLUCOSE.
Training state-of-the-art neural models on GLUCOSE enables them to make commonsense inferences on unseen stories that match humans’ mental models.
ToMCAT uses a generator network to interpret topics and an encoder network to infer document topics.
ToMCAT uses adversarial training and cycle-consistent constraints to ensure the generator and encoder produce coordinated realistic samples.
sToMCAT incorporates document labels to discover more coherent topics.
ToMCAT and sToMCAT produce coherent and informative topics.
ToMCAT and sToMCAT outperform competitive baselines in unsupervised and supervised topic modeling and text classification.
BERT-Attack generates adversarial samples using BERT.
BERT-Attack misleads fine-tuned BERT models and other deep neural models in downstream tasks.
BERT-Attack outperforms state-of-the-art attack strategies in success rate and perturbation percentage.
BERT-Attack generates fluent adversarial samples that preserve semantic consistency.
BERT-Attack has low computational cost enabling large-scale generation of adversarial samples.
The Pareto hypervolume reflects the trade-off between probe complexity and performance for probing contextual word representations.
Parametric and non-parametric metrics measure probe complexity.
Probe performance curves often fail to align with widely accepted rankings between language representations, with non-contextual representations outperforming contextual ones.
Common simplistic probe tasks such as part-of-speech labeling and dependency arc labeling are inadequate for evaluating the properties encoded in contextual word representations.
Full dependency parsing as a probe task with the Pareto hypervolume produces results that align with accepted rankings among contextual word representations.
Automatic key point extraction enables fully automatic analysis of argument collections.
Automatic key point extraction achieves performance comparable to a human expert.
Key point analysis applies to municipal surveys and user reviews.
Argument-to-key point matching models substantially outperform previous approaches.
A reference-free summary evaluation method assesses summary quality using unsupervised contrastive learning.
A summary evaluation metric covers both linguistic qualities and semantic informativeness using BERT.
A reference-free summary evaluation method constructs different types of negative samples for each summary to represent different aspects of summary quality and trains with a ranking loss.
A reference-free summary evaluation method outperforms existing evaluation metrics without requiring reference summaries.
A reference-free summary evaluation method is general and transferable across different datasets.
The corpus focuses on sentence-level hyperbole detection.
Risk behavior content ratings are jointly estimated from movie script language.
Movie representations for risk behavior rating estimation are learned from semantic and sentiment analysis of character language.
Risk behavior co-occurrence is leveraged via a multi-task approach for content rating estimation.
The joint portrayal of risk behaviors reveals insights about filmmaking subtleties not typically noticed by filmmakers.

For AMR-to-text generation, Lightweight Dynamic Graph Convolutional Networks use a dynamic fusion mechanism to capture richer non-local interactions by synthesizing higher order information from input graphs.
For AMR-to-text generation, group graph convolutions and weight tied convolutions reduce memory usage and model complexity in graph convolutional networks.
For AMR-to-text generation, Lightweight Dynamic Graph Convolutional Networks outperform state-of-the-art models while using fewer parameters.
Domain adversarial training affects the learned representations of large pretrained transformer models without improving their performance in unsupervised multi-source domain adaptation.
Large pretrained transformer models are relatively robust across domains in unsupervised multi-source domain adaptation.
Mixture of experts improves performance in unsupervised multi-source domain adaptation for large pretrained transformer models.
An attention-based metric mixes domain expert predictions in unsupervised multi-source domain adaptation for large pretrained transformer models.
Large pretrained transformer based domain experts produce highly homogenous predictions in unsupervised multi-source domain adaptation, making it challenging to learn effective metrics for mixing.
Training with Wikipedia anchors for entity span identification warms up pre-trained models for named entity recognition.
Gazetteer-based distant supervision extracts coarse-grained typed entities for named entity recognition pre-training.
A self-supervised auxiliary task mines fine-grained named entity knowledge via clustering for named entity recognition pre-training.
A coarse-to-fine pre-training framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on three named entity recognition benchmarks.
A coarse-to-fine pre-training framework enables named entity recognition without human-labeled training data in label-few and low-resource scenarios.
Token-level adaptive training for Neural Machine Translation assigns larger weights to low-frequency target tokens during training.
Token-level adaptive training improves Neural Machine Translation quality on sentences containing more low-frequency tokens.
Token-level adaptive training improves lexical diversity in Neural Machine Translation.
Test suite accuracy approximates semantic accuracy for Text-to-SQL models.
Distilled test suites achieve high code coverage for the gold query in Text-to-SQL evaluation.
Distilled test suites enable efficient calculation of a tight upper-bound for semantic accuracy in Text-to-SQL evaluation.
Test suite accuracy matches manual verification results in Text-to-SQL evaluation.
The Spider metric produces false negatives in Text-to-SQL evaluation.
Data Maps characterizes and diagnoses datasets using training dynamics.
Data Maps computes the model's confidence in the true class and the variability of this confidence across epochs for each example.
In data maps, regions ambiguous to the model contribute the most to out-of-distribution generalization.
In data maps, the most populous regions are easy to learn for the model and play an important role in model optimization.
In data maps, regions hard to learn for the model often correspond to labeling errors.
Best-performing persona-based dialogue agents are insensitive to contradictory words.
Dialogue agents employ public self-consciousness on the fly through an imaginary listener.
Dialogue agents with public self-consciousness refrain from uttering contradictions.
Distractor selection in the Rational Speech Acts framework is learned.
Public self-consciousness reduces contradictions in dialogue models.
Public self-consciousness improves persona consistency in dialogue models.
Public self-consciousness improves context-consistency beyond persona in dialogues.
Explainability techniques are evaluated with a comprehensive list of diagnostic properties.
Explainability techniques are compared on text classification tasks and neural network architectures.
Gradient-based explanations perform best across text classification tasks and neural network architectures.
A phrase alignment method handles compositional and non-compositional global phrase alignments.

A phrase alignment method combines an unordered tree mapping algorithm and phrase representation modelling that explicitly embeds the similarity distribution in the sentences onto contextualized representations.
A phrase alignment method achieves performance competitive with experienced human annotators.
Gender bias in dialogue data is replicated and amplified in generative chit-chat dialogue models.
The LIGHT dialogue dataset is the most gender-biased among six existing dialogue datasets.
Counterfactual data augmentation, targeted data collection, and bias controlled training mitigate gender bias in dialogue generation by balancing the genderedness of generated dialogue utterances.
Combining counterfactual data augmentation, targeted data collection, and bias controlled training is particularly effective for mitigating gender bias in dialogue generation.
Counterfactual data augmentation, targeted data collection, and bias controlled training maintain engagement in chit-chat dialogue responses.
G2LC solves lexically-constrained generation as an unsupervised gradient-guided optimization problem.
G2LC uses a differentiable objective function and the gradient to determine which position in the sequence to change by deletion, insertion, or replacement.
G2LC uses gradient guidance for the word updating process of inserted or replaced words.
G2LC does not require parallel data training.
G2LC can be used in the inference stage of any pre-trained generation model.
G2LC achieves state-of-the-art performance on keyword-to-sentence generation and unsupervised paraphrase generation.
Question-Answering Briefs decompose claims into questions and provide answers for fact checking.
QABriefer generates questions for a claim, searches the web for evidence, and generates answers.
QABriefDataset trains the question generation, evidence search, and answer generation components for producing Question-Answering Briefs.
Question-Answering Briefs increase the accuracy and reduce the time of fact checking for crowdworkers.
Question-Answering Briefs increase the accuracy and reduce the time of fact checking for volunteer fact checkers.
Semantic Parser Localizer generates training data in the target language by augmenting machine-translated datasets with local entities scraped from public websites.
Semantic Parser Localizer trains the semantic parser using an XLMR-LSTM architecture and a few-shot boost of human-translated sentences.
Semantic Parser Localizer evaluates the model on natural utterances curated by human translators.
For a subset of languages, Semantic Parser Localizer outperforms the previous state-of-the-art methodology in the hotels and restaurants domains.
Semantic Parser Localizer enables adding a new language capability to a QA system for a new domain in less than a day using machine translation.
KA-S2T models entities in math word problems and their categories as an entity graph.
KA-S2T uses a graph attention network on the entity graph to capture knowledge-aware problem representations.
KA-S2T uses a tree-structured decoder with a state aggregation mechanism to capture long-distance dependency and global expression information in math word problem solving.
An adversarial regularizer brings the encoder representations of Automatic Speech Recognition and Neural Machine Translation tasks closer despite their different modalities.
Aligning the encoder representations of Automatic Speech Recognition and Neural Machine Translation tasks enables the use of a pretrained Neural Machine Translation decoder for speech translation.
Stepwise models perform stepwise summarization by injecting the previously generated summary into the structured transformer as an auxiliary sub-structure.
Stepwise models do not require task-specific redundancy-aware modeling for extractive summarization.
Stepwise models achieve state-of-the-art performance for extractive summarization on CNN/DailyMail without using redundancy-aware modeling or sentence filtering.
Stepwise models achieve state-of-the-art performance for content selection, planning, and ordering in table-to-text generation on Rotowire.
Stepwise Extended Transformers achieves the best performance for extractive summarization and table-to-text generation across CNN/DailyMail and Rotowire.
SRLGRN learns cross paragraph reasoning paths and jointly finds supporting facts and the answer for multi-hop question answering.

SRLGRN uses a heterogeneous document-level graph containing sentence nodes (question, title, and other sentences) and per-sentence semantic role labeling sub-graphs with arguments as nodes and predicates as edges.

Incorporating argument types, argument phrases, and edge semantics from semantic role labeling predicates aids in finding reasoning paths and improves their explainability for multi-hop question answering.
Global-to-Local Neural Networks use entity global representations to model the semantic information of all entities in the document.
Global-to-Local Neural Networks use entity local representations to aggregate the contextual information of multiple mentions of specific entities.
Global-to-Local Neural Networks use context relation representations to encode the topic information of other relations.
Global-to-Local Neural Networks are particularly effective in extracting relations between entities of long distance and having multiple mentions.
GAZP combines a forward semantic parser with a backward utterance generator to synthesize data in the new environment.
GAZP selects cycle-consistent examples for parser adaptation.
GAZP improves logical form accuracy and execution accuracy on the Spider, Sparc, and CoSQL zero-shot semantic parsing tasks.
GAZP outperforms data-augmentation in the training environment.
Semantic parsing performance increases with the amount of GAZP-synthesized data.
Cycle-consistency is central to successful parser adaptation.
Phrase representation in state-of-the-art pre-trained transformers relies heavily on word content and shows little evidence of nuanced composition.
Phrase representation quality in state-of-the-art pre-trained transformers varies across models, layers, and representation types.
Behavioral hypotheses are induced for query generation.
A generic encoder-decoder Transformer framework aggregates arbitrary hypotheses for query generation.
Query generation with behavioral hypotheses improves performance compared to a BART model.
Disconnected reasoning is model behavior that produces correct answers by exploiting a subset of supporting facts without connecting information across multiple facts.
A model-agnostic probe measures the extent to which models cheat via disconnected reasoning.
An automatic transformation reduces disconnected reasoning in multi-hop question-answering datasets using contrastive support sufficiency.
Multi-hop question-answering in the reading comprehension setting has seen little progress.
Large-scale models achieve multi-fact reasoning performance in multi-hop question-answering that is similar to simpler baselines.
The automatic transformation is complementary to adversarial approaches, and their combination further reduces disconnected reasoning.
Bilingual lexicon induction for low-frequency words exhibits a diminishing margin between similarities in the low-frequency regime.
Bilingual lexicon induction for low-frequency words exhibits exacerbated hubness in the low-frequency regime.
A method addresses the diminishing margin between similarities in bilingual lexicon induction for low-frequency words.
A method addresses exacerbated hubness in bilingual lexicon induction for low-frequency words.
Hubness is the primary factor affecting bilingual lexicon induction for low-frequency words.
Addressing hubness improves bilingual lexicon induction accuracy for low-frequency words.
A language model with a fully compositional output embedding layer has a size independent of the training vocabulary.
A fully compositional output embedding layer for language models uses WordNet information including semantically related words and free-text definitions.

A language model with a fully compositional output embedding layer matches or outperforms state-of-the-art output embedding methods and adaptation approaches on language modeling and cross-domain open-vocabulary tasks.
Sample efficiency in language modeling results in greater accuracy for low-frequency words.
Span-Fact corrects factual inconsistencies in system-generated summaries by selecting spans using knowledge from question answering models.
Span-Fact uses single or multi-masking strategies to replace entities in system-generated summaries to ensure semantic consistency with the source text while retaining syntactic structure.
Span-Fact boosts the factual consistency of system-generated summaries without sacrificing summary quality.
Interpretable Multi-dataset Evaluation interprets model and dataset differences and interplay for named entity recognition.
Interpretable Multi-dataset Evaluation identifies strengths and weaknesses of named entity recognition models.
The post-editing corrector module identifies and corrects factual errors in generated summaries.

Artificial examples for pre-training the post-editing corrector module are created by heuristic transformations of reference summaries that are inspired by error analysis of state-of-the-art summarization model outputs.
The post-editing corrector module corrects factual errors in summaries generated by neural summarization models.
The post-editing corrector module achieves better factual consistency than previous models on the CNN/DailyMail dataset.
Transferring from artificial error correction to downstream settings is challenging.
AttnIO uses two directions of attention flows to utilize rich structural information in knowledge graphs for dialog-conditioned path traversal.
AttnIO explores a broad range of multi-hop knowledge paths.
AttnIO flexibly adjusts the range of nodes and edges to attend based on the dialogue context.
AttnIO outperforms all baseline models on the OpenDialKG dataset.
AttnIO generates adequate knowledge paths when trained with only destination nodes as labels.
PARADE is a benchmark dataset for paraphrase identification requiring computer science domain knowledge.
PARADE contains paraphrase pairs with low lexical and syntactic overlap that are semantically equivalent based on computer science domain knowledge.
PARADE contains non-paraphrase pairs with high lexical and syntactic overlap that are not semantically equivalent based on computer science domain knowledge.
Both state-of-the-art neural models and non-expert human annotators have poor performance on PARADE.
Zero-shot paraphrasing for machine translation evaluation uses a sequence-to-sequence paraphraser conditioned on a human reference to score output.
Zero-shot paraphrasing for machine translation evaluation trains a paraphraser as a multilingual NMT system for zero-shot same-language translation.

Training a paraphraser as a multilingual NMT system for zero-shot same-language translation centers the output mode around a copy of the machine translation output, representing the best case scenario for matching a human reference.
Zero-shot paraphrasing for machine translation evaluation does not require human judgements for training.
Zero-shot paraphrasing for machine translation evaluation matches or exceeds the performance of prior metrics on the WMT 2019 segment-level metrics task.

Adapting zero-shot paraphrasing for machine translation evaluation for quality estimation by conditioning on the source instead of the reference outperforms all prior submissions on the WMT 2019 quality estimation task.
A semi-supervised annotation scheme for question answering reduces human effort by having the QA system suggest candidate annotations and human annotators provide binary feedback.
Continuous learning from past question answering annotations improves future annotation performance and reduces overall annotation cost.
An annotation framework for question answering reduces annotation cost.
Dynamic anticipation utilizes latent prediction of embedding-based models to enable more potential path search in sparse knowledge graphs.
Dynamic completion dynamically adds edges during path search to alleviate the sparseness problem in knowledge graphs.
Dynamic anticipation and completion for multi-hop reasoning over sparse knowledge graphs outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
Multilingual AMR-to-text models generate text in twenty one languages.
Multilingual AMR-to-text models outperform monolingual baselines in the majority of languages.
Native speakers judge the text generated by multilingual AMR-to-text models to be fluent.
Aligner ensembling is a method for creating parallel corpora for low-resource machine translation.
Batch filtering is a method for creating parallel corpora for low-resource machine translation.
The high-quality Bengali-English parallel corpus contains a large number of new sentence pairs not available before.
The new Bengali-English test set has extensive quality control.
Using the high-quality Bengali-English parallel corpus improves Bengali-English machine translation performance over previous approaches.
BERT-EMD uses many-to-many layer mapping to allow each intermediate student layer to learn from any intermediate teacher layers.
BERT-EMD uses Earth Mover's Distance to compute the minimum cumulative cost for transforming knowledge from teacher network to student network.
BERT-EMD uses a cost attention mechanism to learn the layer weights for Earth Mover's Distance automatically.
BERT-EMD achieves competitive accuracy and model compression on the GLUE benchmark.
TeaForN addresses exposure bias in sequence generation models.
TeaForN addresses lack of differentiability across timesteps in sequence generation models.
TeaForN uses a stack of N decoders trained to decode along a secondary time axis to enable model-parameter updates based on N prediction steps.
TeaForN is compatible with a wide class of decoder architectures and requires minimal modifications to a standard teacher-forcing setup.
TeaForN improves generation quality on Machine Translation and News Summarization tasks.
Bayesian statistical model comparison for natural language processing estimates the likelihood that one model will outperform another or that two models will produce practically equivalent results.
Bayesian statistical model comparison for natural language processing uses k-fold cross-validation across multiple data sets.
SLM is a pre-training objective for learning a discourse language representation in a fully self-supervised manner.
SLM learns a contextualized sentence-level representation by shuffling the sequence of input sentences and training a hierarchical transformer model to reconstruct the original ordering.
SLM improves performance on GLUE, SQuAD, and DiscoEval compared to the original BERT.
The Adaptive Attentional Network learns adaptive entity and reference representations for few-shot knowledge graph completion.
The Adaptive Attentional Network uses an adaptive neighbor encoder to discern task-oriented roles of entities.
The Adaptive Attentional Network uses an adaptive query-aware aggregator to differentiate contributions of reference entity pairs.
The Adaptive Attentional Network captures fine-grained semantic meanings of entities and references via an attention mechanism.
The Adaptive Attentional Network achieves state-of-the-art performance in few-shot link prediction across different few-shot sizes.
The Multilingual Amazon Reviews Corpus (MARC) is a large-scale dataset for multilingual text classification containing reviews in English, Japanese, German, French, Spanish, and Chinese.
The Multilingual Amazon Reviews Corpus (MARC) is balanced across the five star ratings for each language.
Mean absolute error (MAE) is suitable for evaluating star rating classification because it accounts for the ordinal nature of the ratings.
Local Additivity based Data Augmentation (LADA) creates virtual samples for semi-supervised Named Entity Recognition by interpolating sequences close to each other.
Intra-LADA interpolates tokens within one sentence for semi-supervised Named Entity Recognition data augmentation.
Inter-LADA interpolates between different sentences for semi-supervised Named Entity Recognition data augmentation.
LADA incorporates a novel consistency loss for unlabeled data in semi-supervised Named Entity Recognition.
Factual structures of documents are a discriminative factor between machine-generated text and human-written text.
The factual structure of a document is represented as an entity graph.
Sentence representations are learned using a graph neural network on the entity graph.
Document representations are composed from sentence representations by sequentially modeling consistent relations between neighboring sentences.
A graph-based deepfake detection model distinguishes differences in factual structure between machine-generated text and human-written text.
SREF leverages sense relations to enhance sense embeddings.
SREF implements a try-again mechanism for Word Sense Disambiguation after obtaining basic sense embeddings from augmented WordNet glosses.
SREF achieves state-of-the-art results for Word Sense Disambiguation on all-words and lexical sample datasets among knowledge-based systems.
SREF outperforms previous supervised systems for Word Sense Disambiguation when trained with a small fraction of SemCor data.
CHARM uses keyword extraction and document retrieval to predict unseen personal attribute values from conversations.
CHARM is viable for inferring open-ended personal attributes such as professions and hobbies.
BERT layer roles in Reading Comprehension based Question Answering are defined using Integrated Gradients.
In Reading Comprehension based Question Answering, BERT initial layers focus on query-passage interaction and later layers focus on contextual understanding and answer prediction enhancement.
For quantifier questions in Reading Comprehension based Question Answering, BERT focuses on other numerical quantities in the passage in later layers while correctly predicting the answer.
State-of-the-art dependency parsers exhibit higher root constraint violation rates with smaller training datasets across multiple languages.
An adaptation of the Gabow and Tarjan algorithm enforces the root constraint in dependency parsing while preserving the original decoding runtime.
Uncertainty-aware semantic augmentation captures universal semantic information among multiple semantically-equivalent source sentences.
Uncertainty-aware semantic augmentation enhances hidden representations with universal semantic information.
Uncertainty-aware semantic augmentation outperforms strong baselines and existing methods on various translation tasks.
IIRC is a reading comprehension dataset where the context provides only partial information and the missing information is in linked documents.
IIRC questions have little lexical overlap with the answer contexts due to being written without access to linked documents.
IIRC includes unanswerable questions and questions requiring discrete reasoning.
Human performance on IIRC is substantially higher than baseline model performance.
POINTER generates text under hard lexical constraints by recursively inserting tokens between existing tokens in parallel.
POINTER creates a coarse-to-fine hierarchy that makes text generation intuitive and interpretable.
POINTER achieves logarithmic time complexity during inference via non-autoregressive decoding.
POINTER achieves state-of-the-art performance for constrained text generation tasks.
Infilling techniques predict missing steps in a narrative when generating textual descriptions from a sequence of images.
The visual procedure telling (ViPT) dataset contains 46,200 procedures and approximately 340,000 pairwise images and textual descriptions that are rich in contextual dependencies.
Infilling techniques produce more coherent textual descriptions in visual procedures.
Information-theoretic probing with minimum description length recasts probe training as teaching to effectively transmit the data.
The measure of interest in information-theoretic probing is the description length of labels given representations.

The description length in information-theoretic probing evaluates the amount of effort needed to achieve probe quality, which characterizes either the size of a probing model or the amount of data needed.
Variational coding and online coding are methods for estimating minimum description length in probing.
Variational coding and online coding for minimum description length probing are more informative and stable than standard probes.
Multi-view language representations fuse linguistic typology vectors and multilingual machine translation embeddings using singular vector canonical correlation analysis.
Multi-view language representations embed linguistic typology.
Multi-view language representations strengthen correlations with language relationships.
Multi-view language representations project and assess new languages without expensive retraining of massive multilingual models or ranking models.
ProtoQA is a question answering dataset for training and evaluating prototypical common-sense reasoning.
A generative evaluation task for prototypical common-sense questions requires models to generate a ranked list of answers to cover all prototypical answers for the question.
On the ProtoQA dataset, human performance exceeds model performance by a meaningful gap on all evaluation metrics.
T3 is a target-controllable adversarial attack framework applicable to a range of NLP tasks.
A tree-based autoencoder embeds discrete text data into a continuous representation space for optimizing adversarial perturbations.
A tree-based decoder regularizes the syntactic correctness of generated adversarial text and manipulates it at the sentence or word level.
T3 generated adversarial texts manipulate NLP models to output targeted incorrect answers without misleading humans.
T3 generated adversarial texts have high transferability enabling black-box attacks.
Monolingual adapters adapt multilingual models for zero-shot neural machine translation.
Monolingual adapters are more parameter-efficient than existing adapter layers and achieve as good or better performance.
Monolingual adapters are specific to one language and can be composed to generalize to unseen language pairs.
The auto-regressive property in the standard transformer for document-level neural machine translation provides consistency but causes error accumulation.
Long-Short Term Masking Transformer captures long-range dependence and reduces error propagation in document-level neural machine translation.
Long-Short Term Masking Transformer captures discourse phenomena in document-level neural machine translation.
Gradient-based approaches can generate semantic collisions.
State-of-the-art models for paraphrase identification, document retrieval, response suggestion, and extractive summarization are vulnerable to semantic collisions.
Semantic collisions can be generated to evade perplexity-based filtering.
Event extraction by answering almost natural questions extracts event arguments in an end-to-end manner.
Event extraction by answering almost natural questions extracts event arguments for roles not seen at training time in a zero-shot learning setting.
Specific sparsity cases in hypernymy detection, where x or y is not involved in any pattern, are non-negligible.
Distributional methods are ideal for compensating pattern-based methods in hypernymy detection for specific sparsity cases where x or y is not involved in any pattern.
A complementary framework enables seamless collaboration between pattern-based and distributional models in hypernymy detection for cases each model prefers.
An unsupervised mutual information probe evaluates the mutual dependence between a real clustering and a representation clustering.
SSMBA generates synthetic text training examples by moving randomly on a data manifold using a pair of corruption and reconstruction functions.
SSMBA leverages the manifold assumption to reconstruct corrupted text using masked language models.
SSMBA consistently outperforms existing data augmentation methods and baseline models on both in-domain and out-of-domain data.
DualTKB enables unsupervised text to path and path to text transfers in Commonsense Knowledge Bases.
A slight amount of supervision improves the quality of text to path and path to text transfers in Commonsense Knowledge Bases.
A novel metric measures Commonsense KB completion performance of generative models.
Negative interference in multilingual models affects low-resource languages.
Language-specific parameters exist in multilingual models.
Language-specific parameters in multilingual models are a potential cause of negative interference.
A meta-learning algorithm for multilingual models trains language-specific layers as meta-parameters to improve shared layers' generalization on all languages.
A meta-learning algorithm for multilingual models improves cross-lingual transferability and alleviates negative interference.
A multi-view sequence-to-sequence model extracts conversational structures of unstructured daily chats from different views to represent conversations.
A multi-view sequence-to-sequence model uses a multi-view decoder to incorporate different views to generate dialogue summaries.

TIMEPLEX embeds entities, relations, and time in a uniform, compatible space.

Existing temporal knowledge base completion models overestimate link prediction performance due to imperfect evaluation mechanisms.

Hierarchical Graph Network constructs a hierarchical graph with nodes at the granularity of questions, paragraphs, sentences, and entities for multi-hop question answering.
Hierarchical Graph Network initializes node representations with pre-trained contextual encoders.
Hierarchical Graph Network updates node representations through graph propagation.
Hierarchical Graph Network performs multi-hop reasoning by traversing graph edges for paragraph selection, supporting facts extraction, and answer prediction.

Hierarchical Graph Network supports different question answering sub-tasks simultaneously by weaving heterogeneous nodes into an integral unified graph with hierarchical differentiation of node granularity.
Interview is a large-scale media dialog dataset collected from news interview transcripts.
A dialog model leverages external knowledge and dialog acts via auxiliary losses to generate responses in interview-style conversations.
A dialog model leveraging external knowledge and dialog acts via auxiliary losses generates more specific and topical responses in interview-style conversations.

Overlap of dependency graph structures between training and test data explains more variation in cross-language dependency parsing performance than standard factors when controlling for treebank size.
Sequence-to-sequence VAEs memorize the first words and the sentence length, producing local features of limited usefulness.
Alternative architectures based on bag-of-words assumptions and language model pretraining learn latent variables that are more predictive of topic or sentiment.
Alternative architectures based on bag-of-words assumptions and language model pretraining decrease memorization of the first word and sentence length, yielding more diverse reconstructions.

The large divergence in magnitude and distributions between visually-grounded language datasets and pure-language corpora hinders the exploration of visually-supervised language models.

RL-MMR unifies advanced neural single-document summarization methods and statistical measures from classical multi-document summarization.

Domain Knowledge Empowered Structured Neural Net enhances a deep neural network with distributional constraints constructed from probabilistic domain knowledge for end-to-end event temporal relation extraction.
Domain Knowledge Empowered Structured Neural Net solves constrained inference for end-to-end event temporal relation extraction via Lagrangian Relaxation.

Domain Knowledge Empowered Structured Neural Net improves baseline neural network models for end-to-end event temporal relation extraction with strong statistical significance on datasets from news and clinical domains.
Gating mechanisms filter noisy information in graph convolution neural networks for event detection using trigger candidate information.

Graphs and models in event detection exhibit syntactic importance score consistency.
A scene graph for narrative passages is built using Atomic as external knowledge.
The Graph Dimensional-Iteration Network (GDIN) encodes scene graphs for narrative machine reading comprehension.
Winning subnetworks of fine-tuned BERT achieve performance comparable to the full model.
Subnetworks of the same size as winning subnetworks but sampled from the rest of fine-tuned BERT perform worse than winning subnetworks.
The worst possible subnetworks of fine-tuned BERT under structured pruning remain highly trainable.
Most pre-trained BERT weights are potentially useful.
Good subnetworks of fine-tuned BERT are unstable.

Supervised Selfplay suffers from late-stage training collapses.
Seeded Iterated Learning has higher negative likelihood when evaluated on human corpus.

KERMIT embeds symbolic syntactic parse trees into artificial neural networks.

A method for enriching ABSA test sets generates examples that disentangle confounding sentiments of non-target aspects from the target aspect's sentiment.

Properly aligning input sequences of meaning representation during training leads to highly controllable text generation from meaning representation in both training from scratch and fine-tuning settings.
Phrase-based data augmentation improves controllability on difficult, randomly generated utterance plans in meaning representation to text generation.
Sentence attackability is associated with the sentence's content, proposition types, and tone.
An external knowledge source provides useful information about sentence attackability.
Machine learning models can automatically detect attackable sentences in arguments.
MOCHA is a benchmark for training and evaluating generative reading comprehension metrics.
LERC is a learned evaluation metric for reading comprehension that mimics human judgement scores.
LERC correlates better with human judgements than baseline metrics on held-out annotations.
LERC achieves higher accuracy than baseline metrics on minimal pairs for robustness evaluation.
PyMT5 generates Python methods from natural language documentation strings (docstrings).
PyMT5 generates natural language documentation strings (docstrings) of any common style from Python methods.

A joint multiple criteria model integrates different Chinese word segmentation criteria by sharing all parameters.
A Chinese word segmentation model incorporates transfer learning to handle out-of-vocabulary words.

Language adaptation for universal dependency parsing uses contextual parameter generation and adapter modules.
Language adaptation for universal dependency parsing learns adapters via language embeddings and shares model parameters across languages.
Language adaptation for universal dependency parsing integrates linguistic typology features into the parsing network.

COMETA is a corpus for medical entity linking in social media, consisting of English biomedical entity mentions from Reddit expert-annotated with links to SNOMED CT, with scale, coverage, diversity, and quality.
No single entity linking technique is universally effective for medical entity linking in social media.
The best entity linking solution for medical entity linking in social media relies on combining different views of data.
A minimally supervised approach identifies fine-grained subframes in news media coverage of politically divisive topics.
Fine-grained subframes capture ideological differences in news media coverage of politically divisive topics.
Fine-grained subframes analyze political discourse in news media coverage of politically divisive topics.
SeqMix encourages compositional behavior in neural models for sequence-to-sequence problems.
SeqMix creates new synthetic examples by softly combining input and output sequences from the training set.
SwitchOut and word dropout approximate variants of a single objective.
SeqMix improves translation performance over strong Transformer baselines on multiple datasets.
SeqMix improves performance on tasks requiring strong compositional generalization.
A novel tagging scheme for emotion-cause pair extraction encodes the distance between linked components to simultaneously extract emotions and their causes.
A novel tagging scheme for emotion-cause pair extraction enables linear time complexity.
A novel tagging scheme for emotion-cause pair extraction outperforms the state-of-the-art method.
A routing method selects content under internal restrictions for recipe generation.
RGM generates appropriate recipes from given ingredients and user preferences.
RGM achieves state-of-the-art results for recipe generation.
SpanGCN uses graph convolutional networks over constituent trees for syntax-aware semantic role labeling.
SpanGCN represents nodes as syntactic constituents.
SpanGCN produces initial node representations by composing word representations of the first and last words in each constituent.
SpanGCN performs graph convolutions on the constituent tree to yield syntactically-informed constituent representations.
SpanGCN decomposes constituent representations back into word representations for the semantic role labeling classifier.

The local autoregressive translation (LAT) mechanism predicts a short sequence of tokens for each target decoding position in an autoregressive manner to capture local dependencies among target outputs in non-autoregressive translation.
An efficient merging algorithm aligns and merges output pieces into one final output sequence in non-autoregressive translation.
Compared to the conditional masked language model (CMLM), the local autoregressive translation (LAT) mechanism achieves comparable or better translation performance with fewer decoding iterations.
The local autoregressive translation (LAT) mechanism reduces repeated translations in non-autoregressive translation.
The local autoregressive translation (LAT) mechanism achieves better performance on longer sentences in non-autoregressive translation.
FEnmt is trained using mistranslated fragments from a subset of the training data.

TORQUE is an English reading comprehension dataset built from news snippets containing human-generated questions about temporal ordering.

On-manifold regularization generates pseudo on-manifold samples through interpolation within the data manifold to impose smoothness regularization and improve in-distribution calibration.
Off-manifold regularization encourages models to output uniform distributions for pseudo off-manifold samples to address over-confidence for out-of-distribution data.

Visually Grounded Compound PCFGs learns a constituency parser from unlabeled text and visual groundings.
Visually Grounded Compound PCFGs enables fully-differentiable end-to-end visually grounded learning.
Visually Grounded Compound PCFGs complements image-text alignment loss with a language modeling objective.
Visually Grounded Compound PCFGs achieves state-of-the-art results for constituency grammar induction on MSCOCO test captions.
Visually Grounded Compound PCFGs achieves higher recall on abstract constituent categories than the previous grounded model.
CWEB is a benchmark for grammatical error correction that consists of website text generated by English speakers of varying proficiency.
Low grammatical error density presents a challenge to state-of-the-art grammatical error correction systems.

Minimalist Transfer Learning (MinTL) enables plug-and-play use of pre-trained seq2seq models for joint learning of dialogue state tracking and dialogue response generation.
Levenshtein belief spans (Lev) enable efficient dialogue state tracking with minimal generation length.
Minimalist Transfer Learning (MinTL) achieves state-of-the-art results for end-to-end response generation.
Minimalist Transfer Learning (MinTL) is robust in low-resource settings and achieves competitive results with limited training data.
Levenshtein belief spans (Lev) improve inference efficiency for dialogue state tracking.
Human-driven natural perturbations of seed examples create new training instances and often change the gold label.

When perturbing an existing question with human-driven natural perturbations is sufficiently cheaper than creating a new question from scratch, training BOOLQ models with human-driven natural perturbations increases robustness and generalization while retaining performance on the original BOOLQ dataset.
Off-the-shelf response retrieval models and response generation models generate grayscale data for dialogue response selection without human effort.
Multi-level ranking objectives for training matching models in dialogue response selection capture more fine-grained context-response relevance differences.
Multi-level ranking objectives for training matching models in dialogue response selection reduce the train-test discrepancy in terms of distractor strength.
VCDM uses a continuous latent variable to model the relationship between a phrase in context and its definition.
VCDM uses variational inference for estimation.
VCDM uses contextualized word embeddings.
Cambridge is a dataset for definition modeling.
Robert is the first non-English dataset for definition modeling.

A robust approach for selecting training instances via cross-lingual annotation projection using unsupervised type and token constraints, word-alignment confidence, and density of projected part-of-speech tags.
A Bi-LSTM architecture for part-of-speech tagging that uses contextualized word embeddings, affix embeddings, and hierarchical Brown clusters.
An evaluation of cross-lingual part-of-speech tagging on 12 diverse languages covering multiple language families and morphological typologies.
A fully unsupervised cross-lingual transfer approach for part-of-speech tagging achieves significant accuracy improvements over previous work when using limited and out-of-domain parallel data.
Using multi-source information via cross-lingual annotation projection or output combination improves part-of-speech tagging accuracy for most target languages.
Clustering pre-trained word embeddings with document information for weighted clustering and reranking top words generates topics.
Combinations of word embeddings and clustering algorithms for topic generation are evaluated.

The best combination of word embeddings and clustering algorithms for topic generation matches classical topic models in performance while having lower runtime and computational complexity.
EALM uses an Editorial Agent to predict edit actions (delete, keep, replace) and a Language Model converter to deterministically generate summaries from action signals for unsupervised summarization.

EALM achieves competitive performance for unsupervised summarization without requiring any paired data, including a validation set.

Framing edit-based unsupervised summarization as a Q-learning task makes reinforcement learning techniques available.
Spot The Bot evaluates conversational dialogue systems by replacing human-bot conversations with bot-bot conversations.
Spot The Bot ranks chat bots by their ability to mimic human conversational behavior using human judgments on bot-bot conversations under the assumption of human participation.
Survival Analysis measures which chat bot upholds human-like conversational behavior the longest.
Survival Analysis correlates chat bot performance to characteristics such as fluency and sensibleness.
A method for scaling hidden Markov language models to massive state spaces maintains efficient exact inference, a compact parameterization, and effective regularization.
Hidden Markov language models scaled to massive state spaces are more accurate than previous hidden Markov language models and n-gram-based methods.
Hidden Markov language models scaled to massive state spaces make progress towards the performance of state-of-the-art neural network models.
A task determines whether an emotion-cause pair has a valid causal relationship in a specific context.
A dataset for conditional causal relationship determination contains manually annotated examples and negatively sampled examples from an existing benchmark dataset.
A prediction aggregation module fine-tunes prediction results based on the characteristics of input clauses with low computational overhead.
An end-to-end online framework for conversation disentanglement avoids time-consuming domain-specific feature engineering.
An utterance embedding method for conversation disentanglement uses timestamp, speaker, and message text.

A neural-based approach for named entity recognition on social media texts incorporates both local semantics from running text and augmented semantics.
An attentive semantic augmentation module encodes augmented semantic information obtained from a large-scale corpus.
A gate module aggregates augmented semantic information obtained from a large-scale corpus.

A neural-based named entity recognition approach for social media texts that incorporates both local and augmented semantics outperforms previous studies on three benchmark datasets from English and Chinese social media platforms.
Regularized Representation Alignment aligns word-level and sentence-level representations across languages without external resources.
Regularized Representation Alignment regularizes user utterance representations based on their corresponding labels.
Regularized Representation Alignment regularizes a latent variable model via adversarial training to disentangle latent variables.
Regularized Representation Alignment outperforms state-of-the-art methods in few-shot and zero-shot cross-lingual spoken language understanding.

Generative approaches for information retrieval are as effective as state-of-the-art semantic similarity-based discriminative models for the answer selection task.
Unlikelihood losses are effective for information retrieval.
Generation with Multi-Hop Reasoning Flow enables pre-trained language models to perform dynamic multi-hop reasoning on multi-relational paths from an external commonsense knowledge graph.
Generation with Multi-Hop Reasoning Flow outperforms existing baselines on text generation tasks requiring reasoning over commonsense knowledge.
Generation with Multi-Hop Reasoning Flow infers reasoning paths that provide rationale for the generated text.
SetConv extracts a single representative for each class in imbalanced classification problems.
An episodic training strategy extracts a single representative for each class in imbalanced classification problems.
SetConv is permutation-invariant with respect to the order of inputs.
SetConv outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple large-scale benchmark text datasets.
Initialising and freezing input embeddings with in-domain data provides a useful representation of rare words for English language models trained with limited computational resources.
Initialising and freezing input embeddings with in-domain data provides a useful representation of rare words across multiple domains for English language models.

Five intrinsic metrics evaluate the quality of summarization datasets.
