Incorporating sentence order information reduces error on the WMT16 document alignment shared task.

A bounded memory neural network for long document coreference resolution tracks only a small bounded number of entities at a time.
A bounded memory neural network for long document coreference resolution guarantees linear runtime in document length.
A bounded memory neural network for long document coreference resolution achieves competitive performance on OntoNotes and LitBank compared to models with high memory and computational requirements.
A bounded memory neural network for long document coreference resolution learns an efficient memory management strategy that outperforms a rule-based strategy.
A dataset of hourly time phrases is compiled from fictional books.
A time-of-day classification model assigns wall clock times to book lines without explicit time-descriptive phrases.
Dynamic programming of breakpoints partitions books into time-of-day segments.
The fraction of events taking place past 10 P.M. increases significantly after 1880, coincident with the advent of the electric light bulb and city lights.
Compositional demographic word embeddings are derived compositionally from full or partial user demographic information including gender, age, location, and religion.
Compositional demographic word embeddings outperform generic word embeddings on language modeling and word association tasks for English.
For summarization, content importance is defined from the perspective of information amount using information theory and pre-trained language models.
For summarization, content importance evaluation considers both semantics and context for each semantic unit.
For summarization, content importance modeling with pre-trained language models generalizes to different kinds of semantic units including n-grams and sentences.
For summarization, content importance modeling using information theory and pre-trained language models better models content importance than prior work.
Latent subclass learning induces a latent ontology of probe inputs without requiring fine-grained gold labels in an interpretable and quantifiable form.
ELMo encodes a notion of personhood.
Pretrained contextual encoders exhibit a preference for fine-grained semantic roles on core arguments.
Pretrained contextual encoders exhibit emergent structure that departs from existing annotations.
MAF leverages caption-image datasets for weak supervision in phrase grounding.
MAF models phrase-object relevance using fine-grained visual representations and visually-aware language representations.
MAF uses a contrastive objective to incorporate caption-image pair information for weakly-supervised phrase grounding.
MAF outperforms existing weakly-supervised methods for phrase grounding.
Visually-aware language representations improve unsupervised phrase grounding performance.
A method for targeted opinion word extraction uses syntax-based opinion possibility scores and syntactic connections between words.
A regularization technique for targeted opinion word extraction leverages representation distinctions between words.
Incorporating syntactic structures and a novel regularization technique achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmark datasets for targeted opinion word extraction.

Elastic weight consolidation mitigates catastrophic forgetting in staged multi-domain language model pre-training with minimal performance deterioration on generic tasks and competitive performance on bio-medical tasks.
Multi-XScience is a large-scale multi-document summarization dataset for writing the related-work section of a scientific paper using the paper's abstract and referenced articles.
Multi-XScience is well suited for abstractive models.
Multimodal Aligned Contrastive Decoupled learning (MACD) performs natural language inference without supervision from inference labels.
MACD uses contrastive learning to force a decoupled text encoder to represent visual information.
MACD embeds visual knowledge for plain text natural language inference.
Unsupervised MACD outperforms fully-supervised BiLSTM and BiLSTM+ELMO on STS-B.
Multimodal Routing dynamically adjusts weights between input modalities and output representations for each input sample.
Multimodal Routing identifies the relative importance of individual modalities and cross-modality factors.
Multimodal Routing enables interpretation of modality-prediction relationships at both global and local levels.
A challenge dataset for gender bias uses type B reflexivization to provide unambiguous test cases and spans four languages and four NLP tasks.
Language models exhibit gender bias across all language-task combinations in a challenge dataset spanning four languages and four NLP tasks and based on type B reflexivization.
Gender bias in language models correlates with national labor market statistics.
The inference-based learner-like agent mimics learner behavior to identify good learning materials by examining the agent's performance.
The inference-based learner-like agent uses entailment modeling to infer answers from learning materials.
The inference-based learner-like agent achieves the best performance on fill-in-the-blank and good example sentence selection tasks.
The inference-based learner-like agent identifies example sentences that help college ESL students learn more easily and efficiently.
Room-Across-Room (RxR) is a multilingual Vision-and-Language Navigation dataset supporting English, Hindi, and Telugu.
Room-Across-Room (RxR) contains more paths and instructions than other Vision-and-Language Navigation datasets.
Room-Across-Room (RxR) addresses path biases in Vision-and-Language Navigation.
Room-Across-Room (RxR) elicits more references to visible entities in Vision-and-Language Navigation instructions.
Each word in Room-Across-Room (RxR) instructions is time-aligned to the virtual poses of instruction creators and validators.
Room-Across-Room (RxR) has baseline scores for monolingual Vision-and-Language Navigation, multilingual Vision-and-Language Navigation, and multitask learning with Room-to-Room annotations.
A Hierarchical Transformer divides social media conversation threads into subthreads, represents each subthread with BERT, and encodes all subthreads using a global Transformer layer.
A Coupled Transformer Module captures interactions between the stance classification task and the rumor verification task.
A Post-Level Attention layer incorporates predicted stance labels into rumor verification.
The Coupled Hierarchical Transformer model outperforms existing multi-task learning approaches for rumor verification on benchmark datasets.
Deep Weighted MaxSAT connects deep neural networks and a graphical model in a joint framework for aspect-based opinion extraction.
Deep Weighted MaxSAT rectifies erroneous predictions in aspect-based opinion extraction using a weighted MaxSAT layer.
Deep Weighted MaxSAT is trainable via end-to-end gradient descent for aspect-based opinion extraction.
DIORA's vector averaging approach is locally greedy and cannot recover from errors when computing the highest scoring parse tree in bottom-up chart parsing.

A large-scale training dataset for feedback prediction is constructed from social media feedback data including counts of replies and upvotes.

RE-LM initializes unsupervised neural machine translation by fine-tuning a language model pretrained only on the high-resource language on both the high-resource language and the low-resource language.

An LSTM pointer-generator model performs canonical morphological segmentation.
A sequence-to-sequence model with hard monotonic attention trained with imitation learning performs canonical morphological segmentation.

In low-resource settings, canonical morphological segmentation accuracy is lower for truly low-resource languages than for high-resource languages.
Semantic label smoothing smooths over well formed relevant sequences that have sufficient n-gram overlap with the target sequence and are semantically similar.
Semantic label smoothing shows consistent improvement over state-of-the-art techniques across multiple sequence to sequence datasets.
OpenIE6 establishes a new state of the art for open information extraction while extracting faster.
The Iterative Grid Labeling (IGL) architecture treats open information extraction as a 2-D grid labeling task.

The IGL-based coordination analyzer establishes a new state of the art for coordination analysis.
Refer, Reuse, Reduce generates referring utterances grounded in both the visual and the conversational context.
Refer, Reuse, Reduce generates subsequent references that exhibit linguistic patterns similar to those of humans.
BRSON uses BERT to capture dependency relationships among sentences for modeling coherence of entire paragraphs.
Relational Pointer Decoder incorporates relative ordering information into the pointer network using Deep Relational Module.
Deep Relational Module uses BERT to exploit deep semantic connections and relative ordering between sentences.
Weakly supervised pre-training for automated Chinese essay scoring uses coarse ratings (good and poor) on a large diverse-topic essay dataset.
Supervised cross-prompt fine-tuning for automated Chinese essay scoring uses previously rated essays from existing prompts that have the same score range as the target prompt.
Supervised target-prompt fine-tuning for automated Chinese essay scoring uses target-prompt training data.
Multi-stage pre-training improves domain adaptation for automated Chinese essay scoring.
Response selection in multi-party conversations is a dynamic topic tracking task that matches the topic between the response and relevant conversation context.
A multi-task learning framework performs dynamic topic disentanglement and response selection by encoding only two utterances at a time using large pretrained models.
Topic-BERT embeds topic information into BERT using self-supervised learning.
Mention Extraction and Linking uses a unified extractor to recognize all types of slot mentions in the question sentence.
Mention Extraction and Linking uses a linker to map recognized columns to the table schema to generate executable SQL queries.
Mention Extraction and Linking is trained with automatically generated annotations.
FIND enables humans to debug deep learning text classifiers by disabling irrelevant hidden features.
FIND enables humans to improve CNN text classifiers trained on imperfect datasets, including those with biases and dissimilar train-test distributions.
A lightweight extension of BERT using mutual information maximization derives meaningful sentence embeddings in an unsupervised manner.
Sentence embeddings derived using mutual information maximization outperform other unsupervised baselines on semantic textual similarity tasks and downstream supervised tasks.
Sentence embeddings derived using mutual information maximization outperform SBERT when in-domain labeled data is unavailable.
Sentence embeddings derived using mutual information maximization achieve performance competitive with supervised methods on various tasks.
Unsupervised cross-lingual music genre annotation is feasible when combining distributed concept embeddings and ontologies.
A new domain-dependent cross-lingual corpus serves as a benchmark for state-of-the-art multilingual pre-trained embedding models.
DAGA generates synthetic data for low-resource tagging tasks using language models trained on linearized labeled sentences.
DAGA is applicable to both supervised and semi-supervised settings.
DAGA consistently outperforms baseline methods for low-resource tagging tasks, especially when the amount of gold training data is small.
A self-debiasing framework prevents models from mainly utilizing biases without knowing them in advance.
A self-debiasing framework is complementary to existing debiasing methods.
A self-debiasing framework allows existing debiasing methods to retain improvement on challenge datasets that expose models' reliance on biases without targeting specific biases.
State-of-the-art lifelong language learning methods require an unrealistically large memory module to achieve good performance.
State-of-the-art lifelong language learning methods suffer from negative transfer.

Video-based Multimodal Summarization with Multimodal Output (VMSMO) is the task of selecting a cover frame from a video and generating a textual summary from a corresponding news article.
DIMS uses a conditional self-attention mechanism to capture local semantic information within video.
DIMS uses a global-attention mechanism to handle the semantic relationship between news text and video at a high level.
DIMS achieves state-of-the-art performance on the VMSMO task as measured by automatic metrics and human evaluations.
SLURP includes a new challenging dataset in English spanning 18 domains that is larger and linguistically more diverse than existing datasets.
SLURP includes competitive baselines for spoken language understanding based on state-of-the-art NLU and ASR systems.

ProgVGAT verifies facts by integrating programs and execution into textual inference models.

Graph attention verification networks fuse evidence from verbalized program execution, program structures, and original statements and tables to make fact verification decisions.

DGST transfers text style using a Dual-Generator network architecture.

CMLL uses multi-label learning with a sliding window centered on a specified emotion clause to extract corresponding cause clauses.
EMLL uses multi-label learning with a sliding window centered on a specified cause clause to extract corresponding emotion clauses.

A corpus of Wikinews articles annotated with temporal dependency graphs can be used for training systems to understand temporal relations in text.

The Pretraining Simulation mechanism recalls knowledge from pretraining tasks without requiring pretraining data.

A generalized framework models face acts in persuasion conversations.

Relational position encodings provide relational graph attention networks with sequential information reflecting the relational graph structure.

Char-Entity-Transformer augments self-attention in BERT using a combination of character and entity representations for Chinese named entity recognition.

Two new datasets for named entity recognition in long-text are created.

A small set of probing examples leads to the discovery of orders of magnitude more disguised offensive examples.

SSNMT self-selects training samples of increasing complexity and task-relevance and performs a denoising curriculum.

SSNMT starts extracting and learning from Wikipedia data suitable for high school students and moves towards content suitable for first year undergraduate students.

A larger corpus of human post-edits for English to German neural machine translation is compiled.

A new dataset for zero-shot stance detection captures a wider range of topics and lexical variation than previous datasets.

Dialogue Distillation is a data augmentation method for training open-domain dialogue models using unpaired data.

Universal Few-shot textual Entailment (UFO-Entail) enables pretrained textual entailment models to generalize to new domains with few examples.

Which *BERT organizes advances in contextualized encoders by common themes.

For prompts containing certain given names in under-specified contexts, pre-trained language models generate continuations that substantially differ from those of other names and often exhibit more-than-average negative sentiment.

A task-specific date-time entity extraction model with negation constraint detection identifies relevant date-time entities and their negation constraints.

Conclusions about evaluation metrics for text summarization based on older datasets do not necessarily hold for modern datasets and systems.

An A* parser and a transition-based parser for AM dependency parsing guarantee well-typedness.

Span clustering is a higher-order inference method for coreference resolution.
Cluster merging is a higher-order inference method for coreference resolution.
Higher-order inference has negative to marginal impact on coreference resolution when using a high-performing encoder such as SpanBERT.
A dataset of English sentences from Reddit advice forums r/AskParents and r/needadvice annotated for the presence of advice.

Advice identification is challenging.
Debiased-Chat uses adversarial learning to train dialogue models free from gender bias in neural dialogue generation without degrading response quality.
Debiased-Chat reduces gender bias in neural dialogue generation while maintaining response quality.
An alignment-free cross-lingual semantic role labeling model requires only source language annotations and a parallel corpus of raw text.
An LSTM-based semantic role labeler is jointly trained with a semantic role compressor and multilingual word embeddings.

DAIS is a benchmark dataset for the English dative alternation containing human judgments that systematically varies the definiteness and length of arguments.

LXMERT is unable to generate rich and semantically meaningful imagery with its current training setup.

X-LXMERT maintains visual question answering and image captioning performance comparable to LXMERT.

Lexically constrained rewriting identifies constraint words that support semantic incongruity in homophonic pun generation.
Lexically constrained rewriting rewrites sentences with explicit positive and negative constraints for homophonic pun generation.

Counterfactual off-policy training explores potential responses in neural dialogue generation via counterfactual reasoning.
Counterfactual off-policy training infers the outcome of an alternative policy for an observed response.
Counterfactual off-policy training produces counterfactual responses that have higher quality than responses synthesized from scratch in neural dialogue generation.

A model jointly performs sequence labeling and sentence-level classification.
A model performs multiple sequence labeling tasks simultaneously using a single shared natural language output space.
A model incorporates label semantics and shares knowledge across sequence labeling and sentence-level classification tasks.
A model sets a new state-of-the-art for few-shot slot labeling.

A model sets a new state-of-the-art on the SNIPS dataset.
Pre-trained Transformers used out-of-the-box are calibrated in-domain for natural language inference, paraphrase detection, and commonsense reasoning.
Pre-trained Transformers can have lower calibration error out-of-domain than baselines for natural language inference, paraphrase detection, and commonsense reasoning.
Temperature scaling reduces calibration error in-domain for natural language inference, paraphrase detection, and commonsense reasoning.
Label smoothing helps calibrate posteriors out-of-domain for natural language inference, paraphrase detection, and commonsense reasoning.
Gender bias in text is decomposed along the dimensions of speaker gender, addressee gender, and referent gender.
Eight large-scale datasets are automatically annotated with gender information.
A new crowdsourced evaluation benchmark for gender bias classification is collected.
More fine-grained gender bias classifiers are trained.
Gender bias classifiers enable controlling for gender bias in generative models.
doc2dial is a dataset of goal-oriented dialogues grounded in associated documents.
Dialogue flows in doc2dial are constructed based on higher-level relations across text sections and lower-level relations between discourse units within a section.
doc2dial covers a variety of dialogue scenes in information-seeking conversations.
Multiple dialogue modeling tasks are defined for document-grounded dialogue evaluation.
Baseline approaches are provided for document-grounded dialogue modeling.
A structure-aware Transformer language model incorporates syntactic distance to encode phrasal constituency and dependency connections.
A middle-layer structural learning strategy integrates syntactic structure into a Transformer language model via multi-task learning with semantic tasks.
A structure-aware Transformer language model achieves improved perplexity.
A structure-aware Transformer language model induces accurate syntactic phrases.
Structure-aware fine-tuning improves performance on semantic-dependent and syntactic-dependent tasks.

Knowledge graph embeddings are debiased by training them to be neutral to sensitive attributes such as gender by default using an adversarial loss followed by adding sensitive attributes back in whitelisted cases.

Debiased knowledge graph embeddings maintain accuracy in the triple prediction task.
Electric assigns a scalar energy score to each input token to indicate the token's likelihood given its context.

Entity-pair embeddings are directly learned for knowledge graph alignment.

End-to-end Relation Extraction research contains several patterns of invalid comparisons.

The cybersecurity event detection dataset contains manual annotations for 30 important cybersecurity event types.
The cybersecurity event detection dataset has a large size to support deep learning model development.
The cybersecurity event detection dataset includes more event types than prior datasets.
The cybersecurity event detection dataset supports modeling of document-level information.
State-of-the-art event detection methods encounter challenges in cybersecurity texts.
Joint modeling of e-commerce product attribute prediction and value extraction captures interactions between attributes and values.
Visual information from e-commerce product images is selectively utilized for e-commerce product attribute prediction and value extraction.
A multimodal dataset of e-commerce product attribute values is annotated.

Controllable Debiasing corrects implicit bias in character portrayals by rewriting text.

A meta-reinforcement learning approach for program induction in few-shot complex knowledge base question answering addresses distributional bias in questions.

A meta-reinforcement learning approach for program induction in few-shot complex knowledge base question answering adapts the meta-learned programmer to new questions by retrieving similar questions from training data.

A meta-reinforcement learning approach for program induction in few-shot complex knowledge base question answering learns a programming policy by utilizing trial trajectories and rewards for similar questions in the support set.

A meta-reinforcement learning approach for program induction in few-shot complex knowledge base question answering achieves state-of-the-art performance on the complex question-answering dataset using only a few trial trajectories and minimal meta-training data.
COD3S generates semantically diverse sentences using neural sequence-to-sequence models.
COD3S conditions generation on locality-sensitive hash-based semantic sentence codes.
The Hamming distances of the semantic sentence codes in COD3S highly correlate with human judgments of semantic textual similarity.
COD3S produces diverse responses while maintaining task performance.
Selective binary masking replaces finetuning for utilizing pretrained language models.

Representations from binary masked language models encode information necessary for solving downstream tasks.
Models from selective binary masking and finetuning reside in loss minima connected by a line segment with nearly constant test accuracy.
Scientific claim verification selects abstracts containing evidence supporting or refuting a scientific claim and identifies rationales for the decision.

SciFact provides a challenging testbed for systems that retrieve and reason over corpora containing specialized domain knowledge.

Contextualized number prediction models utilize textual context.
Contextualized number prediction models benefit from general-purpose unsupervised pretraining.
RiSAWOZ is larger than all previous annotated human-to-human conversational datasets.

RiSAWOZ provides dialogue annotations including dialogue goal in natural language, domain, dialogue states, and dialogue acts for both user and system.
RiSAWOZ provides linguistic annotations on discourse phenomena such as ellipsis and coreference.
RiSAWOZ includes benchmark results for natural language understanding, dialogue state tracking, dialogue context-to-text generation, coreference resolution, and ellipsis resolution.
Dialog state tracking formulated as a semantic parsing task over hierarchical representations supports semantic compositionality, cross-domain knowledge sharing, and co-reference.

Small subsets of neurons in pre-trained language models are sufficient for predicting linguistic tasks.
Morphology tasks are localized in fewer neurons than syntax tasks in pre-trained language models.
Neurons in XLNet are more localized and disjoint for predicting linguistic properties than neurons in BERT and other pre-trained language models, which are more distributed and coupled.
Regression on a latent space for prompt-response relationships enables dialogue generation.
Semantically related dialogue sentences have close representations in the latent space.
Dialogue generation via regression on a latent space produces relevant and informative responses.
Weakly Supervised Subevent Knowledge Acquisition extracts subevent relation tuples from text.

An entity masking scheme exploits relational knowledge by using a linked knowledge graph to select informative entities and mask their mentions.

A manually annotated dataset of news articles for political ideology (left, center, right) that is well-balanced across topics and media.

A dataset of social media posts written by active-duty military personnel and containing military-relevant content, sourced from a Korean social Q&A site, includes annotations for suicidal risk levels by military experts and mental health experts.

Shift-Att and Shift-AET outperform neural baselines in word alignment induction.
Shift-AET outperforms GIZA++ in word alignment induction.
Document-level targeted content transfer is a task for rewriting entire recipes to fit dietary restrictions.
A model for document-level targeted content transfer uses GPT-2 and is trained on roughly-aligned recipe pairs.
The model for document-level targeted content transfer generates rewrites that satisfy the dietary restriction, are coherent and diverse, and remain close to the original recipe.
Rewrites for document-level targeted content transfer focus on substantive content changes rather than stylistic changes.
Massively multilingual transformers are less effective in resource-lean scenarios and for distant languages.

Transfer performance with massively multilingual transformers correlates with linguistic proximity between source and target languages and with the size of target language corpora used in pretraining.
A keyphrase generation approach uses a generator to produce keyphrases for documents and a discriminator to distinguish human-curated keyphrases from machine-generated keyphrases.
A keyphrase generation model using Generative Adversarial Networks achieves state-of-the-art performance for abstractive keyphrase generation.
A keyphrase generation model using Generative Adversarial Networks achieves performance comparable to the best performing extractive techniques for keyphrase generation.
A keyphrase generation model using Generative Adversarial Networks is not significantly better than state-of-the-art generative models for keyphrase generation.
GigaBERT is a customized bilingual BERT designed for Arabic NLP and English-to-Arabic zero-shot transfer learning.

GigaBERT outperforms mBERT, XLM-RoBERTa, and AraBERT on Arabic named entity recognition, part-of-speech tagging, argument role labeling, and relation extraction in supervised and zero-shot transfer settings.
Solving word-based substitution codes involves constructing a decoding lattice.
Solving word-based substitution codes involves searching a decoding lattice with a neural language model.
Cross-View Training achieves similar performance to BERT on sequence tagging tasks with lesser financial and environmental impact.
GAIN recognizes relations in document-level relation extraction for long paragraphs.
GAIN constructs a heterogeneous mention-level graph that captures complex interactions among different mentions.
GAIN constructs an entity-level graph that aggregates mentions for the same entities.
GAIN uses a novel path reasoning mechanism to infer relations between entities.
GAIN outperforms the previous state-of-the-art on the DocRED dataset.
ISAAQ uses pre-trained transformers with fine-tuning and ensembling for Textbook Question Answering.

ISAAQ uses bottom-up and top-down attention to identify regions of interest corresponding to diagram constituents and their relationships, improving the selection of relevant visual information for each question and answer options.
ISAAQ achieves state-of-the-art results on all Textbook Question Answering question types.
ISAAQ achieves state-of-the-art results on other demanding datasets.
Iterative Feature Mining guides workers to write more diverse text by iteratively constraining their writing.
Prior data collection workflows are special cases of Iterative Feature Mining.
Iterative Feature Mining is applied to dialog tasks including intent classification and slot-filling.
Iterative Feature Mining creates more challenging versions of dialog test sets.
Standard models exhibit performance drops on the dialog test sets created by Iterative Feature Mining.
Sparse contextualized word representations are enabled by an overcomplete set of semantic basis vectors.
An information theory-inspired synset representation uses the co-occurrence of word senses and non-zero coordinates for word forms.
Sparse contextualized word representations can be applied to part-of-speech tagging.
Pulling back the downstream learning objective provides a principled motivation for the straight-through estimator and SPIGOT in latent structure learning.
New pulled-back estimators provide surrogate gradients for the argmax operation in latent structure learning.
Pulled-back estimators exhibit insights and failure cases compared to popular alternatives in latent structure learning.
CRQDA is a data augmentation method for machine reading comprehension, question generation, and question-answering natural language inference tasks.
CRQDA treats question data augmentation as a constrained question rewriting problem to generate context-relevant, high-quality, and diverse question data samples.
CRQDA uses a Transformer Autoencoder to map discrete questions into a continuous embedding space.
CRQDA uses a pre-trained machine reading comprehension model to revise question representations iteratively via gradient-based optimization.
CRQDA maps revised question representations back into the discrete space to serve as additional question data.
INQUISITIVE is a dataset of questions elicited during document reading.
INQUISITIVE questions target high-level semantic and discourse comprehension of text.
Readers engage in a series of pragmatic strategies to seek information during text comprehension.
Context is important for generating INQUISITIVE questions.
ChrEn is a low-resource Cherokee-English parallel dataset.
ChrEn includes splits for in-domain and out-of-domain evaluation.
A Cherokee monolingual dataset enables semi-supervised learning.
Cherokee-English and English-Cherokee machine translation systems use phrase-based and neural architectures.
Cherokee-English and English-Cherokee machine translation systems employ semi-supervised methods, transfer learning, and multilingual joint training with other languages.
Planner-Generator generates natural and diverse disfluent texts by having a Planner select insertion points and a Generator create disfluent segments.
Pretraining with disfluent texts generated by Planner-Generator achieves state-of-the-art performance on disfluency detection for the Switchboard corpus.
Better representation learning and better training techniques are fundamental factors for low-resource domain adaptation in task-oriented semantic parsing.
BART-based representation learning outperforms encoder-only pre-trained representations for low-resource domain adaptation in task-oriented semantic parsing.
Task-oriented semantic parsers for low-resource domain adaptation are trained using optimization-based meta-learning.
TOPv2 is a multi-domain task-oriented semantic parsing dataset for low-resource domain adaptation.
Beam search enforces uniform information density in text.
Decoding objectives that explicitly enforce uniform information density in text alleviate decoding problems for poorly calibrated language generation models.
In neural machine translation, adherence to uniform information density in text correlates with standard evaluation metrics.
NumerSense is a diagnostic dataset of masked-word-prediction probes for numerical commonsense knowledge.
BERT and RoBERTa perform poorly on numerical commonsense knowledge without fine-tuning.
Fine-tuning with distant supervision improves performance on numerical commonsense knowledge.
The best supervised model for numerical commonsense knowledge performs substantially worse than humans.
Blank Language Model (BLM) generates sequences by dynamically creating and filling in blanks.
Blank Language Model (BLM) can start from a single blank or partially completed text with blanks at specified locations.
Blank Language Model (BLM) iteratively determines which word to place in a blank and whether to insert new blanks, stopping when no blanks remain.
Blank Language Model (BLM) is trained using a lower bound of the marginal data likelihood.
Blank Language Model (BLM) outperforms all other baselines in filling missing text snippets in terms of accuracy and fluency.
A set of writing strategies for science communication is compiled from prescriptive sources.
An annotation scheme enables human recognition of writing strategies in science communication.
An annotated dataset of science writing documents records the use of writing strategies.
Transformer-based classifiers recognize writing strategies in science communication.
The use of writing strategies, such as storytelling and emphasizing the most important findings, varies significantly across publications with different reader audiences.

BAE generates adversarial examples for text classification via a black box attack that replaces and inserts tokens by masking portions of the text and using BERT masked language model to generate alternatives for the masked tokens.
BAE performs a stronger attack than prior work.
BAE generates adversarial examples with improved grammaticality and semantic coherence compared to prior work.
Student-Forcing Optimal Transport reduces exposure bias by matching sequences generated during training and testing.
An extension to optimal transport learning incorporates structural and contextual information for long sequences.
The out-of-vocabulary rate in pre-trained multilingual BERT models introduces information loss on downstream tasks.
The out-of-vocabulary rate in pre-trained multilingual BERT models obstructs model potential on downstream tasks.
PatchBERT mitigates out-of-vocabulary issues in pre-trained multilingual BERT models.
PatchBERT improves performance while maintaining the same parameter count when combined with fine-tuning.

Two large Turkish natural language inference datasets are created by machine-translating two large English natural language inference datasets and validating translation quality and label fidelity with experts.
In-language embeddings are essential for Turkish natural language inference.
Morphological parsing can be avoided for Turkish natural language inference when the training set is large.
A fine-grained evaluation for Chinese Word Segmentation systems diagnoses the strengths and weaknesses of existing models under the in-dataset setting.
A fine-grained evaluation for Chinese Word Segmentation systems quantifies the discrepancy between different criteria.
A fine-grained evaluation for Chinese Word Segmentation systems alleviates the negative transfer problem in multi-criteria learning.
Neural Mask Generator generates domain- and task-adaptive word maskings for self-supervised language model adaptation.
Neural Mask Generator employs off-policy actor-critic with entropy regularization and experience replay.
Neural Mask Generator uses a Transformer-based policy network to assess word importance in text.
Neural Mask Generator outperforms rule-based masking strategies for language model adaptation.
Memory-driven Transformer uses a relational memory to record key information during radiology report generation.
Memory-driven Transformer applies memory-driven conditional layer normalization to incorporate the relational memory into the Transformer decoder for radiology report generation.
Memory-driven Transformer outperforms previous models on language generation metrics and clinical evaluations for radiology report generation.
Memory-driven Transformer achieves the first radiology report generation results on MIMIC-CXR.
Memory-driven Transformer generates long radiology reports containing necessary medical terms.
Memory-driven Transformer produces meaningful image-text attention mappings for radiology report generation.
Visual-textual grounding is possible in diverse video categories beyond instructional videos.
Pretraining on diverse video corpora produces representations that generalize to non-instructional and instructional video domains.
Incremental neural coreference resolution utilizes contextualized encoders and neural components under a constant memory constraint.
Incremental neural coreference resolution proposes and scores each mention span against explicit entity representations from earlier document context.
Incremental neural coreference resolution updates entity representations with new mention spans and retains only a fixed set of salient entities throughout the document.
Incremental neural coreference resolution achieves constant memory usage with minimal performance loss.
CMU-MOSEAS is the first large-scale multimodal language dataset for Spanish, Portuguese, German and French.
CMU-MOSEAS is the largest multimodal language dataset for Spanish, Portuguese, German and French.
CMU-MOSEAS covers diverse topics and speakers.
CMU-MOSEAS provides labels for sentiment, subjectivity, emotions, and attributes.
CMU-MOSEAS enables multilingual studies in multimodal language.
Word2vec, GloVe, and other word embedding algorithms are expressed in a common form.
Common conditions required for producing performant word embeddings are identified.
RuleNN learns linguistic expressions for sentence classification built on predicates extracted via shallow natural language understanding.
RuleNN outperforms statistical relational learning and other neuro-symbolic methods in sentence classification.
RuleNN performs comparably to black-box recurrent neural networks in sentence classification.
RuleNN produces linguistic expressions for sentence classification that are explainable and capture domain semantics.
Allowing domain experts to modify linguistic expressions for sentence classification produced by RuleNN and instill domain knowledge enables human-machine co-creation of models.
A pre-trained model for document-level relation extraction de-emphasizes noisy distant supervision data via multiple pre-training tasks.
Current explainable question answering models have shortcomings in answer-explanation coupling that cause user experience issues.
A hierarchical model with a regularization term strengthens answer-explanation coupling in explainable question answering.
Two evaluation scores quantify answer-explanation coupling in explainable question answering.
F1 and similar scores are insufficient for estimating the usefulness of explainable question answering models in practical settings with human users.
Two evaluation scores for answer-explanation coupling are better aligned with user experience.
Multistage Fusion with Forget Gate models fine-grained interactions between modalities through a multistep fusion schema.
Multistage Fusion with Forget Gate controls the flow of redundant information in multimodal long sequences via a forgetting module.
Multistage Fusion with Forget Gate achieves state-of-the-art performance for multimodal summarization on the How2 dataset.
Multistage Fusion with Forget Gate achieves performance close to the ground-truth transcript model when using high noise ASR transcripts, reducing manual annotation cost.
Random pruning of a large fraction of attention heads in Transformer and BERT models has limited effect on accuracy across multiple NLP tasks.
In Transformer models, attention heads identified as important based on their location are not more critical for model performance than other attention heads.
In BERT models, attention heads in top and bottom layers are equally critical for model performance.
Pruning strategies that avoid removing middle-layer attention heads and consecutive attention heads yield better model performance.
During fine-tuning, the compensation for pruned attention heads is equally distributed across the unpruned attention heads.
MUTANT exposes models to perceptually similar, yet semantically distinct mutations of question-image pairs to improve out-of-distribution generalization in visual question answering.
MUTANT uses a consistency-constrained training objective to understand the effect of semantic changes in question-image pairs on answers.
MUTANT does not require knowledge of train and test answer distributions.
MUTANT achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on VQA-CP.
Open-domain Information eXpression (OIX) is an OIE friendly expression of a sentence without information loss.
Open Information Annotation (OIA) is a predicate-function-argument annotation for sentences.
A labeled dataset of sentence-OIA pairs is constructed.
A dependency-based rule system generates Open Information Annotation from sentences.
Learning Open Information Annotation from a sentence is challenging due to the complexity of natural language sentences.
DORB optimizes multiple metric rewards for language generation by dynamically selecting the next metric reward to optimize using a multi-armed bandit based on expected arm gains.
SM-Bandit optimizes multiple metric rewards for language generation using a single multi-reward bandit.
HM-Bandit optimizes multiple metric rewards for language generation using a hierarchical multi-reward bandit.
GraphGlove is an unsupervised graph word representation learned end-to-end.
In GraphGlove, each word is represented as a node in a weighted graph and the distance between words is the shortest path distance between the corresponding nodes.
GraphGlove modifies the GloVe training algorithm by adopting a method for learning differentiable weighted graphs.
GraphGlove outperforms vector-based methods on word similarity and analogy tasks.
GraphGlove learns graph structures that are hierarchical and similar to WordNet.
GraphGlove learns graph geometries that are highly non-trivial and contain subgraphs with different local topology.
Lexical ambiguity can be measured as the entropy of meanings a word can take.
Lexical ambiguity can be estimated using WordNet and human annotation.
Lexical ambiguity can be estimated using BERT without human annotation and is applicable to a large number of languages.
The BERT-based estimate of lexical ambiguity significantly correlates with the number of synonyms a word has in WordNet across six high-resource languages.
Lexical ambiguity negatively correlates with contextual uncertainty across 18 typologically diverse languages.
Speakers compensate for lexical ambiguity by making contexts more informative.
TNT is a language pre-training model for content moderation.
TNT reconstructs text from substitution, transposition, deletion, and insertion operations.
TNT predicts both operation types and token labels during text normalization.
TNT outperforms strong baselines for hate speech classification.
TNT is applicable to misspelling correction.
A Variational Recurrent Neural Network with structured attention layers and discrete latent states learns dialogue structure without supervision.
Structured attention layers for dialogue structure induction focus on different parts of source sentence embeddings and enforce a structural inductive bias.
A Variational Recurrent Neural Network with structured attention layers and discrete latent states learns semantic structures similar to the templates used to generate two-party dialogue corpora.

A Variational Recurrent Neural Network with structured attention layers and discrete latent states learns an interactive structure that distinguishes speakers or addresses and disentangles dialogues without explicit human annotation on multi-party dialogue datasets.
A schema-guided approach conditions Natural Language Generation on a schema describing the API in natural language.
A template-based method for Natural Language Generation uses a small number of templates, growing linearly with the number of slots, to convey API semantics.

A template-based method for Natural Language Generation generates semantically correct but incoherent utterances by concatenating templates for arbitrary slot combinations and then rewrites them using a pre-trained language model to produce coherent text.
A template-based method for Natural Language Generation is robust to out-of-domain inputs.
A template-based method for Natural Language Generation shows improved sample efficiency.
Training on non-linguistic data with latent structure (MIDI music or Java code) improves natural language test performance without surface form or vocabulary overlap.
Training on artificial parentheses languages with hierarchical recursive structure or with paired tokens without recursion improves natural language test performance.
Zero-shot performance on a test language is highly correlated with typological syntactic similarity to the training language when controlling for vocabulary overlap.
Representations induced by pre-training correspond to cross-linguistic syntactic properties.
ONUS decomposes hard, multi-hop questions into simpler, single-hop sub-questions without supervision.
ONUS improves question answering on the original, out-of-domain, and multi-hop development sets of HotpotQA.
ONUS automatically learns to decompose different kinds of questions.
ONUS matches the utility of supervised and heuristic decomposition methods for question answering while exceeding them in fluency.
Sub-questions shed light on why a question answering system makes a prediction.
A BERT-based temporal dependency parser has multiple variants.
BERT improves temporal dependency parsing.
Deep contextualized neural language models have limitations in temporal dependency parsing.
Mention representations for coreference resolution can be learned in a self-supervised manner to improve resolution accuracy.
Two self-supervised tasks improve mention representation for coreference resolution.
Pre-training mention representations achieves state-of-the-art results on the GAP dataset.
A kernelized non-linear technique isolates gender bias in word embeddings.
The linearity of the gender bias subspace in word embeddings is empirically analyzed.
Gender bias in word embeddings is well captured by a linear subspace.
Dynamic context selection selects a variable number of context sentences for document-level neural machine translation.
A selection module scores candidate context sentences independently of the translation module.
Reinforcement learning trains the selection module and translation module end-to-end.
A novel reward function encourages the selection and utilization of dynamic context sentences.
Dynamic context selection improves the performance of document-level neural machine translation.
Reactive supervision collects sarcasm data by utilizing the dynamics of online conversations.
A first-of-its-kind large dataset of tweets with sarcasm perspective labels and new contextual features is available.
PIPM enhances the prior selection module by providing necessary posterior information.
KDBTS trains the decoder with knowledge selected from the prior distribution, removing the exposure bias of knowledge selection.
WD-Match regularizes feature vectors from different domains in asymmetrical text matching using a Wasserstein distance-based regularizer.
WD-Match generates feature vectors for different domains in asymmetrical text matching that are not easily discriminated.
WD-Match trains via a game-theoretic process that minimizes the matching loss regularized by the Wasserstein distance in asymmetrical text matching.
WD-Match consistently outperforms existing text matching methods on multiple asymmetrical text matching benchmarks.
A self-supervised contrastive learning mechanism learns the relationship between original samples, factual samples, and counterfactual samples.
Learning to contrast counterfactual samples improves reasoning capability and robustness of visual question answering models.
LAReQA evaluates language-agnostic answer retrieval from a multilingual candidate pool.
LAReQA tests for strong cross-lingual alignment by requiring that semantically related cross-language pairs are closer in representation space than unrelated same-language pairs.
Augmenting training data via machine translation achieves strong cross-lingual alignment for language-agnostic answer retrieval.
Model performance on zero-shot cross-lingual tasks targeting weak alignment is not predictive of performance on language-agnostic answer retrieval tasks requiring strong cross-lingual alignment.
Dialogue generation implicitly reconstructs scenario knowledge using dialogue history and future conversation.

Imitation learning transfers scenario knowledge via hierarchical supervising signals from a scenario-based dialogue model to a conventional dialogue model, eliminating the need for future conversation in inference.
Internalizing conversation scenarios via imitation learning improves response diversity and relevance compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
Internalizing conversation scenarios via imitation learning produces responses that express scenario-specific knowledge.
APE studies the contents, structure, and connections between peer review and rebuttal.
A dataset of fully annotated review-rebuttal passage pairs facilitates the study of APE.
APE is formulated as a combination of sequence labeling and text relation classification.
A multitask learning framework based on hierarchical LSTM networks detects argumentative propositions and extracts argument pairs from peer review and rebuttal texts.
MedDialog is a large-scale medical dialogue dataset containing Chinese and English conversations between patients and doctors covering multiple disease specialties.
MedDialog is the largest medical dialogue dataset to date.
Models trained on the Chinese MedDialog dataset generate clinically correct and doctor-like medical dialogues.
Transfer learning with models pretrained on the Chinese MedDialog dataset improves medical dialogue generation in low-resource settings.
A query-based biomedical information retrieval task identifies research publications that support health-related news claims across newswire and research literature genres.
A new dataset pairs health-related news claims with supporting research abstracts.
A two-step method for cross-genre medical information retrieval selects relevant research abstract candidates and re-ranks the candidate list.
Cross-genre medical information retrieval is viable, but incorporating domain-specific knowledge is crucial.
Widget captioning is a task for generating natural language descriptions for mobile user interface elements from multimodal input including image and structural representations.
A large-scale crowdsourced dataset for widget captioning contains language phrases for UI elements spanning unique mobile UI screens.
Feature modalities and learning strategies for widget captioning are analyzed for their impact on caption quality.
AutoPrompt is an automated method for creating prompts for diverse tasks using gradient-guided search.
Masked language models have an inherent capability to perform sentiment analysis and natural language inference without additional parameters or finetuning.
Automatically generated prompts elicit more accurate factual knowledge from masked language models than manually created prompts on the LAMA benchmark.
Automatically generated prompts enable masked language models to be used as relation extractors that outperform supervised relation extraction models.
Quark identifies relevant sentences for multi-hop question answering on HotpotQA using BERT independently of each other.
Quark selects answers for multi-hop questions on HotpotQA using a standard BERT span prediction model on the selected sentences.
Quark produces supporting sentences for multi-hop questions on HotpotQA by using the sentence selection model with the chosen answer.
Quark outperforms complex models on HotpotQA for question answering and support identification.
Quark achieves performance on HotpotQA comparable to RoBERTa.
SampleRank is adapted to neural models for efficient training and decoding of hybrids of graphical models and neural networks.
The neural skip-chain CRF model performs named entity recognition using skip-chain dependencies.
The neural skip-chain CRF model outperforms a strong baseline for named entity recognition on three languages.
The neural skip-chain CRF model achieves state-of-the-art results for named entity recognition on Dutch.
The semantic capacity of a term is higher when the term has a broader meaning scope.
A two-step model evaluates semantic capacity of terms using term co-occurrence information from a text corpus when sufficient co-occurrence information is available.
An adversarial neural network decomposes pretrained representations into independent denotation and connotation representations.
Words with the same denotation but different connotations are closer in denotation space and further apart in connotation space.
Denotation vectors improve viewpoint diversity of document rankings in information retrieval.
Circa is the first large-scale English corpus of polar question and indirect answer pairs, containing yes/no, uncertain, middle-ground, and conditional responses.
BERT-based neural models predict categories (yes/no, uncertain, middle-ground, conditional) for polar question and indirect answer pairs.
Transfer learning from entailment works reasonably for predicting indirect answer categories but does not achieve sufficient performance for robust dialog.
Knowledge distillation transfers structured knowledge from a graph neural network pre-trained on structured inputs to an NLP model for text-based relational reasoning.
A contrastive learning module aligns the latent representations of NLP models and a graph neural network to overcome cross-modal knowledge transfer difficulties in text-based relational reasoning.
Distilling structured knowledge from a graph neural network into NLP models improves relational reasoning performance on the CLUTRR benchmark.
A sequence generative framework normalizes multi-implication Chinese medical procedure mentions by generating all corresponding entities.
Category-based constraint decoding avoids unrealistic results in Chinese medical procedure entity normalization.
Category-based model refining avoids unrealistic results in Chinese medical procedure entity normalization.
A sequence generative framework achieves better performance in multi-implication Chinese medical procedure entity normalization than existing baselines.
Table-sequence encoders use a table encoder and a sequence encoder that help each other during representation learning for joint entity and relation extraction.
Using two distinct encoders is advantageous over using one encoder for joint entity and relation extraction.
Controlled experiments evaluate syntactic nominal number and verbal argument structure generalizations from minimal exposure (as few as two training examples).
An evaluation setup assesses transfer of syntactic generalizations from base grammatical contexts to transformed grammatical contexts.
Neural language models induce proper syntactic generalizations from minimal exposure (as few as two training examples) in most cases.
Structurally supervised models generalize more accurately than LSTM models for syntactic generalization.
Neural language models transfer syntactic generalizations from base grammatical contexts to transformed grammatical contexts.
LNMap learns cross-lingual word embeddings for bilingual lexicon induction in a semi-supervised manner.
LNMap does not require the isomorphic assumption for bilingual lexicon induction.
LNMap uses non-linear mapping in the latent space of two independently pre-trained autoencoders for bilingual lexicon induction.
LNMap outperforms existing models for bilingual lexicon induction on resource-rich and low-resource languages.
LNMap requires non-linear mapping for high performance in bilingual lexicon induction.
LUKE outputs pretrained contextualized representations for both words and entities.
LUKE treats words and entities as independent tokens.
LUKE uses a pretraining task that predicts randomly masked words and entities in an entity-annotated corpus.
LUKE uses an entity-aware self-attention mechanism that considers token types (words or entities) when computing attention scores.
LUKE achieves state-of-the-art results on entity typing, relation classification, named entity recognition, cloze-style question answering, and extractive question answering.
A weakly-supervised approach for aspect-based sentiment analysis uses only a few keywords per aspect and sentiment without labeled examples.
Joint topic embeddings for sentiment and aspect are learned in the word embedding space with regularizations for topic distinctiveness.
Neural models pre-train classifiers using embedding-based predictions and self-train on unlabeled data to generalize word-level discriminative information.
A method for unsupervised disfluency detection combines self-training and self-supervised learning.
Combining self-training and self-supervised learning for unsupervised disfluency detection achieves competitive performance compared to supervised systems that use contextualized word embeddings.
A script induction approach is based on the causal effect between events, formally defined via interventions.
Script induction based on the causal effect between events yields outputs that better match the intuition of what a script represents.
The recurrent interaction network dynamically learns interactions between entity recognition and relation classification to model task-specific features.
Denotation graphs represent the linkage between specific concepts (such as sentences describing images) and abstract generic concepts (such as short phrases) that are visually grounded.
A method incorporates automatically mined denotation graphs into representation learning.
Representations incorporating denotation graphs improve performance on cross-modal image retrieval, referring expression, and compositional attribute-object recognition.
STORIUM includes a dataset of author-generated lengthy stories with fine-grained natural language annotations interspersed throughout the narratives including character goals and attributes.
STORIUM includes an evaluation platform that allows authors to query language models for story continuation suggestions and edit the suggestions.
Automatic metrics based on author edits of model story continuation suggestions correlate with user ratings of generated stories and qualitative feedback from semi-structured user interviews.
In constituency parsing, a supervised parsing model trained on a small number of labeled examples outperforms unsupervised parsing methods.
In constituency parsing, few-shot parsing can be improved by data augmentation and self-training.
For unsupervised constituency parsing, a protocol uses fully unsupervised criteria for hyperparameter tuning and model selection.
For unsupervised constituency parsing, a protocol uses minimal labeled examples for model development and compares to few-shot parsing trained on the same examples.
A human-in-the-loop methodology learns semantic parsers directly from user interaction.
A semantic parser is introspective of its uncertainties and prompts for user demonstrations when uncertain.
A semantic parser imitates user behavior and continues improving itself autonomously.
An annotation-efficient imitation learning algorithm iteratively collects new datasets by mixing demonstrated states and confident predictions and retrains the semantic parser.
A theoretical analysis establishes a cost bound for the annotation-efficient imitation learning algorithm.
TTOS retrieves accurate knowledge base entities and generates human-like responses for task-oriented dialogue simultaneously.
TTOS amalgamates knowledge from two teacher networks to provide comprehensive guidance for building a task-oriented dialogue system.
Each teacher network is trained via reinforcement learning with a goal-specific reward to transfer expertise to the student network.
TTOS uses two discriminators based on generative adversarial networks to transfer knowledge from two teacher networks to the student network.
TTOS discriminators relax the rigid coupling between the student network and the teacher networks.
Russian SuperGLUE evaluates Russian language understanding through nine tasks modeled after the SuperGLUE methodology.
An open-source framework evaluates transformer models on Russian language understanding tasks.
A leaderboard ranks transformer models for Russian language understanding.
VHDA models the complete aspects of goal-oriented dialogs, including linguistic features and structured annotations (speaker information, dialog acts, and goals).
VHDA models each aspect of goal-oriented dialogs using inter-connected latent variables and generates coherent goal-oriented dialogs from latent spaces.
VHDA uses training strategies that overcome issues in training complex variational models.
VHDA improves the robustness of dialog state trackers via generative data augmentation.
VHDA outperforms previous strong baselines in dialog response generation and user simulation.
Certain nouns are systematically associated with higher accuracy by language models in grammatical tasks, and this effect is robust across grammatical tasks and different language models.
Corpus frequency is unrelated to a noun's performance on grammatical tasks.
A novel noun's grammatical properties can be few-shot learned from various types of training data.

A regularization term pushes the output distributions of a neural translation model to be probable under a language model prior while avoiding wrong predictions when the neural translation model disagrees with the language model.
Using a language model only during training in a neural translation model avoids compromising decoding speed.
Incorporating a language model as prior improves neural machine translation in low-resource settings with limited monolingual data.
mRASP pre-trains a universal multilingual neural machine translation model.
mRASP uses random aligned substitution to bring words and phrases with similar meanings across multiple languages closer in the representation space.
Multiple low-resource language pairs can be utilized to improve rich-resource machine translation.
mRASP improves translation quality for exotic languages not present in the pretraining corpus.
Form2Seq arranges constituent elements in natural reading order for sequential processing.
Form2Seq modifies the sequence-to-sequence framework for grouping low-level elements into higher-order constructs.
Cascaded end-to-end training of low-level element classification and higher-order grouping tasks improves performance compared to training in isolation.
Form2Seq outperforms segmentation baselines on form structure extraction tasks.
Form2Seq achieves state-of-the-art results for table structure recognition on the ICDAR 2013 dataset.
Incremental event detection requires learning new event classes incrementally without performance degradation on previous classes.
Knowledge Consolidation Network (KCN) uses prototype enhanced retrospection to mitigate semantic ambiguity in incremental event detection.
Knowledge Consolidation Network (KCN) uses hierarchical distillation to mitigate class imbalance in incremental event detection.
Four alternative protocols for NLI data collection target improving the ease of producing sound training examples or the quality and diversity of training examples.
Five new training sets for NLI are collected using four alternative protocols and a baseline protocol.
NLI datasets collected with the four alternative protocols do not yield better transfer performance to downstream tasks than the baseline dataset.
All four new protocols for NLI data collection reduce annotation artifacts, especially protocols that have annotators edit pre-filled text boxes.
A knowledge-guided reinforcement learning framework performs open attribute value extraction for emerging entities.
A deep Q-network uses knowledge graph information to sequentially compare extracted answers in open attribute value extraction for emerging entities.
A knowledge-guided reinforcement learning framework applies to multiple information extraction systems.
A knowledge-guided reinforcement learning framework outperforms baseline methods in open attribute value extraction for emerging entities.

Intrinsic probing examines how linguistic information is structured within a representation, whereas extrinsic probing only argues for the presence of linguistic information by showing it can be successfully extracted.
A decomposable multivariate Gaussian probe determines whether linguistic information in word embeddings is dispersed or focal.
In fastText and BERT, most morphosyntactic attributes are reliably encoded by only a few neurons.
fastText concentrates its linguistic structure more than BERT.
AnswerFact is a large scale fact checking dataset for product question answering that includes veracity labels and evidence sentences for each answer.
A neural model with tailored evidence ranking components predicts answer veracity in product question answering.
A neural model with tailored evidence ranking components outperforms existing fact checking methods for answer veracity prediction in product question answering.
PARCOM predicts masked sentences in a paragraph.
SSPlanner predicts content and uses it to guide a pretrained language model for surface realization.
SSPlanner outperforms baseline generation models on PARCOM.
A combination of noun and verb types of keywords is the most effective for content selection.
Providing more content keywords increases overall generation quality.
Physical commonsense learning is formulated as a knowledge graph completion problem.
Training data for physical commonsense knowledge graph completion is augmented using BERT.
Constrained Tucker factorization models complex relationships in physical commonsense knowledge graphs by constraining types and adding negative relationships.
A physical commonsense knowledge graph completion method using BERT data augmentation and constrained Tucker factorization outperforms existing state-of-the-art knowledge graph embedding methods.
Topic models reveal latent semantics in eleven multilingual hate speech corpora.
Two bias evaluation metrics measure semantic similarity between topics and search words used in hate speech corpus construction.
Pruned parameters can be rejuvenated to improve neural machine translation performance.
Rejuvenated parameters are reallocated to enhance modeling of low-level lexical information in neural machine translation.
A deletion in compressive summarization is plausible if it maintains sentence grammaticality and factuality.
A deletion in compressive summarization is salient if it removes important information from the summary.

DICE creates Deterministic, Independent-of-Corpus Embeddings for numbers such that their cosine similarity reflects the actual distance on the number line.

Machine translation systems can be stolen by querying with monolingual sentences and training imitation models.

MSGS is an English-language diagnostic set of 20 ambiguous binary classification tasks for testing whether pretrained models prefer linguistic or surface generalizations during finetuning.

Pretrained language models can learn to represent linguistic features with little pretraining data but require substantially more pretraining data to prefer linguistic generalizations over surface ones.
RoBERTa_BASE consistently demonstrates a linguistic bias with approximately 30 billion words of pretraining data.
Modeling points of correspondence between sentences is important for effective sentence fusion with Transformers.
An end-to-end model for cross-lingual NLU jointly aligns slots and predicts slot labels.
MultiATIS++ is a multilingual NLU corpus that extends the Multilingual ATIS corpus to nine languages across four language families.
Joint slot alignment and slot label prediction outperforms a simple label projection method using fast-align on most languages.
Joint slot alignment and slot label prediction achieves competitive performance to the state-of-the-art projection method with reduced training time.
Framing the Winograd Schema task as multiple choice improves performance.
Reuse of a pretrained language modeling head mitigates hyperparameter sensitivity in Winograd Schema evaluations.
A seq2seq pre-training approach builds pre-trained models for AMR parsing using single and joint training on machine translation, syntactic parsing, and AMR parsing.
A multi-task learning fine-tuning method optimizes AMR parsing performance while preserving the response of pre-trained models.
Single and joint pre-trained seq2seq models achieve state-of-the-art performance on AMR parsing without requiring complex models.
The multi-hop graph relation network (MHGRN) performs multi-hop, multi-relational reasoning over subgraphs from external knowledge graphs for knowledge-aware question answering.
The multi-hop graph relation network (MHGRN) unifies path-based reasoning methods and graph neural networks for knowledge-aware question answering.
The multi-hop graph relation network (MHGRN) is scalable for knowledge-aware question answering on CommonsenseQA and OpenbookQA.
Most neural text generators produce texts that are distinguishable from human-written texts, making authorship attribution easier.
Texts generated by GPT2, GROVER, and FAIR are of higher quality and often confuse machine classifiers in authorship attribution tasks.
Relation extraction models rely heavily on type information from entity mentions for predictions.
Shallow heuristics leak via entity mentions in relation extraction datasets, contributing to high benchmark performance.
Entity-masked contrastive pre-training for relation extraction avoids rote memorization of entities and use of superficial cues in mentions.
A multi-resolution emoji prediction dataset contains passage-level multi-class, multi-label, and aspect-level multi-class annotations.
A heuristic annotation method generates aspect-level emoji annotations by leveraging the self-attention mechanism in Transformer networks.
The emoji prediction annotations are validated automatically and manually.
Self-Talk is an unsupervised framework for multiple-choice commonsense question answering.
Self-Talk inquires language models with information seeking questions to discover background knowledge.
Self-Talk improves zero-shot language model performance on multiple commonsense benchmarks.
Self-Talk achieves performance competitive with external knowledge base models on commonsense question answering.
Self-Talk induced knowledge is not always considered helpful by human judges even when it leads to correct answers.
A dataset of social emotions to online topics from Sina Weibo contains trending topics, fine-grained emotion votes from participants, and user comments for context understanding.
A seq2seq model with user comment modeling outperforms other models in multilabel classification of social emotions for online topics and surpasses human prediction.
Multi-head attention has a Bayesian interpretation.
Repulsive attention improves repulsiveness in multi-head attention.
Repulsive attention improves feature diversity in multi-head attention.
Repulsive attention leads to consistent performance improvements across multiple tasks.
The Bayesian interpretation of multi-head attention provides theoretical insights into why and how multi-head attention is used.
Variational word mask (VMASK) automatically learns task-specific important words for text classification.
Variational word mask (VMASK) reduces irrelevant information in text classification.
Variational word mask (VMASK) improves the interpretability of neural text classifier predictions.
Variational word mask (VMASK) improves the prediction accuracy of neural text classifiers.
Dense passage retrieval retrieves passages for open-domain question answering using dense representations alone.
Dense passage retrieval uses a dual-encoder framework to learn embeddings from a small number of questions and passages.
Dense passage retrieval outperforms a strong Lucene-BM25 system in top-20 passage retrieval accuracy.
Dense passage retrieval enables a new state-of-the-art end-to-end question answering system on multiple open-domain QA benchmarks.
Slot Attention shares knowledge between slots and utterances to predict supporting spans for dialogue state values.
Value Normalization converts supporting spans to ontology values for dialogue state tracking.
The MultiWOZ 2.1 dataset includes annotations of the shortest supporting spans for dialogue state values.
SAVN achieves state-of-the-art joint accuracy on MultiWOZ 2.0 and MultiWOZ 2.1.
Value Normalization contributes to model performance with incomplete ontology.
Sentence reordering enables pre-training abstractive document summarization models on unlabeled text.
Next sentence generation enables pre-training abstractive document summarization models on unlabeled text.
Masked document generation enables pre-training abstractive document summarization models on unlabeled text.
Sentence reordering, next sentence generation, and masked document generation improve abstractive document summarization performance on CNN/DailyMail and New York Times benchmarks.

Pre-training with sentence reordering, next sentence generation, and masked document generation achieves comparable abstractive document summarization results to large-scale pre-training methods using significantly less text data.
Rounded Reparameterization Trick reparameterizes Dirichlet distributions for learning VAE-LDA models.
Rounded Reparameterization Trick outperforms existing neural topic models on benchmark and synthetic datasets.
Input marginalization interprets NLP models by marginalizing each token out.
Character-level representations are integrated into a sequence-to-sequence model for Discourse Representation Structure parsing using either a single encoder or a separate encoder.
Character-level representations improve Discourse Representation Structure parsing performance robustly across different language models, languages, and datasets.
For English Discourse Representation Structure parsing, character-level representations yield larger improvements than adding individual linguistic information sources or non-contextual embeddings.
An analysis method based on semantic tags evaluates character-level representations in Discourse Representation Structure parsing.
Character-level representations improve performance on a subset of selected semantic phenomena in Discourse Representation Structure parsing.
Graph structure may not be necessary for textual multi-hop reasoning with the proper use of pre-trained models.
Graph structure and adjacency matrix are prior knowledge for textual multi-hop reasoning.
Graph-attention can be considered as a special case of self-attention.
Graph-attention or the entire graph structure can be replaced by self-attention or Transformers for textual multi-hop reasoning.
Centering-based neural coherence modeling approximates Centering theory to track focus changes between discourse segments.
Centering-based neural coherence modeling constructs structural relationships for hierarchical discourse segments by tracking focus changes.
Centering-based neural coherence modeling incorporates structural information into a structure-aware transformer.
Centering-based neural coherence modeling built on a pretrained language model achieves state-of-the-art performance on automated essay scoring and writing quality assessment.
AC-MIMLLN treats sentences as bags, words as instances, and words indicating an aspect category as key instances for that aspect category.
AC-MIMLLN determines the sentiment of a sentence toward an aspect category by aggregating the sentiments of words indicating that aspect category.
A semantic representation for task-oriented conversational systems represents co-reference and context carryover.
A session-based task-oriented parsing dataset contains compositional queries.
A new family of Seq2Seq models sets state-of-the-art performance for semantic parsing on ATIS, SNIPS, TOP, and DSTC2.
Existing reductions for constituent parsing as labeling do not support discontinuities.
Tree discontinuities in constituent parsing are encoded as nearly ordered permutations of the input sequence.
Discontinuous constituent parsing using the nearly ordered permutation encoding is fast and accurate despite architectural simplicity.
Entities as Experts (EaE) accesses distinct memories of entities mentioned in text.
Entities as Experts (EaE) learns entity representations directly from text.
Entities as Experts (EaE) answers TriviaQA questions better than a much larger encoder-generator Transformer model.
Entities as Experts (EaE) contains more factual knowledge than BERT and previous approaches that integrate external entity knowledge.
Entities as Experts (EaE) uses sparse parameter access during inference.
Correct identification and representation of entities is essential for the performance of Entities as Experts (EaE).
A program learns to pronounce Chinese text in Mandarin without a pronunciation dictionary.
A program establishes a many-to-many mapping between Chinese characters and pronunciations from non-parallel streams of Chinese characters and Chinese pinyin syllables.
A program uses unsupervised methods to decipher Chinese writing into speech.

Acrostic poem generation in English is a generation task where, given an input word, the initial letters of each line spell out the word, the poem's semantics relate to the word, and the poem conforms to a rhyming scheme.
A baseline model for acrostic poem generation combines a conditional neural language model with a neural rhyming model.
Training data for acrostic poem generation is created by training a topic prediction model on topic-annotated poems and then predicting topics for additional poems.
Acrostic poems generated by the baseline model are received well by humans.
The additional constraints of acrostic poem generation do not significantly degrade poem quality.
MSD reduces the effect of overconfidence of the winning score in text classification.
MSD considers the impact of different categories of uncertainty simultaneously in text classification.
