MSD uses mix-up, self-ensembling, and distinctiveness score for uncertainty estimation in text classification.
MSD is applicable with different deep neural networks in text classification.
SWEAT computes the relative polarization of a topical wordset across two distributional representations.
SWEAT uses two wordsets with opposite valence to represent two different poles.
X-SRL is a parallel cross-lingual semantic role labeling dataset in English, French, German, and Spanish with unified predicate and role annotations and human-validated test sets.

A method automatically constructs a parallel cross-lingual semantic role labeling corpus by applying high-quality machine translation to the English CoNLL-09 dataset and projecting annotations using multilingual BERT.
Annotation projection for X-SRL is denser and more precise than a strong baseline.
Multilingual training on X-SRL improves semantic role labeling performance for weaker languages.
A method provides annotations for most unambiguous words in a large corpus.
The Unambiguous Word Annotations dataset provides annotations for most unambiguous words in a large corpus.
Using the Unambiguous Word Annotations dataset extends the coverage of word sense embeddings for propagation-based models.
Using the Unambiguous Word Annotations dataset improves the quality of word sense embeddings for propagation-based models.
Using the Unambiguous Word Annotations dataset improves word sense disambiguation performance for propagation-based models.
Hierarchical methods based on Probabilistic Label Trees outperform Label-Wise Attention Networks for Large-scale Multi-label Text Classification.
Transformer-based approaches achieve better performance than existing methods for Large-scale Multi-label Text Classification on two datasets.
A method combining BERT with Label-Wise Attention Networks achieves state-of-the-art performance for Large-scale Multi-label Text Classification on two datasets.
A graph-aware annotation proximity measure assesses the proximity of annotations in Large-scale Multi-label Text Classification datasets.
No single question answering dataset is robust to generalizability to out-of-domain examples, responses to missing or incorrect data, and handling question variations simultaneously.
Transformers are capable of recognizing a subclass of counter languages including n-ary Boolean Expressions, Dyck-1, and its generalizations.

The learned mechanism of Transformers for recognizing a subclass of counter languages including n-ary Boolean Expressions, Dyck-1, and its generalizations correlates with a construction for that subclass.
Transformers recognize only a subset of regular languages, with performance degrading as language complexity increases.
Self-attention mechanism plays a role in modeling certain behaviors in Transformers for formal language recognition.
Positional encoding schemes influence the learning and generalization abilities of Transformers for formal language recognition.
A dialogue extraction algorithm transforms dialogue history into threads based on dependency relations.
Threads based on dependency relations in dialogue history are self-contained sub-dialogues.
Thread-Encoder encodes dialogue threads and response candidates into compact representations using pre-trained Transformers and computes matching scores with an attention layer.
Dependency relations are helpful for dialogue context understanding.
Unimer is a unified benchmark that integrates multiple semantic parsing datasets with complete logical forms and provides execution engines for multiple meaning representations.
Neural semantic parsing approaches exhibit different performance when trained to generate different meaning representations.
Program alias and grammar rules impact the performance of different meaning representations in neural semantic parsing.

Class-aware feature self-distillation (CFd) learns discriminative features for unsupervised domain adaptation across domains and languages without fine-tuning by self-distilling pre-trained language model features into a feature adaptation module and clustering features of the same class more tightly.
Intent class labels are represented by embeddings of their associated word graphs.
Text embeddings are mapped to the word graph embeddings of the classes for intent classification.
Out-of-scope detection in intent classification achieves state-of-the-art performance.
Overall intent classification accuracy is improved.
AESOP uses a retrieval-based selection module to choose target syntactic structures for paraphrase generation.
AESOP achieves state-of-the-art results for semantic preservation and syntactic conformation on benchmark datasets that use human-annotated exemplars for syntactic control.
Human evaluation shows that AESOP generates higher quality paraphrases than the current best model when using human-annotated target syntactic parses.
AESOP improves classification models' robustness to syntactic perturbation via data augmentation on GLUE tasks.
An event representation for event-centric implicit sentiment analysis combines event type and event triplet.
A model for event-centric implicit sentiment analysis employs hierarchical tensor-based composition.
A dataset for event-centric implicit sentiment analysis contains sentences annotated with event type and event triplet.
Gaussian-masked Directional Transformer is a Transformer variant for Chinese word segmentation.
An attention-only Chinese word segmentation model requires only unigram features for scoring.
An attention-only Chinese word segmentation model achieves state-of-the-art or comparable performance on the SIGHAN Bakeoff benchmark in the strict closed test setting.
An attention-only Chinese word segmentation model achieves the highest segmentation speed on the SIGHAN Bakeoff benchmark in the strict closed test setting.
An extensional definition of grammatical gender systems reduces the problem of comparing gender systems across languages to cluster evaluation.
Novel information theoretic metrics measure similarity between grammatical gender systems.
Grammatical gender system similarity is measured across 20 languages.
Gender system similarities are used to induce a phylogenetic tree over extant Indo-European languages.
Languages on the same branch of the phylogenetic tree induced from gender system similarities are similar, while languages from separate branches are not more similar than chance.
A filtering method removes unacceptable utterance pairs from dialogue corpora based on connectivity and content relatedness.
A filtering method based on connectivity and content relatedness correlates well with human judgment of dialogue quality.
Training neural dialogue agents on dialogue corpora filtered based on connectivity and content relatedness improves response generation quality.
A definition of when a visual question requires dialog history.
A subset of the Guesswhat?! dataset where dialog history completely changes the responses.
The Region under Discussion visually grounds dialog history.

The Region under Discussion constrains the image's spatial features according to a semantic representation of the dialog history inspired by the information structure notion of Question under Discussion.
Plug and play autoencoders for conditional text generation require only learning a mapping within the autoencoder's embedding space (Emb2Emb).
Plug and play autoencoders for conditional text generation use a loss term to keep the mapped embedding on the manifold of the autoencoder.
Plug and play autoencoders for conditional text generation learn offset vectors to navigate the manifold.
Plug and play autoencoders for conditional text generation perform at least as well as strong baselines on style transfer tasks with and without sequence-to-sequence supervision.
SubjQA is an English question answering dataset based on customer reviews with subjectivity annotations for questions and answer spans across six domains.
Subjectivity is an important feature for question answering.
The interactions between subjectivity and question answering performance are more intricate than the interactions between subjectivity and sentiment analysis.
A subjective question may or may not be associated with a subjective answer in question answering.
Implicit structured representations of entity names can be learned without context and external knowledge by combining active learning and weak supervision.

High-quality models of implicit structured representations of entity names can be learned from very few labeled examples without context and external knowledge by combining active learning and weak supervision.
Bidirectional language encoders can be used in incremental mode while retaining most of their non-incremental quality.
The "omni-directional" BERT model is more impacted by incremental access than other bidirectional language encoders.
Truncated training alleviates the impact of incremental access on bidirectional language encoders.
Delaying output until some right context is available alleviates the impact of incremental access on bidirectional language encoders.
Incorporating hypothetical right contexts generated by a language model alleviates the impact of incremental access on bidirectional language encoders.

A testbed for psychometric natural language processing in the health domain contains survey-based psychometric measures, user-generated text, and demographic information for trust, anxiety, numeracy, and literacy dimensions, enabling bias measurement and fairness benchmarking.
A multi-step process aligns user text with survey-based response items for psychometric natural language processing.
Position bias causes BiDAF and BERT to fail in extractive question answering when answers are located in positions not used for answers during training.
Position bias propagates through each layer of BERT in extractive question answering.
Using the prior distribution of answer positions as a bias model reduces position bias in extractive question answering.
A two-stage procedure combines fine-tuning on a small set of demonstrations with reinforcement learning for environment interaction.
A two-stage procedure improves success rate in the ALFWorld environment over existing methods.
Category Name Embedding network (CNE-net) identifies sentiment polarity for new aspect categories incrementally.
Category Name Embedding network (CNE-net) uses a shared encoder and decoder across all categories to reduce catastrophic forgetting.
Category Name Embedding network (CNE-net) uses category name as an input feature for task discrimination.
A dataset for aspect-category sentiment analysis incremental learning is introduced.
Category Name Embedding network (CNE-net) achieves the best performance on the aspect-category sentiment analysis incremental learning dataset.
Self-learning with uncertainty estimation utilizes unlabeled target language data to select high-quality silver labels for cross-lingual transfer.
Language Heteroscedastic Uncertainty, Language Homoscedastic Uncertainty, and Evidential Uncertainty are adapted and analyzed for cross-lingual transfer.
Self-learning with uncertainty estimation outperforms baselines on cross-lingual Named Entity Recognition and Natural Language Inference tasks across 40 languages.
A hierarchical CNN-transformer model with explicit attention is an interpretable multi-task clinical language model for sepsis and mortality prediction.
Projection-weighted canonical correlation analysis reveals relationships between learned features from structured and unstructured variables in clinical prediction.
A protocol evaluates model usability in clinical decision support contexts.
A hierarchical CNN-transformer model with explicit attention generates informative rationales.

A deep latent variable model for semantic sentence embedding performs source separation on parallel sentences to isolate shared semantic content in a latent semantic vector and explain divergent properties with language-specific latent vectors.
A variational probabilistic framework introduces priors that encourage source separation for semantic sentence embedding.
A variational probabilistic framework enables predicting sentence embeddings for monolingual data at test time using the posterior.
High-capacity transformers are used as data generating distributions and inference networks for semantic sentence embedding.
Source separation for semantic sentence embedding yields larger gains on difficult subsets of semantic similarity evaluations where word overlap is not a good indicator of similarity.
Zero-shot Knowledge Distillation for NLP enables the student to learn from the teacher without any task-specific data.
Zero-shot Knowledge Distillation for NLP combines out-of-domain data and adversarial training to learn the teacher's output distribution.

Zero-shot Knowledge Distillation for NLP achieves high classification performance relative to the teacher model while enabling substantial model compression on multiple natural language processing tasks.
Long-range context beyond 2K tokens improves predictions only for tokens that can be copied from distant context in long-range Transformer language models.
Long-range context beyond 2K tokens does not help for sentence-level prediction tasks in long-range Transformer language models.
PG-19 contains a variety of document types and domains.
Long-range context beyond 2K tokens helps most for literary novels compared to textbooks or magazines in long-range Transformer language models.

A scalable, automatic, and domain-agnostic framework curates new supervision data from live production traffic by leveraging implicit user feedback to improve Natural Language Understanding in large-scale conversational AI systems.
A new public dataset supports predicting documents that customer care agents use to facilitate users' needs.
A hybrid IR+DL approach provides the advantages of information retrieval and deep learning models for predicting documents that customer care agents use to facilitate users' needs.
The schema linking corpus for the Spider text-to-SQL dataset allows systematic study of the role of schema linking.
Schema-Linking SQL (SLSQL) converts natural language questions to SQL queries using a simple BERT-based architecture.
Corpus noise causes many errors in text-to-SQL systems evaluated on the Spider benchmark.
Schema linking is the critical factor for text-to-SQL performance on the Spider benchmark.
CodeT5 leverages code semantics from developer-assigned identifiers.
CodeT5 employs a unified framework that supports both code understanding and generation tasks and enables multi-task learning.
An identifier-aware pre-training task distinguishes code tokens that are identifiers and recovers masked identifiers.
A bimodal dual generation task aligns natural language and programming language using user-written code comments.

CodeT5 outperforms prior methods on code defect detection, clone detection, and generation tasks in programming language to natural language, natural language to programming language, and programming language to programming language directions.
Self-supervised refinement of language models uses linguistic perturbations of similar concept relationships.
Self-supervised refinement of language models enables zero-shot commonsense reasoning on multiple benchmarks.
TED-CDB is a unique large-scale Chinese discourse relation corpus of spoken language.
TED-CDB is annotated using an adaptation of Penn Discourse Treebank principles to handle features not present in English.
State-of-the-art discourse relation classifiers perform substantially worse on TED-CDB than on the Chinese Discourse Treebank.
TED-CDB improves discourse relation classification via same-language cross-domain transfer.
TED-CDB improves discourse relation classification via same-domain cross-language transfer for languages other than Chinese.
PathQG generates questions given a query path in a knowledge graph constructed from input text.
PathQG divides the question generation task into the query representation learning module and the query-based question generation module.
The query representation learning module identifies involved facts to form a query using sequence labeling.
PathQG enforces interaction between the query representation learning module and the query-based question generation module using a variational framework.
PathQG shows greater performance improvement over state-of-the-art approaches for complex questions.
Fully unsupervised named entity recognition can be performed using only pre-trained word embeddings.
Gaussian Hidden Markov Model and Deep Autoencoding Gaussian Mixture Model are applied to word embeddings for entity span detection and type prediction in fully unsupervised named entity recognition.
An instance selector based on reinforcement learning distinguishes positive sentences from noisy sentences in fully unsupervised named entity recognition.
Coarse-grained annotations are refined through neural networks in fully unsupervised named entity recognition.
Fully unsupervised named entity recognition using only word embeddings achieves competitive performance without annotated lexicon or corpus.
An edit distance adaptation measures dialog similarity.
An edit distance adaptation for dialog similarity accounts for utterance semantics, conversation flow, and participants.
An edit distance adaptation for dialog similarity outperforms existing document similarity measures in capturing dialog flow.
An edit distance adaptation for dialog similarity is better aligned with human perception of conversation similarity.
Lexical and visual features at the micro-level enable effective strategy induction for open-domain web keyphrase extraction.
Meta-level features at the macro-level aid in strategy selection for open-domain web keyphrase extraction.
Combining effective strategy induction and strategy selection for open-domain web keyphrase extraction outperforms state-of-the-art models.
A qualitative post-hoc analysis illustrates how lexical, visual, and meta-level features function in open-domain web keyphrase extraction.
Five reasoning modules explicitly model each step of the reasoning process and are learned in an end-to-end manner for reasoning over paragraph effects in situation.
A sequential approach that explicitly models each step of the reasoning process with neural network modules is effective and explainable for reasoning over paragraph effects in situation.
A general probing-based framework enables post-hoc interpretation of biases in language models by measuring bias extractability with an information-theoretic approach.
Pushing a language model towards a debiased regime increases the bias encoded in its inner representations.
SimCSE uses an unsupervised contrastive learning approach that predicts an input sentence from itself using dropout as noise.
Dropout acts as minimal data augmentation in unsupervised contrastive learning for sentence embeddings and its removal leads to representation collapse.
SimCSE uses a supervised contrastive learning approach that treats entailment pairs as positives and contradiction pairs as hard negatives from natural language inference datasets.
Contrastive learning for sentence embeddings regularizes the anisotropic space of pre-trained embeddings to be more uniform and better aligns positive pairs with supervised signals.

Using a textual entailment model to identify paraphrase candidates and a pre-trained contextualized language model to re-rank them yields the strongest performance for interpreting compound nominalizations with nominal and adjectival modifiers and prepositional phrases among unsupervised methods.
Monolingual sentence embedding models are extended to new languages by training a new system to mimic the original model on translated sentences.
Extending monolingual sentence embedding models to new languages requires fewer training samples than other methods.
Desired properties for sentence embedding vector spaces are easier to ensure when extending to new languages than with other methods.
Hardware requirements for training multilingual sentence embedding models are lower than with other methods.
Cryptonite is a large-scale dataset based on cryptic crosswords that is linguistically complex and naturally sourced.
Cryptonite examples are cryptic clues requiring disambiguation of semantic, syntactic, and phonetic wordplays and world knowledge.
T5-Large fine-tuned on cryptic clues and a rule-based clue solver achieve low accuracy on Cryptonite.
MLEC-QA is the largest Chinese multi-choice biomedical question answering dataset collected from the National Medical Licensing Examination in China.
MLEC-QA includes biomedical multi-choice questions with extra materials (images or tables) annotated by human experts.

MLEC-QA is the first Chinese multi-choice biomedical question answering dataset to cover the sub-fields of Clinic, Stomatology, Public Health, Traditional Chinese Medicine, and Traditional Chinese Medicine Combined with Western Medicine.
The current best model performs poorly on the MLEC-QA dataset, especially on questions that require sophisticated reasoning.
A regression-based global explainability technique disentangles BERT-based evaluation metric scores along linguistic factors including semantics, syntax, morphology, and lexical overlap.

BERT-based evaluation metrics (including BERTScore, MoverScore, BLEURT) capture semantics, syntax, morphology, and lexical overlap to some degree but are substantially sensitive to lexical overlap, similar to BLEU and ROUGE.
ReGen performs bidirectional generation of text and graph using Reinforcement Learning.
ReGen uses graph linearization to reframe text-to-graph and graph-to-text generation as sequence-to-sequence problems.
ReGen employs Self-Critical Sequence Training by using the model as its own critic for sequence training.
Reinforcement Learning via Self-Critical Sequence Training improves graph and text generation on WebNLG+ 2020 and TekGen datasets.
ReGen achieves state-of-the-art results for text-to-graph and graph-to-text generation on WebNLG+ 2020.
Multi-hop Selective Generator (MSG) incorporates multi-hop reasoning for question-driven summarization.
Multi-hop Selective Generator (MSG) provides justifications for generated summaries.

A human-like multi-hop inference module jointly models relevance to the question and interrelation among sentences to capture important sentences for justifying the summarized answer in question-driven summarization.
Multi-hop Selective Generator (MSG) outperforms state-of-the-art methods on non-factoid question answering.
APIRecX recommends APIs for a new software library by migrating knowledge from existing software libraries.
APIRecX handles out-of-vocabulary API calls by splitting them with Byte Pair Encoding.
APIRecX pre-trains a GPT-based language model on API sequences and fine-tunes it for API recommendation.
Abstractive summarization of Hindi-English code-switched conversations is a defined task.
GupShup is a code-switched conversation summarization dataset for Hindi-English conversations with human-annotated summaries in English and Hindi-English.
The GupShup dataset is characterized by code-switching statistics.
Multi-lingual mBART and multi-view seq2seq models achieve the best performance for abstractive summarization of Hindi-English code-switched conversations.
Qualitative analysis of abstractive summarization models for Hindi-English code-switched conversations reveals insights and shortcomings.

A two-step approach maps probability word problems to formal representations in a declarative language using a sequence-to-sequence model and executes the representations using a probabilistic programming system to provide answers.

A sequence-to-sequence model for mapping probability word problems to formal representations incorporates general-domain contextualized word representations finetuned using transfer learning on an in-domain dataset.
The two-step approach is important for obtaining correct solutions to probability word problems.
ONION is a textual backdoor defense based on outlier word detection.
ONION is the first method that can handle all textual backdoor attack situations.
ONION defends BiLSTM and BERT against five different textual backdoor attacks.
A query efficient attack strategy generates plausible adversarial examples for text classification and entailment tasks.
A query efficient attack strategy jointly leverages attention mechanism and locality sensitive hashing to reduce query count.
A query efficient attack strategy reduces query count compared to prior attacks across all datasets and target models.
A query efficient attack strategy achieves a higher success rate than prior attacks in a limited query setting.
Task-agnostic pretraining is sufficient for most summarization cases, reducing the need for costly task-specific pretraining.
A T5 model with 11 billion parameters and an optimal beam search length penalty achieves state-of-the-art results on two summarization tasks.
RocketQAv2 jointly trains dense passage retrieval and passage re-ranking.
Dynamic listwise distillation unifies listwise training for dense passage retrievers and re-rankers.
Dynamic listwise distillation adaptively improves dense passage retrievers and re-rankers using each other's relevance information.
Hybrid data augmentation constructs diverse training instances for listwise training of dense passage retrievers and re-rankers.
A distantly-supervised question generation method uses heuristically generated questions from summaries as training data.
Declarative summary sentences are transformed into questions using heuristics informed by dependency parsing, named entity recognition, and semantic role labeling.
Questions generated from summaries are combined with news articles to train an end-to-end neural question generation model.
A question answering model trained on synthetic question-answer pairs generated from Wikipedia passages outperforms previous unsupervised models on multiple in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.
Collecting hyperlinks in online news that point to the same article produces high-quality cross-document event coreference data.
HyperCoref is a silver-standard corpus of event mentions for cross-document event coreference resolution.
Models trained on small subsets of HyperCoref achieve performance comparable to models trained on gold-standard data for cross-document event coreference resolution.
Corpus-based open-domain event type induction selects salient predicates and object heads.
Corpus-based open-domain event type induction disambiguates predicate senses using only a verb sense dictionary.
Corpus-based open-domain event type induction obtains event types by jointly embedding and clustering <predicate sense, object head> pairs in a latent spherical space.
Corpus-based open-domain event type induction discovers salient and high-quality event types.
Monitoring geometrical properties of word embeddings detects slowly emerging new topics.
A method evaluates the compatibility between text representations and tasks by adapting computational learning theory tools for text datasets.
Pre-trained MLM representations distinguish between real and randomized labelings for a hard natural language inference task, whereas bag-of-words representations do not.

A calibrated, quantitative measure of the difficulty of a classification-based NLP task enables comparisons between representations without requiring empirical evaluations sensitive to initializations and hyperparameters.
CLIPScore evaluates image captioning without reference captions.
CLIPScore correlates more strongly with human judgements than reference-based metrics such as CIDEr and SPICE.
CLIPScore complements reference-based metrics that emphasize text-text similarities by focusing on image-text compatibility.
RefCLIPScore achieves higher correlation with human judgements than CLIPScore.
CLIPScore has strong correlation with human judgements for clip-art images and alt-text rating but weaker correlation for news captions requiring contextual knowledge.
Document-level relation extraction is decomposed into relation detection and argument resolution.
Modular self-supervision for document-level relation extraction sub-problems is less noise-prone and can be refined end-to-end via variational EM.
Modular self-supervision achieves greater improvement for relation instances whose arguments never co-occur in a paragraph.
Pretraining a masked language model in 24 hours is achievable with a single low-end deep learning server.
Pretraining masked language models at a fraction of the pretraining cost of BERT-base yields models competitive with BERT-base on GLUE tasks.
Three datasets of English tweets covering different topics contain five crowd-sourced judgments per instance for offensive language detection.
The level of annotators' agreement in data selection affects classifier performance and robustness in offensive language detection.
Hard cases with low annotator agreement in offensive language detection are not necessarily due to poor-quality annotation.

A sentence ordering framework that uses iteratively predicted pairwise orderings includes a graph-based classifier for predicting pairwise orderings between linked sentences in an initial sentence-entity graph.

In a sentence ordering framework that uses iteratively predicted pairwise orderings, an iterative process updates the sentence-entity graph with high-confidence pairwise orderings and uses a second classifier to predict the remaining uncertain pairwise orderings.
A sentence ordering framework that uses iteratively predicted pairwise orderings is effective and general across multiple sentence ordering datasets.
When combined with BERT and FHDecoder, a sentence ordering framework that uses iteratively predicted pairwise orderings achieves state-of-the-art performance for sentence ordering.
HiCapsRKL extracts two empirically designed text fragments from medical literature and encodes them into fragment representations.
HiCapsRKL uses capsule routing to process fragment representations into caps-representations.
HiCapsRKL integrates caps-representations as information gain to associate knowledge with medical literature hierarchically.
HiCapsRKL associates knowledge with medical literature more accurately than mainstream methods.
The dataset is the first to comprise stereotypical attributes of a range of social groups.
A method elicits stereotypes encoded by pretrained language models in an unsupervised fashion.
A method links emergent stereotypes from pretrained language models to basic emotions to enable generalized study of emotional effects.
Attitudes toward different social groups vary across pretrained language models.
Emotions and stereotypes shift quickly during fine-tuning of pretrained language models.
SLATE learns interpretable action policy rules from symbolic abstractions of textual observations.
SLATE uses an end-to-end differentiable method for symbolic rule learning.
In the coin collector environment, SLATE outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in text-based reinforcement learning with fewer training games.
SLATE provides human-understandable policy rules that are verifiable for logical consistency and easily debugged.
eWASH extracts syntactic hierarchies from concrete syntax trees and selectively removes less relevant scopes for a given task to incorporate entire file-level context.
eWASH achieves state-of-the-art performance for code completion and summarization in Python on the CodeXGLUE benchmark.
New CodeXGLUE benchmarks evaluate code completion with normalized literals and method body completion/code summarization conditioned on file-level context.
MRF-Chat is a probabilistic method that uses Markov Random Fields to augment existing deep-learning methods for next utterance prediction.
MRF-Chat improves next utterance prediction in retrieval-based open-domain conversational agents.
A novel model for entity-guided text generation produces a target sequence from a given list of entities.
A multi-step decoder injects entity types into the process of generating entity mentions.
Injecting entity types outperforms type embedding concatenation in entity-guided text generation.
Speechformer reduces memory usage in attention layers for speech translation.
Speechformer avoids initial lossy compression of audio features in direct speech translation.
Speechformer aggregates information only at a higher level using more informed linguistic criteria for speech translation.
VeeAlign uses a dual-attention mechanism to compute contextualized representations of concepts for ontology alignment.
VeeAlign exploits both syntactic and semantic information encoded in ontologies.
VeeAlign is flexible and scalable to different domains with minimal effort.
Contextualized Query Embeddings integrate conversational query reformulation directly into dense retrieval for conversational search.
A conversational search dataset contains pseudo-relevance labels.
Contextualized Query Embeddings rewrite conversational queries as dense representations for conversational search and open-domain question answering.
Contextualized Query Embeddings adjust the L2 norm of query token embeddings.
Contextualized Query Embeddings enable hybrid retrieval and error analysis by adjusting the L2 norm of query token embeddings.
The average of contextualized representations from neural language models shares almost the same direction as the first principal component.
The average of contextualized representations from neural language models immediately after random initialization shares almost the same direction as the first principal component.
Contextualized representations from neural language models empirically follow a normal distribution for each dimension.

If contextualized representations from neural language models follow a normal distribution for each dimension, then the average of these representations shares almost the same direction as the first principal component.
A self-supervised pre-training approach detects contextual synonyms of concepts by training on data created by shallow matching.
Data augmentation techniques address class sparsity in multi-class concept extraction.
A new state-of-the-art is set for unsupervised phenotype concept annotation on clinical text in F1 and Recall.
A self-supervised pre-training approach outperforms BioBERT and ClinicalBERT for phenotype concept annotation when fine-tuned with a small amount of labeled data.
Phenotype annotations from a self-supervised pre-training approach improve extrinsic evaluation on ICU benchmarks when used as features.
An unsupervised method for toxicity removal uses a paraphraser guided by small style-conditional language models to preserve content and remove toxicity.
An unsupervised method for toxicity removal uses BERT to replace toxic words with non-offensive synonyms through variable-length mask token replacement.
The first large-scale comparative study evaluates style transfer models for toxicity removal.
META-LMTC is a meta-learning approach for large-scale multi-label text classification with long-tailed label distributions.
META-LMTC constructs more faithful and more diverse tasks using well-designed sampling strategies.
META-LMTC directly incorporates the objective of adapting to new low-resource tasks into the meta-learning phase.
META-LMTC enhances the performance of strong BERT-like models for large-scale multi-label text classification.
A semantic fusion module learns entangled semantics among difficult minority samples and majority samples using adaptive multi-head attention.
A mutual information loss forces learning new representations of entangled semantics in the non-overlapping region of the minority class.

A coupled adversarial encoder-decoder fine-tunes disentangled semantic representations to maintain correlations with the minority class and generates anchor instances to re-embed difficult minority samples into non-overlapping representations.
Anchor instances improve classifier performance on imbalanced text classification tasks.
Language-Aligned Waypoint (LAW) supervision aligns agent actions with natural language instructions for Vision-and-Language Navigation in continuous environments.
A Vision-and-Language Navigation metric measures the number of sub-instructions completed by the agent during navigation.
ConvFiT is a two-stage procedure that converts pretrained language models into universal conversational encoders and then into task-specialised sentence encoders.
Full conversational pretraining is not required to transform pretrained language models into effective conversational encoders.
Pretrained language models can be fine-tuned into task-specialised sentence encoders optimised for the fine-grained semantics of a particular task.
Intent detection can be treated as a semantic similarity task using interpretable nearest neighbours retrieval.
ConvFiT achieves state-of-the-art performance on intent detection tasks, particularly in few-shot setups.
The recurrent Linear Transformer model achieves better incremental performance than the standard Transformer and restart-incremental Linear Transformer for English.
The recurrent Linear Transformer model achieves faster inference speed than the standard Transformer and restart-incremental Linear Transformer for English.
The recurrent Linear Transformer model has reduced non-incremental quality compared to the standard Transformer for English.
Training the recurrent Linear Transformer model to wait for right context improves non-incremental quality for English.
Training the recurrent Linear Transformer model with input prefixes improves the delivery of correct partial outputs for English.
The Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus contains text from patents and US military websites.
The Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus contains machine-generated text from machine translation systems.
The Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus contains evaluation examples from other benchmark NLP datasets.
Blocklist filtering disproportionately removes text from and about minority individuals during the creation of the Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus.
SYSML performs authorship attribution in darknet forums from natural language and model interactions using stylometry and multitask learning.
SYSML uses graph embeddings to construct low-dimensional representations of short user activity episodes in darknet forums.
SYSML outperforms the state-of-the-art in authorship attribution on darknet forums.
Confident Adaptive Transformers increase computational efficiency of Transformer inference while guaranteeing a specifiable degree of consistency with the original model with high confidence.
Confident Adaptive Transformers train additional prediction heads on intermediate layers of Transformers.
Confident Adaptive Transformers dynamically decide when to stop computational effort for each input using a meta consistency classifier.
Confident Adaptive Transformers use a unique extension of conformal prediction to calibrate the early prediction stopping rule.
Topic regularization leverages global topics to resolve exophoric pronouns in dialogues.
An automatic method identifies fine-grained lexical distinctions.
An automatic method extracts rules explaining fine-grained lexical distinctions in a human- and machine-readable format.
Rules explaining fine-grained lexical distinctions are of high quality for teaching non-native speakers when to translate ambiguous words in Spanish and Greek.
A simple first order graph-based model performs morphosyntactic parsing for Sanskrit.
A simple first order graph-based model uses a multigraph input generation method to enable exact search inference for morphosyntactic parsing in Sanskrit.
A simple first order graph-based model employs linguistically motivated search space pruning based on traditional Sanskrit grammatical analysis for morphosyntactic parsing in Sanskrit.
A simple first order graph-based model achieves state of the art results for morphological parsing in Sanskrit.
A simple first order graph-based model achieves state of the art results for dependency parsing in Sanskrit.
SIMMC 2.0 is a dataset of task-oriented user-assistant dialogs in the shopping domain, grounded in immersive and photo-realistic scenes.
A multimodal dialog simulator generates simulated dialog flows emphasizing diversity and richness of interactions.
Manual paraphrasing adapts generated utterances to natural language distribution.
An in-depth analysis characterizes the SIMMC 2.0 dataset.
Four benchmark tasks are defined for the SIMMC 2.0 dataset.
FastIF uses k-Nearest Neighbors to narrow the search space for influence function computation to a subset of candidate data points.
FastIF identifies configurations that balance the speed-quality trade-off for inverse Hessian-vector product estimation.
FastIF introduces a fast parallel variant for influence function computation.
FastIF achieves significant speedup while maintaining high correlation with the original influence values.
Fine-tuning on specific influential data-points corrects errors in natural language inference.
Fine-tuning on unseen data-points corrects errors in natural language inference.
A training scheme learns a shared latent representation of emotion independent from different label formats, natural languages, and disparate model architectures.
A shared latent representation of emotion yields interoperability across label formats, natural languages, and model architectures without penalizing prediction quality.
Ernie-M aligns the representation of multiple languages with monolingual corpora.
Ernie-M uses back-translation during pre-training to generate pseudo-parallel sentence pairs on monolingual corpora.
Ernie-M achieves state-of-the-art results on various cross-lingual downstream tasks.
Unified encoding provides a unified representation for all structures in a transition system.
Unified encoding represents items on structures by two views: structure-invariant view and structure-dependent view.
Unified encoding encodes transition states with constant additional complexity with respect to basic feature extractors.
Unified encoding improves test speed.
ADAPET modifies Pattern Exploiting Training's objective to provide denser supervision during fine-tuning.
ADAPET outperforms Pattern Exploiting Training on SuperGLUE without task-specific unlabeled data.
GeneSis is the first generative approach to lexical substitution.
GeneSis generates substitutes for a word in context using a seq2seq model.
GeneSis attains state-of-the-art results on lexical substitution benchmarks.
GeneSis produces silver data for improving lexical substitution systems.
Human evaluation assesses the quality of GeneSis substitutes.
CAPE applies calibrated noise through differential privacy to preserve semantic links while obscuring sensitive information in text representations.
CAPE employs an adversarial training regime to obscure identified private variables.
CAPE reduces private information leakage more effectively than methods that use only differential privacy or only adversarial learning.
A phone is produced faster on average in languages where it has lower surprisal.
Within a language, more surprising phones are longer on average.
A large-scale silver dataset of dialogues annotated with fine-grained emotions, empathetic response intents, and a Neutral category.
A data curation pipeline that starts with a small seed of manually annotated dialogues and scales to a large-scale dataset.
A multilingual dataset consists of buggy code, fixed code, and commit message triples.
A cascaded method repairs code first and then generates commit messages using both the original buggy code and the fixed code.
A joint model repairs code and generates commit messages simultaneously in a unified framework.
The joint task of code repair and commit message generation is challenging.
The enhanced cascaded model and the joint model outperform baseline methods in code repair quality and commit message quality.
Comparing multiple versions of the same model during development leads to overestimation on the development data.
Comparing an increasing number of models on the test data leads to faster overfitting and expiration of test sets.
A tune-set allows model picking during neural network development so that comparing different versions of a new model can safely be done on the development data.
Shifted absolute position embedding (SHAPE) achieves shift invariance by randomly shifting absolute positions during training.
Shifted absolute position embedding (SHAPE) is empirically comparable to a prior position representation method while being simpler and faster.
Label-aware Contrastive Loss assigns higher weights to closely confusable negative examples than to less similar negative examples in the contrastive objective.
Label-aware Contrastive Loss outperforms previous contrastive methods when the number of classes is large and/or the classes are more confusable.
Label-aware Contrastive Loss results in more differentiated output distributions.
The multi-source fusion model (MSF) uses context consistency, type hierarchy, and background knowledge of types including prototypes and descriptions for zero-shot fine-grained entity typing.
Context consistency, type hierarchy, and background knowledge of types are complementary in zero-shot fine-grained entity typing.
JoGANIC leverages the structure of news image captions to guide representation design.
JoGANIC outperforms state-of-the-art methods on named entity related metrics for news image captioning.
Sentence bottleneck autoencoders use a frozen pretrained transformer language model, a sentence bottleneck, and a single-layer modified transformer decoder.
Sentence bottleneck autoencoders adapt the masked language modeling objective as a generative, denoising objective.
Sentence bottleneck autoencoders produce higher-quality sentence representations than previous methods on text similarity, style transfer, and single-sentence classification tasks.
Sentence bottleneck autoencoders use fewer parameters than large pretrained models.
SHINE models short text datasets as hierarchical heterogeneous graphs with word-level component graphs that provide additional semantic and syntactic information.
SHINE dynamically learns a short document graph to enable label propagation among similar short texts.
SHINE better exploits interactions between nodes of the same types and captures similarities between short texts than existing GNN-based methods.
SHINE outperforms state-of-the-art methods for short text classification in low-label settings.
BiLSTM-based models effectively encode position information into word representations for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
Graph convolutional networks achieve only marginal gains for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
BiLSTM models with part-of-speech and position embeddings outperform state-of-the-art complex neural structures for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
When missing information is not present in the data, collecting more data is more effective than inferring missing information for improving coverage of resources.
Morality frames organizes moral attitudes directed at different entities.
A dataset of political tweets from US politicians includes morality frame annotations.
A relational learning model predicts moral attitudes towards entities and moral foundations jointly.
Moral sentiment towards entities differs highly across political ideologies.
Language models paraphrase natural language inputs into a controlled sublanguage resembling English that is automatically mappable to structured meaning representations.
Semantic parsing using a controlled sublanguage outperforms baseline methods on multiple community tasks with limited training data.
BioLAMA is a benchmark of biomedical factual knowledge triples for probing biomedical language models.

The high correlation between biomedical language model knowledge retrieval predictions and subject-omitted prompt templates prevents biomedical language models from functioning as domain-specific knowledge bases.

A neural architecture transforms named entity recognition data representation from high-resource domains to low-resource domains by learning text patterns that differentiate domains and aligning domains in a shared feature space.
Transforming named entity recognition data to the low-resource domain representation improves performance compared to using only high-resource domain data.
An experimental setup simulates the feedback attribution problem in multi-domain spoken language understanding using small-scale public datasets.
Attribution methods for counterfactual bandit learning in multi-domain spoken language understanding are inspired by multi-agent reinforcement learning.
Directly using overall feedback for counterfactual bandit learning in multi-domain spoken language understanding leads to disastrous performance.
Attribution methods for counterfactual bandit learning in multi-domain spoken language understanding enable training competitive models from user feedback.
Dual Momentum Contrast aligns cross-lingual sentence representations into a unified embedding space for computing semantic similarities (both cross-lingual and monolingual) with a simple dot product.
Dual Momentum Contrast adapts MoCo to improve the quality of cross-lingual sentence representation alignment.
Dual Momentum Contrast achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple cross-lingual tasks including similarity search, bitext mining, and semantic textual similarity.
A bias-constrained model debiases intersectional biases in natural language processing.
An extension of iterative nullspace projection handles multiple identities for debiasing in natural language processing.
There is a gap in robustness between poisoned samples and clean samples in backdoor attacks on NLP models.
Robustness-Aware Perturbations (RAP) is an online defense method that uses word-based perturbations to distinguish poisoned samples from clean samples against backdoor attacks on NLP models.
The feasibility of robustness-aware perturbation-based defense against backdoor attacks on NLP models is theoretically analyzed.
Robustness-Aware Perturbations (RAP) improves backdoor attack defense on sentiment analysis and toxic detection tasks compared to existing online defense methods.
Robustness-Aware Perturbations (RAP) reduces computational costs for backdoor attack defense on sentiment analysis and toxic detection tasks compared to existing online defense methods.
A decoding method based on Monte-Carlo Tree Search is competitive for machine translation.
A blueprint enables the fruitful use of Monte-Carlo Tree Search in language applications.
The best machine translation decoding algorithm depends on the characteristics of the goal metric.
MassiveSumm is the largest and most inclusive automatic summarisation dataset.
MassiveSumm is one of the largest and most inclusive datasets ever published for any NLP task.

Attentive graph convolutional networks (A-GCN) builds context graphs for relation extraction without dependency parsers in an unsupervised manner from n-grams extracted from a lexicon built using pointwise mutual information (PMI).
Attentive graph convolutional networks (A-GCN) uses attention to weight word pairs from contexts within and across n-grams for relation extraction.
Quantum NLP methods are categorized by the use of quantum theory, the linguistic targets modeled, and the downstream application.
Existing dialog models exhibit large performance gaps when processing dialog contexts containing figurative language.
Lightweight solutions translate figurative language to literal forms using external resources while preserving meaning to enable dialog models to handle figurative language.
ValNorm quantifies the valence dimension of affect in human-rated word sets from social psychology.
ValNorm accurately quantifies valence for non-discriminatory, non-social group word sets.
Gender stereotypes in word embeddings vary across languages.
Valence associations of non-discriminatory, non-social group words are consistent across seven languages and over 200 years.
ProtoSeq uses sequential prototypical networks for few-shot emotion recognition in conversations.
TUCORE-GCN is a graph convolutional network that uses contextualized representations of dialogue turns for dialogue-based relation extraction.
Emotion recognition in conversations can be formulated as a dialogue-based relation extraction task.
TUCORE-GCN achieves state-of-the-art performance for dialogue-based relation extraction and emotion recognition in conversations.
Neural Natural Logic Inference builds proof paths by gradually bridging hypotheses and candidate premises using natural logic inference steps.
Neural Natural Logic Inference embeds hypotheses and premises in Hyperbolic space to acquire more precise representations.
Neural Natural Logic Inference outperforms prior work on multiple-choice science question answering.
Neural Natural Logic Inference provides evidence to explain question answering predictions.
COUGH is a challenging dataset for COVID-19 FAQ retrieval.
COUGH includes a FAQ Bank containing approximately 16,000 FAQ items from 55 credible websites including the CDC and WHO.
COUGH includes a Query Bank containing 1,236 human-paraphrased queries.
COUGH includes a Relevance Set containing approximately 32 human-annotated relevant FAQ items for each query.
GOLD augments existing data to train out-of-scope detectors in low-data regimes.
GOLD generates pseudo-labeled candidates using samples from an auxiliary dataset for training out-of-scope detectors.
GOLD uses a novel filtering mechanism to keep only the most beneficial candidates for training out-of-scope detectors.
GOLD outperforms existing methods for out-of-scope detection on multiple benchmarks.
The unique properties of out-of-scope data determine key factors for optimally applying GOLD in out-of-scope detection.
Surface form competition occurs when different surface forms representing the same underlying concept in a given context compete for probability mass.
Domain Conditional Pointwise Mutual Information compensates for surface form competition by reweighing each option according to its a priori likelihood within the context of a specific task.
Domain Conditional Pointwise Mutual Information consistently improves zero-shot multiple choice performance over calibrated and uncalibrated scoring functions.
The extended margin-loss approach uses methods to enforce fairness in class imbalanced learning.
The extended margin-loss approach mitigates both class imbalance and demographic biases.
Many unknowns for a learned visual question answering model are implicitly present in the dataset.
SimpleAug turns implicit knowledge in the dataset into explicit training examples for visual question answering.
Augmented training examples improve learned visual question answering model performance on datasets with language prior shifts and on datasets without language prior shifts.
SimpleAug enables leveraging weakly-labeled or unlabeled images to enhance visual question answering models.
Greedy rationalization approximates the objective of finding the smallest subset of input tokens that predicts the same output as the full sequence efficiently for any model.
A short fine-tuning step enforces compatible conditional distributions in models when making predictions on incomplete context subsets.
Greedy rationalization optimizes the sequential rationale objective better than existing methods.
A new dataset contains human-annotated sequential rationales for language modeling and machine translation.
On a human-annotated dataset of sequential rationales, greedy rationales are more similar to human rationales than rationales from existing methods.

Jointly training one cross attention head with word alignment supervision stresses focus on the target language label in Transformer-based multilingual neural machine translation models for zero-shot translation.
Supervising one cross attention head to focus on word alignments and language labels reduces bias towards translating into the wrong language in zero-shot multilingual neural machine translation.
Word alignment supervision leads to more stable results across different training runs in zero-shot multilingual neural machine translation.
Document summarization has a sparse sentence structure that allows constraining encoder-decoder attention to a subset of input sentences without performance degradation.
An architecture for summarization systems constrains encoder-decoder attention by selecting a subset of input sentences.
A simultaneous machine translation model uses a Mixture-of-Experts Wait-k Policy where each head in multi-head attention is a wait-k expert with a specific waiting words number.
The weights of wait-k experts are adjusted based on test latency and source inputs to produce the best translation in simultaneous machine translation.
Simultaneous machine translation with the Mixture-of-Experts Wait-k Policy achieves higher translation quality than strong baselines under different latency levels.
ReadingBank is the first large-scale benchmark dataset containing reading order, text, and layout information for document images of various document types.
LayoutReader captures text and layout information for reading order prediction using a seq2seq model.
LayoutReader improves the ordering of text lines in the output of open-source and commercial OCR engines.

An end-to-end unsupervised framework for stance detection combines domain adaptation techniques (mixture of experts and domain-adversarial training) with label embeddings to enable out-of-domain prediction of unseen, user-defined labels.
An end-to-end unsupervised framework for stance detection outperforms strong baselines in both in-domain settings with seen targets and out-of-domain settings with unseen targets.
An exhaustive analysis of cross-domain stance detection results identifies important factors influencing model performance.
Knowledge representation strategies in contextual language models are organized by the level of knowledge base supervision, ranging from no supervision to entity- and relation-level supervision.
The Color Dataset (CoDa) provides human-perceived color distributions for 521 common objects.
Language models recover color distributions that correlate more strongly with text distributions than with ground-truth human perception.
Reporting bias is one of the causes of language models having color distributions that differ from human perception.
Multimodal models mitigate the effects of reporting bias on color perception.
Caption enriched samples include automatic captions that represent the image content in hateful memes.
Incorporating automatic captions during fine-tuning improves hateful memes detection performance for unimodal and multimodal models.
Continuing pre-training of language models on augmented and original caption pairs improves classification accuracy for unimodal hateful memes detection.
Adversarial Mixing Policy adds a small adversarial perturbation to the mixing coefficients instead of the examples.
Adversarial Mixing Policy injects slight non-linearity between synthetic examples and synthetic labels.
Adversarial Mixing Policy reduces the error rate compared to Mixup variants, especially in low-resource conditions.
Synthetic adversarial data generation enhances question answering model robustness against human adversaries.

A data generation pipeline for synthetic adversarial question answering data selects source passages, identifies candidate answers, generates questions, and filters or re-labels examples to improve quality.
Synthetic adversarial question answering data improves model performance on the AdversarialQA benchmark.
Synthetic adversarial question answering data improves model generalization on the majority of MRQA benchmarks.
Models trained with synthetic adversarial question answering data are more robust to human-written adversarial examples.
CR-Walker performs tree-structured reasoning on a knowledge graph.
CR-Walker generates informative dialog acts to guide language generation.
CR-Walker views the traversed entity at each hop as part of dialog acts to facilitate language generation, linking entity selection and expression.
CR-Walker achieves more accurate recommendations.
CR-Walker generates more informative and engaging responses.

Stackelberg Adversarial Regularization (SALT) formulates adversarial regularization as a Stackelberg game where the follower generates perturbations and the leader trains the model subject to the perturbations.
In Stackelberg Adversarial Regularization (SALT), the leader recognizes the follower's strategy and considers the anticipated outcomes of the follower when moving.
The leader's advantage in Stackelberg Adversarial Regularization (SALT) improves model fitting to unperturbed data.
Stackelberg Adversarial Regularization (SALT) captures the leader's strategic information using a Stackelberg gradient computed by an unrolling algorithm.
Stackelberg Adversarial Regularization (SALT) outperforms existing adversarial regularization baselines on machine translation and natural language understanding tasks.
A Chinese sentence compression dataset comprises colloquial sentences from a real-life telecommunication domain question answering system.
SOM-NCSCM incorporates a Self-Organizing Map for Chinese sentence compression.
SOM-NCSCM benefits from deep investigation of similarity among data.
Extract, Denoise and Enforce automatically extracts, denoises, and enforces important input concepts as lexical constraints for text-to-text generation.
Extract, Denoise and Enforce demonstrates higher coverage for concept preservation in text-to-text generation.
Selective graph pooling uses parts of the document hierarchy that are invariable across documents.
Selective graph pooling provides insights into clinical trial termination status prediction.
AVocaDo updates vocabulary as an optimizable parameter by expanding with domain-specific vocabulary based on a tokenization statistic.
AVocaDo preserves embeddings of added words from overfitting to downstream data by utilizing pretrained language model knowledge with a regularization term.
AVocaDo achieves consistent performance improvements in biomedical, computer science, news, and reviews domains.
State-of-the-art semi-supervised learning frameworks are not suitable for lightweight models including TextCNN and LSTM.
FLiText is a faster and lighter semi-supervised text classification framework for lightweight models.
FLiText applies a generalized regular constraint to lightweight models via an inspirer network for efficient semi-supervised learning.
FLiText achieves state-of-the-art performance for lightweight models on multiple semi-supervised text classification benchmarks.
COMET treats the knowledge base as a sequence.
COMET uses a Memory Mask to restrict each entity's focus to its relevant entities and dialogue history while excluding irrelevant entities.
Higher-order word co-occurrence statistics largely explain the success of masked language model pre-training.
Masked language models pre-trained on randomly shuffled word order achieve high accuracy on many downstream tasks, including tasks designed to be challenging for models that ignore word order.
Some parametric syntactic probes show high performance on masked language models pre-trained on randomly shuffled word order, indicating deficiencies in these probes.
Meta Distant Transfer Learning employs task representation learning to mine implicit relations among multiple tasks and classes.
Meta Distant Transfer Learning trains a pre-trained language model-based meta-learner to capture transferable knowledge across tasks.
Meta Distant Transfer Learning uses weighted maximum entropy regularizers to make the meta-learner task-agnostic and unbiased.
Meta Distant Transfer Learning is robust to low data availability for target tasks.
CHoRaL generates perceived humor labels on Facebook posts using naturally available user reactions without manual annotation.
CHoRaL provides binary labels and continuous scores for humor and non-humor.
CHoRaL produces a large-scale dataset of humor-labeled Facebook posts about the topic of COVID-19.
Humor detection models trained on humor labels generated by CHoRaL achieve performance comparable to humans.
An empirical analysis examines state-of-the-art span-based event reference systems.
Representation vectors for corresponding classes in Relation and Event Extraction tasks are aligned to achieve class-aware crosslingual representations.
Representation vectors for language-universal word categories (parts of speech and dependency relations) are aligned in crosslingual Relation and Event Extraction.
A filtering mechanism based on adversarial learning facilitates the learning of word category representations from contextualized representations on input texts.
HyperCLOVA is a Korean variant of GPT-3 with 82 billion parameters trained on a Korean-centric corpus of 560 billion tokens using Korean-specific tokenization.
HyperCLOVA achieves state-of-the-art in-context zero-shot and few-shot learning performance on Korean language tasks.
HyperCLOVA studio provides AI prototyping capabilities to non-experts of machine learning.
A revised masked language model evaluates the quality of Chinese word segmentation results.
Improved minimum risk training leverages evaluations of Chinese word segmentation results to train segmenters.
Segment, Mask, and Predict outperforms previous methods on multiple Chinese word segmentation datasets and achieves better robustness.
Summarize-then-Answer generates a question-focused, abstractive explanation for multi-hop reading comprehension.
Summarize-then-Answer uses a semi-supervised training procedure that starts from a supervised model and optimizes a conciseness-promoted reward function.

Summarize-then-Answer generates more compact explanations than an extractive explainer with limited supervision while maintaining that the explanations are minimally sufficient for answering the question.
Profanity-Avoiding Training Framework uses a short list of profanity examples to prevent seq2seq models from generating a broader spectrum of profanity.
Pattern-eliminating training component suppresses the impact of language patterns with profanity in the training set of seq2seq models.
Trigger-resisting training component provides certified robustness for seq2seq models against intentionally injected profanity-triggering expressions in test samples.
Profanity-Avoiding Training Framework prevents seq2seq models from generating profanity.
Local Word Discovery is a computational task that supports the construction of high quality texts and lexicons and is tuned to available knowledge and interests in an Indigenous community.
Local Word Discovery locates known lexemes in a noisy phone representation of speech and uses a morphological transducer to generate hypothetical morphologically-complex words for human validation.
A single iteration of Local Word Discovery increases transcription density.
Local Word Discovery provides at least one correct partial or full-word suggestion for a majority of breath groups.
A syntactically-informed unsupervised paraphrasing model based on conditional variational auto-encoder generates texts in a specified syntactic structure.
A two-stage learning method trains syntactically-informed unsupervised paraphrasing models using non-parallel data.

Fine-tuning with syntax controlling and cycle reconstruction learning objectives combined via Gumbel-Softmax improves syntactic controllability and semantic consistency in syntactically-informed unsupervised paraphrasing.
A syntactically-informed unsupervised paraphrasing model generates diverse paraphrases with specified syntactic structure.
A syntactically-informed unsupervised paraphrasing model generates syntactically adversarial examples for sentiment analysis.
Vector-valued interventions allow fine-grained control over multiple attributes simultaneously via a weighted linear combination of vectors.
Vector-valued interventions allow fine-tuning a model trained without annotations to support attribute control for some attributes.
Vector-valued interventions provide better control over a wider range of tasks than attribute tagging.
Vector-valued interventions maintain translation quality when no control is requested.
Vector-valued interventions enable control in an already trained model with relatively cheap fine-tuning.
Text AutoAugment defines an augmentation policy as a combination of various operations.
Text AutoAugment uses an efficient Bayesian Optimization algorithm to automatically search for the best policy.
Text AutoAugment improves text classification accuracy in low-resource and class-imbalanced regimes.
Domain-agnostic multi-source pretraining leverages external summary data for low-resource dialogue summarization.
Separate pretraining of the dialogue encoder and summary decoder uses large-scale in-domain non-summary data.
Adversarial critics facilitate domain-agnostic summarization during pretraining on out-of-domain summary data.
Domain-agnostic multi-source pretraining generalizes well across different dialogue scenarios with limited training data.
Dual-stream vision-and-language models do not preserve natural language understanding capability significantly better than single-stream models.
Vision-and-language pre-training causes a drop in natural language understanding performance on most tasks.
Limited vocabulary capacity causes under-representation of many languages in cross-lingual language models.
VoCap allocates vocabulary capacity for each language in cross-lingual language models.
k-NN-based target sampling accelerates softmax computation in cross-lingual language model pre-training.
k-NN-based target sampling achieves faster pre-training without sacrificing performance when vocabulary size is increased.
Natural language prompts enable aspect target sentiment classification in zero-shot settings.
Natural language prompts enhance aspect target sentiment classification in few-shot supervised settings.
Reformulating aspect target sentiment classification as a natural language inference task outperforms supervised state-of-the-art approaches in few-shot settings.
Natural language prompts enable aspect target sentiment classification for implicitly stated aspects when trained only on explicitly mentioned aspect terms with few examples.
Modifications to sinusoidal positional encodings that improve monolingual language models do not improve multilingual language models.
Higher variances in multilingual training distributions require higher compression, making compositionality indispensable.
Learned absolute positional encodings tend to approximate sinusoidal embeddings in multilingual settings.
More complex positional encoding architectures lack the inductive bias to effectively learn cross-lingual alignment.
A parallel corpus for 22 Turkic languages combines existing public datasets with newly collected datasets.
Bilingual baselines cover 26 language pairs in the Turkic language family.
Test sets for three different translation domains in Turkic languages are novel and high-quality.

An architectural method using residual adapters and a replay-based strategy achieve performance close to the multitask learning upper bound while requiring substantially less training time for learning new domains in task-oriented dialogue systems.
Continual learning in task-oriented dialogue systems involves trade-offs between parameter usage, memory size, and training time.
Discrete and soft prompting outperform finetuning in crosslingual transfer and in-language training for multilingual natural language inference.
Neural machine translation datasets exhibit length-bias towards short sentences, which contributes to beam-search degradation.
Multi-Sentence Resampling is a data augmentation technique that concatenates multiple sentences to create long training examples.
Multi-Sentence Resampling reduces beam-search degradation for larger beam sizes and improves translation quality.
A cross-lingual summarisation corpus contains long documents in a source language and multi-sentence summaries in a target language.
A cross-lingual summarisation corpus covers twelve directed language pairs for Czech, English, French, and German.
The methodology for creating cross-lingual summarisation corpora can be applied to several other languages.
Cross-lingual document-summary instances are derived from Wikipedia by combining lead paragraphs and articles’ bodies from language-aligned Wikipedia titles.
The cross-lingual summarisation task is analyzed with automatic metrics and validated with a human study.
Frame Semantic-Enhanced Sentence Modeling models internal semantic structure within a sentence by leveraging Frames and Frame Elements for extractive summarization.
Frame Semantic-Enhanced Sentence Modeling models relationships among sentences by leveraging Frame-to-Frame relations for extractive summarization.
MultiDoc2Dial is a task for modeling goal-oriented dialogues grounded in multiple documents.
MultiDoc2Dial is a dataset containing dialogues grounded in multiple documents from four different domains.
PAUSE learns high-quality sentence embeddings from partially labeled datasets in a generic and end-to-end manner.
PAUSE achieves state-of-the-art results on various benchmark tasks using only a small fraction of labeled sentence pairs.
In a real industrial use case with scarce labeled samples, PAUSE enables extending datasets without requiring extensive manual annotation.
Using semantic or syntactic representations as pivots for paraphrase generation involves transforming a sentence into such representations and decoding back.
Pivot-based paraphrase generation pipelines can be compressed into end-to-end frameworks using pretraining.
AMR as a pivot yields higher quality paraphrases than language as a pivot for paraphrase generation.
Compressing pivot-based paraphrase generation pipelines into end-to-end frameworks reduces semantic shift when language is the pivot.
Unsupervised pivot-based methods achieve paraphrase generation quality comparable to supervised sequence-to-sequence models.
CLIFF uses a contrastive learning formulation to train summarization systems to distinguish reference summaries from erroneous summaries.
CLIFF designs four strategies for creating negative samples that resemble errors made by BART and PEGASUS, as identified in human annotations of summary errors.
CLIFF is robust across datasets and models.
CLIFF produces more factual summaries than post error correction, entailment-based reranking, and unlikelihood training.
New human annotations of summary errors document common errors made by BART and PEGASUS.
A dictionary extension method extracts new entities using a type expanded model.
A multi-granularity boundary-aware network detects entity boundaries from both local and global perspectives.

A distantly supervised named entity recognition system using dictionary extension and a multi-granularity boundary-aware network outperforms state-of-the-art distantly supervised systems and supervised models on multiple datasets.
Three counterfactual sets for reading comprehension settings enable evaluation of attribution techniques.
Heuristics connect attribution methods' outputs to high-level model behavior for evaluating understanding of counterfactuals in reading comprehension.
Pairwise attributions are better suited to reading comprehension than token-level attributions across multiple reading comprehension settings.
The customer choice problem framework applies to domain adaptation under budget constraints.
Exclusive data annotation maximizes domain adaptation performance for small budgets.
Combining data annotation with in-domain pre-training maximizes domain adaptation performance for sufficiently large budgets.
CLASSIC performs domain incremental learning for aspect sentiment classification tasks.
CLASSIC uses a contrastive continual learning method to enable knowledge transfer across aspect sentiment classification tasks.
CLASSIC uses a contrastive continual learning method to enable knowledge distillation from old aspect sentiment classification tasks to new tasks.
ARA annotates correspondences between cooking steps across similar recipes.
A neural model predicts correspondences between cooking steps across similar recipes.
Using fine-grained structural information about recipes improves the accuracy of predicting correspondences between cooking steps across similar recipes.
Current word-level quality estimation models are not sufficiently accurate to support post-editing machine translation output.
Word-level quality estimation requires high accuracy to be useful for post-editing machine translation output.
A visualization reflecting the uncertainty of the quality estimation model is preferred for post-editing machine translation when the quality estimation is sufficiently accurate.
Speed gains in post-editing machine translation from quality estimation are not due to blind trust and are accompanied by improved final translation quality.

HM-SGE learns visually grounded word meaning representations using a graph hierarchy with a lower level for modality-specific representations conditioned to another modality via dedicated but communicating graphs and a higher level for joint multimodal representation learning.
HM-SGE jointly estimates the topology of each graph (which models word similarity relations) and the graph embeddings.
HM-SGE simulates human similarity judgments and concept categorization.

Computational methods test attitude changes towards sexuality and gender in the United States by analyzing word choice in sociolinguistic lexical variables (alternate words expressing the same concept).
Acceptance of gay marriage and gender equality is increasing in the United States.
The increase in acceptance of gay marriage and gender equality is driven by specific demographics within the United States.
Passages of Marriage Equality Acts in different states drive linguistic change in sociolinguistic variables related to sexuality and gender.
Structural supervision helps language models represent syntactic state across intervening content in Mandarin Chinese.
Structural supervision improves language model performance on Mandarin Chinese grammar tasks in low-data settings.
Competency problems are complex language understanding tasks where simple feature correlations with labels are spurious.
Realistic datasets increasingly deviate from competency problems as dataset size increases due to human bias.
A simple statistical test identifies dataset artifacts.
Models are inappropriately affected by subtle dataset biases.
Self-supervised quality estimation evaluates machine translation quality at both the sentence and word levels by recovering masked target words.
Self-supervised quality estimation outperforms previous unsupervised methods on multiple quality estimation tasks across language pairs and domains.
A small number of rogue dimensions dominate standard representational similarity measures in contextualized language models.
There is a mismatch between the dimensions that dominate standard representational similarity measures and the dimensions important to model behavior in contextualized language models.
Standardization corrects for rogue dimensions and reveals underlying representational quality in contextualized language models.
