Leading automatic metrics for formality style transfer are evaluated across Brazilian-Portuguese, French, and Italian.
Best practices for automatic evaluation in formality style transfer are outlined.
Several automatic metrics for formality style transfer correlate well with human judgments and are robust across languages.
RoBERTa is sensitive to source code edits that preserve program semantics.
ContraCode pre-trains a neural network to identify functionally similar variants of a program among non-equivalent distractors.
Data augmentation uses an automated source-to-source compiler to generate functionally similar code variants.
ContraCode outperforms RoBERTa on an adversarial code clone detection benchmark.
ContraCode improves code summarization accuracy over competitive baselines.
ContraCode improves TypeScript type inference accuracy over competitive baselines.
SIR uses a multilingual query expansion mechanism based on Word Sense Disambiguation that provides sense definitions as additional semantic information for queries.
SIR achieves better neural ranking performance in French, German, Italian, and Spanish than supervised and unsupervised alternatives when trained only on English data.
Context-Oriented Embedding (COE) achieves state-of-the-art performance on all-words word sense disambiguation benchmarks in the knowledge-based category.
GMH handles mixed long and short distance multi-hop reasoning for knowledge graph completion.
GMH uses a local-global knowledge module to estimate possible paths.
GMH uses a differentiated action dropout module to explore a diverse set of paths.
GMH uses an adaptive stopping search module to avoid over searching.
GMH achieves superior performance in both short and long distance reasoning scenarios.
A few-shot intent detection method uses contrastive pre-training and fine-tuning.
Self-supervised contrastive pre-training discriminates semantically similar utterances without labels for intent detection.
Supervised contrastive learning pulls utterances of the same intent closer and pushes utterances of different intents farther during few-shot intent detection.
Comprehensive Perspective in dialogue summarization considers summary points from all conversational interlocutors and all mentioned persons.
Focus Perspective in dialogue summarization positions the summary based on a user-specified personal named entity.
Occurrence planning of personal named entity and coreference information improves temporal coherence in neural dialogue summarization.
Occurrence planning of personal named entity and coreference information minimizes hallucination in neural dialogue summarization.
Dialogue summarization with personal named entity planning produces fluent and factually consistent summaries under various planning controls.
RockNER creates natural adversarial examples for named entity recognition models by replacing target entities with entities of the same semantic class in Wikidata at the entity level.
RockNER creates natural adversarial examples for named entity recognition models by using pre-trained language models to generate word substitutions at the context level.
OntoRock evaluates the robustness of named entity recognition models.
Named entity recognition models experience a performance drop when evaluated on OntoRock.
Named entity recognition models memorize in-domain entity patterns instead of reasoning from the context when evaluated on OntoRock.
Cross-lingual intermediate fine-tuning improves cross-lingual transfer for dialogue tasks by fine-tuning pretrained multilingual models on different but related tasks.
Cross-lingual intermediate tasks for dialogue tasks are constructed from parallel and conversational movie subtitles datasets.
Cross-lingual intermediate fine-tuning improves cross-lingual dialogue state tracking in low-resource and zero-shot settings.
Cross-lingual intermediate fine-tuning uses a small amount of parallel data that is widely available.
PermGen maximizes the expected log-likelihood of output paragraph distributions over all possible sentence orders.
PermGen uses hierarchical positional embedding for sentence-permuted paragraph generation.
PermGen introduces a new training procedure for sentence-permuted paragraph generation.
PermGen introduces a new decoding procedure for sentence-permuted paragraph generation.
PermGen generates more diverse paragraph outputs with higher quality than existing models.
NB-MLM focuses on predicting words with large weights of a Naive Bayes classifier trained for sentiment analysis during domain adaptation.
NB-MLM provides faster domain adaptation for sentiment analysis than the standard approach.
NB-MLM achieves better sentiment analysis performance than the standard approach.
Document-level text simplification is a task that simplifies documents consisting of multiple sentences.
D-Wikipedia is a large-scale dataset for document-level text simplification constructed from Wikipedia dumps.
D-Wikipedia is reliable for document-level text simplification.
D-SARI is an automatic evaluation metric for document-level text simplification.
A general-purpose Transformer-based model with cross-modal attention outperforms more specialized approaches on a majority of the gSCAN splits.
Many remaining errors in gSCAN reveal a fundamental challenge in systematic generalization of linguistic constructs regardless of visual context.
New challenging gSCAN tasks incorporate relations between objects in the visual environment.
Current models are data inefficient for the narrow scope of commands in gSCAN.

Entities and events in a reader's focus are represented as a neighborhood in a learned latent embedding space that minimizes the distance between mentions and the centroids of their gold coreference clusters.
Hard negatives are sampled from neighborhoods in the latent embedding space to train a fine-grained classifier for mention pairs and their local discourse features.
State-of-the-art results are achieved for event and entity coreference resolution on multiple cross-document coreference corpora.
Training on multiple cross-document coreference corpora improves performance across datasets.
Paired examples as indirect supervision provides stronger cues for learning latent decisions in compositional models.
An additional training objective encourages consistency between latent decisions for two related training examples that share internal substructure.
The training objective for consistency between latent decisions does not require external supervision for the latent output or the end task.
Paired examples as indirect supervision improves in- and out-of-distribution generalization for compositional question answering.
Paired examples as indirect supervision leads to correct latent decision predictions for compositional question answering.
Graphine is a terminology definition dataset where terminologies within each biomedical subdiscipline form a directed acyclic graph.
Graphex is a graph-aware definition generation model that integrates transformer with graph neural network.
Graphex outperforms existing text generation models by exploiting the graph structure of terminologies.
Graphine enables the evaluation of pretrained language models.
Graphine enables the comparison of graph representation learning methods.
Graphine enables the prediction of sentence granularity.
Distant supervision using span constraints improves unsupervised constituency parsing.
A small number of span constraints substantially improves DIORA's unsupervised constituency parsing.
Span constraints based on entities improves unsupervised constituency parsing on English WSJ Penn Treebank.
Round-trip translation consistently improves the demographic parity of sentiment classifiers at test time.
A continuum model for temporal knowledge graph link forecasting extends neural ordinary differential equations to multi-relational graph convolutional networks.
The continuum model for temporal knowledge graph link forecasting preserves the continuous nature of dynamic multi-relational graph data.
The continuum model for temporal knowledge graph link forecasting encodes both temporal and structural information into continuous-time dynamic embeddings.
A graph transition layer for temporal knowledge graphs captures edge formation and dissolution.
Entity relation extraction in visually rich documents uses an adaptation of the biaffine parser.
Entity relation extraction in visually rich documents identifies relations between groups of words using layout information.
Multi-task learning combines entity labeling and relation extraction tasks in model training.
Softmax tree uses sparse hyperplanes at decision nodes to make hard decisions.
Softmax tree uses small softmax classifiers at the leaves.
A variation of tree alternating optimization (TAO) learns the softmax tree.
Softmax trees are more accurate than softmax and other classifiers for classification with a large number of classes.
Softmax trees are faster than softmax and other classifiers for classification with a large number of classes.
RaNet selects the relevant video moment for a language query from a predefined set of candidate moments.
The choice-query interactor matches language queries to candidate video moments at both the sentence level and the token level.
The multi-choice relation constructor uses graph convolution to capture dependencies among candidate video moments for best moment selection.
CoLV integrates knowledge selection and knowledge-aware response generation in separate yet collaborative latent spaces.

CoLV draws a knowledge candidate from a latent space conditioned on the dialogue context and then samples a response from another collaborative latent space conditioned on the context and the selected knowledge.
CoLV outperforms previous methods on knowledge selection and response generation.
Wikipedia is the most common data source for English Machine Reading Comprehension datasets.
English Machine Reading Comprehension datasets have a relative lack of why, when, and where questions.

An addressee-aware module for emotion inference in multi-turn conversations automatically learns whether a participant keeps their historical emotional state or is affected by others in the next turn.
ContReph identifies rephrases in multi-turn dialogues.
ContReph leverages dialogue context and user-agent interaction signals including user implicit feedback and time gap between turns.
ContReph outperforms pairwise rephrase detection models.

In implicit event argument extraction, implicit knowledge transfer enables knowledge transfer from other tasks by building a unified training framework in the machine reading comprehension formulation.
In implicit event argument extraction, explicit data augmentation explicitly generates new training examples by treating machine reading comprehension models as an annotator.
Machine reading comprehension-based data augmentation achieves state-of-the-art performance on two implicit event argument extraction benchmarks.
Machine reading comprehension-based data augmentation demonstrates superior performance in data-low scenarios for implicit event argument extraction.
data-QuestEval is a reference-less and multimodal metric for Data-to-Text semantic evaluation.
Synthetic multimodal corpora enable training of multimodal components for data-QuestEval.
data-QuestEval achieves state-of-the-art correlations with human judgment on the WebNLG and WikiBio benchmarks.
Chinese WPLC is a dataset for evaluating pretrained language models on word prediction given long-range context.
Chinese WPLC uses automatic and manual selection strategies tailored to Chinese to ensure that target words can only be predicted using long-range context beyond the sentence scope.
The target words in Chinese WPLC include common nouns and Chinese 4-character idioms.
Linguistic relations between target words and long-range context in Chinese WPLC include lexical match, synonym, summary, and reasoning.
Chinese WPLC is a challenging dataset for pretrained language models.
A deep-learning model trained on the GLUE benchmark serves as a proxy indicator for the quality of open-domain dialogues.
Using the GLUE tasks as different perspectives on dialogue quality reduces the need for additional training data or quality reference responses.
The proxy indicator for open-domain dialogue quality infers various quality metrics and derives a component-based overall score.
A multilingual counterfactual detection dataset for product reviews in English, German, and Japanese provides professionally annotated counterfactual examples.
A counterfactual detection dataset for product reviews in English, German, and Japanese is compatible with prior counterfactual detection datasets and can be merged to learn accurate models.
Counterfactual detection models trained on a multilingual product review dataset are robust against selectional biases from cue phrase-based sentence selection.
Counterfactual detection in product reviews is language-specific.
R^3Net distinguishes real change from clutter and irrelevant changes.
A relation-embedded module explores potential changed objects in clutter.
A representation reconstruction module uses semantic similarities of corresponding locations in two images to learn the reconstruction representation and model the difference representation.
A syntactic skeleton predictor enhances semantic interaction between change localization and caption generation.
AligNART divides the machine translation task into alignment estimation and translation with aligned decoder inputs to enable simplified one-to-one translation.
Alignment decomposition alleviates the alignment estimation problem in non-autoregressive neural machine translation.
AligNART outperforms previous non-iterative non-autoregressive neural machine translation models that focus on explicit modality reduction on WMT14 English-German and WMT16 Romanian-English.
AligNART achieves translation quality comparable to state-of-the-art connectionist temporal classification based models on WMT14 English-German.
AligNART addresses the token repetition problem in non-autoregressive neural machine translation without requiring sequence-level knowledge distillation.
Four categories of graphs capture text information at various levels in interactive machine reading comprehension tasks.
Methods dynamically build and update graphs during information gathering in interactive machine reading comprehension tasks.
Neural models encode graph representations for reinforcement learning agents in interactive machine reading comprehension tasks.
Graph representations improve the performance of reinforcement learning agents in interactive machine reading comprehension tasks.
Contextual distillation transfers contextual knowledge via Word Relation and Layer Transforming Relation.
Contextual distillation does not require architectural restrictions between teacher and student models.
CMLM learns sentence representations by conditioning on the encoded vectors of adjacent sentences during masked language modeling training.
CMLM outperforms supervised models on SentEval for sentence representation evaluation.
CMLM can be extended to multiple languages and domains.
A multilingual CMLM model co-trained with bitext retrieval and natural language inference tasks outperforms previous state-of-the-art multilingual models on cross-lingual semantic search.
A post-training, model-agnostic approach removes language identifying information from sentence representations while retaining sentence semantics.
TEMP uses self-supervised learning to predict the position of new concepts by ranking generated taxonomy-paths.
TEMP uses pre-trained contextual encoders for taxonomy construction and hypernym detection.
Pre-trained contextual embeddings capture hypernym-hyponym relations.
TEMP uses dynamic margin loss with a novel dynamic margin function to learn detailed differences between taxonomy-paths.
TEMP outperforms prior state-of-the-art taxonomy expansion approaches on public benchmarks.
CRFR performs explicit multi-hop reasoning on knowledge graphs using a conversational context-based reinforcement learning model.
CRFR flexibly learns multiple reasoning fragments that are likely contained in the complete paths of user interests shift.

CRFR uses a fragments-aware unified model to fuse reasoning fragments information from item-oriented and concept-oriented knowledge graphs, enhancing conversational recommender system responses with entities and words from the fragments.
CRFR achieves state-of-the-art performance for conversational recommender systems in recommendation, conversation, and conversation interpretability.
A Vision-Language transformer model achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Navigation from Dialogue History task.
Goal Progress is not a suitable metric for evaluating agents in the Navigation from Dialogue History task.
The sub-path based task setup in Navigation from Dialogue History provides insufficient supervision signal for agents to navigate to the goal region.
NDH-Full is a task setup for navigational agents that uses the full dialogue and the entire navigation path as a single instance.
#HowYouTagTweets characterizes user hashtagging preferences by predicting the likelihood of a user posting with a hashtag.
#HowYouTagTweets combines user history and hashtag contexts in a deep semantic space using a pre-trained BERT and a neural topic model with multitask learning.
#HowYouTagTweets customizes user interests learned from the past to match future hashtags.
#HowYouTagTweets uses a personalized topic attention mechanism to capture salient contents for personalizing hashtag contexts.
#HowYouTagTweets outperforms state-of-the-art recommendation approaches that do not exploit latent topics.
A parallel dataset of poetry translations is available for several language pairs.
Multilingual fine-tuning on poetic data produces more faithful poetry translations in meaning and style than multilingual fine-tuning on non-poetic data despite using less training data.
Multilingual fine-tuning on poetic data produces more faithful poetry translations than bilingual fine-tuning on poetic data.
Label Reasoning Network sequentially reasons fine-grained entity labels by discovering and exploiting label dependencies knowledge entailed in the data.
Label Reasoning Network utilizes an auto-regressive network to conduct deductive reasoning between labels.
Label Reasoning Network utilizes a bipartite attribute graph to conduct inductive reasoning between labels.
Label Reasoning Network achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard ultra fine-grained entity typing benchmarks.
Label Reasoning Network resolves the long tail label problem.
Iterative Graph Network-based Decoder models the previously generated text using a Graph Neural Network at each decoding step.
Iterative Graph Network-based Decoder captures dependency relations in the passage that boost question generation.
Iterative Graph Network-based Decoder outperforms state-of-the-art models on sentence-level question generation tasks.
Self-Collaborative Denoising Learning (SCDL) uses self denoising to exploit reliable labels in distantly-supervised named entity recognition.
Self-Collaborative Denoising Learning (SCDL) uses collaborative denoising to explore unreliable annotations in distantly-supervised named entity recognition.
Self-Collaborative Denoising Learning (SCDL) outperforms state-of-the-art denoising methods in distantly-supervised named entity recognition.

Group Learning performs knowledge and model distillation for fact verification by having multiple student models learn from different delexicalized views of the data using pair-wise consistency losses.
Group Learning learns the best delexicalization strategy for the training data in cross-domain fact verification.
Group Learning outperforms state-of-the-art classifiers that use the original data in cross-domain fact verification.
BERT4GCN utilizes outputs from intermediate layers of BERT and positional information between words to augment a Graph Convolutional Network for encoding dependency graphs.
Augmenting Graph Convolutional Networks with grammatical features from intermediate layers of BERT improves Aspect-based Sentiment Classification model performance.
Retrieval marginalization handles unanswerable queries in multi-document question answering.
Retrieval marginalization mitigates false negatives in supporting evidence annotations for multi-document question answering.
Retrieval marginalization achieves state-of-the-art performance on the IIRC dataset for multi-document question answering.
Retrieval marginalization improves multi-document question answering performance on the HotpotQA dataset in the fullwiki setting.
AttentionRank extracts keyphrases from documents in an unsupervised manner.
AttentionRank uses self-attention to determine the importance of a candidate keyphrase within the context of a sentence.
AttentionRank uses cross-attention to identify the semantic relevance between a candidate keyphrase and the sentences within a document.
Out-of-distribution text examples exhibit two types of distribution shifts: background shift and semantic shift.
Density estimation methods outperform calibration methods on background shift out-of-distribution text and underperform on semantic shift out-of-distribution text.
Challenge data examples constitute a different type of out-of-distribution text as both calibration and density estimation methods fail to detect them.
CodRED is the first human-annotated cross-document relation extraction dataset for knowledge acquisition in the wild.
CodRED requires finding relevant documents to identify relations between two entities.
CodRED requires reasoning over multiple documents to extract relational facts between two entities.
CodRED is challenging for existing relation extraction methods including strong BERT-based models.
MCPredictor utilizes rich text information to obtain more comprehensive event semantic representations at the event level for script event prediction.
MCPredictor considers multiple event sequences for different participants of the subsequent event at the script level for script event prediction.
The default dimension of 768 for dense vectors in passage retrieval is unnecessarily large due to redundancy.
An unsupervised compression pipeline for dense vectors in passage retrieval consists of principal component analysis, product quantization, and hybrid search.
Unsupervised principal component analysis outperforms supervised baselines for compressing dense vectors in passage retrieval in some settings.
An unsupervised compression pipeline for dense vectors in passage retrieval achieves high compression ratios with minimal loss in retrieval accuracy.
A semi-automatic and scalable framework generates disentangled shifts for cross-dataset visual question answering evaluation with little human intervention.
A controllable visual question-answer generation module generates highly-relevant and diverse question-answer pairs with the desired dataset style.
CrossVQA is a benchmark for assessing visual question answering generalization across the VQA2, VizWiz, and Open Images datasets.
Visual shifts matter more than language shifts in cross-dataset visual question answering.
A speaker-aware persistent memory model uses speaker i-vectors to form a persistent memory for feature adaptation in automatic speech recognition.

A gradual pruning method adapts automatic speech recognition models to target speakers without changing the model architecture by pruning less contributing parameters on the model encoder and using the pruned parameters for adaptation while freezing the unpruned parameters.
A unified speaker adaptation approach reduces word error rate for general speaker adaptation.
A unified speaker adaptation approach outperforms the baseline for target speaker adaptation.
A unified speaker adaptation approach outperforms the finetuning method for target speaker adaptation.
A unified speaker adaptation approach improves word error rate with extremely low-resource adaptation data, such as one utterance.
Measures that evaluate specific information retention demonstrate the tradeoff between bias removal and information retention.
OSCaR disentangles biased associations between concepts in word embeddings instead of removing concepts wholesale.
OSCaR mitigates gender bias in word embeddings without erasing semantic information.
Pretrained language models exhibit wide performance gaps across demographic groups defined by gender, age, education, and race.
Pretrained language models systematically disfavor young non-white male speakers.
Pretrained language models learn sociolectal biases, speaking more like some demographic groups than others.
Larger pretrained language models reduce some performance gaps between majority and minority groups, except for BERT models.
A unified structural causal model describes the relationships among variables for various Information Extraction tasks.
Counterfactuals are generated based on an explicit language structure to calculate the direct causal effect during inference in Information Extraction.
A debiasing approach yields more robust predictions for Information Extraction.
Counterfactual IE (CFIE) mitigates spurious correlation issues in Information Extraction.
LPNet dynamically adjusts the position and length of a fixed set of learnable moment proposals during training for natural language video localization.
A boundary-aware loss leverages frame-level information for natural language video localization.
DistDR iteratively improves a weak retriever by alternately finding evidence from the current model and training the model to learn the most likely evidence.
DistDR performs comparably to fully-supervised state-of-the-art methods on multi-hop and single-hop question answering benchmarks without evidence labels.
DistDR finds more accurate evidence over iterations, leading to improved open-domain question answering performance.
NegatER ranks potential negative statements in commonsense knowledge bases using a contextual language model.
NegatER discovers negative statements in commonsense knowledge bases without requiring ground-truth negative examples.
Negatives generated by NegatER are more grammatical, coherent, and informative than negatives generated by contrastive data augmentation approaches.
Using negatives generated by NegatER leads to accuracy improvements in knowledge base completion tasks.
The positive knowledge in language models can be re-purposed to generate negative knowledge for commonsense knowledge bases.
A co-training based fine-tuning method learns generalized semantic equivalences using unlabeled target language data as a complement to multilingual language modeling.
A contrastive learning adaptation method captures semantic relationships in parallel data with few translation pairs.
Retention of training relation triples is a key factor of performance on standard end-to-end relation extraction benchmarks.
A pipeline model that uses intermediate type representations is less prone to over-rely on retention in end-to-end relation extraction.
A graph structure for unsupervised keyphrase extraction represents phrases as vertices and phrase similarities as edges.
A new centrality computation method captures local salient information for unsupervised keyphrase extraction based on a graph structure of phrases.
Global and local context modeling is combined for keyphrase ranking in unsupervised keyphrase extraction.
Joint modeling of local and global context in unsupervised keyphrase extraction outperforms most existing state-of-the-art models and generalizes better to documents of varying domains and lengths.
Both local and global context information are crucial for unsupervised keyphrase extraction.
An auxiliary text-image relation detection module controls the proper exploitation of visual information.

A hierarchical framework bridges the multi-modal connection between multi-modal aspect term extraction and multi-modal aspect sentiment classification and provides separate visual guidance for each submodule.
Adversarial attacks on NLP models can be conducted by transferring text style while preserving semantic meaning.
Backdoor attacks on NLP models can be conducted by transferring text style while preserving semantic meaning.
Popular NLP models are vulnerable to adversarial and backdoor attacks based on text style transfer.
NLP models have limited ability to handle text style features.
Timeline summarization is performed by compressing an event-graph representation of news articles to a salient sub-graph.
A time-aware optimal transport distance enables unsupervised learning of event-graph compression for timeline summarization.
Timeline100 is a dataset for timeline summarization.
Event-graph compression achieves state-of-the-art performance for timeline summarization on multiple real-world datasets.
SpellBERT does not require a predefined confusion set for Chinese Spelling Check.
SpellBERT uses a graph neural network to incorporate radical and pinyin information as visual and phonetic features.
SpellBERT uses masked language model alike pre-training tasks to fuse visual and phonetic features with character representations.
SpellBERT achieves state-of-the-art performance on an OCR dataset containing errors not covered by existing confusion sets.
RICA evaluates robust commonsense inference despite textual perturbations.
A systematic and scalable procedure generates data for robust commonsense inference evaluation using commonsense knowledge bases.

Pre-trained language models perform no better than random guessing in the zero-shot setting, are heavily impacted by statistical biases, and are not robust to perturbation attacks in robust commonsense inference.
Fine-tuning on similar statements offers limited gains for robust commonsense inference because pre-trained language models fail to generalize to unseen inferences.
There is a significant gap between pre-trained language models and human-level language understanding in robust commonsense inference.
Most Transformer modifications do not provide meaningful performance improvements in natural language processing applications.
Most Transformer modifications that yield performance improvements either depend on implementation details or are minor.
PABI quantifies the benefits of incidental supervision signals for a target task without combinatorial experiments.
PABI characterizes the uncertainty reduction of incidental supervision signals in a unified PAC-Bayesian framework.
PABI's predictions correlate with learning performance for named entity recognition and question answering.
Progressive self-training uses a base model to infer pseudo-labels on a subset of unlabeled data that becomes progressively harder and larger at each iteration for aspect term extraction.
A discriminator filters noisy pseudo-labels for aspect term extraction.
Progressive Self-Training with Discriminator outperforms previous baselines and achieves state-of-the-art performance for aspect term extraction on SemEval datasets.
A visually grounded question-asking model produces polar clarification questions to resolve misunderstandings in dialogue.

A visually grounded question-asking model uses an expected information gain objective to derive informative questions from an off-the-shelf image captioner without requiring supervised question-answer data.
A visually grounded question-asking model poses questions that improve communicative success in a goal-oriented 20 questions game with synthetic and human answerers.
Universal-KD matches intermediate layers of teacher and student models in the output space using attention-based layer projection and pseudo classifiers.
Universal-KD can be flexibly combined with existing intermediate layer distillation techniques to improve the results of those techniques.
Universal-KD uses teacher pseudo classifiers to address the capacity gap problem in knowledge distillation without requiring teacher assistant networks.
Universal-KD enables cross-architecture intermediate layer knowledge distillation.
Universal-KD outperforms other knowledge distillation techniques on GLUE tasks.
KIEMP estimates keyphrase importance by measuring phrase syntactic accuracy, phrase information saliency, and phrase-document topic consistency.

KIEMP integrates the measurement of phrase syntactic accuracy, phrase information saliency, and phrase-document topic consistency via an end-to-end multi-task learning model to balance the effects of the three perspectives.
KIEMP outperforms existing state-of-the-art keyphrase extraction approaches on multiple benchmark datasets.
Salience-aware event chain modeling extracts the principal chain of events from natural language text by filtering non-salient events and supportive sentences.
Pre-training large language models on event chains extracted by salience-aware event chain modeling improves performance on narrative prediction and event-based temporal question answering.
A model minimizes Earth Mover's Distance loss between predicted valence, arousal, and dominance score distributions and categorical emotion distributions sorted along valence, arousal, and dominance.
A model simultaneously classifies emotion categories and predicts valence, arousal, and dominance scores for a given sentence.
Predicted valence, arousal, and dominance scores show significant positive correlations with ground truth valence, arousal, and dominance scores.
Further training with valence, arousal, and dominance supervision improves valence, arousal, and dominance prediction performance, especially on small datasets.
Predictions include appropriate emotion words that are not part of the original categorical annotations.
Speaker name substitution handles multiple speakers in dialogue summarization.
Negation scope highlighting improves negation understanding in dialogue summarization.
Multi-task learning with relevant tasks aids reasoning about situations in dialogue summarization.
Pretraining on in-domain data enhances informal language understanding in dialogue summarization.
ConceptNet and GenericsKB exhibit overgeneralization of polarized perceptions and representation disparity across demographic groups.
Downstream models that use ConceptNet exhibit overgeneralization of polarized perceptions and representation disparity across demographic groups.
A filtering-based approach reduces representational harms in commonsense knowledge resources and downstream models but causes a performance drop.
TEA-GNN embeds entities, relations, and timestamps from different knowledge graphs into a vector space and uses Graph Neural Networks to learn entity representations.

TEA-GNN incorporates relation and time information into the Graph Neural Network structure via a self-attention mechanism that assigns weights to nodes using orthogonal transformation matrices computed from relation and timestamp embeddings in a neighborhood.
TEA-GNN outperforms state-of-the-art methods for entity alignment between temporal knowledge graphs by incorporating time information.
Cross-task prototypes model the relations between training tasks in episodic few-shot learning for event detection.
Enforcing prediction consistency among classifiers across tasks makes few-shot event detection models robust to outliers.
Cross-task prototypes and prediction consistency enforcement among classifiers across tasks make few-shot event detection models robust when labeled data of novel event types is limited.
Using generic resources for style transfer without task-specific parallel data outperforms existing unsupervised methods on formality transfer and polarity swap.
Further pre-training BART on generic paraphrases and synthetic pairs from a lexical resource strengthens its rewriting ability for style transfer.
Iterative back-translation for style transfer trains two models in opposite directions to dynamically generate synthetic training pairs for each other.
Differences between formality transfer and polarity swap tasks are revealed by their response to a style transfer method using generic resources without task-specific parallel data.
An 80-item test set evaluates contact-induced semantic shifts in Quebec English.
Diachronic word embedding methods detect contact-induced semantic shifts in synchrony with results comparable to the state of the art on similar diachronic tasks.
Encouraging evaluation results for semantic shift detection do not translate to practical value in detecting new semantic shifts.
Token-level embeddings accelerate manual data exploration in sociolinguistic analyses.
Token-level embeddings provide an efficient way to scale up sociolinguistic analyses.

A multi-task generalized linear model with BERT features jump-starts item difficulty estimates for adaptive language tests with minimal test-taker data and limited item exposures from a large item bank.
A multi-task generalized linear model with BERT features enables principled comparison of test-taker proficiency, item difficulty, and language proficiency frameworks such as the CEFR.
For the Duolingo English Test, item difficulty estimates correlate with lexico-grammatical features associated with reading complexity.
A Chinese Opinion Role Labeling dataset is constructed by manually translating and projecting annotations from a standard English MPQA dataset.
Cross-lingual transfer methods (model transfer and corpus translation) for Chinese Opinion Role Labeling are evaluated for effectiveness in bilingual and multilingual settings.
Interpret Masked Word conjectures the meaning of a masked entity from dialogue context.
Hypernym Generation predicts the hypernym of an entity from dialogue context.
Pipelines for faithful rationalization on information-extraction style tasks perform poorly on reasoning tasks requiring free-text rationales.
State-of-the-art T5-based joint models produce free-text rationales that are associated with the predicted labels for commonsense question-answering and natural language inference.
Label loyalty measures how closely a compressed model mimics the original model in label predictions.
Probability loyalty measures how closely a compressed model mimics the original model in probability distributions.
Combining multiple compression techniques achieves higher accuracy, loyalty, and robustness in BERT compression.
A reordering module organizes words to follow the source language order by learning task-specific reordering strategies from a general-purpose order predictor model.

Word reordering improves target language performance in zero-shot cross-lingual dependency parsing, POS tagging, and morphological tagging more for languages that are distant from the source language.
A zero-shot commonsense reasoning system uncovers unstated presumptions from user commands that follow the template: if (state), then (action), because (goal).
An iterative knowledge query mechanism extracts multi-hop reasoning chains from a neural knowledge base by using symbolic logic rules to reduce the search space.
A dynamic question generation strategy conversationally elicits missing knowledge by generating and presenting contextualized queries to human users.
Knowledge conflicts occur when contextual information contradicts parametric knowledge in question answering.
Question answering models over-rely on memorized information, causing hallucinations.
Specific factors exacerbate over-reliance on memorized information in question answering models.
A method mitigates over-reliance on parametric knowledge in question answering, minimizing hallucination and improving out-of-distribution generalization.
A framework generates knowledge conflicts in question answering.
Aspect-aware words are derived from external affective commonsense knowledge by using each aspect as a pivot.
Beta Distribution derives aspect-aware weights that reflect the importance of aspect-aware words to the aspect.
Aspect-aware words substitute coarse-grained aspects to construct graphs that leverage aspect-related contextual sentiment dependencies in Aspect Category Sentiment Analysis.
Wikily leverages Wikipedia for neural machine translation, image captioning, and dependency parsing without direct supervision from external parallel data or supervised models in the target language.

First sentences and titles of linked Wikipedia pages and cross-lingual image captions are strong signals for extracting bilingual dictionaries and cross-lingual word embeddings to mine parallel text from Wikipedia.
Wikily achieves neural machine translation performance in low-resource languages that is close to or exceeds strong supervised baselines.
Image captioning models trained on Wikily-translated Arabic data achieve better performance than supervised models for Arabic.
Dependency parsing models using Wikily-translated training data outperform recent work on cross-lingual transfer.
Claim-guided Hierarchical Graph Attention Network uses undirected interaction graphs to represent conversation threads.
Claim-guided Hierarchical Graph Attention Network enhances representation learning for responsive posts by considering the entire social contexts.
Claim-guided Hierarchical Graph Attention Network attends over posts that can semantically infer the target claim.
Claim-guided Hierarchical Graph Attention Network exhibits superior capacity for detecting rumors at early stages.
Wiki-Convert is a dataset of sentences annotated with numbers and units to avoid conflating nominal and ordinal number occurrences.
Specialized number encoders improve masked word prediction for sentences containing numbers.
Exponent embeddings are the best number encoders for masked word prediction.
Enhanced literacy skills from number encoders generalize to contexts without annotated numbers.
Political coalition signal prediction predicts the (un)willingness of political parties to form a government coalition from pre-election news coverage.

Political coalition signal prediction is decomposed into predicting whether a reported statement from a politician or a journalist refers to a potential coalition and predicting whether the speaker is in favour of or against the coalition.
A dataset of hand-coded newspaper articles for political coalition signal prediction covers elections in Ireland, Germany, and Austria in English and German.
Contrastive conditioning detects disambiguation errors in machine translation without reference translations.
Contrastive conditioning scores translation quality by conditioning on source variants that provide contrastive disambiguation cues.

Distillation-trained machine translation models overgeneralize more than other machine translation models with comparable BLEU scores in word sense disambiguation and translation of gendered occupation names.
Contrastive conditioning reveals a side effect of knowledge distillation in machine translation that standard evaluation metrics do not capture.
Word order agreement is one of the strongest linguistic predictors of cross-lingual alignment.
Agreement in morphological complexity is one of the strongest linguistic predictors of cross-lingual alignment.
In-family training data is a stronger predictor of cross-lingual alignment than language-specific training data.
Morphological segmentation affects cross-lingual alignment between English and Inuktitut.
Word order agreement affects vector space isomorphism in zero-shot language pairs.
FinQA provides question-answering pairs for numerical reasoning over financial reports written by financial experts.
FinQA includes gold reasoning programs for full explainability.
Popular large pre-trained models perform worse than expert humans in acquiring finance knowledge and in complex multi-step numerical reasoning using finance knowledge.
SQL queries are automatically generated from a database using an abstract syntax tree grammar.
Generated SQL queries cover a high proportion of SQL patterns in the training data.
Hierarchical SQL-to-question generation produces natural language questions from SQL queries.
A sampling strategy improves training efficiency with large generated datasets.
In cross-domain text-to-SQL parsing data augmentation, hierarchical SQL-to-question generation is critical for improving parsing performance.
AUTOSUMM automatically creates deep learning models for extractive and abstractive text summarization.

AUTOSUMM creates smaller, customized extractive and abstractive text summarization models for any dataset using Neural Architecture Search and Knowledge Distillation with knowledge from large language models.
Text summarization models created by AUTOSUMM achieve near state-of-the-art accuracy with reduced model size and inference time.
A two-stage approach couples context modeling with zero pronoun recovering to mitigate zero pronoun problems in document-level natural language generation.

Zero pronoun recovering is learned during the document-level natural language generation task learning process without requiring zero pronoun-annotated document-level natural language generation corpora.
An adversarial bot adjusts model outputs to alleviate error propagation from mis-recovered zero pronouns in document-level natural language generation.
Coupling context modeling with zero pronoun recovering improves pronoun translation in document-level machine translation.
TimeTraveler is the first reinforcement learning method for temporal knowledge graph forecasting.
TimeTraveler travels on historical knowledge graph snapshots to search for answers.
TimeTraveler defines a relative time encoding function to capture timespan information.
TimeTraveler uses a time-shaped reward based on Dirichlet distribution to guide model learning.
TimeTraveler uses a representation method for unseen entities to improve inductive inference.
Mutual dependency maximisation measures the dependency between modalities in multimodal fusion.
Mutual dependency maximisation consistently improves multimodal sentiment analysis across multiple state-of-the-art models and datasets.
Mutual dependency maximisation produces representations that are more robust to modality drops in multimodal sentiment analysis.
Mutual dependency maximisation includes a statistical network that interprets high-dimensional representations in multimodal sentiment analysis.
A transformer language model augmented with an external knowledgebase derived from Retrieval Augmented Generation enhances salience detection in long-form stories.
A transformer language model augmented with a memory mechanism enhances salience detection in long-form stories.
Salience annotations for classic literary works are derived from chapter-aligned summaries in the Shmoop corpus.
The memory and knowledgebase augmented language model improves salience detection performance on long-form stories compared to a baseline without these augmentations.
Both the memory mechanism and the external knowledgebase are crucial for the improvement in salience detection on long-form stories.
A multi-stage framework for neural passage retrieval comprises pre-training with synthetic data, fine-tuning with labeled data, and negative sampling at both stages.
Negative sampling is applied to synthetic data during pre-training for neural passage retrieval.
Fusion methods combine negatives from different negative sampling strategies for neural passage retrieval.
Synthetic data generation and negative sampling have additive benefits for neural passage retrieval.
Fusion of different negative sampling strategies establishes a new state-of-the-art for two passage retrieval tasks.
The performance of six temporal embedding approaches for temporal knowledge graph completion is empirically quantified across a wide range of configurations.
Temporal embeddings for temporal knowledge graph completion are classified into timestamp embeddings and time-dependent entity embeddings.
Timestamp embeddings for temporal knowledge graph completion achieve performance comparable to or better than time-dependent entity embeddings with significantly fewer parameters.
When trained appropriately, the relative performance differences between different temporal embeddings for temporal knowledge graph completion shrink and sometimes reverse compared to prior results.

A unified open-source framework for temporal knowledge graph completion models enables arbitrary combination of temporal embeddings, score functions, loss functions, regularizers, and explicit modeling of reciprocal relations.
For languages closely related to English, syntactic and lexical structural information is encoded in mBERT's contextual representations in a shared cross-lingual embedding space.

For languages not closely related to English, applying an orthogonal transformation learned per language to mBERT's contextual embeddings is beneficial for representing syntactic and lexical structural information.
Orthogonal transformations of mBERT's contextual embeddings enable zero-shot and few-shot cross-lingual parsing.
Back-training aligns natural outputs with noisy inputs in synthetic training data.
Back-training reduces the gap between target domain and synthetic data distribution.
Back-training reduces model overfitting to source domain.
Back-training outperforms self-training in unsupervised domain adaptation for question generation and passage retrieval from the Natural Questions domain to machine learning and biomedical domains.
Consistency filters remove low-quality synthetic data before training.
MLQuestions is a domain-adaptation dataset containing unaligned questions, unaligned passages, and aligned question-passage pairs.

The swap-then-finetune procedure converts a pretrained transformer into a recurrent variant by replacing softmax attention with a linear-complexity recurrent alternative that uses a learned feature map and then finetuning the model.
The swap-then-finetune procedure provides an improved tradeoff between efficiency and accuracy relative to the standard transformer and other recurrent variants.
The swap-then-finetune procedure has lower training cost than training recurrent variants from scratch.
Direct supervision and meta-learning enable automated discovery of optimal masking policies.

The success of intermediate pre-training depends on appropriate pre-train corpus, selection of output format (masked spans or full sentence), and understanding the role of masked language modeling for the downstream task.
Learned masking policies improve performance compared to masking named entities on TriviaQA.
Masking policies learned from one task positively transfer to other tasks in certain cases.
Regular expressions are converted into a special form of finite-state transducers for slot filling.
The approximate inference algorithm of the special form of finite-state transducers is unfolded into a bidirectional recurrent neural model that performs slot filling via sequence labeling.
Converting regular expressions into neural networks for slot filling achieves superior performance in zero-shot and few-shot settings.
Converting regular expressions into neural networks for slot filling remains competitive when sufficient training data is available.
Neural belief tracking uses different uncertainty measures.
Incorporating different uncertainty measures in neural belief tracking improves downstream dialogue policy performance and robustness.
A two-stage approach generates coherent long text from short input text.
A revised self-organising map constructs a directed semantic graph by clustering similar nodes from document-level paths.
Three subgraph alignment methods extract maximum matching paths or subgraphs.
Structure generation complements missing or incomplete titles by extending a document with related but absent keyphrases from other documents for keyphrase generation.

Structure encoding builds a graph of keyphrases and a document to obtain a structure-aware representation of a document that has been extended with related but absent keyphrases for keyphrase generation.
A natural language knowledge base for knowledge-based visual question answering is applicable to any VQA system.
The visual retriever retrieves knowledge using text and images for knowledge-based visual question answering.
The visual reader predicts answers using classification or extraction for knowledge-based visual question answering.
The visual retriever and reader are trained with weak supervision.
A high-quality visual retriever improves the reader's performance on the OK-VQA benchmark.
Noise-robust learning trains named entity recognition models on distantly-labeled data using a new loss function and a noisy label removal step.
Language model augmented self-training improves the generalization ability of named entity recognition models by using contextualized augmentations created by pre-trained language models.
Noise-robust learning and language model augmented self-training achieve superior performance in distantly-supervised named entity recognition compared to existing models.
Audio statements and lie-based annotations are associated with a greater number of strong verbal leakage cue categories.
The number of strong verbal leakage cue categories in a lie detection dataset is the key factor for model performance in cross-dataset and in-dataset testing, not the total number of strong cues.
Verbal leakage cues are a key factor for selecting lie detection datasets.
Dynamic selection of the teacher model improves student model performance in knowledge distillation.
Dynamic selection of training instances using informative subsets achieves comparable student model performance while accelerating knowledge distillation training.
Dynamic adjustment of the supervision contribution of alignment objectives improves student model performance in knowledge distillation.
Differentiable Reward Imitation Learning generates self-contained, summary-centric question-answer pairs with length-constrained, article-summarizing answers.
A new dataset of news articles with questions as titles and paired summaries of varying length is collected.
Generated question-answer pairs capture the central gists of news articles and achieve high answer accuracy.
Nearest-neighbor few-shot inference performs cross-lingual classification using few labeled samples in the target language.
Nearest-neighbor few-shot inference outperforms traditional fine-tuning in cross-lingual classification when using few labeled samples in the target language.
Nearest-neighbor few-shot inference generalizes across different natural language processing tasks.
An event graph built from input sentences captures and organizes related events in a structured way to guide sentence fusion.
A graph flow attention mechanism controls the fusion process via the graph structure.
Event Graph based Sentence Fusion achieves state-of-the-art performance on sentence fusion tasks.
CUVA jointly learns embeddings and cluster assignments for noun and relation phrases in open knowledge graphs in an end-to-end approach.
CUVA outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches on multiple benchmarks for open knowledge graph canonicalization.
CanonicNell is a dataset for evaluating entity canonicalization systems.
An approach automatically detects Finnish dialects using text and audio modalities.
Combining text and audio modalities yields higher accuracy for Finnish dialect detection than using text alone.
Keyphrases are automatically generated (rather than extracted) from input passages for question-answer pair generation.
The question-answer pair generation task is reduced to keyphrase-question-answer triplet joint generation.
A multi-agent communication model iteratively generates and optimizes questions and keyphrases to guide answer generation.
A comprehensive analysis of the multi-agent communication model suggests new directions for question-answer pair generation.
In VAE-based unsupervised relation extraction, relation classification serves as an intermediate variable rather than a latent variable.

In VAE-based unsupervised relation extraction, relation classification is conditioned on the sentence input and the latent variable is conditioned on the relation classification and the sentence input.
Adversarial Scrubber (AdS) debiases contextual representations for text classification.
Adversarial Scrubber converges without leaking demographic information under certain conditions.
Minimum Description Length (MDL) probing evaluates debiasing performance.
Adversarial Scrubber generates representations with minimal demographic attribute information and maximal target task information.
Inflating operation enriches text-image interactions by inserting multiple codes into the encoder input.
Shrinking operation reduces text-image interactions through knowledge distillation.
Inflating and shrinking approach enhances both efficiency and accuracy of late-interaction models for text-image retrieval.
Data-to-text generation involves direct manipulation of retrieved text segments from neighbor source-target pairs through insertion or replacement in partially constructed outputs.
Finding the shortest oracle derivation for training data-to-text generation policies that manipulate text segments reduces to parsing under a particular weighted context-free grammar.
Data-to-text generation via direct splicing of retrieved text segments yields more interpretable and controllable outputs.
TransPrompt is a transferable prompting framework for few-shot learning across similar NLP tasks.
TransPrompt uses a multi-task meta-knowledge acquisition procedure to train a meta-learner that captures cross-task transferable knowledge.
TransPrompt uses two de-biasing techniques to achieve task-agnostic performance and remain unbiased towards any tasks.
TransPrompt outperforms single-task and cross-task baselines on multiple NLP tasks and datasets.
TransPrompt improves performance on previously unseen tasks.
TransPrompt outperforms fine-tuning baselines when trained with full training sets.
Conditional Poisson stochastic beam search samples K candidates without replacement according to the conditional Poisson sampling design.
Conditional Poisson stochastic beam search enables building consistent estimators for sequence models.
Conditional Poisson stochastic beam search samples diverse sets from sequence models.
Conditional Poisson stochastic beam search produces lower variance and more efficient estimators than stochastic beam search in high entropy settings.
Monolingual response generation, cross-lingual response generation, next utterance discrimination, and speaker identification capture dialogue characteristics for neural chat translation.
Capturing dialogue characteristics in neural chat translation generates coherent and speaker-relevant translations.

Heterogeneous topic knowledge embeddings incorporate topic information via pre-encoder, post-encoder, and decoder embeddings to increase the likelihood of selecting target words from the same topic as the source sentence in neural machine translation.
Heterogeneous topic knowledge embeddings outperform baseline methods on English-to-German and English-to-French translation tasks.

A framework for hierarchical multi-label text classification considers both vertical dependencies among levels of the category hierarchy and horizontal correlations among categories at the same level.
A loosely coupled graph convolutional neural network extracts representations for words, documents, and level-wise representations for categories.
Category representations capture vertical dependencies among levels of the category hierarchy and model horizontal correlations among categories at the same level.
A hybrid algorithm predicts the categories of the entire hierarchical structure using document embeddings and category embeddings.
A multi-modal open-domain dialogue model integrates components from state-of-the-art open-domain dialogue agents and state-of-the-art vision models.
A multi-modal open-domain dialogue model incorporates image fusion schemes.
A multi-modal open-domain dialogue model uses domain-adaptive pre-training and fine-tuning strategies.
A multi-modal open-domain dialogue model outperforms existing models in multi-modal dialogue.
A multi-modal open-domain dialogue model performs as well as BlenderBot in text-based conversation.
Incorporating safety components into a multi-modal open-domain dialogue model does not diminish performance with respect to human preference.
A data augmentation method for aspect-based sentiment analysis considers word importance in textual sequences relative to aspects and sentiments.
A data augmentation method for aspect-based sentiment analysis substitutes unimportant tokens using two replacement strategies without altering aspect-level polarity.
A contrastive learning method for few-shot relation extraction exploits relation label information during representation learning.
A method for few-shot relation extraction learns to adaptively focus on hard tasks.
MultiUAT dynamically adjusts training data usage based on model uncertainty on a small set of trusted clean data for multi-corpus machine translation.
MultiUAT outperforms static and dynamic balancing baselines in multilingual and multi-domain machine translation.
Static and similarity-based balancing methods are deficient for cross-domain transfer in machine translation.
Keyword typos in queries reduce the retrieval and ranking effectiveness of Dense Retriever and BERT re-ranker.
A typos-aware training framework makes Dense Retriever and BERT re-ranker robust to typos in queries.
The document graph connects relevant contexts in a document regardless of their distances.
The document graph is constructed using adjacency, syntactic dependency, lexical consistency, and coreference relations.
Source and target document graphs are incorporated into the Transformer architecture using graph convolutional networks.
The document graph improves neural machine translation quality.
The document graph is beneficial for capturing discourse phenomena.
Model size is a bottleneck for cross-lingual transfer in multilingual evaluation metrics.
A distillation method for multilingual evaluation metrics uses synthetic data generation and transfers knowledge from one teacher model to multiple student models trained on related languages.
Metric models distilled with synthetic data generation and multi-teacher knowledge transfer maintain high performance with significantly reduced parameters.
Few-shot evaluation metrics for relation extraction obscure performance variability across relations.
State-of-the-art few-shot relation classification models overly rely on entity type information.
Training routine modifications encourage few-shot relation classification models to better discriminate between relations involving similar entity types.
CAAT is an architecture for simultaneous translation.
CAAT jointly optimizes the policy and translation models.
CAAT adapts the RNN-T model by removing the strong monotonic constraint to allow for reordering in translation.
CAAT introduces a latency loss that can be optimized by a forward-backward algorithm.
CAAT achieves better latency-quality trade-offs than state-of-the-art approaches on speech-to-text and text-to-text simultaneous translation tasks.
Ultra-high dimensional sparse representations have directly controllable sparsity.
Ultra-high dimensional sparse representations enable binarized representations that are efficient for storage and search.
A bucketing method selects and merges embeddings from multiple layers of BERT to represent diverse linguistic aspects.
BPM_MT predicts backchannel using multi-task learning and the KoBERT pre-trained language model.
BPM_MT simultaneously performs backchannel category prediction using acoustic and lexical features and sentiment score prediction based on sentiment cues.
BPM_MT improves performance in predicting the four backchannel categories (continuer, understanding, empathic response, and No BC) over a baseline.
Deep Transformer-based pre-trained models in mixed-domain transfer learning capture only local context and exhibit poor generalization for COVID-19 misinformation detection.

A combination of shallow network-based domain-specific models and convolutional neural networks extracts local and global context from target data in a hierarchical fashion for COVID-19 misinformation detection.
A truth-conditional architecture for time series captioning conditions solely on the chosen valid program that holds true for the input time series to generate text descriptions.
Programs in a truth-conditional architecture for time series captioning are constructed from modules that are small neural networks capturing numerical patterns and temporal information.
Modules in a truth-conditional architecture for time series captioning are shared across multiple programs to enable compositionality and efficient parameter learning.
The modules and their composition in a truth-conditional architecture for time series captioning are learned end-to-end from natural language text descriptions without explicit supervision.
A truth-conditional architecture for time series captioning generates high-precision captions using a small and simple module space.
Development cross-entropy loss in supervised neural machine translation scales as a power law with training data size and non-embedding parameter count.
HypMix is model-, data-, and modality-agnostic.
HypMix captures the complex geometry of input and hidden state hierarchies better than contemporary interpolative data augmentation techniques.
HypMix consistently outperforms state-of-the-art data augmentation techniques on benchmark and low resource datasets across speech, text, and vision modalities.
HypMix demonstrates the efficacy of Riemannian hyperbolic manifolds for interpolation-based data augmentation through adversarial robustness analysis and qualitative inferences.
Transformer language models exhibit parameter norm growth during training, including during T5 pretraining.
Transformer networks approximate discretized networks with saturated activation functions during parameter norm growth.
Saturation characterizes an inductive bias implicit in gradient descent for natural language processing.
In saturated transformers, some attention heads focus locally on a small number of positions while others compute global averages to enable counting.
Online augmentation for discrete adversarial training generates adversarial examples at every training step to adapt to the changing model.
A discrete attack based on best-first search generates adversarial examples for language inputs.
Random sampling attacks generate adversarial examples without expensive search-based procedures.
Random sampling attacks improve robustness over offline augmentation and reduce training time compared to search-based attacks.
Online augmentation with search-based attacks improves robustness compared to offline augmentation.
Word links specify the positions where each source word appears in a document for document-level neural machine translation.
Context information is shared for occurrences of the same source word during encoding in document-level neural machine translation.
An auxiliary loss function constrains consistent translation for occurrences of the same source word in document-level neural machine translation.
Encouraging consistent translation for occurrences of the same source word improves lexical consistency in document-level neural machine translation.
Cross-lingual data selection extracts in-domain sentences for the language without monolingual data in the new domain from a large generic monolingual corpus.
Contrastive learning trains an adaptive layer on multilingual BERT to align source and target language representations.
Domain classifiers transfer between languages in a zero-shot manner.
Joint learning of translation and domain discrimination adapts neural machine translation models to new domains.
Cross-lingual data selection outperforms other data selection baselines for neural machine translation domain adaptation.
CSAGN explicitly encodes speaker dependent information.
CSAGN with the multi-task learning method outperforms previous baselines on conversational semantic role labeling benchmark datasets.
Residual adapter adapts ASR systems to atypical speech and accented speech.
Residual adapter adds a small number of extra parameters to the encoder layers of ASR models.
Residual adapter achieves similar adaptation gains as model fine-tuning while updating only a tiny fraction of the model parameters.
Temporal Complex Event Schema is a graph-based representation that encompasses events, arguments, temporal connections, and argument relations.
Temporal Event Graph Model predicts event instances following Temporal Complex Event Schema.
A schema learning corpus includes documents with associated event graphs and manually constructed gold-standard schemas.
Temporal Complex Event Schemas have superior quality compared to linear representations in intrinsic evaluations.
Temporal Event Graph Model outperforms human schemas and baselines in schema-guided future event prediction.
NeRoBERTa uses nested tree structures composed of syntactic and discourse trees for extractive summarization.
For extractive summarization, NeRoBERTa achieves higher automatic evaluation scores than baseline models.
For extractive summarization, NeRoBERTa produces more coherent summaries than baseline models in human evaluation.
For extractive summarization, NeRoBERTa achieves human evaluation scores comparable to state-of-the-art models.
SFLM generates pseudo labels on weakly augmented text samples and trains language models to predict the same pseudo labels on strongly augmented text samples.
SFLM outperforms state-of-the-art supervised and semi-supervised methods on sentence classification and sentence-pair classification tasks.
SFLM requires only a few in-domain unlabeled data for few-shot learning.
SFLM demonstrates robustness under variations in augmentation techniques, model scale, and few-shot knowledge transfer across tasks.
DialogueCSE uses a matching-guided embedding mechanism to generate context-aware embeddings from context-free candidate response embeddings using multi-turn context-response matching matrices.
DialogueCSE pairs context-aware embeddings with their context-free counterparts and minimizes contrastive loss across the pairs.
DialogueCSE outperforms baseline methods on multiple multi-turn dialogue datasets in sentence embedding evaluation.
DialogueCSE is robust to reductions in training data size.

Neural network agents in iterated language learning primarily strive to maintain the utterance type distribution observed during learning rather than developing a more efficient or systematic language.
Layerwise weight poisoning plants deeper backdoors in pre-trained models.
A combinatorial trigger cannot be easily detected.
Previous defense methods cannot resist layerwise weight poisoning attacks on text classification tasks.
GTR performs video grounding as a set prediction task.
GTR uses two encoders for video and language encoding and a cross-modal decoder for grounding prediction.
GTR uses a Cubic Embedding layer to transform raw videos into a set of visual tokens.
GTR uses Multi-head Cross-Modal Attention to fuse video and language modalities in the decoder.
GTR is optimized with a Many-to-One matching loss.
Large language-independent datasets of weakly similar pairs of informal texts are built from Twitter replies and quotes without manual human effort.
Four new sentence ranking evaluation benchmarks for informal texts are extracted from Twitter collections.
Embeddings trained on Twitter weakly similar pairs learn classical Semantic Textual Similarity and excel on non-paraphrase sentence ranking tasks.
Increasing the corpus size of Twitter weakly similar pairs positively influences embedding quality.
Larger collections of Twitter weakly similar pairs do not contain redundant information about semantic similarities.
A two-stage method for distantly supervised relation extraction identifies possible false negative samples by heuristically leveraging the memory mechanism of deep neural networks.
A two-stage method for distantly supervised relation extraction aligns false negative samples with training data into a unified feature space by adversarial training to assign pseudo labels.
Masked span prediction is more effective than auto-regressive language modeling for dialogue state tracking.
Pre-training on text summarization is effective for dialogue state tracking.
Recurrent state context representation for dialogue state tracking has difficulty recovering from earlier mistakes.
A joint model performs event-event temporal relation classification and predicts relative event time as real numbers.
Incorporating predicted relative event time for temporal relation classification via the Stack-Propagation framework maintains differentiability.
A joint model for event-event temporal relation extraction achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MATRES dataset.
Pseudo Zero Pronoun Resolution uses a pretraining task that trains masked language models on anaphoric relations with explicit supervision.
Pseudo Zero Pronoun Resolution uses a finetuning method that remedies the pretrain-finetune discrepancy.
Pseudo Zero Pronoun Resolution achieves state-of-the-art performance on Japanese zero anaphora resolution.
A detailed analysis of zero anaphora resolution provides new insights on the remaining challenges.
CAST hierarchically splits a large abstract syntax tree into subtrees for code summarization.
CAST encodes the subtrees of an abstract syntax tree using a recursive neural network for code summarization.
CAST reconstructs an abstract syntax tree from its subtree embeddings to obtain the complete tree representation for code summarization.
CAST uses the abstract syntax tree representation and source code embedding for code summarization.
LSP-Align automatically mines cross-lingual entity lexica from web data.
LSP-Align outperforms baseline methods at extracting cross-lingual entity pairs.
XLEnt is a large cross-lingual entity dataset containing entity pairs from many languages aligned with English.
A massively multilingual tagged named entity corpus contains named entity annotations for many languages.
Prototypical contrastive learning reconstructs the semantic constraints of labels for zero-shot cross-domain slot filling.
Dynamic label confusion strategy establishes label dependence between source domains and the target domain on-the-fly for zero-shot cross-domain slot filling.
Prototypical contrastive learning with dynamic label confusion strategy improves performance on unseen slots in zero-shot cross-domain slot filling.
Prototypical contrastive learning with dynamic label confusion strategy sets a new state-of-the-art for the slot filling task.
Cross-lingual entity alignment is redefined as an assignment problem.
SEU performs unsupervised entity alignment without neural networks.
SEU outperforms advanced supervised methods on all public datasets.
SEU has high efficiency, interpretability, and stability.
Meta-learning constructs prototypes for different entity types in few-shot named entity recognition.
Task-specific supervised pre-training on noisy web data extracts entity-related representations for few-shot named entity recognition.
Self-training leverages unlabeled in-domain data for few-shot named entity recognition.
Meta-learning, task-specific pre-training, and self-training for few-shot named entity recognition outperform a PLM-based linear classifier fine-tuned on domain labels.
Meta-learning, task-specific pre-training, and self-training for few-shot named entity recognition achieve state-of-the-art results in few-shot and training-free settings.

An alignment-free label projection method obtains high-quality pseudo-labeled data for cross-lingual aspect-based sentiment analysis by preserving more accurate task-specific knowledge using a translation system.
An aspect code-switching mechanism augments training data with code-switched bilingual sentences for cross-lingual aspect-based sentiment analysis.
Applying knowledge distillation to a cross-lingual aspect-based sentiment analysis model on unlabeled target language data achieves performance comparable to supervised methods.
Pre-finetuning is an additional massively multi-task learning stage between language model pre-training and fine-tuning.
Pre-finetuning consistently improves performance for both discriminative and generative language models on a wide range of natural language processing tasks.
Pre-finetuning improves sample efficiency during fine-tuning.
Pre-finetuning with too few tasks reduces model performance, but beyond a critical number of tasks performance improves linearly with the number of tasks.
A novel intention mechanism models deterministic entity knowledge.

Kernel-Smoothed Translation with Example Retrieval (KSTER) uses kernel smoothing and example retrieval for adapting neural machine translation models.
Kernel-Smoothed Translation with Example Retrieval (KSTER) achieves higher translation quality than existing online adaptation methods.
The first parallel dataset for schema translation includes headers in English, Chinese, French, German, Spanish, and Japanese.
CAST translates headers of tabular data using a header-to-header neural machine translation approach with schema context.
CAST models a target header and its context as a directed graph representing entity types and relations.
CAST encodes the directed graph with a relational-aware transformer and decodes the header using a transformer.
CAST outperforms state-of-the-art neural machine translation models on schema translation.
ConSeC conditions the disambiguation of a target word on the explicit senses assigned to nearby words.
ConSeC sets a new state of the art for English Word Sense Disambiguation.
ConSeC achieves significant improvements over prior systems in cross-lingual Word Sense Disambiguation for languages with varying resource availability.
Human rationales from a small fraction of crowdsourced training data serve as attribution priors to impart human-like rationalization to stance detection models.
Human rationales as attribution priors improve the reasoning of stance detection models for sarcastic inputs in data-scarce settings without reducing predictive performance.

In stance detection models using human rationales as attribution priors, attention weights provide more faithful explanations than a leading attribution method and are computationally cheap and reliable.
ActiveEA uses a structure-aware uncertainty sampling strategy that measures the uncertainty of each entity and its impact on neighboring entities in knowledge graphs.
ActiveEA uses a recognizer that identifies entities appearing in one knowledge graph but not the other while alleviating sampling bias.
ActiveEA improves sampling quality and generalizes across different datasets, entity alignment models, and amounts of entities that appear in one knowledge graph but not the other.
CRYPTOGRU is a hybrid structure of homomorphic encryption and garbled circuit for gated recurrent unit networks that enables low-latency secure inferences.
CRYPTOGRU replaces GC-based tanh activation with GC-based ReLU activation in gated recurrent units.
CRYPTOGRU quantizes sigmoid and ReLU activations to smaller bit-length in gated recurrent units.
CRYPTOGRU achieves lower secure inference latency than a state-of-the-art secure network.
Reading time data is consistent with a weakly super-linear effect of surprisal.
