Non-uniformity in information density is predictive of lower linguistic acceptability.
The strongest trend in information density uniformity is a regression towards a mean surprisal across the language, not at the phrase, sentence, or document level.
Transformers have high dynamic activation ranges that are difficult to represent with a low bit fixed-point format.
Transformer activations contain structured outliers in residual connections that encourage attention to the special separator token.
Per-embedding-group quantization is a quantization scheme for transformer embeddings.
Transformer weights and embeddings can be quantized to ultra-low bit-widths with minimal accuracy loss and significant memory savings.
Neuro-symbolic reinforcement learning with first-order logic for text-based games extracts first-order logical facts from text observations and ConceptNet.
Neuro-symbolic reinforcement learning with first-order logic for text-based games trains a policy using directly interpretable logical operators.
Neuro-symbolic reinforcement learning with first-order logic for text-based games converges faster than state-of-the-art neuro-symbolic methods in the TextWorld benchmark.
Mirror-BERT quickly converts Masked Language Models into universal lexical and sentence encoders via self-supervision without additional data.
Mirror-BERT uses identical and slightly modified string pairs as positive examples for fine-tuning to maximize their similarity.
Mirror-BERT achieves better performance than off-the-shelf Masked Language Models on lexical-level and sentence-level tasks across multiple domains and languages.
Mirror-BERT matches the performance of Sentence-BERT on sentence similarity and question-answer entailment tasks without using annotated task data.
SaLoss is an auxiliary loss function that aligns the multi-head attention mechanism with salient information extracted from the training data using TextRank.
SaLoss yields more faithful explanations than vanilla BERT across multiple feature attribution methods.
Rationales from SaLoss models lead to higher predictive performance in downstream tasks than rationales from vanilla BERT when used to train inherently faithful classifiers.
Musubu enriches taxonomies in low-resource settings by using pretrained language models as knowledge bases.
Musubu uses a classifier based on pretrained language models to identify hierarchical relationships between term pairs.
Musubu uses Hearst patterns to generate queries that leverage implicit knowledge from pretrained language models.
PairSupCon uses instance discrimination to bridge semantic entailment and contradiction understanding with high-level categorical concept encoding.
PairSupCon outperforms the state-of-the-art on clustering tasks.
PairSupCon outperforms the state-of-the-art on semantic textual similarity tasks.
Code-switched inspired losses learn generic multilingual spoken dialogue representations.
A pretraining corpus of multilingual conversations in French, Italian, English, German, and Spanish is automatically constructed from OpenSubtitles.
MIAM is a benchmark for dialogue act classification and two novel multilingual tasks: multilingual mask utterance retrieval and multilingual inconsistency identification.
Different techniques for incorporating medical knowledge into BERT for clinical relation extraction are evaluated on the i2b2/VA 2010 clinical relation extraction benchmark.
Incorporating medical knowledge into BERT improves clinical relation extraction performance on the i2b2/VA 2010 clinical relation extraction benchmark.
Policy compliance detection is decomposed into question answering to check whether policy conditions apply to a scenario and an expression tree to combine answers for the label.
Question answering decomposition for policy compliance detection achieves better accuracy when testing on policies unseen during training.
Question answering decomposition for policy compliance detection explicitly identifies missing information when policy compliance cannot be determined.
Annotation of question answering decomposition for policy compliance detection achieves improved inter-annotator agreement and speed, offsetting the initial annotation cost.
LERGV retrieves logic-level program-like evidence from tables and statements.
LERGV constructs a logic-level graph to capture logical relations between entities and functions in the retrieved evidence.
LERGV uses a graph-based verification network to perform logic-level graph-based reasoning for entailment classification.
A multitask learning approach combines discourse datasets from similar and diverse domains to improve discourse classification.
A multitask learning approach improves discourse classification on the NewsDiscourse dataset.
Label correlations across tasks improve performance for underrepresented classes in discourse classification.
Existing techniques for resource-poor problems in NLP do not improve classification accuracy in discourse classification with class-imbalanced datasets.
Instance-adaptive training frameworks set noise resistance hyperparameters per instance for noise-robust losses.
Instance-level label quality predictors predict noise resistance hyperparameters per instance and are trained jointly with main classification models.
Instance-adaptive training frameworks increase the noise-robustness of models trained with noise-robust losses for natural language processing tasks with label noise.
ContrAstive Pre-Training (CAPT) learns noise invariant sequence representations.
ContrAstive Pre-Training (CAPT) encourages consistency between representations of an original sequence and its corrupted version using unsupervised instance-wise training signals.
ContrAstive Pre-Training (CAPT) alleviates the pretrain-finetune discrepancy induced by noise in pre-training.
ContrAstive Pre-Training (CAPT) aids in capturing global semantics of the input via more effective sentence-level supervision.
ContrAstive Pre-Training (CAPT) is applicable to both language and vision-language tasks and provides consistent improvement on natural language understanding and cross-modal tasks.
Question answering models for numerical machine reading comprehension fail to extrapolate to numbers that are out of the training range.
The E-digit number form represents numbers as digit-by-digit input and alleviates the lack of extrapolation in numerical machine reading comprehension.
Numerical machine reading comprehension requires numbers to be treated differently from regular words in text.
Two methods, one using reinforcement learning, automatically trigger dialog models to generate problematic responses.
State-of-the-art dialog models exhibit safety and contradiction issues when triggered by automatic methods.
Set Generation Networks address end-to-end knowledge base population as a direct set generation problem to avoid considering the order of multiple facts.
Set Generation Networks use transformers with non-autoregressive parallel decoding.
Set Generation Networks use a set-based loss that forces unique predictions via bipartite matching.
The bipartite matching loss in Set Generation Networks is invariant to any permutation of predictions.
Set Generation Networks achieve state-of-the-art performance on two benchmark datasets for knowledge base population.
Contrastive domain adaptation for QA (CAQA) combines question generation and domain-invariant learning to answer out-of-domain questions with limited text corpora.

Contrastive domain adaptation for QA (CAQA) trains a question answering system on source data and generated target domain data using a contrastive adaptation loss in settings with limited text corpora.
An efficient weak-supervised adversarial data augmentation approach generates a factual consistency dataset for abstractive summarization.
Gradient-Based Adversarial Factual Consistency Evaluation for Abstractive Summarization makes accurate and robust factual consistency discrimination.
Gradient-Based Adversarial Factual Consistency Evaluation for Abstractive Summarization traces factual errors interpretably by backpropagated gradient distribution on token embeddings.
Eigenthemes is a light-weight and scalable entity linking method that relies solely on entity names and a referent knowledge base.
Eigenthemes identifies the low-rank subspace in which gold entities lie using singular value decomposition and scores candidate entities by their proximity to that subspace.
Multiple new strong baseline methods for entity linking compare favorably to and sometimes outperform the existing state of the art.
Entity-Agnostic Representation Learning (EARL) learns category representations for entities by utilizing conversation context and the relational structure of knowledge graphs.
Entity-Agnostic Representation Learning (EARL) incorporates unseen entities in knowledge graphs into conversation generation.
Entity-Agnostic Representation Learning (EARL) generates more informative, coherent, and natural responses than baseline models.
Informational fusion quantifies the degree of fusion of morphological features in surface forms without requiring morpheme segmentation for both concatenative and nonconcatenative systems.
Informational fusion recapitulates linguistic classifications for concatenative morphological systems.
Informational fusion measures morphological fusion in nonconcatenative morphological systems.
The degree of fusion varies across part-of-speech within languages.
Intent-Guided Assistant (IGA) generates and rephrases text according to fine-grained author specifications.
Intent-Guided Assistant (IGA) uses text interspersed with tags corresponding to specific rhetorical directives such as adding description, contrast, or rephrasing a sentence.
Intent-Guided Assistant (IGA) is trained on a heuristically-labeled dataset of author intent.
The quality of Intent-Guided Assistant (IGA) generated outputs is confirmed by automatic and crowdsourced evaluations.
Authors prefer Intent-Guided Assistant (IGA) over baseline methods for creative writing tasks.
ARMAN uses a modified semantic score for selecting and masking salient sentences to form pseudo summaries in Persian abstractive summarization.
ARMAN uses modified sentence reordering for Persian abstractive summarization.
ARMAN achieves state-of-the-art performance on Persian summarization tasks.
ARMAN outperforms prior works on textual entailment, question paraphrasing, and multiple choice question answering tasks.
The modified semantic score improves Persian abstractive summarization results according to human evaluation.
Three linguistically motivated transformations to Universal Dependencies produce more cross-lingually stable dependency schemes.
Cross-lingual stability of syntactic relations is strongly related to zero-shot dependency parsing performance.
FAME uses an attention function guided by features reflecting word-specific properties such as shape and frequency.
FAME uses adversarial training to map differently-sized embeddings to the same space.
Feature-guided attention in meta-embeddings is beneficial for handling subword-based embeddings.
FAME sets the new state of the art for part-of-speech tagging in 27 languages, named entity recognition in various settings, and question classification in different domains.
A hybrid architecture augments GPT-2 with Graph Attention Network representations to enable causal, sequential prediction of slot values in dialogue state tracking.
A hybrid architecture captures inter-slot relationships and dependencies across domains in dialogue state tracking.
A simplified sparse training scenario for dialogue state tracking trains models on session-level annotations and evaluates at the turn level.
Graph modules capture inter-slot dependencies in dialogue state tracking.
Graph modules improve predictions for slot values common to multiple domains in dialogue state tracking.
Human evaluation scores on multiple criteria for NLG tasks are often not correlated.
Evaluating NLG metrics solely by correlation with human scores on overall quality is inadequate.
No single automatic evaluation metric correlates well with human scores on all desirable criteria for most NLG tasks.
Perturbation CheckLists perturb NLG outputs to affect only one specific evaluation criteria.
Existing NLG evaluation metrics are not robust to perturbations that affect only one specific evaluation criteria and disagree with human scores on perturbed outputs.
Annotation distribution schemes assign multiple labels per example for a small subset of training examples.
Distributing annotation budget unevenly by assigning multiple labels to a subset of examples improves performance on natural language inference and entity typing tasks.
Extending the MixUp data augmentation framework enables learning from training examples with zero, one, or multiple labels.
Learning from training examples with zero, one, or multiple labels improves performance in low annotation budget and cross-domain settings.
STEL evaluates style measures using a modular, fine-grained, content-controlled, and similarity-based approach.
BERT-based style measures outperform simple style measures based on 3-grams, punctuation frequency, and LIWC.
A unified framework quantitatively evaluates the interpretability of multi-hop reasoning models.
Path recall, local interpretability, and global interpretability are metrics for evaluating multi-hop reasoning interpretability.
An approximate strategy calculates the interpretability metrics using the interpretability scores of rules.
A benchmark for multi-hop reasoning interpretability evaluation includes manually annotated rules.
Many paths generated by multi-hop reasoning models are unreasonable.
Current multi-hop reasoning models have less satisfactory interpretability relative to the upper bound of the multi-hop reasoning interpretability benchmark.
Rule-based models outperform multi-hop reasoning models in performance and interpretability.

Multi-Layer Revision Network (MLRN) alleviates word-level noise in distantly supervised relation extraction by emphasizing inner-sentence correlations prior to extracting relation information within sentences.
Confidence-based Multi-Instance Learning (CMIL) filters noisy sentences and assigns proper weights to relevant sentences in distantly supervised relation extraction.
Visual News Captioner is an entity-aware model for news image captioning.
Visual News is a large-scale benchmark of over one million news images with associated news articles, image captions, author information, and metadata.
Visual News Captioner uses novel multi-modal feature fusion techniques for named entity generation.
Visual News Captioner uses novel attention mechanisms for named entity generation.
The Visual News dataset highlights remaining challenges in captioning news images.
Global document semantics are captured by a neural topic model with normalizing flow and integrated into a text summarization model.
Controlling the amount of global semantics supplied to the text generation module prevents global semantics from overwhelming the contextualized representation.
Paragraph attributions in multiple-choice Machine Reading Comprehension are assessed using post-hoc interpretation methods.
Punishing illogical attributions improves multiple-choice Machine Reading Comprehension model performance without requiring external information or model structure changes.
A self-training method that punishes illogical attributions in multiple-choice Machine Reading Comprehension is analyzed to explain how and why it works.
An unsupervised sentence embedding framework eliminates the need for parallel corpora.

An unsupervised sentence embedding framework uses a multi-task loss function to capture semantic similarity and relatedness between sentences for training a dual encoder model that maps different languages to the same vector space.
An unsupervised sentence embedding framework surpasses supervised state-of-the-art methods for certain under-resourced languages on the Tatoeba dataset and a monolingual benchmark.
An unsupervised sentence embedding framework shows enhanced zero-shot learning capabilities for more than 30 languages when trained on only 13 languages.
Translation-based supervision trains an agent to detect the minimum number of reads required for generating each target token in simultaneous machine translation.
Translation-based supervision generates oracle action sequences by comparing simultaneous translations against full-sentence translations during training.
Translation-based supervision uses a training objective that is easier to train than reinforcement learning techniques in simultaneous machine translation.
Translation-based supervision produces higher quality translations and minimizes average lag in simultaneous machine translation.
Random swapping and deletion perturb discourse relations inside conversations for semi-supervised abstractive conversation summarization.
Dialogue-acts-guided insertion interrupts the development of conversations for semi-supervised abstractive conversation summarization.
Conditional-generation-based substitution substitutes utterances with paraphrases generated based on the conversation context for semi-supervised abstractive conversation summarization.
A mutual guidance framework utilizes the information of an argument in one passage to guide the identification of arguments that form pairs with it in another passage.
An inter-sentence relation graph models the inter-relations between two sentences to facilitate argument pair extraction.
SituatedQA is an open-retrieval question answering dataset that requires systems to produce the correct answer given the temporal or geographical context.
A significant proportion of information seeking questions have context-dependent answers.
Existing question answering models struggle with producing answers that are frequently updated or from uncommon locations.
Existing question answering models fail to generalize to answering questions asked in the present even when provided with an updated evidence corpus.
HRKD captures hierarchical and domain relational information for cross-domain language model compression.
HRKD sets up domain-relational graphs using meta-learning to capture relational information across different domains.
HRKD uses a hierarchical compare-aggregate mechanism to capture hierarchical relationships.
SPECTRA extracts rationales deterministically via constrained inference on a factor graph, forming a differentiable layer.
SPECTRA simplifies training and rationale regularization for rationale extraction.
SPECTRA achieves better performance and plausibility of extracted rationales than previous methods.

A comparative study of stochastic and deterministic rationale extraction methods evaluates predictive power, explanation quality, and model variability for classification and natural language inference tasks.
ShadowLink evaluates the impact of prior probability bias on entity linking systems.
Popular entity linking systems exhibit lower accuracy on less common entities compared to more common entities.
The trigger is a confounder of the context and the event detection result in few-shot event detection.
Intervening on the context via backdoor adjustment resolves the trigger curse in few-shot event detection.
ESTER is a machine reading comprehension dataset containing natural language queries for reasoning about the five most common event semantic relations.
Current state-of-the-art systems perform significantly below human performance on ESTER.
A combination of cross-lingual strategies outperforms any single cross-lingual strategy for zero-shot cross-lingual information extraction.

A combination of cross-lingual strategies supports event extraction, named entity recognition, part-of-speech tagging, and dependency parsing in English-to-Arabic zero-shot cross-lingual information extraction.
No single set of cross-lingual techniques performs best across all information extraction tasks.
A methodology develops automatic frameworks that extract material solubility data from scientific articles.
A training and evaluation dataset contains solute names, solubility values, and solubility units tagged by manual annotation and regular expressions.
A novel pretraining approach leverages automated chemical name and quantity extraction to generate large training datasets without intensive manual tagging.
An analysis identifies causes of classification errors in solubility-related entity recognition.
Tribrid incorporates automatically generated negated perspectives as input for stance classification.
Tribrid uses a weakly supervised method to combine multiple predictions for stance classification.
Tribrid achieves human-like performance on a sizable portion of the input in stance classification by using confidence scores to remove doubtful predictions.
A systematic benchmark compares defense approaches against adversarial word substitution with a consistent attacking setting and representative attacks.
A defense method achieves higher accuracy on clean examples and examples under adversarial word substitution than existing approaches for neural text classification.
A benchmark dataset of health science press release-abstract pairs for exaggeration detection is curated.
MT-PET is a multi-task version of Pattern Exploiting Training that leverages knowledge from complementary cloze-style QA tasks for few-shot learning.
MT-PET outperforms Pattern Exploiting Training and supervised learning in exaggeration detection under limited and abundant data conditions.
Word embedding models are extended to capture semantic and cultural characteristics for discovering multifaceted ideological and worldview characteristics of communities.
Online communities exhibit complex ideological differences across multiple axes of polarization.
PhoMT is a high-quality and large-scale Vietnamese-English parallel dataset.

Among the methods evaluated, fine-tuning the pre-trained sequence-to-sequence denoising auto-encoder mBART achieves the best performance on Vietnamese-English machine translation according to both automatic and human evaluations on the PhoMT dataset.
Binary masks over transformer weights or hidden units uncover subsets of features that correlate with a specific factor of variation.
Binary masks eliminate the need to train disentangled models from scratch for a particular task.
Combining masking with magnitude pruning identifies sparse subnetworks within BERT that strongly encode specific aspects while only weakly encode other aspects.
Disentanglement-via-masking performs as well as or better than variational autoencoders and adversarial training methods for disentangling text representations.
ChemNER uses flexible KB-matching to generate distant labels for fine-grained chemistry named entity recognition.
ChemNER uses ontology-guided multi-type disambiguation to generate distant labels for fine-grained chemistry named entity recognition.
An expert-labeled chemistry named entity recognition dataset contains 62 fine-grained chemistry types including chemical compounds and chemical reactions.
An end-to-end graph-based neural model jointly performs frame semantic parsing.
An end-to-end graph-based neural model for frame semantic parsing represents predicates and roles as graph nodes and their relations as graph edges.
An end-to-end graph-based neural model for frame semantic parsing achieves better performance than pipeline models.
Methods for k-nearest neighbors language models reduce inference overhead while maintaining comparable performance.
A straightforward reference-less grammatical error correction metric is strongly correlated with human judgement.
A straightforward reference-less grammatical error correction metric does not require an annotated dataset for fine-tuning.
A straightforward reference-less grammatical error correction metric relies solely on commonly available tools.
Optimization-based methods for adversarial text attacks do not improve success rates in constrained setups.
Optimization-based methods for adversarial text attacks slightly benefit from approximate gradient information in unconstrained setups with larger search spaces.
Simple heuristics that exploit nearest neighbors without querying the target function achieve high success rates in constrained setups for adversarial text attacks with substantially fewer queries.

Simple heuristics that exploit nearest neighbors without querying the target function achieve near-perfect success rates in unconstrained setups for adversarial text attacks with substantially fewer queries.
TextAttack benchmark tasks for adversarial text attacks are too easy and constraints are too strict, preventing meaningful research on black-box adversarial text attacks.
RAST reduces the search space for dialogue rewriting while maintaining coverage of the core task.
RAST injects loss signals from BLEU or GPT-2 under a REINFORCE framework to alleviate fluency issues in dialogue rewriting.
RAST achieves better performance than state-of-the-art systems in cross-dataset transfer for dialogue rewriting.

SixT achieves zero-shot cross-lingual transfer in neural machine translation by training on one language pair and leveraging an off-the-shelf multilingual pretrained encoder with a two-stage training schedule.
SixT outperforms mBART on zero-shot any-to-English neural machine translation across multiple source languages.
SixT achieves better performance than CRISS and m2m-100 on any-to-English neural machine translation with reduced training computation cost and training data.
Mining alias entities from knowledge bases provides equivalent answers for open-domain question answering.
Answer expansion increases the exact match score for evaluation on Natural Questions, TriviaQA, and SQuAD.
Incorporating equivalent answers improves model training for open-domain question answering on real-world datasets.
Human evaluation confirms the validity of equivalent answers for open-domain question answering.
A weak supervision approach adapts a discourse segmenter trained on written text to French oral conversation transcripts using minimal annotation.
Supervision for discourse segmentation is provided by a latent model bootstrapped by manually defined heuristic rules that use linguistic and acoustic information.
Adapting a discourse segmenter trained on written text to oral conversations via weak supervision improves segmentation in contexts with noisy or lacking speaker turn information.
WordPiece tokenization of single words can be done in O(n) time.
Additional linkages on the vocabulary trie enable smart transitions when trie matching cannot continue.
WordPiece tokenization of general text is done in a single pass combining pre-tokenization and linear-time tokenization.
WordPiece tokenization of general text is faster than HuggingFace Tokenizers and TensorFlow Text.
Lite2Pyramid uses human-labeled Summary Content Units for references and a natural language inference model to judge the presence of these units in system summaries.
Lite3Pyramid substitutes human-labeled Summary Content Units with automatically extracted Semantic Triplet Units using a semantic role labeling model.

Lite2.xPyramid uses a regressor to predict the simulation quality of Semantic Triplet Units for Summary Content Units and retains difficult Summary Content Units to balance automation and manual evaluation.
PyrXSum is a meta-evaluation dataset for summary evaluation based on the XSum dataset.
Lite2Pyramid achieves the highest human-metric correlation at the summary level.
Lite2.xPyramid reduces manual effort with only small drops in human-metric correlation.
Instance bundles of related question-answer pairs are mined from existing data or created using automated heuristics.

Instance bundle training normalizes question-answer scores across neighborhoods of contrasting questions and/or answers and adds a cross entropy loss term in addition to maximum likelihood estimation.
Instance bundle training improves accuracy on HotpotQA and ROPES.
NewsCLIPpings contains unmanipulated but mismatched image-text pairs in news.
NewsCLIPpings provides challenging image-text mismatch samples in news that mislead humans.
Automatic image retrieval strategies capture inconsistent entities or semantic context to retrieve convincing mismatched images for captions.
Simultaneous translation is formulated as a structural sequence-to-sequence learning problem.
A latent variable models read or translate actions at every time step and is integrated out to consider all possible translation policies.
A re-parameterised Poisson prior regularizes translation policies to balance translation quality and latency.
Conditional probing measures usable information contained in neural representations but not in a baseline representation.
Conditional probing conditions on the information in a baseline representation.
After conditioning on non-contextual word embeddings, linguistic properties such as part-of-speech are accessible at deeper layers of neural networks than previously thought.
Joint health outcome span detection and classification uses word-level and sentence-level information.
Label attention appropriately weights word-level and sentence-level information for health outcome detection.
Joint health outcome span detection and classification consistently outperforms decoupled methods on benchmark datasets.
A reinterpretation of the Distributional Inclusion Hypothesis models entailment between predicates of different valencies.
Multivalent Entailment Graphs model entailment relations among open-domain natural language predicates without supervision.
Directional entailment is more helpful for inference than non-directional similarity on questions of fine-grained semantics.
Evidence from multiple predicate valencies answers more questions than evidence from a single predicate valency.
PICARD constrains auto-regressive decoders of language models through incremental parsing.
PICARD finds valid output sequences by rejecting inadmissible tokens at each decoding step.
PICARD enables fine-tuned T5 models to achieve state-of-the-art performance on Spider and CoSQL text-to-SQL tasks.
Model-agnostic instance attribution methods select adversarial deletions for data poisoning attacks on Knowledge Graph Embedding models for link prediction.
A heuristic method replaces one entity in influential triples to generate adversarial additions for data poisoning attacks on Knowledge Graph Embedding models for link prediction.
Data poisoning attack strategies based on instance attribution methods cause more model failure in link prediction than state-of-the-art attacks on Knowledge Graph Embedding models.
BERT generalizes well to subject–verb pairs that never occurred in training.
The absolute frequency of a verb form and its frequency relative to the alternate inflection are causally implicated in BERT's predictions at inference time.
BERT's behavior is consistent with correctly applying the subject-verb agreement rule in general.
BERT struggles to estimate agreement features (singular vs. plural) on infrequent lexical items.
An autoencoder language model with low reconstruction loss may not provide good sequence representations because the decoder may take shortcuts by exploiting language patterns.
A self-learning method for autoencoder pre-training uses a weak decoder with restricted capacity and attention flexibility to produce better text representations.
Pre-training autoencoders with a weak decoder improves dense retrieval effectiveness and few-shot ability on web search, news recommendation, and open domain question answering.
A distribution-balanced loss function addresses both class imbalance and label linkage in multi-label text classification with long-tailed class distribution.
A distribution-balanced loss function outperforms commonly used loss functions in multi-label text classification with long-tailed class distribution.
Deep Attention Diffusion Graph Neural Networks (DADGNN) bridge interaction difficulties between a word and its distant neighbors in text.
Coarse-to-fine grained classification enables fine-grained text classification from coarsely annotated training data.
Label surface names provide the only human guidance for fine-grained text classification from coarsely annotated data.
A label-conditioned fine-tuning formulation adapts pre-trained generative language models to generate pseudo-training data for fine-grained text classification from coarsely annotated data.
A regularization objective applies constraints between coarse and fine labels to the label-conditioned fine-tuning formulation for generating pseudo-training data.
A fully unsupervised k-means clustering approach learns stereotypical character roles from structural plot information.
A fully unsupervised k-means clustering approach induces six of Propp's seven dramatis personae and an additional category for minor characters from structural plot information in Russian folktales.
Plot functions, unigrams, tf-idf weights, and embeddings over coreference chain heads form the best feature set for learning stereotypical character roles from structural plot information.
In stereotypical character role induction, frequently mentioned roles and roles with distinct plot patterns are more strongly differentiated than less frequent or distinct roles.
The quality of coreference chain and plot functions annotations is critical for stereotypical character role induction from structural plot information.
Standard metrics are incapable of measuring progress on numerical reasoning tasks in machine reading comprehension.
Subpar time allocation has been a major obstacle for NLP research in recent years.
GFST uses a source monolingual corpus and an initial model to generate gender-specific pseudo-parallel corpora which are then filtered and added to the training data.
GFST improves gender translation accuracy without damaging generic quality.
GFST is viable for re-training from scratch, fine-tuning, controlling the gender balance of the data, forward translation, and back-translation.
A Pointer Network generates continuous token arrangements for input sentences.
A bijective function recovers the original token order from a continuous token arrangement.
Pointer Network reordering for discontinuous parsing achieves comparable accuracy to state-of-the-art discontinuous parsers while being faster.
Unchanged fixes (predicted code identical to the input buggy code) occur in neural machine translation-based bug fixing due to high similarity between buggy and fixed code.
Fix-Filter-Fix (F^3) connects bug-fixing models with a filter mechanism that removes unchanged fixes (predicted code identical to the input buggy code) between consecutive models.
The F^3 theory quantitatively and accurately calculates the F^3 lifting effect.
F^3 is general across multiple bug-fixing datasets and model combinations.
Models for commonsense reasoning resort to non-robust features in many prominent commonsense reasoning benchmarks.
Models for commonsense reasoning do not learn transferable generalizations for the intended tasks in many prominent commonsense reasoning benchmarks.

Recurrent Neural Network Grammars with left-corner parsing outperform Recurrent Neural Network Grammars with top-down parsing and Long Short-Term Memory models on human reading times for Japanese head-final left-branching structures.
Hierarchical architectures with left-corner parsing are more cognitively plausible than top-down hierarchical architectures or sequential architectures for modeling human sentence processing.
A language model-based generative classifier treats labels for discourse tasks as input and enhances label representations by embedding label descriptions.
A language model-based generative classifier allows pre-trained language models to be used for discourse task labels that were not present in the pre-training data.
A language model-based generative classifier achieves state-of-the-art performance in discourse segmentation.

A language model-based generative classifier achieves state-of-the-art performance in sentence-level discourse parsing when using both gold-standard and automatically generated discourse segmentations.
Fine-grained semantic novelty detection identifies novel natural language scene descriptions given a set of normal natural language scene descriptions.
GAT-MA identifies novel natural language scene descriptions given a set of normal natural language scene descriptions.
A new dataset provides natural language scene descriptions for fine-grained semantic novelty detection.
GAT-MA outperforms multiple existing methods in identifying novel natural language scene descriptions given normal scene descriptions.
Non-target heads exhibit emergent behaviour that can explain the target task or generalize beyond the original task.
Multi-task training leads to non-trivial extrapolation of skills that can be harnessed for interpretability and generalization.
ClassKG exploits keyword-keyword correlation using a keyword graph and graph neural networks.
ClassKG transforms pseudo-label assignment into keyword subgraph annotation.
ClassKG uses a self-supervised task to pretrain a subgraph annotator and finetunes it.
ClassKG outperforms existing methods for weakly-supervised text classification on long-text and short-text datasets.
Off-target translation is dominant in strong multilingual NMT systems trained on massive multilingual corpora.
Auxiliary target language prediction retains target language information in multilingual NMT decoder outputs.
Representation and gradient regularization reduces off-target translation occurrences in multilingual NMT.
Representation and gradient regularization improves zero-shot translation performance in multilingual NMT.
Representation and gradient regularization works well without direct data.
ReasonBert enables language models to reason over long-range relations and multiple, possibly hybrid contexts.
ReasonBert uses generalized distant supervision to create pre-training examples requiring long-range reasoning by connecting multiple pieces of text and tables.
ReasonBert creates pre-training examples that require intersecting multiple pieces of evidence, bridging from one piece of evidence to another, and detecting unanswerable cases.
ReasonBert outperforms strong baselines on extractive question answering tasks requiring single-hop reasoning, multi-hop reasoning, text-only reasoning, table-only reasoning, and hybrid reasoning.
ReasonBert improves sample efficiency in few-shot extractive question answering.
BARThez is the first large-scale pretrained sequence-to-sequence model for French.
BARThez is well-suited for generative tasks.
OrangeSum is a novel summarization dataset.
BARThez achieves competitive performance with state-of-the-art BERT-based French language models on the FLUE benchmark.
mBARThez is a multilingual BART model further pretrained on BARThez' corpus.
mBARThez improves the generative performance of BARThez.
Style Pooling obfuscates stylistic features of human-generated text through style transfer by automatically re-writing the text.
Style Pooling enables a minimal notion of obfuscated style that intersects the various styles seen in training.
Style Pooling enables a maximal notion of obfuscated style that adds stylistic features of all sensitive attributes to text, computing a union of styles.
Syntactic supervision facilitates cross-lingual semantic role labeling in low-resource scenarios.
Question answering model accuracy does not decrease based on the gender or nationality of people in questions.
Question answering model accuracy varies based on the profession of the person in the question.
Question answering model accuracy varies based on question ambiguity.
Joint training of differentiable Dense Passage Retrieval and answer generation enables end-to-end training for Outside-Knowledge Visual Question Answering.
New diagnostic metrics analyze the interaction between retrieval and generation in Outside-Knowledge Visual Question Answering systems.

Joint training of retrieval and generation in Outside-Knowledge Visual Question Answering reduces the number of retrieved documents required for training while improving answer quality and reducing computational requirements.
A method transfers knowledge from a large-scale pretraining on facts from unstructured text to a specific domain knowledge base without entity or relation matching.
A method works for both canonicalized and uncanonicalized knowledge bases.
A method outperforms previous methods on small knowledge base completion datasets without using large pre-trained language models.
A new dataset contains open-domain dialogues with clarifying questions for single- and multi-turn conversations.
State-of-the-art neural baselines are evaluated for asking clarifying questions in open-domain dialogues.
A pipeline with offline and online steps evaluates the quality of clarifying questions in dialogues.
A model trained with self-supervised learning for multi-participant response selection on unannotated web data performs zero-shot dialogue disentanglement.
Fine-tuning the self-supervised model for zero-shot dialogue disentanglement with a small amount of labeled data achieves performance comparable to using the full dataset.
Synthetic data augmentation for Zero-Shot Cross-lingual Question Answering uses Question Generation models to produce cross-lingual synthetic samples without requiring additional annotated data.
Synthetic data augmentation for Zero-Shot Cross-lingual Question Answering outperforms baselines trained on English data only.
Synthetic data augmentation for Zero-Shot Cross-lingual Question Answering achieves state-of-the-art results on MLQA, XQuAD, SQuAD-it, and PIAF.
Challenges to cross-domain AMR parsing mainly arise from the distribution shift of words and AMR concepts.
An approach reduces the domain distribution divergence of text features.
An approach reduces the domain distribution divergence of AMR features.
Sentiment Analysis Enhanced COmparative Network (SAECON) uses domain adaptive knowledge transfer to learn sentiments to individual entities for comparative preference classification.
Sentiment Analysis Enhanced COmparative Network (SAECON) improves comparative preference classification performance over existing approaches.
Massive cross-author comparison identifies and encodes stylistic features of idiolects.
Idiolectal style representations exhibit regularities that encode qualitative and quantitative shifts.
The relative contributions of linguistic elements to idiolectal variation are measured.
Idiolectal variation is distinctive yet consistent.
Model criticism in latent space evaluates high-level structure in long-form generated text.
Different generative processes identify specific failure modes of language models.
Transformer-based language models capture topical structures but have difficulty maintaining structural coherence and modeling coreference.

Relation-augmented training for question answering over knowledge bases uses relation extraction, relation matching, and relation reasoning to align natural language expressions to knowledge base relations and reason over missing connections.
Relation-augmented training for question answering over knowledge bases outperforms existing methods when the knowledge base is incomplete.
Each key in the feed-forward layers of transformer-based language models correlates with textual patterns in the training examples.

Each value in the feed-forward layers of transformer-based language models induces output distributions that concentrate probability mass on tokens likely to appear immediately after each input pattern, particularly in upper layers.
The patterns learned by the feed-forward layers in transformer-based language models are human-interpretable.
In transformer-based language models, lower feed-forward layers capture shallow patterns while upper feed-forward layers capture more semantic patterns.
The output of a feed-forward layer in a transformer-based language model is a composition of its key-value memories.
The composition of key-value memories in a feed-forward layer is refined via residual connections across layers to produce the final output distribution.
Better neural machine translation systems lead to fewer post-editing changes in English-to-Czech professional translation.
The relationship between neural machine translation system quality and post-editing time is not straightforward in English-to-Czech professional translation.
BLEU is not a stable predictor of post-editing time or final output quality for neural machine translation systems in English-to-Czech professional translation.
Aspect category sentiment analysis tasks are cast into natural language generation tasks using natural language sentences to represent the output.

Casting aspect category sentiment analysis into natural language generation allows more direct use of pre-trained knowledge in sequence-to-sequence language models by directly following the task setting during pre-training.
Casting aspect category sentiment analysis into natural language generation achieves the best reported results on several benchmarks.
Casting aspect category sentiment analysis into natural language generation has advantages in few-shot and zero-shot settings.

GUI-based task-oriented dialogue system (GUI-TOD) directly performs graphical user interface operations on real applications and executes tasks without invoking task-oriented dialogue-specific backend APIs.
META-GUI is a dataset for training multi-modal conversational agents on mobile graphical user interfaces.
A multi-model action prediction and response model predicts actions and generates responses for multi-modal conversational agents on mobile graphical user interfaces.
DIALKI leverages document structure to provide dialogue-contextualized passage encodings.
DIALKI uses an auxiliary loss to capture the history of dialogue-document connections.
DIALKI generalizes to unseen documents.
DIALKI generalizes to long dialogue contexts.
StrokeNet represents Chinese characters by Latinized stroke sequences.
StrokeNet maps each stroke of a Chinese character to a specific Latin character, enabling similar Chinese characters to have similar Latin representations.
StrokeNet enables shared subword vocabulary learning and ciphertext-based data augmentation for non-Latin languages in neural machine translation.
StrokeNet improves neural machine translation performance with fewer model parameters.
A contrastive retrieval algorithm for translation memories avoids redundancy and uninformativeness of similar translation memory entries.
A Hierarchical Group Attention module gathers both local context of each translation memory and global context of the whole translation memory set.
A Multi-TM contrastive learning objective learns salient features of each translation memory with respect to the target sentence.
Bayesian Topic Regression (BTR) allows estimation of discrete and continuous treatment effects.
Bayesian Topic Regression (BTR) allows inclusion of numerical confounding factors alongside text data.
Bayesian Topic Regression (BTR) performs supervised representation learning for text features jointly with regression parameter training while respecting the Frisch-Waugh-Lovell theorem.
Bayesian Topic Regression (BTR) recovers ground truth with lower bias than benchmark models when text and numerical features are correlated.
Bayesian Topic Regression (BTR) yields superior prediction results compared to separate estimation of regression weights for text and non-text features.
A 5-class classification task reflects the legislative bill generation process.
The human-labeled dataset provides subsection-level bill-to-bill relationships.
Synthetic data mimics the legislative bill writing process with varying degrees of similarity.
Multi-stage training sequentially fine-tunes BERT variants with synthetic and human-labeled datasets.
Training with both human-labeled and synthetic data improves performance for bill-to-bill relationship classification.
Bill-to-bill similarities can be captured at multiple levels of aggregation.
An unsupervised topic modelling method uses Topological Data Analysis to extract a topological skeleton of the manifold of contextualised word embeddings.
An unsupervised topic modelling method constructs a network of coherent topics with meaningful inter-topic relationships.
Several techniques for improving non-autoregressive translation are compared for translation quality and speed in third-party testing environments.
Length prediction and CTC-based architecture variants establish strong baselines for non-autoregressive translation.
Standardized BLEU, chrF++, and TER scores using sacreBLEU address tokenized BLEU evaluation inconsistencies for four translation tasks.
A template-based method for constrained neural machine translation rearranges the generation of constrained and unconstrained tokens using a template.
A template-based method for constrained neural machine translation has inference speed comparable to unconstrained neural machine translation models.
A template-based method for constrained neural machine translation does not require changes to the model architecture or the decoding algorithm.
A template-based method for constrained neural machine translation outperforms representative baselines in lexically and structurally constrained translation tasks.
A modular framework incorporates lemma constraints in neural machine translation with flexible application of linguistic knowledge and diverse neural machine translation models.
A rule-based cross-lingual inflection module inflects target lemma constraints based on source context in neural machine translation.
English-German health and English-Lithuanian news test suites evaluate lemma constraint incorporation in domain adaptation and low-resource machine translation settings.
A rule-based inflection module incorporates lemma constraints more accurately than a neural inflection module in neural machine translation.
A rule-based inflection module outperforms the existing end-to-end approach for lemma constraint incorporation in neural machine translation with lower training costs.
Semantic-aware contrastive learning distinguishes fine-grained meaning representations and considers overall sequence-level semantics.
A multi-level online sampling algorithm samples confusing and diverse instances.
Three semantic-aware similarity functions measure the distance between meaning representations as a whole.
A ranked contrastive loss pulls together representations of semantic-identical instances and pushes away negative instances.
Semantic-aware contrastive learning achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard semantic parsing datasets.
Open-ended knowledge tracing predicts students' exact code responses to programming questions in computer science education.

Student knowledge-guided code generation combines program synthesis methods using language models with student knowledge tracing methods to predict students' exact code responses in computer science education.
MCROSS uses a momentum contrastive learning framework to align the answer probability between cloze-like and natural query-passage sample pairs.
MCROSS improves performance on extractive question answering benchmarks in supervised and zero-shot settings.
RDI accounts for the impact of secondary attributes on model predictions.
RDI increases sensitivity for secondary attributes over reweighted counterfactually augmented data.
Accounting for secondary attributes improves model robustness in toxicity detection compared to existing robustness methods.
RDI generalizes to coreference resolution.
Label Verbalization and Entailment reformulates relation extraction as an entailment task using hand-made verbalizations of relations that take less than 15 minutes per relation to produce.
Label Verbalization and Entailment achieves zero-shot relation extraction performance close to state-of-the-art systems while using substantially less training data.
Label Verbalization and Entailment outperforms existing supervised systems in few-shot relation extraction with very limited labeled examples.
Few-shot relation extraction is especially effective at discriminating between relations.
The performance difference in low data regimes for relation extraction comes mainly from identifying no-relation cases.
ACENet integrates hybrid knowledge for commonsense reasoning.
ACENet uses multi-layer interaction of answer choices to strengthen correct choice information and guide graph neural network message passing.
ACENet uses a mix attention mechanism of nodes and edges to iteratively select supporting evidence on hybrid knowledge graphs.
RACE uses retrieved similar commits as exemplars for commit message generation.
The exemplar guider learns the semantic similarity between the code diff of the retrieved commit and the current code diff to guide commit message generation.

Legal judgment prediction systems without expert-informed adjustments can be vulnerable to shallow and distracting surface signals from corpus construction, case distribution, and confounding factors.
A deconfounding method for legal judgment prediction uses domain expertise to identify statistically predictive but legally irrelevant information.
Adversarial training prevents legal judgment prediction systems from relying on statistically predictive but legally irrelevant information.
A deconfounded legal judgment prediction model consistently aligns better with expert rationales than prediction-only baselines.
A benchmark dataset of European Court of Human Rights cases includes reference expert annotations for validation and testing partitions.
MasakhaNER 2.0 is the largest human-annotated named entity recognition dataset for 20 African languages.
The choice of source transfer language significantly affects named entity recognition performance in an Africa-centric setting.
Choosing the best transfer language improves named entity recognition zero-shot performance in an Africa-centric setting compared to using English as the source language.
kFolden induces k sub-models for a task with k training labels, each trained on a subset of k-1 categories with one category masked as unknown.
kFolden encourages sub-models to equally attribute probability to the seen k-1 labels for unknown labels during training.
kFolden simultaneously resolves in-distribution and out-of-distribution examples via out-of-distribution simulations.
Benchmarks for out-of-distribution detection in text classification are developed using existing text classification datasets.
kFolden improves out-of-distribution detection performance and improves in-domain classification accuracy.
Spider-DK is a benchmark for text-to-SQL translation that evaluates model robustness to domain knowledge in real-world question paraphrases.
Text-to-SQL models fail to generalize to unseen domains for questions requiring domain knowledge that is infrequent in the training data.
Text-to-SQL models fail to transfer domain knowledge to unseen domains despite learning it from training data.
FiE fuses information from multiple passages in the transformer encoder by providing cross-sample attention over all tokens across samples via a global representation.
FiE aggregates answer scores in the global space of all samples using an alternative answer span probability calculation.
FiE outperforms the state-of-the-art on Natural Questions and WebQuestions while using fewer parameters and lower inference latency.
FiE coupled with synthetic data augmentation outperforms larger models on TriviaQA.
Reinforced Counterfactual Data Augmentation includes a generator that automatically generates massive and diverse antonymous sentences.

Reinforced Counterfactual Data Augmentation includes a discriminator with an original-side sentiment predictor and an antonymous-side sentiment predictor that jointly evaluate generated sample quality and help iteratively generate higher-quality antonymous samples.
Reinforced Counterfactual Data Augmentation uses the discriminator as the final sentiment classifier without requiring an additional classifier.
Reinforced Counterfactual Data Augmentation generates more diverse training samples for sentiment classification than strong data augmentation baselines.
Reinforced Counterfactual Data Augmentation solves the spurious association problem in sentiment classification.
CISLR is a video dataset for word-level Indian Sign Language recognition covering diverse topics and domains.
A prototype-based one-shot learner uses American Sign Language to learn generalized features for word recognition in Indian Sign Language.
Gesture features learned in American Sign Language enable one-shot predictions for Indian Sign Language recognition.
Reference-free metrics for evaluating generated text are equivalent to using one generation model to evaluate another.
Reference-free metrics for evaluating generated text can be optimized at test time to find the approximate best-possible output.
Reference-free metrics for evaluating generated text are inherently biased toward models which are more similar to their own.
Reference-free metrics for evaluating generated text can be biased against higher-quality outputs, including those written by humans.
A multi-reference knowledge-grounded dialogue dataset enables systematic assessment of the one-to-many efficacy of knowledge-grounded dialogue models.
Metrics systematically assess the one-to-many efficacy of knowledge-grounded dialogue models.
A span-based variational model uses wake-sleep optimization with an ameliorated evidence lower bound objective to learn one-to-many generalization in knowledge-grounded dialogue.

When structured commonsense reasoning tasks that do not involve source code are framed as code generation tasks, pre-trained language models of code are better few-shot structured commonsense reasoners than language models of natural language.
MAVEN-ERE is a unified large-scale human-annotated dataset for event coreference, temporal, causal, and subevent relation extraction with improved annotation schemes.
Event relation extraction on MAVEN-ERE is challenging.
Joint learning improves event relation extraction performance by considering relation interactions.

Comparative Opinion Quintuple Extraction is the task of identifying comparative sentences from product reviews and extracting comparative opinion quintuples (Subject, Object, Comparative Aspect, Comparative Opinion, Comparative Preference).
Three datasets for Comparative Opinion Quintuple Extraction are constructed by making supplementary annotations on existing comparative opinion mining corpora.

A BERT-based multi-stage approach identifies comparative sentences in product reviews and extracts comparative opinion quintuples (Subject, Object, Comparative Aspect, Comparative Opinion, Comparative Preference).
A BERT-based multi-stage approach outperforms baseline systems in Comparative Opinion Quintuple Extraction.
Fine-grained contrastive learning (FineCL) improves relationship representation learning for relation extraction by leveraging fine-grained information about silver label noise.
Learning order denoising assesses silver label quality in relation extraction by training a language model and recording the learning order of training instances.
Early-learned silver labels in relation extraction are more accurate than later-learned silver labels on average.
For span finding in end-to-end information extraction system training, the best method depends on task properties.

For span finding in end-to-end information extraction system training, tagging approaches often yield higher precision, whereas span enumeration and boundary prediction approaches yield higher recall.
For span finding in end-to-end information extraction system training, span type information benefits boundary prediction approaches.
For span finding in end-to-end information extraction system training, additional contextualization does not improve span finding in most cases.
Virtual Data Augmentation constructs virtual data embeddings using a multinomial mixture of original token embeddings.
Virtual Data Augmentation uses a masked language model to ensure semantic relevance of the augmented data.
Virtual Data Augmentation uses Gaussian noise to provide diversity in the augmented data.
Virtual Data Augmentation uses a regularized training strategy to balance semantic relevance and augmentation diversity.
Virtual Data Augmentation reduces performance degradation of pre-trained language models under adversarial attacks.
A kNN approach re-ranks documents based on similarity with the query and user-relevant documents.
Cross-Encoder models re-rank documents after meta-learning pre-training and per-query fine-tuning on feedback documents.
Four information retrieval datasets are transformed to support relevance feedback evaluation.
Direct integration of relevance feedback improves neural re-ranking performance.
Fusing lexical ranking with the best neural re-ranker outperforms other methods in the relevance feedback scenario.
Breakpoint modeling builds intermediate representations that facilitate direct querying and training of beliefs at arbitrary points in text.
Breakpoint modeling improves processing efficiency, belief accuracy, and belief consistency while maintaining end-task performance.
Breakpoint modeling achieves state-of-the-art performance on the TRIP benchmark.
Proposition prediction tasks evaluate belief accuracy and belief consistency.
TranSHER uses relation-specific translations between head and tail entities to relax hyper-ellipsoid restrictions in knowledge graph embedding.
TranSHER provides more direct guidance on optimization for knowledge graph embedding.
TranSHER captures more semantic characteristics of entities with complex relations.
TranSHER achieves state-of-the-art performance on link prediction.
TranSHER generalizes well to datasets in different domains and scales.
Specific numerical capabilities for Numerical Question Answering are characterized.
Dataset perturbations for specific numerical capabilities are designed.
Existing Numerical Question Answering systems experience significant performance degradation under dataset perturbations for specific numerical capabilities.
Achieving robust numerical capabilities in Numerical Question Answering remains an open question.
mSimCSE extends SimCSE to multilingual settings.
Contrastive learning on English data learns high-quality universal cross-lingual sentence embeddings without any parallel data.
mSimCSE improves cross-lingual retrieval and multilingual STS performance over previous sentence embedding methods in unsupervised and weakly supervised settings.
Unsupervised mSimCSE achieves performance comparable to fully supervised methods for cross-lingual retrieval of low-resource languages and multilingual STS.
mSimCSE achieves enhanced performance with cross-lingual NLI data.
CGoDial evaluates multi-domain goal-oriented dialog systems in Chinese.
CGoDial covers three types of goal-oriented dialog datasets: slot-based with table-formed knowledge, flow-based with tree-formed knowledge, and retrieval-based with candidate-formed knowledge.
CGoDial includes dialog data collected from real conversations or with spoken features added via crowd-sourcing.

CGoDial provides experimental settings for evaluating model capabilities in general prediction, fast adaptability, and reliable robustness through combinations of full training, few-shot training, standard testing, and hard testing.
MBR-EXEC selects output programs by marginalizing over program implementations that share the same semantics.
MBR-EXEC approximates semantic equivalence by executing programs on a small number of test inputs.
MBR-EXEC outperforms execution-unaware selection methods in program selection for natural language to code translation.
Curriculum Knowledge Distiller (CKD) bridges source and target languages using emojis for cross-lingual sentiment analysis.
Curriculum Knowledge Distiller (CKD) distills Intermediate Sentiment Classifiers on source language corpus with emojis to obtain different attention weights of texts.
Curriculum Knowledge Distiller (CKD) distills Intermediate Sentiment Classifiers into the Target Language Sentiment Classifier using a curriculum learning mechanism.
Polyglot Prompting learns a unified semantic space for multiple languages and multiple tasks using multilingual prompt engineering.
The interpretable multilingual evaluation methodology explains the behavior of Polyglot Prompting.
Prompted versions of 24 multilingual datasets for 6 natural language processing tasks cover 49 languages.
Continual relation extraction models have learned representations that lack robustness to analogous relations during subsequent learning.
Adversarial class augmentation (ACA) encourages learning precise and robust representations in continual relation extraction.
Adversarial class augmentation (ACA) is easy to implement and model-agnostic.
Adversarial class augmentation (ACA) consistently improves the performance of state-of-the-art continual relation extraction models on two popular benchmarks.
KiloGram is a large-scale and diverse dataset for studying abstract visual reasoning in humans and machines.
KiloGram includes segmentation maps and part labels for abstract visual reasoning stimuli.
Pre-trained multi-modal models demonstrate limited abstract visual reasoning.
Fine-tuning multi-modal models improves their abstract visual reasoning.
Explicit part descriptions aid abstract visual reasoning for humans and multi-modal models, with greater benefit from joint encoding of linguistic and visual inputs.
Alignments from a state-of-the-art multimodal model for Electronic Health Records do not consistently reflect basic anatomical information.
Substituting 'left' for 'right' in medical text does not substantially change attention highlights in a state-of-the-art multimodal model for Electronic Health Records.
Allowing a multimodal model for Electronic Health Records to opt out of attending to the image improves alignments with human annotations with minimal supervision.
Few-shot finetuning improves alignments with human annotations in multimodal models for Electronic Health Records with minimal supervision.
Global Differentiable Learning explores optimal reasoning paths from the latent probability space to solve intermediate reasoning processes without expert annotations.
Dynamic Adaptive Reasoner enhances generalization for unseen questions.
Global Differentiable Learning and Dynamic Adaptive Reasoner achieve higher performance on multi-hop question answering than BreakRC.
Global Differentiable Learning and Dynamic Adaptive Reasoner show better interpretability in multi-hop question answering.
Retriever-reader models overfit the top-rank retrieval passages.
Standard training for retriever-reader models fails to reason over the entire retrieval passages.
A learnable passage mask mechanism enforces answer generation to focus on the entire retrieval passages in retriever-reader models.
A learnable passage mask mechanism consistently outperforms baselines on open question answering, dialogue conversation, and fact verification tasks.
A human-machine collaboration approach for dialogical data collection combines human expert annotator intervention with machine generated dialogues from 19 configurations.
DIALOCONAN is the first dataset of fictitious multi-turn dialogues for hate speech countering between a hater and an NGO operator, covering 6 targets of hate.
Robustness of LSTMs and their extensions to programmable string transformations can be certified.
LSTMs and their extensions can be trained to be efficiently certifiable for robustness to programmable string transformations.

LSTMs and their extensions that are efficiently certifiable for robustness to programmable string transformations are more robust to combinations of string transformations than models from existing techniques.
Robustness certification for efficiently certifiable LSTM models has high accuracy.
Model extraction can cause severe privacy leakage in extracted BERT models even when the models being extracted employ state-of-the-art defensive strategies.
AfroMT is a standardized, clean, and reproducible machine translation benchmark for eight widely spoken African languages.
A suite of analysis tools for system diagnosis accounts for the unique properties of eight widely spoken African languages.
A data augmentation strategy for pretraining multilingual sequence-to-sequence models leverages word-level alignment information.
A data augmentation strategy for pretraining multilingual sequence-to-sequence models leverages pseudo-monolingual data.
Low-resource focused pretraining of multilingual sequence-to-sequence models on 11 languages improves machine translation performance over strong baselines.

Low-resource focused pretraining of multilingual sequence-to-sequence models on 11 languages improves machine translation performance over cross-lingual transfer baselines in data-constrained scenarios.
Representation-level augmentation for code search processes both code and natural language queries without requiring additional data processing or training cost.
A general format of representation-level augmentation unifies existing augmentation methods for code search.
Linear extrapolation, binary interpolation, and Gaussian scaling are representation-level augmentation methods for code search.
Representation-level augmentation has theoretical advantages over traditional contrastive learning methods for code search.
Representation-level augmentation consistently improves code search model performance.
A localized geometric method matches equivalent entities across knowledge graphs in low-dimensional hyperbolic space.
A hyperbolic neural network encodes linguistic information of entities and knowledge graph structures into a low-dimensional hyperbolic space.
An instance-specific geometric mapping function based on rotation matches entity pairs across knowledge graphs in low-dimensional hyperbolic space.
Contrastive loss trains entity matching models in low-dimensional hyperbolic space.
A localized geometric method in low-dimensional hyperbolic space outperforms state-of-the-art methods for entity matching.
PAIR outputs a reflection level score for a counselor response given the corresponding client prompt.
A counselor reflection dataset includes multiple reflection levels.
PAIR contrasts positive and negative prompt-response pairs using multi-gap and prompt-aware margin ranking losses.
PAIR outperforms several baselines in counselor reflection scoring.
OREO-LM uses a Knowledge Interaction Layer to enable language models to guide knowledge graph walks towards answers and incorporates retrieved knowledge to improve language model predictions.
OREO-LM achieves state-of-the-art results in the Closed-Book setting for open-domain question answering.
Knowledge graph reasoning in OREO-LM enhances open-domain question answering performance by inferring missing relational facts.
OREO-LM provides reasoning paths as interpretable rationales for model decisions.
Transition freedom is superior to statistical metrics such as mutual information and conditional probability for unsupervised tokenization.
Different languages require different variants of the transition freedom metric, such as derivative, variance, and peak values, for successful tokenization.
Larger training corpora do not necessarily result in better tokenization quality.
Compressing tokenization models by eliminating statistically weak evidence improves tokenization performance.
Normalized Pointwise Mutual Information (NPMI) uses a source language model to approximate the joint likelihood of a source-target pair and the likelihood of the source for normalization.
Normalized Pointwise Mutual Information (NPMI) better captures the dependence between source and target language in translation.
BioReader augments input prompts by fetching and assembling relevant scientific literature chunks from a neural database centered on PubMed.
BioReader outperforms state-of-the-art methods on multiple downstream tasks while using fewer parameters.
Domain knowledge in BioReader can be altered or supplemented without retraining to generate correct predictions.
Combining state-of-the-art factual consistency models identifies problematic instances in popular summarization datasets.
SummFC is a filtered summarization dataset with improved factual consistency.
Models trained on SummFC achieve improved performance in nearly all quality aspects.
GREENER builds a graph of connections between news media outlets based on audience overlap.
GREENER uses graph neural networks to represent each news media outlet.
GREENER improves the prediction of news media factuality and bias over state-of-the-art methods.
Augmenting GREENER with representations from news articles, Twitter, YouTube, Facebook, and Wikipedia improves the prediction of news media factuality and bias.
A sentence-level transformer pre-training objective for answer sentence selection predicts whether two sentences are extracted from the same paragraph.
A sentence-level transformer pre-training objective for answer sentence selection predicts whether a given sentence is extracted from a given paragraph.
A sentence-level transformer pre-training objective for answer sentence selection predicts whether two paragraphs are extracted from the same document.
Transformers pre-trained with sentence-level objectives incorporating paragraph-level semantics within and across documents outperform RoBERTa and ELECTRA for answer sentence selection.
RLPrompt uses a parameter-efficient policy network to generate optimized discrete prompts for large language models.
RLPrompt incorporates reward stabilization to handle complex and stochastic reward signals from large language model environments, enhancing training efficiency.
RLPrompt outperforms existing fine-tuning and prompting methods on few-shot classification and unsupervised text style transfer.
Optimized discrete prompts for large language models are often ungrammatical gibberish text.
Ungrammatical gibberish prompts are transferrable between different large language models while retaining performance.
CTRLsum controls generated summaries through a set of keywords.
CTRLsum automatically extracts keywords during training without requiring additional human annotations.

CTRLsum uses a control function to map control signals to keywords at test time, enabling the same model to control summaries on various dimensions without affecting the training process or pretrained models.

CTRLsum supports entity-centric and length-controllable summarization, as well as contribution summarization for scientific papers, invention purpose summarization for patent filings, and question-guided summarization for news articles.
CTRLsum performs comparably or better than strong pretrained systems in standard, unconstrained summarization.
Supervised Prototypical Contrastive Learning (SPCL) is a loss function for emotion recognition in conversation.
Supervised Prototypical Contrastive Learning (SPCL) addresses the imbalanced classification problem in emotion recognition in conversation through contrastive learning.
Supervised Prototypical Contrastive Learning (SPCL) does not require a large batch size for training.
A difficulty measure function for emotion recognition in conversation is based on the distance between classes.
CN-AutoMIC is a large-scale Chinese commonsense knowledge graph generated from multilingual pretrained language models.
A generate-by-category strategy reduces invalid generation in commonsense knowledge graph construction.
Cascaded filters discard low-quality results in commonsense knowledge graph construction.
A bootstrapping iteration process reuses generated results to increase diversity and density in commonsense knowledge graph construction.
CN-AutoMIC has higher quality and diversity than a direct translation of similar English commonsense knowledge graphs.
CN-AutoMIC exhibits deficiency patterns and differences between relations that reveal pending problems in commonsense knowledge generation.
A dataset of NLP papers from the ACL Anthology with extracted meta-information and a generated citation network.
Geographical disparities in NLP paper acceptance and citation persist when controlling for venue of publication and sub-field of NLP.
The geographical disparity in NLP paper acceptance and citation has increased since the early 2000s.
Non-autoregressive text generation composes Ci by accommodating tune patterns through controlling rhythm and essential meaning of each sentence.
Non-autoregressive text generation enables synchronous Ci generation.
Reinforcement learning with rigid constraints and content diversity rewards maintains format and content requirements in Ci generation.
Non-autoregressive text generation with reinforcement learning outperforms strong baselines in automatic evaluation and human judgements for Ci generation.
Par3 provides paragraph-level alignments between non-English language novels in the public domain and their human and automatic English translations.
Expert literary translators prefer reference human translations over machine-translated paragraphs.
State-of-the-art automatic MT metrics do not correlate with expert literary translator preferences.
Machine translation outputs for literary texts contain discourse-disrupting errors and stylistic inconsistencies in addition to mistranslations.
A post-editing model produces machine translation outputs for literary texts that are preferred by expert literary translators over standard machine translation outputs.
Graph Hawkes Transformer performs entity prediction and time prediction for future events in temporal knowledge graphs.
Graph Hawkes Transformer includes a Transformer variant that captures instantaneous structural information in temporal knowledge graphs.
Graph Hawkes Transformer includes a Transformer variant that captures temporal evolution information in temporal knowledge graphs.
Graph Hawkes Transformer uses a relational continuous-time encoding function that facilitates feature evolution with the Hawkes process.
Graph Hawkes Transformer achieves superior performance on long-term evolutionary tasks in temporal knowledge graphs.
mPLUG is a vision-language foundation model for cross-modal understanding and generation.
mPLUG incorporates cross-modal skip-connections in its vision-language architecture.
mPLUG is pre-trained end-to-end on large-scale image-text pairs using discriminative and generative objectives.
mPLUG achieves state-of-the-art performance on image captioning, image-text retrieval, visual grounding, and visual question answering.
mPLUG demonstrates strong zero-shot transferability on vision-language and video-language tasks.
Autoregressive multi-hop document retrieval for fact verification jointly scores knowledge source documents and sentences from previously retrieved documents.
A natural logic-based proof system dynamically terminates multi-hop document retrieval for fact verification when evidence is sufficient.

Multi-hop document retrieval for fact verification with an autoregressive formulation and a natural logic-based proof system achieves competitive performance on standard benchmarks with significantly reduced memory requirements.
