Multi-hop document retrieval for fact verification with an autoregressive formulation and a natural logic-based proof system shows improved stability on adversarial datasets compared to threshold-based methods.
A natural logic-based proof system in multi-hop document retrieval for fact verification improves human accuracy in predicting model decisions compared to using evidence alone.
PLOG uses table-to-logic pretraining for logical table-to-text generation.
CONTLOG is a controlled logical table-to-text dataset collected from an existing dataset.
PLOG achieves higher logical fidelity than strong baselines in logical table-to-text generation.
The Optimal BERT Surgeon (oBERT) uses approximate second-order information to prune weight blocks in BERT models.
Compounding compression approaches produce highly compressed BERT models that maintain high task accuracy for edge device deployment.
Re3 constructs a structured overarching plan by prompting a general-purpose language model.
Re3 generates story passages by repeatedly injecting contextual information from both the plan and current story state into a language model prompt.
Re3 reranks different continuations for plot coherence and premise relevance.
Re3 edits the best continuation for factual consistency.
Re3 produces stories with a coherent overarching plot and relevance to the initial premise at higher rates than the base model.
Template-based slot querying facilitates transfer learning from semantic role labeling to event argument extraction.
Natural language-like queries address label mismatch between semantic role labeling and event argument extraction.
Argument augmentation recovers distant arguments in event argument extraction.
Template-based slot querying enables zero-shot event argument extraction on English benchmarks.
Template-based slot querying generalizes to cross-domain and multilingual event argument extraction.

RetroMAE uses a Masked Auto-Encoder workflow that applies different masks to the same input sentence for the encoder and decoder, generating sentence embeddings from the encoder's masked input and recovering the original sentence via masked language modeling using the sentence embedding and the decoder's masked input.
RetroMAE uses an asymmetric model structure with a full-scale BERT-like transformer as encoder and a one-layer transformer as decoder.
RetroMAE uses moderate masking ratios for the encoder and aggressive masking ratios for the decoder.
RetroMAE improves state-of-the-art performance on dense retrieval benchmarks including BEIR and MS MARCO.
PCLL enhances generative replay for lifelong learning by incorporating tasks' statistics.
PCLL captures task-specific distributions using a conditional variational autoencoder conditioned on natural language prompts for pseudo-sample generation.
PCLL uses a distillation process to consolidate past knowledge by reducing noise in pseudo samples.
PCLL outperforms competitive baselines in lifelong learning for task-oriented dialogue natural language understanding tasks.

A simple and model-agnostic multi-task learning framework for non-autoregressive neural machine translation uses weak autoregressive decoders that solely rely on the non-autoregressive decoder's information during training.
A simple and model-agnostic multi-task learning framework consistently improves the accuracy of multiple non-autoregressive translation baselines without adding decoding overhead.
CTL++ tests neural network systematic generalization by splitting unary symbolic functions into groups and evaluating on compositions of group elements not seen during training.
Recent Transformer variants that solve CTL fail on CTL++.
Neural networks require a specific amount of overlap between function groups to learn composition.
Learned symbol representations in neural network outputs are compatible for functions from different groups when composition is successful but not when it fails.
R2D2 addresses unfaithful Data-to-Text generation by combining generator training, faithfulness discrimination, replacement detection, and unlikelihood learning.
Two methods sample unfaithful sentences for robust Data-to-Text training.
Poor entity retrieval capability is one of the primary sources of unfaithfulness in Data-to-Text systems.
Named entity based metrics evaluate the fidelity of Data-to-Text generations.
R2D2 achieves state-of-the-art performance on FeTaQA, LogicNLG, and ToTTo.

Entailer recursively combines a backward-chaining model that generates premises entailing an answer hypothesis with a verifier that checks the model's belief in those premises and the entailment through self-querying.

Entailer is the first system to generate multistep chains of reasoning that are both faithful (the answer follows from the reasoning) and truthful (the chain reflects the system's own internal beliefs).
Entailer generates chains that users judge to clearly show how an answer follows from a set of facts and outperforms a high-performance baseline in user evaluation while preserving answer accuracy.
MedJ is a dataset of expert-annotated medical jargon terms from electronic health record notes.
MedJEx is trained on an auxiliary Wikipedia hyperlink span dataset, where hyperlink spans provide explanations for terms, and then fine-tuned on expert-annotated medical jargon data.
A contextualized masked language model score is beneficial for detecting domain-specific unfamiliar jargon terms.
Training on the auxiliary Wikipedia hyperlink span dataset improves performance on biomedical named entity recognition benchmarks.

Arbitrarily applying pretraining-based metrics to grammatical error correction evaluation yields unsatisfactory correlation with human judgments due to excessive attention to inessential system outputs such as unchanged parts.
PT-M2 uses pretraining-based metrics to score only the corrected parts in grammatical error correction evaluation.
PT-M2 achieves higher correlation with human judgments than existing methods in grammatical error correction evaluation.
PT-M2 is robust for evaluating competitive grammatical error correction systems.
Bloom Library provides multimodal and multilingual datasets in over 300 languages for language modeling, image captioning, visual storytelling, and speech synthesis/recognition.
Bloom Library provides among the most multilingual datasets for language modeling, image captioning, visual storytelling, and speech synthesis/recognition.
Bloom Library establishes the first known baselines for downstream tasks in low-resource languages.

Bloom Library yields baselines for some low-resource languages on language modeling, image captioning, visual storytelling, and speech synthesis/recognition that are comparable to state-of-the-art performance for high-resource languages.
A self-training method for zero-shot text classification requires only class names and an unlabeled dataset without domain expertise or trial and error.
A self-training method for zero-shot text classification fine-tunes a zero-shot classifier on its most confident predictions.
Fine-tuning a zero-shot classifier on its most confident predictions improves performance on diverse text classification tasks.
Generative entity typing (GET) generates multiple types for an entity mention according to the role the entity plays in the text using a pre-trained language model.
Curriculum learning with self-paced adjustment trains the generative entity typing model on heterogeneous data according to type granularity and data heterogeneity.
Generative entity typing with curriculum learning outperforms state-of-the-art entity typing models on datasets of multiple languages and downstream tasks.
MSE loss on the attention score is insufficient for recovering the self-attention information in quantized large Transformers.
Attention-map loss recovers self-attention information in quantized large Transformers.
Attention-output loss recovers self-attention information in quantized large Transformers.
Attention-map loss and attention-output loss achieve state-of-the-art accuracy for quantization-aware training with sub-2-bit weight quantization.
Guided question generation schemes use input conditioning and reinforcement learning to generate Socratic subquestions for guiding math word problem-solving.
Large language models constrained with desirable question properties generate Socratic subquestions of higher quality for guiding math word problem-solving.
The difficulty level of math word problems determines whether Socratic questioning improves or hinders human problem-solving performance.
ConCoRD boosts the consistency and accuracy of pre-trained language models using pre-trained natural language inference models without fine-tuning or re-training.

ConCoRD samples candidate outputs for test inputs and instantiates a factor graph that accounts for the pre-trained language model's belief about each answer choice's likelihood and the pre-trained natural language inference model's beliefs about pairwise answer choice compatibility.
A weighted MaxSAT solver efficiently computes high-quality answer choices under the factor graph used by ConCoRD.
ConCoRD consistently boosts the accuracy and consistency of off-the-shelf closed-book question-answering and visual question-answering models using off-the-shelf natural language inference models.
MGDoc encodes page-level, region-level, and word-level information simultaneously.
MGDoc uses a unified text-visual encoder to obtain multi-modal features across different granularities and project them into the same hyperspace.
MGDoc employs a cross-granular attention mechanism to model region-word correlation.
MGDoc introduces specific pre-training tasks to reinforce learning the hierarchy between regions and words.
MolT5 pretrains models on unlabeled natural language text and molecule strings via self-supervised learning.
MolT5 enables molecule captioning and text-based de novo molecule generation.
MolT5 helps overcome data scarcity in chemistry by pretraining on single-modal data.
A cross-modal embedding-based metric evaluates molecule captioning and text-based molecule generation.
Information status and LSTM-based discourse predictability influence Hindi word order choices, especially for non-canonical object-fronted orders.
ENTIGEN is a benchmark dataset for evaluating the change in image generations conditional on ethical interventions across gender, skin color, and culture.
Text-to-image generative models cover diverse social groups while preserving image quality when ethical interventions are added to prompts.
Text-to-image generative models generate anti-stereotypical images (e.g., creating images of men for makeup prompts) when ethical interventions are added.
Certain phrases such as 'irrespective of gender' trigger large changes in model predictions for gender bias in ethical interventions.
Multi-source domain generalization is primarily a problem of mitigating source domain underfitting.
In reading comprehension domain generalization, improving source domain learning leads to faster improvement in zero-shot out-of-domain utility.

In reading comprehension domain generalization, improved source domain learning achieves superior out-of-domain generalization compared to existing domain generalization approaches that limit overfitting.
Bias sentence identification uses distilled auxiliary knowledge from functional news discourse structure and PDTB discourse relations.
Incorporating both global functional discourse structure and local rhetorical discourse relations improves recall and precision for bias sentence identification.
The discourse role of a sentence in a news story and its relation with nearby sentences reveal the ideological leanings of an author even when the sentence appears neutral.
A metric for measuring token-to-token interactions within each layer of the Transformer incorporates multi-head attention, residual connection, and layer normalization.
Aggregation of layer-wise interpretations of token-to-token interactions produces input attribution scores for model predictions.
Input attribution scores from aggregation of layer-wise interpretations of token-to-token interactions are more faithful and robust than those from gradient-based methods.
MILR mines logical rules from annotations based on frequencies.
MILR uses consistency regularization as an auxiliary loss to penalize instances that violate mined rules during training.
MILR infers relations from a global perspective using integer programming.
MILR achieves higher relation extraction performance than LogiRE.
MILR produces relation extraction predictions that are more logically consistent than those of LogiRE.
Cross-lingual neural fuzzy matching exploits target-language monolingual corpora in computer-aided translation.
Cross-lingual sentence embeddings retrieve translation proposals from target-language monolingual corpora.
A neural model estimates the post-editing effort of translation proposals from target-language monolingual corpora.
Cross-lingual neural fuzzy matching increases the amount of useful translation proposals in computer-aided translation.
Neural estimation of post-editing effort enables the combination of translation proposals from monolingual corpora and translation memories.
Human evaluation shows that translation proposals retrieved by cross-lingual neural fuzzy matching are more useful than indicated by automatic evaluation.
An end-to-end text revision system iteratively detects editable spans with their edit intents and revises the detected spans.
Leveraging datasets from other text editing tasks and specifying editable spans enables more accurate modeling of iterative text refinement.
Leveraging datasets from other text editing tasks and specifying editable spans leads to outperforming previous baselines on multiple standard text revision tasks.
Edit intentions are connected to writing quality.
JDDC 2.1 is a large-scale multimodal multi-turn dialogue dataset collected from a mainstream Chinese E-commerce platform, with product knowledge bases and image category annotations.

JDDC 2.1 is the first dataset with annotations for multimodal dialogue response generation, multimodal query rewriting, multimodal dialogue discourse parsing, and multimodal dialogue summarization tasks over the same dialogue sessions.
An open-topic scenario for false information detection on social networks accommodates both seen and unseen topics simultaneously.
Existing false information detection models exhibit impaired detection performance for both seen and unseen topics in the open-topic scenario, resulting in poor overall performance.
Contrastive Adversarial Learning Network (CALN) employs unsupervised topic clustering to capture topic-specific features for seen topics.
Contrastive Adversarial Learning Network (CALN) employs unsupervised adversarial learning to align data representation distributions for unseen topics.
The community-based question-answer summarization task creates a concise summary from community-based question-answer pairs.
COQASUM is a benchmark dataset for community-based question-answer summarization based on the Amazon QA corpus.
A multi-stage data annotation process creates the COQASUM dataset.
DedupLED is a baseline method for community-based question-answer summarization.
Sentence-type transfer and deduplication removal are key challenges for community-based question-answer summarization.
IsoVec incorporates global measures of isomorphism into the skipgram loss function.
IsoVec increases the relative isomorphism of trained word embedding spaces.
IsoVec improves the mapping of trained word embedding spaces to a shared cross-lingual space.
IsoVec improves bilingual lexicon induction in general data conditions, under domain mismatch, and with training algorithm dissimilarities.
Language neutrality in multilingual language models is a function of the overlap between language-encoding sub-networks.
Sub-networks for different languages are topologically similar (language-neutral).
Language-neutral sub-networks are effective initializations for cross-lingual transfer with limited performance degradation.
Counterfactual Prompt Learning is a prompt tuning method for vision and language models that employs counterfactual generation and contrastive learning in a joint optimization framework.

Counterfactual Prompt Learning constructs counterfactual examples by identifying a minimal non-spurious feature change between semantically-similar positive and negative samples that causes concept change.
Counterfactual Prompt Learning learns generalizable prompt representations from both factual and counterfactual examples via contrastive learning.
Counterfactual Prompt Learning achieves superior few-shot performance on multiple vision and language tasks compared to previous prompt tuning methods.
Bottom-up Automatic Intervention (BAI) performs multi-granular intervention with identified multifactorial confounders.
QASem is a comprehensive semantic representation that jointly covers verbal, nominalized, and discourse-based predications.
QASem parsing uses pre-trained sequence-to-sequence language models to generate natural language questions and answers.
QASem parsing uses input and output linearization strategies.
QASem parsing uses data augmentation and multitask learning for scarce training data.
The unified QASem parsing tool converts text into explicit semi-structured information units.
An unsupervised method analyzes latent space evolution in fine-tuned NLP models without the probing framework.
Higher layers of fine-tuned NLP models evolve towards task-specific concepts.
Lower layers of fine-tuned NLP models retain generic concepts from pre-training.
Some concepts in higher layers of fine-tuned NLP models acquire polarity towards the output class.
Concepts that acquire polarity towards the output class in fine-tuned NLP models can be used for generating adversarial triggers.
PHEE is the largest public dataset for pharmacovigilance event extraction.
PHEE uses a hierarchical event schema that provides coarse and fine-grained information about patients’ demographics, treatments and side effects.
Retrieval as attention (RAA) performs retrieval and reading within a single Transformer for knowledge-intensive tasks using end-to-end training based on question answering task supervision.
An end-to-end trained single Transformer matches or outperforms state-of-the-art dense retrievers and readers in retrieval and question answering on in-domain datasets.
End-to-end adaptation of the single Transformer model improves performance on out-of-domain datasets in supervised and unsupervised settings.
Each feed-forward network update in transformer-based language models decomposes into sub-updates that promote human-interpretable concepts.
Language model prediction control via feed-forward network decomposition reduces the toxicity of GPT2.
An early exit rule based on feed-forward network decomposition improves computation efficiency in transformer-based language models.
Language models generate test cases to identify harmful behaviors in target language models.
Target language models discuss specific demographic groups in offensive ways.
Target language models generate personal and hospital phone numbers as contact information.
Target language models leak private training data in generated text.
Target language models exhibit harmful behaviors over the course of a conversation.
Genetic Prompt Search automatically searches for the best prompt for few-shot learning using a genetic algorithm.
Genetic Prompt Search is gradient-free and requires no update of model parameters, using only a small validation set.
Genetic Prompt Search outperforms manually designed prompts in few-shot learning.
Genetic Prompt Search outperforms other parameter-efficient tuning methods such as prompt tuning in few-shot learning.
Sub-event generation for an unseen process evaluates understanding of the coherence of sub-event actions and objects.
SubeventWriter iteratively constructs sub-event sequences by generating one sub-event at each iteration.
SubeventWriter uses a coherence controller to decode coherent sub-events.
ALRE-IR is an interpretable model for inductive reasoning over temporal knowledge graphs that captures deep causal logic by learning rule embeddings.
ALRE-IR adaptively extracts and assesses reasons from historical events to make predictions based on causal logic.
A one-class augmented matching loss optimizes temporal knowledge graph reasoning models.
ALRE-IR has zero-shot reasoning ability on related datasets with common relation vocabulary.
Weighted low-rank estimation for language models uses multiple numerical optimization strategies to incorporate parameter importance.
A metric predicts significant performance drops caused by singular value decomposition in language model compression.
Numerical optimization strategies for weighted low-rank estimation yield better compression performance for Transformer-based language models than state-of-the-art methods.
A character token planning model predicts visual tokens for characters only in story visualization.
A visual token completion model generates the remaining visual token sequence for story visualization.
Training with character-token alignment encourages characters to appear in story visualization images.
Story visualization via visual planning and token alignment preserves characters.
Label-Correctness Sensitivity measures the impact of ground-truth label demonstrations in in-context learning.
Ground-truth Label Effect Ratio (GLER) measures the impact of ground-truth label demonstrations in in-context learning.
Correct input-label mappings have varying impacts on in-context learning performance depending on the experimental configuration.
Prompt template verbosity controls the noise resilience of in-context learning.
Language model size controls the noise resilience of in-context learning.
Far Boundary adversarial examples enable fair evaluation of Adversarial Examples Detection methods.
Adversarial Examples Detection methods rely on the shortcut that adversarial examples are near model decision boundaries.
Existing Adversarial Examples Detection methods perform worse than random guess when tested with Far Boundary adversarial examples.
ADDMU detects both regular and far-boundary adversarial examples by combining data uncertainty and model uncertainty estimation.
The two types of uncertainty in ADDMU characterize adversarial examples and identify those that contribute most to model robustness in adversarial training.
PAR retrieves and extracts factual statements about legislators to leverage social context information.
PAR constructs a heterogeneous information network to incorporate social context.
PAR uses relational graph neural networks to learn legislator representations.
PAR trains with three objectives to align representation learning with expert knowledge, model ideological stance consistency, and simulate the echo chamber phenomenon.
PAR advances the state-of-the-art in political perspective detection and roll call vote prediction.
PAR learns representations that reflect political reality and provide new insights into political behavior.
DICR uses dual imitation to explicitly align recommendation paths in a recommendation module with user interest shift paths in a conversation module.
DICR achieves bidirectional promotion between recommendation and conversation modules.
DICR generates responses with accurate recommendations and coherent explanations.
DICR outperforms state-of-the-art conversational recommendation systems in recommendation and conversation performance.

LAPA uses a three-stage training procedure that includes pre-training pre-trained language models on unsupervised corpora, inserting an adapter layer and meta-training on source domain labeled data, and fine-tuning adapters on target domain labeled data.
LAPA achieves state-of-the-art performance in supervised, unsupervised, and low-resource paraphrase generation settings on benchmark datasets.
LAPA achieves competitive paraphrase generation performance with a small fraction of trainable parameters and labeled data.
EnFoRe provides stronger supervision during training for outside-knowledge visual question answering.
EnFoRe recognizes question-relevant entities to retrieve more specific knowledge for outside-knowledge visual question answering.
EnFoRe achieves superior retrieval performance on the OK-VQA dataset.
Combining knowledge retrieved by EnFoRe with state-of-the-art VQA models achieves state-of-the-art performance on OK-VQA.
Speech relation extraction is a new task for extracting relations from speech.
A training dataset for speech relation extraction is created using text-to-speech systems.
A testing dataset for speech relation extraction is created via crowd-sourcing with native English speakers.
SpeechRE is a new encoder-decoder model for end-to-end speech relation extraction.
Comprehensive experiments distinguish the challenges in speech relation extraction.
WeTS evaluates translation suggestion using a golden corpus annotated by expert translators for four translation directions.
Synthetic corpora generation methods for translation suggestion enable performance improvement through pre-training.
The segment-aware self-attention based Transformer model uses segment-aware self-attention for translation suggestion.
The segment-aware self-attention based Transformer model outperforms existing methods on translation suggestion for four translation directions.
KECP transforms extractive question answering into a non-autoregressive Masked Language Modeling generation problem.
KECP enhances query representations using rich semantics from an external knowledge base and the passage context.
KECP uses joint training with Masked Language Modeling and contrastive learning objectives.
KECP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art approaches in few-shot extractive question answering.
The Semantic Framework of Temporal Constraints (SF-TCons) structures the interpretation of temporal constraints for temporal question answering over knowledge graphs.

SF-TQA generates query graphs for temporal question answering by exploring relevant facts of mentioned entities with the exploration restricted by the Semantic Framework of Temporal Constraints (SF-TCons).
SF-TQA outperforms existing methods for temporal question answering over different knowledge graphs.
Advbench is a security datasets collection for security-oriented adversarial NLP.
Security-oriented adversarial NLP tasks are reformalized to adjust the emphasis toward real-world attacker goals.
A heuristic rule-based method fulfills actual adversarial goals to simulate real-world attack methods in security-oriented adversarial NLP.
Advbench is suitable for initiating the research paradigm in security-oriented adversarial NLP.

D4 is a Chinese dialogue dataset for depression-diagnosis-oriented chat that contains doctor-patient conversations during depression diagnosis, each annotated with diagnosis results and symptom summaries by professional psychiatrists.
Response generation, topic prediction, dialog summary, and severity classification of depressive episode and suicide risk are tasks for the depression diagnosis process.
A consultation dialogue system trained on D4 is more empathy-driven and diagnostic-accurate than rule-based bots.
IELM evaluates the open relational information in pre-trained language models via open information extraction.
TAC KBP-OIE and Wikidata-OIE are large-scale factual open information extraction datasets established via distant supervision.
Pre-trained language models achieve competitive performance in zero-shot open information extraction.
Zero-shot pre-trained language models outperform supervised open information extraction methods on factual open information extraction datasets without training data.
Using a small number of manual counterfactuals and automatic counterfactual generation in an encoding vector space improves out-of-distribution sentiment classification accuracy.

Using a small number of manual counterfactuals and automatic counterfactual generation in an encoding vector space improves out-of-distribution sentiment classification accuracy compared to adding more in-distribution training samples.

Using a small number of manual counterfactuals and automatic counterfactual generation in an encoding vector space improves out-of-distribution sentiment classification accuracy compared to alternate counterfactual approaches.
In speech translation, a strong cascaded baseline can diminish all improvements from joint training.
Inducer-tuning is a variant of prefix-tuning that incorporates the residual form of adapter-tuning.
Inducer-tuning mitigates the initialization issue in prefix-tuning.
Inducer-tuning closes the performance gap between prefix-tuning and fine-tuning.
DuQM is a Chinese dataset of natural questions with linguistic perturbations for evaluating the robustness of question matching models.
DuQM organizes linguistic perturbations into categories and subcategories.
DuQM enables diagnosis of model strengths and weaknesses through breakdown by linguistic phenomena.
Artificial adversarial examples are ineffective on natural texts.
UniGeo is a geometry benchmark that unifies calculation and proving problems through a shared sequence format.
Geoformer is a unified multi-task model that solves geometry calculation and proving problems via sequence generation.
Mathematical Expression Pretraining (MEP) improves geometry reasoning models by predicting mathematical expressions in problem solutions.
Unifying the formulation of geometry calculation and proving problems improves reasoning ability on both tasks.
ULN evaluates vision-and-language navigation agents using multi-level underspecified instructions.
Granularity Specific Sub-networks (GSS) ground multi-level instructions with minimal additional parameters.
The Exploitation-to-Exploration (E2E) module estimates grounding uncertainty and conducts multi-step lookahead exploration.
Existing vision-and-language navigation models are brittle when processing multi-level underspecified instructions.
A framework with Granularity Specific Sub-networks and the Exploitation-to-Exploration module outperforms baselines on ULN.
Five new public splits for the PAN dataset isolate biases related to text topic and author writing style.
BERT-like models are competitive with state-of-the-art methods for authorship verification.
BERT-like models for authorship verification are biased towards named entities.
DarkReddit is a new dataset for authorship verification.
Training authorship verification models without named entities improves generalization on DarkReddit.
STRUDEL decomposes dialogues to imitate the hierarchical, systematic and structured mental process of human dialogue understanding.
STRUDEL dialogue comprehension modeling framework integrates structured dialogue summarization into a dialogue reasoning module for transformer encoder language models.
STRUDEL-based dialogue comprehension improves performance on dialogue question answering and dialogue response prediction.
PASTA performs table-based fact verification via sentence-table cloze pre-training.
PASTA uses six sentence-table cloze task types: Filter, Aggregation, Superlative, Comparative, Ordinal, and Unique.
PASTA uses a pre-training corpus of synthesized sentence-table pairs from WikiTables.
PASTA achieves state-of-the-art performance on the TabFact and SEM-TAB-FACTS table-based fact verification benchmarks.
PASTA achieves near-human performance on the TabFact test set.
An approach automatically extracts TLDR summaries for scientific papers from citation texts.
CiteSum is a benchmark for scientific extreme summarization created without human annotation.
Models pre-trained on CiteSum outperform most fully-supervised methods for scientific extreme summarization without fine-tuning.
Models pre-trained on CiteSum achieve state-of-the-art results for scientific extreme summarization with very few examples.
Models pre-trained on CiteSum achieve significant gains for news extreme summarization in zero-shot settings.
Models pre-trained on CiteSum achieve state-of-the-art results for news extreme summarization in few-shot settings.
Models pre-trained on CiteSum achieve the best performance among unsupervised and zero-shot methods for news headline generation.
AX-MABSA is an extremely weakly supervised multi-label framework for Aspect Category Sentiment Analysis that does not use labelled data.
AX-MABSA relies on a single word per class as initial indicative information.
An automatic word selection technique chooses seed categories and sentiment words.
Unsupervised language model post-training improves performance for extremely weakly supervised multi-label Aspect Category Sentiment Analysis.
A multi-label generator model generates multiple aspect category-sentiment pairs per review sentence.
AX-MABSA outperforms other weakly supervised baselines on four benchmark datasets.
ISS is a data-aware metric that measures incremental structural stability of Meaning Representations.
ISS is highly correlated with the final model performance of neural semantic parsers.
PlayNTell is a data-efficient multi-modal encoder-decoder model for automatic playlist captioning.
PlayNTell leverages linguistic and musical knowledge to generate correct and thematic captions.
The editorial playlists dataset benchmarks playlist captioning with data from two major music streaming services.
PlayNTell improves playlist captioning performance over state-of-the-art algorithms.
UniRPG uses a neural programmer to parse questions into programs by generating operations and copying arguments from tables and text.
UniRPG uses a symbolic program executor to derive answers from tables and text based on generated programs.
UniRPG uses a distant supervision approach that automatically constructs pseudo programs without derivation annotation.
UniRPG improves performance on discrete reasoning question answering tasks without derivation annotation.
UniRPG enhances interpretability and scalability without derivation annotation.
DropMix combines Dropout and Mixup for textual data augmentation and regularization using saliency maps.
DropMix drops and patches fine grained shapes of the saliency map to enhance regularization.
Source code availability in major computational linguistics conferences shows positive trends, particularly in conferences that actively promote reproducibility.
Source code releases for papers published in EMNLP 2021 are insufficient for reproducibility.
No single privacy-preserving strategy works best for all NLP tasks and privacy regimes in differentially-private learning.
Each NLP task and privacy regime requires a tailored privacy-preserving strategy to achieve adequate performance in differentially-private learning.
Sparse Latent Typing enables language models to sparsely extract sentence-level keywords with diverse latent types.
Sparse Latent Typing enables language models to learn interpretable latent type categories in a self-supervised manner without external knowledge.
Sparse Latent Typing improves performance on Information Extraction downstream tasks in supervised and few-shot settings.
GammaE encodes entities and queries to answer first-order logic queries on knowledge graphs.
GammaE reduces model uncertainty by utilizing the linear property and strong boundary support of the Gamma distribution.
GammaE designs a closed union operator using the Gamma mixture method.
GammaE outperforms state-of-the-art models on logical query benchmarks.
An entity-based document-context filter for relation extraction retains useful information in documents by using bridge entities in text paths.
A cross-document relation extraction model uses cross-path entity relation attention to allow entity relations across text paths to interact.
Phrase Pair Injection and Corpus Filtering combine a parallel corpus with parallel phrases extracted from a pseudo-parallel corpus for Neural Machine Translation training.
Phrase Pair Injection and Corpus Filtering improve Machine Translation for the low-resource language pairs Hindi-Marathi, English-Marathi, and English-Pashto across 6 translation directions.
Model errors are defined by the distance between the model-ranked hypothesis array and the ideally ranked hypothesis array.
Top region evaluation uses an exact top-k decoding algorithm to find top-ranked hypotheses in the whole hypothesis space.
Monte Carlo sampling evaluation simulates the hypothesis space from a broader perspective.
State-of-the-art Transformer models perform at random chance level in the top region of the hypothesis space.
Model errors are affected by neural machine translation architecture depth, width, and data-augmentation techniques.
Beam search decoding correlates with Minimum Bayes Risk decoding in neural machine translation.

The CEFR-based Sentence Profile (CEFR-SP) corpus contains English sentences annotated with sentence difficulty levels based on the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages by English-education professionals.
A sentence-level assessment model handles unbalanced level distribution in sentence difficulty level assessment.
A sentence-level assessment model for unbalanced level distribution outperforms strong baselines from readability assessment in sentence difficulty level assessment.
Model cascading utilizes a collection of models of varying capacities to output predictions accurately and efficiently.
Cascading improves computational efficiency and prediction accuracy.
Introducing additional models in the cascade increases computational efficiency.
Measuring calibration to human majority in tasks with inherent human disagreements is theoretically problematic.
Measuring calibration to human majority in tasks with inherent human disagreements is empirically problematic on the ChaosNLI dataset.
Instance-level calibration measures capture class frequency, ranking, and entropy of human judgements.
Large language models generate paraphrases that humans have difficulty identifying as machine-paraphrased.
Human experts rate the quality of paraphrases generated by GPT-3 as comparable to original texts.
Neural-PCRF incorporates unary influences from the input and pairwise relations between type variables for ultra-fine entity typing.
Neural-PCRF derives pairwise potentials from type phrase representations to capture prior semantic information and enable accelerated inference.
Neural-PCRF uses mean-field variational inference for efficient type inference on large type sets and is unfolded as a neural network module for end-to-end training.
Neural-PCRF outperforms backbone models with minimal additional computational cost in ultra-fine entity typing.
Neural-PCRF achieves competitive performance against state-of-the-art cross-encoder models for ultra-fine entity typing while being orders of magnitude faster.
Models for hate speech detection in under-resourced languages achieve strong performance with a small amount of target-language fine-tuning data.
The benefits of additional target-language fine-tuning data for hate speech detection in under-resourced languages decrease exponentially.
Initial fine-tuning on English data partially substitutes for target-language data in hate speech detection for under-resourced languages.
Initial fine-tuning on English data improves model generalisability in hate speech detection for under-resourced languages.
Dungeons and Dragons is a dialogue system challenge requiring generation of the next conversational turn and prediction of the game state from dialogue history.
A Dungeons and Dragons gameplay dataset includes player dialogue turns, dice rolls, and automatically annotated partial game state.
A large language model generates the next turn in Dungeons and Dragons dialogue as a character or as the Dungeon Master, producing in-character or out-of-character responses.
UniRel unifies the representations of entities and relations by jointly encoding them within a concatenated natural language sequence.
UniRel unifies the modeling of entity-entity and entity-relation interactions with an Interaction Map built upon the self-attention mechanism in Transformer blocks.
VAE-Dprior generates task-specific natural language with none or a handful of task-specific labeled examples.
VAE-Dprior uses conditional priors for the latent content space and the latent label space that satisfy the 𝜖-disentangled property.
Conditional priors in VAE-Dprior disentangle representations without specific regularizations.

The content prior in VAE-Dprior samples diverse content representations from the content space of seen tasks and fuses them with novel task representations to generate semantically diverse texts in low-resource settings.
Blind and low vision users highly value contextual information in image descriptions.
The lack of context-awareness in referenceless evaluation metrics makes them inadequate for advancing image accessibility for blind and low vision users.
A contextual version of the referenceless metric CLIPScore addresses the disconnect between referenceless metrics and the needs of blind and low vision users.
Object Use Classification determines whether a physical object mentioned in a sentence was used or likely will be used.
The Object Use Classification dataset evaluates whether a physical object mentioned in a sentence was used or likely will be used.
A model for object use classification uses data augmentation methods and FrameNet during fine-tuning of a pre-trained language model.
AfriCLIRMatrix is a test collection for cross-lingual information retrieval in 15 African languages.
AfriCLIRMatrix covers more African languages than any existing information retrieval test collection.
AfriCLIRMatrix is accompanied by BM25, dense retrieval, and sparse-dense hybrid baselines.
Successive Prompting iteratively breaks down complex questions into simple tasks, solves each simple task, and repeats until the final solution is obtained.
Successive Prompting provides multiple opportunities to query in-context examples at each reasoning step.
Successive Prompting enables learning question decomposition separately from question answering using synthetic data.
Successive Prompting allows using bespoke fine-tuned components for reasoning steps where large language models underperform.
A synthetic dataset generation method bootstraps a model's ability to decompose and answer intermediate questions.
Entropy-based vocabulary substitution (EVS) enables incremental learning in multilingual neural machine translation by processing new language pairs without changing vocabulary size.
Entropy-based vocabulary substitution (EVS) learns new knowledge from updated training samples incrementally while maintaining high translation quality for original language pairs.
Entropy-based vocabulary substitution (EVS) meets high translation quality for both original and incremental languages, low model training cost, and low preprocessing time overhead simultaneously.
Entropy-based vocabulary substitution (EVS) alleviates catastrophic forgetting in incremental learning for multilingual neural machine translation.
Algorithms operate directly on weighted pushdown automata without conversion to context-free grammars.
Algorithms for weighted pushdown automata employ a more general definition of pushdown automaton.
Algorithms for weighted pushdown automata reduce space requirements.
Algorithms for weighted pushdown automata reduce runtime.
An algorithm for weighted pushdown automata is more space-efficient and more time-efficient than Lang's algorithm for the same class of pushdown automata.
Compressive Graph Selector Network captures global structural information in long document question answering in a compressive and iterative manner.
The local graph network builds graph structures of chunked text segments at token, sentence, paragraph, and segment levels to capture short-term dependencies.

The global graph network selectively receives information from each level of the local graph, compresses the information into global graph nodes, and applies graph attention to global graph nodes for long-range reasoning over the entire text in an iterative manner.
The evidence memory network saves selected results from previous steps to alleviate redundancy in evidence selection.
Computational methods measure affective responses to music using listener comments from a Chinese social music platform.
Musical, lyrical, contextual, demographic, and mental health factors have marginal effects on listener affective responses to music.
Discloser user activity shows notable differences in the wang-yi-yun social phenomenon.
An automated procedure collects pairs of sentences with negation and their affirmative interpretations.
A dataset of sentence pairs with negation and their affirmative interpretations is collected.
A plug-and-play neural generator produces affirmative interpretations from negated statements.
Sentence pairs with negation and affirmative interpretations improve T5's ability to generate affirmative interpretations from negated statements.
Sentence pairs with negation and affirmative interpretations improve a RoBERTa-based classifier's performance on natural language inference.
Incorporating a neural generator of affirmative interpretations into a RoBERTa-based classifier improves sentiment analysis performance.
Long-range Context Relation captures correlations of distant clauses in legal contracts.
Term-Definition Relation captures the relation between important terms and their corresponding definitions in legal contracts.
Similar Clause Relation captures similarities between clauses of the same type in legal contracts.
ConReader exploits Long-range Context Relation, Term-Definition Relation, and Similar Clause Relation for contract clause extraction.
ConReader makes contract clause extraction predictions more interpretable.
ConReader achieves state-of-the-art performance on two contract clause extraction tasks in conventional and zero-shot settings.
Fast Weight Layers provide efficient dynamic evaluation for language models by expressing gradient updates as linear attention.
Fast Weight Layers can be applied during training to enable models to learn to use gradient updates.
Fast Weight Layers integrate easily with existing transformer models.
Fast Weight Layers require little extra compute or memory to operate.
Fast Weight Layers improve language modeling perplexity.
The architectural bottleneck principle states that a probe should look exactly like a component to estimate how much information the component could extract.
An attentional probe exactly resembles a transformer's self-attention head.
In BERT, ALBERT, and RoBERTa, a sentence's syntax tree is mostly extractable by the attentional probe.
BERT, ALBERT, and RoBERTa have access to syntactic information while composing their contextual representations.
Popular pre-trained language model-based metrics exhibit higher social bias than traditional metrics on race, gender, religion, physical appearance, age, and socioeconomic status.
The choice of metric paradigm (matching, regression, or generation) has a greater impact on fairness than the choice of pre-trained language models.
Debiasing adapters injected into pre-trained language model layers mitigate bias in language model-based metrics while retaining evaluation performance for text generation.
Pneg generates adversarial negative responses for dialogue response selection by prompting a large-scale language model.
Pneg outperforms other methods for synthesizing adversarial negative responses in dialogue response selection.
Natural language inference models exhibit a bias towards the non-entailment label on instances with low word overlap.
Existing debiasing methods are ineffective for addressing the bias towards non-entailment on low word-overlap instances in natural language inference.
The word overlap bias in natural language inference models does not stem from pre-training.
Eliminating minority examples does not affect the generalizability of debiasing methods for the word overlap bias in natural language inference.
ReLiSt uses a relationship selector that specifies a latent variable to pick the relationship for the next sentence.
ReLiSt uses a story continuer that generates the next sentence while expressing the selected relationship coherently.
ReLiSt generates stories with relationships that are more faithful to desired relationships while maintaining content quality.
ReLiSt provides interpretability through relationship assignments to sentences during inference.
Dialogs for connected memories enable users to search personal media collections through multi-turn, interactive conversations.
COMET is a task-oriented dialog dataset grounded in simulated personal memory graphs.
A multimodal dialog simulator generates synthetic dialog flows grounded in memory graphs.
Four tasks benchmark meaningful progress for connected memory dialog systems.
A new dataset for emoji prediction in Hindi contains tweets augmented from a smaller set of unique tweets.
CausalFedGSD is a federated learning algorithm that balances model performance and user privacy.
CausalFedGSD achieves comparable performance to centralized models for emoji prediction while reducing data requirements and minimizing privacy risks.
SCROLLS is a suite of tasks that require reasoning over long texts.
SCROLLS consists of datasets with naturally long texts.
SCROLLS contains summarization, question answering, and natural language inference tasks across multiple domains including literature, science, business, and entertainment.
SCROLLS is challenging for language models.
SumMC generates a guided summary of the context conditioning on the mention for entity linking.
SumMC formulates entity linking as a multiple-choice problem to select an entity from candidate entities.
A new entity linking dataset links noun phrases from WikiHow to Wikidata.
SumMC achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised performance for entity linking on multiple datasets.
The DocRED dataset annotation is incomplete, resulting in prevalent false negative relation instances.
Re-DocRED is a revised DocRED dataset that includes additional relation triples for previously missed annotations.
Document-level relation extraction models achieve improved performance when trained and evaluated on Re-DocRED compared to the original DocRED.
Multi-head cross attentions model atomic-level congruity between text and image for sarcasm detection.
Graph neural networks model composition-level congruity between text and image for sarcasm detection.
Knowledge resources such as image captions are integrated into multi-modal sarcasm detection.
GPT-3 explanations are as grammatical as human explanations for both easy and hard test samples.
GPT-3 explanations are supportive of the data labels for easy examples.
Human explanations are more generalizable than GPT-3 explanations for easy examples.
Human explanations are more supportive of the data labels and more generalizable than GPT-3 explanations for hard examples.
The hardness of in-context examples impacts the quality of GPT-3 explanations.
Sample hardness impacts the supportiveness and generalizability of human explanations, but to a lesser extent than for model-generated explanations.
An energy-based interpretation explains why adversarial training degrades fluency in text style transfer.
A text style transfer approach incorporates a pretrained language model by restructuring the discriminator and generator to enable both components to leverage the pretrained model.
Text style transfer with a pretrained language model incorporated via discriminator and generator restructuring achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks.
Using past experience on semantically similar subgraphs reduces learning difficulty and promotes performance in knowledge base question generation.
DSM models diverse subgraphs with a meta-learner for knowledge base question generation.
A graph contrastive learning-based retriever identifies semantically similar subgraphs for knowledge base question generation.
DSM achieves state-of-the-art performance on knowledge base question generation benchmarks.
DSM benefits question answering tasks when used as a data augmentation method.
CONQRR rewrites conversational questions in dialogue context into standalone questions.
CONQRR uses a novel reward function to directly optimize retrieval performance via reinforcement learning.
CONQRR can be adapted to any off-the-shelf retriever.
CONQRR achieves state-of-the-art performance on an open-domain conversational question answering dataset with conversations from three different sources.
CONQRR is robust to out-of-domain dialogues and to zero query rewriting supervision.
Opinion-aspect pairs and implicit sentences represent a review as a mix of structured and unstructured data.
A method synthesizes training pairs with opinion-aspect pairs and implicit sentences as input and a textual summary as output.
A summarization model uses an opinion-aspect pair encoder and an implicit sentence encoder.
Argumentative Essay Generation (AEG) is a task for automatically generating argumentative essays with strong persuasiveness from writing prompts.
ArgEssay is a large-scale dataset for argumentative essay generation.

A dual-decoder Transformer model for argumentative essay generation uses a planning decoder to generate a sequence for essay content planning and a writing decoder that incorporates the planning information to write an essay.
A dual-decoder model for argumentative essay generation is pre-trained on a large news dataset.

A dual-decoder model with content planning for argumentative essay generation generates more coherent and persuasive argumentative essays with higher diversity and less repetition than baseline models.

AdaMix introduces and tunes a mixture of adaptation modules corresponding to a selected parameter-efficient fine-tuning method in each Transformer layer of a pre-trained language model while keeping most model weights frozen.
AdaMix matches the computational cost and the number of tunable parameters of the underlying parameter-efficient fine-tuning method.
AdaMix outperforms state-of-the-art parameter-efficient fine-tuning and full model fine-tuning for natural language understanding and natural language generation tasks.

Training generative language models with global differential privacy, natural language prompts, and the prompt-mismatch loss generates synthetic textual datasets with specific desired attributes and statistical properties of the training data.

Synthetic textual datasets generated by training generative language models with global differential privacy, natural language prompts, and the prompt-mismatch loss do not leak information from the original data.
Synthetic textual datasets generated by training generative language models with global differential privacy, natural language prompts, and the prompt-mismatch loss are fluent and accurate.

Training classifiers on private synthetic data generated by training generative language models with global differential privacy, natural language prompts, and the prompt-mismatch loss outperforms training classifiers with DP-SGD.
NewsClaims is a benchmark for attribute-aware claim detection in the news domain.
The claim detection problem is extended to include extraction of additional attributes such as the claimer and the main object.
NewsClaims benchmarks claim detection systems on emerging scenarios with unseen topics and limited training data.
Zero-shot and prompt-based baselines show promising performance on NewsClaims but remain behind human performance.
COCO-DR combats distribution shifts in zero-shot dense retrieval.
COCO-DR uses continuous contrastive learning to adapt language models to target distributions by continuing pretraining on target corpora.
COCO-DR uses implicit distributionally robust optimization to reweight samples from source query clusters to improve robustness over rare queries.
COCO-DR outperforms significantly larger zero-shot dense retrieval models on the BEIR benchmark.
In zero-shot dense retrieval, effectiveness in combating distribution shifts correlates with improved zero-shot accuracy.
After prompt tuning for specific tasks, some neurons in pre-trained Transformers, termed skill neurons, have activations that are highly predictive of task labels.
Perturbing skill neurons in pre-trained Transformers causes a significant performance drop on the corresponding tasks.
Skill neurons are task-specific, with similar tasks having similar distributions of skill neurons.

Skill neurons are generated during pre-training of Transformers, as they remain crucial for tasks under other fine-tuning methods that freeze neuron weights, including adapter-based tuning and BitFit.
CLIPBERTSCORE combines CLIPScore to detect image-summary factuality and BERTScore to detect document-summary factuality.
Human judgments of factuality for documents and images constitute a meta-evaluation benchmark for multimodal factuality metrics.
CLIPBERTSCORE achieves higher correlations with human judgment than existing factuality metrics for document summarization in the zero-shot setting.
CLIPBERTSCORE performs competitively with strong multimodal factuality metrics that are fine-tuned for the task.
CLIPBERTSCORE improves factuality of multimodal summary generation when employed as a reward in reinforcement learning.
Existing NLP task definitions for fact-checking cannot refute misinformation as professional fact-checkers do for the majority of claims.
Evidence for realistic fact-checking must be sufficient to refute the claim.
Evidence for realistic fact-checking must not be leaked from existing fact-checking articles.
All existing fact-checking datasets fail to provide evidence that is both sufficient to refute the claim and not leaked from existing fact-checking articles.
Models trained on a large-scale fact-checking dataset rely on leaked evidence and are unsuitable in real-world scenarios.
The deterioration of kNN-MT performance due to noisy retrieved pairs results from not fully exploiting the prediction of the NMT model.
Confidence-enhanced kNN-MT uses NMT confidence to refine the modeling of kNN distribution and the interpolation weight.
Confidence-enhanced kNN-MT injects two types of perturbations into the retrieved pairs for robust training.
Confidence-enhanced kNN-MT exhibits better robustness than current kNN-MT models.
InstructDial consists of a repository of 48 diverse dialogue tasks in a unified text-to-text format created from 59 openly available dialogue datasets.
InstructDial enables zero-shot performance on unseen dialogue datasets and tasks including dialogue evaluation and intent detection.
InstructDial enables better few-shot performance than zero-shot performance on unseen dialogue datasets and tasks.
InstructDial incorporates novel meta-tasks to ensure models adhere to instructions.
Generating composite topics by exploiting co-occurrences within the original set of topics decouples the strict interpretation of topics from the original neural topic model.
A combinatorial formulation selects a final set of composite topics from a candidate pool.
ELMER explicitly models token dependency during non-autoregressive text generation.
ELMER exits token generation at lower layers for more confident tokens.
Layer Permutation Language Modeling pre-trains ELMER by permuting the exit layer for each token.
ELMER narrows the performance gap between non-autoregressive and autoregressive pre-trained language models in text generation.
ELMER achieves faster inference than autoregressive pre-trained language models.
An interpretability method tracks input tokens' attributions for both source sentence and target prefix contexts in Neural Machine Translation.
An interpretability method is applicable to any encoder-decoder Transformer-based model.
FormLM models online forms by enhancing a pre-trained language model with form structural information.
FormLM recommends form creation ideas, including question and options recommendations and block type suggestions.
The first public dataset of online forms enables training and evaluation of models for online forms.
FormLM outperforms general-purpose language models on question recommendation and block type suggestion tasks.
Catastrophic forgetting in Continual Learning for Named Entity Recognition is caused by the missing causal effect from old data.
A unified causal framework retrieves causality from both new entity types and Other-Class in Continual Learning for Named Entity Recognition.
Curriculum learning mitigates label noise in Continual Learning for Named Entity Recognition.
A self-adaptive weight balances the causal effects between new entity types and Other-Class in Continual Learning for Named Entity Recognition.
A corpus of social media posts about conflict contains 13,000 annotators and 210,000 judgements of social norms.
An experimental setup uses personalization methods to model annotators for predicting social norm perceptions.
ROUGE is unsuitable for the evaluation of Semantic Overlap Summarization.
SEM-F1 evaluates Semantic Overlap Summarization using sentence-level precision and recall.
SEM-F1 has higher correlation with human judgment and higher inter-rater agreement than ROUGE when evaluating Semantic Overlap Summarization.
Non-parametric domain adaptation for end-to-end speech translation leverages in-domain text translation corpus.

Non-parametric domain adaptation for end-to-end speech translation incorporates an additional encoder to unify the decoder's output representations for text and speech translation by reducing representation mismatch in triplet training data.

Non-parametric domain adaptation for end-to-end speech translation uses a k-nearest-neighbor classifier with an external datastore built from domain-specific text translation corpus and universal output representation to produce the final translation distribution.
Non-parametric domain adaptation for end-to-end speech translation outperforms in-domain fine-tuning when using only in-domain text translation data.
A new objective penalizes low mutual information to increase mutual information in multi-domain Neural Machine Translation.
Increasing mutual information in multi-domain Neural Machine Translation results in domain-specialized models.
Comment-Aware Self-Training integrates cross-modal semantics by capturing hinting features from user comments retrieved by jointly leveraging visual and lingual similarity.
Comment-Aware Self-Training employs self-training in a teacher-student framework for multimodal classification tasks.
Comment-Aware Self-Training achieves better performance than previous state-of-the-art models that do not employ comment modeling or self-training.
Considering the similarity between context and switched tokens produces grammatically consistent code-switched sentences during training and inference.
Basic, Tiniest Subword (BTS) units form Korean letters by adding strokes or combining them.
The BTS-based word embedding framework generates Korean word embeddings.
BTS units improve Korean word embedding performance on intrinsic and extrinsic tasks.
An experimental framework characterizes the impact of sparsification on multilingual pre-trained language models during fine-tuning.
Compression may improve model robustness over dense models.
Under certain sparsification regimes, compression may aid the performance of low-resource languages.

Super-NaturalInstructions is a benchmark of 1,616 diverse NLP tasks with expert-written instructions covering 76 distinct task types for evaluating cross-task generalization under instructions by training on a subset of tasks and evaluating on unseen tasks.
Tk-Instruct is a transformer model trained to follow in-context instructions including plain language task definitions and k-shot examples.
Tk-Instruct outperforms existing instruction-following models in cross-task generalization under instructions with a significantly smaller model size.
CoCoa converts monolingual Hindi text to Hindi-English code-switched text with fine-grained control over the degree of code-switching and lexical choices for formality.
CoCoa synthesizes code-switched text that is faithful to syntactic and lexical attributes relevant to code-switching at test-time.
Human evaluations establish that the generated code-switched text is of superior quality and faithful to desired attributes.
CoCoa reduces perplexity on a language modeling task compared to competitive baselines.
CoCoa improves performance on a downstream code-switched sentiment analysis task compared to competitive baselines.
T-STAR uses Abstract Meaning Representation graphs as a style-agnostic intermediate representation for text style transfer.
T-STAR improves content preservation in text style transfer with negligible loss in style accuracy.
T-STAR reduces hallucinations in text style transfer.
CodeRetriever learns function-level code semantic representations through large-scale code-text contrastive pre-training.
CodeRetriever uses an unsupervised approach to build semantic-related code pairs from documentation and function names for unimodal contrastive learning.
CodeRetriever builds code-text pairs from documentation and in-line comments for bimodal contrastive learning.
CodeRetriever achieves state-of-the-art performance on code search tasks across multiple programming languages and at function-level, snippet-level, and statement-level granularities.
EusCrawl is a Basque corpus created by manually identifying and scraping websites with high-quality content.
EusCrawl has higher quality than mC4 and CC100 according to native annotators.
Pre-training on EusCrawl, mC4, or CC100 yields similar performance on downstream NLU tasks for Basque.
NLU performance in low-resource languages is not primarily constrained by data quality, and factors like corpus size and domain coverage can be more important.
AdapterShare explicitly models task correlation among multiple natural language understanding tasks.
AdapterShare is automatically learned based on gradients computed on tiny held-out validation data.
AdapterShare outperforms AdapterFusion on five dialogue understanding tasks and natural language understanding tasks.
A dialogue generation model with a transferable latent structure enables lightweight and transparent transfer from general-domain dialogue data to downstream tasks.
A dialogue generation model with a transferable latent structure yields better dialogue responses than strong baselines in automatic and human evaluations.
A dialogue generation model with a transferable latent structure uses fewer parameters and runs faster than strong baselines.
A dialogue generation model with discrete latent variables is explainable by interpreting those variables.

SCOPE uses a shared encoder and two parallel decoders, one for Chinese spelling check and one for fine-grained Chinese pronunciation prediction, with an adaptive weighting scheme to balance the two tasks.
An iterative correction strategy improves Chinese spelling check during inference.
SCOPE achieves state-of-the-art performance on three Chinese spelling check benchmarks.
Adaptivity and granularity of the auxiliary Chinese pronunciation prediction task positively affect Chinese spelling check performance.
A larger number of training instructions is critical for instruction tuning.
Instructions are underutilized in instruction tuning due to the scarcity of labeled data.
Unlabeled Data Augmented Instruction Tuning (UDIT) constructs pseudo-labeled data from unlabeled plain texts for instruction tuning.
Trained prompt tokens can degrade downstream task performance.
XPrompt eliminates negative prompt tokens through hierarchical structured pruning at different granularity levels.
XPrompt closes the performance gap between prompt tuning and fine-tuning for models of moderate and small scales.
In-context learning performance is highly unstable across samples of examples.
Example selection for in-context learning is formulated as a sequential decision problem.
A reinforcement learning algorithm identifies generalizable policies for selecting demonstration examples in in-context learning.
Example selection policies for in-context learning generalize to unseen tasks.
Diminishing improvement of example selection policies on larger language models suggests emerging capabilities of large language models.
A dataset contains color-blind participants' spontaneous linguistic productions of color terms in online discussion platforms.
Red-green color-blind speakers use the "red" and "green" color terms in less predictable contexts than normal-sighted speakers.
Red-green color-blind speakers use the "red" and "green" color terms in linguistic environments evoking mental image to a lower extent than normal-sighted speakers.
Reorder and then Parse transforms discontinuous constituent trees into pseudo-continuous ones by reordering words in the sentence.
Reorder and then Parse models the relative position changes of words as a list of actions.
Reorder and then Parse achieves state-of-the-art results on two discontinuous constituency treebanks.
Reorder and then Parse shows a distinct speed advantage in discontinuous constituency parsing.
Large pre-trained models output common sense and summarize facts that cannot be directly retrieved from search engines.
Posterior-based reweighing treats generated knowledge as a noisy source in dialogue generation.
Noisy training strategy treats generated knowledge as a noisy source in dialogue generation.
Constraining context access in neural language models improves simulation of human reading behavior.
Gaps in context access between neural language models and humans are associated with specific syntactic constructions.
A manually constructed counterfactual dataset for Logic2Text containing logical forms with rare co-occurrences of table headers and logical form operators and their natural language references.
State-of-the-art Logic2Text models exhibit degraded performance on counterfactual samples compared to the original test set.
A Logic2Text model that incorporates the hierarchical structure of logical forms.
A Logic2Text training procedure that uses automatically generated counterfactual data for training.
Structure-Aware FLONET (SA-FLONET) infuses structural constraints derived from the connectivity structure of flowcharts into the RAG framework.
Structure-Aware FLONET (SA-FLONET) uses natural language inference to predict the polarity of indirect yes/no answers.
Structure-Aware FLONET (SA-FLONET) achieves higher success rates than FLONET in flowchart grounded response generation tasks.
Structure-Aware FLONET (SA-FLONET) achieves higher success rates than FLONET in zero-shot flowchart grounded response generation tasks.
A textless speech emotion conversion method decomposes the speech signal into discrete learned representations of phonetic-content units, prosodic features, speaker, and emotion.
A textless speech emotion conversion method translates phonetic-content units to a target emotion to modify speech content.
A textless speech emotion conversion method predicts prosodic features based on the translated phonetic-content units.
A textless speech emotion conversion method models non-verbal vocalizations including laughter insertion and yawning removal.
A textless speech emotion conversion method outperforms current approaches and text-based systems in perceived emotion and audio quality.
An ablation study evaluates the components of a textless speech emotion conversion method.
Regional identity (US vs UK) of emerging slang word meanings is predictable from historical slang records.
Both communicative need and semantic distinction contribute to slang semantic variation and their relative importance fluctuates throughout history.
dial2vec performs self-guided contrastive learning for unsupervised dialogue embeddings.
dial2vec captures interaction patterns between interlocutors to guide the learning of interlocutor embeddings.
A benchmark for dialogue embeddings evaluation uses six dialogue datasets and three tasks: domain categorization, semantic relatedness, and dialogue retrieval.
dial2vec obtains informative and discriminative embeddings for interlocutors by leveraging conversational interactions.
interlocutor-level pooling is the best aggregation strategy for dial2vec.
NER fine-tuning in the source language causes tokens to increasingly attend to themselves.
SLICER divides transformed token representations and the classifier into disjoint slices that are independently classified during training for named entity recognition.
SLICER reduces the extent to which named entity tokens attend to themselves.
SLICER yields consistent transfer gains for named entity recognition to low-resource languages, especially when the target language is distant from the source language.

Utterance Relational Reasoner implicitly extracts dependencies among dialogue utterances and between dialogue utterances and candidate response options and uses relational graph convolutional networks for reasoning in multi-turn response selection.
Option Dual Comparator perceives differences between candidate response options through dual comparison to reduce interference from noise options in multi-turn response selection.
Implicit Relational Reasoning Graph Network achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MuTual and MuTualplus benchmarks for multi-turn response selection.
Implicit Relational Reasoning Graph Network is the first model to surpass human performance on the MuTual benchmark for multi-turn response selection.
CoPlan alleviates cascading errors in planning-based radiology report generation by coordinating a content planner and a text generator during training and inference.
CoPlan improves content order in time series critical radiology report generation scenarios.
CoPlan improves clinical factual accuracy in time series irrelevant radiology report generation scenarios.
Language Model Decomposition (LMD) represents a language model using a linear combination of other language models as basis.
Language Model Decomposition (LMD) has a closed-form solution.
A goodness-of-fit metric for Language Model Decomposition, similar to the coefficient of determination, measures the linear dependency of a set of language models.
BERT and eleven BERT-like language models are highly linearly dependent.
POET pre-trains language models using programs and their execution results.
POET enables language models to harvest reasoning knowledge from program executors via a data-driven approach.
POET can be instantiated by different kinds of program executors.
POET enhances language model performance in numerical reasoning, logical reasoning, and multi-hop reasoning.
EDIN evaluates unknown entity discovery and indexing in temporally segmented settings for entities not in the knowledge base and without descriptions and labeled mentions.
EDIN poses additional challenges compared to zero-shot entity linking.
EDIN-pipeline performs end-to-end detection, clustering, and indexing of unknown entity mentions in context.
Indexing a single embedding per entity that unifies information from multiple mentions outperforms indexing mentions independently.
SMaLL-100 is a shallow distilled multilingual machine translation model for 100 low-resource languages.
SMaLL-100 uses uniform sampling across all language pairs during training.
SMaLL-100 achieves higher translation quality than previous massively multilingual models of similar size while reducing inference latency and memory usage on low-resource benchmarks.
SMaLL-100 achieves comparable translation quality to M2M-100 (1.2B) with reduced model size and faster inference.
UniRef unifies Reference Expression Generation and Reference Expression Comprehension.
The Image-Region-Text Fusion layer fuses image, region, and text using image cross-attention and region cross-attention.
The Image-Region-Text Fusion layer generates pseudo input regions for Reference Expression Comprehension to share a uniform representation space with Reference Expression Generation.
Vision-conditioned Masked Language Modeling is a pre-training objective for Reference Expression Generation.
Text-Conditioned Region Prediction is a pre-training objective for Reference Expression Comprehension.
UniRef outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods on Reference Expression Generation and Reference Expression Comprehension.
Cross-modal contrastive learning suffers from incorrect normalization of the sum retrieval probabilities of each text or video instance.
Many test instances are over- or under-represented during retrieval in cross-modal contrastive learning, significantly hurting retrieval performance.

Normalized Contrastive Learning (NCL) uses the Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm to compute instance-wise biases that normalize the sum retrieval probabilities of each text or video instance for fair representation during cross-modal retrieval.

Normalized Contrastive Learning (NCL) achieves state-of-the-art text-video retrieval performance across multiple model architectures on ActivityNet, MSVD, and MSR-VTT without modifying model architectures.
Co-guiding Net uses a two-stage framework to achieve mutual guidances between multiple intent detection and slot filling.
Co-guiding Net uses two heterogeneous graph attention networks on two heterogeneous semantics-label graphs to represent relations among semantics nodes and label nodes.
Cross-modal similarity from a pretrained vision-language model measures learning difficulty for image captioning curriculum learning.

Curriculum learning for image captioning using cross-modal similarity from a pretrained vision-language model achieves superior performance and competitive convergence speed without requiring heuristics or additional training costs.

Curriculum learning for image captioning using cross-modal similarity from a pretrained vision-language model improves performance on difficult examples and unseen data, demonstrating enhanced generalization.
Simultaneous learning of extractive summarization and text segmentation produces robust sentence representations.
An optimization-based regularizer promotes diversity in selected summary sentences for extractive summarization.
Joint text segmentation and extractive summarization achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard extractive summarization benchmarks.
Text segmentation improves cross-genre transferability in extractive summarization.
Existing formats for casting sequence tagging as a Seq2Seq task commonly exhibit hallucination.
A new format for casting sequence tagging as a Seq2Seq task is more robust and almost completely devoid of hallucination.
The new format for casting sequence tagging as a Seq2Seq task improves multilingual sequence tagging in zero-shot transfer learning and joint training settings.
MuRAG is the first multimodal retrieval-augmented transformer that accesses an external non-parametric multimodal memory to augment language generation.
MuRAG is pre-trained on a mixture of large-scale image-text and text-only corpora with a joint contrastive and generative loss.
MuRAG achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on question answering tasks that require retrieving and reasoning over both images and text.
monoQA is a T5-based text generation model that uses multi-task learning for simultaneous passage reranking and direct answer extraction.
monoQA outperforms existing strong baselines on the OR-QuAC and OR-CoQA datasets.
