Two-stage reranking for grammatical error correction uses an edit generator to propose edits and a scorer to classify edits as correct or false.
Two-stage reranking achieves state-of-the-art results on the BEA 2019 English dataset when using a weak edit generator.
Two-stage reranking achieves state-of-the-art results on the BEA development and test sets when using a larger model.
Two-stage reranking achieves state-of-the-art results on Russian grammatical error correction despite using smaller models and less data than previous approaches.
Two-stage reranking can use edit generators implemented as either encoder-decoder or sequence labeling models.
HEGEL captures high-order cross-sentence relations for long document summarization.
HEGEL updates and learns sentence representations using hypergraph transformer layers.
HEGEL fuses different types of sentence dependencies including latent topics, keywords coreference, and section structure.
Cross-stitch bi-encoders enable full interaction between text encoders and knowledge graph encoders for distantly-supervised relation extraction.

A cross-stitch mechanism for distantly-supervised relation extraction shares and updates representations between text encoders and knowledge graph encoders at any layer with dynamically controlled sharing via cross-attention-based gates.
Full interaction between text encoders and knowledge graph encoders yields strong improvements in distantly-supervised relation extraction.
Counterfactual data augmentation via perspective transition automatically augments high-quality responses with different semantics for open-domain dialogues.
A counterfactual generation model infers semantically different responses by replacing the observed reply perspective with substituted perspectives.
A data selection method filters out detrimental augmented responses.
Counterfactual data augmentation via perspective transition outperforms competitive baselines on multiple downstream tasks.
Large language models outperform existing zero-shot and few-shot baselines for clinical information extraction without being trained specifically for the clinical domain.

Large language models can be leveraged for clinical information extraction tasks requiring structured outputs, including span identification, token-level sequence classification, and relation extraction.
New datasets for few-shot clinical information extraction benchmarking are created through manual re-annotation of the CASI dataset for new tasks.

FactorSum factorizes abstractive summarization into two steps via an energy function: generating abstractive summary views that cover salient information in subsets of the input document and combining them into a final summary following a budget and content guidance.
FactorSum achieves higher ROUGE scores on long document summarization benchmarks including PubMed, arXiv, and GovReport.
FactorSum outperforms PEGASUS trained in the target domain in domain adaptation for long document summarization when trained only on PubMed.
FactorSum achieves performance gains in long document summarization through more flexible budget adaptation and processing of shorter contexts from partial document views.
The performance of the cross-encoder re-ranker in cross-lingual dense retrieval is heavily influenced by the number of training samples and the quality of negative samples.
A query generator used as the teacher for dual-encoder retrievers in cross-lingual dense retrieval reduces dependency on the number of training samples and the quality of negative samples.
A novel enhancement method for dual-encoder retrievers aligns queries from different languages using the query generator without requiring additional parallel sentences.
Dual-encoder retrievers for cross-lingual dense retrieval using a query generator as the teacher and the novel enhancement method outperform state-of-the-art methods on two benchmark datasets.
Multilingual embedding spaces contain a low-rank subspace that primarily encodes non-semantic information such as syntactic information.
Singular value decomposition applied to multiple monolingual corpora identifies a low-rank subspace encoding non-semantic information in multilingual embedding spaces.
Projecting multilingual embeddings into the null space of the low-rank subspace encoding non-semantic information increases language agnosticism without finetuning.
Pre-trained masked language models are connected to non-autoregressive generation for machine translation.
XLM-D transforms an off-the-shelf cross-lingual pre-training model into a non-autoregressive translation model with a lightweight decorator.
The decorator in XLM-D ensures representation consistency of the pre-trained model.
The decorator in XLM-D introduces only one additional trainable parameter.
XLM-D achieves state-of-the-art performance on machine translation benchmarks.
XLM-D achieves a large inference speed-up for machine translation.
Self-supervised graph masking pre-training does not require supervision signals and does not adjust the architecture of pre-trained encoder-decoder models.
Self-supervised graph masking pre-training applied to T5 achieves state-of-the-art results on WebNLG+2020 and EventNarrative graph-to-text generation.
MT-GenEval evaluates gender accuracy in machine translation from English to eight widely-spoken languages.

MT-GenEval provides realistic, gender-balanced, counterfactual data with unambiguous gender in the input segment for gender accuracy evaluation in machine translation from English to eight widely-spoken languages.
MT-GenEval includes multi-sentence segments requiring inter-sentential gender agreement for gender accuracy evaluation in machine translation from English to eight widely-spoken languages.
ONE2SET exhibits serious calibration errors, particularly over-estimation of the 'no corresponding keyphrase' token.
ONE2SET introduces excessive 'no corresponding keyphrase' tokens as padding in training instances.
The training mechanism of ONE2SET for assigning targets to slots is unstable and aggravates over-estimation of the 'no corresponding keyphrase' token.
WR-ONE2SET extends ONE2SET with an adaptive instance-level cost weighting strategy that dynamically penalizes over-estimated slots to smooth the uneven training distribution.
WR-ONE2SET extends ONE2SET with a target re-assignment mechanism that refines slot assignments and reduces supervisory signals of over-estimated slots.
Prompt-Based Meta-Learning structurally combines meta-learning and prompt-tuning for few-shot text classification.
Prompt-Based Meta-Learning assigns label word learning to base-learners and template learning to the meta-learner.
Prompt-Based Meta-Learning achieves state-of-the-art performance on few-shot text classification across multiple datasets.
Prompt-Based Meta-Learning reduces the data requirement for meta-training in few-shot text classification.
Prompt-Based Meta-Learning improves the convergence of the prompting method in few-shot text classification.
CA-WEAT quantifies biases with multilingual cultural awareness.
Human biases in monolingual static embeddings vary across languages and are not universal.
Human biases are less evident in contextual embedding models, and the original human association might be reversed.
Multilinguality attenuates and reverses human biases, particularly in contextual multilingual embedding models.
Top-down parsing generates less memory load for right-branching English.
Bottom-up parsing is less memory-demanding for Chinese.
The brain utilizes bottom-up parsing strategies for Chinese.
The brain utilizes top-down parsing strategies for English.
The brain adopts parsing strategies with less memory processing load according to language structure.
Multi-level Multilingual Knowledge Distillation (MMKD) distills semantic knowledge at token, word, sentence, and structure levels for pre-training multilingual language models.

Token-level, word-level, sentence-level, and structure-level alignment objectives encourage consistency between source and target language representations and correlation similarity between teacher and student models.
MMKD outperforms baseline models of similar size on cross-lingual natural language inference and question answering tasks.
MMKD achieves significant performance gains on low-resource languages in cross-lingual natural language understanding.
ATTEMPT uses an attention module to interpolate source prompts and a target prompt for each target task instance.
ATTEMPT updates only the target task prompt and attention weights shared across tasks during training.
ATTEMPT overcomes the instability of prompt tuning.
ATTEMPT significantly outperforms prompt tuning and matches or exceeds fully fine-tuned models and other parameter-efficient methods on diverse NLP tasks.
ATTEMPT outperforms previous work in few-shot learning settings.
Adaptive Label Smoothing with Self-Knowledge dynamically adjusts the smoothing parameter per instance based on the model probability distribution in natural language generation.
Adaptive Label Smoothing with Self-Knowledge uses self-knowledge as a prior label distribution for softening target labels in natural language generation.
Knowledge distillation provides a regularization effect in label smoothing.
Dynamic adjustment of the smoothing parameter provides a regularization effect in label smoothing.
Adaptive Label Smoothing with Self-Knowledge improves model generalization and calibration in natural language generation.

A thorough evaluation of multilingual sentence embeddings and Abstract Meaning Representation retrofitted multilingual sentence embeddings is conducted on five transfer tasks in downstream applications.
Retrofitting multilingual sentence embeddings with Abstract Meaning Representation achieves state-of-the-art performance on semantic textual similarity and transfer tasks.
Concadia is a Wikipedia-based dataset of images with corresponding English-language descriptions, captions, and surrounding context.
Augmenting image-to-text models with textual context representations improves the generation of descriptions and captions.
The internal representation of a parent phrase in language models can be predicted by applying an affine transformation to the internal representations of its constituent parts.

The accuracy of predicting a parent phrase's internal representation from the internal representations of its constituent parts in language models does not correlate with human judgments of semantic compositionality.
Language models may not accurately distinguish between compositional and non-compositional phrases.
MoSE learns modality-split relation embeddings for each modality during multimodal knowledge graph completion training.
MoSE combines modality-split predictions using ensemble methods with weights that model modality importance dynamically during multimodal knowledge graph completion inference.
Large-scale intermediate pre-training of decomposition-based transformers uses distant supervision from comparable texts, particularly large-scale parallel news.
DecompT5 outperforms baseline language models in semantic parsing on Overnight and TORQUE.
DecompEntail outperforms state-of-the-art models, including GPT-3, in question answering on HotpotQA and StrategyQA.
POS tag transfer from multiple high-resource languages to a low-resource target language is formalized as graph-based label propagation.
A graph is created with words as nodes and alignment links as edges by aligning words for all language pairs in multilingual sentence translations.
Node labels are propagated from multiple high-resource source languages to a low-resource target language using a Graph Neural Network augmented with transformer layers.
Graph-based multilingual label propagation creates training sets that enable training POS taggers for a diverse set of low-resource languages.
Graph-based multilingual label propagation achieves state-of-the-art performance for unsupervised POS tagging of low-resource languages when combined with enhanced contextualized embeddings.
CLAIMDECOMP provides decompositions of complex claims into yes-no subquestions that cover explicit propositions and implicit facets.
State-of-the-art models generate reasonable subquestions for complex claims but predicting the comprehensive set of subquestions from the original claim without evidence is challenging.
Subquestions decomposing complex claims help identify relevant evidence to fact-check the full claim and derive the veracity through their answers.
Entity Alignment model compatibility can be measured.
Compatibility can be injected into Entity Alignment models.
Compatibility model parameters can be optimized.
Integrating compatibility into state-of-the-art neural Entity Alignment models reduces the labeled data requirement for achieving comparable effectiveness.
A neural transducer is trained using a discriminative parser that hierarchically aligns source and target phrases and a sequence-to-sequence model that translates aligned phrases one-by-one.
A neural transducer has two inference modes: one using only the sequence-to-sequence model at the sequence level, and another combining the parser with the sequence-to-sequence model.
A neural transducer incorporating hierarchical phrases is formalized as a source-conditioned synchronous grammar.
An efficient variational inference algorithm trains neural transducers incorporating hierarchical phrases.
Query-based Instance Discrimination Network uses metric-based comparison between query embeddings and token embeddings to extract relational triples.
Query-based Instance Discrimination Network extracts all types of relational triples in one step.
Query-based Instance Discrimination Network eliminates error propagation in relational triple extraction.
Query-based Instance Discrimination Network learns instance-level representations of relational triples via contrastive learning.
Query-based Instance Discrimination Network produces relational triples that enclose rich class-level semantics and access to high-order global connections.
Entity-to-entity stance detection involves identifying entities in their canonical names and discerning stances jointly.
A new dataset for entity-to-entity stance detection contains sentence-level annotations from news articles of different ideological leanings.
A generative framework for entity-to-entity stance detection generates canonical names for entities and stances among them.
A graph encoder for entity-to-entity stance detection summarizes entity activities and external knowledge about entities.
Entity-to-entity stance detection is useful for understanding media quotation and stance landscape.
Entity-to-entity stance detection is useful for inferring entity ideology.
Hierarchy-guided BERT with Global and Local hierarchies (HBGL) utilizes the large-scale parameters and prior language knowledge of BERT to model both global and local hierarchies.
Hierarchy-guided BERT with Global and Local hierarchies (HBGL) avoids the intentional fusion of semantic and hierarchical modules by directly modeling semantic and hierarchical information with BERT.
A detailed, complete taxonomy categorizes generative Knowledge Graph Construction methods.
The advantages and weaknesses of generative Knowledge Graph Construction methods are categorized by generation target.
Templates are structural elements that directly target compositional generalization in code search.
CTBERT represents code using automatically extracted templates as building blocks.
Templates are complementary to data flow graphs in GraphCodeBERT by enhancing structural context around variables.
Existing continued pretraining methods lack promptability in certain settings.
Continued pretraining incorporating a trainable prompt during multi-task learning improves promptability in zero- and few-shot settings compared to existing methods.
Continued pretraining using MAML-style meta-learning yields subpar promptability.
Text Hallucination Mitigating (THAM) framework mitigates text hallucination in video-grounded dialogue.
Text Hallucination Regularization (THR) loss is derived from an information-theoretic text hallucination measurement approach.
Text Hallucination Mitigating (THAM) framework enhances interpretability of video-grounded dialogue systems.
ProofInfer adds entire layers to the proof tree at each step, generating all nodes in the layer simultaneously.
A divide-and-conquer algorithm encodes the proof tree as plain text without losing structure information.
ProofInfer achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art models with reduced training data.
RobustLR evaluates the logical robustness of language models to minimal logical edits and logical equivalence conditions.
Language models trained on deductive reasoning datasets are not robust to minimal logical edits and logical equivalence conditions.
Language models trained on deductive reasoning datasets have particular difficulty with logical negation operators.
Multi-granularity optimization for non-autoregressive translation collects model behaviours on translation segments of various granularities and integrates feedback for backpropagation.
Multi-granularity optimization for non-autoregressive translation outperforms baseline models trained with cross-entropy loss on multiple WMT benchmarks.
A cross-lingual strategy measures faithfulness of attribution methods in Natural Language Inference using word alignments and eliminates the drawbacks of erasure-based evaluations.
XNLI augmented with highlight-based explanations forms a multilingual Natural Language Inference dataset with highlights.
The attribution methods that are best for plausibility in Natural Language Inference differ from those that are best for faithfulness.
kNN-EC performs non-parametric emotion classification using nearest neighbor retrieval.
BERT-whitening reduces memory storage of the datastore for emotion classification.
BERT-whitening accelerates retrieval speed in emotion classification.
kNN-EC improves emotion classification performance compared to the pre-trained model.
SocioProbe probes sociodemographic knowledge in pre-trained language models using classifier probing and information-theoretic minimum description length probing.
Pre-trained language models encode sociodemographic knowledge such as gender and age.
Sociodemographic knowledge is distributed across multiple layers in some pre-trained language models.
Pre-trained language models require large amounts of pre-training data to acquire sociodemographic knowledge.
Pre-trained language models that excel in general language understanding do not necessarily have more sociodemographic knowledge.
SummaFusion fuses multiple summary candidates to generate a novel abstractive second-stage summary.
SummaFusion improves the qualitative properties of fused summaries on multiple summarization datasets.
SummaFusion is especially effective when fusing summary candidates of lower quality, such as in few-shot summarization setups.
LiteVL adapts the pre-trained image-language model BLIP into a video-text model for downstream tasks without video-language pre-training.
LiteVL adds temporal attention modules with dynamic temporal scaling to the image encoder of BLIP to enhance temporal modeling.
LiteVL uses a non-parametric pooling mechanism to adaptively reweight fine-grained video embeddings conditioned on the text.
LiteVL outperforms previous video-language pre-trained models on text-video retrieval and video question answering without video-language pre-training.
A causal model using probabilities of necessity and sufficiency formalizes the distinction between irrelevant features and features with context-dependent effects in spurious correlations.
The distinction between irrelevant features and features with context-dependent effects in spurious correlations explains the results of existing debiasing methods on different spurious features.

The distinction between irrelevant features and features with context-dependent effects in spurious correlations demystifies the encoding of spurious features in model representations after debiasing.
A tree Transformer uses a new description method for tree structures to encode node positions.
Local and global soft bias are used as positional encodings in a tree Transformer.
A tree Transformer with a new description method for tree structures outperforms strong baselines on code summarization and completion tasks in two programming languages.
Combining local and global paradigms improves model performance for code representation in tree Transformers.
GPT-3 lacks Theory of Mind out-of-the-box on SocialIQa and ToMi tasks.
Large language models struggle substantially at SocialIQa and ToMi tasks.
The limitations of large language models in Theory of Mind arise from their data, neural architecture, and training paradigms.
Topic-aware latent code-guided text generation models use discrete latent codes to model topics.
A span-level bag-of-words training objective encourages discrete latent codes to model topics.
Topic-aware latent code-guided text generation models generate topic-relevant and coherent long texts.

TemporalWiki assesses language models' adaptability to updated knowledge using training data from differences between consecutive snapshots of English Wikipedia and evaluation data from differences between consecutive snapshots of English Wikidata.
Factual knowledge in language models can be safely updated with minimal training data via continual learning.
An elaboration model generates hallucinated documents from reference summaries in abstractive summarization.
An elaboration model generates negative summaries from hallucinated documents in abstractive summarization.
A controlled generator produces faithful and unfaithful summaries conditioned on control codes in abstractive summarization.
Augmenting negative summaries from hallucinated documents improves faithfulness without sacrificing informativeness in abstractive summarization.
GeoMLAMA probes geo-diverse commonsense knowledge in multilingual pre-trained language models.
In multilingual pre-trained language models, larger variants do not necessarily store geo-diverse concepts better than smaller variants of the same model.
Multilingual pre-trained language models are not intrinsically biased towards knowledge from Western countries.
In multilingual pre-trained language models, the native language of a country may not be the best language for probing knowledge about that country.
In multilingual pre-trained language models, a language may better probe knowledge about a non-native country than about the country where that language is native.
Light-weight adapters can be integrated with pre-trained language models and serve as a repository for facts learned from different knowledge bases.
Knowledge Probing using Response Selection (KPRS) measures knowledge in task-oriented dialogue models.
Knowledge injection with light-weight adapters improves performance on knowledge probing using response selection and response generation tasks.
Vocabulary distribution alignment regularizes source-domain vocabulary distributions using Kullback-Leibler divergence.
Feature distribution alignment learns domain-invariant representations across source domains using an adversarial training strategy.
Prompt-based Distribution Alignment achieves state-of-the-art results for domain generalization in sentiment analysis and natural language inference.
DialogConv is a lightweight fully convolutional architecture for response selection.
DialogConv uses convolution exclusively to extract matching features between context and response.

DialogConv models dialogues in three views: embedding view, word view, and utterance view, and applies convolution operations on each view to capture richer semantic information from multiple contextual perspectives.
DialogConv has a smaller model size than state-of-the-art response selection models.
DialogConv achieves faster inference than state-of-the-art response selection models on CPU and GPU devices.
The k-nearest neighbor language model achieves only marginal improvements in few- and zero-shot end-task accuracy over non-retrieval language models.
kNN-Prompt automatically expands verbalizers for end-task class labels using task-specific synonyms.
kNN-Prompt achieves significant improvements in zero-shot end-task accuracy across diverse tasks.
kNN-Prompt enables domain adaptation without further training.
The performance gains of kNN-Prompt increase with the size of the retrieval model.
Cross-modal dilutions maintain relevance and topical coherence with the image and existing text while inducing misclassification in multimodal classifiers.
Cross-modal dilutions maintain higher relevance and topical coherence than baseline dilutions.
Cross-modal dilutions are more effective at demonstrating the brittleness of multimodal classifiers than baseline dilutions.

arXivEdits is an annotated corpus of 751 full scientific papers with gold sentence alignment across multiple revision versions and fine-grained span-level edits with underlying intentions for 1,000 sentence pairs.
A neural CRF sentence alignment model reliably matches sentences between different versions of scientific papers.
Edit extraction in scientific paper revisions is formulated as a span alignment problem.
A span alignment method extracts more fine-grained and explainable edits in scientific paper revisions than the diff algorithm.
An intention classifier classifies the underlying intentions of fine-grained edits in scientific paper revisions.
Existing domain-adaptive pre-training methods are suboptimal.
Soft-masking attention heads based on their importance preserves general knowledge in language models during domain adaptation.
Contrasting representations from a general language model and a domain-adapted language model learns an integrated representation that combines general and domain-specific knowledge.
JANUS jointly trains autoregressive and non-autoregressive models using an auxiliary loss.
JANUS simultaneously enhances model performance in autoregressive and non-autoregressive generation.
JANUS alleviates the distribution discrepancy problem.
BART-JANUS supports non-autoregressive generation.
JANUS transfers autoregressive knowledge to non-autoregressive generation.
JANUS improves performance on multiple sequence generation tasks.
Information-Transport-based Simultaneous Translation (ITST) treats translation as information transport from source to target.
ITST decides whether to translate the current target token based on the accumulated received information from source tokens.
ITST achieves state-of-the-art performance on text-to-text simultaneous translation and speech-to-text simultaneous translation tasks.
Knowledge Dependent Answerability (KDA) measures the answerability of multiple choice questions given knowledge of the target fact.
Knowledge Dependent Answerability (KDA) is measured using student responses from a human survey.
KDA_disc and KDA_cont approximate Knowledge Dependent Answerability (KDA) by leveraging pre-trained language models to imitate students' problem-solving behavior.
KDA_disc and KDA_cont combined with n-gram based similarity metrics have strong predictive power for expert-labeled multiple choice question quality measures.
Q-TOD extracts essential dialogue context information into a natural language query to retrieve relevant knowledge records for response generation.
Q-TOD uses a natural language query not confined to the knowledge base schema to alleviate domain adaptation issues.
Q-TOD decouples knowledge retrieval from response generation to eliminate knowledge base scalability issues.
Three publicly available task-oriented dialogue datasets include query annotations.
Q-TOD outperforms strong baselines and sets a new state of the art on three task-oriented dialogue datasets.

Compositionality-aware neural-symbolic inference for graph parsing uses model confidence to perform fine-grained reasoning on nodes and edges, specifically targeting those with high uncertainty in the neural parser.
Accounting for compositionality and uncertainty in neural-symbolic inference for graph parsing outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods that do not account for these aspects.
One type of rule (conjunction-of-disjunctions or disjunction-of-conjunctions) has an advantage over the other for achieving high quality knowledge base completion.
Logical neural networks learn both conjunction-of-disjunctions and disjunction-of-conjunctions rules for knowledge base completion via end-to-end gradient-based optimization.
The LNN-based approach for learning rules in knowledge base completion improves performance over state-of-the-art rule-based methods.
Combining the LNN-based approach with knowledge graph embeddings improves performance for knowledge base completion.
TET uses a local transformer to infer missing entity types by independently encoding information from each neighbor.
TET uses a global transformer to aggregate information from all neighbors into a single long sequence for reasoning about complex entity types.
TET uses a context transformer to integrate neighbor content through information exchange between neighbor pairs while preserving the graph structure.
TET uses class membership information of types to semantically strengthen entity representations.
CONDAQA is the first English reading comprehension dataset that requires reasoning about the implications of negated statements in paragraphs.

CONDAQA includes clusters of question-answer pairs generated by paraphrasing the negated statement, changing the scope of the negation, and reversing the negation to prevent models from using spurious shortcuts.
CONDAQA is challenging for current state-of-the-art models.
SLING evaluates linguistic knowledge in Chinese language models using minimal sentence pairs for syntactic and semantic phenomena.
SLING's minimal pairs are derived by applying syntactic and lexical transformations to naturally-occurring, linguist-annotated sentences from the Chinese Treebank 9.0.
Language models achieve lower accuracy than humans on the SLING benchmark.
BERT-base-zh achieves higher accuracy on the SLING benchmark than larger language models.
Most language models exhibit a strong gender and number bias on the linguistic phenomena in the SLING benchmark.
Language models perform better on local linguistic phenomena than hierarchical ones on the SLING benchmark.
A dataset of U.S. high school newspaper articles written by students from across the country measures language ideologies in text data selection.
Newspapers from larger schools in wealthier, more educated, and urban areas are more likely to be classified as high quality by the GPT-3 quality filter.
The GPT-3 quality filter's quality assessment is unaligned with factuality and literary acclaim.
A generative self-supervised learning objective for sentence representation reconstructs phrases by breaking sentences into important phrases.

Generative self-supervised sentence representation learning based on phrase reconstruction outperforms state-of-the-art contrastive methods on semantic textual similarity benchmarks and downstream semantic retrieval and reranking tasks.
Conversion between real and complex hyperbolic space via fast Fourier transform enables relation-specific geometric transformations in complex hyperbolic knowledge graph embeddings.
Fourier transform-based complex hyperbolic approaches provide a solution for attention-based transformations in complex hyperbolic space.
Complex hyperbolic knowledge graph embeddings with fast Fourier transform outperform Euclidean and real hyperbolic embedding models on knowledge graph completion tasks.
PromptHate constructs simple prompts and uses in-context examples to exploit the implicit knowledge in the pre-trained RoBERTa language model for hateful meme classification.
PromptHate outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on hateful meme classification.
High model uncertainty leads abstractive summarization models to assign higher probability to hallucinated content during generation.
A decoding strategy optimizes for pointwise mutual information of the source and target token when model uncertainty is detected.

Optimizing for pointwise mutual information of the source and target token when model uncertainty is detected decreases the probability of hallucinated tokens while maintaining the performance of top-performing decoding strategies.
The query decoder generates the corresponding query from a latent representation of a neural search engine.
The query decoder decodes a meaningful query from its latent representation.
The query decoder decodes a query that retrieves the relevant paragraph by moving in the right direction in latent space.
The query decoder generates a synthetic dataset of query reformulations for MSMarco.
A pseudo-relevance feedback T5 model for query suggestion outperforms query reformulation and PRF information retrieval baselines.
The ASSET corpus linguistic annotations clarify the bases of automatic text simplification evaluation.
A method for computing syntactically rich text embeddings using dependency tree structures for open information extraction.
A discriminative training approach for open information extraction that classifies tokens in generated facts as real or fake.
Paraphrased versions of the CaRB, OIE2016, and LSOIE datasets for open information extraction.
In jargon definition modeling, self- and correlative definitional information of target jargon is extracted from the Web.
In jargon definition modeling, final definitions for jargon are generated by incorporating extracted self- and correlative definitional information of target jargon.
Combining extraction and generation for jargon definition modeling produces definitions that outperform state-of-the-art models in automatic and human evaluation.
Topic-semantic contrastive learning with efficient positive and negative sampling strategies based on topic semantics models relations among short text samples.
Topic-semantic contrastive learning refines topic representations in short text topic modeling.
Topic-semantic contrastive learning enriches learning signals in short text topic modeling.
TSCTM mitigates data sparsity in short text topic modeling with or without data augmentation.
Hyper-adapters generate adapters from language and layer embeddings.
A rescaling fix improves convergence of hyper-networks.
Hyper-adapters are more parameter efficient than regular adapters.
Hyper-adapters converge faster than alternative approaches.
Hyper-adapters learn to encode language relatedness, enabling positive transfer across languages.
A hierarchical generative Variational Autoencoder with structural constraints reflecting competing hypotheses about the nature of argumentation predicts winning arguments in multi-party debates.
Predictive models that assume winning arguments are further from the initial argument of the opinion holder are more likely to succeed in most multi-party debate settings.
TASA produces fluent and grammatical adversarial contexts for question answering models while maintaining the gold answers.
Existing question answering models rely on keyword matching between the question and context and ignore relevant contextual relations for answer prediction.
TASA lowers the confidence of question answering models on the gold answer with a perturbed answer sentence.
TASA misguides question answering models towards a wrong answer with a distracting answer sentence.
TASA generates more effective adversarial attacks for question answering models than existing textual attack methods while maintaining context quality.
RASAT integrates relational structures into a T5-based Transformer seq2seq model using relation-aware self-attention.
RASAT incorporates almost all types of existing relational structures in the literature for text-to-SQL.
RASAT incorporates co-reference relations for multi-turn text-to-SQL.
RASAT achieves state-of-the-art execution accuracy on three text-to-SQL benchmarks.
BART, a state-of-the-art pre-trained Seq2Seq model, is vulnerable to robustness issues that cause degeneration in faithfulness and informativeness for text generation tasks.
AdvSeq constructs implicit adversarial samples by perturbing word representations.
AdvSeq constructs explicit adversarial samples by word swapping.
AdvSeq improves the faithfulness and informativeness of Seq2Seq generation under automatic and human evaluation.
Sparser attention values in Transformer models improve text generation diversity.
The Trap of Mediocrity is representation degeneration caused by the attentive mixture of hidden states during training.
An attention regularization loss that controls the sharpness of the attention distribution in Transformer models corresponds to learning a Bayesian approximation of posterior attention.
An attention regularization loss that controls the sharpness of the attention distribution in Transformer models improves text generation diversity and novelty while maintaining quality.
Heterogeneous task-related graphs automatically identify and adapt the roles that words play for stance detection and sentiment analysis tasks.
A multi-task interaction module captures word-level interactions between stance detection and sentiment analysis tasks to obtain richer task representations.
The multi-task interaction network (MTIN) outperforms state-of-the-art methods in stance detection and sentiment analysis tasks on two real-world datasets.
VideoQA datasets are categorized by modalities as normal, multi-modal, or knowledge-based.
VideoQA datasets are categorized by technical challenges as factoid or inference.
VideoQA techniques are categorized into those for factoid question answering and those for inference question answering.
VideoQA research is trending from factoid to inference tasks and towards robustness and interpretability.
CRIPP-VQA is a video question answering dataset for reasoning about the implicit physical properties of objects in a scene.

CRIPP-VQA contains videos of objects in motion annotated with counterfactual reasoning questions about the effect of actions, planning questions to reach a goal, and descriptive questions about visible properties of objects.
CRIPP-VQA enables evaluation of models on out-of-distribution masses, coefficients of friction, and initial velocities.
Video question answering models exhibit a performance gap between questions about implicit physical properties and explicit properties of objects.
FaD-VLP uses weakly-supervised triplets constructed from fashion image-text pairs for fashion vision-and-language pre-training.
Triplet-based tasks constructed from fashion image-text pairs improve standard multimodal pre-training for fashion vision-and-language tasks.
FaD-VLP uses a flexible decoder-based model architecture to perform both fashion retrieval and captioning tasks.

IndicNLG Benchmark includes datasets for biography generation from Wikipedia infoboxes, news headline generation, sentence summarization, paraphrase generation, and question generation in 11 Indic languages.
Multilingual language-specific pre-trained models show strong performance on Natural Language Generation for Indic languages.
Models trained on the IndicNLG Benchmark datasets are useful for other related Natural Language Generation tasks.

Dataset creation for Natural Language Generation in modest-resource languages can be done by scraping news articles and Wikipedia infoboxes, light cleaning, and pivoting through machine translation data.
IndicNLG Benchmark is the first Natural Language Generation benchmark for Indic languages.
M2D2 is a fine-grained, massively multi-domain corpus for studying domain adaptation in language models.
M2D2 organizes domains into a two-level hierarchy using ontologies derived from Wikipedia and ArXiv categories.
Adapting language models along a domain hierarchy using fine-grained domain-specific data leads to larger in-domain performance gains than using larger amounts of weakly relevant data.
There is a trade-off between in-domain specialization and out-of-domain generalization within and across ontologies.
There is a strong correlation between out-of-domain performance and lexical overlap between domains.

StoryER evaluates stories via three sub-tasks: Ranking outputs a preference score corresponding to human preference, Rating outputs specific ratings with confidences, and Reasoning outputs comments for story aspects such as opening and character-shaping.
A story evaluation dataset contains ranked story pairs and aspect-based ratings and comments.
StoryER uses a finetuned Longformer-Encoder-Decoder with the encoder for preference score and aspect prediction and the decoder for comment generation.
StoryER shows high correlation to human preference in story evaluation.
Joint learning of preference scores, aspect ratings, and comments improves each single task in story evaluation.
Contrastive explanations identify salient input tokens that explain why a language model predicted one token instead of another.
Contrastive explanations improve the verification of major grammatical phenomena compared to non-contrastive explanations.
Contrastive explanations improve contrastive model simulatability for human observers.
Contrastive explanations identify groups of contrastive decisions where language models use similar evidence.
Contrastive explanations characterize the input tokens used by language models during language generation decisions.
Training teachers with a mixup method informed by training dynamics calibrates student models for emotion-related tasks.

Student models distilled from teachers trained with a mixup method informed by training dynamics achieve the lowest calibration error and best performance on in-domain and out-of-domain test sets for emotion-related tasks.
WeDef defines sample reliability based on agreement between weak classifier predictions and labels in the poisoned training set.
WeDef refines the weak classifier iteratively using reliable samples.
WeDef trains a binary poison classifier to distinguish the most unreliable samples from the most reliable samples.
WeDef trains the sanitized model on samples predicted as benign by the poison classifier.
WeDef defends against backdoor attacks with word, sentence, and paraphrase triggers.
Informative visual signals in multimodal machine translation are decomposed into source-specific information and target-specific information.
Mutual information quantifies source-specific information and target-specific information in visual signals for multimodal machine translation.
Two methods for objective optimization leverage visual signals in multimodal machine translation.
Label prompt dropout randomly removes label descriptions during the learning process.
Label prompt dropout leads to improved class representations.
Examining out-of-sample data points in descending order of fine-tuned task loss outperforms previous error-detection mechanisms for identifying label errors.
A method introduces realistic, human-originated label noise into crowdsourced datasets such as SNLI and TweetNLP.
Human-originated label noise has similar properties to real, hand-verified label errors.
Human-originated label noise is harder to detect than synthetic noise.
Pre-trained language models detect real label errors more effectively than existing models on IMDB, Amazon Reviews, and Recon.
Prompt-and-Rerank performs zero-shot and few-shot arbitrary textual style transfer using general-purpose pre-trained language models.
Prompt-and-Rerank decomposes arbitrary textual style transfer into textual similarity, target style strength, and fluency.
Prompt-and-Rerank generates candidate texts in the target style via zero-shot or few-shot prompting and re-ranks them by textual similarity, target style strength, and fluency.

Prompt-and-Rerank enables small pre-trained language models to perform on par with state-of-the-art large-scale models for textual style transfer with significantly reduced compute and memory requirements.
Delimiter-pair choice affects style transfer performance.
Pre-trained language models exhibit biases in the direction of style transfer.
SynGEC incorporates dependency syntactic information into the encoder of grammatical error correction models.
An extended syntax representation scheme unifies grammatical errors and syntax in a single tree structure.
Parse trees for ungrammatical sentences are derived by projecting trees from grammatically correct sentences.
GOPar is a parser trained on projected parse trees from parallel grammatical error correction data.
Graph convolution networks encode syntactic information from GOPar and fuse it with Transformer encoder outputs for grammatical error correction.
SynGEC outperforms strong baselines on standard English and Chinese grammatical error correction benchmarks.
LittleBird improves speed and memory footprint over BigBird while maintaining accuracy.
LittleBird uses a more flexible and efficient position representation method based on Attention with Linear Biases (ALiBi).
Pack and unpack attention is more effective than BigBird's global information representation method.
LittleBird can process long inputs even when pre-trained on short inputs.
LittleBird can be trained efficiently by reusing existing pre-trained language models for short inputs.
Languages written in non-Latin and non-alphabetic scripts, which are mostly Asian, are the best transfer languages for Masked Language Modelling in low-resource languages.
Subword Evenness (SuE) predicts the success of cross-lingual transfer to low-resource languages.
Transfer languages with low Subword Evenness scores yield low perplexity for low-resource languages.
Subword Evenness (SuE) is a better predictor of cross-lingual transfer success than text-based measures and linguistic proximity measures.
LightEA uses Random Orthogonal Label Generation for entity alignment.
LightEA uses Three-view Label Propagation for entity alignment.
LightEA uses Sparse Sinkhorn Operation for entity alignment.
LightEA has scalability for entity alignment.
LightEA has robustness for entity alignment.
LightEA allows tracing the label propagation process to explain entity alignment.
Discrete Cross-Modal Alignment employs a shared discrete vocabulary space to accommodate and match speech and text modalities.
Discrete Cross-Modal Alignment uses a vector quantization module to discretize continuous speech and text representations into a finite set of virtual tokens.
Discrete Cross-Modal Alignment maps corresponding speech and text to the same virtual token in a shared codebook using ASR data.
Discrete Cross-Modal Alignment achieves performance on par with supervised speech translation baselines in zero-shot speech translation.
Large generative language models can be categorized into three behavior groups with broad differences.
The outputs of large generative language models in the largest behavior group are consistent with positional heuristics that succeed on subject control but fail on object control.
Raising the salience of agent and patient relations causes significant changes in the outputs of most large generative language models.
COLD is a benchmark for Chinese offensive language analysis.
COLDATASET is a dataset for Chinese offensive language detection.
COLDETECTOR is a baseline detector for Chinese offensive language detection trained on COLDATASET.
Popular Chinese pre-trained language models expose varying degrees of offensive issues.
Anti-bias contents and keywords referring to certain groups or revealing negative attitudes trigger offensive outputs more easily in Chinese pre-trained language models.
A novel algorithm samples informative orders over the combinatorial space of label orders.
Set cardinality and output are jointly modeled by listing the set size as the first element and using the autoregressive factorization of Seq2Seq models.
A model-independent data augmentation approach endows Seq2Seq models with order-invariance and cardinality signals.
Augmented set generation data improves performance across multiple benchmarks and models without additional annotations.
Data selection based on incorrect model predictions provides the most improvement for morphological inflection.
Data selection based on model confidence provides a clear benefit for morphological inflection.
Data selection based on high-entropy provides improvement for morphological inflection.
Introducing additional high-confidence, low-entropy, or correctly inflected forms can reduce model performance for morphological inflection.
Noisy parallel corpora in neural machine translation are filtered by separating samples based on norms of source-side and target-side context vectors.
Noisy parallel data in neural machine translation is refurbished by generating pseudo labels through online knowledge distillation.

Norm-based filtering of noisy parallel corpora in neural machine translation achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art noisy corpora filtering techniques while being more efficient and easier to operate.
R2F simplifies document-level natural language inference into sentence-level tasks by retrieving evidence sentences, estimating sentence-level credibility, and fusing the results.
A document-level natural language inference setting includes complementary evidence and entailment label annotations on hypothesis sentences for interpretability studies.
R2F is robust to diverse evidence retrieval methods.
R2F improves the interpretability of document-level natural language inference predictions.
UniPrompt uses a unified prompt for all languages in zero-shot cross-lingual transfer.
UniPrompt's unified prompt is model-based and language-agnostic.
UniPrompt allows pre-computation of prompts during inference to avoid extra computation cost.
An initialization method for the target label word improves cross-lingual transferability.
MPGN selects candidate temporal moments via subtitle-based moment sampling.
MPGN generates pseudo queries by exploiting visual and textual information from selected temporal moments.
MPGN localizes video corpus moments without explicit annotation.
SEEN detects life event inconsistency.
SEEN detects additional information in life events.
SEEN detects forgotten events.
A fusing mechanism incorporates event graphs of stories to enhance textual representations.
SEEN provides support evidence to explain need detection results by selecting related nodes from the event graph.
A paradigm enables scalable data collection for discourse comprehension of news documents by viewing open-ended questions through the lens of discourse.
DCQA captures discourse and semantic links between sentences in news documents as free-form, open-ended questions.
DCQA provides valuable supervision for answering open-ended questions.
Pre-training methods utilize existing question-answering resources and synthetic data to accommodate unanswerable questions.
The first automatic methods for segmenting unsegmented substitution ciphers use Byte Pair Encoding and unigram language models.
Non-deterministic ciphers with existing keys are solved using a lattice and a pretrained language model.
The IA cipher, a real historical cipher, is fully solved for the first time.
Representation normalization eliminates dataset bias by providing isotropic data distribution.
Kernel-Whitening is a Nystrom kernel approximation method that debiases nonlinear spurious correlations.
Kernel-Whitening is an end-to-end framework with time consumption similar to fine-tuning.
Kernel-Whitening improves BERT's performance on out-of-distribution datasets without sacrificing in-distribution accuracy.
GenSE synthesizes sentence pairs from open-domain unlabeled corpus using a generator/discriminator model.
GenSE filters noisy sentence pairs using a discriminator to acquire high-quality positive and negative sentence pairs.
GenSE uses a prompt-based contrastive approach for sentence representation learning with both annotated and synthesized data.
GenSE surpasses state-of-the-art methods on semantic textual similarity and domain adaptation tasks.
An expectation-maximization based contrastive learning framework for weakly supervised phrase grounding uses phrase-object contrastive loss to adaptively refine target predictions.

An expectation-maximization based contrastive learning framework for weakly supervised phrase grounding outperforms previous methods on standard phrase grounding benchmarks, including a state-of-the-art method that uses a pre-trained vision-language model.

A theoretical analysis using maximum likelihood estimation with latent variables explains the effectiveness of an expectation-maximization based contrastive learning framework for weakly supervised phrase grounding.
Debiased pruning masks remove biased weights in finetuned language models for Natural Language Understanding tasks.
Debiased pruning masks are stored with high memory efficiency.
Debiased pruning masks allow switching among several debiasing behaviors at inference time.
Debiased pruning masks prune early layers and attention heads more aggressively in language models.
Context-free grammars discover shortcuts in sentence classification datasets.
Synchronous context-free grammars discover shortcuts in sentence pair datasets.
Grammar induction reveals both simple and high-level shortcut features in multiple NLP datasets.
Grammar induction automatically identifies groups of test examples on which conventional classifiers fail.
Grammar induction produces shortcut features that generate diagnostic contrast examples.
Grammar induction produces shortcut features that improve worst-group accuracy when incorporated into robust optimization methods.
Hybrid Tensor-Train decomposition retains full rank and reduces operations and parameters.
Hypoformer outperforms lightweight state-of-the-art methods on standard translation tasks across parameter and speed scales.
Hypoformer achieves higher translation quality and speed than vanilla Transformer in extreme low resource scenarios.
Top-down iterative fine-tuning of pretrained language models does not always work due to different convergence speeds of different layers or modules.
Component-wise gradient norm clipping adjusts the convergence speed for different components during fine-tuning of pretrained language models.
Component-wise gradient norm clipping improves generalization performance, convergence speed, and training stability during fine-tuning of pretrained language models.
AfroLID is a neural language identification toolkit for 517 African languages and varieties.
AfroLID uses a manually curated multi-domain web dataset covering 14 African language families and five orthographic systems.
AfroLID outperforms existing language identification tools on most African languages.
Controlled case studies reveal capabilities and limitations of AfroLID for language identification of African languages.
A linguistically-motivated error analysis reveals capabilities and limitations of AfroLID for language identification of African languages.
Bidirectional Information Decoupling Network (BiDeN) explicitly incorporates both the past and future contexts for multi-turn dialogue encoding.
Bidirectional Information Decoupling Network (BiDeN) generalizes to a wide range of dialogue-related tasks.
The AMazon Argument Mining (AM2) corpus consists of Amazon reviews on headphones annotated with a theoretical argumentation model for evaluating argument quality.
Argumentational features lead to statistically significant improvements over the state-of-the-art review helpfulness predictors in both text-only and text-and-image settings.
Controllable recipe editing adapts a base recipe to satisfy a user-specified dietary constraint.
SHARE performs simultaneous ingredient substitution before generating natural-language steps using the edited ingredients.
SHARE decouples ingredient and step editing to enable explicit integration of available ingredients in step generation.
RecipePairs is a dataset of similar recipe pairs, with each recipe satisfying one of seven dietary constraints.
SHARE produces recipes that are appropriate for a target dietary constraint.
Multimodal conversational query rewrite (McQR) converts in-context queries to fully-specified versions by completing ellipsis and coreference in multimodal visual conversations.
McQueen is a manually annotated dataset for multimodal conversational query rewrite containing visual conversations and queries with fully-specified rewrite versions.
McQueen provides image box annotations for entities in the rewritten queries.

The Aligned Multimodal Movie Treebank (AMMT) is an English language treebank derived from dialog in Hollywood movies that includes transcriptions of audio-visual streams with word-level alignment, part of speech tags, and dependency parses in the Universal Dependencies formalism.
The Aligned Multimodal Movie Treebank (AMMT) is the only multimodal treebank in the Universal Dependencies formalism.
The Efficient Audio Alignment Annotator (EAAA) speeds up audio alignment annotation processes.
ACE-RPE uses an anchor locating mechanism to bridge the semantic gap between text and image modalities and to locate anchors from different modalities.
ACE-RPE calculates the distance of each text token and image patch by computing their shortest paths from located anchors.
ACE-RPE embeds anchor-based distance to guide the computation of cross-attention.
ACE-RPE achieves state-of-the-art results on Image-Text Retrieval, Visual Entailment, Visual Reasoning, and Weakly-supervised Visual Grounding tasks.
Large dual encoders generalize well to out-of-domain retrieval tasks.
Scaling up the embedding size in dual encoders yields diminishing improvement for retrieval tasks.
Generalizable T5-based dense Retrievers (GTR) outperform previous sparse and dense retrievers on the BEIR dataset.
Generalizable T5-based dense Retrievers (GTR) require a small fraction of MS Marco supervised data to match the out-of-domain performance achieved with full MS Marco supervised data.
A fast base classifier-based conformal predictor, calibrated on samples labeled by a zero-shot model, restricts the number of likely labels for cross-encoder-based zero-shot text classification.
In cross-encoder-based zero-shot text classification, a conformal predictor reduces the number of target labels without excluding the most probable label according to the zero-shot model.
A conformal predictor reduces inference time for cross-encoder-based zero-shot models while maintaining the error rate below a predefined threshold.
Fine-grained tags for the Winoground dataset identify the abilities required to solve the task.

Solving the Winoground task requires compositional language understanding and additional abilities such as commonsense reasoning and the ability to locate small, out-of-focus objects in low-resolution images.
A main challenge in visuolinguistic models for the Winoground task may lie in fusing visual and textual representations, rather than in compositional language understanding.
Input text is grouped by locality into pages for encoding and decoding in abstractive text summarization.
A model with restricted context by locality outperforms memory-efficient attention models in abstractive text summarization.
A mining-based approach for zero-shot learning mines labeled examples from unlabeled corpora using regular expressions, optionally filters them through prompting, and finetunes a pretrained model.
A mining-based approach for zero-shot learning is more flexible and interpretable than prompting.
A mining-based approach for zero-shot learning outperforms prompting on a wide range of tasks when using comparable templates.

The success of prompting in zero-shot learning is partly explained by language models being exposed to similar examples during pretraining, which can be directly retrieved through regular expressions.
Asking simple questions to an open-domain question answering system generates named entity recognition datasets.
Named entity recognition models trained on datasets generated by asking simple questions to an open-domain question answering system outperform strong low-resource models across multiple benchmarks.

Named entity recognition models trained on datasets generated by asking simple questions to an open-domain question answering system show competitive performance with rich-resource models that leverage in-domain dictionaries provided by domain experts.

In few-shot named entity recognition, named entity recognition models trained on datasets generated by asking simple questions to an open-domain question answering system outperform the previous best model and achieve state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks.
Pair-Based Joint Encoding (PBJE) generates pairs and clauses features simultaneously in a joint feature encoding manner to model the causal relationship in clauses.
Pair-Based Joint Encoding (PBJE) balances the information flow among emotion clauses, cause clauses and pairs.

Pair-Based Joint Encoding (PBJE) constructs a heterogeneous undirected graph and applies Relational Graph Convolutional Network (RGCN) to capture the multiplex relationship between clauses and the relationship between pairs and clauses.
LogCo extracts enclosing subgraphs and relational paths between two entities to enable entity-independence in inductive relation prediction.
LogCo applies a contrastive strategy to relational path instances and enclosing subgraphs to handle deficient supervision of logic.
LogCo outperforms state-of-the-art methods for inductive relation prediction on twelve datasets.

A neural image retriever achieves higher 0-shot performance with neural captions generated without awareness of hard distractors than with human captions for image retrieval on a dataset with hard distractors.
Human subjects achieve almost chance-level performance when using neural captions for image retrieval on a dataset with hard distractors.
Just Fine-tune Twice achieves Selective Differential Privacy for large transformer-based language models.
Just Fine-tune Twice fine-tunes large language models first with redacted in-domain data and then with the original in-domain data using a private training mechanism.
Systematic methods handle imperfect policy functions that miss sensitive tokens under selective differential privacy.
Selective Differential Privacy guarantees are analyzed empirically using the canary insertion attack.
CLH3G generates headlines consistent with the author's style by using the author's historical headlines.
An auxiliary task using contrastive learning learns the author's stylistic features in the encoder.
Two methods use the author's stylistic features to guide the pointer and the decoder during headline generation.
HydraSum is a text summarization architecture that uses a mixture-of-experts with multiple decoders.
HydraSum's multiple decoders automatically learn contrasting summary styles under the standard training objective without extra supervision.
HydraSum provides a simple mechanism to obtain stylistically-diverse summaries by sampling from individual decoders or their mixtures.
A small modification to the gating strategy during training enforces stricter style partitioning for dimensions such as abstractiveness and specificity.
ANS generates effective synthetic open category samples during training without requiring prior knowledge or external datasets.
Auxiliary one-versus-rest binary classifiers effectively utilize generated negative samples and avoid the complex threshold-seeking stage.
DeepQSE captures fine-grained relevance between queries and sentences via a query-aware sentence encoder processing the concatenation of title, query, and candidate sentences.
DeepQSE captures contextual information of webpages by jointly modeling query-aware sentence representations with a document-aware relevance encoder.
Efficient-DeepQSE decomposes query-aware snippet extraction into coarse-grained candidate sentence selection with cacheable sentence representations and fine-grained relevance modeling.
Efficient-DeepQSE achieves higher inference speed than DeepQSE without sacrificing effectiveness.
DeepQSE and Efficient-DeepQSE are effective for query-aware snippet extraction.
Concept-based curriculum masking evaluates masked language modeling difficulty of tokens using a linguistic difficulty criterion.
Concept-based curriculum masking masks easy words and phrases first and then masks related words based on a knowledge graph.
Concept-based curriculum masking reduces pre-training cost while maintaining performance comparable to BERT.

Reflect annotates dialogues with explicit common ground materialized as inferences approximating shared knowledge and beliefs and contains diverse human-generated responses each following one common ground.
Current response generation models produce generic and dull responses because they act reflexively without explicitly modeling common ground.

Current dialogue data has a low proportion of high-quality responses (sensible, specific, and interesting), models trained on this data produce a lower proportion of high-quality responses, and the Reflect dataset has a high proportion of high-quality responses.
Prompting GPT-3 to consider common ground increases the proportion of high-quality responses.
Power set encoding reformulates overlapped speaker diarization as a single-label prediction problem.
Speaker overlap-aware neural diarization uses a context-independent scorer to model global speaker discriminability.
Speaker overlap-aware neural diarization uses a context-dependent scorer to model local speaker discriminability.
Speaker overlap-aware neural diarization uses a speaker combining network to combine and reassign speaker activities.
Power set encoding achieves lower diarization error than state-of-the-art methods based on target speaker voice activity detection for overlapped speaker diarization.
Speaker overlap-aware neural diarization achieves lower diarization error than power set encoding for overlapped speaker diarization.
EMAT encodes external knowledge into a key-value memory.
EMAT uses fast maximum inner product search for memory querying.
New pre-training tasks enable EMAT to encode informative key-value representations.
Augmenting parametric models with EMAT produces more accurate results while retaining high throughput.
EMAT runs faster than retrieval-augmented models on multiple knowledge-intensive tasks.
ProNCI is a manually annotated dataset of proper noun compounds with free-form semantic interpretations.
ProNCI includes non-compositional proper noun compounds that have not been previously explored.
Adding targeted knowledge about the common noun improves the generation of semantic interpretations for proper noun compounds.
Integrating model-generated semantic interpretations of proper noun compounds with an Open IE system increases yield while maintaining high precision.
GuoFeng is a human-annotated benchmark test set covering five challenging genres for comprehensive evaluation of zero pronoun translation in Chinese-English.
Systematic evaluation of advanced models identifies current challenges in zero pronoun translation.
ClidSum is a benchmark dataset for cross-lingual dialogue summarization.
ClidSum supports supervised and semi-supervised benchmark settings for cross-lingual dialogue summarization.
mDialBART extends mBART via further pre-training with multiple objectives to capture structural characteristics and key content in dialogues and the transformation from source to target language.
mDialBART outperforms pipeline models for cross-lingual dialogue summarization.
Text classification in-the-wild introduces non-stationary training and testing stages.
ModularPrompt performs label-modular prompt tuning for text classification tasks.
ModularPrompt uses a sequence of soft label prompts, each encoding modular knowledge for a class label.
ModularPrompt demonstrates strong generalization in non-stationary text classification settings.
Bi-directional iterative prompt-tuning treats event argument extraction as a cloze-style task to leverage entity information and pre-trained language models.
Bi-directional iterative prompt-tuning introduces the argument roles of contextual entities into prompt construction to explore event argument interactions.
Bi-directional iterative prompt-tuning constructs a semantic verbalizer using role label semantic knowledge.
Bi-directional iterative prompt-tuning designs three kinds of templates for event argument extraction.
SentBS conducts evaluation throughout the generation process to select suitable sentences for subsequent generations.
SentBS improves the agreement between generated text and desired structure in structure-controlled summarization.
The keyword-question rewriting task enables query understanding for all query surface forms.
CycleKQR performs unsupervised bidirectional keyword-question rewriting using non-parallel data.
CycleKQR improves QA performance by rewriting queries into the appropriate form while retaining the original semantic meaning.
The dataset contains keyword-question pairs.
Invariant Language Modeling uses a round-robin training schedule where environments update subsets of the model to optimize their own environment-specific losses.
Invariant Language Modeling removes structured noise.
Invariant Language Modeling ignores specific spurious correlations without affecting global performance.
Invariant Language Modeling achieves better out-of-domain generalization.
Invariant Language Modeling has negligible computational overhead compared to standard training.
Context-situated pun generation, given context keywords, identifies suitable pun words for the context and generates puns based on the context and pun words.
CUP is a dataset of context words and pun pairs for context-situated pun generation.
A pun word retrieval module identifies suitable pun words for context keywords in context-situated pun generation.
A pun generation module generates puns from context keywords and pun words in context-situated pun generation.
A pipeline system for context-situated pun generation generates successful puns more often than other modeling variations.
A learnable mapping transforms BERT embeddings for document retrieval.
Probabilistic product quantization assigns real-valued codewords from transformed embeddings for efficient document retrieval.
Embedding transformation and product quantization are optimized end-to-end by minimizing a probabilistic contrastive loss.
Mutual information maximization improves codeword representativeness for accurate document quantization.
AMAL enables few-shot learning with pre-trained language models using meta-learning-driven low-rank adapter pooling.
AMAL achieves state-of-the-art results for few-shot text classification.

A transformer architecture for open-domain question answering casts the retriever and reranker as sequential internal passage-wise attention mechanisms, feeds computed representations to the reader, and progressively refines hidden representations at each stage of passage retrieval and reranking.

Integrating the retriever and reranker as internal passage-wise attention mechanisms in a transformer architecture uses model capacity more efficiently and has better gradient flow than weakly-coupled multi-model approaches.
A pre-training method effectively trains transformer-based question answering models that integrate the retriever and reranker as internal passage-wise attention mechanisms.

A transformer-based question answering model that integrates the retriever and reranker as internal passage-wise attention mechanisms achieves state-of-the-art results on Natural Questions and TriviaQA for a fixed parameter budget.
LSSD alleviates convergence inconsistency in multilingual neural machine translation.
LSSD achieves the best performance for each language pair simultaneously in multilingual neural machine translation.
LSSD uses language-specific best checkpoints to teach the current model during training.
LSSD obtains consistent improvements for all language pairs in multilingual neural machine translation.
LSSD achieves state-of-the-art performance in multilingual neural machine translation.
ASoul estimates soft labels for pseudo out-of-domain samples when training out-of-domain intent detectors.
ASoul captures semantic connections between pseudo out-of-domain samples and in-domain intents using an embedding graph.
ASoul produces soft labels for pseudo out-of-domain samples using a co-training framework that assumes close samples have similar labels.
Claim Span Identification (CSI) identifies claim spans in Twitter posts.
CURT is a Twitter corpus with token-level claim span annotations.
DABERTa identifies claim spans in Twitter posts using an adapter-based variation of RoBERTa.
DABERTa outperforms standard token classification baselines for claim span identification in Twitter posts.
SQUIRE is the first sequence-to-sequence framework for multi-hop knowledge graph reasoning.
SQUIRE uses an encoder-decoder Transformer structure to translate queries to paths in multi-hop knowledge graph reasoning.
SQUIRE learns and predicts in an end-to-end fashion, resulting in better and faster convergence.
SQUIRE does not rely on existing edges to generate paths and can complete missing edges, especially in sparse knowledge graphs.
SQUIRE outperforms prior methods on standard and sparse knowledge graphs while converging faster.
A retrieval shortcut exists in conversational search that causes models to retrieve passages solely based on partial history while disregarding the latest question.

Naively trained dense retrievers in conversational search heavily exploit a retrieval shortcut that causes retrieval based solely on partial history while ignoring the latest question, resulting in poor performance on history-independent questions.
Training with model-based hard negatives mitigates dependency on the retrieval shortcut in conversational search.
Training with model-based hard negatives improves dense retrievers on conversational search benchmarks.
Token-level equivalence measures for question answering evaluation have concrete limitations including a false impression of graduality and missing dependence on the question.
Answer equivalence (AE) is an asymmetric notion that accepts answers equivalent to or improving over the reference answer.
A dataset of human judgements for answer equivalence in question answering on SQuAD is introduced.
BERT matching (BEM) approximates answer equivalence more accurately than token-level F1 measure.
Answer equivalence (AE) and BERT matching (BEM) reduce the number of required answers for minimal accurate prediction sets.
A very small proportion of adversary clients using rare word embeddings suffices for backdoor attacks in federated learning for text classification without degrading clean sentence performance.
An extremely small proportion of adversary clients suffices to poison the global model for backdoor attacks in less complex datasets.
Gradient ensemble enhances backdoor performance in federated learning.
Face-sensitive image-to-emotional-text translation captures visual sentiment cues through facial expressions.
Face-sensitive image-to-emotional-text translation selectively matches and fuses visual sentiment cues with the target aspect in textual modality.
EDMem is an encoder-decoder framework with an entity memory for incorporating entity knowledge into informative text generation.
Entity knowledge in EDMem is stored as latent representations in a memory pre-trained on Wikipedia along with encoder-decoder parameters.
EDMem uses three decoding methods that constrain entity generation by linking entities in the memory to precisely generate entity names.
EDMem is a unified framework for entity-intensive question answering and generation tasks.
EDMem outperforms memory-based auto-encoder models and non-memory encoder-decoder models.
An approximation to minimum Bayes risk decoding has no equivalent to the beam search curse.
Sampling-based approximations to minimum Bayes risk decoding decouple the cost of exploration from the cost of robust estimation of expected utility.
Larger hypothesis spaces are beneficial in minimum Bayes risk decoding.
Mode-seeking strategies aid in constructing compact sets of promising hypotheses for translation.
Minimum Bayes risk decoding identifies good translations in compact sets of promising hypotheses.

ProsocialDialog contains multi-turn dialogues covering diverse unethical, problematic, biased, and toxic situations with responses that encourage prosocial behavior and are grounded in commonsense social rules.
Canary detects dialogue safety issues by generating commonsense social rules.
Prost generates socially acceptable dialogues in both in-domain and out-of-domain settings.
Canary guides conversational agents and off-the-shelf language models to generate prosocial responses.
Adapters achieve performance comparable to full model fine-tuning for machine translation while tuning only a fraction of model parameters.
The performance of parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods for machine translation decreases as the number of tuned parameters decreases.
The performance drop of parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods for machine translation is more severe for distantly related language pairs.
Using parameter-efficient fine-tuning with a larger pre-trained model outperforms full fine-tuning with a smaller model for machine translation.
Parameter-efficient fine-tuning outperforms full fine-tuning for machine translation when training data is limited.
LEXDEMOD provides agent-specific deontic modality and modal trigger annotations for English contracts.
Agent-specific multi-label deontic modality classification detects deontic modality for a specific agent.
Agent-specific deontic modality and trigger span detection identifies deontic modality and modal triggers for a specific agent.
The linguistic diversity of modal expressions in LEXDEMOD generalizes reasonably from lease agreements to employment and rental agreements.
LEXDEMOD supports red flag detection in legal contracts.
Boundary-Driven Table-Filling represents aspect sentiment triplets as relation regions in a two-dimensional table.
Boundary-Driven Table-Filling transforms aspect sentiment triplet extraction into the detection and classification of relation regions.
Boundary-Driven Table-Filling employs a relation representation learning approach that exploits word-to-word and relation-to-relation interactions to learn table representation.
SciNCL generates contrast samples via controlled nearest neighbor sampling over citation graph embeddings.
SciNCL learns continuous similarity for scientific document representations.
SciNCL samples hard-to-learn negatives and positives for contrastive learning.
SciNCL avoids collisions between negative and positive samples by controlling the sampling margin.
General-domain language models trained with SciNCL achieve better performance than in-domain pretrained baselines.
Over-parameterized teachers produce expressive yet student-unfriendly knowledge and are limited in overall knowledgeableness.
The knowledgeable score for teacher parameters interpolates expressiveness and student-friendliness scores.
A sparse teacher method uses the knowledgeable score to remove student-unfriendly parameters.
Sparse teachers are dense with knowledge.
Sparse teachers lead to student models with strong performance relative to competitive baselines.
