ZeroGen generates a dataset from scratch for zero-shot tasks using pre-trained language models without supervision.
ZeroGen trains a small task model on the synthesized dataset.
ZeroGen achieves efficient inference for zero-shot tasks.
The chapter ordering task reconstructs the original order of chapters in a novel from a random permutation of the text.
Chapter ordering is formulated as a constraint solving problem using minimum feedback arc set and traveling salesman problem optimization criteria.
Graph weights for chapter ordering are generated from models for character occurrences and chapter boundary detection using relational chapter scores derived from RoBERTa.
Prompting ELECTRA scores the originality of target options without introducing new parameters.
Prompting ELECTRA handles multi-token predictions without extra computation overhead.
Prompting ELECTRA outperforms masked language models in few-shot learning across a wide range of tasks.
In prompt-based few-shot learning, ELECTRA learns distributions that align better with downstream tasks.
Mean Projection (MP) removes target information from embedding spaces with a single projection and is efficient.
Tukey Median Projection (TMP) removes target information from embedding spaces with a single projection and has theoretical guarantees.
A single Mean Projection (MP) projection removes linear separability of the target information in embedding spaces.
Mean Projection (MP) has less impact on the overall embedding space than iterative nullspace projections.
Applying random projections after Mean Projection (MP) leads to the same overall effects on the embedding space as iterative nullspace projections.
Iterative nullspace projections introduce random effects on the embedding space.
CODER incurs negligible computational overhead during reranking, enabling combination with any state-of-the-art dual encoder method.
CODER-trained models can function as stand-alone retrievers.
The improvement in retrieval performance from CODER is more pronounced when more relevance information per query is available.
CODER establishes new state-of-the-art results on the TripClick collection.
XLM-R achieves high in-language performance early in pretraining, with lower-level linguistic skills acquired before more complex ones.
XLM-R learns cross-lingual transfer at different points in pretraining for different language pairs.
XLM-R's final model layer exhibits performance degradation over pretraining time across many languages and tasks, while linguistic knowledge propagates to lower layers.
A unified primal-dual framework unifies question generation and question answering.
A question generation component in a unified primal-dual framework jointly encodes the answer and the passage and produces the question.
A question answering component in a unified primal-dual framework re-asks the generated question on the passage to ensure the target answer is obtained.
An iterative prompting framework progressively elicits relevant knowledge from pre-trained language models for multi-step inference.
Existing prompting methods are restricted to queries with a single identifiable relation or predicate.
Existing prompting methods are agnostic to input contexts, making it difficult to capture variabilities across different inference steps.
An iterative context-aware prompter dynamically synthesizes prompts conditioned on the current step's contexts for multi-step inference.
SNaC evaluates narrative coherence in long summaries through fine-grained annotations.
A taxonomy categorizes coherence errors in generated narrative summaries.
Coherence errors generated by state-of-the-art summarization models are characterized.
JABER, Char-JABER, and SABER are Arabic BERT-style pre-trained language models.
AT5S and AT5B are Arabic T5-style pre-trained language models.
JABER, Char-JABER, SABER, AT5S, and AT5B achieve state-of-the-art performance on Arabic natural language understanding and generation tasks.
DocInfer uses optimal evidence selection based on the REINFORCE algorithm to identify the most important context sentences for a given hypothesis.
DocInfer transcends the input length limitation of modern BERT-like Transformer models while presenting the entire evidence together for inferential reasoning.
CaseHoldNLI evaluates legal judicial reasoning.
DocInfer outperforms state-of-the-art methods on DocNLI, ContractNLI, ConTRoL, and CaseHoldNLI.
DocInfer improves performance on annotation-scarce tasks including fact verification, multiple-choice question answering, and contract clause retrieval.
INDICXNLI is an NLI dataset for 11 Indic languages.
INDICXNLI is created by machine translation of the English XNLI dataset.
The impact of language model choice, language choice, multi-linguality, and mix-language input on cross-lingual transfer is analyzed.
The structural generalization limitation of sequence-to-sequence models occurs in semantic parsing, syntactic parsing, and text-to-text tasks.
Neurosymbolic models with built-in linguistic knowledge can often overcome the structural generalization limitation of sequence-to-sequence models.
FigMemes contains politically-opinionated memes annotated for six types of figurative language.

COM-MRC uses a context augmentation strategy that enumerates all masked contexts for each aspect term.

ROSE conducts selective updates when adapting pre-trained models to downstream tasks, filtering out invaluable and unrobust updates of parameters.

The counterfactual recipe generation task requires surface-level compositional generalization for incorporating a new ingredient into a base recipe and deeper-level compositional generalization for adjusting actions related to the changing ingredient.

Coordinated topic modeling imitates human behavior in describing a text corpus by using a reference representation of well-defined topics as axes of a semantic space to produce an easily understandable representation.

The synthesized dataset for spatial question answering and spatial role labeling facilitates transfer learning.

MultI-ConvFiT transforms general-purpose sentence encoders into task-specialized sentence encoders via contrastive fine-tuning on annotated multi-label data.

Topic features guided by difficulty knowledge complement traditional linguistic features for readability assessment.

A dispute tactics framework unifies toxicity detection and argument structure analysis and incorporates dialogue acts for resolving disputes such as asking questions and providing clarification.

Constrained decoding that decomposes actions into verb templates and objects leverages pre-trained language models as actionable world models in text-based game environments.

Heavy undocumented preprocessing causes irreproducible results for BERT-based evaluation metrics.
Missing code causes irreproducible results for BERT-based evaluation metrics.

A human-in-the-loop pipeline discovers interpretable task-specific attention patterns in transformer models.

End-to-end unsupervised vision-and-language pre-training directly encodes images.

Problematic validation and testing practices in current Parameter-Efficient Tuning studies combined with the instability of these methods lead to unreliable conclusions.

Monte Carlo dropout and deep ensembles for uncertainty quantification in machine translation evaluation cannot distinguish between different kinds of uncertainty and are computationally expensive.

Context or word biases in lexical semantic datasets can be measured and visualized.

Defining causal relationships quantifies the effect of annotation artifacts on model outcomes.

A framework operationalizes mental health stigma by curating prompts from clinical psychology literature and evaluating models' propensity to generate gendered words.

Annotator heuristic traces measure low-effort annotation strategies indicating cognitive heuristic usage during multiple-choice reading comprehension annotation.

Three real-world labeled datasets for Patent Landscape Studies-oriented classification tasks cover two diverse domains.

HPT handles hierarchical text classification from a multi-label masked language model perspective.

ECTSum contains long unstructured earnings call transcripts and short expert-written bullet point summaries derived from Reuters articles.

An algorithm for the backward pass in pathsum computation for sparse acyclic weighted finite-state automata with failure transitions runs in O(s|Q||Σ|) time, where s is the average out symbol fraction, |Q| is the number of states, and |Σ| is the size of the alphabet.

An algorithm for pathsum computation in sparse acyclic semiring-weighted finite-state automata with failure transitions runs in O(|E| + s|Σ||Q||Tmax| log |Σ|) time, where |E| is the number of transitions, s is the average out symbol fraction, |Q| is the number of states, |Σ| is the size of the alphabet, and |Tmax| is the size of the largest connected component of failure transitions.

An algorithm for pathsum computation in sparse acyclic ring-weighted finite-state automata with failure transitions runs in O(|E| + s|Σ||Q||πmax|) time, where |E| is the number of transitions, s is the average out symbol fraction, |Q| is the number of states, |Σ| is the size of the alphabet, and |πmax| is the longest path length of failure transitions.

An algorithm for pathsum computation in sparse acyclic semiring-weighted finite-state automata with failure transitions that have a topology satisfying a condition exemplified by conditional random fields runs in O(|E| + s|Σ||Q| log |Σ|) time, where |E| is the number of transitions, s is the average out symbol fraction, |Q| is the number of states, and |Σ| is the size of the alphabet.
Static token embedding bias and ineffective BERT layers cause drawbacks in BERT sentence embeddings.

Unsupervised neural and statistical sequence modeling affirms the existence of header signs in proto-Elamite without domain expert supervision.

A multilingual hard negative sample augmentation module shares knowledge of indistinguishable passages across multiple languages and synthesizes new hard negative samples by interpolating representations of queries and existing hard negative samples.

A multilingual negative sample cache queue stores negative samples from previous batches in each language to increase the number of multilingual negative samples used in training beyond the batch size limit.
A lightweight adaptive false negative sample filter uses generated pseudo labels to separate unlabeled false negative samples and converts them into positive passages in training.
mHFN achieves state-of-the-art performance in multilingual dense passage retrieval on eleven typologically diverse languages.

Autoregressive MoE language models are substantially more compute efficient than dense language models in language modeling and priming tasks, but not in fine-tuning.

The distance between the distributions of grammatical relations induced from Multilingual BERT across languages is highly consistent with syntactic difference in linguistic formalisms.
