The mixed-effects transformer learns contextual variation from minimal data and generalizes well to unseen contexts.
Multimodal Evaluation (ME) automatically generates question-answer pairs for testing models' understanding of the visual scene, text, and related knowledge.
Training with Multimodal Evaluation data improves model performance on standard VCR evaluation.
Semantically low-level information assists the learning of high-level information but not vice versa.
Vision Language models underutilize visual information compared to text.
TABS uses beam search to find better adversarial examples in pre-trained NL code models.
TABS uses contextual semantic filtering to reduce the number of candidate adversarial words by considering the surrounding context and semantic similarity.
TABS achieves high attack success rate, low query count, and high semantic similarity on natural language code search classification and retrieval tasks.
The speaker-aware co-attention framework extracts structured information from medical dialogues.
The speaker-aware dialogue encoder uses multi-task learning to consider speaker identity and perceive the semantics of utterances and candidate items for medical dialogue information extraction.

The co-attention fusion network handles complex interactions between utterances and correlations between utterances and candidate items by aggregating utterance information for medical dialogue information extraction.
The speaker-aware co-attention framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods for medical dialogue information extraction.
A learning strategy leveraging the hierarchical structure of medical ontologies enhances generalizability to long tail concepts for discriminative and generative models in medical term normalization.

A learning strategy leveraging the hierarchical structure of medical ontologies achieves state-of-the-art performance on seen concepts for discriminative and generative models in medical term normalization.

A learning strategy leveraging the hierarchical structure of medical ontologies enables efficient zero-shot knowledge transfer across text typologies and datasets for discriminative and generative models in medical term normalization.
Retrieval performance heavily impacts whether models rely on parametric knowledge or non-parametric knowledge.
In best-performing settings, models rely mostly on non-parametric knowledge.
Contradictions among knowledge sources only marginally affect model confidence.
Calibration discourages models from presenting a single answer when multiple conflicting answer candidates are present in retrieved evidence.
GENIE runs standardized human evaluations across different text generation tasks.
GENIE automatically crowdsources annotations for submissions and evaluates them along axes including correctness, conciseness, and fluency.
GENIE maintains annotator quality by detecting and excluding noisy annotators using a probabilistic model.
MEE provides annotations for event mentions in typologically different languages.
MEE comprehensively annotates entity mentions, event triggers, and event arguments.
Experiments on MEE reveal challenges and opportunities for multilingual event extraction.
Fine-tuning has flat or negative scaling curves on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing.
In-context learning has positive scaling curves on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing.
In-context learning is outperformed by smaller fine-tuned models on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing.
Prompt-tuning outperforms fine-tuning on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing.
Prompt-tuning exhibits a more positive scaling curve on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing than fine-tuning.
Larger models are better at modeling the syntax of the output space on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing.
Larger models are more prone to certain types of overfitting on out-of-distribution compositional generalization in semantic parsing.
Computational framing analysis approaches are compiled into a comprehensive survey.
Memory-assisted prompt editing pairs GPT-3 with a growing memory of recorded cases of user intent misunderstandings and user feedback for clarification.
Memory-assisted prompt editing produces enhanced prompts for new queries by leveraging user feedback from similar past cases for error correction.
Memory-assisted prompt editing increases the accuracy of deployed GPT-3 on queries with user intent misunderstandings.
SSR-PU learning is a unified positive-unlabeled learning framework for document-level relation extraction with different levels of labeling.
Positive-unlabeled learning is applied to document-level relation extraction for the first time.
Positive-unlabeled learning that handles prior shift of training data is used for document-level relation extraction.
Squared ranking loss uses none-class score as an adaptive threshold for multi-label ranking.
Squared ranking loss is Bayesian consistent with multi-label ranking metrics.
SSR-PU learning achieves state-of-the-art results on document-level relation extraction under fully supervised, incomplete labeling, and extremely unlabeled settings.
PEVL reformulates discretized object positions and language in a unified language modeling framework.
PEVL facilitates explicit vision-language alignment during pre-training.
PEVL enables flexible prompt tuning for downstream tasks.
PEVL achieves state-of-the-art performance for detector-free vision-language pre-training models on position-sensitive tasks including referring expression comprehension and phrase grounding.
PEVL improves performance on position-insensitive tasks with grounded inputs.
CONDA reformulates data generation as context generation for a given question-answer pair.
CONDA leverages question-answer datasets for training context generators.
QA datasets requiring high-level reasoning abilities (such as abstractive and common-sense reasoning) provide the best performance boost in few-shot and zero-shot settings.
Recurring obstacles occur in ethics consideration sections of ACL 2021 papers.
Joint fine-tuning on source and target language instances provides consistent gains for few-shot cross-lingual transfer across varying shot counts.
Multi-stage few-shot cross-lingual transfer training with joint multilingual fine-tuning preceding bilingual source-target specialization improves performance.
Tutor-KD controls the difficulty of training examples during pre-training.
A tutor network generates training examples that are easy for the teacher model but difficult for the student model.
The tutor network is trained using a policy gradient method.
Tutor-KD outperforms state-of-the-art knowledge distillation methods on the GLUE benchmark for student models of varying sizes.
RACo uses a large-scale commonsense corpus for retrieval in commonsense reasoning tasks.
RACo trains a commonsense retriever with specific strategies.
RACo outperforms other knowledge-enhanced methods on multiple commonsense reasoning tasks.
RACo achieves state-of-the-art performance on the CommonGen and CREAK benchmarks.
Syntax-aware latent graph leverages syntax dependency relation label information to learn sentiment representations.
Multi-graph fusion module fuses semantic information of surrounding contexts of aspects adaptively.
Affective refinement strategy captures significant affective clues in aspect-based sentiment analysis.
Multi-graph fusion network outperforms state-of-the-art methods on three aspect-based sentiment analysis datasets.
Document-level embeddings for event coreference resolution are obtained with a Longformer-based encoder.
Sentence-level embeddings for event coreference resolution are learned based on local context with a trigger-mask mechanism.
Latent topic-level representations for event coreference resolution are inferred with an event topic generator.
Interactions among event mentions at document, sentence, and topic levels are captured with a multiple tensor matching method.
EdgeFormer is a parameter-efficient Transformer for on-device sequence-to-sequence generation under strict computation and memory constraints.
EdgeFormer uses two novel principles for cost-effective parameterization.
EdgeFormer uses a layer adaptation innovation for improving networks with shared layers.
EdgeFormer outperforms previous parameter-efficient Transformer baselines under computation and memory constraints.
EdgeLM is the first publicly available pretrained on-device sequence-to-sequence model that can be easily fine-tuned for sequence-to-sequence tasks.
Temporal generalization is closely affiliated with lexical semantic change.
A lexical-level masking strategy post-trains converged language models for temporal generalization.
A lexical-level masking strategy for post-training outperforms continual training in temporal generalization on multiple language models, classification tasks, and benchmark datasets.
Common English pretraining corpora contain significant amounts of non-English text.
Non-English text in English pretraining corpora facilitates cross-lingual transfer for models trained on them.
Target language performance of models pretrained on English pretraining corpora is strongly correlated to the amount of in-language data seen during pretraining.
Models pretrained at scale on English pretraining corpora are not truly monolingual.

MOCHA is a multi-task training strategy for long text generation grounded on the cognitive theory of writing that teaches writing subskills including planning and reviewing in addition to end-to-end generation.
MOCHA generates more coherent texts than strong baselines on both few-shot and fully-supervised settings according to human evaluations.
BBTv2 prepends continuous prompts to every layer of a pre-trained model for few-shot learning.
BBTv2 uses a divide-and-conquer gradient-free algorithm to optimize the prompts at different layers alternately.
BBTv2 achieves comparable performance to full model tuning and state-of-the-art parameter-efficient methods in few-shot learning while using fewer tunable parameters.
Syntactic Multi-view Learning models constituency and dependency trees as word-level graphs.
Syntactic Multi-view Learning fuses heterogeneous information from constituency and dependency graphs using multi-view learning.
Syntactic Multi-view Learning aggregates finetuned constituency and dependency representations with sentential semantic representations for tuple generation.
Tiny-attention adapters use attention with extremely small per-head dimensionality.
Tiny-attention adapters modify hidden states at each position directly conditioned on hidden states at all other positions.
Tiny-attention adapters average the weights of multiple attention heads during deployment to reduce inference computation cost.
Tiny-attention adapters outperform other parameter-efficient transfer learning methods and full fine-tuning on the GLUE benchmark while updating a very small fraction of parameters.
Tiny-attention adapters achieve performance comparable to GPT-3 and PET on the FewGLUE benchmark.
DASCL leverages specialized dictionaries when fine-tuning pretrained language models.
Keyword simplification replaces any word in the corpus that appears in relevant dictionaries with a common, fixed token.

A supervised contrastive objective draws closer the embeddings of original texts and texts with words from relevant dictionaries replaced by a common fixed token for the same class while pushing apart embeddings of different classes.

Texts with words from relevant dictionaries replaced by a common fixed token for the same class are more textually similar than their original counterparts, which draws the embeddings of the same class closer together.

Combining DASCL and cross-entropy improves classification performance in few-shot learning settings and social science applications relative to cross-entropy alone and alternative contrastive and data augmentation methods.
MUS finetunes a pre-trained encoder model on the citation graph using link prediction tasks.
MUS extracts abstract sentences by considering multi-granularity information.
Citation graphs are helpful for scientific paper extractive summarization in a simple unsupervised framework.
GSS uses a gated sentence encoder to polish sentence representation using graph information.
GSS uses a graph information fusion module to polish sentence representation using graph information.
A decoder biasing technique incorporates visual context into automatic speech recognition for robots.
A decoder biasing technique prevents degradation of automatic speech recognition output when the visual context is incorrect.
A decoder biasing technique improves automatic speech recognition for spoken utterances containing visible entities.
For in-domain tasks, there is a cross point of sample size at which parameter efficient learning methods outperform full finetuning when training with fewer samples.
For in-domain tasks, larger pre-trained language models have larger cross points of sample size for parameter efficient learning methods to outperform full finetuning.
For cross-domain and cross-dataset tasks, Adapter is the best performing parameter efficient learning method.
For cross-domain and cross-dataset tasks, Adapter outperforms full finetuning when the task dataset size is below a certain size.
Parameter efficient learning methods achieve better faithfulness in generated text than full finetuning, especially for small training sets.
Adapter applied to MT-NLG 530b achieves state-of-the-art results on the Xsum benchmark.
MM-Align performs inference on multimodal sequences with randomly missing modalities.
MM-Align uses an alignment dynamics learning module based on optimal transport theory to impute missing modalities.
MM-Align uses a denoising training algorithm to improve imputation quality and prediction accuracy.
MM-Align performs more accurate and faster inference under different missing conditions.
MM-Align alleviates overfitting under different missing conditions.
IKD-MMT supports image-free inference for multimodal machine translation using inversion knowledge distillation.
IKD-MMT generates multimodal features from source texts alone using a multimodal feature generator with a knowledge distillation module.
IKD-MMT is the first image-free approach that rivals or surpasses image-must frameworks for multimodal machine translation.
Automatic Document Selection automatically identifies smaller yet domain-representative document subsets for language model pretraining.

Automatic Document Selection improves language model performance on perplexity and downstream tasks in the target domain compared to random selection with reduced data, training iterations, and compute resources.
The KCL objective learns inter-class discriminative features and maintains intra-class diversity to alleviate in-domain overfitting.
The KCC method forms compact clusters by mining true hard negative samples to bridge the gap between clustering and representation learning.
Multitask Instruction-based Prompting treats variations in input format, genre, and fallacy types across datasets as multiple tasks.
Multitask Instruction-based Prompting recognizes 28 unique fallacy types across domains and genres.
GraphQ IR unifies the semantic parsing of graph query languages.
GraphQ IR bridges the semantic gap between natural and formal languages with a natural-language-like expression.
GraphQ IR maintains the graph structure with formally defined syntax.
GraphQ IR can be losslessly compiled into various downstream graph query languages.
GraphQ IR outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in semantic parsing of graph query languages under i.i.d., out-of-distribution, and low-resource settings.
CapOnImage is a task for generating dense captions at different locations of an image based on contextual information.
A multi-modal pre-training model with multi-level pre-training tasks learns the correspondence between texts and image locations progressively from easy to difficult.
Location embeddings are enhanced by incorporating neighbor locations as context to reduce caption redundancy.
CapOnImage2M is a benchmark for the CapOnImage task consisting of product images with multiple spatially localized captions per image.
A novel data augmentation technique creates noisy synthetic data for training seq-to-seq models on the Semantic Overlap Summarization task.
Models trained on noisy synthetic data outperform pre-trained vanilla summarization techniques on the Semantic Overlap Summarization task in the news domain.
Models trained on noisy synthetic data achieve performance close to models trained on golden training data for the Semantic Overlap Summarization task in the news domain.
WassOS is an unsupervised abstractive opinion summarization model that uses the Wasserstein distance.
WassOS computes the summary distribution as the Wasserstein barycenter of document distributions.
WassOS creates disentangled latent semantic and syntactic representations for the summary.
WassOS outperforms state-of-the-art models in automatic evaluation of opinion summarization.
WassOS produces summaries with better meaning preservation than state-of-the-art models according to human evaluations.
SpanProto uses a two-stage approach for few-shot named entity recognition that includes span extraction and mention classification.
SpanProto transforms sequential tags into a global boundary matrix to focus on explicit boundary information during span extraction.
SpanProto uses prototypical learning to capture semantic representations for labeled spans and adapt to novel-class entities during mention classification.
SpanProto splits out false positives generated by the span extractor that are not labeled in the current episode set and uses a margin-based loss to separate them from prototype regions.
SimpleSTC performs inductive short text classification using only words.
SimpleSTC constructs a word graph from an external large corpus to compensate for the lack of semantic information in short texts.
SimpleSTC learns a text graph to handle the lack of labeled data in short text classification.
SimpleSTC achieves state-of-the-art performance in short text classification with lower memory consumption and faster inference speed.
The lack of simple guidelines for systematic climate reporting is a reason why few NLP publications report key figures for environmental impact examination.
A climate performance model card enables systematic climate impact reporting for NLP research with only limited information about experiments and computer hardware.
Differentiable data augmentation for contrastive sentence representation learning creates hard positives from original training examples.
A prefix attached to a pre-trained language model enables differentiable data augmentation during contrastive sentence representation learning.
Differentiable data augmentation for contrastive sentence representation learning uses supervised prefix-tuning followed by joint contrastive fine-tuning with unlabeled or labeled examples.
Differentiable data augmentation is more label-efficient than state-of-the-art contrastive learning methods in low labeled data settings for sentence representation learning.
Textual adversarial examples have embeddings that diverge off the manifold of natural sentence embeddings in the contextualized embedding space of pretrained language models.
Textual Manifold-based Defense learns the embedding space manifold of the underlying language model and projects novel inputs back to the approximated structure before classification.
Textual Manifold-based Defense outperforms previous defenses under various attack settings while maintaining clean accuracy.
Textual Manifold-based Defense is the first manifold-based defense for natural language processing.
Hyper-Network based adaptation generates task-specific parameters conditioned on instructions for multi-task instructional learning of natural language generation models.
Meta-learning adaptation to multi-task instructional learning improves generalization to unseen natural language generation tasks in zero-shot settings.
Meta-learning improves the effectiveness of instructions for natural language generation.
Meta-learning is most impactful for strictly zero-shot natural language generation tasks that are hard for language models.
CoPoet is a collaborative poetry writing system that uses user instructions to specify attributes of the desired text.
CoPoet uses a language model fine-tuned on a diverse collection of poetry writing instructions.
CoPoet satisfies unseen compositional instructions.
Poems written with CoPoet on diverse topics are preferred by third-party evaluators over poems written without CoPoet.
Continual PostTraining (CPT) continually extends language models for few-shot end-task learning without forgetting previous skills.
Continual PostTraining (CPT) incrementally post-trains language models with a sequence of unlabeled domain corpora.
Heterogeneous Label Graph (HLG) represents statistical dependencies based on labels' co-occurrence patterns and hierarchies in slot labels and rich relations among label nodes.
ReLa-Net enhances semantic-label interactions by capturing label correlations from the Heterogeneous Label Graph.
Label-aware inter-dependent decoding exploits label correlations during decoding.
ReLa-Net significantly outperforms previous models for joint multiple intent detection and slot filling on the MixATIS dataset.
PLOS (large-scale) and eLife (medium-scale) are lay summarisation datasets containing biomedical journal articles and expert-written lay summaries.
PLOS and eLife lay summaries exhibit differing levels of readability and abstractiveness that can be leveraged for different applications.
Benchmarking and manual evaluation of PLOS and eLife demonstrate their utility for lay summarisation and identify key challenges of the task.
Graph pooling operations automatically contract dependency trees into core relational topologies while eliminating redundant information.
A symmetrical expanding path with graph unpooling operations fuses contracted core syntactic interactions with the original sentence context.
A bipartite graph matching objective function captures reflections between core relational topologies and golden relational facts.
The ASR Confidence Embedding (ACE) layer jointly encodes ASR word-level confidence scores and transcribed text into a contextualized representation for ASR error detection.
ASR word-level confidence scores complement the textual signal in ASR error detection.
A novel ASR error detection dataset consists of ASR outputs from the LibriSpeech corpus with annotated transcription errors.
A fine-grained multi-label approach to predicting incivility and hateful or intolerant content addresses conceptual and performance issues.
A dataset of tweets about immigration from the US and UK is annotated with multiple labels for different aspects of incivility and intolerance.
IM2 divides dialogue qualities into different categories.
IM2 measures dialogue qualities within categories using specific dialogue metrics.
IM2 generates an overall evaluation score by combining category-specific dialogue metrics.
IM2 utilizes newly developed dialogue metrics for specific dialogue quality categories.
IM2 correlates more strongly with human judgments than existing dialogue metrics across multiple datasets.

Standard adversarial training methods that reduce vulnerability to fickle adversarial examples increase vulnerability to obstinate adversarial examples in natural language inference and paraphrase identification tasks.
Balanced Adversarial Training incorporates contrastive learning to increase robustness against both fickle and obstinate adversarial examples.
Violations of the implicit assumptions about annotators in the standard human evaluation protocol for natural language generation cause annotator ratings to not reflect their preferences.
Using Likert scales in natural language generation human evaluation can reverse the direction of true preference in certain cases.
System-level probabilistic assessment (SPA) is a human evaluation protocol for open-ended natural language generation tasks.
System-level probabilistic assessment recovers the ordering of GPT-3 models by size in story generation evaluation.
The standard human evaluation protocol fails to recover the ordering of GPT-3 models by size in story generation evaluation.
An alternating knowledge distillation approach uses both content-based and graph-based models to predict prerequisite relations for unseen concepts.
Robust connectivity patterns emerge in the early training phase of adversarial training before parameters converge.
Robust early-bird tickets are searched for using structured sparsity in the early stage of adversarial training.
Robust early-bird tickets are fine-tuned in the remaining training time for adversarial training.
A ticket convergence metric automatically terminates the searching process for robust early-bird tickets.
Using robust early-bird tickets for adversarial training achieves substantial training speedups while maintaining or improving robustness.
Stereotypes are encoded in the attention layer of pre-trained text encoders.
A debiasing technique for pre-trained text encoders redistributes attention scores to remove preference for historically advantaged groups and ensure equal attention to all social classes.
A debiasing technique for pre-trained text encoders reduces social stereotypes without degrading semantic integrity.
Logical reasoning with span-level predictions creates transparent Natural Language Inference decisions based on logical rules.
Improved interpretability in Natural Language Inference models is achieved without loss of predictive accuracy.
Natural Language Inference models identify the exact hypothesis spans responsible for each prediction.
Natural Language Inference models make sensible span-level decisions without using span labels during training.
Natural Language Inference models improve performance both in-distribution and out-of-distribution when trained with fewer examples.
ASQA is a task for answering ambiguous factoid questions with long-form summaries that resolve ambiguity by combining information from multiple sources.
The ASQA dataset contains ambiguous factoid questions and corresponding long-form summaries.
The ASQA metric assesses whether a summary enables answering different interpretations of an ambiguous question.
The ASQA metric correlates with human judgments of answer quality.
Strong baselines underperform human performance on the ASQA task.
SpeechUT uses hidden-unit as an interface to decompose the speech-to-text model into a speech-to-unit model and a unit-to-text model.
SpeechUT enables joint pre-training of the speech-to-unit and unit-to-text models with unpaired speech and text data.
SpeechUT achieves state-of-the-art performance on the LibriSpeech automatic speech recognition task and the MuST-C speech translation task.
Cross-document Event Coreference Search identifies all event mentions that corefer with a query event mention in a large document collection.
A Cross-document Event Coreference Search dataset is derived from Wikipedia and leverages annotations from the Wikipedia Event Coreference dataset (WEC-Eng).
Deep Passage Retrieval is adapted for Cross-document Event Coreference Search.
A coreference scoring scheme is integrated into the Deep Passage Retrieval architecture for Cross-document Event Coreference Search.
The Neighbor Information Aggregation module aggregates multi-hop neighbor information for knowledge graph entity typing.
The Entity Type Inference module mitigates the adverse impact of irrelevant neighbor information in knowledge graph entity typing.
The Type Co-occurrence Regularization module prevents overfitting to false negative examples in knowledge graph entity typing.
Co-occurrence relations between types alleviate the false-negative problem in knowledge graph entity typing.
CEM establishes a causal graph for Machine-Human Chatting Handoff based on user state and labor cost.
CEM uses user state to correct prediction bias according to the causal relationship in multi-task learning.
CEM trains an auxiliary cost simulator to calculate unbiased labor cost through counterfactual learning.
CEM generally improves the performance of existing Machine-Human Chatting Handoff methods without requiring elaborate model changes.
Cross-modal contrastive learning learns a shared space for all languages using a coarse-grained sentence-level objective and a fine-grained token-level objective.
Cross-modal contrastive learning learns cross-modal and cross-lingual alignment with a small amount of image-text pairs.
Cross-modal contrastive learning improves neural machine translation performance over text-only baselines in zero-shot and few-shot scenarios.
ReCo represents threshold and scene factors of causal pairs in causal chains using exogenous variables.
ReCo estimates threshold and scene contradictions across exogenous variables with structural causal recurrent neural networks (SRNN).
ReCo outperforms strong baselines on causal chain reasoning datasets in Chinese and English.
Reliable causal chain knowledge distilled by ReCo improves BERT's performance on downstream causal-related tasks relative to other knowledge sources.
Cross-Align encodes source and target sentences separately with shared self-attention modules in shallow layers and constructs cross-lingual interactions with cross-attention modules in upper layers.
Cross-Align uses a two-stage training framework with a simple Translation Language Modeling objective in the first stage and self-supervised alignment objective finetuning in the second stage.
Cross-Align achieves state-of-the-art performance for word alignment on multiple language pairs.
Existing word alignment approaches capture few interactions between input sentence pairs, which degrades word alignment quality severely, especially for ambiguous words in monolingual context.
QRelScore is a context-aware relevance evaluation metric for question generation.
QRelScore uses word-level hierarchical matching to handle complicated reasoning with the context.
QRelScore uses sentence-level prompt-based generation to handle diverse generation from multiple evidences in the context.
QRelScore has higher correlation with human judgments than existing metrics.
QRelScore is more robust to adversarial samples than existing metrics.
ReSel decomposes N-ary relation extraction from scientific articles into a two-stage procedure that retrieves the most relevant paragraph or table and then selects the target entity.
ReSel uses a feature set that captures multi-level lexical and semantic similarities between the query and document paragraphs or tables for retrieval.
ReSel uses a cross-modal entity correlation graph and a multi-view architecture to model semantic and document-structural relations between entities for entity selection.
ReSel outperforms state-of-the-art baselines for extracting N-ary relation tuples from scientific articles.
Compression-based post-editing removes extrinsic entity errors from summaries by taking a summary with errors marked by special tokens and outputting a compressed, well-formed summary.
Compression-based post-editing improves factual consistency of summaries while maintaining summary quality.
Compression-based post-editing improves entity precision in summaries.
Compression-based post-editing can be applied on top of another post-editor to further improve entity precision.
An extensive comparison of post-editing approaches reveals trade-offs between factual consistency, informativeness, and grammaticality.
TASD uses a three-layered multi-head attention network for table-structure-aware text generation.
TASD uses a multi-pass decoder framework to polish generated text for table descriptions.
TASD generates faithful and fluent descriptive texts for different types of tables.
TextFusion uses a Fusion Predictor to dynamically fuse token representations, hiding multiple private token representations behind a single unrecognizable representation.
TextFusion employs an adversarial training regime to privatize token representations.
TextFusion preserves inference privacy without significantly sacrificing performance across diverse classification tasks.
Prompts for multilingual relation classification are constructed from relation triples with minimal translation of class labels.

Prompt construction from relation triples with minimal translation of class labels outperforms fine-tuning XLM-R_EM and null prompts in fully supervised and few-shot multilingual relation classification.
Prompt construction from relation triples with minimal translation of class labels outperforms the random baseline in zero-shot multilingual relation classification.
Prompt construction from relation triples with minimal translation of class labels requires little in-language knowledge for multilingual relation classification.
Efficiently tuned parameters function as task embeddings for inter-task transferability prediction.
Inter-task transferability prediction using efficiently tuned parameters as task embeddings outperforms existing methods and is computationally efficient.
The ability of efficiently tuned parameters to predict inter-task transferability is disentangled from their in-task performance.
Parameters from early training checkpoints function as task embeddings for inter-task transferability prediction, improving computational efficiency.
Kernel-based methods for approximating causal self-attention are unnecessarily complex.
Decaying fast weights runs fast on GPU.
Decaying fast weights outperforms kernel-based methods in autoregressive language modeling.
Decaying fast weights achieves performance close to full attention in GPT-2.
Decaying fast weights achieves competitive performance on WikiText-103.
Knowledge graph completion and alignment tasks are synergistic and best solved together.
Relation alignment in multilingual knowledge graphs uses a loss term based on maximal bipartite matching between sets of embedding vectors.
A loss function for joint knowledge graph completion and alignment combines relation alignment loss, knowledge graph completion loss, and optionally text embedding losses.
Joint knowledge graph completion and alignment improves knowledge graph completion compared to a model that combines known facts in all languages.
Joint knowledge graph completion and alignment outperforms strong baselines for entity alignment and relation alignment.
Fine-tuning the head of an autoregressive language model has higher susceptibility to membership inference and extraction attacks than other fine-tuning methods.
Fine-tuning smaller adapters in autoregressive language models has lower vulnerability to known extraction attacks than fine-tuning the head.
Imitation learning and related low-level metrics are misleading for embodied dialogue research, do not align with its goals, and may hinder progress.
Models trained with imitation learning take spurious actions during evaluation.
Existing models fail to ground query utterances essential for task completion.
Evaluation for embodied dialogue should focus on higher-level semantic goals.
Infinite SCAN jointly estimates the number of senses of words and their changes through time.
Infinite SCAN combines a dynamic topic model on Gaussian Markov random fields with a logistic stick-breaking process that realizes Dirichlet process.
Infinite SCAN tracks the semantic changes of several well-known target words in the CCOHA corpus.
Knowledge distillation can decrease model fairness in English natural language processing models.
The graph-based model generation framework for few-shot relation extraction uses one general model for all tasks and many tiny task-specific models for each individual task.
The graph-based model generation framework for few-shot relation extraction employs a general model to generate and pass universal knowledge to tiny task-specific models.
The graph-based model generation framework for few-shot relation extraction fine-tunes tiny task-specific models for specific tasks.
The graph-based model generation framework for few-shot relation extraction decouples the complexity of the entire task space from that of individual tasks while absorbing universal knowledge.
The graph-based model generation framework for few-shot relation extraction achieves state-of-the-art performance on few-shot relation extraction tasks.
A simple transformer-based model outperforms specialized architectures on ReaSCAN and a modified version of gSCAN.
Identifying the target location in the grid world is the main challenge for neural models on compositional generalization tasks.
A particular split in ReaSCAN that tests depth generalization is unfair.
Transformers can generalize to deeper input structures on an amended version of the ReaSCAN split.
RefEx is a grounded compositional generalization task for investigating how transformers reason compositionally.
A single self-attention layer with a single head generalizes to novel combinations of object attributes on the RefEx task.
A precise mathematical construction of the transformer’s computations is derived from the learned network.
A large dataset of human annotated demographic text perturbations exists.
A neural perturbation model outperforms heuristic alternatives for demographic perturbation.
Language models pre-trained on demographically perturbed corpora are typically more fair.
Language models finetuned on perturbed GLUE datasets exhibit less demographic bias on downstream tasks.
Fairness improvements from training on demographically perturbed data do not come at the expense of performance on downstream tasks.
CDConv evaluates contradiction detection in Chinese conversations.
CDConv contains 12K multi-turn conversations annotated with Intra-sentence Contradiction, Role Confusion, and History Contradiction.
Automatic conversation generation methods simulate common user behaviors that trigger chatbots to make contradictions.
State-of-the-art Chinese chatbots can be easily goaded into making contradictions.
Properly modeling contextual information is critical for dialogue contradiction detection.
G-MAP augments domain-specific pre-trained language models with a memory built from a frozen general pre-trained language model to retain general knowledge.
G-MAP introduces a new memory-augmented layer for pre-trained language models.
G-MAP employs different augmentation strategies to build and fuse memory into domain-specific pre-trained language models.
G-MAP achieves state-of-the-art results on text classification, question answering, and named entity recognition tasks in biomedical, computer science, news, and review domains.
A grammar inducer learns video-span correlation without manually designed features.
A grammar inducer trained on uncurated YouTube data with no text-video alignment achieves strong and robust performance on unseen datasets despite domain shift and noisy labels.
A grammar inducer trained on uncurated YouTube data with no text-video alignment outperforms state-of-the-art systems trained on in-domain data on unseen datasets.
HolisticBias includes nearly 600 descriptor terms across 13 demographic axes.
HolisticBias was assembled through a participatory process including experts and community members with lived experience of the descriptor terms.
HolisticBias measures previously undetectable biases in language model token likelihoods and offensiveness classifiers.
VisToT generates a descriptive sentence conditioned on a table and an associated image.
WikiLandmarks is a multimodal table-to-text dataset of world landmarks.
VT3 generates descriptive sentences conditioned on image and table pairs.
Visual cues from images help infer missing information from incomplete or sparse tables for natural language generation.
Visual cues from images strengthen the importance of useful information from noisy tables for natural language generation.

A two-stage training method for continual learning of large-scale pretrained neural machine translation models uses local features of the real loss to search for low forgetting risk regions and train within them without accessing previous training data or introducing model separation.
A method searches for low forgetting risk regions in neural machine translation parameter space using loss curvature.
A method searches for low forgetting risk regions in neural machine translation parameter space using parameter impacts on model output.

A two-stage training method for continual learning of large-scale pretrained neural machine translation models achieves significant improvements in domain and language adaptation tasks compared to strong baselines.
An entropy-based predictor quantifies the diffuseness of self-attention in GPT-2.
Distance-based predictors capture the incremental change in attention patterns across timesteps in GPT-2.
Entropy- and distance-based predictors from GPT-2 attention patterns provide a better fit to self-paced reading and eye-tracking data than a baseline that includes GPT-2 surprisal.

Instance Regularization estimates the complexity of restoring original sentences from corrupted ones by incorporating the corruption degree in the ennoising process and the prediction confidence in the denoising process.
Instance Regularization improves pre-training efficiency, effectiveness, and robustness on natural language understanding and reading comprehension benchmarks.
Generative Multi-hop Retrieval performs multi-hop retrieval by generating the entire text sequences of the retrieval targets.
Generative Multi-hop Retrieval enables the query and the documents to interact in the language model’s parametric space instead of L2 or inner product space.
Generative Multi-hop Retrieval achieves comparable or higher performance than bi-encoder models on multi-hop retrieval tasks.
Generative Multi-hop Retrieval has superior GPU memory and storage footprint compared to bi-encoder models.
Iterative inference for document-level relation extraction processes difficult entity pairs in an easy-to-hard manner to refine relation predictions.
Extended Cross Attention units exploit both feature information and previous predictions of entity pairs during relational inference.
A two-stage training strategy for document-level relation extraction first trains a base module and then trains the entire model with contrastive learning for the inference module.
Character-level white-box adversarial attacks against transformer models are generated by substituting attachable subtokens.
Visual and length constraints minimize character modifications in character-level white-box adversarial attacks.

Character-level white-box adversarial attacks via attachable subtoken substitution achieve higher success rates and lower edit distances than previous attack methods on sentence-level and token-level tasks.
Human evaluation verifies that character-level white-box adversarial examples preserve their original labels.
Hierarchical blocking on prefix parameters simulates a higher-level discourse structure of human written text.
Attention sparsity on prefix parameters at different network layers and sparse transformations on the softmax function are applied for soft-prompt based text generation.
Structured design of prefix parameters yields more coherent, faithful, and relevant text generations than baseline prefix-tuning on all generation tasks.
R-GQA retrieves the most similar question-answer pair and uses it as a prompt to generate event arguments.
R-GQA outperforms prior methods in fully supervised, domain transfer, and few-shot learning settings for event argument extraction.
JointEnc is a clustering-based sampling strategy for few-shot learning in event argument extraction.
ConvTrans automatically transforms easily-accessible web search sessions into conversational search sessions.
ConvTrans eliminates gaps in session quality and query form between web search sessions and conversational search sessions.

Retrieval models trained on ConvTrans-generated conversational search sessions achieve comparable retrieval performance to those trained on high-quality expensive artificial conversational search data.
Infilling with language models generates hard, representative synthetic non-factual summaries for training fact-correction models.
FactEdit outperforms prior methods in correcting erroneous summaries on CNN/DM and XSum.
FactEdit maintains competitive summarization quality.
VIP uses contextual prompts to learn input-specific contextualization of soft prompt tokens with a small-scale sentence encoder.
VIP uses quantized prompts to map contextualized prompts to learnable codebook vectors with a Vector quantization network.
VIP outperforms soft prompt tuning on multiple language understanding tasks.
VIP learns more robust prompt representations than soft prompt tuning.
ReasTAP injects high-level table reasoning skills into models during pre-training without requiring a complex table-specific architecture.
Seven table reasoning skills are defined, including numerical operation, temporal comparison, and conjunction.
An example generator for each table reasoning skill synthesizes questions over semi-structured tables using sampled templates.
ReasTAP models table pre-training as a sequence generation task to generate precise answers for synthetic examples.
ReasTAP achieves state-of-the-art results on table question answering, table fact verification, and faithful table-to-text generation tasks.
Unbounded gradients in the attention computation adversely impact the convergence of linear transformer models.
Attention dilution trivially distributes attention scores over long sequences while neglecting neighbouring structures.
The scaling of attention matrices causes unbounded gradients in linear attention and is unnecessary.
transNormer replaces the scaling operation in linear attention with a normalization to stabilize gradients.
transNormer uses diagonal attention to confine attention to only neighbouring tokens in early layers.

transNormer outperforms vanilla transformer models and existing linear transformer variants on text classification, language modeling, and the Long-Range Arena benchmark while being more space-time efficient.
Generated dialogue summaries are frequently faithfully inconsistent with the source dialogues.
A model-level faithfulness evaluation method using multi-choice questions created by rule-based transformations is a strong proxy for the factual correctness of summarization models.
TRIPS progressively reduces the visual sequence with a text-guided patch-selection layer for efficient vision-and-language pre-training training and inference.
TRIPS uses a patch-selection layer that dynamically computes text-dependent visual attention to identify attentive image tokens and fuses inattentive image tokens in an end-to-end manner.
TRIPS does not introduce extra parameters to Vision Transformers.
TRIPS improves computational efficiency of vision-and-language pre-training while maintaining competitive or better downstream task performance.
HashFormers support an unlimited vocabulary using a fixed-sized embedding matrix.
HashFormers use computationally cheap hashing functions to bucket tokens into embeddings.
HashFormers include variants that do not require an embedding matrix.
HashFormers are more memory efficient than standard pre-trained transformers and maintain comparable predictive performance after fine-tuning on text classification tasks.
ConsistTL continuously transfers knowledge from the parent model to the child model during the child model's training.

For each training instance of the child model, ConsistTL constructs a semantically-equivalent instance for the parent model and encourages prediction consistency between the parent and child models for that instance.
ConsistTL results in significant improvements over strong transfer learning baselines for low-resource neural machine translation.
ConsistTL improves the inference calibration of the child model.
Sequence-to-sequence and transformer-based models trained on ALFRED are not sufficiently sensitive to changes in input language instructions.
ALFRED-L evaluates generalization to unseen task structures requiring the same types of language understanding as ALFRED.
ALFRED models are overly reliant on the sequence of object visits in typical ALFRED trajectories and fail to adapt to sequence modifications.
ALFRED models trained with additional augmented trajectories adapt better to sequence modifications in input language instructions.
TaCube pre-computes data cubes containing aggregation and arithmetic results for tables to answer numerical reasoning questions.
TaCube covers a comprehensive set of computational operations over table segments.
TaCube achieves state-of-the-art results on the WikiTQ table question answering benchmark.
TaCube improves performance on arithmetic operations including sum, average, subtraction, and division for numerical reasoning.
Bernice is the first multilingual RoBERTa language model trained from scratch on Twitter data with a custom tweet-focused tokenizer.
Bernice matches or exceeds the performance of social media adapted models and multilingual baselines on Twitter benchmarks while using less training data.
Training language models completely on in-domain data with a specialized domain-specific tokenizer is more efficient in terms of compute and data.
The Twitter-based MultiPIT corpus provides crowdsourced and expert annotations for paraphrase identification using two different paraphrase definitions.
The Twitter-based MultiPIT corpus provides a multi-reference test set for paraphrase generation.
The Twitter-based MultiPIT corpus provides a large automatically constructed training set for paraphrase generation.
The best pre-trained language model fine-tuned on the Twitter-based MultiPIT corpus achieves state-of-the-art performance for automatic paraphrase identification.
Paraphrase generation models trained on the Twitter-based MultiPIT_Auto training set generate more diverse paraphrases than models trained on other paraphrase corpora.
MatchPrompt realizes open relation extraction with efficient knowledge transfer from only a few pre-defined relational instances.
MatchPrompt mines the specific meanings of relation clusters for interpretability.
MatchPrompt is the first prompt-based framework for unlabeled relation clustering.
MatchPrompt achieves state-of-the-art results for open relation extraction.
In pre-trained transformer models, fine-tuning any single transformer module in isolation with the rest frozen achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning on downstream tasks.
In parameter-efficient fine-tuning of language models, LayerNorms achieve acceptable performance on various target tasks with a minimal fraction of updateable parameters.
In pre-trained language models, the higher-magnitude weights of LayerNorms compared to other components explain their transfer learning performance in parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
Multimodal Cross-Lingual Summarization for videos (MCLS) generates cross-lingual summaries from multimodal video inputs.
Video-guided Dual Fusion network (VDF) integrates multimodal and cross-lingual information using diverse fusion strategies at both encoder and decoder.
A triple-stage training framework transfers knowledge from monolingual multimodal summarization data to assist Multimodal Cross-Lingual Summarization for videos (MCLS).
RankGen scores generated sequences given a prefix for English text.
RankGen serves as a scoring function in beam search for decoding English text from any pretrained language model.

RankGen is trained using contrastive learning to map an English prefix close to the ground-truth sequence and far from random sequences from the same document and sequences generated by a language model conditioned on the prefix.
RankGen outperforms nucleus sampling, top-k sampling, and typical sampling in English text generation as measured by automatic metrics and human evaluations.
RankGen outputs are more relevant to the prefix and exhibit improved continuity and coherence for English text compared to baseline methods.
Bilingual synchronization involves transforming an initial target sequence into a valid translation of the source to restore parallelism between source and target.

A generic edit-based system, when fine-tuned, matches or exceeds the performance of dedicated systems for simulated interactive machine translation, machine translation with Translation Memory, and Translation Memory cleaning.
Task instances in joint information extraction are represented as nodes in a dependency graph with edges inferred from multiple layers of a pretrained language model.
A dependency tree between information types for joint information extraction is learned using the Chow-Liu algorithm to approximate the joint distribution of the types from data.
Cross-type patterns generated from a dependency tree serve as anchor knowledge to guide the learning of representations and dependencies between instances in joint information extraction.
Joint information extraction with learned cross-task dependencies from data outperforms strong baselines on multilingual datasets.
LogME positively correlates with language model fine-tuned performance on natural language processing tasks.
Quantitative transferability metrics are more robust than human practitioner rankings for language model selection.
An automated measure evaluates summary dispersion as the number of source documents required to cover the summary content.
In certain multi-document summarization datasets, a single source document often covers the entire reference summary.
G-Eval is a framework that uses large language models with chain-of-thoughts and a form-filling paradigm to assess natural language generation output quality.
G-Eval with GPT-4 achieves higher human correlation than previous methods for text summarization evaluation.
LLM-based evaluators exhibit a bias towards LLM-generated texts.
Memsizer projects source sequences into lower dimension representations.
Memsizer uses efficient recurrent-style incremental computation.
Memsizer yields linear computation time and constant memory complexity at inference time.
Memsizer employs a lightweight multi-head mechanism that renders the computation as light as a single-head model.

Memsizer provides an improved balance between efficiency and accuracy over the vanilla transformer and other efficient transformer variants in machine translation, abstractive text summarization, and language modeling.
A new multimodal task evaluates grounded predicate-noun dependencies in a controlled setup.
Model performance on grounded predicate-noun dependencies varies considerably across state-of-the-art vision-and-language models, with some models performing well and others at chance level.
Pretraining data quality (not only quantity) is essential for model performance on grounded predicate-noun dependencies.
Vision-and-language models that perform best on grounded predicate-noun dependencies leverage fine-grained multimodal pretraining objectives in addition to standard image-text matching objectives.
BERT, RoBERTa, and DeBERTa recognise the structure of the English comparative correlative but fail to use its meaning.
PATS adds larger noise to parameters with lower sensitivity and smaller noise to parameters with higher sensitivity during fine-tuning of pretrained language models.
PATS consistently improves the performance of fine-tuned pretrained language models of different sizes on GLUE benchmark tasks.
Well-performing models fine-tuned with PATS exhibit more concentrated distributions of parameter sensitivities.
Title2Event is a sentence-level dataset for open event extraction from Chinese news titles without restricting event types.
Title2Event is the largest manually annotated Chinese dataset for open event extraction.
News title characteristics make event extraction challenging.
Fine-tuning generative multilingual models solely on English causes catastrophic forgetting of non-English generation in zero-shot cross-lingual generation without parallel data.
Prompt tuning overcomes catastrophic forgetting for zero-shot cross-lingual generation between less-related languages.
Mixing unlabeled multilingual data improves zero-shot cross-lingual generation.
Factoring prompts into recombinable language and task components improves zero-shot cross-lingual generation.
A protocol for evaluating the faithfulness of input salience methods in text classification uses partially synthetic data to obtain ground truth for feature importance ranking.
An analysis evaluates the faithfulness of four standard salience method classes on multiple datasets and lexical shortcuts for BERT and LSTM models.
Some popular salience method configurations perform poorly on simple lexical shortcuts in text classification.
A salience method considered simplistic performs well for BERT on lexical shortcuts in text classification.
CleanCoNLL corrects annotation errors in the English CoNLL-03 corpus for named entity recognition.
CleanCoNLL includes entity linking annotations to improve explainability of named entity recognition labels and to safeguard annotation quality.
CleanCoNLL reduces the proportion of correct predictions that are falsely counted as errors due to annotation noise in named entity recognition evaluation.
The theoretical upper bound for high resource, coarse-grained named entity recognition is not yet reached.
ConvFinQA is a large-scale dataset for studying the chain of numerical reasoning in conversational finance question answering.
ConvFinQA challenges models to model long-range, complex numerical reasoning paths in real-world finance conversations.
TIARA applies multi-grained retrieval to focus on relevant knowledge base context including entities, exemplary logical forms, and schema items.
TIARA uses constrained decoding to control the output space and reduce generation errors.
TIARA outperforms previous state-of-the-art models, including those using pre-trained language models or oracle entity annotations, on the WebQuestionsSP benchmark.
TIARA outperforms previous state-of-the-art models, including those using pre-trained language models or oracle entity annotations, in all question categories on the GrailQA benchmark.
DiSTRICT uses a retriever to perform in-context tuning for Dialogue State Tracking without hand-crafted templates.
DiSTRICT outperforms existing approaches in zero-shot and few-shot Dialogue State Tracking settings with a smaller model.
Two benchmark datasets for Multilingual Multimodal Machine Translation cover seven languages.
LVP-M3 performs multilingual multimodal machine translation through token encoding, language-aware visual prompt generation, and language translation stages.
KnowSQL is a benchmark for knowledge-intensive text-to-SQL that includes domain-specific questions across multiple domains.
A formulaic knowledge bank serves as a domain knowledge base for knowledge-intensive text-to-SQL.
ReGrouP leverages formulaic knowledge during text-to-SQL parsing.
ReGrouP achieves significant overall improvement on the KnowSQL benchmark.
LILA is a unified mathematical reasoning benchmark that evaluates tasks across mathematical abilities, language format, language diversity, and external knowledge.
LILA provides explainable solutions for mathematical reasoning tasks through Python programs.
Two evaluation datasets assess out-of-distribution performance and robustness to language perturbation in mathematical reasoning.
BHASKARA is a general-purpose mathematical reasoning model trained on a unified mathematical reasoning benchmark.
Multi-tasking improves mathematical reasoning performance compared to single-task models.
The best mathematical reasoning model achieves limited performance on a unified mathematical reasoning benchmark, indicating room for improvement.
kNN-RE consults training relations at test time using nearest-neighbor search in relation extraction.
kNN-RE handles implicit expressions in relation extraction.
kNN-RE handles long-tail relation types in relation extraction.
kNN-RE leverages distant supervision data for relation extraction.
Triplet semantic matching via prompt learning learns non-leaf attachment from imbalanced training samples.
The result context relates the final prediction to subtask results in prompt-based multi-task learning.
A two-stage retrieval and re-ranking approach improves inference efficiency.
An interpretable, scalable analogy-finding algorithm extracts entities and relations from pairs of procedural texts and finds domain mappings based on relational similarity.
An analogy-finding algorithm identifies correct mappings for procedural texts and stories from cognitive-psychology literature with high accuracy.
An analogy-finding algorithm extracts analogies from a large dataset of procedural texts with high precision.
An analogy-finding algorithm is robust to paraphrasing of input texts.

Adding an extra training task to distinguish poisoned data from clean data during victim model training and using all clean training data instead of removing the original clean data corresponding to poisoned data make textual backdoor attacks more harmful.

Adding an extra training task to distinguish poisoned data from clean data during victim model training and using all clean training data instead of removing the original clean data corresponding to poisoned data are universally applicable to different attack models.

Adding an extra training task to distinguish poisoned data from clean data during victim model training and using all clean training data instead of removing the original clean data corresponding to poisoned data improve attack performance in clean data fine-tuning, low-poisoning-rate, and label-consistent attacks.
Pathological demonstrations for few-shot sequence labeling are generated by gradually removing intuitively useful information from standard demonstrations.
Random token demonstrations improve few-shot sequence labeling performance.
In few-shot sequence labeling with random demonstrations, the length of demonstrations and the relevance of tokens are the main factors affecting performance.
Demonstrations increase model prediction confidence for captured superficial patterns in few-shot sequence labeling.
Human raters rate the feasibility of two-turn dialogs sampled from 9 diverse data sources for a futuristic humanoid robot and a digital assistant.
Some data sources commonly used to train dialog systems contain a high proportion of utterances that human raters do not view as possible for a machine.
The machine embodiment (futuristic humanoid robot or digital assistant) has only a marginal effect on the feasibility ratings of utterances.
HierGNN learns a hierarchical document structure through a latent structure tree computed by a sparse matrix-tree computation.
HierGNN propagates sentence information over the hierarchical document structure using a novel message-passing node propagation mechanism to identify salient information.
HierGNN uses graph-level attention to concentrate the decoder on salient information.
HierGNN produces summaries that are more relevant and less redundant than baseline models.
HierGNN synthesizes summaries by fusing multiple source sentences more often than compressing a single source sentence.
OpenCQA is a task for answering open-ended questions about charts with descriptive texts.
A practical setting for OpenCQA provides a chart and the accompanying article as input.
A practical setting for OpenCQA provides only the relevant paragraph(s) to the chart as input.
A practical setting for OpenCQA requires the model to generate an answer solely based on the chart.
Top performing models on OpenCQA produce fluent and coherent text but struggle with complex logical and arithmetic reasoning.
Three probing test accuracies predict fine-tuning performance with lower error than baseline methods.
FLANG uses financial keywords and phrases for masking.
FLANG uses a span boundary objective.
FLANG uses an in-filing objective.
FLUE provides new benchmarks for 5 NLP tasks in the financial domain.
EUR-Lex-Sum is a multi- and cross-lingual dataset for long-form summarization of legal acts from the European Union law platform.
EUR-Lex-Sum provides cross-lingual paragraph-aligned document and summary data in several of the 24 official European languages.
EUR-Lex-Sum includes a subset of cross-lingually aligned legal acts available in all 24 official European languages.
EUR-Lex-Sum presents challenging sub-problems for domain-specific cross-lingual summarization.
Extractive monolingual and cross-lingual baselines are provided for the EUR-Lex-Sum dataset.
An unsupervised polysemy quantification method computes polysemy scores using dependency structures for words in multiple languages.
An unsupervised polysemy quantification method computes polysemy scores using discrete Ollivier Ricci curvature on a graph of contextual nearest neighbors for words in multiple languages.
A polysemy quantification method shows significant correlations with human-annotated resources in English.
A polysemy quantification method achieves higher correlation with human-annotated resources than prior quantification studies in English.
Moderate-sized masked language models can approximately execute the Inside-Outside algorithm for the English PCFG.
The Inside-Outside algorithm is optimal for masked language modeling loss on PCFG-generated data.
Models pre-trained on PCFG-generated data recover approximate parse trees.
Models pre-trained on PCFG-generated data recover marginal span probabilities computed by the Inside-Outside algorithm.
Masked language modeling has an implicit bias towards the Inside-Outside algorithm.
ChatGPT performs well on natural language processing tasks favoring reasoning capabilities, such as arithmetic reasoning.
ChatGPT faces challenges when solving natural language processing tasks such as sequence tagging.
Hard-to-cheat synthetic contexts for multi-step reasoning datasets are created programmatically using QDMR representations.
TeaBReaC covers a broad set of reasoning patterns for multi-step question answering.
TeaBReaC pretraining particularly improves performance on complex multi-step questions.
TeaBReaC pretraining improves model robustness on contrast sets.
TeaBReaC pretraining improves performance and robustness of numerate language models pretrained with recent methods.
prompt-tuning enables pretrained language models to be effective long-tailed learners.
the classifier structure and parameterization are critical for pretrained language models to be effective long-tailed learners, while the input structure has minimal impact.
the critical role of classifier structure and parameterization in long-tailed classification also applies to few-shot classification.
A few instances of confidently incorrect predictions damage users' trust in the system's performance, and this trust does not easily recover over time.
Users are more forgiving of an NLP system when it is unconfidently correct than when it is confidently incorrect, despite equivalent payoffs from a game-theoretic perspective.
Each user can maintain multiple mental models of an NLP system depending on the type of question.

An unsupervised approach improves cross-lingual representations of low-resource languages in pre-trained multilingual language models by bootstrapping word translation pairs from monolingual corpora and using them to improve language alignment.
Pre-trained multilingual language models can be improved for low-resource languages by relying only on non-parallel resources.
The Multi-modal Complaint Dataset (MCD) contains reviews and images of products from Amazon.
A federated meta-learning-based multi-modal multi-task framework identifies complaints using emotion recognition and sentiment analysis as auxiliary tasks.

Multi-modal complaint identification with emotion recognition and sentiment analysis as auxiliary tasks via federated meta-learning outperforms baselines and state-of-the-art approaches in centralized and federated meta-learning settings.
A token-level weighted network refines intra-modality representations for text-video retrieval.
A graph-based message passing attention network achieves global-local alignment across modalities for text-video retrieval.
GHAN outperforms the state-of-the-art on text-video retrieval benchmarks.
Open-domain video commentary generation produces commentary for videos across diverse domains without relying on domain-specific information.
A dataset for open-domain video commentary generation consists of transcribed commentary aligned with videos of diverse human actions across multiple domains.
Neural architectures generate open-domain video commentary.
Models for open-domain video commentary generation exhibit trade-offs between textual and visual inputs.
External knowledge is important for open-domain video commentary generation.
Certified error control of candidate set pruning for two-stage relevance ranking guarantees that the test error after pruning is controlled under a user-specified threshold with high probability.

Certified error control of candidate set pruning for two-stage relevance ranking improves second-stage reranking speed while satisfying pre-specified accuracy constraints in both in-domain and out-of-domain settings.
Empirical baselines for candidate set pruning in two-stage relevance ranking fail to satisfy pre-specified accuracy constraints.
Multi-choice question answering is performed by a series of binary classifications that classify (question, true answer) as positive and (question, false answer) as negative.
Performing multi-choice question answering by a series of binary classifications is more effective than the standard n-class classification approach across various models and datasets.
ExPUNations (ExPUN) augments an existing pun dataset with crowdsourced keywords, explanations, and fine-grained funniness ratings.
Explanation generation aids pun classification.
Keyword-conditioned pun generation generates puns conditioned on keywords.
Keywords annotated in the ExPUNations dataset improve the generation of novel humorous texts in human evaluation.
Natural language explanations in the ExPUNations dataset improve the accuracy and robustness of humor classifiers.
Few-shot relation extraction model upper layers are prone to learn relation-specific knowledge.
HyperNetwork-based Decoupling explicitly decouples the roles of upper and lower layers in few-shot relation extraction models.
HyperNetwork-based Decoupling uses an encoder, a network generator for producing relation classifiers, and produced-then-finetuned classifiers for every N-way-K-shot episode.

HyperNetwork-based Decoupling employs a two-step training framework with a class-agnostic aligner that encourages the encoder to learn general relation knowledge and the generated classifiers to acquire relation-specific knowledge.
Transforming text from a high-resource domain to a low-resource domain by changing style-related attributes generates synthetic training data for named entity recognition.
Constrained decoding with key data selection ingredients generates valid and coherent synthetic training data for named entity recognition.
Relative Distance Encoding is incorporated as a bias in graph structure-aware modules for knowledge graph-to-text generation.
Planning Selection filters the most relevant linearized sequences for knowledge graph-to-text generation.
Similarity Distinction distinguishes similar input knowledge graphs using contrastive learning.
Visually-Asymmetric Consistency Learning captures fine-grained visual and layout features for extracting entities from visually-rich form-like documents by incorporating color priors.
Visually-Asymmetric Consistency Learning outperforms the LayoutLM series baseline on benchmark datasets for extracting entities from visually-rich form-like documents.
DuReader-retrieval is a large-scale Chinese benchmark for passage retrieval.
DuReader-retrieval development and test sets have reduced false negatives through manual annotation of results pooled from multiple retrievers.
DuReader-retrieval training set excludes queries that are semantically similar to development and testing queries.
DuReader-retrieval includes two out-of-domain testing sets for cross-domain evaluation.
DuReader-retrieval includes a set of human translated queries for cross-lingual retrieval evaluation.
Graph-based explanations from models that encode knowledge graph facts separately from the question and then combine the representations for answer selection are unfaithful.

Graph-based explanations from models that encode knowledge graph facts separately from the question and then combine the representations for answer selection are incomplete because they exclude the transformer's reasoning on the question.
A novel proxy measure evaluates the faithfulness of graph-based explanations in commonsense question answering.
Two architecture changes enable the extraction of faithful graph-based explanations in commonsense question answering.

A large negation benchmark dataset of semi-automatically generated descriptive sentences about commonsense knowledge that are true or false, with negation in the majority of sentences in multiple forms.
Large language models are proficient at classifying affirmative sentences but struggle with negative sentences.
Large language models lack a deep understanding of negation and rely on superficial cues.
Fine-tuning language models on negative sentences improves negation handling but does not resolve the lack of generalization.
PromptEHR formulates Electronic Healthcare Records generation as a text-to-text translation task by language models to enable flexible event imputation.
PromptEHR controls Electronic Healthcare Records generation using prompt learning conditioned on numerical and categorical demographic features.
PromptEHR generates synthetic Electronic Healthcare Records with improved preservation of longitudinal patterns and cross-modality connections.
PromptEHR generates synthetic Electronic Healthcare Records with low privacy risks.
PreQuEL predicts machine translation output quality from the source text without requiring the actual translation.
PreQuEL can be defined relative to a specific machine translation system or generally relative to the state-of-the-art.
A data augmentation method for PreQuEL uses parallel corpora.
The data augmentation method improves PreQuEL performance.
The data augmentation method improves Quality-Estimation performance.
Lightweight unlearning layers learned with a selective teacher-student objective allow updating large language models without full retraining after data removals.
A fusion mechanism combines different unlearning layers for sequential forgetting operations.
In in-context learning, semantic information aggregates into label word representations during the shallow computation layers’ processing.
In in-context learning, the consolidated information in label words serves as a reference for large language models’ final predictions.
An anchor re-weighting method for in-context learning.
A demonstration compression technique speeds up inference in in-context learning.
An analysis framework diagnoses in-context learning errors in GPT2-XL.
A dataset for phrase grounding in visual dialogues that includes both noun phrases and pronouns is constructed.

Incorporating generalist instruction tuning into specialist models enhances performance for tasks with broad coverage.
For tasks with broad coverage, incorporating generalist instruction tuning into specialist models yields greater performance gains when task-specific training data is limited.
Incorporating generalist instruction tuning into specialist models improves understanding and reasoning abilities.
Hallucinatory information in generalist instruction tuning data negatively affects model performance for tasks requiring factual knowledge.
A novel dataset of human-assessed explanations for mental health analysis decisions is established.
Automatic evaluation metrics for explanation quality in mental health analysis are benchmarked.
ChatGPT exhibits strong in-context learning ability for mental health analysis but has a significant performance gap compared to advanced task-specific methods.

ChatGPT generates explanations for mental health analysis decisions that approach human performance.
CAPSTONE utilizes pseudo queries during training and progressively enhances the relevance between the generated query and the real query.
CAPSTONE enables the retrieval model to compute document representations by attending to both the document and query.
CAPSTONE outperforms previous dense retrieval models on both in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.
UPRISE automatically retrieves prompts for zero-shot task inputs.
UPRISE generalizes to unseen task types when trained on diverse tasks.
UPRISE generalizes to larger-scale language models when trained using a small language model.

Least-to-most prompting may be unnecessary for text-to-SQL parsing.
Detailed reasoning steps in chain of thought prompting tend to cause more error propagation in text-to-SQL parsing.

Large language models exhibit limited ability in solving natural language inference tasks.
Large language models fail to capture the human disagreement distribution in natural language inference.

A multivariate directed acyclic graph represents math word problem solutions for multivariate operators.
A generation-based deductive model uses a re-encoder to maintain deductive properties while avoiding expensive enumeration.
A generation-based deductive model solves math word problems with multivariate operators.
Adaptive Knowledge-Retention Pruning faithfully replicates the embedding space and feature space of dense language models to conserve pre-trained knowledge.
Adaptive Knowledge-Retention Pruning uses a reconstruction error that includes cumulative error from preceding layers and applies adaptive rectification.
Adaptive Knowledge-Retention Pruning achieves a superior balance between accuracy, sparsity, robustness, and pruning cost.
clembench uses constrained game-like settings to evaluate chat-optimized language models as conversational agents.

Maieutic Prompting induces a tree of abductive and recursive explanations.

LLM-enhanced self-training generates domain-specific raw corpora iteratively for cross-domain constituency parsing.

DIVE outperforms state-of-the-art models in descriptiveness and diversity for visual commonsense generation.
