Generating reasoning graphs with MetaLogic is challenging for current models, including giant pre-trained language models.
A novel dataset of African American Language texts from various regions and contexts is presented for evaluating bias against African American Language.
Six pre-trained large language models show lower performance on African American Language than on White Mainstream English on counterpart generation and masked span prediction tasks.
Visual-linguistic ambiguity is a chicken-and-egg problem where object localization requires anaphora resolution and anaphora resolution requires object localization.
Find2Find is a dataset for joint anaphora resolution and object localization that targets visual-linguistic ambiguity and consists of 500 anaphora-annotated recipes with corresponding videos.
An end-to-end joint multitask learning framework fuses visual and textual information for anaphora resolution and object localization.
Joint multitask learning improves anaphora resolution and object localization compared to single-task learning.
Generating synthetic scanpaths for language model augmentation eliminates the need for human gaze data.
Gradient propagation throughout a model enables fine-tuning the scanpath generator to downstream tasks.
Language models augmented with synthetic scanpaths outperform the base language model and achieve performance comparable to language models augmented with real human gaze data.
Activation-quantization-aware scaling (AQAS) considers the combined effects of weight and activation quantization.
Sequence-length-aware calibration (SLAC) aligns calibration sequence lengths to target tasks.
dINT is a hybrid data format combining integer and denormal representations that addresses underflow in W4A8 quantization.
Activation-quantization-aware scaling, sequence-length-aware calibration, and dINT achieve task accuracies comparable to full-precision models in LLMs including OPT and LLaMA.
dINT-compatible arithmetic units improve hardware efficiency relative to 8-bit integer MAC units.
A behavior vector quantifies the behavior of transformer-based program repair models when generating an output.
A behavior discriminator (BeDisc) identifies false behaviors in transformer-based program repair models.
Early abortion eliminates false behaviors while preserving repair accuracy in transformer-based program repair models.
Masked bypassing improves top-1 repair accuracy for transformer-based program repair models.
Value Injection Method (VIM) uses argument generation and question answering to inject targeted value distributions into large language models via fine-tuning.
Large language models injected with values via Value Injection Method (VIM) outperform baseline approaches in predicting opinions and behaviors.

A hybrid method that combines argumentation frameworks and semantics with Transformer-based architectures and neural graph networks automatically predicts the winning stance in complete professional argumentative debates.

HutCRS models conversations as a two-stage hierarchical interest tree where stage I identifies user-preferred aspects and stage II asks about attributes related to positive aspects or recommends items.
The Hierarchical-Interest Policy Learning (HIPL) module integrates the decision-making process for selecting aspects to inquire about and determining when to ask about attributes or recommend items.
HutCRS classifies attribute-level feedback to capture attribute instances accepted by users that are not in their historical interaction data.
Spectral Probing identifies spectral profiles for any given task.
Spectral Probing uses a fully learnable frequency filter.
Spectral Probing enables more granular analyses of spectral profiles than handcrafted filters.
Distinctive spectral profiles quantify cross-task similarity in a linguistically intuitive manner.
Distinctive spectral profiles remain consistent across languages.
Distinctive spectral profiles can serve as robust, lightweight task descriptors.
AugURE increases the diversity of positive pairs for contrastive learning by augmenting within-sentence pairs.
AugURE increases the diversity of positive pairs for contrastive learning by extracting cross-sentence pairs.
Noise-contrastive estimation loss has limitations for relation representation learning.
AugURE with margin loss achieves state-of-the-art performance for unsupervised relation extraction when combined with K-Means clustering.
End-to-end task-oriented dialogue is categorized into Modularly EToD and Fully EToD.
Large language models can self-improve their reasoning ability using only unlabeled datasets.
A pre-trained large language model generates high-confidence rationale-augmented answers for unlabeled questions using Chain-of-Thought prompting and self-consistency.
Large language models are fine-tuned using self-generated rationale-augmented answers as target outputs for self-improvement.
Self-improvement of large language models works in extreme low-resource cases with limited training questions and Chain-of-Thought prompts.
Fine-tuning on diverse reasoning paths is critical for self-improvement of large language models.
Pre-trained language models can accurately plan when visual observations are directly encoded as input prompts.
Direct encoding of visual observations as input prompts outperforms prior planning approaches on the ALFWorld and VirtualHome benchmarks.
A representative subset of in-context demonstrations requires high quality and diversity.
Demonstrations with high quality and diversity bolster model performance in in-context learning.
A two-stage Determinantal Point Process method selects representative in-context demonstrations by incorporating quality and diversity.
ALCE evaluates end-to-end systems that retrieve supporting evidence and generate answers with citations.
Automatic metrics for fluency, correctness, and citation quality correlate strongly with human judgements.
Current systems for generating text with citations often lack complete citation support.
TAGNet aggregates paths within a fixed window for each source-target pair in knowledge graph completion.
The complexity of TAGNet is independent of the number of layers.
TAGNet reduces the number of propagated messages while achieving competitive performance on knowledge graph completion.
RaVE is a dataset of rationale variations for case outcome classification in European Court of Human Rights cases, with annotations from two legal experts exhibiting weak agreement.
A two-level task-independent taxonomy categorizes rationale disagreements and includes case outcome classification-specific subcategories.
Disagreements in rationale construction for case outcome classification mainly stem from underspecification of the legal context.
State-of-the-art case outcome classification models show limited agreement with legal experts in rationale construction.
Expository Text Generation automatically generates accurate and stylistically consistent expository text for a topic by intelligently searching a knowledge source.
IRP iteratively performs content planning, fact retrieval, and rephrasing.
IRP produces factual and organized expository texts that accurately inform readers.
Three diverse datasets for expository text generation are collected.
Incorporating data generated by Large Language Models improves cross-lingual performance in multilingual commonsense reasoning with limited training data.
ChatGPT and GPT-4 produce natural and coherent text in most languages for multilingual commonsense reasoning data augmentation.
ChatGPT and GPT-4 struggle to generate meaningful text in Tamil for multilingual commonsense reasoning data augmentation.
ChatGPT generates less plausible alternatives than the original dataset for multilingual commonsense reasoning data augmentation.
GPT-4 generates examples with logical consistency competitive with the original dataset for multilingual commonsense reasoning data augmentation.
User-facing decontextualization of snippets from scientific documents requires clarifying where edits occur and handling references to other documents.
Decontextualization of user-facing snippets from scientific documents is decomposed into question generation, question answering, and rewriting.
A dataset of gold decontextualizations for user-facing snippets from scientific documents is collected from experienced scientific article readers.
QaDecontext is a prompting strategy for decontextualizing user-facing snippets from scientific documents.
Rewriting is easy for current language models, but question generation and answering remain challenging for decontextualizing user-facing snippets from scientific documents.
Masked-Diffuse LM is a diffusion language model inspired by linguistic features.
A linguistic-informed forward process adds corruptions to text through strategically soft-masking.
Predicting the categorical distribution with cross-entropy loss in every diffusion step efficiently connects continuous and discrete spaces.
Masked-Diffuse LM achieves better generation quality and lower training cost than state-of-the-art diffusion models.
TWOSL transforms standard multilingual sentence encoders into slot labelers.
TWOSL uses SL-adapted contrastive learning with few slot labeling examples to transform sentence encoders into task-specific span encoders.
TWOSL recasts slot labeling from token classification to span classification, which is less data-intensive.
TWOSL is effective and robust across diverse languages on multilingual task-oriented dialogue datasets.
TWOSL is especially effective in transfer-free few-shot setups for slot labeling.
Language models pre-trained using only the first character of each token exhibit high performance retention on standard NLU benchmarks and probing tasks compared to full-token models.
Selective Prompt Tuning learns to select proper prompt layers by inserting a prompt controlled by a learnable probabilistic gate at each intermediate layer of a pretrained model.
SPT-DARTS optimizes learnable gates for prompt layer selection using a bi-level optimization framework.
Selective Prompt Tuning achieves better performance than state-of-the-art prompt tuning baselines with comparable or fewer tunable parameters.
CS2W is a Chinese spoken-to-written style conversion dataset from transcribed conversational texts.
CS2W covers disfluencies, grammatical errors, ASR transcription errors, and colloquial words in Chinese spoken-to-written conversion.
A dynamic context graph represents information about an utterance and its conversational context with a subgraph whose node count varies per utterance.
A graph neural network encodes the dynamic context graph to exploit its structure and represent unseen nodes.
Dynamic context modeling is superior to static approaches for both simple and complex questions.
Modeling the structure of context is better at processing discourse information, including handling ellipsis and resolving coreference, and at handling longer interactions.
ArgTersely evaluates sentence-level counter-argument generation using a manually annotated dataset from the ChangeMyView debate forum.
Arg-LlaMA generates high-quality sentence-level counter-arguments.
Arg-Judge evaluates counter-argument generation using a BERT model trained on human preference data.
A dataset of compound and non-compound words covers 56 diverse languages and contains 255k examples from Wiktionary.
Large Language Models perform poorly on decompounding, especially on words that are tokenized unfavorably by subword tokenization.
A two-stage procedure for decompounding uses a fully self-supervised objective followed by optional supervised fine-tuning on annotated Wiktionary data.
Self-supervised decompounding models outperform prior unsupervised decompounding models.
Fine-tuned decompounding models outperform prior language-specific decompounding tools.
CompoundPiece improves decompounding performance compared to SentencePiece tokenization.
Verbalised scene graphs transform visual relation triplets into structured captions to serve as additional image descriptions in multimodal pretraining.
Masked relation prediction encourages relating entities from image regions using visually masked contexts in multimodal pretraining.
Example-guided question answering for dialogue state tracking minimizes task shift between services for continual learning.
Example-guided question answering alleviates service-specific memorization in dialogue state tracking.
Example-guided question answering teaches models to contextualize given questions and examples to extract information from conversations in dialogue state tracking.

A small-parameter model achieves higher performance in dialogue state tracking continual learning by learning from in-context examples retrieved by a retriever that identifies turns with similar dialogue state changes.

Example-guided question answering combined with dialogue-level memory replay achieves state of the art performance on dialogue state tracking continual learning metrics without complex regularization or parameter expansion.
The DIsh NamE Recognition (DiNeR) task requires models to recognize dish names composed of diverse combinations of food, actions, and flavors from recipe instructions.
The DiNeR dataset is a large realistic Chinese dataset for dish name recognition.
The DiNeR dataset involves linguistic phenomena including anaphora, omission, and ambiguity.
T5 and large language models serve as strong baselines for the DiNeR task.
Selective Context prunes redundant content from the input context of large language models.
Selective Context reduces memory usage and inference time for large language models while maintaining comparable task performance.

Social norms across Chinese and American cultures are discovered and compared by leveraging Zhihu discussions and the SocialChemistry dataset as proxies for contrasting cultural axes, aligning social situations cross-culturally, and extracting norms via in-context learning.
A dataset contains social norms aligned with social situations across Chinese and American cultures with free-text explanations.
Explainable social norm entailment tests the ability of models to reason about social norms across cultures.
Existing language models under 3B parameters have room for improvement in reasoning about social norms across cultures.
Cross-cultural norm differences align with the social orientations framework and reveal situational and descriptive nuances between Chinese and American cultures.
XLM-RoBERTa input token embeddings linearly separate tokens by writing system.
mT5 input token embeddings have nearest neighbors that are frequently translations across multiple writing systems.
Large language models exhibit a significant per-statement correlation with human judgments of profoundness regardless of statement type or prompting technique.
Large language models systematically overestimate the profoundness of nonsensical statements except for Tk-instruct, which uniquely underestimates the profoundness of statements.
Few-shot learning prompts draw large language model ratings closer to human judgments compared to chain-of-thought prompting.
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback increases the bias of large language models to overestimate the profoundness of statements.
The Arabic Level of Dialectness (ALDi) is a continuous linguistic variable representing the spectrum of dialectness in Arabic text at the sentence level.
The AOC-ALDi dataset is derived from the AOC dataset and contains sentences from news articles and user comments manually labeled with their level of dialectness.

A model trained on the AOC-ALDi dataset identifies levels of dialectness on corpora with dialects and genres not included in the training data, providing a more nuanced picture than traditional Dialect Identification systems.
ALDi reveals Arabic speakers’ stylistic choices in different situations, a property useful for sociolinguistic analyses.
Anthropomorphism in dialogue systems can reinforce gender stereotypes.
Anthropomorphism in dialogue systems can affect conceptions of acceptable language.
Rhetorical parallelism detection identifies juxtaposed phrases sharing the same sequence of linguistic features such as phonological, syntactic, and semantic features in rhetoric.
A new Latin dataset and an adapted Chinese dataset evaluate rhetorical parallelism detection.
A family of metrics evaluates performance on rhetorical parallelism detection.
Baseline systems detect rhetorical parallelism.
Novel sequence labeling schemes detect rhetorical parallelism.
SciTechNews contains tuples of a publicly available scientific paper, its corresponding news article, and an expert-written short summary snippet.
A discourse structure-based framework integrates a scientific paper's discourse structure with its metadata to guide the generation of science journalism articles.
A discourse structure-based framework improves the elaboration of a content plan meaningful for the target audience compared to baseline methods.
A discourse structure-based framework improves the simplification of the information selected for science journalism compared to baseline methods.
A discourse structure-based framework improves the production of a coherent final report in a layman's style compared to baseline methods.

Heuristic-Analytic Reasoning (HAR) strategies incorporate interlinked dual processes of heuristic and analytic reasoning in fine-tuning and in-context learning for pre-trained language models on physical commonsense reasoning tasks.
Heuristic-Analytic Reasoning (HAR) strategies improve the coherence of rationalizations for model decisions in physical commonsense reasoning tasks.
Heuristic-Analytic Reasoning (HAR) strategies achieve state-of-the-art results on the Tiered Reasoning for Intuitive Physics (TRIP) benchmark.
The improved coherence from Heuristic-Analytic Reasoning (HAR) strategies results from more faithful attention to relevant language context in each reasoning step.
Input reformulations during finetuning for challenging translation tasks leverage model strengths from pretraining to improve downstream performance.
Input reformulations for translation tasks are simple data-level modifications that require no additional training data collection and no inference-time data modification.
Input reformulations for translation tasks apply to single language pair and massively multilingual translation tasks.
Input reformulations for translation tasks improve performance on the Flores200 translation benchmark.
ChatGPT exhibits consistent advantages in out-of-domain intent discovery and generalized intent discovery under zero-shot settings, but is at a disadvantage compared to fine-tuned models.
Large language models face challenges in clustering for open-world intent discovery.
Large language models face challenges in domain-specific understanding for open-world intent discovery.
Large language models face challenges in cross-domain in-context learning scenarios for open-world intent discovery.
VEC evaluates visual concepts (such as shape and material) and embodied concepts (such as temperature) in language models.
The understanding of certain visual concepts in language models emerges with model scaling, but the scaling law does not apply to all basic concepts.
Vision-augmented language models achieve human-level understanding of embodied concepts.
Rich semantics in visual representation serve as a valuable source of embodied knowledge.
A distillation method transfers embodied knowledge from vision-language models to language models, achieving performance gains equivalent to massive language model parameter scaling.
The coupling of the Smith-Waterman local alignment algorithm with SBERT embedding distance constructs narrative alignments by quantifying text similarity between scenes and book units.
Phenotype-driven medical vision-language representation learning efficiently bridges the gap between visual and textual modalities for radiology report generation.
Contrasting radiology images with individual report sentences learns fine-grained medical vision-language representations.
RobustGEC evaluates context robustness of Grammatical Error Correction systems by testing on human-annotated variants with context perturbations.
State-of-the-art Grammatical Error Correction systems lack sufficient robustness against context perturbations.
A method mitigates the effect of context perturbations on Grammatical Error Correction systems.
For tasks with structured outputs that cannot be solved directly by LLMs, generating input text from a target output structure via LLM prompting enables the generation of high-quality synthetic data.
A large-scale synthetic dataset for closed information extraction is generated.
Human evaluation shows the synthetic dataset for closed information extraction has superior quality compared to existing datasets.
Small models termed SynthIE, finetuned on the synthetic dataset, outperform prior state-of-the-art models of equal size in closed information extraction.
RankC evaluates knowledge consistency across languages independently from accuracy.
Increasing model size leads to higher factual probing accuracy in most languages but does not improve cross-lingual consistency.
New factual associations inserted in English transfer only to languages with which English has a high RankC score.
Fine-tuned large language models simulate how previous students would respond to unseen test items.
GPT-4 generates test items for silent sentence reading efficiency following expert-developed rules.
A fine-tuned large language model filters test items based on criteria from psychological measurements.
An optimal-transport-inspired technique generates parallel tests.
Generated test scores are highly correlated with standard test scores for students in grades 2 to 8.
A corpus of folk tales covers 27 diverse cultures across six continents.
Folk tales from different cultures express different prevalent values and morals.
Folk tales exhibit cross-cultural trends in problematic gender biases.
Public-domain fiction stories exhibit reduced value expression.
In folk tales, traditional gender biases are associated with values, morals, and agency.
Most existing summarization metrics favor longer summaries even after accounting for other factors.
A Bayesian normalization technique reduces the bias of summarization metrics toward longer summaries.
A Bayesian normalization technique improves the concordance between human annotators and most summarization metrics for summary coherence.
Adaptive Hyper-modality Learning (AHL) learns an irrelevance/conflict-suppressing representation from visual and audio features under the guidance of language features at different scales.
Adaptive Hyper-modality Learning (AHL) is valid and necessary for multimodal sentiment analysis.
MULTITuDE is a benchmarking dataset for multilingual machine-generated text detection containing authentic and machine-generated texts in 11 languages generated by 8 multilingual LLMs.
Generalization of zero-shot and fine-tuned machine-generated text detectors to unseen languages (both linguistically similar and dissimilar) and unseen LLMs is evaluated using MULTITuDE.
Performance improvement of machine-generated text detectors when trained on multiple languages is evaluated using MULTITuDE.
API-Bank evaluates tool-augmented LLMs' capabilities in planning, retrieving, and calling APIs.
A comprehensive training set of tool-use dialogues from diverse APIs supports training tool-augmented LLMs.
Lynx surpasses Alpaca in tool utilization performance.
GPT-4 excels in planning for tool utilization.
Error analysis identifies key challenges for tool-augmented LLMs.
CRUSH4SQL uses an LLM to hallucinate a minimal database schema adequate for answering a natural language query.
CRUSH4SQL retrieves a subset of the actual database schema by composing results from multiple dense retrievals using a hallucinated database schema.
Database schema hallucination is useful as a bridging mechanism for schema subsetting in Text-to-SQL for large databases.
The semi-synthetic benchmark for schema subsetting on large databases is created by taking a union of schemas from the SPIDER dataset.
SocialDB is a real-life benchmark for schema subsetting on large databases sourced from a large data warehouse.
CRUSH4SQL achieves higher recall than state-of-the-art retrieval-based augmentation methods for schema subsetting on large databases.
A dataset of names that are strongly demographically aligned represents demographic groups for translation bias analysis.
A translation evaluation procedure based on round-trip translation measures the translation quality of names.
Translation systems have lower ability to correctly translate female-associated names than male-associated names.
The disparity in translation quality for female-associated names is more pronounced for names associated with Black and Hispanic minorities.
PRESENCE jointly reweights samples during language model pre-training using self-influence scores as an indicator of sample importance.
PRESENCE promotes novelty and stability for language model pre-training.
MeaeQ filters task-relevant data from a public text corpus using a zero-shot sequence inference classifier and API service information.
MeaeQ uses a clustering-based data reduction technique to obtain representative data for model extraction attacks.
MeaeQ achieves higher functional similarity to victim models than baseline methods with fewer queries.
ChatGPT and GPT-4 frequently fail to generate logically consistent predictions despite showing enhanced language understanding and reasoning ability.
Prompt designing, few-shot learning, and using larger large language models are unlikely to resolve the inconsistency issue in large language models.
A pre-training method for text encoders uses contrastive learning with intent pseudo-labels to produce embeddings well-suited for intent classification tasks.
Pre-trained Intent-aware Encoder (PIE) aligns encodings of utterances with their intent names.
Pre-trained Intent-aware Encoder (PIE) achieves higher accuracy than the previous state-of-the-art in N-way zero-shot and one-shot intent classification settings.
Multilingual large language models underperform on code-switching tasks relative to smaller-scale fine-tuned models.
Current multilingualism in large language models does not inherently imply proficiency with code-switching texts.
Lazy-k is a constrained decoding method for token classification in information extraction.
Constrained decoding approaches improve model performance in token classification for information extraction, especially for smaller models.
Lazy-k provides flexibility between decoding time and accuracy.
NLI4CT supports determining the inference relation between a natural language statement and a clinical trial report.
NLI4CT supports retrieving supporting facts to justify an inference relation in clinical trial reports.
Existing natural language inference models perform poorly on determining inference relations in clinical trial reports.
MT2 integrates sentence-level translation, document-level translation, translation memory, and terminology constrained translation.
Translation-specific In-Context Learning models translation tasks as context-learning tasks that integrate contextual information.
A retrieval and alignment method obtains large scale context-enhancement training data for translation.
A computation-efficient framework steers frozen pre-trained language models towards more commonsensical generation.
A reference-free evaluator assigns a sentence a commonsensical score by grounding the sentence to a dynamic commonsense knowledge base from four relational aspects.
An extension of the NADO controllable generation method trains an auxiliary head to guide a fixed pre-trained language model to satisfy a commonsense oracle.
Sentence embeddings from BERT suffer from a bias towards uninformative words, which limits performance in semantic textual similarity tasks.
Ditto computes sentence embeddings by weighting words with model-based importance estimations and taking the weighted average of word representations from pre-trained models.
Ditto is applicable as a postprocessing operation to any pre-trained language model.
Ditto does not add parameters and does not require learning.
Ditto alleviates the anisotropy problem in sentence embeddings.
Ditto improves the performance of pre-trained language models on semantic textual similarity benchmarks.
The select step uses a semantic similarity metric to select unannotated forum conversations for forum conversation summarization.
The prompt step uses a semantic similarity metric to retrieve suitable prompts for ChatGPT from a small annotated validation set for forum conversation summarization.
The filter step removes low-quality instances from the generated dataset for forum conversation summarization.

The select-prompt-filter approach improves forum conversation summarization over standard knowledge distillation by leveraging in-domain pseudo-labeled data when using the same amount of training data.
Outlier dimensions in large language models that occur in pre-training persist in fine-tuned models.
A single outlier dimension in large language models can complete downstream tasks with a minimal error rate.
Outlier dimensions in large language models can encode crucial task-specific knowledge.
In large language models, the value of a representation in a single outlier dimension drives downstream model decisions.
TRAVEL integrates FAQ question tags into a reinforcement learning framework to minimize the negative impact of irrelevant information in dynamic conversation contexts.
KnowGen generates new knowledge by altering existing entity attributes and relationships, resulting in artificial entities that are distinct from real-world entities.
ALCUNA assesses large language models' abilities in knowledge understanding, differentiation, and association.
Large language models perform unsatisfactorily when handling new knowledge, particularly in reasoning between new and internal knowledge.
Empathic similarity modeling identifies personal story similarity based on empathic resonance rather than semantic or lexical similarity.
Empathic similarity is operationalized by main events, emotional trajectories, and overall morals or takeaways of stories.
EmpathicStories is a dataset of personal stories annotated with empathic similarity features and story pairs annotated with empathic similarity scores.
Empathic similarity models outperform semantic similarity models on correlation and retrieval metrics for story pairs.
Empathic similarity-based story retrieval leads to higher user empathy than semantic similarity-based story retrieval.
In-context knowledge editing (IKE) edits factual knowledge without gradient or parameter updating.
In-context knowledge editing (IKE) reduces over-editing on similar but unrelated facts compared to gradient-based knowledge editing methods.
In-context knowledge editing (IKE) reduces knowledge forgetting on previously stored knowledge compared to gradient-based knowledge editing methods.
In-context knowledge editing (IKE) scales to large language models including OPT-175B.
The generalized left-corner transformation supports semiring-weighted production rules.
The generalized left-corner transformation provides finer-grained control over which left corners may be moved.
The generalized left-corner transformation and speculation define equivalent weighted languages.
The generalized left-corner transformation replaces left recursion with right recursion, whereas speculation does not.

Model-checking with natural language is a textual entailment problem defined in a purely logical sense for investigating the effect of generalised quantifiers on transformer-based language models' behaviour.
Model-checking with natural language enables the automatic construction of datasets for assessing transformer-based language models' ability to learn the meanings of generalised quantifiers.
Transformer-based language models generally can comprehend the logical semantics of the most common generalised quantifiers.
Distinct generalised quantifiers influence transformer-based language models in varying ways.
A coarse-grained table extraction module selects tables based on rows and columns relevant to the claim.
A fine-grained cell selection graph combines unstructured and structured evidence and enables multi-hop and numerical reasoning.
Leveraging row and column semantics of tables achieves higher evidence recall for structured evidence extraction on FEVEROUS than prior work.
A standalone preference model scores human-generated and LLM-generated answers to longform questions on the same scale.
An axiomatic framework generates preference training data pairs based on specific principles.
A small amount of axiomatic signals enables small preference models to outperform GPT-4 in preference scoring for longform question answering.
PEFTDebias acquires debiasing parameters along a specific bias axis in an upstream phase.
PEFTDebias incorporates and freezes debiasing parameters during fine-tuning in a downstream phase.
PEFTDebias reduces downstream biases in foundation models.
Debiasing parameters in PEFTDebias are specific to a bias axis.
Debiasing parameters in PEFTDebias are transferable across downstream tasks.
CT-GAT constructs adversarial examples for different tasks by extracting transferable features across tasks.
CT-GAT achieves superior attack performance with small cost.
A corpus contains hateful tweet-paragraph pairs with candidate counterhate arguments for hateful content toward individuals.
A methodology assures the authenticity of counterhate arguments and their specificity to the individual of interest.
Retrieving counterhate arguments from online articles is an efficient alternative to generation approaches and avoids hallucinating unsupported arguments.
Identifying counterhate arguments for hateful content toward individuals not included in the training set is more challenging than for individuals included in the training set.
Cross-lingual transfer in sentiment analysis usually increases bias relative to monolingual transfer.
Racial biases are more prevalent than gender biases in sentiment analysis systems using cross-lingual transfer.
A resource for studying bias in cross-lingual sentiment analysis includes sentiment models and evaluation code.
REM performs post-hoc refinement of utterances with low source-faithfulness in knowledge grounded conversations by mining key entities from source knowledge.
REM reduces entity hallucination in utterances of knowledge grounded conversations.
The eLife biomedical lay summarisation dataset is augmented with article-specific knowledge graphs that contain detailed information on relevant biomedical concepts.
Graph-based domain knowledge integration increases the readability of generated text in biomedical lay summarisation.
Graph-based domain knowledge integration improves the explanation of technical concepts in biomedical lay summarisation.
INSTRUCTSCORE is a fine-grained explainable evaluation metric for text generation.
INSTRUCTSCORE is a text evaluation metric based on LLaMA, fine-tuned using explicit human instruction and the implicit knowledge of GPT-4.
INSTRUCTSCORE produces a score for generated text and a human readable diagnostic report.
INSTRUCTSCORE surpasses unsupervised metrics based on larger language models.
INSTRUCTSCORE achieves performance on par with state-of-the-art metrics that are fine-tuned on human ratings, without using human-rated data.

Copying-style degeneration in knowledge-grounded dialogue generation is primarily due to the weak likelihood objective that allows models to cheat by duplicating knowledge segments via superficial pattern matching based on overlap.
Multi-level Adaptive Contrastive Learning (MACL) dynamically samples negative examples and penalizes degeneration behaviors at both the token-level and sequence-level.
Synthetic data generated using the error distribution from transcribed data improves error correction model performance over random perturbations.
Language-specific adjustments to a BPE tokenizer vocabulary balance adaptation to unseen distributions and retention of transcribed error knowledge.
Sequence-Level Self-Distillation generates distilled data using the non-autoregressive model itself, eliminating the need for additional teacher networks.
Sequence-Level Self-Distillation adapts to different non-autoregressive models without precise adjustments.
Sequence-Level Self-Distillation consistently improves non-autoregressive models on both raw data and distilled data without sacrificing inference speed.
ConvRe is a benchmark for converse relations derived from knowledge graph completion datasets.
ConvRe features two tasks (Re2Text and Text2Re) formulated as multi-choice question answering for evaluating LLMs' ability to match relations and associated text.
The ConvRe evaluation protocol includes variants to the test text and few-shot example text.
Various scaling trends were observed in experiments on three popular LLM families.
LLMs resort to shortcut learning and face challenges on the ConvRe benchmark.
Impressions investigates the semiotics of images and how visual features and design choices elicit specific emotions, thoughts and beliefs.
An annotation task inspired by Visual Arts image analysis techniques collects annotations for image impact, pragmatic image description, impressions and aesthetic design choices.
Existing multimodal image captioning and conditional generation models struggle to simulate plausible human responses to images.
The Impressions dataset improves the ability of multimodal image captioning and conditional generation models to model impressions and aesthetic evaluations of images.
Attitude and theme-guided rebuttal generation generates peer review rebuttals using attitude roots and themes.
A peer review discourse structure dataset includes attitude roots, attitude themes, and canonical rebuttals.
Peer review annotation uses concepts including aspects a review sentence relates to for attitude and theme annotation.
Domain-specific models annotate attitude roots and themes in peer reviews.
An intervention-based strategy corrects causal term identification under the invariant entity constraint by using a constituency parser.
A bottom-up relation expansion algorithm on WordNet uncovers commonsense relations between entities.
Generating commonsense counterfactuals enhances the stability of base relation extraction models in low-resource, out-of-domain, and adversarial-attack settings.
A principled, probabilistic approach enables training explainable multi-hop question answering systems without rationale supervision.
Modeling rationales as sets captures interactions between documents and sentences within a document for multi-hop reasoning.

Explainable multi-hop question answering systems trained without rationale supervision using a principled, probabilistic approach select rationales more accurately than previous methods while maintaining similar answer prediction accuracy.
NeuSTIP performs link prediction and time interval prediction in temporal knowledge graphs.
NeuSTIP learns temporal rules using Allen predicates to ensure temporal consistency between neighboring predicates in the rule body.
NeuSTIP uses a scoring function that evaluates the confidence of candidate answers for link and time interval predictions by utilizing learned temporal rules.
NeuSTIP achieves competitive performance on link prediction in temporal knowledge graphs.
NeuSTIP sets a new state of the art for time prediction in temporal knowledge graphs.
ViT-TTS is the first visual text-to-speech model that uses scalable diffusion transformers.
ViT-TTS generates audio by complementing the phoneme sequence with visual information.
A self-supervised learning framework enhances the visual-text encoder and denoiser decoder to mitigate data scarcity in visual acoustic information learning.
A diffusion transformer scalable in parameters and capacity learns visual scene information for visual text-to-speech.
ViT-TTS outperforms cascaded systems and other baselines in visual text-to-speech regardless of scene visibility.
ViT-TTS achieves results comparable to rich-resource baselines when trained on low-resource data.

The Diffusion Weighted Graph Framework characterizes local structure discriminately for each sample by diffusing neighborhood relationships along semantic paths guided by the nearest neighbors for multiple hops in new intent discovery.
For each sample, the Diffusion Weighted Graph Framework samples positive keys and weighs them by semantic similarities and local structures for contrastive learning in new intent discovery.
The Graph Smoothing Filter utilizes structure relationships to filter high-frequency noise in semantically ambiguous samples on the cluster boundary for new intent discovery.
The Diffusion Weighted Graph Framework outperforms state-of-the-art models on multiple benchmark datasets for new intent discovery.
LAPDOG consists of a story retriever and a dialogue generator.
LAPDOG's story retriever uses a persona profile as queries to retrieve relevant information from story documents to augment the persona profile.
LAPDOG's dialogue generator uses dialogue history and the augmented persona profile to generate personalized responses.

LAPDOG uses a joint training framework that collaboratively learns the story retriever and dialogue generator, with the story retriever optimized towards response generation metrics to retrieve content for the dialogue generator.
Using inherited automatic toxicity scores for model and technique comparisons produces inaccurate findings.

DiffS2UT applies the diffusion forward process in the continuous speech representation space and the diffusion backward process in the discrete speech unit space.
DiffS2UT preserves the semantic structure of the continuous speech representation space in the diffusion process.
DiffS2UT achieves comparable performance to auto-regressive baselines on textless direct speech-to-speech translation with fewer decoding steps.
MingOfficial contains structured career records, annotated personnel types, and historical texts for Ming Dynasty officials.
A graph neural network combines structured career records and historical texts to investigate social structures of Ming Dynasty officials.
MingOfficial enables exploratory analysis of Ming Dynasty officials' identities.
MingOfficial enables the identification of nuanced identities such as civil officials holding military power.
PreWoMe handles any type of information-seeking question in long-form question answering.
PreWoMe extracts presuppositions in the question and exploits them as working memory to generate feedback and action about the question.
PreWoMe handles misleading and normal information-seeking questions in long-form question answering.
KCTS guides a frozen language model to generate text aligned with reference knowledge at each decoding step using a knowledge classifier score and Monte-Carlo Tree Search.
RIPA detects hallucinations at the token level.
KCTS is a plug-and-play and model-agnostic decoding method that reduces hallucinations in natural language generation.
Tree of Clarifications recursively constructs a tree of disambiguations for ambiguous questions using few-shot prompting and external knowledge.
Tree of Clarifications uses the tree of disambiguations to generate a long-form answer.
Tree of Clarifications outperforms existing few-shot baselines on the ASQA benchmark.
Tree of Clarifications outperforms fully-supervised baselines on disambiguation metrics of the ASQA benchmark.
For linguistic data, the intrinsic geometric dimension predicts the coding length under a language model.
High compression of a linguistic dataset predicts rapid language model adaptation to the dataset.
Only some intrinsic dimension estimators encapsulate the relationship between information-theoretic compression, geometric compression, and ease-of-adaptation.
Conversation Chronicles incorporates time intervals and fine-grained speaker relationships in multi-session conversations.
Conversation Chronicles maintains coherent and consistent interactions across multiple sessions.
ReBot uses chronological summarization and dialogue generation modules for multi-session conversation modeling.
ReBot demonstrates long-term context understanding in multi-session conversations.
A sequential hierarchical classification model for disaster tweets determines damage severity and humanitarian categories.
The sequential hierarchical classification model for disaster tweets uses modality-specific models and multimodal classification approaches including multi-task learning.
The sequential hierarchical classification model for disaster tweets provides results at multiple abstraction levels.
Text-to-text multilingual large language models have strong bilingual lexicon induction capabilities.
Few-shot prompting with in-context examples from nearest neighbours achieves the best performance for bilingual lexicon induction on many language pairs.
Context-aware neural machine translation faces core challenges in discourse phenomena, context usage, model architectures, and document-level evaluation.
Paragraph-to-paragraph (PARA2PARA) translation provides a more realistic setting for document-level translation.
A Chinese-English novel dataset supports document-level translation research.
ULF denoises weak supervision data by using models trained on all but the held-out labeling functions to identify and correct biases specific to the held-out labeling functions.
ULF refines the allocation of labeling functions to classes by re-estimating the assignment on highly reliable cross-validated samples.
Adapt in Contexts retrieves cross-domain elements similar to the query for unsupervised domain adaptation in an in-context learning setting.
Adapt in Contexts learns target domain distribution and discriminative task signals from augmented cross-domain in-context examples.
Adapt in Contexts uses different prompting and training strategies for different language model architectures to learn target domain distribution via language modeling.
Natural Language Learned Features (NLLF) represent examples by vectors of answers to natural language binary questions related to the target task.
Natural Language Learned Features (NLLF) are generated by a BERT model trained in a Natural Language Inference fashion using weak labels from a Large Language Model.
Large Language Models can handle intermediary subtasks and produce useful weak labels for training, even when they struggle with the main task using in-context learning.
Natural Language Learned Features (NLLF) can be generated for any natural language binary question using zero-shot inference.
Decision trees using Natural Language Learned Features (NLLF) surpass pre-trained transformers in performance for some tasks.

TheoremQA is a domain-expert-curated theorem-driven question-answering dataset covering Math, Physics, EE&CS, and Finance for evaluating AI models' capabilities to apply theorems to solve challenging science problems.
GPT-4 substantially outperforms open-sourced models on challenging science problems requiring theorem application.
Explore-Instruct enhances domain-specific instruction coverage through active exploration via Large Language Models.
Explore-Instruct uses a search algorithm on representative domain use cases to obtain diversified and domain-focused instruction-tuning data.
Explore-Instruct improves model performance over multiple baselines, including domain-specific data enhancement methods.
ScdNER uses a binary classifier to predict the probability of token sequences being entity spans.
ScdNER uses a span-based key-value memory to obtain global features of entities from span probabilities, reducing the impact of non-entity sequences.
ScdNER uses a gate mechanism to adaptively fuse local and global information for entity type prediction.
A new paradigm constructs multimodal dialogues by extending text-only dialogues with turn-level and entity-level visual knowledge.
ReSee-WoW and ReSee-DD are multimodal dialogue datasets constructed by extending text-only dialogues with turn-level and entity-level visual knowledge.
ReSee adds visual representation to vanilla dialogue models by modality concatenations.
ReSee outperforms state-of-the-art methods on automatic and human evaluations for multimodal dialogue.
A multilingual pretraining corpus for 16 African languages is compiled by auditing existing pretraining corpora and applying improved web crawling strategies to extract cleaner data.
A T5-based language model pretrained on high-quality pretraining data for African languages demonstrates improved performance on multiple downstream NLP tasks compared to existing pretrained models.
Data quality plays a critical role in pretraining language models for low-resource languages.
Dense KNN adapts to test distribution shift and deleted training data in hashtag prediction without requiring re-training.
Dense KNN outperforms static parametric baselines for hashtag prediction under temporal distribution shift without requiring gradient-based re-training.
Dense KNN handles dynamically deleted user data with negligible computational cost and performance degradation.
A publicly available year-long Twitter dataset for longitudinal hashtag prediction exhibits temporal distribution shift.
Temporal question answering on semi-structured tables requires temporal reasoning.
TEMPTABQA is a dataset for temporal question answering on semi-structured tables containing question-answer pairs from Wikipedia Infobox tables across multiple domains.
Top-performing large language models lag behind human performance in temporal question answering on semi-structured tables.
Predicting edit operations on an instance-per-instance basis enables grade-specific text simplification.
Instance-per-instance edit operation prediction for grade-specific text simplification improves output quality over corpus-level search-based heuristics.

Critic-driven decoding combines the probabilistic output of a generator language model with the output of a text critic classifier that assesses the match between input data and generated text to mitigate hallucinations in data-to-text generation.
Critic-driven decoding does not require changes to the language model's architecture or training procedure.
Critic-driven decoding is compatible with any language model and any decoding procedure that operates on word probabilities.
The text critic classifier in critic-driven decoding does not require additional training data and uses the base language model's training data and synthetic negative examples.
SEER formulates exemplar selection for HybridQA as a Knapsack Integer Linear Program.
SEER incorporates diversity constraints to prioritize exemplars with desirable attributes for HybridQA.
SEER incorporates capacity constraints to ensure the prompt size respects capacity budgets for HybridQA.
Self-evolution learning (SE) generates adaptive and model-friendly pseudo samples for few-shot text classification.
Self-evolution learning adapts to the model's learning ability during training to generate pseudo samples for few-shot text classification.
Instance-specific label smoothing regularization generates soft labels by linearly interpolating model outputs and one-hot labels for label mixing up in few-shot text classification.
Self-evolution learning outperforms existing mixup methods in few-shot text classification.
Self-evolution learning enhances the model's generalization ability in few-shot text classification.
INFORM uses information entropy as a criterion for selecting Chain-of-Thought prompts.
INFORM uses information entropy to automatically generate Chain-of-Thought prompts with higher information entropy scores.
INFORM uses information entropy to adaptively determine the number of samples during inference.
INFORM achieves superior reasoning performance and efficiency.
Crystal introspects for knowledge statements related to the given question for commonsense reasoning.
Crystal makes an informed prediction grounded in previously introspected knowledge for commonsense reasoning.
Crystal tunes the knowledge introspection and knowledge-grounded reasoning modes via reinforcement learning to mutually adapt with rewards from self-feedback.
Crystal enhances the transparency of the commonsense reasoning process.
Reinforcing a neural model with self-feedback is feasible for commonsense reasoning.
Confidence-based Pruning gradually grows the number of samples for utility estimation in Minimum Bayes Risk decoding.
Confidence-based Pruning prunes hypotheses unlikely to have the highest utility based on confidence estimates from bootstrap sampling in Minimum Bayes Risk decoding.
Confidence-based Pruning requires fewer samples than standard Minimum Bayes Risk decoding.
Confidence-based Pruning reduces the number of calls to the utility function compared to standard Minimum Bayes Risk decoding.
Confidence-based Pruning achieves statistically indistinguishable accuracy from standard Minimum Bayes Risk decoding.
Reasoning via Planning (RAP) repurposes large language models as both world models and reasoning agents.
Reasoning via Planning (RAP) incorporates a Monte Carlo Tree Search-based planning algorithm for strategic exploration in the reasoning space.
Reasoning via Planning (RAP) incrementally builds a reasoning tree under the guidance of a world model and task-specific rewards while balancing exploration and exploitation.

Coarse-to-Fine Chain-of-Thought (CoF-CoT) breaks down Natural Language Understanding tasks into multiple reasoning steps that allow for the acquisition and leveraging of essential concepts from different granularities.

Coarse-to-Fine Chain-of-Thought (CoF-CoT) leverages semantic-based Abstract Meaning Representation structured knowledge as an intermediate step to capture the nuances and diverse structures of utterances and to understand connections between the varying levels of granularity of utterances.
The mathematical foundation for significance testing of model-based metrics incorporates metric model errors in sample variance calculation.
Accounting for metric model errors in sample variance calculation for model-based metrics alters the conclusions of significance tests.
The sentence transformation testset evaluates surface structure bias in contrastive learning for unsupervised sentence embedding.
Novel splits of benchmark datasets probe models based on semantic and surface structure similarity.
OssCSE counters surface structure bias by balancing the learning target with data augmentation.
OssCSE preserves word semantics using recall loss to prevent catastrophic forgetting.
OssCSE achieves state-of-the-art performance on semantic textual similarity tasks.
A comprehensive methodology studies the soundness of the Social-IQ benchmark.
Social-IQ contains substantial biases that can be exploited by moderately strong language models to achieve high performance without context or question.
DeSIQ is a challenging dataset constructed by applying simple perturbations to Social-IQ.
DeSIQ reduces the biases in the Social-IQ dataset.
Model size, model style, learning settings, commonsense knowledge, and multi-modality affect performance on the DeSIQ benchmark.
R2H evaluates multi-modal navigation helpers that respond to help requests.
The Respond to Dialog History (RDH) task evaluates the ability to generate informative responses from dialog history.
The Respond during Interaction (RdI) task evaluates response effectiveness and efficiency during cooperation with a task performer.
SeeRee is a task-oriented multi-modal response generation model that can see and respond.
A zero-shot approach constructs navigation-helper agents using a multi-modal large language model.
TRIGO evaluates generative language models' reasoning ability on trigonometric formulas and their capability to manipulate, group, and factor number terms.
TRIGO poses a new challenge for advanced generative language models including GPT-4.
RoBoCoP contains a comprehensive database of Romance cognates and borrowings for Romanian, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, and French.
RoBoCoP contains a benchmark for automatic detection of Romance cognates for any pair among Romanian, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, and French.
Automatic identification of Romance cognates is possible for difficult task formulations.
FlowSUM uses normalizing flows for flexible latent posterior modeling in variational summarization.
FlowSUM uses a controlled alternate aggressive training (CAAT) strategy with an improved gate mechanism.
FlowSUM enhances the quality of generated summaries.
FlowSUM enables knowledge distillation for summarization with minimal impact on inference time.
The training strategy, gate initialization, and the type and number of normalizing flows affect summary quality in variational summarization.
GROOViST accounts for cross-modal dependencies in visual grounding evaluation for visual storytelling.
GROOViST accounts for temporal misalignments (the order mismatch between entities in the story and the image sequence) in visual grounding evaluation for visual storytelling.
GROOViST accounts for human intuitions on visual grounding in visual storytelling.
GROOViST has a modular design that allows the contribution of each component to be assessed and interpreted individually.
Longer pretraining and larger models lead to higher social bias in Large Language Models.
Quantization shows a regularizer effect on social bias in Large Language Models with an optimal trade-off at reduced pretraining time.
ViperGPT's performance gains over BLIP-2 are attributable to its task-specific module selection and are lost when using a task-agnostic module selection.
ViperGPT retains much of its performance when its module selection is altered by removing modules or retaining only BLIP-2.
Modular approaches for visual question decomposition benefit from representing subtasks with natural language instead of code on some benchmarks.
CTC is extended to include the copy operation in the edit space for efficient management of textual overlap in text editing.
Non-autoregressive text editing with copy-aware latent alignments outperforms Seq2Edit models and achieves results comparable to Seq2Seq with significant speedup.
Non-autoregressive text editing with copy-aware latent alignments generalizes to German and Russian.
Non-autoregressive text editing with copy-aware latent alignments is robust under various scenarios.
Non-autoregressive text editing with copy-aware latent alignments generates fluent and flexible outputs.
Contrastive phrasal highlighting generates explanations for semantic divergence model predictions using phrase alignment guided erasure.
Contrastive phrasal highlights match human rationales of cross-lingual semantic differences better than popular post-hoc saliency techniques.
Contrastive phrasal highlights help people detect fine-grained meaning differences in human translations and critical machine translation errors.
Current summarization models often produce unfaithful outputs for medical input text.
FaMeSumm uses contrastive learning on designed sets of faithful and unfaithful summaries for medical summarization.
FaMeSumm incorporates medical terms and their contexts to encourage faithful generation of medical terms.
FaMeSumm achieves state-of-the-art results on faithfulness and general quality metrics for medical summarization.
FaMeSumm generates more faithful medical summaries as evaluated by doctors.
Exchange-of-Thought enables cross-model communication during problem-solving.
Exchange-of-Thought integrates the Memory, Report, Relay, and Debate communication paradigms.
Exchange-of-Thought includes a confidence evaluation mechanism to counterbalance the risks of incorrect reasoning chains.
Exchange-of-Thought achieves superior performance to established baselines on diverse complex reasoning tasks.
Exchange-of-Thought achieves superior results in a cost-effective manner on diverse complex reasoning tasks.
In auto-regressive language models, early MLP sublayers enrich the last-subject representation to encode subject-related attributes during factual attribute extraction.
In auto-regressive language models, relation information propagates to the prediction during factual attribute extraction.
In auto-regressive language models, the prediction representation queries the enriched subject to extract the attribute during factual attribute extraction.
In auto-regressive language models, attention heads that encode subject-attribute mappings in their parameters typically perform factual attribute extraction.
A scalable recipe reproduces RETRO for autoregressive language models with a large retrieval corpus.
RETRO reduces repetition in text generation compared to GPT.
RETRO achieves higher factual accuracy in text generation compared to GPT.
RETRO reduces toxicity in text generation compared to GPT when using a nontoxic retrieval database.
RETRO outperforms GPT on knowledge-intensive tasks in the LM Evaluation Harness benchmark.
RETRO performs comparably to GPT on non-knowledge-intensive tasks in the LM Evaluation Harness benchmark.
RETRO++ improves open-domain question answering results over RETRO.
RETRO++ outperforms retrieval-augmented GPT across different model sizes.
Location-aware visual question generation (LocaVQG) generates engaging questions from location-aware information represented by surrounding images and a GPS coordinate.
A dataset generation pipeline for location-aware visual question generation uses GPT-4 to produce diverse and sophisticated questions.
A lightweight model for location-aware visual question generation fits on edge devices such as mobile phones.

A lightweight model for location-aware visual question generation outperforms baselines in human evaluation of engagement, grounding, and coherence, and in automatic evaluation metrics including BERTScore and ROUGE-2.
Model-agnostic bias mitigation is possible in neural text classification.
Inducing Wasserstein independence between target label representations and sensitive attribute representations enables bias mitigation in neural text classification.
Bias mitigation in neural text classification is possible without requiring annotations of sensitive attributes during testing and training.
Bias mitigation in neural text classification achieves a comparable or better fairness-accuracy trade-off compared to existing methods.
JEEBench evaluates problem solving abilities of large language models using pre-engineering mathematics, physics, and chemistry problems from the IIT JEE-Advanced exam.
Long-horizon reasoning on top of deep in-domain knowledge is essential for solving problems in JEEBench.

GPT-4 exhibits failure modes including errors in algebraic manipulation, difficulty in grounding abstract concepts into mathematical equations, and failure to retrieve relevant domain-specific concepts.
GPT-4 cannot assess risk from negative marking for incorrect answers through prompting alone.
A post-hoc confidence-thresholding method over self-consistency improves response selection for handling negative marking.
GraphNarrative is a dataset for natural language generation from large-scale, open-domain knowledge graphs.
A method trims generated sentences to remove portions not present in the input knowledge graph by utilizing the sentence's dependency parse tree.
Structured concept predictor (SCP) predicts concepts and their structures and integrates them into image captioning.
Weighted graph convolutional networks (W-GCN) depict concept relations driven by word dependencies and learn differentiated contributions from concepts for decoding.
CoheSentia assesses human-perceived coherence of automatically generated texts using a global perspective that assigns a single coherence score to a text.
CoheSentia assesses human-perceived coherence of automatically generated texts using an incremental perspective that assigns a coherence score per sentence and identifies reasons for incoherence.
The incremental annotation perspective for coherence assessment has higher inter-annotator agreement than the holistic perspective.
Standard language models fine-tuned for coherence detection exhibit varied performance across different factors contributing to (in)coherence.
A novel method generates synthetic scans resembling real historical documents.
PHD reconstructs masked image patches of historical documents.
PHD demonstrates language understanding capabilities.
PHD solves historical question answering tasks.
WiCE is a fine-grained textual entailment dataset built on natural claim and evidence pairs from Wikipedia.
WiCE provides entailment judgments for sub-sentence units of claims.
WiCE provides minimal subsets of evidence sentences that support each subclaim.
An automatic claim decomposition strategy decomposes claims into sub-sentence units using GPT-3.5.
An automatic claim decomposition strategy improves entailment models' performance on multiple datasets at test time.
Real claims in the WiCE dataset involve challenging verification and retrieval problems that existing models fail to address.
DASC achieves strong controllability in multi-attribute dialogue generation via a weighted decoding paradigm.
DASC improves generation quality by grounding dialogue generation in an attribute semantics space.
DASC reduces model sizes for multi-attribute dialogue generation by interpolating multiple attribute embeddings.
DASC achieves high control accuracy for simultaneous control of 3 dialogue attributes in out-of-distribution robustness tests.
DASC produces interesting and reasonably sensible responses for simultaneous control of 3 dialogue attributes in out-of-distribution robustness tests.
DeMem uses a reinforcement learning feedback loop via proximal policy optimization to unlearn pre-training data.
DeMem fine-tunes language models with a negative similarity score as a reward signal to learn a paraphrasing policy to unlearn pre-training data.
DeMem generalizes better and achieves a better balance between privacy and language model performance than strong baselines and state-of-the-art methods.
A dataset of English sentences with human-annotated visualness scores is curated.

A fine-tuning strategy adapts vision-language models by modifying the contrastive learning objective to map non-visual text to a common NULL image and visual text to corresponding images in the document.
Expression tree decoding strategy accounts for parallel and dependent relations among math expressions in mathematical equation generation.

Layer-wise parallel decoding strategy generates expression trees for mathematical equations by decoding independent math expressions in parallel at each layer and generating dependent parent node math expressions layer by layer.
A bipartite matching algorithm aligns predicted math expressions with annotated math expressions for each layer in expression tree decoding.
Expression tree decoding strategy outperforms baseline methods for mathematical equations with complex structures.
VLIS combines the visual conditioning capability of vision-language models with the language understanding of unimodal text-only language models without further training.
VLIS extracts pointwise mutual information of each image and text from a visual-language model and uses it as an importance sampling weight to adjust token likelihood from a text-only model.
VLIS improves vision-language models on commonsense understanding tasks and complex text generation tasks.
A dataset of historical United States Supreme Court opinions annotated by domain experts for jurisprudential philosophy classification enables systematic language model evaluation.
Generative language models perform poorly on classifying legal reasoning by jurisprudential philosophy when using prompts that match human annotator instructions.

Fine-tuning language models on a dataset of historical United States Supreme Court opinions annotated for jurisprudential philosophy classification yields stronger performance for that task than prompting.
LEGAL-BERT, an in-domain language model, achieves the best performance for jurisprudential philosophy classification when fine-tuned on a dataset of historical United States Supreme Court opinions.

Applying a language model fine-tuned for jurisprudential philosophy classification on historical United States Supreme Court opinions reveals historical trends in jurisprudence that align with prominent qualitative historical accounts and identifies areas for refinement in those accounts.
Automatic Prompt Optimization uses natural language gradients to automatically improve prompts for large language models.
Automatic Prompt Optimization forms natural language gradients from minibatches of data to criticize the current prompt.
Automatic Prompt Optimization propagates natural language gradients into the prompt by editing the prompt in the opposite semantic direction of the gradient.
Automatic Prompt Optimization uses a beam search and bandit selection procedure to guide gradient descent steps and improve algorithmic efficiency.
Automatic Prompt Optimization outperforms prior prompt editing techniques.
The Induction-Augmented Generation (IAG) framework utilizes inductive knowledge along with retrieved documents for implicit reasoning.
A novel prompting method based on inductive reasoning patterns derives inductive knowledge using large language models.
IAG-GPT utilizes GPT-3 generated knowledge for answer prediction.
IAG-Student incorporates a student inductor model to remove dependencies on GPT service at inference time.
The inductor model in IAG-Student is trained via knowledge distillation and optimized by back-propagating generator feedback using differentiable beam scores.
IAG sets a new state-of-the-art on the CSQA2.0 and StrategyQA benchmarks.
ViPE converts arbitrary text into a visualisable description.
ViPE is trained on synthetic visual descriptions generated by GPT-3.5 without human annotations or images.
ViPE is more robust than GPT-3.5 in synthesising visual elaborations.
ViPE exhibits an understanding of figurative expressions comparable to human experts.
A corpus of goal-step event pairs from WikiHow with manual annotations of step essentiality for goal events.
Humans have a consistent understanding of step essentiality for goal events.
Existing state-of-the-art natural language understanding models perform poorly at detecting step essentiality for goal events compared to humans.
Self-Ensemble of N-best Generation Hypotheses by Lexically Constrained Decoding merges high-quality fragments from N-best hypotheses.
Self-Ensemble of N-best Generation Hypotheses by Lexically Constrained Decoding conducts token-level quality estimation on N-best hypotheses.
Self-Ensemble of N-best Generation Hypotheses by Lexically Constrained Decoding applies tokens as lexical constraints in decoding to enforce presence or absence of tokens.
Self-Ensemble of N-best Generation Hypotheses by Lexically Constrained Decoding outperforms strong N-best reranking methods on paraphrase generation, summarisation, and constrained text generation.
Byte Pair Encoding reduces sequence length and increases vocabulary size in symbolic music tokenization.
Byte Pair Encoding enables faster inference in symbolic music generation and classification tasks.
Text-Transport estimates causal effects from natural language under any text distribution.
Text-Transport transports causal effects between domains without requiring strong assumptions in the target domain.
Text-Transport provides statistical guarantees on the uncertainty of causal effect estimation.
Causal effects of linguistic attributes shift significantly between text domains in hate speech on social media.
Transport of causal effects is necessary for conducting causal inference on natural language in hate speech contexts.
Evidence-based label smoothing prevents penalizing dense retrieval models for assigning high relevance to false negatives.
Reciprocal nearest neighbors estimates relevance by considering relationships between documents and queries beyond simple geometric distance.
Reciprocal nearest neighbors reranks candidates in post-processing.
Reciprocal nearest neighbors improves the ranking effectiveness of dense retrieval models when used for label smoothing and for reranking.
Reciprocal nearest neighbors enhances the ranking context of dense retrieval by considering relationships between documents and queries beyond simple geometric distance.
Action-object knowledge is extracted from task instructions using large language models.
Joint inference on object phrases and symbolic knowledge is performed using per-object aggregation masking.
Localization and tracking performance is improved on the TREK-150-OPE-Det task.
Tracking performance is improved on the TREK-150-OPE task.
Object detection performance is improved on the Ego4D SCOD task.
MTurk workers prefer reliable, reasonable payments over uncertain, very high payments.
MTurk workers frequently lie on demographic questions.
MTurk workers express frustration when work is rejected without explanation.
MTurk workers view quality control methods such as minimum response time requirements and Master’s qualifications as biased and largely ineffective.
A unified Conversational Recommendation System jointly learns recommendation and dialogue tasks via Contextualized Knowledge Distillation.
hard gate in Contextualized Knowledge Distillation selectively gates between two task-specific teachers in a context-specific manner.
soft gate in Contextualized Knowledge Distillation integrates knowledge from both task-specific teachers in a context-specific manner.
A unified Conversational Recommendation System improves recommendation performance and enhances response fluency.
A unified Conversational Recommendation System achieves comparable recommendation diversity.
WikiWebQuestions is a high-quality question answering benchmark for Wikidata with real-world data and SPARQL annotation.
A few-shot sequence-to-sequence semantic parser for Wikidata converts natural language questions to SPARQL queries.
SPARQL for Wikidata semantic parsing is modified to use unique domain and property names instead of IDs.
LLaMA is fine-tuned by adding few-shot training data to the Alpaca fine-tuning data.
A few-shot sequence-to-sequence semantic parser for Wikidata outperforms the state-of-the-art on the QALD-7 Wikidata dataset.
ART deduces the correct future event from a video and rule-based premises with an explanation of the reasoning process.
Video-ART contains videos annotated with premises, future event candidates, reasoning process explanations, and auxiliary commonsense knowledge.
ARTNet uses commonsense knowledge to identify the target video character and perceive its visual clues related to the future event.
ARTNet applies rule-based premises to reason from visual clues to future events using a non-parametric rule reasoning network and a reasoning-path review module.
Transformer-based machine translation models have a tipping point at which they correctly default to idiomatic translations.
A dataset of natural sentences containing idiomatic expressions in French, Finnish, and Japanese provides multilingual examples for machine translation.
Strategic upweighting of training loss on potentially idiomatic sentences increases the weight given to the training loss of potentially idiomatic sentences.
Retrieval-augmented models incorporate external examples to translate idiomatic expressions.

The combination of strategic upweighting of training loss and retrieval augmentation improves machine translation accuracy on idiomatic expressions and shows potential benefits for non-idiomatic expressions.
AIG-ASAP is a dataset of AI-generated student essays that have been perturbed to evade detection while maintaining quality.
Existing AI-generated content detectors are easily circumvented by straightforward automatic adversarial attacks on student essays.
Word substitution and sentence substitution perturbation methods effectively evade AI-generated content detection while maintaining the quality of student essays.
FinEntity is an entity-level sentiment classification dataset for financial news that annotates financial entity spans and their sentiment (positive, neutral, negative).
Penalty decoding suppresses the self-reinforcement effect in open-ended text generation.
Penalty decoding incorporates a forgetting mechanism that disregards distant tokens to reduce the burden of penalty selection.
Penalty decoding incorporates a length penalty to address overly short sentences caused by excessive penalties.
Penalty decoding helps resolve issues with sampling methods deviating from factual information.
Penalty decoding generates sentences that resemble human output.
DueT inserts adapters into image and text encoders that are frozen and initialized from uni-modal pre-trained models.
DueT enables efficient learning with a reduced number of trainable parameters by training only adapters.
DueT adapters use a gating mechanism to enable effective transfer and connection of knowledge from pre-trained uni-modal encoders and prevent catastrophic forgetting.

DueT outperforms simple fine-tuning, a method that fixes the image encoder and trains only the text encoder, and LoRA-based adapter methods in accuracy and parameter efficiency for zero-shot image and text retrieval in English and Japanese.
Tree structures constructed from discourse rhetorical structure represent documents for cross-document event coreference resolution.
Shortest dependency paths extracted from tree structures represent interactions between event mention pairs for cross-document event coreference resolution.
A multi-layer perceptron captures similarities of event mention pairs using shortest dependency paths to resolve coreferent events.
RegNLP is a multidisciplinary research space that connects scientific knowledge to regulatory processes based on systematic methodologies.
Token elimination removes tokens from retrieved passages that are not essential for answer generation.
Token elimination reduces run-time with minimal performance degradation and can sometimes improve performance.
A synthetic environment enables controlled and systematic testing of situational understanding in chat-oriented models by assessing the ability of models to track and enumerate environment states.
A synthetic environment enables close analysis of model performance dynamics to understand underlying causes for performance patterns.
ChatGPT is unable to retain correct environment states across time.
ChatGPT has non-persistent in-context memory despite accessing the full dialogue history.
ChatGPT is susceptible to hallucinated updates that artificially inflate accuracies.
BioPlanner automatically evaluates language models on experimental protocol planning in biology.
BioProt is a dataset of biology protocols with corresponding pseudocode representations.

BioPlanner uses a language model to convert natural language protocols to pseudocode and then evaluates language models on reconstructing pseudocode from high-level descriptions and admissible functions.
Retrieved pseudocode representations enable the generation of accurate novel protocols.
A generated protocol is successfully executed in a biological laboratory.
