An extended ElecDeb60To16 dataset includes the Trump-Biden presidential debate with token-level annotations of claims, premises, support relations, attack relations, and six fallacy categories: Ad Hominem, Appeal to Authority, Appeal to Emotion, False Cause, Slippery Slope, and Slogans.
Transformer-based neural network architectures combine text, argumentative features, and engineered features to detect and classify fallacious arguments.
Complementing transformer-generated text representations with non-text features improves fallacious argument detection and classification.
A multilingual dataset of factual associations paired with counterfactuals supports the evaluation of foundation models' encyclopedic knowledge recall in multiple languages.
Meta's LLaMA achieves the highest performance in recalling encyclopedic knowledge among evaluated foundation models in both multilingual and English-only evaluations.
LLaMA has significant limitations in recalling facts in non-English languages.
LLaMA has difficulties in recalling facts involving the location or gender of the fact subject.
Machine Translation, Web Search, and Large Language Models struggle with increasing multilingual coverage and precision of entity names and descriptions in Wikidata.
M-NTA is an unsupervised approach that combines Machine Translation, Web Search, and Large Language Models to generate high-quality textual information for non-English languages in knowledge graphs.

WikiKGE-10 is a human-curated benchmark for evaluating approaches that increase multilingual coverage and precision of textual information in knowledge graphs in 10 languages across 7 language families.
UltraChat is a large-scale, diverse, and high-quality dataset of multi-turn instructional conversations generated without human queries.
UltraChat has superior scale, average length, diversity, and coherence.
UltraLM is a conversational language model fine-tuned on UltraChat.
UltraLM outperforms WizardLM and Vicuna in conversational evaluations.
Struct-XLM uses a reinforcement learning policy network to discover structural information and integrate it via a structural multi-head attention module.
Struct-XLM uses a translation ranking task to provide delayed rewards based on structural representation for optimizing the policy network and improving cross-lingual representation alignment.
Struct-XLM enhances cross-lingual transfer on the XTREME benchmark.
Wug test datasets for English, German, Tamil, and Turkish are novel and uncontaminated.
ChatGPT underperforms purpose-built systems in morphological capabilities in English, German, Tamil, and Turkish, with the most severe underperformance in English.
Claims of human-like language skills for ChatGPT are premature and misleading based on morphological analysis.
Crossmodal bottleneck models use text descriptions accompanying images to guide concept induction.
Crossmodal bottleneck models treat concepts as discrete latent variables.
Crossmodal bottleneck models induce concepts that are predictive of the label.
Crossmodal bottleneck models induce concepts that can be predicted reliably from both image and text.
Crossmodal learning encourages the induction of interpretable concepts in bottleneck models.
Crossmodal learning facilitates disentanglement in bottleneck models.
BanglaAbuseMeme is a dataset for Bengali abusive meme classification.
Multimodal models that use both textual and visual information outperform unimodal models for Bengali abusive meme classification.
Misclassifications by the best text-based, image-based, and multimodal models for Bengali abusive meme classification are qualitatively analyzed.
Injecting random knowledge tuples into domain-adaptive large language models yields performance comparable to or better than injecting aligned knowledge tuples.
Pruning and purifying the external knowledge base is a technique for knowledge injection into domain-adaptive large language models.
Pruning and purifying the external knowledge base ensures that injecting aligned knowledge tuples outperforms injecting random knowledge tuples in domain-adaptive large language models.
ESGDoc is a dataset of 1,093 ESG annual reports from 563 companies spanning from 2001 to 2022.
The construction-modelling-modification process eliminates the need for pairwise modelling of section headings, making document segmentation practically feasible.
The construction-modelling-modification process allows each section heading to leverage both local and long-distance context by incorporating structured information.
The construction-modelling-modification process outperforms the state-of-the-art baseline while reducing running time.
The construction-modelling-modification process handles documents of any length, demonstrating scalability.
Multi-Aspect Feedback is an iterative refinement framework that integrates multiple feedback modules, including frozen language models and external tools, each focusing on a specific error category.
Multi-Aspect Feedback addresses multiple error types in language model-generated reasoning chains.
VerMouth is the first large-scale dataset of claim-response pairs linked to debunking articles that incorporates social media platform communication style and basic emotions.
The author-reviewer pipeline combines large language models and human annotators to efficiently collect high-quality claim-response pairs.
Models trained on VerMouth exhibit improved output quality and generalization capabilities.
The divide-and-conquer dialog state tracking paradigm segments multi-domain dialogs into single-domain dialogs.
The divide-and-conquer dialog state tracking paradigm improves generalization of dialog state tracking models for dialogs involving unseen domain combinations.
The multi-domain dialog synthesis framework merges single-domain dialogs into multi-domain dialogs and modifies them to simulate domain relations.
The multi-domain dialog synthesis framework produces multi-domain dialogs that enable dialog state tracking models to capture value transfer between domains.

Using a stronger machine translation system and mitigating the mismatch between training on original text and inference on machine translated text improves the translate-test approach for cross-lingual classification.
Various sources of cross-lingual transfer gap cause the optimal cross-lingual classification approach to be task-dependent.
The precipitous loss landscape of vanilla prompt tuning, where slight input changes cause large loss fluctuations, is an essential factor causing training instability.
Random-noise-based and adversarial-based perturbation-based regularizers smooth the loss landscape in prompt tuning and operate flexibly in both text space and embedding space.
Prompt Tuning with Perturbation-based regularizer (PTP) alleviates training instability in prompt tuning.
Prompt Tuning with Perturbation-based regularizer (PTP) boosts the performance of prompt tuning.
Zero-shot neural machine translation performance varies significantly across language directions.
Target-side translation quality is the most influential factor in variations of zero-shot neural machine translation performance.
Vocabulary overlap consistently impacts zero-shot neural machine translation capabilities.
Linguistic properties, such as language family and writing system, affect zero-shot neural machine translation performance, especially in smaller models.
The off-target issue in zero-shot neural machine translation is a symptom of inadequate performance, indicating that zero-shot translation challenges extend beyond the off-target problem.
CopVQA operates a pool of pathways that capture diverse causal reasoning flows through interpreting and answering stages.
CopVQA decomposes the responsibility of each stage (interpreting and answering) into distinct experts and a cognition-enabled component (CC).
The two cognition-enabled components (CCs) in CopVQA strategically execute one expert for each stage at a time.
CopVQA prioritizes answer predictions governed by pathways involving both cognition-enabled components (CCs) while disregarding answers produced by either CC.
CopVQA improves Visual Question Answering performance and generalization across baselines and domains on real-life and medical data.
FedPepTAO uses an efficient partial prompt tuning approach that improves performance and efficiency.
FedPepTAO uses a novel adaptive optimization method that addresses client drift problems on both device and server sides.
FedPepTAO achieves higher accuracy and reduced training time compared to baseline approaches.
ContraSciView is a dataset of review-pair contradictions from ICLR and NeurIPS conferences.
A baseline model detects contradictory statements in peer review pairs.
Automatically identifying contradictions among peer reviewers on a given article is a research task.
The Event Dependency Relation dataset (EDeR) provides human-annotated event dependency relations and integrates with existing OntoNotes annotations.
Event dependency relation recognition benefits semantic role labelling and co-reference resolution.
A sparse semantic representation can be as powerful as or better than dense representations in the vision and language domain.
STAIR maps images and texts to a sparse token space using (sub-)words from the vocabulary as tokens.
STAIR is interpretable.
STAIR is easy to integrate with existing information retrieval systems.
STAIR outperforms CLIP on image-text retrieval and image classification tasks.
A parallel dataset contains unsupportive statements and their reframings to growth mindset supportive language by teachers trained in growth mindset supportive language, with an annotation guide.
A growth mindset supportive language prompt framework revises unsupportive statements to growth mindset supportive language.
An evaluation framework grounded in psychological theory assesses growth mindset supportive language using input from students and teachers.

Reframings of unsupportive statements to growth mindset supportive language by teachers trained in growth mindset supportive language and by large language models are perceived by teachers and students as more effective in fostering a growth mindset and promoting challenge-seeking behavior.

Reframings of unsupportive statements to growth mindset supportive language by large language models are perceived as more effective than those by teachers trained in growth mindset supportive language in fostering a growth mindset and promoting challenge-seeking behavior.
Large-scale human evaluations are beneficial for applying large language models in educational domains.
CoSyn introduces novel ways to encode user context and conversational context for detecting implicit hate speech.

CoSyn employs a context interaction mechanism that captures the interplay between user context and conversational context and independently assesses the amounts of information to retrieve from noisy contexts for detecting implicit hate speech.
CoSyn uses hyperbolic space for encoding and interacting user context and conversational context to account for the scale-free dynamics of social media for detecting implicit hate speech.
CoSyn outperforms existing methods in detecting implicit hate speech.
StemCorrupt brings about fundamental changes in the underlying data distribution, revealing inherent compositional concatenative structure.
Selecting a subset of datapoints with high diversity and high predictive uncertainty improves data-efficiency for morphological inflection.
Languages with non-concatenative morphology, such as those with morphonological alternations, derive less benefit from synthetic examples with high predictive uncertainty in morphological inflection.
Phonotactic violations induced by StemCorrupt cause reduced benefit of synthetic examples with high predictive uncertainty in non-concatenative languages.
HackAPrompt is a dataset of adversarial prompts for evaluating prompt hacking in large language models.
An ontology categorizes the types of adversarial prompts in prompt hacking.
Current large language models are vulnerable to prompt hacking.
SpEL uses two refined fine-tuning steps for structured prediction in entity linking.
SpEL uses a context sensitive prediction aggregation strategy for entity linking.
SpEL reduces the size of the model's output vocabulary for entity linking.
SpEL addresses the training versus inference tokenization mismatch in entity linking systems.
SpEL outperforms the state-of-the-art on the AIDA benchmark for entity linking to Wikipedia.
SpEL is compute efficient in terms of model parameters and inference speed.
Large Language Models exhibit a unique modular structure within their layers that emerges as model size increases.
Large Language Models show subtle changes in representation and intermediate predictions across layers.
CRaSh derives improved emulators from Large Language Models without training.
Optimal solutions from fine-tuning with and without the full Large Language Model are linearly connected and lie in the same basin of the loss landscape.
RAPL refines relation prototypes for few-shot document-level relation extraction using relation descriptions.
RAPL generates task-specific NOTA prototypes for few-shot document-level relation extraction using realistic NOTA instances.
RAPL outperforms state-of-the-art methods for few-shot document-level relation extraction.
LLM-based agents exhibit emergent collaborative behaviors in multi-agent cooperative text games.
LLM-based agents exhibit high-order Theory of Mind capabilities in multi-agent cooperative text games.
LLM-based agents have limitations in planning optimization due to systematic failures in managing long-horizon contexts and hallucination about the task state in multi-agent cooperative text games.
Explicit belief state representations enhance task performance and the accuracy of Theory of Mind inferences for LLM-based agents in multi-agent cooperative text games.
DADA adapts Standard American English-trained language models to multiple English dialects by composing adapters that handle specific linguistic features.
DADA allows for targeted adaptation to specific English dialect variants and simultaneous adaptation to various English dialects.
GeNTE is a natural bilingual test set for gender-neutral translation from English to Italian created based on a survey of the perception and use of neutral language.
Existing reference-based evaluation approaches for gender-neutral translation have limitations.
A reference-free evaluation method is more suitable for assessing gender-neutral translation than reference-based evaluation methods.
Dynamic Module Expansion and Adaptation dynamically determines the architecture for acquiring new knowledge based on task correlation.
Dynamic Module Expansion and Adaptation selects the most similar previous tasks to facilitate adaptation to new tasks.
Dynamic gradient scaling balances the learning of the current task and replayed tasks.
Dynamic Module Expansion and Adaptation consistently outperforms existing methods in different lifelong sequence generation settings.
Fine-tuning encoder-based language models on a reduced training dataset selected by influence scores does not degrade performance in many language classification tasks.
VivesDebate-Speech is a corpus of spoken argumentation for leveraging audio features in argument mining.
Integrating audio features into the argument mining pipeline improves performance.
Numerical scaling offsets are the bottleneck of LLM quantisation.
Block quantisations for LLMs share scaling factors across packed numbers.
Nearly-lossless 6-bit LLM quantisation achieves higher arithmetic density and memory density than float32 and prior art 8-bit quantisation without data calibration or re-training.
LLMs have a lower quantisation granularity inherent in their statistical properties.
Optimal fine-tuning strategies and lower quantisation granularity enable nearly-lossless 4-bit LLM quantisation on downstream tasks.
Manual Counterfactually Augmented Data is more effective than automatically generated Counterfactually Augmented Data for improving model robustness in harmful language detection.

Counterfactually Augmented Data generated by ChatGPT is more effective than Counterfactually Augmented Data generated by Polyjuice or Flan-T5 for improving model robustness in harmful language detection.
The changes introduced by automatically generated Counterfactually Augmented Data are often insufficient to flip the original label in harmful language detection.

BullyExplain is a benchmark dataset for explainable cyberbullying detection in code-mixed language with each post annotated for bully, sentiment, target, and rationale labels, where rationales indicate the specific phrases responsible for identifying the post as a bully.
GenEx is a unified generative framework that reimagines the multitask problem of explainable cyberbullying detection as a text-to-text generation task.
Multi-level Contrastive Learning captures characters' global information in a fine-grained manner for script-based character understanding.
Some linguistic properties in pre-trained multilingual language models can be accessed through prompting, whereas others are harder to capture.
PLJP uses domain-specific models to provide candidate labels and find proper precedents efficiently.
PLJP uses large language models to make the final prediction with in-context precedents comprehension.
HomCoE uses active testing for evaluating conversational information retrieval systems.
HomCoE strategically selects a subset of data for human annotation.
HomCoE calibrates evaluation results to remove evaluation biases.
HomCoE achieves high consistency with human evaluation results while using low human labor.
EpiK-Eval is a question-answering benchmark that evaluates large language models' proficiency in formulating a coherent and consistent knowledge representation from segmented narratives.
Large language models exhibit significant weaknesses in consolidating knowledge from segmented narratives into a coherent and consistent representation.
Weaknesses in large language models' knowledge consolidation from segmented narratives stem from the intrinsic nature of prevailing training objectives.
Oolong systematically transforms the language of the GLUE benchmark by altering one axis of crosslingual variation at a time to measure performance drops in pretrained language models.

In cross-lingual transfer, pretrained language models can largely recover from syntactic-style shifts but cannot recover from vocabulary misalignment and embedding matrix re-initialization even with continued pretraining.
Good-quality tokenizers in the transfer language do not facilitate vocabulary alignment in cross-lingual transfer.
Reinforcement Learning and Teacher Forcing are compatible in Transformers.
Policy Gradient networks trained on ROUGE, BLEU, and Semantic Similarity generate query-focused summaries.
A Passage Embedding scheme based on Cluster Hypothesis enables a better semantic similarity reward for Reinforcement Learning training in query-focused summarization.
Query-focused summarization with Reinforcement Learning achieves comparable performance to supervised baselines in a zero-shot setting.
A gold-standard test dataset evaluates Query-focused Summarization and Long-form Question Answering.
Public test data is encrypted with a public key and licensed to disallow derivative distribution.
Training exclusion controls are demanded from closed API holders, and test data is protected by refusing evaluation without them.
Data that appears with its solution on the internet is avoided, and the web-page context of internet-derived data is released along with the data.
Construction procedures for metaphor identification datasets introduce unwanted biases for positive and negative classes.
Metaphor identification datasets built by construction procedures can be gamed by ignoring the potential metaphorical expression or its context.
Metaphor identification datasets sampled from natural corpora do not have the bias introduced by construction procedures.
Metaphor identification datasets sampled from natural corpora are more challenging and reliable than metaphor identification datasets built by construction procedures.
PER-KBQA demonstrates intermediate step-by-step reasoning processes to the knowledge-base question answering model.
PER-KBQA aligns and executes logical forms over the knowledge base to obtain intermediate reasoning processes.
Explicit intermediate reasoning processes make complex question answering over knowledge bases easier.
ScanDL is a discrete sequence-to-sequence diffusion model for generating synthetic scanpaths on texts.
ScanDL leverages pre-trained word representations and jointly embeds stimulus text and fixation sequences to capture multi-modal interactions.
ScanDL outperforms state-of-the-art scanpath generation methods in within- and across-dataset evaluations.
ScanDL exhibits human-like reading behavior.
Leap-of-Thought (LoT) dynamically routes tokens within transformer layers.
Leap-of-Thought (LoT) allows tokens to remain accessible in subsequent transformer layers while reducing the number of tokens processed per layer.
Leap-of-Thought (LoT) uses dynamic token routers that learn to selectively process tokens essential for the task.
Leap-of-Thought (LoT) improves computational efficiency without significant loss in accuracy.

M3Seg employs a region-based segment encoder that maximizes mutual information between global segment representations and local contextual sentence representations using pre-trained language model sentence representations for unsupervised linear topic segmentation in ASR transcripts.
M3Seg employs an edge-based boundary detection module that minimizes mutual information between different segments for unsupervised linear topic segmentation in ASR transcripts.
M3Seg outperforms state-of-the-art methods for unsupervised linear topic segmentation in ASR transcripts.
Calc-X is a unified dataset collection for arithmetic reasoning that demonstrates the appropriate use of a calculator in reasoning chains.
Calcformers are language models that use a calculator for arithmetic reasoning.
Calcformers achieve higher accuracy in generating correct arithmetic results than vanilla language models.
Perfect performance on cross-domain text-to-SQL benchmarks is unfeasible due to multiple interpretations of the provided samples.
Standard benchmark evaluation underestimates the true performance of cross-domain text-to-SQL models.
The relative performance of cross-domain text-to-SQL models changes when evaluated by manual assessment and equivalent rewriting.
A GPT-4-based text-to-SQL model surpasses the gold standard reference queries in the Spider benchmark in human evaluation.
A method transforms a latent embedding space into a comprehensible conceptual space for understanding embeddings.
An algorithm derives a conceptual space with dynamic on-demand granularity.
An evaluation method uses human raters or LLM-based raters to verify that conceptualized vectors represent the semantics of the original latent vectors.
Evidential closure is the condition that output must be synonymous with claims for which LLMs have evidence.
The absence of evidential closure causes hallucination in LLMs.
Multimodal LLMs require perceptual learning, extensional learning, and intensional learning to satisfy evidential closure.
Unimodal LLMs must produce output that is synonymous with strings in a validated evidence set.
Learn-Babble-Prune produces faithful LLM output by rejecting output that is not synonymous with claims for which LLMs have evidence.
Edit span prediction for local sequence transduction tasks represents an edit span as a position in the source text and corrected tokens.
Edit span prediction for local sequence transduction tasks reduces the length of the target sequence.
Edit span prediction for local sequence transduction tasks reduces computational cost for inference.

Edit span prediction achieves comparable performance to baseline methods in paraphrasing, formality style transfer, grammatical error correction, and text simplification while reducing target sequence length.
Task-specific fine-tuning of edit span prediction models achieves state-of-the-art performance in paraphrasing, formality style transfer, grammatical error correction, and text simplification.
ChatGPT annotates latent concepts in pre-trained language models for fine-grained interpretation analysis.
ChatGPT annotations of latent concepts are more accurate and semantically richer than human annotations.
GPT-based annotations are used in probing frameworks for interpretation analysis of pre-trained language models.
GPT-based annotations are used in neuron interpretation for pre-trained language models.
The TCN dataset provides annotated concepts for interpretation analysis of pre-trained language models.
IfQA is the first counterfactual open-domain question-answering dataset.
IfQA questions are based on counterfactual presuppositions using 'if' clauses.
Answering IfQA questions requires identifying relevant information and reasoning about imagined situations that may contradict parametric knowledge.
IfQA is highly challenging for existing open-domain QA methods.

Out-of-Distribution Generalization in Natural Language Processing: Past, Present, and Future is the first comprehensive review of out-of-distribution generalization in natural language understanding, including recent progress, methods, evaluations, challenges, and future research directions.
Dual importance-based model division divides model parameters into two parts for separate modeling of original and new translation tasks.
Dual importance-based model division adapts the original model to various new translation tasks while retaining performance on original tasks.
Dual importance-based model division outperforms strong baselines in different incremental translation scenarios.
A 4-bit labeling encoding for dependency trees represents any projective dependency tree as a sequence of 4-bit labels, one per word.

In the 4-bit labeling for dependency trees, each word's label bits represent whether the dependency direction is right or left, whether the dependent is outermost, whether left children exist, and whether right children exist.
The 4-bit labeling for dependency trees provides an injective mapping from dependency trees to labels that is encodable and decodable in linear time.
A 7-bit labeling extension for dependency trees represents an extra plane of arcs, covering almost full non-projectivity.
The 7-bit labeling for dependency trees obtains substantial accuracy gains over previous best sequence labeling encodings.
ACL OCL provides metadata, PDF files, citation graphs, structured full texts with sections and figures, and links to Semantic Scholar.
Interest in 'Syntax: Tagging, Chunking and Parsing' is waning in computational linguistics.
Interest in 'Natural Language Generation' is resurging in computational linguistics.
Automatic scaling analysis of political-party manifestos can be efficiently solved in a multilingual setting using state-of-the-art models.
Label aggregation outperforms long-input-Transformer-based models in automatic scaling analysis of political-party manifestos in a multilingual setting.
GDP-Zero performs goal-oriented dialogue policy planning using Open-Loop Monte Carlo Tree Search without model training.
GDP-Zero uses a large language model as a policy prior, value function, user simulator, and system model in Monte Carlo Tree Search via prompting.
GDP-Zero produces responses that are preferred over ChatGPT in the PersuasionForGood task.
GDP-Zero produces responses that are rated more persuasive than ChatGPT in interactive evaluations of the PersuasionForGood task.
All Things Considered predicts news article ideology by comparing multiple articles on the same story and identifying partisan events.
Partisan event selection occurs in news media.
Article alignment and cross-document comparison detect partisan events and article ideology more effectively than competitive baselines.
Partisan event selection is a high-level form of media bias present in mainstream media despite strong norms of objectivity and nonpartisanship.
q2d is an automatic data generation pipeline that generates information-seeking dialogs from questions.
q2d uses a large language model (PaLM) to create conversational versions of question answering datasets for training query generation models.
For query generation on the QReCC dataset, models trained on q2d-generated data achieve performance comparable to models trained on human-generated data.
q2d generates training data for dialog models in new domains without requiring existing dialog data.
q2d-generated dialogs are of high quality and humans struggle to distinguish them from human-written dialogs.
mFACT evaluates the faithfulness of non-English summaries by leveraging translation-based transfer from multiple English faithfulness metrics.
mFACT is best suited to detect hallucinations compared to alternative metrics.
Multilingual large language models tend to hallucinate often in languages different from English.
Weighing the loss of each training example by its faithfulness score is a training procedure to reduce hallucinations in cross-lingual transfer.
Weighing the loss of each training example by its faithfulness score increases performance and faithfulness in cross-lingual transfer.
ZSAQ is a zero-shot sharpness-aware quantization framework for pre-trained language models.
SAM-SGA optimization improves quantization accuracy and model generalization by solving a minimax problem.
The convergence rate of the minimax optimization problem is proven and applicable to other nonconvex-PL minimax optimization frameworks.
ZSAQ brings consistent and significant performance gains on discriminative and generative pre-trained language models across multiple tasks.
The Multi-Levenshtein Transformer simultaneously edits multiple fuzzy matches from memory.
The Multi-Levenshtein Transformer uses multi-way alignment algorithms and imitation learning for training and inference.
Editing multiple examples in machine translation increases the number of target spans copied from the examples in memory.
Transformer language models treat atypically animate entities as animate, but do not adapt as well as humans.
Transformer language models detect subtle clues of atypical animacy in very short contexts.
Transformer language models are sensitive to lexical semantic nuances for animacy despite learning from limited signal.
Speech representations extracted at the end of a streaming input are significantly different from those extracted from a complete utterance.
Future-Aware Streaming Translation (FAST) adapts an offline speech translation model for streaming input.
Future-Aware Inference (FAI) incorporates future context through a trainable masked embedding.
Future-Aware Distillation (FAD) transfers future context from an approximation of full speech to streaming input.
FAST achieves better trade-offs between translation quality and latency than strong baselines.
FAST alleviates the mismatch problem between offline training and online inference.
VIBE captures changing language features in Twitter classification by modeling latent topic evolution.
VIBE uses two Information Bottleneck regularizers to distinguish past and future topics.
VIBE uses distinguished topics as adaptive features via multi-task training for timestamp and class label prediction.
VIBE uses retrieved unlabeled data from online streams created after the training data time.
VIBE outperforms state-of-the-art continued-pretraining methods for Twitter classification using a minimal amount of data.
TAGS integrates an additional sequence tagging task to enable the encoder to distinguish the words of aspect sentiment triplets.
TAGS uses sequence tagging probabilities to guide the decoder in generating aspect sentiment triplets.
TAGS employs a self-decoding process for aspect sentiment triplets to acquire their semantic representations.
TAGS aligns the decoder's hidden states with the semantic representations of aspect sentiment triplets.
QA-NatVer uses question answering to predict natural logic operators for natural logic-based fact verification.
QA-NatVer does not require annotated training data for natural logic-based fact verification.
QA-NatVer outperforms a state-of-the-art pre-trained seq2seq natural logic system and a state-of-the-art prompt-based classifier in few-shot fact verification on the FEVER dataset.
QA-NatVer surpasses all existing approaches on a Danish fact verification dataset without requiring additional annotation.
QA-NatVer produces more plausible proofs with fewer erroneous natural logic operators compared to previous natural logic-based fact verification systems.
Properly instructed large language models deliver competitive or superior results to state-of-the-art supervised methods for relevance ranking on popular information retrieval benchmarks.
NovelEval is a test set based on the latest knowledge for evaluating the ability of language models to rank unknown knowledge.
Permutation distillation distills the ranking capabilities of ChatGPT into small specialized models.
A small specialized model distilled from ChatGPT outperforms larger supervised models for relevance ranking.
TSEE utilizes text sequence features, video appearance features, and optical flow features for text-video event extraction.
The Multi-level Event Contrastive Learning module aligns optical flow features with event triggers and event triggers with event types.
The Dual Querying Text module enhances interaction between text and video modalities.
TSEE outperforms state-of-the-art methods in text-video event extraction.
CHAP uses causal hierarchical attention and the pointer mechanism to integrate structures into the Transformer decoder for AMR parsing.
CHAP outperforms baseline models on multiple benchmarks without using additional data.
CQE detects combinations of values and units in text.
CQE detects the behavior of quantities (e.g., rising or falling).
CQE is the first system to detect concepts associated with identified quantities.
CQE uses dependency parsing and a dictionary of units for quantity extraction.
CQE provides normalization and standardization of detected quantities.
kNN-MT implicitly executes gradient descent on the output projection layer of Neural Machine Translation models.
kNN-MT with adapters achieves translation performance comparable to fine-tuning on in-domain test sets and better performance on out-of-domain test sets.
Fine-tuning outperforms kNN-MT on the recall of in-domain low-frequency words.
Optimizing context representations with additional adapter layers bridges the gap in recall of in-domain low-frequency words between fine-tuning and kNN-MT.
Ling-CL develops linguistic curricula derived from data, existing knowledge of linguistic complexity, and model behavior during training.
Ling-CL identifies sets of linguistic metrics that inform the challenges and reasoning required for specific NLP tasks.
COMBO matches large language model-generated passages with retrieved passages into compatible pairs using discriminators trained with silver compatibility labels.
COMBO uses a Fusion-in-Decoder-based reader model to handle passage pairs for answer generation.
COMBO outperforms competitive baselines on multiple open-domain question answering benchmarks.
COMBO demonstrates greater efficacy in open-domain question answering scenarios with a higher degree of knowledge conflicts.
CLAIR evaluates image captions using the zero-shot language modeling capabilities of large language models.
CLAIR has stronger correlation with human judgments of caption quality than existing evaluation measures.
CLAIR produces noisily interpretable results by using a language model to identify the underlying reasoning for the assigned score.
A unified tree structure represents math word problem solution expressions with permutable elements that are identical across expression variants.
MWP-NAS is a non-autoregressive solver that parses math word problems and deduces solution expressions using the unified tree structure.
A path-based metric evaluates partial accuracy of expressions in unified trees for math word problem solution variants.
Knowledge selection methods are categorized by their timing relative to response generation as: coupled with generation, after generation, or before generation.
GATE selects context-related knowledge for response generation in a generator-agnostic manner from different knowledge structures and under variable knowledge requirements.
Knowledge selection before generation is lightweight and effective for facilitating large language models to generate more informative responses.

Learnable Contextual Embedding dynamically stores and utilizes reasoning information based on current mask prediction results to adaptively capture and refine pertinent information for mask prediction in referring image segmentation.

Progressive Alignment Network leverages the output from the previous layer as a filter for the current output to reduce inconsistencies between predictions from different stages in referring image segmentation.

Progressive Alignment Network iteratively aligns predictions to guide the incorporation of more discriminative information for reasoning in referring image segmentation, enhancing prediction quality and reducing error propagation.
JMCELN achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard referring image segmentation benchmarks, particularly on intricate datasets.
FISH-DIP is a sample-aware dynamic sparse finetuning strategy that selectively focuses on a fraction of parameters informed by feedback from highly regressing examples during finetuning.
FISH-DIP leverages the dynamism of sparsity to mitigate the impact of well-learned samples and prioritize underperforming instances for improved generalization.
FISH-DIP improves performance over full finetuning for sequence labeling in low resource settings.
FISH-DIP performs comparably or better than in-context learning and other parameter-efficient finetuning approaches for sequence labeling in extreme low-resource settings.
Sparse Universal Transformer reduces computation complexity of Universal Transformers using Sparse Mixture of Experts while retaining parameter efficiency and generalization ability.
Sparse Universal Transformer achieves generalization on formal language tasks including logical inference and CFQ.
Sparse Universal Transformer achieves parameter and computation efficiency on natural language benchmarks such as WMT'14.
SCENE synthesizes negative examples zero-shot from positive examples.
SCENE perturbs positive examples with a mask infilling model and determines if the perturbed examples are negative using a self-training heuristic.
SCENE closes a substantial portion of the performance gap on SQuAD 2.0 for unanswerable questions when trained only on answerable examples.
SCENE extends to boolean question answering and recognizing textual entailment.
GazeVQA is a Video Question Answering dataset for multiview eye-gaze task-oriented collaborations.
GazeVQA uses a QA format covering thirteen different reasoning types to capture multiple aspects of task information and user intent.
AssistGaze answers Video Question Answering questions with textual, image, and video answer types.
AssistGaze grounds perceptual input into semantic information while reducing ambiguities.
GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 outperform commercial machine translation systems in document-level translation according to human evaluation.
GPT-4 demonstrates a stronger ability for probing linguistic knowledge than GPT-3.5.
PRESTO is a large-scale public dataset of contextual multilingual conversations between humans and virtual assistants.
PRESTO contains disfluencies, code-switching, and revisions that occur in real-world natural language understanding tasks.
PRESTO is the only large-scale human-generated conversational parsing dataset that provides structured context such as user contacts and lists.
The conversational phenomena in PRESTO are challenging to model, especially in low-resource setups.
Dialogue segmentation points are defined using document-grounded dialogues.
SuperDialseg is a large-scale supervised dataset for dialogue segmentation based on two prevalent document-grounded dialogue corpora and inheriting their dialogue-related annotations.
A benchmark for dialogue segmentation includes multiple models across multiple categories and several proper evaluation metrics.
Supervised learning achieves high performance for dialogue segmentation on in-domain datasets.
Models trained on SuperDialseg generalize well to out-of-domain dialogue segmentation data.
Human verification confirms the quality of the automatically constructed dialogue segmentation dataset.
MIXAG is a data augmentation method for cross-lingual abusive language detection that interpolates pairs of instances based on the angle of their representations.
Data augmentation strategies enhance few-shot cross-lingual abusive language detection.
MIXAG improves cross-lingual abusive language detection in multidomain and multilingual environments across all target languages.
Domain adaptation for cross-lingual abusive language detection reduces false negatives for abusive texts but decreases precision.
Neural graph-based parsing uses supertagging with valency constraints expressed as an integer linear program.
Neural graph-based parsing reduces the graph prediction problem to the maximum matching problem.
Neural graph-based parsing uses an incremental early-stopping training strategy to prevent overfitting.
Supertagging with valency constraints and the reduction of the graph prediction problem to maximum matching improve structural generalization on the COGS dataset.
Structural constraints are important for generalization in semantic parsing.
DocGen generates synthetic documents relevant to the query by expanding the query, highlighting the query, and generating the document using the few-shot capabilities of large language models.
DocGen-RL optimizes the DocGen pipeline using reinforcement learning with the estimated relevance of the document as a reward.
Rewrite-Retrieve-Read replaces the retrieve-then-read pipeline for retrieval-augmented large language models by performing query rewriting.
A small language model rewriter is trained by reinforcement learning using feedback from the large language model reader to align queries with the black-box large language model reader.
Active retrieval augmented generation methods actively decide when and what to retrieve during generation.
FLARE iteratively predicts upcoming sentences to form retrieval queries and regenerates low-confidence sentences using retrieved documents.
FLARE achieves superior or competitive performance on long-form knowledge-intensive generation tasks.
Combining a self-supervised loss with an unsupervised loss eliminates the need for parallel validation data in translation-based style transfer.

An unsupervised loss for translation-based style transfer leverages the original language model loss over the style-transferred output and a semantic similarity loss between the input and style-transferred output.
Translation-based style transfer reduces translationese to the level of a random classifier while preserving content and target-style fluency.
A multimodal graph neural network detects aphasia types using speech and co-speech gesture patterns.
A multimodal graph neural network learns correlations between speech and gesture modalities for each aphasia type to generate gesture-sensitive textual representations for aphasia type detection.
Gesture features outperform acoustic features for aphasia type detection.
TapTap applies table pre-training to tabular prediction tasks.
TapTap generates synthetic tables for privacy protection, low-resource settings, missing value imputation, and imbalanced classification.
TapTap outperforms existing methods in privacy protection, low-resource settings, missing value imputation, and imbalanced classification scenarios.
Models trained on synthetic tables generated by TapTap compete with models trained on real data in some scenarios.
TapTap integrates with LightGBM, Multilayer Perceptron, and Transformer models.
ViSoBERT is the first monolingual pre-trained language model for Vietnamese social media texts.
ViSoBERT is pre-trained on a large-scale corpus of high-quality and diverse Vietnamese social media texts using the XLM-R architecture.
ViSoBERT surpasses prior models on multiple Vietnamese social media tasks while using fewer parameters.
Worst-group performance of compressed BERT language models depends on the compression method, not solely on model size.
Model compression of BERT language models does not always result in worse performance on minority subgroups.
A dataset of natural language to temporal logic pairs is released.
Lifted natural language to temporal logic characterizes common logical structures without domain constraints.
Using Large Language Models in dataset creation enhances corpus richness.
Full natural language to temporal logic transformation is achieved by combining a lifted model with atomic proposition recognition or by further finetuning on specific domains.
Models finetuned on specific domains achieve higher accuracy than sequence-to-sequence baselines with reduced training data requirements.
GNAT couples the Smith-Waterman algorithm with modern text similarity metrics for narrative alignment.
The background of alignment scores fits a Gumbel distribution, enabling the definition of rigorous p-values for alignment significance.
GNAT performs narrative alignment for summary-to-book alignment, translated book alignment, short story alignment, and plagiarism detection.
The problem of diversity of representation in large language model generations is formalized.
New evaluation datasets measure diversity in generated responses along people and culture axes.
New metrics measure diversity in generated responses along people and culture axes.
Large language models understand the notion of diversity of representation.
Large language models can reason and critique their own responses for improving diversity of representation.

Collective-critique and self-voting (CCSV) improves people diversity in large language model generations by leveraging the models' diversity reasoning capabilities without handcrafted examples or prompt tuning.
GPTAraEval evaluates ChatGPT on Modern Standard Arabic and Dialectal Arabic across 44 language understanding and generation tasks and over 60 datasets.
GPTAraEval is the first extensive performance analysis of ChatGPT in Arabic NLP.
Smaller Arabic-finetuned models consistently outperform ChatGPT on Arabic NLP tasks.
ChatGPT and GPT-4 exhibit relative shortcomings in handling Dialectal Arabic compared to Modern Standard Arabic.
GPT-4 serves as a potential alternative for human evaluation in Arabic NLP.

Natural Language Decompositions of Implicit Content uses a large language model to generate sets of propositions inferentially related to observed text and validates their plausibility via human judgments.
Incorporating explicit representations of implicit content is useful for assessing argument similarity, making sense of opinion data, and modeling legislative behavior.
SocialSense uses a large language model to induce a belief-centered graph on an existing social network for response forecasting.
SocialSense uses graph-based propagation to capture social dynamics for response forecasting.
SocialSense handles unseen user scenarios and lurker scenarios in response forecasting.
Task-specific models are not necessary to achieve state-of-the-art performance in coreference resolution.
A pretrained seq2seq transformer is finetuned to map input documents to tagged sequences encoding coreference annotation.
The seq2seq model for coreference resolution outperforms or closely matches state-of-the-art systems on multiple datasets.
A seq2seq model that generates only tagged spans achieves high performance for coreference resolution.
Model size, amount of supervision, and choice of sequence representations are key factors for coreference resolution performance.
3D Relative Position Multi-head Attention (3DRP-MA) focuses on language-described object relations by analyzing relative spatial relationships from multiple directions in the context of object pairs.
A soft-labeling strategy alleviates spatial ambiguity caused by redundant points in 3D visual grounding by using a constant and discriminative distribution.
Pre-trained language models may encode rich knowledge in their pre-trained parameters but fail to fully utilize it for knowledge-intensive tasks.
Knowledge Rumination utilizes related latent knowledge in pre-trained language models without external retrieval.
Knowledge Rumination uses the prompt 'As far as I know' to review and inject latent knowledge for consolidation.
The knowledge stored in pre-trained language models can be better exploited to enhance performance on commonsense reasoning tasks and GLUE benchmarks.
Cognate Transformer uses MSA Transformer for automated phonological reconstruction.
Cognate Transformer predicts reflex words in daughter languages from cognate words of other daughter languages.
Cognate Transformer with masked word prediction pre-training improves performance on automated phonological reconstruction and cognate reflex prediction compared to existing models.
GSLM is the first generative language model based on word-size continuous tokens.
GSLM replaces the lookup table for lexical types with a Lexical Embedding function.
GSLM replaces cross entropy loss with a contrastive loss.
GSLM replaces multinomial sampling with k-NN sampling.
GSLM is more memory efficient than discrete unit GSLMs.
AD-NLP is a unified benchmark for text anomaly detection that covers various anomaly types from syntax to stylistics.
AD-NLP evaluates and analyzes strong shallow baselines and state-of-the-art neural approaches for text anomaly detection.
AD-NLP provides insights into the knowledge neural models learn for text anomaly detection.

TCFLE-8 is a balanced corpus of learner written productions for French as a Foreign Language collected from the Test de Connaissance du Français (TCF) certification exam, with each essay scored by at least two raters according to CEFR levels.
Linguistic properties of learner essays relate to proficiency levels in the TCFLE-8 corpus.
RoBERTa and feature-based models achieve state-of-the-art performance for Automated Essay Scoring in French.
GD-COMET is a geo-diverse version of the COMET commonsense inference model.
GD-COMET generates commonsense inferences for a broad range of cultures.
GD-COMET captures and generates culturally nuanced commonsense knowledge.
Spoiler matching assigns an episode number to a spoiler for a specific TV show.
Spoiler matching is a semantic text matching task.
A dataset of comments and episode summaries supports spoiler matching evaluation.
A dataset of comments and episode summaries can serve as a benchmark for long-range language models.
Nonverbal Message Extraction and Generation is a task for extracting nonverbal messages in written text and generating nonverbal messages for spoken text.
The first nonverbal message extraction dataset for Chinese is created from novels.
Three baselines extract single-span or multi-span nonverbal messages from the surrounding context of a target utterance.
Nonverbal message triples extracted from novels augment clean nonverbal message triples from scripts to generate nonverbal messages that are relevant, fluent, valid, and factually consistent.
Adding predicted nonverbal messages to inputs improves Chinese dialogue machine reading comprehension and emotion classification.
GLEN is a general-purpose event detection dataset covering thousands of event types.
A multi-stage event detection model handles large ontology sizes for event detection.
Label noise is the largest challenge for improving event detection performance on the GLEN dataset.
FACTSCORE breaks long-form text generations from language models into atomic facts.
FACTSCORE computes the percentage of atomic facts supported by a reliable knowledge source.
An automated model estimates FACTSCORE using retrieval and a strong language model.
GPT-4 and ChatGPT have higher factuality than public language models.
Vicuna and Alpaca are among the best public language models in terms of factuality.
LaTeX mathematical expressions contain explicit structural symbols.
Symbols in LaTeX mathematical expressions, particularly letters, are minimal units with context-dependent semantics that represent variables or constants.
Language models are suitable for correction of handwritten mathematical expression recognition.

Recognize and Language Fusion Network (RLFN) integrates recognition and language features and jointly optimizes with a string decoder recognition model to output corrected sequences of handwritten mathematical expressions.
Recognize and Language Fusion Network (RLFN) outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods for correction of handwritten mathematical expression recognition on the CROHME datasets.
An efficient semantic pipeline estimates depression severity from social media writings.
Semantic rankings rank test user sentences against an index of representative training sentences corresponding to depressive symptoms and severity levels.
Sentences from semantic ranking results serve as evidence for predicting symptom severity.
Different aggregation methods map evidence to one of four Beck Depression Inventory (BDI-II) options per symptom.
Composable text operations are performed efficiently in the compact latent space of text.
An ordinary differential equations (ODE) sampler processes arbitrary plug-in operators in the text latent space.
Pretrained language models are connected to the latent space via efficient adaptation to decode sampled vectors into text sequences.
Diverse control operators (sentiment, tense, formality, keywords, etc.) from different domains are supported for text operations.
Composing control operators generates or edits high-quality text and improves generation quality and efficiency over previous methods.

A three-step approach synthesizes query-based summarization triples for instructive dialogue summarization using summary-anchored query generation, query filtering, and query-based summary generation.
InstructDS is a unified model for instructive dialogue summarization trained on multi-purpose instructive triples.
The approach outperforms state-of-the-art models on dialogue summarization and dialogue reading comprehension tasks.
The model exhibits higher generalizability and faithfulness in instructive dialogue summarization.
S2abEL is the first dataset for entity linking in scientific tables.

S2abEL provides entity linking annotations for machine learning results tables from scientific papers with hand-labeled cell types, attributed sources, and entity links from the PaperswithCode taxonomy.
A neural baseline method for entity linking on scientific tables handles many out-of-knowledge-base mentions.
A neural baseline method for entity linking on scientific tables outperforms a state-of-the-art generic table entity linking method.
The best baselines for entity linking on scientific tables fall below human performance.
GlobalBench dynamically tracks progress on all NLP datasets in all languages.
GlobalBench tracks the estimated per-speaker utility and equity of language technology across all languages.
GlobalBench identifies the most under-served languages and rewards research efforts directed towards the most under-served languages.
Punjabi, Portuguese, and Wu Chinese are among the most under-served languages with relatively high population that are overlooked by composite multilingual benchmarks.
CodeBERTScore extends BERTScore by encoding the natural language input along with the generated code tokens to model the consistency between generated code and its preceding natural language context.
CodeBERTScore has a higher correlation with human preference than existing metrics.
CodeBERTScore has a higher correlation with functional correctness than existing metrics.
Large language models generate diverse implicit toxic outputs that are exceptionally difficult to detect via zero-shot prompting.
A reinforcement learning based attacking method induces implicit toxicity in large language models.
A reinforcement learning based attacking method uses a reward that prefers implicit toxic outputs over explicit toxic and non-toxic outputs.
Reinforcement learning fine-tuning of language models improves attack success rates against toxicity classifiers.
Fine-tuning toxicity classifiers on annotated examples generated by the reinforcement learning attacking method enhances detection of implicit toxic language.
Sentence as Chunk sequence (SaC) recognizes chunk spans as tuple relations and arguments for open information extraction.
Chunk-OIE is a BERT-based model that performs end-to-end sentence chunking and tuple extraction for open information extraction using the Sentence as Chunk sequence representation.
Sentence as Chunk sequence benefits the open information extraction task.
A benchmark simulates real-world evaluation of open information extraction models under distribution shifts of syntax and expression for the same knowledge meaning.
A large-scale testbed consists of knowledge-invariant cliques, each containing sentences with the same structured knowledge meaning but different syntactic and expressive forms.
A robustness metric defines robustness as consistent accuracy across all knowledge-invariant cliques.
Existing open information extraction models exhibit performance degradation under knowledge-invariant evaluation.
GrAPES evaluates AMR parsers on categories including seen and unseen labels, structural generalization, and coreference.
GrAPES reveals the abilities and shortcomings of current AMR parsers.
PROSE is a benchmark for document-level Chinese-English spoken language translation containing diverse pro-drop instances.
Pro-drop reduces the performance of Neural Machine Translation systems in Chinese-English translation.
Mention-Aware Semantic Augmentation leverages the semantic embedding of dropped pronouns to augment training pairs.
Mention-Aware Semantic Augmentation improves omitted pronoun retrieval and overall translation quality in Chinese-English translation.
AmbiQT is a benchmark for text-to-SQL under ambiguity where each text query has two plausible SQL interpretations.
Text-to-SQL systems and decoding algorithms do not generate all valid interpretations for ambiguous queries.
Beam search and its variants treat SQL queries as a string and produce unhelpful token-level diversity in the top-k.
LogicalBeam is a decoding algorithm that navigates the SQL logic space using plan-based template generation and constrained infilling.
LogicalBeam generates all candidate SQL interpretations in the top-k more effectively than state-of-the-art models.
A conceptual framework unifies evaluation metrics for structured prediction tasks including event extraction, relation extraction, syntactic parsing, and semantic parsing.
Evaluation metrics for structured prediction tasks are derived by matching common substructures of task outputs represented as objects of certain data types, possibly followed by normalization.
New evaluation metrics for structured prediction tasks can be naturally derived in a bottom-up way based on output structure.
Specific task characteristics motivate metric design decisions for structured prediction tasks.
A transformer-based model generates live updates for soccer matches from microblog posts.
A mechanism controls the number of live updates for soccer matches.
A mechanism reduces the redundancy of duplicate and similar live updates for soccer matches.
Reasoning-based object detection allows interaction with object detection systems using natural language instructions.
DetGPT uses multi-modal models and open-vocabulary object detectors to perform reasoning based on user instructions and visual scenes.
DetGPT locates objects of interest based on desires expressed by the user even when the objects are not explicitly mentioned.
Pathological Graph-driven Cross-modal Alignment (PGCA) decouples cross-modal alignment by constructing a Pathological Graph to learn fine-grained visual cues and align them with textual words.
Pathological Graph comprises heterogeneous nodes representing tissue and lesion attributes connected by intra- and inter-attribute edges using prior domain knowledge.
Graph embedding and updating modules refine visual features of subtle tissues and lesions and align them with textual words via contrastive learning.
Retrieval-Augmented Style Transfer utilizes the style of diverse templates for question generation.

Retrieval-Augmented Style Transfer uses a Reinforcement Learning training approach with a consistency reward computed by a Question-Answering model and a diversity reward based on mimicry of retrieved templates.
Retrieval-Augmented Style Transfer generates more diverse questions than previous diversity-driven baselines while maintaining comparable question consistency.
MedEval is a multi-level, multi-task, and multi-domain medical benchmark for language model evaluation.
MedEval provides multi-level expert annotations to support a wide range of medical tasks.
Generic and domain-specific language models show varying effectiveness across medical tasks.
Instruction tuning is important for few-shot usage of large language models in medical domains.
NormSage automatically extracts culture-specific norms from multi-lingual conversations.
NormSage extracts candidate norms directly from conversations using GPT-3 prompting.
NormSage provides explainable self-verification for correctness and relevance of norms.
NormSage extracts high-quality culture-aware norms from multi-lingual conversations in English and Chinese.
NormSage assesses norm adherence and violation with respect to a conversation on-the-fly, providing textual explanations.
NormSage generates explanations for norm adherence and violation that match human-written quality.
AIDIL uses an attribute siamese network to mine similar attributes between source and target concepts for word-pair metaphor detection.
AIDIL uses a domain contrastive learning strategy to learn the semantic inconsistency of concepts in source and target domains for word-pair metaphor detection.
AIDIL outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods for word-pair metaphor detection.
AIDIL demonstrates generalization ability across multiple datasets for word-pair metaphor detection.
A multitasking framework leverages the reply-to structure from addressee recognition to facilitate relation-based structure learning in dialogue discourse parsing.
A reinforcement learning agent identifies training examples from addressee recognition that are most helpful for dialogue discourse parsing.
A task-aware structure transformer captures the shared and private dialogue structure of different tasks to promote dialogue discourse parsing.
KQG-CoT retrieves supportive logical forms from an unlabeled data pool considering logical form characteristics.
KQG-CoT writes a prompt that explicitly shows the reasoning chain for generating complicated questions based on selected demonstrations.
KQG-CoT+ extends KQG-CoT by sorting logical forms by complexity to ensure prompt quality.
KQG-CoT and KQG-CoT+ consistently outperform other prompting baselines on multiple KBQG datasets.
KQG-CoT+ surpasses existing few-shot state-of-the-art methods on the PathQuestions dataset.
Multilingual language models exhibit abstract monolingual grammatical representations that function similarly to human grammatical representations.
Grammatical representations in multilingual language models are similar across languages.
Grammatical representations in multilingual language models can causally influence text produced in different languages.
NormLens is a multimodal benchmark for evaluating model alignment with human judgment and model explanation quality for defeasible commonsense norms in visually grounded contexts.
State-of-the-art models exhibit poor alignment with human judgment and explanation quality for defeasible commonsense norms in visually grounded contexts.
A method aligns models with human judgment for defeasible commonsense norms in visually grounded contexts through distillation of social commonsense knowledge from large language models.
Incorporating both gallery and query data is necessary to address hubness in cross-modal retrieval.
Dual Bank Normalization (DBNorm) reduces the occurrence of hubs during inference by leveraging two banks constructed from query and gallery samples.
Dual inverted softmax normalizes similarity based on two banks to reduce the similarity between hubs and queries and improve the similarity between non-hubs and queries.
Dual dynamic inverted softmax normalizes similarity based on two banks to reduce the similarity between hubs and queries and improve the similarity between non-hubs and queries.
Dual Bank Normalization, dual inverted softmax, and dual dynamic inverted softmax outperform previous methods in addressing hubness and improving retrieval performance.
Topic-agnostic and topic-aware embeddings are trained using contrastive learning on an unlabeled news dataset covering a variety of topics for stance detection.
TATA achieves state-of-the-art performance on zero-shot stance detection.

Dynamic top-k estimation dynamically determines the optimal number of tokens to display for feature attribution in text classification by leveraging sequential properties of attribution scores, is method-agnostic, and accounts for sentence length bias.

Perturbation-based feature attribution methods and Vanilla Gradient exhibit the highest agreement with humans and other methods when using a static k for token highlighting in natural language inference tasks.

Using dynamic k for feature attribution eliminates the advantage of perturbation-based methods and Vanilla Gradient over other methods and primarily improves Integrated Gradient and GradientXInput in natural language inference tasks.
Sequential properties of feature attribution scores are informative for consolidating attribution signals for human interpretation.
Well-calibrated confidence scores balance cost and annotator load in task-oriented parsing while improving accuracy with a small number of interactions.
Confidence-based thresholding reduces the number of incorrect low-confidence programs executed at the cost of usability.
The DidYouMean system balances usability and safety by rephrasing low-confidence inputs.
A mathematical model of the evolution of lexical meaning explains polysemy emergence through word frequency, non-conformism, and semantic discriminability.
Word senses diversify into multiple coexisting senses when word frequency is low, non-conformist usage bias is strong, and semantic discriminability is high.

The promotion of word sense diversification by low word frequency, strong non-conformist usage bias, and high semantic discriminability is statistically validated with historical language data using multiple alternative operationalizations.
SentiStream is a co-training framework for adaptive online sentiment analysis in evolving data streams.
SentiStream comprises unsupervised, semi-supervised, and stream merge modules.
SentiStream achieves higher accuracy and computational efficiency than existing approaches for online sentiment analysis in evolving data streams.
HiddenTables is a cooperative game for table question-answering that uses natural language schemas to protect data privacy.
Large language models cannot generalize to complex table queries, handle compositional dependencies, or align natural language to programmatic commands when concrete table schemas are provided.
HiddenTables supports tables of arbitrary row count and reduces token usage.
PyQTax is a dataset of question-table-answer triplets with fine-grained taxonomic labels.
Z-Fold fully utilizes Transformer structural features for post-training quantization of large language models.
SymGen uses large language models to generate annotation-expensive symbolic language data.
SymGen uses an informative prompt to steer generation of symbolic language data.
SymGen uses an agreement-based verifier to improve the correctness of generated symbolic language data.
A small task model trained on generated symbolic language data achieves comparable or better performance than large language models while reducing inference and deployment costs.
Generated symbolic language data from a few human demonstrations is as effective as a large amount of human-annotated data for training task models, reducing annotation effort.
Fine-tuned models suffer from performance degradation due to temporal distribution shifts in financial sentiment analysis.

Combining out-of-distribution detection with time series modeling enhances models' capability to adapt to evolving temporal distribution shifts in financial sentiment analysis in volatile financial markets.
Reproducibility in model comparison is a probabilistic function of the model comparison conclusion.
Reproducibility in model comparison is qualitatively dominated by the signal-to-noise ratio of the model performance estimator from a corpus splitting strategy.
The mixture estimator for NLP model performance employs a regularized corpus splitting strategy using blocked 3×2 cross-validation.
The mixture estimator for NLP model performance achieves a high signal-to-noise ratio for model performance estimation.
Inter-RAT discovers causal rationales for neural network predictions.
Confounders cause spurious correlations between input and rationales in selective rationalization.
The sparsity constraint in rationalization causes spurious correlations between rationales and prediction results.
Inter-RAT removes spurious correlations between input and rationales using causal intervention based on backdoor adjustment.
Inter-RAT removes spurious correlations between rationales and prediction results using causal intervention based on backdoor adjustment.
Large language models cannot limit their vocabulary to levels appropriate for younger age groups.
The child-directed lexical simplification dataset contains examples from LLM-generated children's stories.
State-of-the-art lexical simplification models rely on large language models and have reduced performance on children-directed material.
Some lexical simplification models achieve comparable or improved performance on children-directed data after fine-tuning.
A comprehensive taxonomy categorizes reasoning and operation types for tabular data analysis.
CRT-QA is the first Table QA dataset with multi-step operation and informal reasoning.
CRT-QA contains fine-grained annotations of questions' directness, composition types of sub-questions, and human reasoning paths for investigating LLMs' reasoning ability.
CRT-QA contains unanswerable and indeterminate questions that occur in real-world situations.
ARC uses external tools such as Pandas to solve table reasoning tasks without handcrafted demonstrations.
CRT-QA is challenging for baseline methods.
Seed word quality for unsupervised Aspect Category Detection is enhanced by adding seed words to the initial set.
High-quality sentences are selected for training instead of using the entire dataset in unsupervised Aspect Category Detection.
Noise resolution in unsupervised Aspect Category Detection is treated as a data augmentation task for low-resource scenarios.
Aspect Category Detection is jointly trained with Aspect Term Extraction and Aspect Term Polarity.
Joint training of Aspect Category Detection with Aspect Term Extraction and Aspect Term Polarity facilitates shared representation learning.
Intonation Units (IUs) serve as basic tokens for code-switching metrics.
Multilinguality and code-switching probability metrics are calculated separately for alternating-language multi-word strings and single-word incorporations.
Individual differences in code-switching behavior according to multilinguality and code-switching probability metrics are independent.
Bilinguals tend to have multi-word code-switching occur across Intonation Unit boundaries rather than within them.
Not distinguishing between multi-word and single-word code-switching items blurs the tendency for multi-word code-switching to occur across Intonation Unit boundaries.
The squared norm of static word embedding encodes the information gain, defined as the Kullback-Leibler divergence of the word's co-occurrence distribution to the unigram distribution.
The encoding of information gain by the squared norm of static word embedding is explained by the theoretical framework of the exponential family of probability distributions.
The relationship between the squared norm of word embedding and information gain extends to contextualized word embeddings in language models and neural networks with a softmax output layer.

The KL divergence of a word's co-occurrence distribution to the unigram distribution and the squared norm of its word embedding are useful for keyword extraction, proper-noun discrimination, and hypernym discrimination.
An alignment learning framework uses synthetic feedback without requiring extensive human annotations or proprietary large language models.
Reward modeling uses synthetic feedback by contrasting responses from vanilla large language models of various sizes and prompts.
A reward model simulates high-quality demonstrations to train a supervised policy and optimize the language model with reinforcement learning.
ALMoST outperforms open-sourced models trained on InstructGPT outputs or human-annotated demonstrations in alignment benchmarks.
ALMoST is preferred over Alpaca and Dolly-v2 in human evaluation.
Analyses demonstrate the efficacy and importance of synthetic feedback for aligning large language models.
A recipe enables uptraining existing multi-head language model checkpoints into multi-query attention models with low additional compute cost.
Grouped-query attention uses an intermediate number of key-value heads (more than one but less than the number of query heads).
Uptrained grouped-query attention achieves language modeling quality close to multi-head attention while maintaining inference speed comparable to multi-query attention.
Large language models with code capabilities (CodeT5, Codex, and ChatGPT) face significant distribution shift challenges from samples in new domains (by organization, project, or module).
For code summarization and code generation, combining multitask learning with few-shot finetuning on retrieved training examples outperforms direct finetuning in very low-data scenarios.
For code generation, models adapted to multiple domains simultaneously achieve performance equivalent to models adapted to single domains.
Adapter-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning outperforms full fine-tuning in low-resource text classification.
Adapter-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning maintains its advantage over full fine-tuning when active learning is applied in low-resource text classification.
Adapter-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning yields more stable representations in the early and middle layers of language models than full fine-tuning in low-resource text classification.
InterSent enables interpreting embedding transformations as compositional sentence operations.
InterSent uses operator networks and a bottleneck encoder-decoder model to produce meaningful and interpretable sentence embeddings.
InterSent improves the interpretability of sentence embeddings on textual generation tasks while maintaining strong performance on semantic similarity tasks.
CLIP exhibits consistently lower performance for poorer households than for wealthier households across various topics and countries.
InfoSeek is a visual question answering dataset for visual information-seeking questions that require knowledge beyond common sense.
State-of-the-art pre-trained multi-modal models face challenges in answering visual information-seeking questions.
Fine-tuning state-of-the-art pre-trained multi-modal models on the InfoSeek dataset elicits the use of fine-grained knowledge learned during pre-training.
Using accurate visual entity recognition to retrieve relevant documents improves performance on the InfoSeek dataset.
BasahaCorpus contains short fictional narratives for readability assessment in Hiligaynon, Minasbate, Karay-a, and Rinconada.
A hierarchical cross-lingual modeling approach leverages a language's placement in the family tree to increase available training data for readability assessment.
Highly informative linguistic features for readability assessment are similar across mutually intelligible languages.
An evaluation paradigm for subword tokenization uses cognitive plausibility as an evaluation criterion.
The Unigram algorithm yields less cognitively plausible tokenization behavior.
The Unigram algorithm yields worse coverage of derivational morphemes.

Statements Crucial for Awareness of Interpretive Nonsense (SCAINs) are statements that, if missed by a listener, may cause misinterpretation of subsequent statements relative to the speaker's intended meaning.

Statements Crucial for Awareness of Interpretive Nonsense (SCAINs) are identified by omitting two consecutive statements from a dialogue and generating text to make the subsequent statement more specific.
Statements Crucial for Awareness of Interpretive Nonsense (SCAINs) are distinct from statements that are merely important.
Casting token relations as a partial order reduces the runtime complexity of pairwise token relation tasks from quadratic to linear.
Predicting real numbers for tokens in parallel and sorting them yields total orders of tokens.
Each total order of tokens implies a set of arcs oriented from smaller to greater tokens.
The intersection of multiple total orders of tokens results in a partial order that is decoded into a directed graph representing linguistic structure.
Partial-order-based modeling achieves state-of-the-art or comparable performance on dependency parsing and coreference resolution.
Linear complexity and parallelism lead to substantial speed-ups in graph-based coreference resolution and dependency parsing.
IBADR debiases natural language understanding models without predefining biased features.
IBADR iteratively expands a sample pool by incorporating generated pseudo samples.
IBADR uses a sample generator conditioned on bias indicators to produce pseudo samples with fewer biased features.
IBADR outperforms existing dataset refinement approaches for debiasing natural language understanding models.
IBADR is compatible with model-centric debiasing methods.
CompPrompts is a dataset of image captions with increasing compositionality, including single objects, object-property associations, and multiple interacting objects.
Text-only recovery probes reconstruct captions from single-vector text representations of vision-language models.
CLIP's text encoder fails to capture compositional inputs such as object relationships, attribute-object associations, counting, and negations.
ControlledImCaps is a benchmark for evaluating multimodal matching on fine-grained compositional images and captions.
Text-only recoverability is a necessary but not sufficient condition for modeling compositional factors in contrastive vision-language models.
