ZEROTOP decomposes semantic parsing into abstractive and extractive question-answering problems.
Large language models fail to detect unanswerable questions in semantic parsing and thus cannot handle questions for missing slots.
Fine-tuning a language model on public QA datasets with synthetic negative samples handles unanswerable questions in zero-shot semantic parsing.
Small scene graph datasets provide sufficient information for enhancing structured understanding in pretrained vision and language models.
The SG Component in the image transformer predicts scene graph information.
Fine-grained captions generated from scene graphs highlight compositional aspects of the scene.
Integrating structured representations from scene graphs improves vision-language model performance on multiple datasets with minimal degradation in zero-shot capabilities.
SCITAB consists of expert-verified scientific claims.
The scientific claims in SCITAB originate from authentic scientific publications.
The scientific claims in SCITAB require compositional reasoning for verification.
SCITAB poses a significant challenge to state-of-the-art models for scientific claim verification.
Chain-of-Thought prompting does not significantly improve claim verification performance on SCITAB.
SCITAB presents unique challenges in table grounding, claim ambiguity, and compositional reasoning.
JointMatch adaptively adjusts classwise thresholds based on per-class learning progress to reduce bias towards easy classes.
JointMatch uses two differently initialized networks that teach each other via cross-labeling.
JointMatch maintains divergence between two networks by weighing disagreement data more heavily and utilizing high-quality agreement data for training.
JointMatch achieves high accuracy in extremely-scarce-label settings for text classification.
Distributional and example-specific similarity measures correlate with language model performance on multilingual benchmarks.
Distributional and example-specific similarity measures do not correlate with language model accuracy on other downstream benchmarks.
Distributional and example-specific similarity measures do not correlate with each other on other downstream benchmarks.
A pipeline extracts relevant commonsense from existing knowledge graphs for emotion recognition in code-mixed conversations.
A fusion technique combines commonsense information with dialogue representation for emotion recognition in code-mixed conversations.
Incorporating commonsense improves emotion recognition in code-mixed conversations.
Video Misleading Headline (VMH) is a multimodal dataset containing videos and annotations of headline representativeness.
Video Misleading Headline (VMH) includes annotations of why annotators view a video as misleading.
The blindfolded navigation task uses only the initial environmental layout, the language instruction, and the action sequence during training.
Intermediate environmental layouts can be reasonably reconstructed from the internal activations of a trained model.
Language instructions influence the reconstruction accuracy of environmental layouts.

Propaganda content in political news is more likely to be embedded in sentences that attribute causality or assert contrast to nearby sentences, and in sentences involving opinionated evaluation, speculation, and discussions of future expectation.
A teacher model identifies PDTB-style discourse relations between nearby sentences for propaganda identification.
A teacher model identifies common discourse roles of sentences in a news article for propaganda identification.
Teacher predicted probabilities for discourse structures are used as additional features for propaganda identification.
Knowledge distillation incorporates discourse structures for propaganda identification.
Incorporating discourse structures improves precision and recall for propaganda content identification.
Users rely on QA system predictions even when lacking sufficient information to assess the correctness of those predictions.
Relevant background information reduces users' over-reliance on incorrect QA system predictions.
Background information increases users' confidence in their judgments about QA system predictions, irrespective of the accuracy of those judgments.
FairBalance uses a cycle consistent adversarial network for bias neutralization without parallel text.
FairBalance preserves bias-independent content.
FairBalance uses auxiliary guidance to highlight sequences of bias-inducing words.
SocKET is a theory-driven benchmark containing 58 NLP tasks for testing social knowledge, grouped into five categories: humor & sarcasm, offensiveness, sentiment & emotion, and trustworthiness.
Current large language models attain only moderate performance on the SocKET benchmark.
There is significant potential for task transfer among different types and categories of social knowledge tasks, as predicted by theory.
Pretrained large language models possess innate but limited capabilities for social language understanding.
Training on one category of social knowledge tasks improves zero-shot performance on other categories.
Fidelity-Enriched Contrastive Search is a decoding method that augments the contrastive search framework with context-aware regularization terms.
Fidelity-Enriched Contrastive Search promotes tokens semantically similar to the provided source and penalizes repetitiveness in generated text.
Fidelity-Enriched Contrastive Search enhances faithfulness across various language model sizes while maintaining output diversity comparable to well-performing decoding algorithms.
Confidence-based ensembling of perspective-aware models combines predictions using individual confidence to capture subjectivity in linguistic phenomena annotation.
Confidence-based ensembling of perspective-aware models improves classification performance for irony and hate speech detection in in-domain and cross-domain settings.
Confidence-based ensembling of perspective-aware models is effective when perspectives are automatically extracted from annotations without annotator metadata.
DEPN locates neurons associated with privacy texts using integrated gradients.

DUMB includes nine tasks for low-, medium-, and high-resource evaluation of Dutch language models.
DUMB includes four tasks that were previously unavailable in Dutch.
Relative Error Reduction (RER) compares language model performance on DUMB to a strong baseline, enabling consistent evaluation across different model sets.
Current Dutch monolingual language models under-perform on the DUMB benchmark.
Sparse low-rank adaptation enables dynamic adjustments to the intrinsic rank during the fine-tuning of pre-trained language models.
Sparse low-rank adaptation uses a gate unit optimized with the proximal gradient method to control the rank cardinality under sparsity.
Sparse low-rank adaptation eliminates parameter blocks corresponding to zeroed-out ranks during inference to produce a rank-optimal low-rank adaptation module.
A sparsifying scheduler for sparse low-rank adaptation examines the impact of non-zero parameter count on model memorization and generalization.
Sparse low-rank adaptation outperforms baseline methods with reduced parameters and training time.
Quality estimation improves appropriate reliance on machine translation in realistic high-stakes medical settings.
Backtranslation helps physicians detect clinically harmful errors that quality estimation alone often misses in realistic high-stakes medical settings.
Unsupervised Domain-Adaptive Pre-Training of a base Pre-trained Language Model (not a Sentence Encoder) improves few-shot sentence classification accuracy.
Applying Domain-Adaptive Pre-Training on Sentence Encoders disrupts the effects of their general-domain Sentence Embedding Pre-Training.
Applying general-domain Sentence Embedding Pre-Training on a domain-adapted base Pre-trained Language Model requires executing Sentence Embedding Pre-Training for each domain.
AdaSent decouples Sentence Embedding Pre-Training from Domain-Adaptive Pre-Training using a domain-agnostic adapter trained on the base Pre-trained Language Model.
AdaSent matches or surpasses the performance of full Sentence Embedding Pre-Training on Domain-Adaptive Pre-Training adapted Pre-trained Language Models while reducing training costs.
Hybrid Inverted Index (HI2) uses embedding clusters and salient terms collaboratively to accelerate dense retrieval.
Hybrid Inverted Index (HI2) employs a cluster selector and a term selector to construct compact inverted lists for efficient searching.
Hybrid Inverted Index (HI2) learns the cluster selector and term selector using unsupervised algorithms and end-to-end knowledge distillation.
Hybrid Inverted Index (HI2) achieves lossless retrieval quality with competitive efficiency.
Embedding clusters and salient terms complement each other in Hybrid Inverted Index (HI2).
NormDial is a high-quality dyadic dialogue dataset with turn-by-turn annotations of social norm adherences and violations for Chinese and American cultures.
Social norm observance detection is a task that detects social norm adherences and violations in dialogue.
NormDial is synthetically generated in Chinese and English using a human-in-the-loop pipeline that prompts large language models with expert-annotated social norms.
BCBR uses a rule miner that models rules by beta contribution.
BCBR constructs forward and reverse logic constraints based on beta rules.
BCBR reconstructs rule consistency loss using bidirectional constraints to regulate document-level relation extraction model outputs.
BCBR achieves higher relation extraction performance than original document-level relation extraction models.
BCBR achieves higher logical consistency than original document-level relation extraction models.
TOD-Flow infers a graph representing the underlying task structure of task-oriented dialogues from dialogue data annotated with dialog acts.
TOD-Flow learns what a dialogue model can, should, and should not predict, reducing the search space and providing a rationale for predictions.
TOD-Flow graphs resemble human-annotated task structure graphs better than prior approaches.
Integrating TOD-Flow with dialogue models improves dialog act classification and end-to-end response generation performance on MultiWOZ and SGD benchmarks.
Language-specific probes test aspects of morphological generalization.
Three leading morphological inflection systems employ distinct generalization strategies over conjugational classes and feature sets on orthographic and phonologically transcribed inputs.
MoPe identifies with high confidence whether a given text is in the training data of a pre-trained language model given white-box access to the model parameters.
MoPe adds noise to the model in parameter space and measures the drop in log-likelihood at a given point.
The drop in log-likelihood measured by MoPe approximates the trace of the Hessian matrix with respect to model parameters.
MoPe is more effective than existing loss-based attacks and perturbation-based methods for identifying training data membership.
The loss of a training point alone is insufficient to determine its extractability.
In Pythia and GPT2 language models, the pretraining frequency of the query country and the in-context city affects the likelihood of overwriting memorized knowledge with a counterfactual prefix.
In Pythia and GPT2 language models, specific attention heads promote either the memorized answer or the in-context answer in the logits.
In Pythia and GPT2 language models, scaling the value vector of specific attention heads controls the likelihood of generating the in-context answer on new data at runtime.
PRCA fits black-box large language models into retrieval question answering frameworks.
PRCA refines retrieved information for retrieval question answering using a token-autoregressive strategy that maximizes reinforcement learning rewards.
PRCA enhances retrieval question answering performance on multiple datasets.
An interpreting method quantifies the degree of functional specialization in multi-head attention.
A multi-task training method increases functional specialization in multi-task learning.
A multi-task training method mitigates negative information transfer in multi-task learning.
The degree of functional specialization that evolves in multi-head attention after multi-task training is affected by the similarity of tasks.
A multi-task training method that increases functional specialization boosts performance in multi-task learning and transfer learning without adding parameters.
CESAR unifies dialog tasks in the same format.
CESAR allows programmatic induction of compositional instructions without manual effort.
InstructDial++ evaluates dialog tasks with compositional instructions.
CESAR scales to provide rich instructions.
Models trained on InstructDial++ follow compositional prompts.
CORECT captures conversation-level cross-modality interactions using modality-specific representations.
CORECT captures utterance-level temporal dependencies using modality-specific representations.
Rationale generated by Large Language Models for relation classification is applied in continual relation extraction.
Multi-task rationale tuning strategy trains models to learn current relations robustly in continual relation extraction.
Contrastive rationale replay distinguishes analogous relations in continual relation extraction.
CodeFusion is a pre-trained diffusion model for code generation from natural language.
CodeFusion iteratively denoises a complete program conditioned on encoded natural language.
CodeFusion achieves a better balance between diversity and quality than state-of-the-art auto-regressive systems for natural language to code generation.
An ELECTRA-based keyword extractor denoises mention contexts and constructs better query expressions for sparse retrieval in few-shot entity linking.
A distant supervision method automatically generates training data for keyword extractors from overlapping tokens between mention contexts and entity descriptions.
Keyword-enhanced sparse retrieval outperforms state-of-the-art models across all test domains on the ZESHEL dataset.
Piantadosi et al.'s derivation minimizes a lower bound of the channel capacity hypothesis cost (CCH-lower) rather than the channel capacity hypothesis cost.
Word lengths in a language are proportional to the expectation of surprisal plus the variance-to-mean ratio of surprisal.
Word length is better predicted by word frequency than by the channel capacity hypothesis or CCH-lower.
Estimating surprisal's expectation or expectation plus variance-to-mean ratio with better language models leads to worse word length predictions.
REFLEX constructs a belief graph using backward chaining to materialize relevant model beliefs, including beliefs about answer candidates, and their inferential relationships.
REFLEX identifies and minimizes contradictions in the belief graph using a formal constraint reasoner.
REFLEX improves consistency without harming overall answer accuracy.
REFLEX produces answers supported by faithful chains of reasoning from a consistent belief system.

Denoised Neighborhood Aggregation retrieves k-nearest neighbors of a query as positive keys to capture semantic similarities between data and aggregates information from neighbors to learn compact cluster representations.
Denoised Neighborhood Aggregation uses three principles to filter false neighbors for better representation learning.
The learning objective of Denoised Neighborhood Aggregation is equivalent to a clustering loss that captures semantic similarities to form compact fine-grained clusters.
Denoised Neighborhood Aggregation retrieves more accurate neighbors for fine-grained category discovery.
Denoised Neighborhood Aggregation outperforms state-of-the-art models on fine-grained category discovery.
Model probing combined with textual analyses examines the stereotype-encoding behavior of pre-trained language models.
A small subset of attention heads in pre-trained language models is primarily responsible for encoding demographic stereotypes.
Attention maps on the attention heads responsible for encoding demographic stereotypes in pre-trained language models identify stereotypes toward specific minority groups.
Attention-head pruning debiases pre-trained language models without compromising language modeling capabilities or downstream task performance.

Bias experts are binary classifiers placed between the auxiliary model and the main model for debiasing, each trained on a binary classification task derived from the multi-class classification task via the One-vs-Rest approach.
Bias experts improve the bias identification ability of the auxiliary model.
A debiased model using bias experts consistently outperforms the state-of-the-art on various challenge datasets.
Cream enhances the language-image understanding capability of Large Language Models by capturing intricate details overlooked in existing methods.
Cream combines vision and auxiliary encoders with a contrastive feature alignment technique for effective comprehension of language information in visually situated contexts.
New datasets for visually-situated language understanding tasks are generated.
CoLT5 employs conditional computation to devote more resources to important tokens in both feedforward and attention layers.
CoLT5 achieves state-of-the-art performance on the long-input SCROLLS benchmark.
CoLT5 has faster training and inference than LongT5.
CoLT5 gains performance benefits from extremely long input sequences.
TextReact augments predictive chemistry with text descriptions retrieved from the literature.
TextReact retrieves text descriptions relevant for a given chemical reaction and aligns them with the molecular representation of the reaction.
TextReact enhances the alignment of text descriptions with molecular representations via an auxiliary masked LM objective incorporated in the predictor training.
TextReact outperforms state-of-the-art chemoinformatics models trained solely on molecular data on reaction condition recommendation and one-step retrosynthesis.
HyperLoRA leverages expert linguistic knowledge to enable resource-efficient adaptation to English dialects via hypernetworks.
HyperLoRA disentangles dialect-specific and cross-dialectal information to improve generalization to unseen English dialects in a task-agnostic fashion.
HyperLoRA is more scalable in the number of parameters for adapting to English dialects.
GradSim groups languages for multilingual training using gradient similarity.
GradSim achieves the largest performance gains among language similarity measures in multilingual training.
Gradient similarity for language grouping is better correlated with cross-lingual model performance than other language similarity measures.
Dataset topics play an important role in language grouping for multilingual training, alongside linguistic features.
Lower layers of transformer models encode language-specific features and higher layers capture task-specific information.
Dynosaur uses the metadata of existing datasets to automatically construct instruction-tuning data by identifying relevant data fields and generating instructions with large language models.
Dynosaur reduces the API cost for generating instruction-tuning data.
Dynosaur produces instruction-tuning data that achieves better performance on instruction-following tasks than existing methods with comparable data sizes.
Dynosaur supports continuous model improvement by generating instruction-tuning data when new annotated datasets become available.
Replaying tasks with diverse instruction embeddings mitigates forgetting and improves generalization to unseen tasks in continual learning for instruction-tuning.
The Anaphor-Assisted framework uses anaphor to identify relations between entities in document-level relation extraction.
The Anaphor-Assisted framework achieves fine-grained cross-sentence entity interactions in document-level relation extraction.
The Anaphor-Assisted framework achieves state-of-the-art performance on document-level relation extraction tasks.
A data efficient semi-supervised method resolves coreferences and narrative grounding in multimodal contexts using image-narration pairs.
Losses for labeled and unlabeled data are incorporated in a cross-modal framework for multimodal coreference resolution and narrative grounding.
KEBAP enables comprehensive analysis of ASR systems at both speech and text levels.
KEBAP facilitates balanced assessment of speech recognition accuracy and user readability.
KEBAP provides speech-level resources that incorporate diverse noise environments and speaker characteristics.
KEBAP provides distinct text-level error types.
The MUStARD++ dataset is extended with human-collected gaze features and predicted gaze features for multimodal sarcasm detection.
Predict-and-use improves multimodal sarcasm detection by harnessing predicted gaze.
Extrinsic and intrinsic evaluations confirm the quality of predicted gaze features for multimodal sarcasm detection.
Predict-and-use is the first method for multimodal sarcasm detection that harnesses predicted gaze.
Multimodal sarcasm detection using combined collected and synthetic gaze data achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MUStARD++ dataset.
Target-to-source augmentation generates diverse augmented samples for Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction by mixing labels and syntactic templates from different samples.

Target-to-source augmentation uses fluency and alignment discriminators to provide feedback for optimizing the generator of new sentences via reinforcement learning for Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction.
Adaptive end-to-end metric learning performs zero-shot cross-domain slot filling through a cascade-style joint learning framework.
Adaptive end-to-end metric learning uses context-aware soft label representations to mitigate data and label shift in zero-shot cross-domain slot filling.
Adaptive end-to-end metric learning uses slot-level contrastive representation learning to mitigate data and label shift in zero-shot cross-domain slot filling.
ALCAP systematically learns multimodal alignment between audio and lyrics using contrastive learning.
ALCAP achieves state-of-the-art performance on two music captioning datasets.

For health information, knowledge in the prompt biases ChatGPT to reduce answer correctness, with the bias occurring in retrieve-then-generate pipelines, varying with user question phrasing, and depending on question type.
StrAE learns multi-level representations through strict adherence to explicit structure.
StrAE uses a novel contrastive objective over tree-structured representations.
StrAE's results are directly attributable to the informativeness of the input structure.
Self-StrAE defines its own compositions using a localised-merge algorithm.
Self-StrAE performs comparably to models that use informative structure such as constituency parses.
DC-DGNN models temporal user and product dynamics for sentiment analysis.
DC-DGNN uses a dual-channel text encoder to extract current local and global contexts from review documents for users and products.
DC-DGNN integrates user review streams into the dynamic graph neural network by treating users and products as nodes and reviews as new edges.
DC-DGNN dynamically updates node representations along with the evolution of the dynamic graph for final score prediction.
Learn and Consolidate encourages existing multilingual neural machine translation models to acquire language-agnostic multilingual representations from new data.
Learn and Consolidate preserves the model architecture without introducing parameters.
Learn and Consolidate improves translation performance for initially weak language pairs in multilingual neural machine translation.
Learn and Consolidate mitigates performance degeneration for originally well-performing language pairs in multilingual neural machine translation.
The CoT Collection is an instruction-tuning dataset containing 1.84 million chain-of-thought rationales across 1,060 tasks.
Chain-of-thought fine-tuning with the CoT Collection improves the chain-of-thought reasoning capabilities of smaller language models on unseen tasks.
Instruction tuning with the CoT Collection enables language models to outperform ChatGPT in few-shot learning on domain-specific tasks when using demonstrations of maximum length.
Multi-Source Probing probes the dialogue comprehension abilities of open-domain dialogue models.
Multi-Source Probing aggregates features from multiple sources to accomplish diverse dialogue comprehension task goals.
Multi-Source Probing conducts dialogue comprehension tasks in a generative manner consistent with dialogue model pre-training.
Open-domain dialogue models encode semantic information in intermediate hidden states to facilitate dialogue comprehension tasks.
Models of different scales and structures possess different conversational understanding capabilities.
StereoMap maps LLMs' perceptions of social groups defined by socio-demographic features using the dimensions of Warmth and Competence.
StereoMap enables the investigation of keywords and verbalizations of reasoning in LLMs' judgments to uncover underlying factors influencing LLMs' perceptions.
LLMs exhibit diverse perceptions of social groups defined by socio-demographic features, characterized by mixed evaluations along Warmth and Competence dimensions.
LLMs demonstrate awareness of social disparities by stating statistical data and research findings to support their reasoning.
Decreasing the distance between the attractor noun and the verb increases subject-verb agreement errors in humans and neural network models.
Neural network models approach chance level in subject-verb agreement when the attractor noun is close to the verb, while humans mostly overcome attractor interference.
Attractor-verb distance linearly affects human reaction times during subject-verb agreement tasks.
UNO Arena evaluates sequential decision-making capability of large language models.
UNO Arena uses novel metrics based on Monte Carlo methods for dynamic evaluation of sequential decision-making.
TUTRI uses reflection on game history and strategy for sequential decision-making in large language models.
The Citation Field Diversity Index quantifies cross-field engagement in academic citations.
Natural Language Processing has experienced a decline in cross-field engagement over time, which is unlike most academic fields.
Natural Language Processing has increased citations to papers in Natural Language Processing and decreased the number of papers that bridge between academic fields.
Citations from Natural Language Processing papers are predominantly to computer science, with linguistics, mathematics, and psychology receiving a small proportion.
Guideline Learning is a framework for in-context information extraction that reflectively learns and follows guidelines.
Guideline Learning automatically synthesizes guidelines based on error cases during the learning phase.
Guideline Learning retrieves helpful guidelines during inference to improve in-context learning.
A self-consistency-based active learning method enhances the efficiency of Guideline Learning.
Guideline Learning improves the performance of in-context information extraction for event extraction and relation extraction.
The text-to-table task is redefined for improved compatibility with long text-processing tasks.
CPL is a dataset of Chinese private lending judgments derived from a real-world legal academic project.

TKGT is a two-stage domain-aware pipeline that semi-automatically generates domain knowledge graph classes from raw text using mixed information extraction and then transforms them into tables using hybrid retrieval augmented generation guided by the knowledge graph classes.
TKGT achieves state-of-the-art performance on traditional text-to-table datasets and the CPL dataset.
The stance extraction network (SEN) extracts whether multi-modal evidence supports or refutes a claim in a unified framework.
The support-refutation score uses co-occurrence relations of named entities to determine whether textual evidence supports or refutes a claim.
The stance extraction network incorporating support-refutation scoring outperforms state-of-the-art baselines for detecting out-of-context mis- and disinformation.
Scalable-DSC corrects wrong slot values in the dialogue state predicted by any DST model.

Structural Template Prompt (STP) converts predicted dialogue state from any DST model into a standardized natural language sequence as a part of the historical context, associates it with dialogue history information, and generates a corrected dialogue state sequence based on predefined template options.
A predictive state simulator creates simulated predicted dialogue states as training data to enhance model generalization.
A training strategy uses dialogue state predicted by DST as training data to mitigate inconsistent error type distribution between training and inference.
HyperRouter dynamically generates router parameters through a fixed hypernetwork and trainable embeddings to achieve a balance between training and freezing routers.
HyperRouter achieves superior performance and efficiency gains across a wide range of tasks compared to existing routing methods.

Language Representation Projection (LRP2) converts non-English representations into English-like equivalents without additional parameters to transfer factual knowledge from English to non-English languages.

Language Representation Projection (LRP2) reverts English-like representations back into representations of the corresponding non-English language without additional parameters to transfer factual knowledge from English to non-English languages.
Language Representation Projection (LRP2) improves factual knowledge retrieval accuracy for diverse non-English languages on the mLAMA dataset.
An expert-annotated dataset for detecting net zero and reduction targets set by corporate and national institutions.
ClimateBERT-NetZero detects net zero and reduction targets set by corporate and national institutions.
ClimateBERT-NetZero combined with question-answering models analyzes the ambitions of net zero and reduction targets.
Communication patterns regarding net zero and reduction targets in corporate earning calls evolve over time.
Mental health conversational agents research exhibits diversity in modeling and experimental design techniques.

Computer science papers on mental health conversational agents emphasize large language model techniques and automated response quality metrics with minimal application focus, while medical papers employ rule-based conversational agents and health outcome metrics.
Transparency, ethics, and cultural heterogeneity are considerations in mental health conversational agents research.
EDIS is a dataset for cross-modal image search in the news domain that consists of a large-scale set of multimodal image-text pairs from actual web search engine results and curated datasets.

EDIS requires models to understand named entities and events from text queries, ground entities onto images or text descriptions, and fuse textual and visual representations for accurate image search.
EDIS challenges state-of-the-art image retrieval methods due to dense entities and the large-scale candidate set.
Fusing textual features with visual features is critical for improving image retrieval results on EDIS.
Decoding method performance for large language models is task-dependent and influenced by alignment, model size, and quantization.

Some decoding methods for large language models achieve superior performance at the cost of extensive hyperparameter tuning, creating a trade-off between optimal results and implementation practicality.
Targeted temporal fact extraction retrieves temporal facts from textual resources when Knowledge Base Question Answering fails to retrieve them from the knowledge base.
Targeted temporal fact extraction is modeled as an open-domain question answering task using off-the-shelf language models.
Integrating targeted temporal fact extraction with Knowledge Base Question Answering improves temporal question answering answer accuracies without requiring additional training.
A dataset of pseudo-code prompts covers 132 tasks from Super-NaturalInstructions, including classification, question answering, and generative language tasks.
Pseudo-code instructions improve performance of pre-trained language models on classification, question answering, and generative language tasks.
Code comments, docstrings, and structural clues in pseudo-code prompts contribute to improved performance of pre-trained language models.
ToxiCloakCN is an enhanced Chinese offensive language detection dataset derived from ToxiCN and augmented with homophonic substitutions and emoji transformations.
Large language models underperform in detecting offensive content in Chinese text that has been perturbed by homophonic substitutions or emoji transformations.
A modular and interpretable grounded dialogue system converts partner utterances into executable code that performs grounding using a large language model.
A modular and interpretable grounded dialogue system determines the next appropriate response using a symbolic planner and stored dialogue state.
A modular and interpretable grounded dialogue system substantially outperforms the previous state-of-the-art in task success on the OneCommon dialogue task.
Mutual nearest neighbor contrastive learning geometrically aligns source and target code domains to capture common semantic characteristics and separate private semantic characteristics.
An instance re-weighting scheme dynamically assigns weights to target code instances to reduce the contribution of irrelevant target instances for domain alignment.
MNCRI outperforms state-of-the-art methods for cross-domain code vulnerability detection.
CtxSimFit combines similarity to the original sentence with contextual cohesiveness to evaluate stylistic text rewrites.
Humans prefer contextual rewrites as more fitting and natural over non-contextual rewrites in formality, toxicity, and sentiment transfer tasks.
ROUGE and SBERT correlate poorly with human preferences for stylistic text rewrites.
CtxSimFit correlates strongly with human preferences for stylistic text rewrites.
Context-infused versions of common automatic metrics correlate better with human preferences than sentence-level metrics for stylistic text rewrites.
USP categorizes natural language processing tasks into one of three task types.
USP uses a task-type-specific selector to select the most suitable queries and zero-shot model-generated responses as pseudo-demonstrations.
USP requires only a small amount of unlabeled data and an inference-only large language model.
USP achieves stronger performance than standard zero-shot baselines on natural language understanding, natural language generation, and reasoning tasks.
USP achieves performance comparable to or superior to few-shot baselines on natural language understanding, natural language generation, and reasoning tasks.
InfoGap locates information gaps and inconsistencies at the document and fact level across languages.
There are large discrepancies in factual coverage of LGBT people's biographies across English, Russian, and French Wikipedia.
Biographical facts with negative connotations are more likely to be highlighted in Russian Wikipedia than in English or French Wikipedia.
MEGA is the first comprehensive benchmark for generative large language models that evaluates models on 16 NLP datasets across 70 typologically diverse languages.
TrueTeacher generates synthetic data for factual consistency evaluation by annotating model-generated summaries with a large language model without relying on human-written summaries.
A student model trained on TrueTeacher-generated synthetic data outperforms the state-of-the-art model and the large language model teacher on the TRUE benchmark.
TrueTeacher outperforms existing synthetic data generation methods for factual consistency evaluation.
TrueTeacher is robust to domain-shift in synthetic data generation for factual consistency evaluation.
TrueTeacher generalizes to multilingual scenarios for factual consistency evaluation.
REMOTE exploits unlabeled medical images to augment medical report generation datasets.
MedCLIPScore evaluates medical report generation without reference reports.
REMOTE achieves competitive medical report generation performance with minimal labeled training data.
START leverages large language models to self-construct synthetic training data for warming up.
START iteratively utilizes fine-grained preference supervision signals from large language model sampled responses to encourage robust, comprehensive, and attributable generation.
START achieves performance gains in attribution on long-form question-answering and multi-step reasoning tasks without relying on human annotations or more advanced models.
START excels in aggregating information across multiple sources.
A heterogeneous graph for social media posts includes post, word, and emoji nodes connected by edges that model interactions among these elements.
A self-supervised graph pre-training framework for text and emoji co-modeling uses node-level graph contrastive learning and edge-level link reconstruction learning.
There are no set definitions or criteria for typological diversity claims in NLP research.
New metrics approximate language sample diversity along several axes.
Language sample diversity measurements vary considerably across NLP papers.
Skewed language selection leads to overestimated multilingual performance.
In vision-and-language models, bias amplification in pretraining and after fine-tuning are independent.
In vision-and-language models, continued pretraining on gender-neutral data reduces group disparities on VQAv2 and retrieval tasks without significantly compromising task performance.
POE eliminates seemingly wrong options by scoring each option in multiple choice reasoning.
POE masks eliminated options and makes the final prediction from the remaining options in multiple choice reasoning.
In multiple choice reasoning, POE achieves higher performance on logical reasoning tasks than on other reasoning tasks.
POE applies to few-shot settings in multiple choice reasoning.
POE applies to large language models such as ChatGPT in multiple choice reasoning.

A method evaluates the robustness of natural language understanding models to speech recognition errors using back transcription, a fine-grained error categorization technique, and synthesized speech.

Using synthesized speech instead of real audio recordings for evaluating natural language understanding model robustness to speech recognition errors does not significantly change the evaluation outcomes.
A Transformer-based Handwritten Text Recognition approach transcribes handwritten Old Occitan language.
A Handwritten Text Recognition system for Old Occitan uses data augmentation techniques for text and image data.
A Handwritten Text Recognition model for Old Occitan uses a Swin image encoder and a BERT text decoder.
A Handwritten Text Recognition model for Old Occitan is pre-trained on a large-scale augmented synthetic data set and fine-tuned on a small human-labeled data set.
A Transformer-based Handwritten Text Recognition approach for Old Occitan outperforms state-of-the-art models.
Synthetic data created through paraphrasing gold texts improves out-of-distribution robustness for hate speech detection in English across different models from a computational standpoint.
Synthetic data created through paraphrasing gold texts fails to reliably reflect the characteristics of real-world data on a number of linguistic dimensions for hate speech detection in English.
Synthetic data created through paraphrasing gold texts results in drastically different class distributions for hate speech detection in English.
Synthetic data created through paraphrasing gold texts heavily reduces the representation of specific identity groups in hate speech detection in English.
Synthetic data created through paraphrasing gold texts heavily reduces the representation of intersectional hate in hate speech detection in English.
PIXEL exhibits a substantial gap between visual and linguistic understanding.
PIXEL's lower layers capture superficial visual features and higher layers learn more syntactic and semantic abstractions.
Certain orthographic constraints at the input level facilitate earlier learning of surface-level features in PIXEL variants.
Connectors in multimodal large language models are categorized as feature-preserving or feature-compressing.
Sub-tasks from MMBench, MME, and SEED-Bench are categorized into coarse-grained perception, fine-grained perception, and reasoning.
Feature-preserving and feature-compressing connectors exhibit significant performance differences across coarse-grained perception, fine-grained perception, and reasoning tasks.
Stochastic variants of BPE and MaxMatch are heavily biased towards a small set of tokenizations per word.
Uniform tokenization sampling serves as a drop-in replacement for the stochastic aspects of existing tokenizers.
Uniform tokenization sampling improves machine translation quality.
IEMPATHIZE and TwittEmp are annotated with 8 empathy intent labels.
Empathy intent recognition improves empathy detection accuracy.
Reasons why empathy intent recognition improves empathy detection are analyzed.
Cascaded Label Signal Network captures feature exchange between empathy and empathy intent representations using cascaded interactive attention and label signal enhancement modules.
Cascaded Label Signal Network outperforms baseline methods for joint empathy detection and empathy intent recognition.
AMPO iteratively develops a multi-branched prompt using failure cases as feedback.
AMPO uses Pattern Recognition, Branch Adjustment, and Branch Pruning modules to handle multiple patterns in complex tasks.
AMPO achieves optimization efficiency through a minimal search strategy.
APEL actively searches for a simple input on which candidate programs produce different outputs for a natural language utterance.
APEL infers the correct program by having non-programmers select the appropriate output for an input on which candidate programs produce different outputs.
APEL achieves the same annotation accuracy as expert annotators for text-to-SQL.
APEL exposes subtle errors in existing text-to-SQL annotations.
ATFormer uses attention-inspired modules to predict the performance of optimized tensor operators by capturing global and long-range dependencies within a complete scheduling space.
ATFormer reduces inference time by predicting the optimal implementation of tensor operators.
ATFormer adapts to different workloads and hardware via transfer learning using pre-trained parameters.
StoryAnalogy is the first large-scale story-level analogy corpus containing story pairs from diverse domains with human annotations on two similarities from the extended Structure-Mapping Theory.
Story-level analogy identification and generation are evaluated for the first time.
Analogy identification tasks are difficult for sentence embedding models and large language models including ChatGPT and LLaMa.
StoryAnalogy data improves the quality of analogy generation in large language models.

A Two-Dimensional Coordinate System unifies two conflicting views on in-context learning by explaining its behavior through two orthogonal variables: perception (presence of similar examples in demonstrations) and cognition (task recognition ability of large language models).
The peak inverse rank metric detects the task recognition ability of large language models.
The Two-Dimensional Coordinate System interprets in-context learning for generation tasks.
A corpus contains literal meanings for emojis defined by L1 speakers in English, Portuguese, and Chinese.
Disagreement levels on emoji definitions vary by emoji and are consistent across languages.
A correlation exists between tweet sentiment and the figurative use of an emoji.
DUCK infuses structural information into entity representations using prior knowledge of entity types.
DUCK defines entity types based on an entity's relations with other entities in a knowledge graph.
DUCK represents relations as boxes on the hypersphere using spherical polar coordinates.
DUCK places entities inside boxes corresponding to their relations to cluster entities of similar type.
DUCK outperforms other type-aware approaches.
DUCK matches the results of generative models having more parameters.
The learned large language model should adhere to the proximity of the behavior large language model that collects the training samples.
BPO constructs a trust region for large language model alignment by staying close to the behavior large language model.
BPO achieves significant performance improvements across a wide range of tasks with the same amount of preference data when integrated with various direct alignment from preferences methods.
An intellectual humility codebook is manually curated and validated using 350 posts about religion from subreddits.
LLM-based models automate the measurement of intellectual humility in online discourse.
Automated detection of intellectual humility in online discourse does not reach human performance levels.
QUDSELECT uses instruction-tuning to simultaneously predict the anchor sentence and generate the associated question.
QUDSELECT uses a selective decoding strategy that samples multiple QUD candidates during inference and selects the best candidate using criteria scorers.
In Open-world Relation Extraction, popular relations can often be implicitly inferred through specific patterns, and long-tail relations tend to be explicitly expressed.
KNoRD classifies explicitly and implicitly expressed relations from known and novel classes within unlabeled data.
KNoRD consistently outperforms existing methods on Open-world Relation Extraction benchmarks.
A debiasing technique prevents shortcut learning during entity disambiguation training for overshadowed entities.
The debiasing technique achieves state-of-the-art performance on entity disambiguation without compromising inference speed.
The digital language coherence marker monitors cognitive changes in people with dementia.
A task learns the temporal logical consistency of utterances in short transcribed narratives.
The digital language coherence marker distinguishes between people with mild cognitive impairment, Alzheimer's Disease, and healthy controls.
The digital language coherence marker is highly associated with clinical bio-markers.
The digital language coherence marker shows potential for generalization to other related conditions.
Academic Writing Formalization (AWF) is a formal text style transfer task that transfers informal-academic text to formal-academic.
Doolittle is a large-scale non-parallel dataset for Academic Writing Formalization.
Metric-oriented reinforcement learning (MORL) incorporates different levels of automatic feedback into the training process of large language models.
Existing text transfer models and grammatical error correction models address certain aspects of Academic Writing Formalization but have a significant performance gap compared to human performance.

Language models fine-tuned with metric-oriented reinforcement learning rival the chatbot ChatGPT in Academic Writing Formalization but still have a non-negligible performance gap compared to ground truth formal-academic texts in the Doolittle dataset.
A combination of basic visual features such as color and texture is more effective than Vision Transformer features for classifying images of abstract versus concrete concepts.
Vision Transformers show better performance in nearest neighbor analysis of visual feature variability across multiple images of the same concept.
Current automatic poetry generation systems produce quatrains that are underdiverse in structural, lexical, semantic, and stylistic dimensions compared to human quatrains.
Style-conditioning and character-level modeling increase the diversity of automatically generated quatrains across multiple dimensions.
Adaptive Temperature Scaling (ATS) predicts per-token temperature scaling parameters that adapt based on token-level features and are fit over a supervised fine-tuning dataset.
ATS improves calibration compared to prior calibration methods on multiple natural language evaluation benchmarks.
ATS does not impede the performance improvements from reinforcement learning from human feedback.
RAt implicitly injects specific concept bias into input prompts during text-to-image prompt refinement.
RAt attacks input prompts by optimizing a token distribution matrix using text-to-image finetuning with a token-level bias obfuscation loss as regularization.
RAt generates images with higher quality and explicit visual bias towards a specific concept group when attacking input prompts.
PALS applies active learning in a personalized context to learn individual preferences in subjective NLP tasks.
PALS employs five new measures to determine text relevance for learning individual user preferences in subjective NLP tasks.
PALS reduces the number of necessary annotations while maintaining a given quality level in subjective NLP tasks.
Arbitrary text spans serve as basic generation units in a dynamic vocabulary for language models.
Generating multi-tokens atomically improves language model generation quality and efficiency.
A dynamic vocabulary for language models supports plug-and-play deployment.
A dynamic vocabulary for language models can be applied to different domains without additional training.
A dynamic vocabulary for language models generates reliable citations in question answering tasks without compromising answer accuracy.

Adversaries without knowledge of users' queries, knowledge base data, or language model parameters can exploit Retrieval-Augmented Generative models by uploading crafted deceptive content to the retrieval database with access to the retriever to intentionally change the model's behavior.

A task for referring expression generation and comprehension in multi-agent embodied environments requires agents to consider each other's visual perspective for producing and understanding references to objects and spatial relations.
A dataset of human-written referring expressions paired with human comprehension judgments is collected for multi-agent embodied referential communication.
Automated models for referring expression generation and comprehension in multi-agent embodied environments perform worse than pairs of human agents.
Training an open-weight speaker model with evidence of communicative success when paired with a listener improves communicative success and outperforms the strongest proprietary model.
METAREFLECTION uses offline reinforcement learning to learn instructions for Language Agents by augmenting a semantic memory with past reflections.
METAREFLECTION achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art prompt optimization techniques while requiring fewer LLM calls.
Summaries are generated with fine-grained control over readability.
Instruction-based readability control generates summaries at specified readability levels.
Reinforcement learning minimizes the gap between requested and observed readability in summary generation.
A decoding approach uses lookahead to estimate the readability of upcoming decoding steps for summary generation.
Readability control for news summarization is improved.
VideoFeedback is the first large-scale dataset of human-provided multi-aspect scores for synthesized videos from multiple video generative models.
VideoScore is an automatic video quality assessment metric trained on the VideoFeedback dataset.
VideoScore consistently achieves higher correlation with human judgments than prior metrics across multiple benchmarks.
DoFaiR is a benchmark that quantifies the trade-off between diversity interventions and demographic factuality in Text-to-Image models.
Diversity-oriented instructions increase the diversity of gender and racial groups in DALLE-3 generations but cause historically inaccurate demographic distributions.
Fact-Augmented Intervention uses a Large Language Model to reflect on factual information about historical gender and racial compositions and incorporates it into Text-to-Image generation.
Fact-Augmented Intervention improves demographic factuality under diversity interventions while preserving diversity in Text-to-Image generation.
Relation-aware ensemble learning combines knowledge graph embedding methods using relation-specific weights.
RelEns-DSC searches relation-wise ensemble weights independently.
RelEns-DSC has the same computation cost as general ensemble methods.
RelEns-DSC improves knowledge graph embedding performance compared to general ensemble methods.
XEUS extends the language coverage of self-supervised speech models to thousands of languages.
A new speech corpus covering thousands of languages is created and will be publicly released.
A novel dereverberation objective is introduced to handle diverse conditions of multilingual speech data.
XEUS sets a new state-of-the-art on the ML-SUPERB benchmark with fewer parameters or less pre-training data.
Adversarial perturbations reduce the correctness of large language model-generated code for introductory programming assignments.
The detectability of adversarial perturbations affects the reduction in correctness of large language model-generated code for introductory programming assignments.
A conversion method transforms dense LLMs into fine-grained MoE architectures.
Multilingual activation patterns of LLMs exhibit differences that can be leveraged for sparse activation and pruning.
Configuring different pruning rates for different layers based on activation level differences achieves better results for model pruning.
Guided sparse activation and pruning based on expert activation frequencies outperforms random expert pruning and unpruned models in some languages.
Individual annotator ratings on potentially offensive text can be predicted.
Combining predicted individual annotator ratings with the predicted target group of the text predicts the ratings of target group members.
Predicting variance among annotators provides a metric for model uncertainty.
Annotators’ ratings can be predicted using demographic information and opinions on online content.
Non-invasive questions on annotators’ online experiences minimize the need to collect demographic information for predicting annotators’ opinions.
Large language models face significant challenges in holding and processing information effectively even as model size increases, especially under complex task conditions.
Prompting strategies have diverse impacts on large language model performance.
Large language models struggle to autonomously discover optimal problem-solving patterns without heavily relying on manually corrected prompts.
CUTE tests the orthographic knowledge of Large Language Models.
Most Large Language Models know the spelling of the tokens they use but fail to use this knowledge effectively for text manipulation.
MathTrap is a dataset created by introducing carefully designed logical traps into the problem descriptions of MATH and GSM8K.
Large language models possess the requisite mathematical knowledge and trap-related knowledge but fail to spontaneously combine them for solving problems with logical traps.
Natural language prompts, few-shot demonstrations, and fine-tuning improve large language models' ability to solve mathematical reasoning problems with logical traps.
AdaZeta improves the performance and convergence of zeroth-order fine-tuning methods for large language models.
A fast-forward, low-parameter tensorized adapter enhances dimension-dependent zeroth-order estimation accuracy in large language model fine-tuning.
An adaptive query number schedule guarantees convergence in large language model fine-tuning tasks.
Text-based explanations lead to over-reliance by healthcare practitioners in human-AI collaborative chest X-ray analysis.
Combining text-based explanations with saliency maps alleviates over-reliance by healthcare practitioners in human-AI collaborative chest X-ray analysis.

Explanation quality, defined by the amount of factually correct information and alignment with AI correctness, impacts the usefulness of explanation types to healthcare practitioners in human-AI collaborative chest X-ray analysis.
A novel data collection pipeline enables efficient black-box distillation for machine translation evaluation metrics.
Quantization compresses xCOMET without degrading evaluation quality.
xCOMET-lite maintains high evaluation quality relative to xCOMET-XXL.
xCOMET-lite achieves higher performance than COMET-22 and BLEURT-20 on the WMT22 metrics challenge dataset with reduced parameter count.
CasCoD decomposes the Chain-of-Thoughts distillation process into two cascaded learning steps.
CasCoD restructures training objectives by removing the answer from model outputs and concatenating the question with the rationale as input.
CasCoD focuses student models on learning rationales without interference from preset answers.
CasCoD improves reasoning generalizability.
Bayesian Win-Rate Sampling uses Bayesian inference to infer the true win rate of generative language models.
Bayesian Dawid-Skene uses Bayesian inference to infer the true win rate of generative language models.
Bayesian Win-Rate Sampling and Bayesian Dawid-Skene improve the accuracy of win rate estimation with LLM evaluators.

Large Language Model utility for data annotation is structured into the core aspects of Annotation Generation using Large Language Models, Assessment of Annotations generated by Large Language Models, and Utilization of Annotations generated by Large Language Models.
An in-depth taxonomy categorizes data types that Large Language Models can annotate.
Learning strategies for models utilizing Large Language Model-generated annotations are comprehensively reviewed.
Primary challenges and limitations of using Large Language Models for data annotation and synthesis are detailed.
A coherent taxonomy organizes LLM-based evaluation metrics for Natural Language Generation evaluation and provides a structured framework for understanding and comparing these methods.
Multimodal connectors have limitations caused by insufficient training data.
TUNA uses retrieval-augmented tag tokens that contain object names and attributes.
TUNA outperforms baselines with the same language model and training data on multiple benchmarks.
TUNA has zero-shot capability with specific datastores.
Inference-time Policy Adapters (IPA) tailors extreme-scale language models without fine-tuning.
IPA guides a large base language model during decoding with a lightweight policy adapter trained via reinforcement learning to optimize arbitrary user objectives.
IPA consistently improves over off-the-shelf language models on multiple challenging text generation tasks including toxicity reduction and lexically constrained generation.
Tailoring GPT-2 with IPA can outperform GPT-3.
Tailoring GPT-3 with IPA outperforms GPT-3 and sometimes GPT-4.
KnowTuning trains large language models to identify difficult fine-grained knowledge in answers.
KnowTuning trains large language models to distinguish between reliable and unreliable knowledge in terms of completeness, factuality, and logicality.
KnowTuning generates more facts with reduced factual errors in fine-grained facts evaluation.
The CLIP model in isolation is prone to object hallucinations.
The object hallucination problem in CLIP is not solely due to the interaction between vision and language modalities.
Counterfactual data augmentation creates negative samples with a variety of hallucination issues to mitigate object hallucinations.
Counterfactual data augmentation mitigates object hallucinations in the CLIP model.
Using a CLIP model enhanced by counterfactual data augmentation as a visual encoder alleviates object hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models.
DISCERN interprets systematic biases in text classifiers using language explanations.
DISCERN iteratively generates natural language descriptions of systematic errors using an interactive loop between two large language models.
Language explanations of systematic errors improve text classifiers by augmenting training sets with synthetically generated instances or via active learning.
Language explanations of systematic errors induce greater performance improvements in text classifiers than using exemplars of systematic bias.
Language explanations of systematic errors enable more effective and efficient interpretation of systematic biases by users compared to cluster exemplars.
Increasing the number of demonstrations in in-context learning disperses model attention from the query, hindering the model's understanding of key content.
FocusICL uses token-level triviality filtering to avoid attention diversion by unimportant contents in demonstrations.
FocusICL uses demonstration-level hierarchical attention to ensure sufficient attention towards the current query.
FocusICL uses an efficient hyperparameter searching strategy based on model perplexity of demonstrations.
FocusICL scales well with many-shot demonstrations.
A framework incorporates causal mediation analysis to measure direct effects of interventions on model bias and indirect effects mediated through different model components in vision-language models.
Image features contribute more to bias than text features in vision-language models on the MSCOCO and PASCAL-SENTENCE datasets.
The image encoder contributes more to bias than the text encoder and the deep fusion encoder in vision-language models.
Contributions from language and vision modalities to bias in vision-language models are aligned and non-conflicting.
Blurring gender representations within the image encoder reduces bias with minimal performance loss or increased computational demands on the MSCOCO and PASCAL-SENTENCE datasets.
MMTE provides human evaluation metrics for assessing Metaphorical Equivalence, Emotion, Authenticity, and Quality in machine translation of figurative language.
MMTE includes a multilingual parallel metaphor corpus generated by post-editing.
Translations of figurative expressions in machine translation display different traits from translations of literal expressions.
Order-based alignment methods are unified into a single framework.
Order-based alignment methods are inefficient in utilizing reward values.
Value-based Calibration aligns Large Language Models with human preferences.
Value-based Calibration outperforms existing alignment methods on AI assistant and summarization datasets.
Value-based Calibration exhibits generalizability, robustness, and diversity in different settings.
HetLoRA allows heterogeneous LoRA ranks across clients based on individual system resources.
HetLoRA applies rank self-pruning locally for data-aware aggregation and distribution of LoRA modules.
HetLoRA uses sparsity-weighted aggregation at the server for data-aware aggregation and distribution of LoRA modules.
HetLoRA combines the advantages of high and low-rank LoRAs to improve convergence speed and final performance over homogeneous LoRA.
HetLoRA has enhanced computation and communication efficiency compared to full fine-tuning.
Iterative retrieval enables retrievers to make iterative decisions through policy optimization.
Iterative retrieval uses a reinforcement learning training procedure that incorporates feedback from large language models.
Iterative retrieval adds a small number of parameters to an off-the-shelf dense retriever to create a stateful iterative retriever.
Iterative retrieval outperforms previous methods in selecting in-context learning exemplars for semantic parsing tasks.
Iterative retrieval generalizes across different inference large language models beyond the large language model used during training.
Whiteboard-of-Thought prompting unlocks the visual reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models across modalities.
Whiteboard-of-Thought prompting provides multimodal large language models with a metaphorical whiteboard to draw reasoning steps as images and then processes these images.
Whiteboard-of-Thought prompting requires no demonstrations or specialized modules and leverages models' existing ability to write code with libraries like Matplotlib and Turtle.
Whiteboard-of-Thought prompting achieves state-of-the-art performance on four difficult natural language tasks involving visual and spatial reasoning.
Whiteboard-of-Thought prompting achieves high accuracy in settings where GPT-4o with chain-of-thought fails completely.
Social Bias Probing probes social biases in language models by assessing disparate treatment based on sensitive demographic group affiliation.
SoFa evaluates social biases in language models using a diverse range of identities and stereotypes beyond binary comparisons.
Language models encode social biases that are more nuanced and broader in scope than previously recognized.
Different religious identities lead to the most pronounced disparate treatments in language models.
Language models mirror real-life adversities faced by women and people with disabilities.
Large language models make basic mistakes in reasoning involving conditionals and epistemic modals.
Zero-shot chain-of-thought prompting reduces errors in conditional and epistemic modal reasoning by large language models.
The best performing large language models make basic errors in epistemic modal reasoning.
The best performing large language models display logically inconsistent judgments across inference patterns involving conditionals and epistemic modals.
The best performing large language models give answers about complex conditional inferences that do not match reported human judgments.
G-MoEfication converts pretrained dense language models to Mixture-of-Experts models without requiring ReLU-based activation sparsity.
G-MoEfication is effective for multitask, multilingual, fine-tuning, and zero-shot evaluation.
Classifiers identify and annotate hate-speech counter-response strategies for use in misinformation scenarios.
Hate-speech counter-response strategies are applicable to misinformation.
An LLM-based argument graph framework generates persuasive responses by using hate-speech counter-response strategies as control codes to adjust style and content.
An LLM-based argument graph framework generates expert-like responses that are more engaging, natural, and factual than existing alternatives.
Attention allocation patterns in decoder-only Transformer-based models stay consistent across most layers.

There is a clear correlation between the L2 norm of key embeddings and attention scores over cached KV pairs, such that a low L2 norm of a key embedding leads to a high attention score during decoding.
The influence of a KV pair is potentially determined by the key embedding itself before being queried.
L2 norm-based KV cache compression reduces the KV cache size without losing accuracy on language modeling, needle-in-a-haystack, and passkey retrieval tasks.
L2 norm-based KV cache compression remains compatible with FlashAttention because it does not rely on attention scores.
LLaMA-2 achieves accurate predictions of dynamical system time series without fine-tuning or prompt engineering.
LLaMA-2 exhibits an in-context neural scaling law for dynamical system time series prediction, characterized by increasing accuracy with context window length.
A flexible and efficient algorithm extracts probability density functions of multi-digit numbers directly from language models.
Text2Chart31 is a dataset for chart generation that includes 31 unique plot types from the Matplotlib library and tuples of descriptions, code, data tables, and plots.
Reinforcement learning-based instruction tuning for chart generation does not require human feedback.
Reinforcement learning-based instruction tuning for chart generation enables smaller models to outperform larger open-source models in data visualization tasks.
Reinforcement learning-based instruction tuning for chart generation enables smaller models to be comparable to state-of-the-art proprietary models in data visualization tasks.
GradSim reveals when and why updated knowledge ripples in language models.
GradSim is computed as the cosine similarity between gradients of the original fact and its related knowledge.
Ripple effect performance correlates with GradSim across different language models, knowledge editing methods, and evaluation metrics.
Negation, Over-Ripple, and Multi-Lingual failure cases of ripple effects are associated with low GradSim.
FoodieQA is a manually curated, fine-grained image-text dataset that captures the intricate features of food cultures across various regions in China.
FoodieQA provides three multiple-choice question-answering tasks based on multiple images, a single image, or text-only descriptions.
Large language models surpass human accuracy in text-based question answering on FoodieQA.
Open-sourced vision-language models underperform human accuracy on visual question answering tasks in FoodieQA.
Closed-weights vision-language models perform closer to human accuracy on visual question answering tasks in FoodieQA.
PALM optimizes the feature space of the text encoder branch.
PALM achieves greater training efficiency than prompt learning methods that operate in the input space.
PALM achieves comparable or better performance than baseline methods in few-shot audio recognition with lower computational demand.
Two simple perturbations in popular pretrained language models control for different types of information to enable contrasts for brain alignment.
Improvements in alignment between language models and brain recordings are due to more than improvements in next-word prediction and word-level information.
Understanding Pun with Image Explanations (UNPIE) is a benchmark for evaluating multimodal models on lexical ambiguity resolution in puns using images that explain both meanings.
Understanding Pun with Image Explanations (UNPIE) includes the Pun Grounding, Disambiguation, and Reconstruction evaluation tasks.
Socratic Models and Visual-Language Models show greater performance gains over text-only models in lexical ambiguity resolution as task complexity increases.
In few- and zero-shot Topic-Dependent Argument Mining, fine-tuning is mandatory for acceptable model performance.
In few- and zero-shot Topic-Dependent Argument Mining, carefully composed training samples maintain near-maximum model performance with reduced training sample size.
A new Topic-Dependent Argument Mining dataset is published for benchmarking.

Contrastively trained vision-language models exhibit a composition attribution issue where attribution scores for relations and attributes are substantially lower than for object terms, causing deficient compositional reasoning.
Composition Attribution Enhancement incorporates interpretable attribution methods to increase attention to composition words for relationships and attributes in text for vision-language models.
Composition Attribution Enhancement enables vision-language models to adjust and rectify text attribution.
Composition Attribution Enhancement enhances the ability of vision-language models to discern intricate details and construct sophisticated interpretations of visio-linguistic elements.
CompAct uses an active strategy to condense retrieved documents for question answering without losing key information.
CompAct improves multi-hop question answering performance and document compression rate.
CompAct operates as a cost-efficient plug-in module with various off-the-shelf retrievers and readers.
The Structured Context Hypothesis states that speakers modulate information rate based on location within a hierarchically-structured discourse model.
Hierarchical discourse structure is a significant predictor of information contours in discourse.
Deeply nested hierarchical discourse structure is more predictive of information contours than shallow hierarchical discourse structure.
A comprehensive taxonomy for memes categorizes memes along three dimensions: forms, functions, and topics.
Order bias in dialogue aspect-based sentiment quadruple analysis is caused by the gap between ideal and actual training objectives.
The Multi-Granularity Denoising Generation model (MGDG) achieves word-level denoising in dialogue aspect-based sentiment quadruple analysis via sequence labeling.
The Multi-Granularity Denoising Generation model (MGDG) achieves utterance-level denoising in dialogue aspect-based sentiment quadruple analysis via topic-aware dialogue segmentation.

Denoised Attention in the Multi-Granularity Denoising Generation model (MGDG) integrates multi-grained denoising information to generate denoised output for dialogue aspect-based sentiment quadruple analysis.

Segmentation-aided Order Bias Mitigation (SOBM) utilizes dialogue segmentation to supplement order diversity, concurrently mitigating the one-to-many learning challenge and order bias in dialogue aspect-based sentiment quadruple analysis.
SADD achieves state-of-the-art performance for dialogue aspect-based sentiment quadruple extraction.
A regression framework for Semantic Textual Similarity represents semantic similarity as a continuous value.
Translated ReLU is a loss function for training Semantic Textual Similarity models in a regression setting.
Smooth K2 Loss is a loss function for training Semantic Textual Similarity models in a regression setting.
A regression framework with Translated ReLU and Smooth K2 Loss enables further optimization of contrastive learning pre-trained models.
A dataset of press releases and corresponding news articles for analyzing journalistic coverage of press releases.
A method to identify news articles that challenge and contextualize press releases.
Human-written news articles that challenge and contextualize press releases to a greater extent take more creative angles and use more informational sources.
LLMs show better alignment with humans for angle recommendations than for informational source recommendations.
LLMs suggest angles and informational sources that are less creative than those suggested by humans.
The Chinese-English cross-cultural recipe retrieval benchmark contains manually annotated, culturally adapted, and textually diverse recipe retrieval samples.
CARROT is a plug-and-play cultural-aware recipe information retrieval framework that incorporates cultural-aware query rewriting and re-ranking methods.
CARROT improves preservation of the original recipe and cultural appropriateness for the target culture.
Unown automatically generates training instances for fact-checking systems from textual and tabular content.
Unown generates supporting and refuting claims with advanced negation artifacts.
Unown accommodates various strategies for evidence selection and claim generation.
MMFC is a multi-modal fact-checking dataset.
Unown examples are of comparable quality to expert-labeled data for fact-checking.
SportsGen synthesizes basketball game narratives.
SportsGen enables rigorous evaluation of large language model reasoning for basketball narratives under varying narrative lengths and information density.
Most large language models frequently fail to accurately aggregate basketball scores due to frequent scoring patterns.
Open-source large language models suffer from significant score hallucinations in basketball narrative analysis.
Reasoning effectiveness in basketball narrative analysis is influenced by narrative complexity, information density, and domain-specific terms.
Green/red lists for watermarking are split based on context to be semantically balanced.
Watermark keys are generated using locally sensitive hashing on context semantics to resist paraphrasing attacks.
Bias in watermarking is dynamically adapted to balance text quality and robustness.
A semantically balanced green/red list reduces text quality degradation in watermarked text.
Tree-of-Lens (ToL) agent uses a novel ToL grounding mechanism for the Screen Point-and-Read (ScreenPR) task.
Tree-of-Lens (ToL) agent constructs a Hierarchical Layout Tree from a point coordinate and a GUI screenshot.
Tree-of-Lens (ToL) agent comprehends the content of the indicated area and articulates the layout and spatial relationships between elements.
ScreenPR benchmark includes GUIs from mobile, web, and operating systems for the Screen Point-and-Read task.
Tree-of-Lens (ToL) agent identifies incorrect actions in mobile GUI navigation execution trajectories.
The Psychological Depth Scale (PDS) measures an LLM's ability to produce authentic and narratively complex stories that provoke emotion, empathy, and engagement.
Humans can consistently evaluate stories based on the Psychological Depth Scale (PDS).
The Mixture-of-Personas (MoP) prompting strategy automates the Psychological Depth Scale (PDS).
GPT-4 stories are as deep as or surpass highly-rated human-written stories from Reddit in psychological depth.
Large language models are over-sensitive to lexical variations in task instructions, even when the variations are imperceptible to humans.
COPLE optimizes prompts lexically through iterative combinatorial optimization using a search strategy based on word influence and feedback from proxy tasks.
Widely-used human-crafted prompts for current benchmarks suffer from the lexical sensitivity of large language models.
COPLE recovers the declined model ability in instruction-following and downstream task solving.
Performance gaps exist in multimodal, multilingual retrieval between training on native German captions and training on machine-translated or human-translated English-to-German captions.
Caption augmentation strategies reduce performance gaps in multimodal, multilingual retrieval when training on machine-translated or human-translated English-to-German captions.
Budget-aware evaluation compares reasoning strategies by accounting for both performance metrics and computational cost.
The apparent superiority of complex reasoning strategies over simpler baselines is often due to larger computational resources rather than algorithmic ingenuity.
Chain-of-thought self-consistency with comparable compute resources frequently outperforms complex reasoning strategies.
Multi-agent debate and Reflexion can degrade in performance when more compute budget is utilized, unlike self-consistency.
The Final-over-Final Condition holds in corpora of typologically diverse mixed head direction languages.
Gemini Pro respects the Final-over-Final Condition in German, Russian, Hungarian, and Serbian.
Gemini Pro does not respect the Final-over-Final Condition in Basque.
A consistent and theoretically grounded approach to annotating decompositional entailment provides a clear protocol for determining valid decompositional entailment.
The Recognizing Decompositional Textual Entailment dataset has higher internal consistency than prior decompositional entailment datasets.

Training an entailment classifier on the Recognizing Decompositional Textual Entailment dataset via knowledge distillation and employing it in an entailment tree reasoning engine improves accuracy and proof quality.
The reversal curse in large language models is influenced by knowledge clarity, entity correlation modeling, and pairwise relationship reasoning capability.
