PORE augments training samples with entity order-reversal and semantically preserved question-answer pairs to enhance bidirectional entity correlation modeling.
PORE employs entity-interleaved pairwise relationship data to elevate relationship reasoning capability.
PORE constructs high-clarity data using knowledge clarity to improve the recall of reverse relationships.
Two new datasets evaluate the reversal curse in large language models in data-sufficient and data-constrained situations.
The acceptance rate of draft tokens in language model inference is context-dependent.
Context-aware dynamic draft trees model the context-dependence of draft token acceptance rates in speculative sampling.
Context-aware dynamic draft trees provide higher speedup than static draft trees for language model inference.
Context-aware dynamic draft trees preserve the distribution of the generated text in language model inference.
The Big Five Inventory shows satisfactory reliability when applied to large language models.
Large language models can represent different personalities using specific prompt instructions.
Latent Concept Attribution (LACOAT) uses a training-derived latent space that reflects context-specific facets of words.
Latent Concept Attribution (LACOAT) maps the representations of salient input words into a training-derived latent space to generate context-based explanations for NLP model predictions.
Test sets of popular NLI datasets are categorized into three difficulty levels by exploiting training dynamics.
Test sets of popular NLI datasets categorized into three difficulty levels by exploiting training dynamics exhibit reduced spurious correlation measures.
Examples in test sets of popular NLI datasets labeled as highest difficulty show decreased performance.
Examples in test sets of popular NLI datasets labeled as highest difficulty encompass more realistic and diverse linguistic phenomena.

Training on a subset of the training set of popular NLI datasets selected by NLI characterization by exploiting training dynamics achieves performance comparable to training on the full dataset and surpasses other dataset characterization techniques.
Vision Token Merging reduces lengthy vision feature sequences in chart understanding by gradually merging most similar vision tokens.
TinyChart achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple chart understanding benchmarks.
TinyChart outperforms chart-understanding multimodal large language models with up to 13B parameters and GPT-4V on ChartQA.
TinyChart has higher throughput during inference than larger chart-understanding models.
FOOL is a coarse-grained word sense disambiguation dataset with two test sets for typical scenarios and two test sets for adversarial contexts with opposing meanings.
Language models exhibit reduced performance on word sense disambiguation tasks in adversarial contexts compared to typical contexts.
T5-large and BERT-large achieve higher performance than GPT-4o on word sense disambiguation tasks with artificial adversarial contexts.
Language models struggle to disambiguate homonyms in artificial and realistic adversarial contexts despite strong performance on regular word sense disambiguation tasks.
Adaptive Axes recovers social stereotypes from the contexts surrounding targeted entities.
Adaptive Axes uses adaptive semantic axes enhanced by large language models.
Adaptive Axes captures in-domain framing of social stereotypes.
Adaptive Axes tracks social stereotypes over time for in-domain texts.
Adaptive Axes tracks social stereotypes along domain-specific semantic axes for in-domain texts.
RealVul is the first LLM-based framework for PHP vulnerability detection.
RealVul uses improved code sampling methods and normalization techniques to isolate potential vulnerability triggers and streamline PHP code by eliminating unnecessary semantic information.
RealVul uses improved data synthesis methods to generate PHP vulnerability samples.
RealVul improves vulnerability detection effectiveness and generalization compared to existing methods.
An American Sign Language fingerspelling tokenizer discretizes fingerspelling sign sequences into tokens.
A loss function preserves both semantic and visual information of the signal in American Sign Language fingerspelling tokens.
A curated dataset evaluates copyright compliance in LLM text generation from multiple aspects and tests safeguard bypassing attacks.
A lightweight, real-time defense mechanism prevents the generation of copyrighted text in LLMs by refusing malicious requests.
Current LLMs frequently output copyrighted text.
Jailbreaking attacks increase the volume of copyrighted text generated by LLMs.
Substantial portions of the intermediate matrices for speculative sampling can be computed concurrently.
Distributing speculative sampling workload across multiple GPU threads enables simultaneous operations on matrix segments within thread blocks.
Distributing speculative sampling workload across multiple GPU threads improves profiling time without compromising accuracy.
Probability distributions for speculative sampling are approximated using sigmoid instead of softmax.
Approximating softmax with sigmoid for speculative sampling improves profiling time with only a minor decline in accuracy.
InfiniPot is a KV cache control framework that enables pre-trained large language models to manage extensive sequences within fixed memory constraints without requiring additional training.

Continual Context Distillation (CCD) is an iterative process that compresses and retains essential information using novel importance metrics to maintain critical data without access to future context.
InfiniPot outperforms models trained for long contexts in various natural language processing tasks.
Free-form analogies aid student language models in understanding scientific concepts.
Student language models improve scientific question answering performance by generating analogies.
Student language models can use analogies for self-learning new knowledge.

Instruction-tuning Large Language Models on question translation data without annotated answers encourages alignment between English and multiple languages, including languages not seen during instruction-tuning.
OpenLLaMA-3B trained with RLHF exhibits a pronounced alignment tax in NLP tasks.
Model averaging by interpolating between pre-RLHF and post-RLHF model weights achieves the strongest alignment-forgetting Pareto front among competing methods.
Model averaging enhances the alignment-forgetting Pareto front by increasing feature diversity in transformer layers with overlapping feature spaces.
Averaging low-level transformer layers mitigates the alignment tax.
Heterogeneous Model Averaging (HMA) heterogeneously combines transformer layers with different combination ratios to maximize alignment performance while minimizing alignment tax.
Social media posts contain stigmatizing language related to people who use substances.
Social media posts contain directed stigma towards people who use substances.
A computational framework analyzes stigma in online conversations about substance use.
A computational framework de-stigmatizes online conversations about substance use using Informed and Stylized LLMs.
Online conversations about substance use contain linguistic features that propagate stigma towards people who use substances.
A framework for investigating fine-tuning large language models for classification includes generation-based and encoding-based approaches.
Re3-Sci2.0 is a large-scale dataset of scientific document revisions with labeled edits for edit intent classification.
Belief-R tests language models' belief revision ability when presented with new evidence.
The delta reasoning framework assesses language models in belief revision tasks based on human suppression of prior inferences.
Language models generally struggle to appropriately revise their beliefs in response to new information.
Models adept at belief revision often underperform in scenarios without necessary updates.
18 open-weight and closed-source LLMs exhibit a political preference for Biden over Trump in the context of the 2024 U.S. presidential election.

Instruction-tuned and reinforced LLMs exhibit a more pronounced political preference for Biden compared to their base versions when responding to political questions about the 2024 U.S. presidential election nominees.
Interaction with LLMs reduces support for Trump among some Trump supporters even when the LLMs are not instructed to persuade.
Many U.S. registered voters express a desire for further interaction with LLMs on political subjects.
The symbol grounding problem originates from the computational theory of mind.
The symbol grounding problem arises with natural language only when questionable theories of meaning are presupposed.
The symbol grounding problem applies to large language models only when they are interpreted as relying on a language of thought or adopting questionable theories of meaning.
The symbol grounding problem does not apply to large language models.
Community-contributed scientific leaderboards are often incomplete and sometimes contain incorrect information.
SciLead is a manually-curated dataset of scientific leaderboards that overcomes incompleteness and incorrect information.
Three experimental settings for leaderboard construction simulate real-world scenarios with fully defined, partially defined, or undefined TDM triples.
An LLM-based framework constructs scientific leaderboards for settings with fully defined, partially defined, or undefined TDM triples.
Various large language models correctly identify TDM triples but struggle to extract result values from publications.
OneNet performs few-shot entity linking via Large Language Model prompting without fine-tuning.
An entity reduction processor uses Large Language Model prompting to summarize and filter out irrelevant entities for input simplification in entity linking.
A dual-perspective entity linker uses Large Language Model prompting to combine contextual cues and prior knowledge for precise entity linking.
An entity consensus judger uses Large Language Model prompting and a consistency algorithm to alleviate hallucination in entity linking reasoning.
OneNet outperforms state-of-the-art entity linking methods on seven benchmark datasets.
DMAN dynamically extracts token-level attribution scores using Integrated Gradients for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
DMAN aggregates attribution representations from multiple semantic granularities in natural language.
DMAN integrates attribution scores with syntactic information to capture relationships between aspects and their relevant contexts.
EXPLORA estimates parameters of a scoring function that evaluates static exemplar subsets for complex reasoning tasks without incorporating confidence information.
EXPLORA reduces the number of LLM calls required for static exemplar subset selection.
EXPLORA improves performance on complex reasoning tasks.
Existing evaluations of model memory have limitations.
The forgetting curve measures the memorization capability of long-context models.
The forgetting curve is robust to the tested corpus and experimental settings, does not rely on prompt, and can be applied to any model size.
Transformer extension techniques are effective for memorization in long-context models.
The effective length of RNN/SSM based models for memorization is questionable.
MirrorStories is a corpus of personalized short stories integrating name, gender, age, ethnicity, reader interest, and story moral.
Human evaluators can identify identity elements in LLM-generated personalized stories.
Personalized LLM-generated stories have higher engagement than generic human-written stories and generic LLM-generated stories.
Personalized LLM-generated stories have higher textual diversity while preserving the intended moral.
Some language models map uncertainty expressions to probabilistic responses in a human-like manner.
Language models exhibit systematically different behavior in mapping uncertainty expressions based on the truth value of the statement.
Language models are more susceptible to bias from prior knowledge than humans when interpreting uncertainty expressions.
TopViewRS evaluates top-view spatial reasoning by disentangling abilities such as object recognition and understanding relative positions.
Vision-language models exhibit a large performance gap compared to humans on top-view spatial reasoning tasks.
Vision-language models sometimes perform below random baseline on top-view spatial reasoning tasks.
Chain-of-Thought reasoning improves vision-language model performance on top-view spatial reasoning tasks.

Multilingual Cross-market Product-based Question Answering (MCPQA) provides answers to product-related questions in a main marketplace by utilizing information from another resource-rich auxiliary marketplace in a multilingual context.
A large-scale multilingual benchmark for cross-market product-based question answering spans 17 marketplaces across 11 languages.
Incorporating cross-market information improves performance in multilingual product-related question answering.
Tree of Problems (ToP) is a simpler alternative to Tree of Thoughts (ToT) for complex tasks that can be divided into identical subtasks.
Tree of Problems (ToP) outperforms Tree of Thoughts (ToT) and Graph of Thoughts (GoT) on complex reasoning tasks.
Tree of Problems (ToP) outperforms Chain-of-Thought (CoT) on complex reasoning tasks.
Architectures and pre-training techniques of scientific large language models are connected across scientific fields and data modalities.
FAC2E dissociates language-related capabilities from cognition-related capabilities in the evaluation of large language models.
FAC2E breaks down the application of a capability into recalling relevant knowledge, utilizing knowledge, and solving problems.
FAC2E evaluates each sub-step of each fine-grained capability to provide a two-faceted diagnosis for large language models.
Large language models exhibit a common shortfall in knowledge utilization.
A knowledge-enhanced method mitigates the shortfall in knowledge utilization in large language models.
Contrastive self-supervised learning makes dependency parsing models robust to word order variations.
Contrastive self-supervised learning improves dependency parsing performance on relatively free word order languages.
Large language models are inherently more familiar with responses generated by large language models than with human-generated responses.
Large language models exhibit lower perplexity for responses generated by large language models than for human-generated responses before fine-tuning.
Large language models' familiarity with responses generated by large language models significantly impacts learning performance during fine-tuning.
Fine-tuning with responses generated by large language models helps large language models maintain capabilities in other reasoning tasks after task-specific fine-tuning.
BiasAlert detects social bias in open-text generations of large language models.
BiasAlert integrates external human knowledge with inherent reasoning capabilities for reliable bias detection.
BiasAlert outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in social bias detection.
BiasAlert enables reliable large language model fairness evaluation in various scenarios.
BiasAlert supports bias mitigation in large language models across various scenarios.
CliMedBench uses multiple expert-guided core clinical scenarios to assess the medical ability of Large Language Models in clinical scenarios across multiple pivot dimensions.
CliMedBench comprises questions derived from real-world medical reports of top-tier tertiary hospitals and authentic examination exercises.
Chinese medical Large Language Models underperform in medical reasoning and factual consistency in clinical scenarios.
Some general-domain Large Language Models show potential in clinical scenarios.
Limited input capacity hinders the practical use of medical Large Language Models in clinical scenarios.
In-Dialogue Learning (IDL) fine-tunes pre-trained large language models to characterize persona using dialogue history for personalized dialogue generation without pre-defined profiles.
In-Dialogue Learning (IDL) improves the quality of generated responses in personalized dialogue.
In-Dialogue Learning (IDL) produces higher-quality responses in human evaluations for personalized dialogue.
WikiAtomic is a dataset for analyzing how large language models allocate knowledge between local context and global parameters when answering open-ended questions in knowledge-consistent scenarios.
Large language models consistently rely on both contextual knowledge and parametric knowledge when answering open-ended questions in knowledge-consistent scenarios.
Increasing context size reduces hallucinations in large language models when answering open-ended questions in knowledge-consistent scenarios.
Advancements in in-context learning interpretation and analysis are organized from theoretical and empirical perspectives.
A repository of resources for in-context learning interpretation and analysis is established.
Open questions and challenges in in-context learning interpretation and analysis are identified.
LitSearch is a retrieval benchmark with 597 realistic literature search queries about recent ML and NLP papers.
LitSearch queries are created using GPT-4 generation from paragraphs with inline citations and author-written questions about recently published papers, with expert validation.
BM25 underperforms state-of-the-art dense retrievers on LitSearch.
LLM-based reranking strategies improve the best-performing dense retriever on LitSearch.
Commercial search engines perform poorly on LitSearch relative to the best dense retriever.
TRoTR evaluates the diachronic change of context in text reuse using topic relatedness.
TRiC evaluates topic relatedness between pairs of recontextualizations.
TRaC evaluates overall topic variation within sets of recontextualizations.
The TRoTR benchmark provides human-annotated biblical text reuse for topic relatedness.
Established SBERT models exhibit greater sensitivity to textual similarity than topic relatedness in recontextualization evaluation.
Fine-tuning established SBERT models reduces their sensitivity to textual similarity relative to topic relatedness in recontextualization evaluation.
OPPU stores user-specific behavior patterns and preferences using personalized parameter-efficient fine-tuning modules.
OPPU enables individual ownership and use of large language models by plugging in personal PEFT parameters.
OPPU integrates parametric user knowledge from personal PEFT parameters with non-parametric knowledge from retrieval and profiles to adapt large language models to user behavior shifts.
OPPU outperforms existing prompt-based methods on the LaMP benchmark.
IPR provides detailed step-by-step guidance for agent training.
IPR uses the Monte Carlo method to estimate step-level rewards.
IPR generates contrastive action pairs by comparing agent actions with expert trajectory steps using step-level rewards.
IPR outperforms strong baselines on three complex agent tasks.
IPR augments action efficiency in agent tasks.
False premise hallucination is the phenomenon in which large language models generate hallucinated text when confronted with false premise questions.
False premise hallucinations in large language models are caused by a small subset of attention heads that disturb the knowledge extraction process.
FAITH mitigates false premise hallucinations in large language models by constraining false premise attention heads during inference.
Retrieval-augmented retrieval (RAR) uses both examples and documentation in a two-step method to select relevant examples and documentation for code generation in low-resource programming languages.
Retrieval-augmented retrieval (RAR) outperforms example retrieval and grammar retrieval in code generation for low-resource programming languages.
When used for example selection, retrieval-augmented retrieval (RAR) selects better examples than example retrieval.
When used for documentation selection, retrieval-augmented retrieval (RAR) selects better documentation than grammar retrieval.
TV-TREES is the first multimodal entailment tree generator.

TV-TREES promotes interpretable joint-modality reasoning by searching for trees of entailment relationships between simple text-video evidence and higher-level conclusions that prove question-answer pairs.
The task of multimodal entailment tree generation evaluates reasoning quality.
TV-TREES achieves interpretable, state-of-the-art zero-shot performance for video question answering using full video clips.
CorrSynth generates more diverse data that is faithful to the prompt using a correlated sampling strategy.
CorrSynth overcomes the complexity drawbacks of classifier-based guidance and similar guidance-based techniques.
CorrSynth improves data diversity compared to competitive baselines.
Lookback Lens detects contextual hallucinations in large language models by computing the ratio of attention weights on context versus generated tokens.
Lookback Lens is as effective as detectors using entire hidden states or text-based entailment for detecting contextual hallucinations.
Lookback Lens transfers across tasks and different large language models without retraining.
Classifier-guided decoding using Lookback Lens reduces contextual hallucinations in large language models.
MTA4DPR transfers knowledge from teacher models to student models using multiple teaching assistants in an iterative process.
In each iteration of MTA4DPR, the student model learns from teaching assistants that are more performant and from more difficult data.
MTA4DPR produces student models that achieve state-of-the-art performance among models of the same size on multiple datasets.
MTA4DPR produces student models that are competitive with larger DPR models, including those based on large language models.
The Term Frequency model detects AI-generated peer-reviews by identifying token repetition patterns.
The Review Regeneration model detects AI-generated peer-reviews by measuring output similarity upon re-prompting.
A defensive strategy reduces the effect of paraphrasing attacks on the Term Frequency model and the Review Regeneration model.
The Review Regeneration model is more robust to attacks than the Term Frequency model.
The Term Frequency model performs better than the Review Regeneration model in the absence of attacks.
The Term Frequency model and the Review Regeneration model outperform existing AI text detectors in detecting AI-generated peer-reviews.
SEEKR performs knowledge retention in continual learning of large language models through selective attention guidance.
SEEKR performs attention distillation on selected attention heads for finer-grained knowledge retention.
SEEKR identifies the most valuable attention heads for knowledge retention using forgettability-based and task-sensitivity-based measures.
SEEKR achieves comparable or better performance in continual learning of large language models with significantly less replayed data than existing methods.
EO-IUR uses editing operation labels generated by a sequence labeling module to guide the generation model to focus on critical tokens.
A token-level heterogeneous graph represents dialogues.
A two-dimensional utterance augmentation strategy uses editing operation-based incomplete utterance augmentation and LLM-based historical utterance augmentation.
EO-IUR outperforms previous state-of-the-art baselines in open-domain and task-oriented dialogue.
Hierarchical contrastive learning brings vector representations of related CWEs closer together in code vulnerability type classification.
Mixing self-supervised contrastive learning loss into the loss function addresses class collapse and enhances model robustness in code vulnerability type classification.
Max-pooling enables the model to handle longer vulnerability code inputs in code vulnerability type classification.
Code vulnerability type classification using hierarchical contrastive learning outperforms state-of-the-art methods, with greater improvements on higher-quality datasets.
VTG uses evolving long short-term memory to retain both valuable documents and recent documents.
VTG uses a two-tier verifier equipped with an evidence finder to rethink and reflect on the relationship between claims and citations.
VTG employs active retrieval and diverse query generation to enhance the precision and breadth of retrieved documents.
VTG outperforms baseline methods on multiple knowledge-intensive tasks.
QUITE is a question answering dataset for real-world Bayesian reasoning scenarios with categorical random variables and complex relationships.
QUITE provides natural language verbalizations of premises and evidence statements for Bayesian reasoning, with answers as estimated probabilities.
Logic-based models outperform out-of-the-box large language models on causal, evidential, and explaining-away reasoning types.
Instruction-tuned Large Vision-Language Models deteriorate in classification performance for fine-grained visual categorization tasks.
Instruction-tuned Large Vision-Language Models struggle to generate descriptive visual attributes for concepts present in input images.
Instruction-tuned Large Vision-Language Models suffer from a modality gap between textual and visual inputs for the same concept.
Finer is a multiple granularity attribute-centric benchmark for evaluating Large Vision-Language Models' fine-grained visual comprehension ability.
Finer is a training mixture that provides improved explainability for Large Vision-Language Models' fine-grained visual comprehension.
Intent understanding is defined as a natural language reasoning task about how a customer uses a product, independent of product ontologies.
FolkScope's property-ambiguity limits its ability to align user intents with products having the most desirable property.
FolkScope's category-rigidity limits its ability to recommend useful products across diverse categories.
The Product Recovery Benchmark evaluates E-Commerce intent understanding systems for category-rigidity and property-ambiguity.
Word Usage Graph annotations are checked for robustness and correctness by continuing the SemEval annotation algorithm for two more rounds and comparing against an established annotation paradigm.
The reproducibility of Word Usage Graph annotations is tested by resampling a new, smaller set of word uses from the SemEval source corpora and annotating them.
DataTales evaluates language models' ability to transform financial tabular data into accessible narratives requiring analysis of large datasets and understanding of specialized terminology.
Language models struggle to achieve the precision and analytical depth required for transforming tabular data into accessible narratives.
Nested Named Entity Recognition-tailored instructions enhance nested Named Entity Recognition performance.
Output format critically influences nested Named Entity Recognition performance.
Existing nested Named Entity Recognition methodologies are less effective when used with Large Language Models.
Label information and descriptions of nested cases are crucial for Large Language Model capabilities in nested Named Entity Recognition in specific domains.
Nested Named Entity Recognition methods using Large Language Models with tailored instructions do not outperform BERT-based models.
Concept Induction is an unsupervised task that learns a soft clustering among words to define concepts directly from data and generalizes Word Sense Induction.
A bi-level approach for Concept Induction leverages a local lemma-centric view and a global cross-lexicon view to induce concepts.
The local lemma-centric view and the global cross-lexicon view are mutually beneficial for inducing concepts and word senses.
Static embeddings represent concepts induced by Concept Induction.
Current automatic metrics for translation evaluation are insensitive to nuanced differences in translation quality.

Current automatic metrics for translation evaluation are most insensitive to nuanced differences in translation quality when translation quality is high and the variance among alternative translations is low.
Current automatic metrics for translation evaluation often overestimate or underestimate translation quality.
MTLS is a pre-training method that improves multilingual capability by making texts into linguistic symbols.
MTLS replaces the vocabulary in pre-trained language models by mapping relations between linguistic symbols and semantics.
MTLS uses universal semantics as bridges to link symbols from different languages to the model's embedding space.
MTLS improves multilingual capabilities with minimal English pre-training data.
TimeR4 integrates relevant temporal knowledge from Temporal Knowledge Graphs into Large Language Models for Temporal Knowledge Graph Question Answering.
TimeR4 uses a retrieve-rewrite module that rewrites questions using background knowledge from Temporal Knowledge Graphs to acquire explicit time constraints.
TimeR4 uses a retrieve-rerank module that retrieves semantically and temporally relevant facts from Temporal Knowledge Graphs and reranks them according to temporal constraints.
TimeR4 fine-tunes a retriever with a contrastive time-aware learning framework.
RE2 facilitates bidirectional encoding in unidirectional decoder-only Large Language Models by using the first pass to provide global information for the second pass.
RE2 is compatible with most thought-eliciting prompting methods, including Chain-of-Thought.
RE2 can be integrated with different Large Language Models.
RE2 can be integrated with ensemble strategies.
StructKGC is a contrastive learning framework for knowledge graph completion that accommodates diverse topologies in knowledge graphs.
StructKGC employs Vertex-level, Neighbor-level, Path-level, and Relation composition level contrastive learning tasks for knowledge graph completion.
Synergistic training of structure-aware contrastive learning tasks during pre-trained language model fine-tuning enables a nuanced capture of subgraph semantics in knowledge graphs.
Structure-aware contrastive learning tasks mutually reinforce each other in knowledge graph completion.

Pretrained large language models prefer formal texts and texts with fewer spelling errors in data with conflicting knowledge, resulting in faster learning and more favorable treatment of knowledge when resolving conflicts.

The preference of pretrained large language models for formal texts and texts with fewer spelling errors in data with conflicting knowledge is generalizable across models and languages and is more pronounced in larger models.
Pretrained large language models trust data that is consistent with the majority of the pretraining data.
Manipulating the consistency of data with the majority pretraining data can instill new learning preferences and erase old ones in pretrained large language models.
CoRE enriches the representation of query images and class prototypes by retrieving relevant textual information from large web-crawled databases.
CoRE performs zero-shot low-resource image classification without training.
A benchmark for low-resource image classification covers medical imaging, rare plants, and circuits.
CoRE outperforms state-of-the-art methods that rely on synthetic data generation and model fine-tuning.
Counterfactual distillation automatically generates high-quality counterfactual data by changing the task label of an input example while preserving similarity to the original input.
Counterfactual distillation uses multi-view chain-of-thought to enhance the diversity of reasoning samples.
Counterfactual distillation enhances the reasoning capabilities of small language models.
Counterfactual distillation improves robustness to out-of-distribution examples.
The OATH-Frames typology characterizes online attitudes towards homelessness with nine hierarchical frames capturing critiques, responses, and perceptions.
The OATH-Frames annotations dataset for online attitudes towards homelessness incorporates varying degrees of language model assistance.
Language model assistance in annotating online attitudes towards homelessness significantly reduces annotation time with minimal performance loss compared to domain experts.
Modeling OATH-Frames provides more value than existing sentiment and toxicity classifiers for analyzing online attitudes towards homelessness.
Analysis of online posts about homelessness using OATH-Frames reveals trends in attitudes across states, time periods, and vulnerable populations.
An unsupervised setting for task-oriented dialogue requires only a well-defined API schema and unlabeled dialogues.
Expectation-maximization infers turn-level annotations as latent variables using a noisy channel model to build end-to-end task-oriented dialogue agents.

An unsupervised end-to-end task-oriented dialogue system built with expectation-maximization and a noisy channel model achieves a higher dialogue success rate than a GPT-3.5 baseline on the MultiWOZ benchmark.
Continual pre-training for constructing language models for new languages converges faster and saves computational resources in a scalable manner.
Continual pre-training for constructing language models for new languages adheres to an extended scaling law with a joint data-parameter scaling term.
In continual pre-training for constructing language models for new languages, the compute-optimal data-parameter allocation depends on scaling factors.
In continual pre-training for constructing language models for new languages, the effectiveness of transfer scale is influenced by training duration and linguistic properties.
In continual pre-training for constructing language models for new languages, the effectiveness of transfer scale is robust to data replaying.
Grouping words by segmentation properties compares model performance on linguistic tasks across different segmentation groups.
Segmentation consistency predicts large language models' ability to recognize and generate lemmas from inflected forms.
Many natural-language prompts have corresponding 'evil twins' that are unintelligible to humans but provably elicit similar behavior in language models.
Evil twins transfer between language models.
Solving a maximum-likelihood problem finds evil twins.
DistilRoBERTa shows limited comprehension of slang when paraphrasing climate-related tweets from Nigeria and the UK.
ChatGPT 4 and Gemini show comparable effectiveness when paraphrasing slang in climate-related tweets from Nigeria and the UK.
LLaMA3 shows less coverage when paraphrasing slang in climate-related tweets from Nigeria and the UK.
ChatGPT 4, Gemini, and LLaMA3 exhibit limitations in slang coverage, especially for Nigerian slang, when paraphrasing climate-related tweets from Nigeria and the UK.
A pipeline generates basic geometry problems with aligned text and images using GPT-4 and GPT-4V.
GeoGPT4V is a dataset of geometry problems that includes generated geometry problems with text-image alignment.
The GeoGPT4V dataset improves geometry problem-solving performance on MathVista and MathVision benchmarks.
The retrieval-and-demonstration approach improves rare word translation accuracy in direct speech translation.
The retrieval-and-demonstration approach adapts direct speech translation models to incorporate retrieved examples for rare word translation by prepending examples, similar to in-context learning.
The retrieval-and-demonstration approach uses a cross-modal retriever (speech-to-speech, speech-to-text, text-to-text) to locate suitable examples for rare word translation.
The speech-to-speech retrieval approach outperforms other modalities for rare word translation in direct speech translation.
The speech-to-speech retrieval approach exhibits higher robustness to unseen speakers.
SemDI captures semantic dependencies within the context using a unified encoder to identify causal relations between events.
SemDI uses a Cloze Analyzer to generate a fill-in token based on comprehensive context understanding to identify causal relations between events.
SemDI uses a fill-in token to inquire about the causal relation between two events.
SemDI surpasses state-of-the-art methods for identifying causal relations between events on multiple benchmarks.
Cross-lingual knowledge aggregation reduces performance disparity across languages for large language models.
In cross-lingual knowledge aggregation for large language models, each component significantly contributes to reducing performance disparity across languages.
HEART delineates elements of narrative style that lead to empathy with the narrator of a story.
LLMs using HEART produce human-level annotations of narrative elements.
A dataset of empathy judgments for personal stories is created via a large-scale crowdsourcing study.
Vividness of emotions and plot volume elucidate the pathways by which narrative style cultivates empathy towards personal stories.
LLM-based multi-agent collective decision-making systems exhibit a severe lack of diversity, relying heavily on dictatorial and plurality voting.
GEDI is an electoral collective decision-making module that incorporates various ordinal preferential voting mechanisms for LLM-based multi-agent systems.
Integrating certain collective decision-making methods improves the reasoning capabilities and robustness of leading large language models without requiring intricate system designs.
Some collective decision-making mechanisms generate positive synergies with only three agents.
Voting-based collective decision-making methods are robust against single points of failure and exhibit diversity in hit-rate@k and subject-wise impacts.
Mentor-KD uses an intermediate-sized task-specific fine-tuned model to augment Chain-of-Thought annotations during reasoning distillation.
Mentor-KD uses an intermediate-sized task-specific fine-tuned model to provide soft labels for the student model during reasoning distillation.
Transformers trained on both form and meaning favor hierarchical syntactic generalization more than Transformers trained on form alone.
Semantic training signals promote hierarchical syntactic generalization in Transformers.
GTHM constructs a coarse-to-fine visual view tree that organizes visual objects, attributes, and relationships hierarchically.
GTHM models the visual-token matching process on a coarse-to-fine visual view tree as a cooperative game.
GTHM defines the Tree-based Shapley Value to assess the contribution of each visual view and determine optimal visual granularity.
GTHM uses the Tree-based Shapley Value to implement adaptive weight contrastive decoding for vision-aware decoding.
GTHM alleviates visual hallucinations across different large vision-language model families without additional training or post-processing.
Short universal adversarial phrases deceive judge-LLMs into predicting inflated scores.
Transferred short universal adversarial phrases cause judge-LLMs to predict maximum scores for any assessed text.
A surrogate attack transfers adversarial phrases from a surrogate model to unknown judge-LLMs.
A practical algorithm determines short universal adversarial phrases.
Judge-LLMs are more susceptible to adversarial attacks in absolute scoring than in comparative assessment.
Lack of context and unfamiliarity with difficult concepts is a major reason for adult readers’ difficulty with domain-specific text.
Targeted concept simplification is a simplification task for rewriting text to help readers comprehend text containing unfamiliar concepts.
WikiDomains is a dataset of definitions from academic domains paired with a difficult concept within each definition.
Human judges preferred explanations about the difficult concept more than simplifications of the concept phrase.
No single model achieved superior performance across all quality dimensions.
Automated metrics show low correlations with human evaluations of concept simplification.
Relevance-aware Adaptive Learning (ReAL) overcomes in-domain and cross-domain distractions in end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
Relevance-aware Adaptive Learning (ReAL) uses a top-k adaptive contrastive loss for pre-training the retriever in end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
Relevance-aware Adaptive Learning (ReAL) utilizes divergence-driven feedback from a frozen generator to pre-train the retriever in end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
Relevance-aware Adaptive Learning (ReAL) uses the metric score distribution as an anchor to align retrieval with generation in end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
Performance-Guided Knowledge Distillation (PGKD) distills the knowledge of Large Language Models into smaller, task-specific models via teacher-student Knowledge Distillation.

Performance-Guided Knowledge Distillation (PGKD) establishes an active learning routine between the student model and the Large Language Model that uses hard-negative mining, student model validation performance, and early-stopping protocols to generate new training data.
Performance-Guided Knowledge Distillation (PGKD) addresses training challenges for highly multi-class, sparsely annotated datasets in industrial text classification.
Performance-Guided Knowledge Distillation (PGKD) outperforms traditional BERT-base models and other knowledge distillation methods on multi-class classification tasks.
Models fine-tuned with Performance-Guided Knowledge Distillation (PGKD) are faster and less expensive than Large Language Models for text classification inference.
LLM-Evolve extends established benchmarks to sequential problem-solving settings.
LLM-Evolve evaluates LLMs over multiple rounds and builds a demonstration memory from feedback for models to query in future tasks.
LLMs achieve performance improvements by learning from past interactions.
The quality of retrieval algorithms and feedback significantly influences LLMs' performance improvement when learning from past interactions.

Most-similar retrieval augmented generation, denoising steps, and auto-correction with syntax error feedback in coordination yield autoformalization results that are syntactically, terminologically, and semantically more consistent.
Most-similar retrieval augmented generation, denoising steps, and auto-correction with syntax error feedback in coordination are applicable across different large language models.
Mimicking persona-based attributes in large language models leads to annotation variability in hate detection.
Incorporating geographical signals in large language models leads to better regional alignment in hate detection.
Large language models are sensitive to numerical anchors in hate detection.
Three new multimodal language models predict reading comprehension for a single participant, single question, and single paragraph from eye movements.
The task of predicting reading comprehension for a single participant, single question, and single paragraph from eye movements is highly challenging.
Benchmarking against a strong text-only baseline is important for predicting reading comprehension for a single participant, single question, and single paragraph from eye movements.

In some cases, eye movements provide limited improvements over a text-only baseline for predicting reading comprehension for a single participant, single question, and single paragraph from eye movements.
An infrastructure enables further progress on predicting reading comprehension for a single participant, single question, and single paragraph from eye movements.
Simul-MuST-C uses minimal chunks generated by a large language model to align target text with source text in simultaneous multilingual speech translation.
Segmented-based monotonicity in simultaneous speech translation training data yields the best quality-latency trade-off for grammatically distant language pairs.
A context window extension strategy minimizes disturbance between rotary angle distributions to maintain consistency with the pre-training phase.
The perturbation of rotary angle distribution caused by length extension is analyzed.
Distributional disturbance in rotary angle distributions is reduced for context window extensions of LLaMA2 to 8k and 16k compared to baseline methods.
Performance on the LongBench-E benchmark is improved compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.
Performance on the Hugging Face Open LLM benchmark is maintained after context window extension.
Safety Arithmetic is a training-free framework for enhancing large language model safety for Base models, Supervised fine-tuned models, and Edited models.
Harm Direction Removal avoids harmful content in large language models.
Safety Alignment promotes safe responses in large language models.
NoIntentEdit is a dataset of edit instances that could compromise large language model safety if used unintentionally.
Safety Arithmetic improves safety measures for large language models while reducing over-safety and maintaining model utility.
Auto Evol-Instruct evolves instruction datasets using large language models without any human effort.
Auto Evol-Instruct automatically analyzes and summarizes suitable evolutionary strategies for instruction data.
Auto Evol-Instruct iteratively improves the evolving method based on issues exposed during instruction evolution.
Auto Evol-Instruct produces a method that outperforms human-designed methods on multiple benchmarks.
Models fine-tuned on short-completion data exhibit a statistically significant negative quadratic relationship between prompt loss token weight and performance.
Models fine-tuned on short-completion data with small prompt loss token weights outperform models fine-tuned on long-completion data on multiple-choice and short-generation benchmarks.
For models fine-tuned on short-completion data, large prompt loss token weights improve performance on long-generation benchmarks.
A diverse description generation framework leverages Large Language Models to combine and refine information from web-based image-text pairs, synthetic captions, and detection tags.
RWKV-CLIP is the first RWKV-driven vision-language representation learning model combining the effective parallel training of transformers with the efficient inference of RNNs.
RWKV-CLIP is a robust vision-language representation learner across different model scales and pre-training datasets.
RWKV-CLIP achieves state-of-the-art performance on linear probing, zero-shot classification, and zero-shot image-text retrieval tasks.
The definition-only zero-shot relation extraction setting trains relation extraction models using only relation definitions expressed in natural language.
REPaL generates initial seed instances from relation definitions and an unlabeled corpus using large language models.
REPaL fine-tunes a bidirectional Small Language Model with initial seed instances to learn relations for the target domain.

REPaL expands pattern coverage and mitigates bias from initial seeds by integrating feedback from the Small Language Model's predictions and synthesis history using multi-turn conversation of large language models.
Definition-oriented seed synthesis enhances pattern coverage in zero-shot relation extraction.
Indiscriminately increasing seed quantity leads to performance saturation in zero-shot relation extraction.
A unique taxonomy divides puzzles into rule-based and rule-less categories.
LLMs face significant challenges in complex puzzle scenarios.
LLMs exhibit a disparity with human-like reasoning in puzzles requiring advanced logical inference.
PerSE infers specific preferences from an in-context personal profile and evaluates the alignment between generated content and personal preferences.
PerSE provides detailed comments and fine-grained scoring to enhance interpretability.
PerSE achieves higher Kendall correlation and accuracy than GPT-4 with zero-shot reviewers.
PerSE demonstrates transferability to new domains.
CryptoTrade combines the analysis of on-chain and off-chain data for cryptocurrency trading.
CryptoTrade uses a reflective mechanism to refine daily trading decisions by analyzing prior trading outcomes.
A benchmark for cryptocurrency trading strategies is established.
CryptoTrade achieves higher returns than time-series baselines across various cryptocurrencies and market conditions.
CryptoTrade does not achieve higher returns than traditional trading signals across various cryptocurrencies and market conditions.
Self-contrast enhances encoder-based text classifiers for fine-grained emotion classification.
Self-contrast is a form of linear extrapolation.
Linear Layer Extrapolation dynamically adjusts the contrastive strength based on the selected intermediate layer.
Linear Layer Extrapolation outperforms standard classification techniques in fine-grained emotion classification tasks.
Optimizing reward models for truthfulness on popular truthfulness datasets tends to result in a left-leaning political bias.
Existing open-source reward models trained on standard human preference datasets exhibit a left-leaning political bias.
The left-leaning political bias in existing open-source reward models increases with model size.
SaySelf teaches LLMs to express fine-grained confidence estimates.
SaySelf generates self-reflective rationales by using an LLM to summarize uncertainties via natural language based on inconsistency in multiple sampled reasoning chains.
SaySelf calibrates confidence estimates using reinforcement learning with a reward function that motivates accurate high-confidence predictions and penalizes overconfidence in erroneous outputs.
SaySelf reduces confidence calibration error and maintains task performance.
Self-reflective rationales generated by SaySelf are reasonable and contribute to confidence calibration.
HiCon-EG organizes arguments hierarchically to explore entailment relations at diverse concept levels.
HiCon-EG enhances scalability and efficiency in acquiring common-sense knowledge by leveraging statistical language distribution instead of manual labeling.
Entailment relations derived from HiCon-EG bolster abstract detection tasks.
CoEvol is an LLM-based multi-agent cooperation framework for improving responses in instruction fine-tuning.
CoEvol uses an iterative debate-advise-edit-judge paradigm to refine responses.

ATAP is the first commonsense knowledge graph completion framework that utilizes automatically generated continuous prompt templates combined with pre-trained language models.
ATAP uses a new prompt template training strategy that guides pre-trained language models to generate optimal prompt templates for commonsense knowledge graph completion tasks.

ATAP achieves state-of-the-art performance on commonsense knowledge graph completion benchmark datasets.
Reconstruction techniques reduce reconstruction errors in large language model pruning.
Minimizing reconstruction error in large language model pruning can overfit calibration data, leading to increased language perplexity and poor downstream task performance.
Self-generating calibration data mitigates the trade-off between reconstruction error and generalization in large language model pruning.
Counterfactual examples with small perturbations on target-domain data can be used for bias detection without relying on templates or specific datasets.
Classifiers for sentiment, emotion, hate speech, and offensive text exhibit positive biases related to the language spoken in a country.

Affect predictions correlate with pre-trained language models' likelihood in English and in unknown languages such as Basque and Maori.

Pruning via Merging uses manifold learning and the Information Bottleneck measure to merge similar layers, reducing model size while preserving model performance.

Context-aware auto-prompting tailors slot queries to the current dialogue context, increasing flexibility and reducing ambiguities.

A hypothesis-testing framework assesses whether large language models possess genuine reasoning abilities or primarily depend on token bias in logical reasoning tasks.

Most large language models struggle with logical reasoning.
The success of large language models on logical reasoning tasks largely depends on recognizing superficial patterns with strong token bias.
CHESS assigns a unique threshold to each activation channel in the feed-forward network layers.

CHESS implements sparse kernels to accelerate LLM inference.
CHESS achieves lower performance degradation on downstream tasks while activating fewer parameters than existing methods and speeds up LLM inference.
TokenTune approximates gradient computation by backpropagating through a subset of input tokens during the backward pass.
TokenTune caches only a subset of intermediate activations during the forward pass.
TokenTune can be combined with existing memory-efficient fine-tuning methods to further reduce memory cost.
TokenTune achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning and representative memory-efficient fine-tuning methods while reducing memory footprint.
LLoCO learns long contexts offline through context compression and in-domain parameter-efficient finetuning with LoRA.

MIPD is a corpus of Polish web articles containing disinformation.

KELLER incorporates professional legal knowledge about crimes and law articles to guide large language models in reformulating legal cases into concise sub-facts of crimes containing the essential information of the case.

Datasets for natural language processing in Computational Social Science exhibit varying levels of data duplication.

LLM agents tend to conform to the model's inherent social biases when simulating political debates from assigned political perspectives.

Large language models struggle in certain settings for Complex Word Identification, lexical complexity prediction, and multi-word expression complexity evaluation within multilingual and multidomain setups.

Bio-RFX uses sentence-level relation classification before entity extraction to tackle entity ambiguity.
Bio-RFX exploits structural constraints between entities and relations to guide the hypothesis space.

A new pretraining scheme reduces reliance on punctuation in sentence segmentation.

Architectural modifications improve speed in sentence segmentation.

A multilingual sentence segmentation model trained on diverse sentence-segmented data acts as a drop-in replacement for existing segmentation tools.

Partitioning the parameters of original Feed-Forward Networks in a dense large language model into multiple experts defines the experts for a Mixture-of-Experts model.

The Self-Bootstrapped Visual-Language Model selects key knowledge retrieved by Dense Passage Retrieval and answers questions.

A sequence labeling classifier with a dynamic sliding window predicts sentence spans and segments long text sequences on the fly in clinical record narratives.

RevMUX enables parameter-efficient data multiplexing for LLM batch inference.
RevMUX incorporates a reversible design in the multiplexer.
RevMUX reuses the multiplexer's reversible design in the demultiplexer to restore individual samples for classification.
RevMUX enhances LLM inference efficiency while retaining classification performance.
A large learning rate when preparing an initialization checkpoint and a complete learning rate decay process during continual pre-training are crucial for version updates of Large Language Models.
Learning rate path switching uses one main path for pre-training with maximal learning rate and multiple branching paths for updates with newly-added training data.
Learning rate path switching reduces training cost while maintaining pre-training performance for version updates of Large Language Models.
VLEU uses Large Language Models to sample diverse textual inputs covering the entire range of potential Text-to-Image prompts.
VLEU measures Text-to-Image model generalizability by computing the Kullback-Leibler divergence between the visual text marginal distribution and the conditional distribution over generated images.
RoSE self-improves as it answers reasoning questions.
RoSE stores answered reasoning questions and their reasoning steps in a streaming experience pool.
RoSE sorts experience pool questions by similarity to the current question, divides them into buckets, and extracts one question per bucket to ensure diversity.
RoSE selects questions with low uncertainty and high complexity from each bucket to maximize assistance in answering new questions.
RoSE achieves high performance on complex reasoning tasks without human-provided demonstrations or pre-set unlabeled data.
ScalingFilter evaluates text quality using the perplexity difference between two language models trained on the same data, eliminating the influence of a reference dataset.
ScalingFilter is equivalent to an inverse utilization of scaling laws.
ScalingFilter improves zero-shot performance of pre-trained models in downstream tasks.
Semantic diversity is a metric that uses text embedding models for semantic representations to measure bias introduced by quality filtering.
Semantic diversity is a reliable indicator of dataset diversity.
ScalingFilter achieves an optimal balance between downstream performance and semantic diversity.
CoBa manages task convergence balance with minimal computational overhead for multitask fine-tuning of large language models.

CoBa dynamically adjusts task weights using Relative Convergence Scores, Absolute Convergence Scores, and Divergence Factor to balance task convergence and mitigate individual task divergence during multitask fine-tuning of large language models.
CoBa fosters equilibrium in task improvement during multitask fine-tuning of large language models.
CoBa enhances large language models' performance in multitask fine-tuning relative to baselines.
Q-Spatial Bench is a manually annotated benchmark for quantitative spatial reasoning.
Reasoning about distances between objects is particularly challenging for state-of-the-art vision-language models.
The top-performing vision-language model achieves higher success rates when a reasoning path using a reference object emerges naturally in the response.

A conflict-based data collection method utilizes inter-conflict cues in multi-person communications.

Quantum RNNs for text classification use Parametrised Quantum Circuits as cells.
Quantum RNNs for text classification use the quantum state from the previous time-step as the hidden state.

A methodological workflow computationally identifies community practices in social media texts.

A dataset of political tweets in the U.S. context is labeled for impolite style and intolerant substance.

Preference-Guided Reflective Sampling (PRS) uses a tree-based generation framework for efficient sampling.

Limited access to language model generations undoes the effects of watermarking schemes designed to be robust to paraphrasing.

TruthQuest benchmarks suppositional reasoning based on the principles of knights and knaves puzzles.

Weight scaling as reparameterization (WeSaR) stabilizes and accelerates pre-training of large language models.

Mixture-of-Skills (MoS) is a general, model-agnostic reinforcement learning framework for automatically optimizing data usage during fine-tuning of large language models.

An early-stop mechanism reduces the inference costs of multi-pass decoding in grammatical error correction.

MORPHEUS models roles from personalized dialogue history by exploring and utilizing latent space through a three-stage training process.

Knowledge conflicts for large language models are categorized into context-memory conflict, inter-context conflict, and intra-memory conflict.
Latent language models have consistent conditional distributions by definition and support efficient generation and scoring of sequences.

ECCO evaluates program efficiency through natural language based code generation and history-based code editing.

Credibility-aware Generation (CAG) enables models to discern and process information based on its credibility.

TaylorMLP preserves the ownership of large language models by transforming their weights into Taylor-series parameters.
TaylorMLP prevents abuse of large language models by inducing low-speed token generation through increasing the terms in the Taylor-series.

Personalized Pieces (Per-Pcs) enables privacy-preserving sharing and assembly of personalized parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) for large language models through collaborative efforts.

Last token representations of named entities and multi-token words exhibit an erasure effect where information about previous and current tokens is rapidly forgotten in early layers of large language models.

A benchmark dataset for probabilistic reasoning provides question-answer pairs for estimating percentiles, drawing samples, and calculating probabilities of statistical distributions.

Loong is a long-context benchmark for extended multi-document question answering that aligns with realistic scenarios.

The Language Model Critic framework produces intermediate-step rewards during reinforcement learning training.

RoCEL models row contexts and column contexts separately to capture their distinct semantics in table entity linking.

CushionCache mitigates activation outliers in subsequent tokens when inserted as a prefix.

Dynamic Contrastive Decoding dynamically amplifies knowledge from selected documents during the generation phase.

Dynamic Contrastive Decoding applies contrastive decoding with a specialized weight calculation to adjust the final logits for sampling answer tokens.

The behavior of efficient transfer learning methods for vision-language models varies with transfer difficulty.

Model-based Preference Optimization (MPO) fine-tunes Large Language Models for abstractive summarization without human feedback.

Generative Retrieval is more adaptable to evolving knowledge than Dual Encoders.

Model architectures influence the emotion prediction performance of vision large language models in conversational contexts.

Dictionary learning extracts sparsely activated representations from dense language model embeddings in superposition for automated medical coding.

Dictionary features are human interpretable.
Dictionary features elucidate the hidden meanings of medically irrelevant tokens.
Dictionary features steer model behavior.
LLM4Decompile is the first and largest open-source LLM series (1.3B to 33B) trained for binary code decompilation.
LLM4Decompile-End decompiles binary code directly.
LLM4Decompile-End outperforms GPT-4o and Ghidra on HumanEval and ExeBench benchmarks in re-executability rate.
LLM4Decompile-Ref refines decompiled code from Ghidra.
LLM4Decompile-Ref improves re-executability rate over LLM4Decompile-End.
Post-OCR synthetic data with multiple noise levels is constructed using weak supervision.
Character Error Rate thresholds distinguish effective synthetic data from ineffective synthetic data for post-OCR correction.
Self-Correct-Noise Test-Time Adaptation combines self-correction and noise generation mechanisms.
Self-Correct-Noise Test-Time Adaptation dynamically adjusts models to test data without labels.
Self-Correct-Noise Test-Time Adaptation reduces Character Error Rate.
EFSum is an evidence-focused fact summarization framework for knowledge-augmented zero-shot question answering.
EFSum optimizes a large language model as a fact summarizer for knowledge-augmented zero-shot question answering through distillation and preference alignment.
EFSum produces helpful and faithful summaries for knowledge-augmented zero-shot question answering.
EFSum improves knowledge-augmented zero-shot question answering performance of large language models when noisy facts are retrieved.
Class-wise low variance regularization debiases language models for any protected attribute in downstream tasks without requiring protected attribute labels.
Class-wise low variance regularization outperforms debiasing baselines requiring protected attribute labels without degrading target task performance.
GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4 default to standard varieties of English.
GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4 responses to non-standard varieties of English exhibit stereotyping.
GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4 responses to non-standard varieties of English exhibit demeaning content.
GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4 responses to non-standard varieties of English exhibit lack of comprehension.
GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4 responses to non-standard varieties of English exhibit condescending responses.
A logical structure tree represents hierarchical logic flow using relation connectives as non-terminal nodes and textual arguments as terminal nodes.
A logical structure tree is constructed in an unsupervised manner guided by constituency trees and a taxonomy of connectives for ten common logical relations.
A textualized logical structure tree is used as a hard text prompt for logical fallacy reasoning in LLMs.
A relation-aware tree embedding is used as a soft prompt for logical fallacy reasoning in LLMs.
The logical structure tree improves precision and recall for logical fallacy detection and classification.
Reflection-Reinforced Self-Training (Re-ReST) uses a reflector to refine low-quality generated samples during self-training for language agents.
Using a reflector efficiently generates high-quality samples for self-training in language agent tasks.
Self-training improves performance on language agent tasks.
Re-ReST further improves performance on language agent tasks beyond self-training.
Reflection can be employed during inference without ground-truth feedback for language agents.
Direct Preference Optimization has an inherent algorithmic length reliance.

The discrepancy between sequence-level KL divergences for chosen and rejected sequences in Direct Preference Optimization results in overestimated or underestimated rewards due to varying token lengths.
Direct Preference Optimization produces biased rewards for sequences of different token lengths.
SamPO is a downsampling approach that eliminates length reliance in Direct Preference Optimization.
SamPO mitigates verbosity in Direct Preference Optimization through debaised rewards.
Large language models learn from parallel multilingual input.
Parallel multilingual input enhances large language models' input comprehension compared to conventional in-context learning.
Involving more languages in the input of large language models activates fewer neurons and leads to more focused neural activation patterns.

The neural activation pattern in large language models when multiple languages are included in the input mirrors synaptic pruning in neuroscience, demonstrating a similarity between artificial and biological brains.
A multi-objective differentiable masking scheme applied to both weights and neurons discovers knowledge-critical subnetworks in pretrained language models.
Highly sparse knowledge-critical subnetworks are critical for expressing specific collections of relational knowledge in GPT2 variants.
Removing knowledge-critical subnetworks suppresses specific collections of relational knowledge while preserving most of the model's initial abilities.
Multi-prompt decoding decodes multiple candidate generations from a prompt bank during inference.
Multi-prompt decoding produces a more diverse and higher quality candidate space than single-prompt decoding.
Multi-prompt decoding improves Minimum Bayes Risk decoding across multiple conditional generation tasks.
Multi-prompt decoding improves text generation quality across multiple tasks, models, and evaluation metrics.
A hierarchical framework decomposes complex tasks into manageable subgoals using separate LLMs for subgoal prediction and low-level action generation.
A reward model leverages multimodal environment feedback to automatically generate reward signals for unannotated datasets.
Environment Preference Optimization generates preference signals from the environment’s feedback and uses them to train LLM-based agents.
A hierarchical framework for long-horizon decision-making achieves state-of-the-art performance on the ALFRED benchmark.

A benchmark evaluates the robustness of instruction-following large language models against prompt injection attacks by assessing their ability to discern which instructions to follow and which to disregard.

Certain instruction-following large language models excessively prioritize embedded instructions in prompts by focusing on the latter parts of the prompt without fully understanding the overall context.
Instruction-following large language models with stronger contextual understanding and instruction-following capabilities tend to be more easily compromised by prompt injection attacks.
ClimRetrieve is a benchmarking dataset for information retrieval from corporate climate disclosures with question-source-answer pairs and multi-level relevance labels.
A use case integrates expert knowledge into information retrieval for corporate climate disclosures using embeddings.
Embeddings have critical limitations in knowledge-intensive downstream domains such as climate change communication.
Language models have difficulties generating fallacious and deceptive reasoning.
Language models leak honest counterparts when generating deceptive outputs and believe the counterparts to be false.
Requesting fallacious procedures for harmful behavior bypasses safeguard mechanisms in safety-aligned language models.
HaluAgent enables smaller language models to actively select tools for detecting hallucinations in text, code, and mathematical expressions.
HaluAgent integrates a language model with a multi-functional toolbox and a fine-grained three-stage detection framework that includes a memory mechanism.
HaluAgent uses synthesized detection trajectories from Chinese and English datasets for fine-tuning to enable bilingual hallucination detection.
HaluAgent achieves hallucination detection performance comparable to GPT-4 without tool enhancements on in-domain and out-of-domain datasets with minimal fine-tuning.
LoRA-Guard extracts language features from large language models and adapts them for content moderation using low-rank adapters.
LoRA-Guard uses a dual-path design to maintain language generation performance of large language models.
LoRA-Guard maintains content moderation accuracy with orders of magnitude lower parameter overhead than existing guardrail methods.
MCMS unifies multi-lingual, cross-lingual, and multi-document news summarization.
GLOBESUMM is a dataset of multilingual news reports restructured into event-centric format.
Protocol-guided prompting produces high-quality and cost-effective reference annotations.
GLOBESUMM contains conflicts between news reports, redundancies, and omissions.
RAFT is a grammar error-free black-box attack that fools LLM detectors.
RAFT preserves text quality by exploiting word-level transferability of LLM embeddings.
RAFT fools multiple LLM detectors across various domains.
RAFT attacks are realistic and indistinguishable from human-written text.
Current LLM detectors are not adversarially robust.
TRAIT performs task-oriented in-domain data augmentation.
TRAIT selects in-domain data from general corpora to enrich domain knowledge.
TRAIT generates task-oriented synthetic passages that contain guidance on using domain knowledge to answer downstream task questions.
MedAdapter is a unified post-hoc adapter for test-time adaptation of large language models towards biomedical applications.
MedAdapter adapts large language models by fine-tuning a small BERT-sized adapter to rank candidate solutions generated by large language models.
MedAdapter adapts both white-box and black-box large language models for biomedical reasoning without requiring extensive computational resources or sharing data with third parties.
MedAdapter yields enhanced performance when combined with train-time adaptation.
A user-centric data collection pipeline gathers and validates a corpus written for children and sometimes by children.
Stratified Masking dynamically adjusts masking probabilities based on child language data to prioritize vocabulary and concepts suitable for children.
KidLM understands lower grade-level text.
KidLM avoids stereotypes to maintain safety.
KidLM captures children's unique preferences.
Data Advisor is an enhanced LLM-based method for generating data that accounts for the characteristics of the desired dataset.
Data Advisor monitors the status of generated data, identifies weaknesses in the current dataset, and advises the next iteration of data generation based on pre-defined principles.
Data Advisor enhances the safety of large language models against fine-grained safety issues without sacrificing model utility.
MedReadMe is a dataset of manually annotated readability ratings and fine-grained complex span annotations for medical sentences, featuring 'Google-Easy' and 'Google-Hard' categories.
State-of-the-art sentence-level readability metrics are benchmarked, including unsupervised, supervised, and prompting-based methods using large language models.
Adding a feature that captures the number of jargon spans to existing readability formulas improves their correlation with human judgments and stability.
Fine-tuning-based unlearning methods alter the knowledge retrieval process of large language models instead of erasing the problematic knowledge embedded in the model parameters.
Fine-tuning-based unlearning mechanisms impact the global behavior of large language models, affecting unrelated knowledge or capabilities.
A framework for investigating Misinformation Oversight Bias, Gender Bias, Authority Bias, and Beauty Bias on LLM and human judges does not require groundtruth annotations.
A dataset based on the revised Bloom’s Taxonomy evaluates LLM and human judges.
Human and LLM judges are vulnerable to perturbations to various degrees when evaluating LLM performance.
Cutting-edge LLM judges possess considerable biases when evaluating LLM performance.
Attacks on LLM judges are feasible by exploiting Misinformation Oversight Bias, Gender Bias, Authority Bias, and Beauty Bias.
GAMA uses an Audio Q-Former that aggregates features from multiple layers of an audio encoder.
CompA-R is a synthetically generated instruction-tuning dataset for complex audio reasoning.
GAMA uses a soft prompt that incorporates high-level semantic evidence from event tags of the input audio during instruction-tuning for complex audio reasoning.
CompA-R-test is a human-labeled evaluation dataset for open-ended audio question-answering that requires complex reasoning.
GAMA outperforms existing Large Audio-Language Models on diverse audio understanding tasks.
GAMA achieves state-of-the-art performance on deductive reasoning benchmarks.
GAMA achieves state-of-the-art performance on hallucination evaluation benchmarks.
Multilingual ASR models exhibit gender performance gaps where the advantaged gender varies across languages and models.
Gender performance gaps in multilingual ASR models are not explained by acoustic or lexical properties.
The ease of distinguishing speaker gender in a language via probes correlates with reduced gender performance gaps in multilingual ASR models, favoring female speakers.
AgentReview disentangles the impacts of multiple latent factors in peer review.
AgentReview addresses the privacy issue in peer review analysis.
Reviewers' biases cause variation in peer review decisions.
FairFlow mitigates dataset biases by learning to be undecided in predictions for data samples or representations associated with known or unknown biases.
FairFlow uses a suite of data and model perturbation operations that generate different biased views of input samples.
FairFlow uses a contrastive objective that learns debiased and robust representations from biased views of samples.
FairFlow outperforms existing debiasing methods on out-of-domain and hard test samples without compromising in-domain performance.
In NLP and ML publications, the term 'democratization' is most frequently used to convey ease of access to or use of technologies without meaningfully engaging with theories of democratization.
In NLP and ML publications, research using invocations of 'democra*' other than the most frequent usage tends to be grounded in theories of deliberation and debate.
QGEval evaluates generated questions and automatic metrics for question generation across fluency, clarity, conciseness, relevance, consistency, answerability, and answer consistency.
The evaluation dimensions fluency, clarity, conciseness, relevance, consistency, answerability, and answer consistency are appropriate for question generation evaluation.
Most question generation models perform unsatisfactorily in terms of answerability and answer consistency.

Existing automatic metrics for question generation fail to align well with human judgments when evaluating generated questions across fluency, clarity, conciseness, relevance, consistency, answerability, and answer consistency.
Curriculum Consistency Learning (CCL) guides models to learn consistency in alignment with their current capacity to differentiate between features in conditional sentence generation tasks.
Curriculum Consistency Learning (CCL) promotes task independence across conditional sentence generation tasks.
Curriculum Consistency Learning (CCL) simplifies implementation for conditional sentence generation tasks.
Models using Curriculum Consistency Learning (CCL) are adept at managing complex instances in conditional sentence generation tasks.
ArtELingo-28 is a vision-language benchmark based on WikiArt spanning 28 languages with 140 annotations per image.
ArtELingo-28 emphasizes diversity of opinions over languages and cultures.
Cross-lingual transfer is more successful for culturally-related languages.
Standardize aligns large language models with expert-defined standards via retrieval-style in-context learning.
Integrating knowledge artifacts from expert-defined standards into the generation process enhances the standard alignment of content produced by large language models.
BiasWipe mitigates unintended bias in text classifiers.

BiasWipe prunes neuron weights responsible for unintended bias to achieve fairness without loss of original performance.
