COINCIDE selects visual instruction tuning data for efficient finetuning of Large Vision-Language Models.
COINCIDE clusters training data using internal activations from a small model to identify vision-language concept-skill compositions required by a target Large Vision-Language Model.
COINCIDE samples data from diverse clusters by considering cluster density and transferability to other concept-skill compositions.
COINCIDE achieves superior performance and data selection efficiency compared to strong baselines on multiple vision-language datasets.
Actionability is the degree to which bias measure results enable informed action in natural language processing.
The intended use and reliability assessment of bias measures in natural language processing are often unclear or absent.
Placing chain of thought sequences after target labels (instead of before) consistently improves downstream performance in model distillation.
Placing chain of thought sequences after target labels in model distillation eliminates the need for student models to generate reasoning sequences at test time.

Chain of thought sequences placed after target labels in model distillation improve performance even when they are not coherent reasoning sequences, and performance gains are robust to token permutations.
A small number of key tokens in chain of thought sequences placed after target labels in model distillation are sufficient to achieve the same improvements as full rationales.
FOCI is a multiple-choice benchmark for fine-grained object classification.
FOCI retains classification difficulty by mining negative labels with a CLIP model.
FOCI tests for a complementary skill to established image understanding and reasoning benchmarks.
CLIP models exhibit better performance than Large Vision-Language Models on fine-grained object classification.
There is inadequate alignment for fine-grained object distinction between the image encoder and the language model in Large Vision-Language Models.
Universal Document Linking links similar documents to enhance synthetic query generation across multiple datasets with different characteristics.
Universal Document Linking uses entropy to choose similarity models.
Universal Document Linking uses named entity recognition to make document link decisions using similarity scores.
Universal Document Linking achieves state-of-the-art performance in zero-shot information retrieval across diverse datasets and IR models.
Disclosure of AI assistance in writing, particularly when AI generates new content, decreases average quality ratings for argumentative essays and creative stories.
Disclosure of AI assistance in writing increases the variation in quality evaluations of the same writing among individuals.
Individual writing confidence and familiarity with AI writing assistants moderate the impact of AI assistance disclosure on writing quality evaluations.
Disclosure of AI assistance reduces the proportion of writings with AI content generation assistance among top-ranked writings.
Modular Pluralism plugs a pool of smaller specialized community language models into a base language model.
Modular Pluralism supports Overton, steerable, and distributional pluralism through distinct collaboration modes of multiple language models.
Modular Pluralism is compatible with black-box language models.
Modular Pluralism allows adding new community language models to cover underrepresented communities.
Modular Pluralism achieves Overton, steerable, and distributional pluralism across multiple language models.
Language models are generally faithful to inputs from specialized community language models.
Domain-specific neurons exist in multimodal large language models.
Language model modules in multimodal large language models process projected image features in three stages.

Manipulation of domain-specific neurons in multimodal large language models changes Visual Question Answering accuracy.

CasiMedicos-Arg includes natural language explanations for correct and incorrect diagnoses in clinical cases written by doctors.
CasiMedicos-Arg explanations are manually annotated with argument components (premises and claims) and argument relations (support and attack).
SEACrowd provides standardized corpora for nearly 1,000 Southeast Asian languages across three modalities.
SEACrowd benchmarks evaluate AI model quality for 36 Southeast Asian languages across 13 tasks.
NeuroTrialNER is a new and fully open annotated corpus for named entity recognition of neurological diseases, therapeutic interventions, and control treatments.
NeuroTrialNER comprises 1093 clinical trial summaries from ClinicalTrials.gov.

NuNER is a compact language representation model for Named Entity Recognition pre-trained using Large Language Model annotations.
The NuNER dataset is a Named Entity Recognition pre-training dataset created using Large Language Model annotations.

NuNER solves downstream Named Entity Recognition problems in a data-efficient manner, outperforming similar-sized foundation models and competing with much larger language models in the few-shot regime.
The size and entity-type diversity of the pre-training dataset are key to achieving good Named Entity Recognition performance.
Make Some Noise (MSN) is a training framework that replaces supervised fine-tuning for large language models.
Make Some Noise (MSN) introduces noise at the input to train language models on a denoising task.
Make Some Noise (MSN) enhances the parallel decoding capability of language models without affecting their original task capability.
Tree-based retrieval-augmented Jacobi (TR-Jacobi) is a decoding strategy that improves inference speed for models trained with Make Some Noise.
Make Some Noise (MSN) improves inference speed without compromising model performance in general and code domains.
Make Some Noise (MSN) achieves comparable acceleration ratios to state-of-the-art models that use additional model structure on Spec-Bench.
Certain pretraining set development techniques yield higher downstream model accuracy than others.
Web crawl snapshots are categorized by toxicity, quality, type of speech, and domain.
Refining pretraining sets with attribute information improves quality.
Bootstrapped Policy Learning (BPL) adaptively tailors a progressively challenging subgoal curriculum for each complex goal through goal shaping, ensuring a smooth knowledge transition.
Two universal curriculum patterns remain effective across different dialogue environments, independent of specific environmental constraints.

Group Steiner Trees identify the most relevant candidate entities for unsupervised named entity disambiguation in low resource domains by leveraging contextual similarities across all mentions in a document.
Unsupervised named entity disambiguation with Group Steiner Trees outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised methods on domain-specific datasets.
Modeling phonological, morphological, and lexical distance as Bayesian noise processes synthesizes artificial languages controllably distant from a high-resource language neighbour.
Performance degradation in large language models on closely-related languages and dialects is a function of noise parameters modeling phonological, morphological, and lexical distance.
Parameter posteriors for closely-related language and high-resource language neighbour pairs follow the trends computed from artificial languages.
Task performance on an unseen closely-related language is estimated from high-resource language neighbour performance using parameter posteriors.
Observed performance degradation on a closely-related language is diagnosed by its phonological, morphological, and lexical distances from its high-resource language neighbour.

Three tasks for satire comprehension are defined: Satirical Image Detection for detecting whether an image is satirical, Satirical Image Understanding for generating the reason behind an image being satirical, and Satirical Image Completion for selecting the other half of an image from two options to form a satirical image.
The ***YesBut*** dataset contains satirical and non-satirical images with diverse artistic styles, and satirical images depict a normal scenario and a conflicting scenario that is funny or ironic.
Current Vision-Language Models exhibit poor zero-shot performance on satire comprehension tasks as evaluated by the ***YesBut*** dataset according to both automated and human evaluation.
Large language models tend to satisfy lexical constraints that appear in specific positions within the input in Lexically Constrained Generation.
Control decoding parameters minimally impact large language model performance in Lexically Constrained Generation.
Large language models struggle with handling the inherent complexity of certain lexical constraints, such as compound words, in Lexically Constrained Generation.
Divide and Conquer Generation enhances large language model performance in Lexically Constrained Generation tasks for both white-box and black-box models.
Controllable preference optimization explicitly specifies preference scores for different objectives to guide model response generation.
Controllable preference optimization guides model responses to match various preferences among helpfulness, honesty, and harmlessness.
Controllable preference optimization mitigates the alignment tax in multi-objective alignment.
Training quality is imbalanced across model layers in low-data training and fine-tuning.
TempBalance balances training quality across model layers during low-data training and fine-tuning in natural language processing and scientific machine learning.
TempBalance provides greater performance improvements with less tuning data in natural language processing and scientific machine learning tasks.
Question answering with source citation in ambiguous settings, where multiple valid answers exist, involves resolving knowledge conflicts and providing source citations.
Five datasets for question answering with source citation in ambiguous settings extend existing reading comprehension datasets with citation meta-data for distractors and paraphrasing.
An ambiguous multi-hop question answering dataset with source citation features real-world, naturally occurring contexts.
Two metrics evaluate models' performance on question answering with source citation in ambiguous settings.
Rule-based, prompting, and finetuning approaches function as baselines for question answering with source citation in ambiguous settings across five large language models.
Fine-tuning causes tuning-induced mis-calibration in large language models.
Efficient Trustworthy Distillation (FIRST) utilizes a small portion of teacher's knowledge to obtain a reliable large language model in a cost-efficient way.
The concentrated knowledge phenomenon during large language model distillation reduces computational burden.
Trustworthy maximization optimizes the utilization of concentrated knowledge before transferring it to the student large language model.
FIRST achieves better accuracy and less mis-calibration across in-domain and out-of-domain scenarios for large language models.
Sentence-level perplexity relative to token-level frequency quantifies frequency bias in language models.
Syntactic Smoothing reduces frequency bias in language models by adjusting the maximum likelihood objective to distribute the learning signal to syntactically similar tokens.
Syntactic Smoothing reduces anisotropy in language model representations.
The degree of anisotropy in a language model correlates with its frequency bias.
EFUF performs gradient ascent with three tailored losses to eliminate hallucinations in multimodal large language models without paired data.
EFUF consistently reduces hallucinations in multimodal large language models while preserving generation quality with modest computational overhead.
AnaloBench benchmarks analogical reasoning in language models by requiring the recall of related experiences from large information pools.
AnaloBench benchmarks analogical reasoning in language models by applying analogical reasoning to complex and lengthy scenarios.
AnaloBench uses the largest collection of human-written analogies to date.

Scaling language models offers minimal gains in analogical reasoning for recalling relevant scenarios from large information pools.
QSalience predicts the salience of inquisitive questions.
A new dataset provides linguist-annotated salience scores for (context, question) pairs.
Highly salient questions are more likely to be answered in the same article.

An end-user-focused iterative debiasing framework applies System 2 thinking processes to prompts to induce logical, reflective, and critical text generation via single, multi-step, instruction, and role-based variants.
System 2-based Implicative Prompts produce language model outputs with lower mean bias and competitive performance on downstream tasks compared to other prompting techniques.
Document Screenshot Embedding (DSE) uses document screenshots as a unified input format for document retrieval.
Document Screenshot Embedding (DSE) does not require content extraction preprocessing.
Document Screenshot Embedding (DSE) preserves all information in a document (text, image and layout).
Document Screenshot Embedding (DSE) uses a large vision-language model to encode document screenshots into dense representations.

In mixed-modality slide retrieval, Document Screenshot Embedding (DSE) achieves better retrieval performance than OCR text retrieval methods.
The first crowdsourced dataset for detecting abusive sentences depicting identity groups as deviating from the norm includes 7 identity groups.
Scoring-aware Multi-reward Reinforcement Learning (SaMRL) uses QWK-based rewards with a mean-squared error penalty for multi-trait automated essay scoring.
Autoregressive score generation leverages token generation probabilities for robust multi-trait score predictions in automated essay scoring.
Scoring-aware Multi-reward Reinforcement Learning (SaMRL) enhances scoring of previously inferior prompts in multi-trait automated essay scoring.
SLNAG autonomously integrates novel data and assesses LLMs' ability to comprehend emerging concepts.

FOCUS uses causal inference to form precise and contextually relevant connections between newly emerging expressions and their meanings by leveraging real-world linguistic shifts as contextual beacons.
FOCUS achieves higher precision and relevance than baseline methods for comprehending Internet slang and memes.
Annotation alignment is the extent to which LLMs and humans agree when annotating the safety of user-chatbot conversations.
GPT-4 correlates more strongly with average human safety ratings than the median human annotator does.
Larger datasets are needed to determine whether GPT-4 exhibits disparities in correlation strength across different demographic groups.
There is substantial idiosyncratic variation in correlation within demographic groups, indicating that race and gender do not fully capture differences in alignment.
GPT-4 cannot predict when one demographic group finds a conversation more unsafe than another.
DEEP is an end-to-end large language model framework for detecting factual errors in text summarization.
DEEP uses a diverse set of LLM prompts to identify factual inconsistencies, treating their outputs as binary features for ensembling models.
DEEP calibrates ensembled models to produce empirically accurate probabilities of factual consistency.

DEEP achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple benchmarks for detecting factual errors in text summaries.
PriORE performs topic-oriented open relation extraction in a zero-shot manner.

PriORE reduces randomness in generative open relation extraction by converting it to a more robust relation classification task.
PriORE improves topic-oriented open relation extraction in factualness, topic relevance, informativeness, coverage, and uniformity, especially on specialized and narrow topics.
Video-LLaVA unifies visual representation into the language feature space.
Video-LLaVA learns from a mixed dataset of images and videos, mutually enhancing image and video understanding.
Video-LLaVA mutually benefits images and videos within a unified visual representation.
Video-LLaVA outperforms models specialized for images or videos.
A speech-based preference dataset is collected by human listening to response pairs.
Prompting strategies based on radio-industry best practices increase the speech-suitability of instruction-tuned language models.

Manospheric authors on Reddit have a distinct linguistic style characterized by uncivil language and a greater focus on gendered topics.

Dataset diversity has a greater impact than training data volume on the cross-task, cross-domain, and in-domain generalization performance of a truthfulness hyperplane.
Claude 3.5 Sonnet fails to solve most Connections games.
Human players solve more Connections games than Claude 3.5 Sonnet.
A taxonomy describes the knowledge types required for clustering and categorizing words in the Connections game.

A speech-specific risk taxonomy covers hostility (malicious sarcasm and threats), malicious imitation (by age, gender, ethnicity), and stereotypical biases (by age, gender, ethnicity).
A small-scale dataset evaluates Large Multimodal Models' capability to detect 8 speech-specific risk categories.

Confidence Aware Label Smoothing (CALS) iteratively updates the label smoothing parameter based on preference labels and model forecasts.

MOSEL provides 950,000 hours of speech data for the 24 official EU languages under open-source compliant licenses.
MOSEL provides automatic transcripts for 441,000 hours of unlabeled speech data under the CC-BY license.
GPT-3.5-Turbo and Llama 3-70B-Instruct prefer hiring candidates with White female-sounding names over other demographic groups across 40 occupations.

SignCLIP projects spoken language text and sign language videos into the same space.

SignCLIP enables text-to-video and video-to-text retrieval for sign language in the in-domain setting.

A benchmark assesses large language models' awareness of how much they know by evaluating their ability to recall the precise amount of required information for specific topics.

A dataset for multilingual FActScore evaluation contains texts generated by strong multilingual LLMs.

Using Wikipedia as the knowledge source may hinder the true FActScore of long-form text in medium- and low-resource languages due to limited coverage.

A framework for literature review table generation decomposes the task into separate schema and value generation steps using language models.
arxivDIGESTables is a dataset of 2,228 literature review tables extracted from ArXiv papers that synthesize 7,542 research papers.
DecontextEval is an automatic evaluation method that aligns table elements with the same underlying aspects despite differing surface forms.
Literature review table generation benefits from additional context to ground the generation, such as table captions and in-text references.
Generated novel aspects from language models can be useful even when the models fail to fully reconstruct a reference table.
Refining and integrating cognitive and noncognitive aspects constructs student profiles for personality-aware student simulation in language learning.
Large language models produce diverse student responses according to language ability and personality traits in language learning simulations.

Otter inserts extra parameters into the transformer architecture to predict calibration signals such as rewards alongside tokens.

Otter integrates with existing inference engines with only a one-line code change.

New evaluation methods and metrics for pun understanding in large language models are designed for the in-context learning paradigm.
New evaluation metrics for pun understanding in large language models are designed to align with human cognition.

ImageInWords is a human-in-the-loop framework for curating hyper-detailed image descriptions.

Fine-tuning with IIW data improves comprehensiveness, specificity, and reduces hallucinations compared to models trained with prior work, even with only 9k samples.
Descriptions generated by IIW models produce the highest fidelity images in text-to-image generation.
IIW models boost compositional reasoning on ARO, SVO-Probes, and Winoground.
IIW-Eval is a benchmark containing human judgement labels, object and image-level annotations, and existing image caption datasets enriched by IIW models.
A fine-grained and unified definition of causality involves interactions between humans and objects.
CELLO is a dataset of causal questions spanning the four levels of causality: discovery, association, intervention, and counterfactual.
CELLO includes explicit causal graphs that detail interactions between humans and objects.
Current large vision-language models struggle with causal reasoning tasks.
CELLO-CoT is a causally inspired chain-of-thought prompting strategy for causal reasoning.
Some popular knowledge graph embedding models suffer from Z-paradox, a deficiency in expressiveness.
MQuinE does not suffer from Z-paradox.
MQuinE has strong expressiveness to model symmetric, asymmetric, inverse, 1-to-N, N-to-1, N-to-N, and composition relations with theoretical justification.
Z-paradox degrades the performance of existing knowledge graph embedding models on some challenging test samples.
MQuinE mitigates the negative impact of Z-paradox on link prediction tasks.
MQuinE outperforms existing knowledge graph embedding models on link prediction tasks.
Middleware is a class of tools that aids in proactive exploration within massive environments.
Middleware shields large language models from environmental complexity.
Middleware improves GPT-4's performance in knowledge base tasks and database tasks.
The survey 'The Odyssey of Commonsense Causality' covers taxonomies, benchmarks, acquisition methods, qualitative reasoning, and quantitative measurements in commonsense causality.
Incomplete Knowledge Graph Question Answering is a task where the provided knowledge graph lacks some factual triples required for each question.
Incomplete Knowledge Graph Question Answering datasets evaluate the ability of large language models to integrate internal and external knowledge.

Generate-on-Graph (GoG) is a training-free method for incomplete knowledge graph question answering that uses a Thinking-Searching-Generating framework to treat the large language model as both agent and knowledge graph while generating new factual triples.
Story embeddings represent fictional stories such that reformulations of the same story yield similar embeddings.
Story embeddings achieve state-of-the-art performance on multiple retrieval tasks.
Story embeddings' computational notion of narrative similarity aligns with human perception.

LLoVi decomposes the short- and long-range modeling aspects of long-range video question-answering into two stages: generating textual descriptions of short video clips densely sampled from a long input video and aggregating these descriptions with a large language model to answer questions.
LLoVi uses a multi-round summarization prompt that first summarizes noisy short-term visual captions and then answers a given question.
The choice of visual captioner and large language model is critical for long-range video question-answering performance.
LLoVi is extended to grounded VideoQA, which requires both question answering and temporal localization.
Translation-specific understanding and general understanding are misaligned in large language models.
DUAT performs cross-lingual interpretation for difficult-to-translate words and enhances translation with the generated interpretations.
DUAT uses reframed external tools for detecting difficult words and generating interpretations.
Challenge-WMT is a benchmark for evaluating translation on samples prone to mistranslation.
DUAT improves translation quality and reduces translation literalness.
An evaluation framework enables simulating social interactions with LLMs in settings without information asymmetry and with information asymmetry.
LLMs perform better in settings without information asymmetry than in settings with information asymmetry.
Addressing information asymmetry is a fundamental challenge for LLM-based agents.
LawBench uses 20 tasks to assess Large Language Models' ability to perform Chinese legal tasks.
LawBench assesses Large Language Models' legal capabilities at three cognitive levels corresponding to Bloom's cognitive taxonomy.
The first comparative analysis of Large Language Models' performance on Chinese legal tasks reveals their relative strengths and weaknesses.
VideoQuestions is a dataset of YouTube videos with manually annotated entity-centric information-seeking questions.

ECIS-VQG generates entity-centric information-seeking questions from videos using Transformers, rich context signals (titles, transcripts, captions, embeddings), and a combination of cross-entropy and contrastive loss.
In low-rank adaptation, Fast Forward repeats the most recent optimizer step until the loss stops improving on a tiny validation set.
In low-rank adaptation, Fast Forward reduces FLOPs without compromising model performance.
ConsisEval evaluates hard-to-easy inconsistency using question pairs with a strict order of difficulty.
Consistency score quantitatively measures hard-to-easy inconsistency.
Large language models can fail at easier problems even when they solve harder ones.
GPT-4 exhibits inconsistency on specific questions due to distraction by redundant information and misinterpretation of questions.
Models with stronger capabilities typically exhibit higher consistency, but exceptions exist.
Hard data enhances consistency for fine-tuning and in-context learning.
TCSinger is the first zero-shot singing voice synthesis model capable of style transfer across cross-lingual speech and singing styles and multi-level style control.
TCSinger includes a clustering style encoder that uses a clustering vector quantization model to stably condense style information into a compact latent space.
TCSinger includes a Style and Duration Language Model that concurrently predicts style information and phoneme duration, benefiting both.
TCSinger includes a style adaptive decoder that uses a mel-style adaptive normalization method to generate singing voices with enhanced details.

TCSinger improves synthesis quality, singer similarity, and style controllability in zero-shot style transfer, multi-level style control, cross-lingual style transfer, and speech-to-singing style transfer.
Targeted unlearning for LLMs unlearns only information about a specific target from unlearning documents.
Successful LLM unlearning must avoid outputting gibberish, fabricating facts about the unlearning target, and releasing factual information under jailbreak attacks.

A causal intervention framework for targeted unlearning models the knowledge of the unlearning target as a confounder between LLM input and output and the unlearning process as a deconfounding process.
A simple unlearning algorithm for targeted unlearning includes Who's Harry Potter as a special case.

The simple unlearning algorithm for targeted unlearning avoids outputting gibberish, fabricating facts about the unlearning target, and releasing factual information under jailbreak attacks without explicit optimization.
Diverse single-round and multi-round prompting schemes for zero-shot fallacy classification use task-specific instructions including extraction, summarization, and Chain-of-Thought reasoning.
Large language models under single-round prompting schemes outperform full-shot baselines in out-of-distribution fallacy classification.
Large language models under single-round prompting schemes outperform full-shot baselines in some open-domain fallacy classification tasks.
Multi-round prompting schemes yield greater improvements in zero-shot fallacy classification than single-round prompting schemes, especially for small large language models.
Visual tables are hierarchical descriptions of visual scenes that include a scene description and multiple object-centric descriptions of categories, attributes, and knowledge.
Visual tables offer explainability and controllable editing as unique properties over visual embeddings.
Visual tables deliver instance-level world knowledge and detailed attributes essential for visual reasoning.
A generator trained on small-scale annotations creates visual tables.
Visual tables outperform previous structural and text-based representations on visual reasoning benchmarks.
Visual tables enhance state-of-the-art multi-modal large language models on visual reasoning benchmarks.
EX2 generates concept descriptions that incorporate textual features important for Vision-Language Models.
EX2 aligns a large language model with Vision-Language Model preferences using reinforcement learning.
Spurious descriptions have a major role in Vision-Language Model representations despite providing no helpful information.
Vision-Language Models rely significantly on non-visual attributes like habitat to represent visual concepts.
Different Vision-Language Models prioritize different attributes in their representations.
sNeuron-TST steers large language models for text style transfer using style-specific neurons.
sNeuron-TST identifies style-specific neurons associated with source and target styles in large language models for text style transfer.
sNeuron-TST deactivates source-style-only neurons to increase the probability of target-style words in generated text for text style transfer.
Deactivating source-style-only neurons negatively impacts the fluency of generated text in text style transfer.
An improved contrastive decoding method accounts for rapid token probability shifts across layers due to deactivated source-style neurons in text style transfer.
A dataset of 1,002 stepwise math reasoning chains includes teacher annotations of the first error step.
Finding the mistake in a student solution is challenging for current large language models.
Student solution verifiers detect the first error step in math reasoning chains.
Student solution verifiers improve tutor response targeting to student errors and increase correctness while reducing hallucinations compared to existing baselines.
Training on secondary unsupervised tasks with data from the target task increases morphological inflection performance.
Secondary unsupervised tasks do not increase morphological inflection performance for specific combinations of secondary task and training setup.
Models learn from additional unsupervised tasks themselves, not just from additional data, in morphological inflection.
Five datasets derived from IIT-CDIP contain thousands of instances of sensitive personal data, including US Social Security Numbers, birth places and dates, and home addresses.
A modular data de-identification pipeline replaces sensitive personal data with synthetic, but realistic, data.
Replacing sensitive personal data with synthetic, but realistic, data preserves document utility for document understanding applications.
AttInGrad is a new explanation method for automated medical coding that is superior to previous explanation methods.
Adversarial robustness training improves explanation plausibility for automated medical coding.

An unsupervised approach produces plausible and faithful explanations of comparable or better quality to supervised approaches for automated medical coding without requiring human-annotated evidence spans.
Covert Harms and Social Threats (CHAST) is a set of seven metrics grounded in social science literature for evaluating covert harms in language model generated conversations.
Large language models generate conversations containing covert harms that express malign views in seemingly neutral language and are unlikely to be detected by existing methods.
Large language models manifest more extreme views and opinions when dealing with non-Western concepts such as caste compared to Western concepts such as race.
The least-to-most visual reasoning paradigm interleaves decomposing a question into sub-questions and invoking external tools for resolving sub-questions.
A data synthesis approach creates visual reasoning examples by dividing the complex synthesis task into simple sub-tasks and relying on open-sourced models in a bottom-up manner.
A data synthesis approach is reproducible, cost-efficient, and produces quality-guaranteed data.
A visual reasoner generally enhances the reasoning abilities of a wide range of existing vision-language models in a plug-and-play fashion.
A visual reasoner consistently improves multiple vision-language models on multiple visual question answering benchmarks.
Adversarial examples generated by recent attack methods exhibit reduced confidence levels and greater divergence from the training data distribution compared to original examples.
DA3 considers distribution shifts of adversarial examples to improve attacks’ effectiveness under detection methods.
The Non-detectable Attack Success Rate (NASR) integrates attack success rate and detectability for evaluating adversarial attacks.
DA3 generates adversarial examples that are effective under detection methods.
DA3 generates adversarial examples that are transferable across white-box and black-box language models.
A neural collaborative filtering approach predicts individual annotator ratings on text toxicity by incorporating annotator-specific information.
An in-context learning approach predicts individual annotator ratings on text toxicity by incorporating annotator-specific information.
An intermediate embedding-based architecture predicts individual annotator ratings on text toxicity by incorporating annotator-specific information.
The embedding-based architecture outperforms the neural collaborative filtering approach and in-context learning in predicting individual annotator ratings on text toxicity.
Using imputed demographics (predicted from survey information) as features achieves comparable performance to using true demographic data for predicting individual annotator ratings on text toxicity.
A low-cost membership inference attack uses an ensemble of small quantile regression models to determine document membership in a model's training set.
A membership inference attack using quantile regression ensembles achieves comparable or improved accuracy to state-of-the-art shadow model approaches with significantly reduced computation.
A membership inference attack using quantile regression ensembles is more effective against multi-epoch trained target models.
A membership inference attack using quantile regression ensembles works without knowledge of the target model's architecture and tokenizer.
Self-AMPLIFY generates rationales from post hoc explanation methods applied to Small Language Models.
Self-AMPLIFY targets input samples, generates rationales, and builds a prompt for In-Context Learning.
Self-AMPLIFY is the first fully automated method that applies post hoc explanation methods to autoregressive language models to generate rationales.
Interest-Augmented Multimodal Generator rewrites item text descriptions using the most frequent tags extracted from multimodal information of user-interacted items.
Interest-Augmented Multimodal Generator uses a decoupled text-to-text and image-to-image retriever to search for the top-K similar item text and image embeddings.
Interest-Augmented Multimodal Generator fuses retrieved information in a cross-modal manner using an attention module for user interests to guide personalized generation with the rewritten text.
Rewrite and Retrieve is a new paradigm for personalized generation.
The Creator-Aggregator Multi-Stage (CAMS) framework supports human-LLM hybrid text answer aggregation for close-ended crowd answers.
The typologically motivated transformation of Universal Dependencies proposed by William Croft is significant across diverse languages.
The typologically motivated transformation of Universal Dependencies proposed by William Croft has advantages and limitations.
Incubator handles complicated and mutually dependent classes in text classification.
Incubator jointly generates one sample for every class given the instruction of all class definitions.
Incubator is fine-tuned with the cluster centers of semantic textual embeddings to emphasize uniformity and diversity in generated samples.
Incubator takes label interdependency and user preference into consideration.
Incubator enables logical text mining by incubating multiple classifiers.
Redirecting the generative target to the wavelet domain accelerates training and inference of Speech DDPMs.
Redirecting the generative target to the wavelet domain achieves comparable or superior performance in speech synthesis.
Redirecting the generative target to the wavelet domain with different wavelet bases is effective for speech enhancement.
Fixed-size memory in long-context prefilling results in wasted computational and GPU memory resources.
Incremental Memory optimizes computational efficiency in long-context prefilling by starting with a small memory size and gradually increasing it.
Decremental Chunk based on Incremental Memory reduces chunk size while increasing memory size to ensure stable and lower GPU memory usage in long-context prefilling.
Decremental Chunk based on Incremental Memory is faster and reduces GPU memory consumption compared to fixed-size memory while achieving comparable performance on the LongBench Benchmark.
Anthology conditions large language models to particular virtual personas using open-ended life narratives (backstories).
Anthology improves matching between language model responses and human respondent distributions in nationally representative human surveys.
Anthology improves consistency metrics of language model responses in nationally representative human surveys.
LFTQA-Eval evaluates automated metrics for Long-Form Table Question Answering with a focus on faithfulness and comprehensiveness.
Existing automatic metrics for Long-Form Table Question Answering poorly correlate with human judgments.
Existing automatic metrics for Long-Form Table Question Answering fail to consistently differentiate between factually accurate responses and coherent but factually incorrect responses.
CURE uses large language models to extract contextual information about symptoms of mental disorders.
CURE employs an uncertainty-aware decision fusion network that combines predictions of multiple models based on quantified uncertainty values.
KoMOS is a Korean mental health dataset annotated by experts.
CURE detects mental disorders when symptom information is incomplete.

Text quality has a greater impact on the performance of models optimized with direct preference optimization than on models optimized with reward-model-based reinforcement learning from human feedback.
Filtered direct preference optimization uses a trained reward model to monitor text quality in the preference dataset during training.
Filtered direct preference optimization discards low-quality samples by comparing them with texts generated by the model being optimized, producing a more accurate preference dataset.
Data augmentation improves confidence calibration and uncertainty estimation in Named Entity Recognition in cross-genre, cross-lingual, and in-domain settings.
Lower perplexity of sentences generated by data augmentation leads to more effective confidence calibration in Named Entity Recognition.
Increasing the size of data augmentation improves confidence calibration and uncertainty estimation in Named Entity Recognition.
Lou is the first dataset providing high-quality reformulations for German text classification across seven tasks, including stance detection and toxicity classification.
Gender-fair language reformulations result in substantial label flips in language model predictions for German text classification.
Gender-fair language reformulations reduce prediction certainty in language models for German text classification.
Gender-fair language reformulations significantly alter attention patterns in language models for German text classification.
Language model rankings are consistent between original and gender-fair language reformulated instances for German text classification.
The impact of gender-fair language on German text classification shows consistent patterns in multi-lingual and English language models.
CopyBench measures both literal and non-literal copying in language model generations.
Non-literal copying (event copying and character copying) occurs in small language models.
Larger language models exhibit more literal and non-literal copying than smaller models.
Training-time alignment reduces literal copying but may increase non-literal copying.
Inference-time mitigation methods reduce literal copying but not non-literal copying.
KNN-Instruct uses KNN deduction to produce meaningful new instructions by summarizing and learning from similar existing instructions.
KNN-Inst-12k* is a dataset of instructions for supervised fine-tuning.
Qwen-2-7B fine-tuned on KNN-Inst-12k* outperforms other 7B models on the MT-Bench.
Standard length normalization in decoding for LLM-based recommendation inflates scores for items containing tokens with generation probabilities close to 1 (ghost tokens), causing amplification bias.
Existing decoding methods for LLM-based recommendation generate multiple similar or repetitive items for a user, causing homogeneity issue.
Debiasing-Diversifying Decoding (D3) disables length normalization for ghost tokens to alleviate amplification bias.
Debiasing-Diversifying Decoding (D3) incorporates a text-free assistant model to encourage tokens less frequently generated by LLMs for counteracting recommendation homogeneity.
ALC3 iteratively applies auto-correction, correction with human feedback, and filtering to the training dataset.
ALC3 achieves oracle performance using fewer human feedback examples than the number of noisy examples in the dataset for three NLP tasks.
REAR incorporates an assessment module that precisely assesses the relevance of retrieved documents for open-domain question answering.
REAR uses a training method based on bi-granularity relevance fusion and noise-resistant training for open-domain question answering.
REAR significantly outperforms competitive RAG approaches on open-domain question answering tasks.
The majority of inference computations for large generative models can be performed with 4-bit weights and activations while maintaining good accuracy.
QUIK compresses most weights and activations to 4-bit while keeping a small fraction of outliers in higher precision.
GPU kernels for the QUIK format provide highly-efficient layer-wise runtimes.
4-bit quantization combined with 2:4 sparsity enables accurate inference for large language models.
An autoregressive pre-training method processes document images via next patch prediction with a regression head and texts via next token prediction with a classification head.
Incorporating both visual and textual data improves the performance of pixel-based language models.
A unidirectional pixel-based model trained solely on visual data achieves comparable results to state-of-the-art bidirectional models on several language understanding tasks.
Long-form RobustQA is a dataset of 26K human-written long-form answers across seven domains, where each answer integrates short extractive answers from multiple documents into a coherent narrative.
RAG-QA Arena compares model-generated answers against Long-form RobustQA answers using large language models as evaluators.
RAG-QA Arena and human judgments on answer quality are highly correlated.
Most answers from the most competitive large language model are not preferred to Long-form RobustQA answers.
TroL reuses layers in a token-wise manner for large language and vision models.
TroL increases the number of forward propagation layers without physically adding more layers.
TroL outperforms open-source large language and vision models with larger model sizes.
TroL rivals the performance of closed-source large language and vision models with substantial model sizes.
M3Hop-CoT identifies misogynous memes through a combination of a CLIP-based classifier and a multimodal chain-of-thought module with entity-object-relationship integration.
M3Hop-CoT induces emotions, target awareness, and contextual knowledge in meme analysis via a three-step multimodal prompting principle.
M3Hop-CoT generalizes to multiple benchmark meme datasets.
Safety guardrails reduce the argumentative strength of hate speech counterspeech generated by large language models.
Attacking the implicit negative stereotype and hateful parts of hate speech results in higher-quality counterspeech.
ArMeme is an Arabic memes dataset with manual annotations of propagandistic content.
ArMeme is the first resource for Arabic multimodal research.

Dynamic Rewarding with Prompt Optimization employs a search-based prompt optimization framework that enables language models to self-improve and generate optimized prompts without additional training or human supervision.

Dynamic Rewarding with Prompt Optimization uses a dynamic rewarding mechanism to identify and rectify model-specific alignment weaknesses, enabling language models to adapt quickly to alignment challenges.
Dynamic Rewarding with Prompt Optimization enables base language models to outperform their supervised fine-tuned and reinforcement learning from human feedback tuned counterparts in alignment tasks.
Dynamic Rewarding with Prompt Optimization's automatically optimized prompts outperform human-curated prompts in alignment tasks.
A KL regularization term between a standard and enriched input format achieves more targeted input representations for feedback generation.
A preference optimization step achieves student answer-adaptive feedback generation.
The Informativeness Index I2 measures the degree to which feedback divulges the correct answer.
OpenCHAIR evaluates open-vocabulary object hallucinations for image captioning using generative foundation models.
OpenCHAIR evaluates open-vocabulary object hallucinations for image captioning with greater diversity and accuracy than the CHAIR benchmark.
MOCHa mitigates open-vocabulary hallucinations in image captioning without using a closed object list.
MOCHa uses a multi-objective reward function that targets the trade-off between fidelity and adequacy in image captioning generations without strong supervision.
Matching-Augmented Reasoning preprocesses each visual object individually to identify faces, names, and their alignments for visual-based entity question answering.
Matching-Augmented Reasoning encodes face and name information and stores vector representations in vector databases for visual-based entity question answering.
Matching-Augmented Reasoning retrieves matching faces and names and organizes them into a matching graph for visual-based entity question answering.
Matching-Augmented Reasoning derives answers to visual-based entity questions by reasoning over a matching graph.
Matching-Augmented Reasoning improves visual-based entity question answering compared to state-of-the-art methods using multimodal large language models.
SimulMask models simultaneous translation during fine-tuning by masking attention for a desired decision policy.
SimulMask improves translation quality for simultaneous translation compared to state-of-the-art prompting optimization strategies while reducing computational cost.
Large Language Models exhibit values more aligned with younger demographics than with older demographics in the US population.
The inclination of Large Language Models towards younger demographics varies across different value categories.
Incorporating age identity information in prompts does not mitigate value discrepancies between Large Language Models and different age cohorts.
Atomic Self-Consistency (ASC) improves the recall of relevant information in LLM responses.
Atomic Self-Consistency (ASC) merges authentic subparts from multiple LLM samples into a composite answer.
Atomic Self-Consistency (ASC) outperforms Universal Self-Consistency on multiple question answering datasets.
Merging multiple LLM samples has untapped potential for enhancing long-form generations.
Program-of-Thought introduces more reasoning errors, such as incorrect formulas or flawed logic, than Chain-of-Thought in GPT-4 and Llama series models.
Human-Think Language uses full Chain-of-Thought reasoning to control code generation.
Human-Think Language directs model attention to Chain-of-Thought reasoning during Program-of-Thought to generate more logical code.

Human-Think Language uses reinforcement learning with rewards based on the accuracy of Chain-of-Thought and Program-of-Thought responses to prevent repetitive reasoning steps in large language models for difficult math problems.
Human-Think Language exhibits strong transferability to out-of-domain datasets by controlling model information flow.
RPG uses a generator preference extractor, an entity retriever, and a gate-controlled preference regulator in the generator for end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
The generator preference extractor improves entity retrieval by filtering interference from irrelevant attributes.
The generator preference extractor provides focused guidance to the generator through inter-turn attribute prediction.
RPG outperforms existing methods and improves dialogue quality in end-to-end task-oriented dialogue systems.
Reducing knowledge inconsistency between external knowledge in alignment data and intrinsic knowledge in foundation large language models mitigates hallucinations.

Knowledge Consistent Alignment (KCA) uses a well-aligned large language model to formulate assessments based on external knowledge for evaluating the knowledge boundaries of foundation large language models.
Knowledge Consistent Alignment (KCA) includes strategies for handling knowledge-inconsistent instances in alignment data.
Knowledge Consistent Alignment (KCA) reduces hallucinations across multiple benchmarks on foundation large language models of varying architectures and sizes.

Casablanca is a multi-dialectal Arabic dataset covering Algerian, Egyptian, Emirati, Jordanian, Mauritanian, Moroccan, Palestinian, and Yemeni dialects with annotations for transcription, gender, dialect, and code-switching.
Baselines for multidialectal Arabic speech recognition exploit Casablanca.
A structural framework decomposes prompts into eight key components.
Role and Requirement are the two most prevalent components in in-the-wild prompts.
The variation in roles and capabilities specified in prompts has increased over time.
Using Capability and Demonstration components in prompts can lead to more satisfying responses from GPT-4.
InferAligner aligns large language models for harmlessness during the inference phase.
InferAligner decouples harmlessness from helpfulness.
InferAligner focuses solely on enhancing a model's capabilities on downstream tasks during training.
InferAligner utilizes safety steering vectors extracted from an aligned model to guide a model towards harmlessness alignment during inference.
InferAligner aligns domain-specific models in finance, medicine, and mathematics and multimodal large language models such as LLaVA for harmlessness.
InferAligner diminishes the attack success rate of harmful instructions and jailbreak instructions while maintaining performance in downstream tasks.
RA2FD uses a teacher-student framework to distill faithfulness from an oracle RAG model that generates multiple knowledge-infused responses.
RA2FD trains retrieval-free models to generate faithful responses using sequence-level distillation and contrastive learning from teacher-generated labels.
RA2FD achieves higher faithfulness than previous retrieval-free generation methods while maintaining inference efficiency.
InterleavedBench is the first benchmark for evaluating interleaved text-and-image generation.
InterleavedBench covers diverse real-world use cases.
InterleavedEval is a reference-free metric for evaluating interleaved text-and-image generation that uses GPT-4o.
InterleavedEval evaluates text quality, perceptual quality, image coherence, text-image coherence, and helpfulness.
InterleavedBench and InterleavedEval correlate with human judgments and outperform reference-based metrics.
Language models exhibit non-trivial property inheritance behavior when in-context examples and instructions are carefully controlled.
Some language models rely on shallow, non-semantic heuristics during property inheritance under minimal task reformulation.
Language models have not mastered the computational principles of semantic property inference.
AutoScraper is a two-stage framework for generating web scrapers that handles diverse and changing web environments efficiently.
AutoScraper leverages the hierarchical structure of HTML and similarity across web pages to generate web scrapers.
The executability metric measures the performance of web scraper generation tasks.
TinyDialogues is a synthetic dataset matched to child-directed speech.
Local discourse ordering in child-directed speech affects language model performance, but global developmental ordering does not.
Child-directed speech is not uniquely valuable for training language models.
ACAL uses an annotator selection strategy after data sampling.
ACAL efficiently approximates the full diversity of human judgments.
Annotator-centric metrics value minority and majority perspectives equally when assessing model performance.
ACAL improves data efficiency for subjective NLP tasks.
ACAL's success depends on the availability of a sufficiently large and diverse pool of annotators.
A reference-free metric for abstractive summarization evaluation correlates well with human relevance and is computationally cheap.
Combining a reference-free metric for abstractive summarization evaluation with reference-based metrics improves robustness in low quality reference settings.
MoDULA is a Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning Mixture-of-Expert paradigm for multi-task learning.
MoDULA trains universal experts, domain-specific experts, and routers separately.
MoDULA-Res connects universal and task-specific experts through residual connections to maintain general capability.
MoDULA-Flan and MoDULA-Res outperform existing fine-tuning methods on various large language models.
MoDULA-Res improves performance on multiple tasks and reduces training costs without losing general capability.
MoDULA allows efficient addition of new tasks without retraining existing experts.
MoDULA circumvents data balancing issues, enhancing training efficiency and model stability.
Combining tokenizer analysis, model weight-based indicators, and prompting techniques automatically detects under-trained tokens in Large Language Models.
Under-trained tokens are prevalent across a diverse set of Large Language Models.
Diverse and informative language feedback enhances generalization of reinforcement learning embodied agents to new tasks.
Diverse and informative language feedback enables fast adaptation of reinforcement learning embodied agents to new tasks.
SEAN-GNN learns representative, multi-faceted semantic anchors in the token embedding space.
SEAN-GNN forms a semantic-anchor graph by projecting sentences onto semantic anchors, with node attributes quantifying semantic information and edge weights quantifying temporal information.
SEAN-GNN integrates and refines learned features by performing message passing on the semantic-anchor graph.
SEAN-GNN generates meaningful semantic anchors for different emotions.
SEAN-GNN generates discriminative graph patterns for different emotions.
FinDVer evaluates explainable claim verification by LLMs on long, hybrid-content financial documents.
FinDVer contains expert-annotated examples for different real-world financial scenarios.
GPT-4o lags behind human experts in explainable claim verification on FinDVer.
Two real Speech Relation Extraction datasets facilitate training and evaluation of models on real speech.
Multi-level Cross-modal Alignment Model (MCAM) maps speech features into a semantic space suitable for text decoding via an alignment adapter.
Multi-level Cross-modal Alignment Model (MCAM) aligns speech and text encoder features at multiple levels during training.
Decreasing monosemanticity does not enhance model performance when the model changes.
Monosemanticity exhibits a positive correlation with model capacity in the preference alignment process.
Feature correlation serves as a proxy for monosemanticity.
Feature decorrelation regularization enhances representation diversity in dynamic preference optimization.
Feature decorrelation regularization enhances activation sparsity in dynamic preference optimization.
FibecFed adaptively samples data within each device using Fisher information for federated fine-tuning of large language models.
FibecFed dynamically selects layers for global aggregation and sparse parameters for local update with Low-Rank Adaptation in federated fine-tuning of large language models.
FibecFed achieves higher accuracy than baseline approaches in federated fine-tuning of large language models.
FibecFed reduces fine-tuning time compared to baseline approaches in federated fine-tuning of large language models.
LEMoE is an advanced Mixture of Experts (MoE) adaptor for lifelong model editing.
Catastrophic forgetting, inconsistent routing, and order sensitivity influence the effectiveness of conventional MoE adaptors in lifelong model editing.
KV anchor routing enhances routing consistency between training and inference in lifelong model editing.
Clustering-based editing order planning determines the sequence of edits for lifelong model editing.
LEMoE outperforms previous model editing techniques in lifelong model editing and maintains performance in batch model editing.
Generative models extract medical decision trees from text.
Generative models for medical decision rule extraction combine sequence-to-sequence generation and autoregressive generation.
Medical decision trees are linearized as natural language, augmented natural language, or JSON code sequences.
Language models rely more heavily on heuristics, such as lexical overlap, in the earlier stages of reasoning when more steps are required to reach an answer.
Language models decrease their reliance on heuristics as they progress closer to the final answer in multi-step reasoning tasks.
Language models track only a limited number of future steps in multi-step reasoning tasks.
Language models dynamically combine heuristic strategies with rational strategies in multi-step reasoning tasks.
MiniConGTS integrates a minimalist grid tagging scheme and token-level contrastive learning to improve and utilize pretrained representations for aspect sentiment triplet extraction.
MiniConGTS achieves comparable or superior performance to state-of-the-art methods for aspect sentiment triplet extraction with a more compact design and reduced computational overhead.
GPT-4's performance is formally evaluated for aspect sentiment triplet extraction in few-shot learning and Chain-of-Thought scenarios.
An automated method constructs synthetic query-candidate pairs for legal case retrieval.
LEAD is a legal case retrieval dataset constructed from synthetic query-candidate pairs.
Training models on synthetic query-candidate pairs for legal case retrieval achieves state-of-the-art results on legal case retrieval benchmarks.
A verification framework and resources jointly evaluate answer correctness and evidence appropriateness in long contexts by considering semantic relevancy and structural diversity.
Question answering and evidence selection exhibit task misalignment in long contexts.
Large language models infer causal relations from the order of two entity mentions in text, such as inferring that X causes Y when X is mentioned before Y.
Large language models suffer from the post hoc fallacy by inferring that X causes Y when X occurs before Y in temporal relations.
Large language models correctly deduce the absence of causal relations from temporal and spatial relations.
Large language models have difficulty inferring causal relations from counterfactuals.
Applying existing token reduction methods directly to State Space Models leads to substantial performance drops.
A unified post-training token reduction method for State Space Models integrates token importance and similarity to combine pruning and merging for fine-grained intra-layer token reduction.

A unified post-training token reduction method for State Space Models improves accuracy on multiple benchmarks compared to existing methods while reducing computational demands and memory requirements.
Modality selection adaptively chooses input modalities while maintaining model quality.
MOSEL is an automated inference serving system for multi-modal ML models that selects input modalities per request based on user-defined performance and accuracy requirements.
MOSEL improves system throughput while maintaining an accuracy guarantee.
MOSEL reduces job completion times relative to modality-agnostic approaches.
AGRaME enables ranking at varying levels of granularity by leveraging multi-vector embeddings while maintaining encoding at a single coarser level of granularity.

ReBo eliminates the need for human curation in the generation of questions, answers, and distractors.

Large language models such as GPT-4 outperform small models in simplifying complex content words and Chinese idioms in Chinese Lexical Simplification.

Classic estimation techniques for n-gram language models outperform transformers on random n-gram language models with arbitrary next-symbol probabilities.
Transformers outperform methods specifically designed to learn n-gram language models on random n-gram language models with shared parameters for next-symbol probabilities.
Token Embedding Variability is a proxy for estimating pre-training stability.
Multi-head Low-Rank Attention reduces pre-training instability.

LLMs with in-context learning underperform smaller encoder-only models based on RoBERTa in temporal relation classification.

NLG-Eval is a large-scale natural language generation evaluation corpus annotated by humans and GPT-4.
Themis uses multi-perspective consistency verification for training.
Themis uses rating-oriented preference alignment for training.
Themis conducts flexible and interpretable evaluations without references.

Post-editing machine translation outputs for feminine gender requires greater technical and temporal effort and higher financial costs than for masculine gender.

Syntactic templates are strings of parts-of-speech.
Models produce templated text in downstream tasks more frequently than human-reference texts.
Templates in model-generated text are predominantly found in pre-training data and persist through fine-tuning and alignment processes such as RLHF.

HiFT updates only a subset of parameters at each training step.

UniGen generates a dataset for sentiment classification that is independent of the target domain.

Word alignment serves as a preference signal for optimizing large language model-based machine translation models.
Preference data for machine translation is constructed by selecting chosen and rejected translations from multiple machine translation tools.

Image tokens possess significantly different semantics compared to text tokens, making pre-trained language models no more effective in modeling image tokens than randomly initialized models.
Text tokens in image-text datasets are too simple compared to normal language model pre-training data, causing catastrophic degradation of language models' capability.
Roleplay-doh enables domain-experts to create LLM-simulated patients by eliciting qualitative feedback and transforming it into principles that govern LLM-prompted roleplay.

Some natural language processing tasks are already encoded within pre-trained large language models, while others greatly benefit from instruction tuning.

A future-constrained generation problem formalization for text generation enables minimizing undesirable behaviors and enforcing faithfulness to instructions.

Future-constrained generation minimizes undesirable behaviors and enforces faithfulness to instructions in keyword-constrained generation, toxicity reduction, and factual correctness in question-answering.
Model fusion enhances shared knowledge among models and forgets unshared knowledge.
Model fusion reduces social biases in fine-tuned language models.

Multi-level information RAG enhances answer generation through entity retrieval and query expansion.

Multi-level information RAG retrieves more actual relevant knowledge.
Larger models and extensive pre-training consistently enhance in-domain accuracy and data efficiency in dense retrieval.

Character profiling evaluates large language models' character understanding capability by summarizing character profiles.

The largest existing interlinear glossed text corpus enables research on crosslingual transfer and interlinear glossed text generation.

A large-scale multimodal prompt dataset for video generation pairs text prompts with in-context examples via retrieval methods.

VIMI produces contextually rich and personalized videos grounded in the provided inputs.

The Greatest Good Benchmark evaluates the moral judgments of large language models using utilitarian dilemmas.
Large language models have consistently encoded moral preferences that diverge from established moral theories and lay population moral standards.
Most large language models have a marked preference for impartial beneficence and rejection of instrumental harm.
Large language models circumvent multi-hop reasoning requirements in more subtle ways than fine-tuned pre-trained language model predecessors.

Large language models ignore misleading lexical cues but are challenged by misleading reasoning paths.
Community-Cross-Instruct aligns large language models to online communities to elicit their beliefs.

Community-Cross-Instruct uses an advanced large language model to generate instruction-output pairs from a community's online discussions for finetuning a foundational large language model to faithfully represent the community.

Community-Cross-Instruct uses an advanced large language model to generate instruction-output pairs from a community's online discussions for evaluating the alignment of a finetuned model to the community.
Community-Cross-Instruct generates instruction-output pairs in a fully unsupervised manner.
Most of sixteen widely used Large Language Models fail to identify whether online language is offensive or non-offensive.
Large Language Models generate erroneous responses when detecting offensive speech.
Large Language Models over-rely on profanity when detecting offensive speech.

SLGAN is a unified framework that uses Generative Adversarial Networks for sequence labeling tasks.
SLGAN mitigates the limitation of Generative Adversarial Networks in backpropagating loss to discrete data.

SLGAN achieves state-of-the-art or competitive performance on sequence labeling tasks regardless of task or language.
Bait alleviates the effects of intention shift in jailbreak attacks.
Bait is an initial response to a harmful query that prompts large language models to rectify or supplement the knowledge within the bait.

BaitAttack adaptively generates necessary components to persuade targeted large language models that they are engaging with a legitimate inquiry in a safe context.
BaitAttack mitigates intention shift in jailbreak attacks.
DocCGen detects the correct libraries for structured domain-specific languages using library documentation that best matches the natural language query.

LLM-based KGQA methods exhibit hallucination on commonsense questions, particularly when targeting long-tail entities, and have non-verifiable reasoning processes.

An empirical Bayes estimator balances direct and regression estimates for each question subgroup.
The empirical Bayes estimator provides more precise subgroup-level estimates of large language model accuracy than direct and regression approaches.
Confidence intervals for empirical Bayes estimates of model performance have near-nominal coverage and are narrower than those for the direct estimator.
The empirical Bayes estimator provides more precise subgroup-level estimates of model performance for tabular and vision data.
HalluMeasure decomposes LLM responses into atomic claims and evaluates each atomic claim against a reference context.

APPLS is the first granular meta-evaluation testbed for evaluating metrics for Plain Language Summarization.

A range of unbounded and bounded linearizations cast dependency graph parsing as a sequence labeling task.

FBI examines the proficiency of Evaluator LLMs in assessing factual accuracy, instruction following, coherence in long-form writing, and reasoning proficiency in other LLMs.

When Atlas can choose between parametric knowledge and retrieved context, it relies more on retrieved context.

Effects of individual non-deterministic decisions, including sample choice and order, are isolated by mitigating the effects of other factors while observing performance variation across multiple runs.

SCOI selects in-context examples for machine translation using syntactic and lexical coverage measures.

MT-Eval evaluates multi-turn conversational capabilities of large language models.

Retrieval-augmented generation consistently outperforms unsupervised fine-tuning for both existing knowledge and entirely new knowledge in large language models.

Synchronous LLMs act as full-duplex dialogue agents.

HALO leverages external text corpora to evaluate the difference in the logit distribution of input text under retrieved human-written and LLM-rewritten contexts.
HALO complements basic detection features.

Detoxification with Self-Constrained Decoding detoxifies large language models without parameter fine-tuning.

Detoxification with Self-Constrained Decoding diminishes toxicity and enhances output safety.

EvolveSearch achieves state-of-the-art performance on multi-hop question-answering benchmarks.
Persona-based LLM prompting is a low-cost method for rewriting evaluation prompts to emulate diverse writing styles.
Variations in writing style and prompt formatting significantly impact Large Language Model performance estimates, even with identical semantic content.
Distinct writing styles consistently trigger either low or high performance across a range of models and tasks, regardless of model family, size, or recency.
Reasoning traces of large reasoning models frequently contain sensitive user data that can be extracted via prompt injections or accidentally leak into outputs.
Increased reasoning steps amplify privacy leakage in the reasoning traces of large reasoning models.
Increased reasoning steps cause large reasoning models to reason more verbosely.
Reasoning in large reasoning models improves utility but enlarges the privacy attack surface.
JEDI performs extractive atomic fact decomposition and interpretable inference in one forward pass without using generative models.
A large corpus provides synthetic rationales for multiple NLI benchmarks.
JEDI improves robustness in out-of-distribution and adversarial settings compared to models based solely on extractive rationale supervision.
Encoder-only architectures and synthetic rationales enable interpretability and robust generalization in Natural Language Inference.
STARQA is the first public human-created dataset for complex analytical question answering over structured databases, featuring nested joins, time series analytics, and statistical operations.
Text2SQLCode decomposes the Text2SQL task by using SQL for data fetch and Python for reasoning.
Complex analytical questions are challenging for both existing Text2SQL systems and Text2SQLCode.
Existing Text2SQL systems exhibit typical error patterns on complex analytical questions.
SportReason requires combining and reasoning across free-text, structured tables, and semi-structured infoboxes for numerical sports question answering.
SportReason includes human-verified question-answer pairs grounded in evidence from free-text, structured tables, and semi-structured infoboxes.
Multi-evidence retrieval remains a challenge for retrieval-augmented reasoning.

ReMedy reformulates machine translation evaluation as a reward modeling task.

ReMedy delivers superior capability in detecting translation errors and evaluating low-quality translations.
SwiftKV prefills later layers' KV cache using an earlier layer's output, enabling prompt tokens to skip those later layers.
SwiftKV uses a lightweight knowledge-preserving distillation procedure to adapt existing LLMs with minimal accuracy impact.

SwiftKV reduces prefill computation across multiple LLM families while incurring minimum quality degradation.
SwiftKV increases aggregate throughput and reduces time per output token in end-to-end inference serving.
Generative Annotation uses speech sound features to retrieve candidate entities for named entity correction in automatic speech recognition.
Generative Annotation uses a generative method to annotate entity errors in automatic speech recognition transcripts and replace the text with correct entities.
Generative Annotation is effective for named entity correction when the word forms of the wrongly-transcribed words and the ground-truth entity are significantly different.
Generative Annotation improves entity accuracy in named entity correction.
GUI-Bee collects environment-specific data through exploration.
GUI-Bee continuously fine-tunes GUI grounding models with environment-specific data.
Q-ICRL optimizes exploration efficiency and exploration data quality for GUI action grounding.
NovelScreenSpot evaluates the alignment of GUI action grounding models to novel environments.
NoThinking, which skips the thinking process and directly generates the final solution, is better for relatively simple tasks in terms of performance and efficiency.
AdaptThink is a reinforcement learning algorithm that teaches reasoning models to choose the optimal thinking mode adaptively based on problem difficulty.
AdaptThink uses a constrained optimization objective that encourages choosing NoThinking while maintaining overall performance.

AdaptThink uses an importance sampling strategy that balances Thinking and NoThinking samples during on-policy training to enable cold start and allow exploration and exploitation of both thinking modes.
AdaptThink reduces inference costs while enhancing performance.
BYOKG-RAG uses large language models to generate question entities, candidate answers, reasoning paths, and OpenCypher queries.
BYOKG-RAG uses graph tools to link generated graph artifacts to the knowledge graph and retrieve relevant graph context.

PricingLogic is the first benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models on tourism pricing tasks with multiple overlapping fare rules, covering basic customer-type pricing and bundled-tour calculations with interacting discounts.

DP-ST leverages semantic triples for neighborhood-aware private document generation under local differential privacy guarantees.

A communication protocol transfers both natural language tokens and token-wise state transition trajectories between agents.
The sequence of state changes in large language models after generating each token better reflects the information hidden behind the inference process.

UniDebugger is the first end-to-end framework for unified software debugging through multi-agent synergy.
UniDebugger uses agents that mimic components of the developer's cognitive process for software debugging, rather than independent expert actions.
UniDebugger coordinates agents through a three-level design based on a cognitive model of software debugging to adaptively handle software bugs of varying complexities.
UniDebugger outperforms state-of-the-art software repair methods without requiring ground-truth root-cause code statements.
Add-One-In compares the contribution value of different samples when incorporated into the subset.
Add-One-In uses LLMs to evaluate the value of each sample option during the selection process.

Add-One-In selected data achieves higher performance than the full dataset.
Add-One-In selected data achieves competitive performance with recent powerful studies while requiring fewer selections.
Neither output reward models nor process reward models consistently outperform across all vision-language tasks.
Superior vision large language models do not necessarily yield better rewarding performance.
ViLBench is a vision-language benchmark that requires intensive process reward signals.

FacLens probes hidden representations of fact-seeking questions to predict non-factuality prior to response generation.

ADPO formulates Image Difference Captioning as a preference optimization problem under the Bradley-Terry-Luce model.
ADPO directly aligns the captioning policy with pairwise difference preferences via Direct Preference Optimization.
An adversarially trained hard negative retriever selects counterfactual captions for Image Difference Captioning.
ADPO jointly trains the captioning policy and the hard negative retriever via policy-gradient reinforcement learning.
ADPO generates more fine-grained and accurate difference descriptions than existing baselines.
An end-to-end text-to-expression model focuses on emotional dynamics.
An end-to-end text-to-expression model learns expressive facial variations in a continuous latent space.
An end-to-end text-to-expression model generates diverse, fluid, and emotionally coherent facial expressions from text.
EmoAva is a large-scale and high-quality dataset of text–3D expression pairs.
An end-to-end text-to-expression model outperforms baseline methods in generating emotional facial expressions on multiple evaluation metrics.
Conan-embedding-v2 generates text embeddings from a 1.4B-parameter large language model trained from scratch.

A soft-masking mechanism gradually transitions between causal and bidirectional attention masks during training.
A dynamic hard negative mining method exposes models to increasingly difficult negative examples during training.

ESGenius is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models on Environmental, Social, and Governance and sustainability question answering.

Weather and Climate Event Forecasting (WCEF) predicts weather and climate events using numerical meteorological raster data and textual event data.

FillerSpeech enables natural filler insertion and control over filler style in speech synthesis.

Consistency verification of noise injection selects high-quality instruction data without relying on external model.

MAC-Tuning separates the learning of answer prediction and confidence estimation for LLM multi-compositional problem reasoning during fine-tuning on instruction data.

RecGPT achieves genuine zero-shot generalization capabilities for sequential recommendation.

CoDe-KG extracts sentence-level knowledge graphs by combining coreference resolution with syntactic sentence decomposition.

VisualWebInstruct creates reasoning-focused multimodal instruction datasets by leveraging web search on seed images and processing HTML data.

KLUE ensures faithful unlearning in real-world knowledge QA settings.
