The representations of S3M variants optimized for word recognition exhibit a global linear geometry that links English nouns and verbs to their regular inflected forms.
The global linear geometry in the representations of S3M variants optimized for word recognition does not directly track phonological or morphological units.

The geometric structure in the representations of S3M variants optimized for word recognition challenges the presumed necessity of distinct linguistic representations of phonology and morphology.
A process-level reward module mitigates the unawareness of intermediate reasoning steps in outcome-level supervision without requiring additional annotation.

Guided-CoT is a new chain-of-thought-like prompt for detecting antisemitic content.

FB-Bench is a fine-grained, multi-task benchmark for evaluating LLMs' responsiveness to human feedback across task types, response deficiency types, and feedback types in Chinese real-world usage scenarios.

PerspectiveMod is a dataset of online comments annotated for whether a comment requires moderation and the reason.
PerspectiveMod intentionally varies the level of moderation experience in the source data between professional and non-professional moderation environments.
PerspectiveMod intentionally varies the annotator profiles between experts and trained crowdworkers.

Magic Image is optimized using gradients derived from harmful and benign training samples.

ViPE compresses video features into a small set of perceptual queries using a hierarchical merge strategy.
ViPE integrates representations into the LLM through a lightweight LoRA-like mechanism.

Value profiles represent individuals using natural language descriptions of underlying values compressed from in-context demonstrations.

ReviewRL generates comprehensive and factually grounded scientific paper reviews.

FUSE models a social network with four types of Large Language Model agents: spreaders, commentators, verifiers, and standers to simulate the progressive distortion of true news.

Recall with Reasoning (RwR) unlocks Mamba's long-context memory ability by distilling chain-of-thought summarization from a teacher model.

NewsHomepages is a dataset of news website homepages from local, national, and topic-specific outlets captured over a five-year period.

A taxonomy categorizes ambiguity types in data visualization code generation.

Increased query-context overlap above a critical threshold improves test-time perplexity and accelerates model learning.

Static word embeddings optimized for sentence semantic representation are derived from a pre-trained Sentence Transformer via sentence-level principal component analysis and knowledge distillation or contrastive learning.

OWL is a dataset of aligned excerpts from books in English, with official translations in Vietnamese, Spanish, and Turkish, and new translations in Sesotho, Yoruba, Maithili, Malagasy, Setswana, and Tahitian.

Perturbations such as masking characters or shuffling words reduce memorization accuracy, but performance remains above chance.
Confounding factors in experimental setups cause conflicting evidence regarding the role of morphology in language modeling.

Conclusions that morphological alignment of tokenization, tokenization efficiency, and dataset size explain why agglutinative languages have higher perplexities than fusional languages include confounding factors.
Token bigram metrics intrinsically predict the difficulty of causal language modeling.
Token bigram metrics are gradient proxies for morphological complexity without requiring expert annotation.
Text embeddings from multimodal and unimodal models exhibit stronger alignment with MEG brain activity during listening than speech embeddings from the same models.
Multimodal text embeddings show a temporal peak in alignment with MEG brain activity, indicating benefit from speech embeddings.
Speech embeddings from multimodal models show similar alignment with MEG brain activity as those from unimodal models.
Cross-modal knowledge transfer between text and speech is asymmetric: text benefits from speech information but speech does not benefit from text information.
A comprehensive relation corpus covers all potential relations in real-world scenarios for audio event relation modeling in text-to-audio generation.
An audio event corpus encompasses commonly heard audios for audio event relation modeling in text-to-audio generation.
New evaluation metrics assess audio event relation modeling in text-to-audio generation from various perspectives.

Gated prompt tuning models audio event relations in existing text-to-audio generation models with negligible extra parameters by appending learnable relation and event prompts to the text prompt before feeding to the models.
Persona-Dialogue is the first large-scale multi-party spoken dialogue dataset that incorporates speaker profiles.
PAChat simultaneously models linguistic content and speaker features to map character settings to speaker identities in speech.
PAChat achieves speaker-specific responses, character understanding, and targeted reply generation in multi-party dialogue scenarios.
ProcWorld evaluates partially observable embodied spatial reasoning and long-term planning for large language models and vision language models.
ProcWorld evaluates challenging embodied navigation and object manipulation tasks with diverse data distribution.
ProcWorld supports configurable observation modes from text-only descriptions to vision-only observations.
ProcWorld requires constrained reasoning with dynamic states subject to physical reachability.
ProcWorld remains highly challenging for existing state-of-the-art models and in-context learning methods due to constrained reachability and combinatorial spatial reasoning.

Critique-Rethink-Verify (CRV) system trains smaller reasoning models by critiquing chain-of-thought reasoning according to the cognitive capabilities of smaller models, rethinking and refining the chain-of-thought based on critiques, and verifying the correctness of the refined results.
Cognitive Preference Optimization (CogPO) algorithm enhances the reasoning abilities of smaller models by aligning their reasoning processes with their cognitive capacities.
CRV+CogPO framework outperforms other methods on challenging reasoning benchmarks.
Path Drift is a vulnerability in Long-CoT models where reasoning trajectories drift from aligned paths, resulting in content that violates safety constraints.
First-person commitments induce goal-driven reasoning that delays refusal signals in Path Drift.
Ethical evaporation occurs when surface-level disclaimers bypass alignment checkpoints in Path Drift.
Condition chain escalation progressively steers models toward unsafe completions through layered cues in Path Drift.
The Path Drift Induction Framework induces Path Drift using cognitive load amplification, self-role priming, and condition chain hijacking.
Each stage of the Path Drift Induction Framework independently reduces model refusal rates, and their combination further compounds the effect.
Path-level defense against Path Drift uses role attribution correction and metacognitive reflection (reflective safety cues).
Moderate sparsity ratios substantially harm multilingual performance.
M-Wanda incorporates language-aware activation statistics to model cross-lingual variation in its pruning criterion.
M-Wanda dynamically adjusts layerwise sparsity based on cross-lingual importance.
M-Wanda improves multilingual performance at minimal additional costs.
M-Wanda is the first pruning method explicitly optimized to retain multilingual performance.
HierarchicalTopK trains a single sparse autoencoder to optimize reconstructions across multiple sparsity levels simultaneously.
HierarchicalTopK achieves Pareto-optimal trade-offs between sparsity and explained variance and outperforms traditional sparse autoencoders trained at individual sparsity levels.
HierarchicalTopK preserves high interpretability scores at higher sparsity levels.
IL-PCSR is an Indian legal corpus that provides a common testbed for Statute Retrieval and Precedent Retrieval tasks to exploit the dependence between them.
An LLM-based re-ranking approach leverages the dependence between Statute Retrieval and Precedent Retrieval tasks.
ChartMind bridges the gap between real-world chart analysis applications and traditional academic benchmarks.
ChartLLM extracts key contextual elements for chart question answering.
ChartLLM reduces noise in chart question answering.
ChartLLM outperforms instruction-following, OCR-enhanced, and chain-of-thought paradigms for chart question answering.
Compositional structured prompt attacks extract private information from MRAG systems in a black-box setting by query manipulation.
Large multimodal models in MRAG systems directly generate outputs resembling retrieved content.
Large multimodal models in MRAG systems produce descriptions that indirectly expose sensitive information.
DrFrattn directly learns adaptive read/write policies from the attention mechanism.
DrFrattn achieves an improved balance between translation accuracy and latency.
LLMs often generate outputs containing leaked information even when the training data contains little leaked information.
The scarcity of leaked instances in the training data makes leakage detection more difficult.
Few-shot learning mitigates the impact of leakage rate on leakage detection performance.
VideoPASTA trains models to distinguish accurate video representations from adversarial examples that violate spatial, temporal, or cross-frame relationships.
VideoPASTA learns robust video representations capturing fine-grained spatial details and long-range temporal dynamics with a small number of preference pairs.
VideoPASTA is model agnostic.
Targeted alignment effectively addresses core video-language challenges without massive pretraining or architectural modifications.
VideoPASTA achieves video-language alignment without human annotation or captioning, relying solely on 32-frame sampling.
VideoPASTA is a scalable plug-and-play solution that integrates with existing models and preserves their original capabilities.
CoVoGER is a benchmark for generative error correction that covers automatic speech recognition and speech-to-text translation across 15 languages and 28 language pairs.
CoVoGER is constructed by decoding Common Voice 20.0 and CoVoST-2 with Whisper and SeamlessM4T models of multiple sizes, providing 5-best lists via a mixture of beam search and temperature sampling.
The mixture decoding strategy yields the best generative error correction performance in most settings.
CityEQA is a task for embodied agents to answer open-vocabulary questions through active exploration in dynamic city spaces.
CityEQA-EC is the first benchmark dataset with human-annotated tasks across six categories in a realistic 3D urban simulator.
PMA uses a Planner to break down embodied question answering into sub-tasks in dynamic city spaces.
PMA uses a Manager to maintain an object-centric cognitive map for spatial reasoning in dynamic city spaces.
PMA uses specialized Actors for navigation, exploration, and collection sub-tasks in dynamic city spaces.
PMA significantly outperforms frontier-based baselines on the CityEQA task.
POATE is a jailbreak technique that uses contrastive reasoning to provoke unethical responses.
POATE crafts semantically opposing intents and integrates them with adversarial templates to steer models toward harmful outputs.
POATE achieves higher attack success rates than existing methods and is robust across diverse language model families.
Intent-Aware CoT decomposes queries to detect malicious intent.
Reverse Thinking CoT reasons in reverse to evaluate and reject harmful responses.
A generative framework for Kanji learning explicitly models the mnemonic construction process as driven by a set of common rules.
A generative framework for Kanji learning learns common rules using a novel Expectation-Maximization-type algorithm.
A generative framework for Kanji learning learns latent structures and compositional rules for mnemonic generation.
A generative framework for Kanji learning enables interpretable and systematic mnemonic generation.
A generative framework for Kanji learning provides insight into the mechanisms behind effective mnemonic creation.
User feedback in human-LLM dialogues provides insights into user behavior.
Incorporating the contents of user feedback beyond polarity improves model performance on short human-designed questions but not on longer and more complex questions.
SilVar is an end-to-end multimodal model that uses speech instructions for reasoning-based visual question answering and object localization.
A new dataset challenges models with speech-based reasoning tasks for object localization.
A unified framework analyzes the dynamic relationships among LLM-enabled authorship obfuscation, authorship mimicking, and authorship verification for authorship privacy.

The interactions among LLM-enabled authorship obfuscation, authorship mimicking, and authorship verification transform human-authored text, with effects quantified at a single point in time and iteratively over time.

Demographic metadata, such as gender and academic background, modulates the performances, inter-task dynamics, and privacy risks of LLM-enabled authorship obfuscation, authorship mimicking, and authorship verification.
Benchmark Profiling computes an Ability Impact Score that quantifies the contribution of each of ten cognitively grounded abilities to model success on a benchmark.
Most benchmarks draw on multiple abilities instead of a single ability.
Datasets with similar benchmark labels rely on distinct mixtures of abilities.
Code-generation benchmarks reward broad, multi-skill improvement and show only modest gains from narrow domain-specific fine-tuning.
Abilities irrelevant to the task can negatively affect model performance.
GAP minimizes global capability loss via layer-adaptive pruning rates.
GAP formulates the pruning task as a combinatorial optimization problem constrained by a total parameter budget.
GAP uses an efficient dynamic programming solution to determine optimal layer-wise compression rates.
Without tuning, GAP achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art methods at high pruning rates and outperforms state-of-the-art methods at low pruning rates.
With tuning, GAP achieves the best performance among the compared methods.
SafeScientist proactively refuses ethically inappropriate or high-risk tasks.
SafeScientist integrates prompt monitoring, agent-collaboration monitoring, tool-use monitoring, and an ethical reviewer component for safety oversight.
SciSafetyBench evaluates AI safety in scientific contexts with high-risk scientific tasks across multiple domains, specially designed scientific tools, and tool-related risk tasks.
SafeScientist improves safety performance compared to traditional AI scientist frameworks without compromising scientific output quality.
SafeScientist's safety pipeline is robust against diverse adversarial attack methods.
A lightweight encoder-decoder model (T5 or BERT2BERT) for structuring radiology reports outperforms large language models adapted using prompt-based techniques on a human-annotated test set.
Some LoRA-finetuned large language models achieve modest gains over lightweight encoder-decoder models on the Findings section of radiology reports.
LoRA-finetuned large language models incur substantially greater computational resources than lightweight encoder-decoder models for structuring radiology reports.
M3Retrieve evaluates multimodal retrieval across medical domains, medical fields, and tasks with licensed text documents and multimodal queries.
Gaze-aware reward models use human gaze prediction for reinforcement learning from human feedback.
Gaze-based distribution of sparse rewards at token level uses human gaze prediction for reinforcement learning from human feedback.
Gaze-informed reinforcement learning from human feedback achieves faster convergence during policy optimization.
Gaze-informed reinforcement learning from human feedback reduces computational requirements during policy optimization.
Human visual attention patterns provide valuable signals for policy training in reinforcement learning from human feedback.
MuseScorer is a fully automated, psychometrically validated system for frequency-based originality scoring.

MuseScorer integrates a Large Language Model with externally orchestrated retrieval to retrieve semantically similar prior idea-buckets and zero-shot prompt the Large Language Model to determine if a new idea belongs to an existing bucket or forms a new one.
MuseScorer matches human annotators in idea clustering structure and participant-level scoring.
MuseScorer demonstrates strong convergent and external validity.
Accelerated Vision-Language Models exhibit instance-level answer divergence from the unaccelerated model despite minimal aggregate performance degradation.
Accelerated Vision-Language Models sometimes change correct answers to incorrect answers.
Input perturbations magnify answer inconsistencies in accelerated Vision-Language Models.
Accelerated medical Vision-Language Models exhibit answer inconsistencies.
PlanGEN is a model-agnostic and easily scalable agent framework with constraint, verification, and selection agents.
PlanGEN uses constraint-guided iterative verification to improve inference-time algorithms.
PlanGEN uses adaptive selection of inference-time algorithms based on instance complexity to boost performance on complex planning and reasoning problems.
TextTopoOOD evaluates out-of-distribution detection in text-rich networks under attribute-level shifts, structural shifts, thematically-guided label shifts, and domain-based divisions.
TNT-OOD fuses local topological structure into node-level text representations via a cross-attention module.
TNT-OOD generates node-specific transformation parameters via a HyperNetwork.
TNT-OOD enhances in-distribution/out-of-distribution distinction across structural and textual shifts.
TextTopoOOD presents a nuanced challenge for evaluating out-of-distribution detection in text-rich networks.
Over-search and under-search behaviors are prevalent in agentic RAG systems across multiple QA datasets.
Response accuracy in agentic RAG systems correlates with model uncertainty in search decisions.
β-GRPO is a reinforcement learning-based training method that uses a confidence threshold to reward high-certainty search decisions.
β-GRPO improves agentic RAG performance on QA benchmarks.
Reasoning paths frequently reuse similar reasoning patterns, enabling efficient model-free token prediction without requiring separate draft models.
STAND is a model-free speculative decoding approach that exploits the inherent redundancy in reasoning trajectories.
STAND uses stochastic drafting, a memory-efficient logit-based N-gram module, optimized Gumbel-Top-K sampling, and data-driven tree construction.
STAND reduces inference latency while maintaining accuracy compared to standard autoregressive decoding.
STAND outperforms state-of-the-art speculative decoding methods across single-trajectory decoding, batch decoding, and test-time tree search.
A quality-oriented synthesis approach for Argument Mining preserves annotation quality through structure-aware paraphrasing.
A diversity-oriented synthesis approach for Argument Mining generates novel argumentative texts with diverse topics and argument structures.
Combining quality-oriented and diversity-oriented synthetic data enhances Argument Mining model performance in full-data and low-resource settings.
Synthetic data volume positively correlates with Argument Mining model performance.
Continuous-Time Attention allows attention weights to evolve along a pseudo-time dimension governed by diffusion, wave, or reaction-diffusion dynamics.
Continuous-Time Attention improves gradient stability during training.
Continuous-Time Attention mitigates the exponential decay of distant interactions.
Continuous-Time Attention improves the optimization landscape.
Continuous-Time Attention achieves consistent performance gains over standard and long-sequence Transformer variants.
Logit flow traces how logits propagate across layers and positions toward the final prediction.

Single-hop knowledge prediction in large language models consists of four distinct stages: entity subject enrichment, entity attribute extraction, relation subject enrichment, and relation attribute extraction.
Failures in multi-hop reasoning in large language models often stem from the relation attribute extraction stage due to conflicting logits.
Back attention enables lower layers to leverage higher-layer hidden states from different positions during attention computation.
Back attention enables a 1-layer transformer to achieve the performance of a 2-layer transformer in multi-hop reasoning.
PunMemeCN assesses Vision-Language Models' understanding of Chinese pun memes through pun meme detection, sentiment analysis, and chat-driven meme response.
State-of-the-art Vision-Language Models struggle with Chinese pun memes, especially homophone wordplay, even when using Chain-of-Thought prompting.
Punchlines in memes conceal potentially harmful content from AI detection.
A multimodal model detects Other-initiated Repair requests in Dutch dialogues by integrating linguistic and prosodic features grounded in Conversation Analysis.
Prosodic cues complement linguistic features for detecting Other-initiated Repair requests in Dutch dialogues.
BiasRAG exposes fairness vulnerabilities in retrieval-augmented generation via a two-phase backdoor attack.
BiasRAG compromises the query encoder during pre-training to align target groups with intended social biases for long-term persistence.

BiasRAG injects adversarial documents into knowledge bases during post-deployment to reinforce the backdoor, subtly influencing retrieved content without detection under standard fairness evaluations.
BiasRAG achieves high attack success rates while preserving contextual relevance and utility.
Question ambiguity is linearly encoded in the internal representations of large language models.
A small number of neurons in large language models encode question ambiguity information during the pre-filling stage.
Probes trained on Ambiguity-Encoding Neurons generalize across datasets for ambiguity detection.
Probes trained on Ambiguity-Encoding Neurons outperform prompting-based and representation-based baselines in ambiguity detection.
Ambiguity-Encoding Neurons emerge from shallow layers of large language models.
Manipulating Ambiguity-Encoding Neurons controls large language model behavior from direct answering to abstention.
ToxiRewriteCN is the first Chinese detoxification dataset designed to preserve sentiment polarity.
ToxiRewriteCN contains annotated triplets of toxic sentences, sentiment-aligned non-toxic rewrites, and labeled toxic spans.
ToxiRewriteCN covers five real-world scenarios: standard expressions, emoji-induced toxicity, homophonic toxicity, single-turn dialogues, and multi-turn dialogues.

Commercial and mixture-of-experts models perform best in detoxification overall, but all models struggle to balance safety with emotional fidelity in subtle or context-heavy settings including emoji, homophone, and dialogue-based inputs.
The South Levantine Arabic dataset supports STIPA.
STIPA model evaluation covers 51 language families.
Fine-tuned ASR models produce accurate IPA transcriptions with limited supervision in extremely low-resource settings.
Vision-language models often display notable miscalibration across diverse tasks and settings.
Vision-language models that think with images consistently exhibit better calibration.
Visual Confidence-Aware Prompting is a two-stage prompting strategy that improves confidence alignment in multimodal settings.
MultiMedST is a medical speech translation dataset covering all translation directions among Vietnamese, English, German, French, and Chinese (Simplified and Traditional).
MultiMedST is the largest medical machine translation dataset.
MultiMedST is the largest many-to-many multilingual speech translation dataset across all domains.

The first systematic study on medical speech translation includes empirical baselines, bilingual-multilingual comparative study, end-to-end vs. cascaded comparative study, task-specific vs. multi-task sequence-to-sequence comparative study, code-switch analysis, and quantitative-qualitative error analysis.
Language mixing in reasoning language models is influenced by the prompt language, task difficulty level, and subject area.
Forcing reasoning language models to reason in Latin or Han scripts improves task accuracy.
The script composition of reasoning traces in reasoning language models aligns with the script composition of the model's internal representations.
Language mixing in reasoning language models reflects latent processing preferences.
A distillation-based T5 generalist model supports zero-shot multi-label text classification and few-shot fine-tuning.
A distillation-based T5 generalist model accommodates variable label sets through domain-agnostic pretraining.
A distillation-based T5 generalist model models dependency between labels.
A distillation-based T5 generalist model outperforms baselines of similar size on few-shot multi-label classification tasks.
An LLM-based pipeline with linguistically-grounded discourse segmenters extracts supporting and opposing statements for each answer option from chains of thought.
A rank-based evaluation framework for human label variation prioritizes answer rankings over exact scores.
Ranking methods for human label variation show better alignment with human label distributions than exact score methods.
MODELCITIZENS is a dataset of social media posts with toxicity annotations across diverse identity groups.
MODELCITIZENS posts are augmented with LLM-generated conversational scenarios to reflect the impact of conversational context on toxicity.
State-of-the-art toxicity detection tools underperform on MODELCITIZENS and show further degradation on context-augmented posts.
LLAMACITIZEN-8B and GEMMACITIZEN-12B are toxicity detection models finetuned on MODELCITIZENS.
LLAMACITIZEN-8B and GEMMACITIZEN-12B outperform GPT-o4-mini on in-distribution toxicity detection evaluations.
PISCES precisely erases entire concepts from model parameters by directly editing directions that encode concepts in parameter space.
PISCES decomposes MLP vectors into interpretable features using a disentangler model.
PISCES improves erasure efficacy over leading erasure methods.
PISCES improves erasure specificity over leading erasure methods.
PISCES improves erasure robustness over leading erasure methods.
NeuroMerging uses a decomposition of task-specific representations into two complementary neuronal subspaces that regulate input sensitivity and task adaptability.
NeuroMerging mitigates task interference within neuronal subspaces for training-free model fusion across diverse tasks.
Aligning neuronal mechanisms in model merging mitigates task interference and improves knowledge fusion.
FinMTEB evaluates text embedding models on financial tasks across multiple task types in English and Chinese.
Fin-E5 is a finance-adapted text embedding model.
Fin-E5 is fine-tuned from e5-Mistral-7B-Instruct using a persona-based synthetic dataset for financial embedding tasks.
Domain-specific text embedding models outperform general-purpose models on financial tasks.
Performance on general text embedding benchmarks is a poor predictor of performance on financial tasks.
Bag-of-Words models outperform dense embedding models on financial semantic textual similarity tasks.
LENS is a longitudinal English learner corpus with error annotations from multiple first languages that enables the study of language learning over time.
Large language models can be used for high-precision, semi-automated annotation of L1 interference features.
FoREST evaluates Frame of Reference comprehension in large language models through spatial question answering and text-to-image layout generation.
Large language models exhibit a performance gap across different Frame of Reference classes, which affects their ability to generate accurate layouts for text-to-image generation.
Spatial-Guided prompting improves large language models' ability to extract essential spatial concepts.
Icon2 extracts layer-wise direction vectors to encode sophisticated human preferences.
Icon2 filters self-synthesized instructions based on inherent consistency using layer-wise direction vectors.
Icon2 applies bidirectional inherent control during decoding to steer token representations for generating response pairs with clear alignment distinctions.
Icon2 improves alignment performance and reduces computational costs for large language models.
All current cross-subject data splitting methods for brain-to-text decoding suffer from data leakage, causing overfitting and overestimation of decoding models.
A cross-subject data splitting criterion for brain-to-text decoding using fMRI and EEG signals avoids data leakage.
T2ARD incorporates a single-domain model and target graph adaptation strategies for cross-domain rumor detection.
T2ARD uses a graph adaptation module that updates the graph structure and node attributes via multi-level self-supervised contrastive learning to derive invariant graph representations.
T2ARD uses annotations from Large Language Models on the target graph to produce pseudo-labels for model adaptation.
T2ARD achieves state-of-the-art performance in cross-domain rumor detection.
DischargeSim evaluates large language models on their ability to act as personalized discharge educators.
DischargeSim simulates post-visit, multi-turn conversations between DoctorAgents and PatientAgents with diverse psychosocial profiles.

DischargeSim assesses dialogue quality via automatic and LLM-as-judge evaluation, personalized document generation including free-text summaries and structured AHRQ checklists, and patient comprehension through a downstream multiple-choice exam.
Large language models have significant gaps in discharge education capability and their performance varies widely across patient profiles.
Model size does not always yield better education outcomes in discharge education, indicating trade-offs in strategy use and content prioritization.
RALS includes the first dataset jointly covering lexical complexity prediction annotations and lexical simplification for Romanian.
RALS includes a comparison of lexical simplification approaches for Romanian.
RALS includes a methodology that orders simplification candidates from simple to complex using a pairwise ranking approximation method based on human judgments.
RALS includes novel pipelines for lexical complexity prediction and lexical simplification.
RALS provides the first lexical simplification system for Romanian.

Multidimensional evaluation metrics assess hallucination rates in generated references and measure semantic coverage and factual consistency of generated abstracts and literature reviews against human-written counterparts.
The most advanced large language models generate hallucinated references in reference generation tasks.
Large language model performance in literature review composition varies across academic disciplines.
SliceMoE routes contiguous slices of a token’s hidden vector.
SliceMoE uses a shared lightweight router to predict top-k experts for each slice.
SliceMoE processes slices independently and re-assembles outputs while maintaining per-token FLOP efficiency.
SliceMoE interleaves slices from different tokens within an expert, leading to smoother expert utilization.
SliceMoE exhibits interpretable expertise over syntactic and semantic sub-spaces.
Brain alignment tracks the development of knowledge of linguistic rules more closely than the development of world knowledge and reasoning.
The correlation between next-word prediction, behavioral alignment, and brain alignment fades once models surpass human language proficiency.
Model size is not a reliable predictor of brain alignment when controlling for the number of features.
Language brain alignment benchmarks remain unsaturated.
MoSEs provides stylistics-aware uncertainty quantification for AI-generated text detection.
The Stylistics Reference Repository (SRR) activates appropriate reference data for input text and provides it to the Conditional Threshold Estimator (CTE).
The Conditional Threshold Estimator (CTE) jointly models linguistic statistical properties and semantic features to dynamically determine the optimal threshold.
MoSEs yields prediction labels with confidence levels.
MoSEs shows greater improvement in low-resource AI-generated text detection compared to baselines.
Dynamic Model-bank Single-Utterance Test-time Adaptation (DMSUTA) maintains a dynamic model bank for automatic speech recognition test-time adaptation.

Dynamic Model-bank Single-Utterance Test-time Adaptation (DMSUTA) selects a subset of model checkpoints for each test sample based on confidence and uncertainty criteria in automatic speech recognition test-time adaptation.

Dynamic Model-bank Single-Utterance Test-time Adaptation (DMSUTA) filters out potentially collapsed models from the model bank to preserve model plasticity and long-term stability in automatic speech recognition test-time adaptation.
Dynamic Model-bank Single-Utterance Test-time Adaptation (DMSUTA) continually adapts to evolving domain shifts in automatic speech recognition test-time adaptation.

Dynamic Model-bank Single-Utterance Test-time Adaptation (DMSUTA) demonstrates superior robustness to domain shifts in automatic speech recognition test-time adaptation compared to existing continual test-time adaptation baselines.
Statistics-based token alignment enables fusion of language models with different structures.
Merging coefficients for language model fusion are determined by the magnitude of parameter updates before and after fine-tuning.
FuseChat-7B outperforms baselines of various sizes on instruction-following benchmarks.
SG-KBQA injects schema contexts into entity retrieval and logical form generation to capture knowledge base semantics and structure.
SG-KBQA outperforms state-of-the-art models on KBQA benchmark datasets for generalization to unseen knowledge base elements and their novel compositions.
DialPrompt guides users through a multi-turn dialogue to express preferences on 15 essential optimization dimensions for high-quality text-to-image synthesis prompts.
Fifteen essential dimensions for high-quality text-to-image synthesis prompts were mined from advanced users.
A multi-turn dataset for dialogue-based text-to-image synthesis prompt generation was curated.
DialPrompt improves user-centricity over existing approaches.
DialPrompt maintains competitive image quality compared to existing approaches.
RARE directly retrieves ads for queries in real-time using LLM-generated Commercial Intentions as an intermediate semantic representation.
Commercial Intentions are generated by a customized LLM injected with commercial knowledge, which enhances domain relevance.
Each Commercial Intention corresponds to multiple ads, resulting in a lightweight and scalable set of Commercial Intentions.
RARE increases consumption, Gross Merchandise Volume, click-through rate, and shallow conversions in online sponsored search advertising.
RARE outperforms ten competitive baselines in four major categories in offline experiments.

Fann or Flop is the first benchmark for assessing Arabic poetry comprehension by large language models across twelve historical eras, 21 poetic genres, and metrical forms ranging from classical structures to contemporary free verse.
Fann or Flop includes a curated corpus of poems with explanations to assess semantic understanding, metaphor interpretation, prosodic awareness, and cultural context.
Most state-of-the-art large language models struggle with Arabic poetry understanding despite strong performance on standard Arabic benchmarks.

Representation potentials are the latent capacity of foundation model representations to capture task-specific information within a single modality while also providing a transferable basis for alignment and unification across modalities.
Foundation models often exhibit structural regularities and semantic consistencies in their representation spaces, making them strong candidates for cross-modal transfer and alignment.
Three evaluation datasets assess LLMs' ability to forget direct factual knowledge, semantic shifts, and causally related knowledge.

Prompted knowledge cutoffs are effective when directly queried with information after the cutoff date but struggle to induce forgetting when the forgotten content is causally related to the query and not directly asked.
ExGLM uses a graph-language synergistic alignment module to align graph structures with language models for semantic consistency across modalities.
ExGLM uses a judge-and-improve paradigm to iteratively evaluate, refine, and prioritize responses for higher interpretability.
ExGLM is more efficient than existing methods.
ExGLM produces more interpretable outputs than existing methods.
GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 405b, and Mistral Large 2 exhibit statistically significant zero-shot and few-shot preferences for InChI and IUPAC names in molecular property prediction tasks.

In molecular property prediction with large language models, the preference for InChI and IUPAC names over other molecular string representations is potentially due to representation granularity, favorable tokenization, and prevalence in pretraining corpora.

GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 405b, and Mistral Large 2 using InChI and IUPAC names for molecular property prediction achieve few-shot performance that rivals or surpasses conventional approaches.

GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 405b, and Mistral Large 2 using InChI and IUPAC names for molecular property prediction provide explainable predictions through chain-of-thought reasoning, which task-specific models lack.

A systematic taxonomy for evaluating large audio-language models categorizes evaluations into four dimensions: General Auditory Awareness and Processing, Knowledge and Reasoning, Dialogue-oriented Ability, and Fairness, Safety, and Trustworthiness.
The first survey specifically focused on large audio-language model evaluations provides clear guidelines for the community.
Challenges in large audio-language model evaluations are highlighted, with insights into promising future directions.
Self-Adjust Softmax (SA-Softmax) multiplies the input vector element-wise by the softmax output vector.
Self-Adjust Softmax (SA-Softmax) has a normalized variant that shifts the input vector by its minimum value and scales it by the range of the input vector before multiplication.
Self-Adjust Softmax (SA-Softmax) provides enhanced gradient properties compared to the vanilla softmax function.
LLM-persona combinations favor Western European nations.
LLMs with assigned nation-personas treat the nation-persona's own region more favorably.
Eastern European, Latin American, and African nations are treated more negatively by LLMs with assigned nationality personas.
Evaluations of other nations by LLMs with assigned nationality personas correlate with human survey responses but do not closely match the human survey responses.
Prior prompt engineering (pPE) for reinforcement fine-tuning employs reasoning, planning, code-based reasoning, knowledge recall, and null-example utilization strategies.
Prior prompt engineering (pPE) trained language models outperform inference-time prompt engineering (iPE) prompted language models on in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks.
The null-example prior prompt engineering (pPE) approach achieves the highest performance on AIME2024 and GPQA-Diamond benchmarks among the tested prior prompt engineering approaches.
Different prior prompt engineering (pPE) strategies instill distinct behavioral styles in language models after reinforcement fine-tuning.
Passage embeddings in Dense Passage Retrieval often lie on lower-dimensional, non-linear manifolds in out-of-distribution settings.
Euclidean and cosine distance fail to capture semantic similarity for passage retrieval in out-of-distribution settings.
MA-DPR measures query-passage distance by the shortest path in a nearest-neighbor graph modeling the manifold structure of passages.
MA-DPR outperforms Euclidean and cosine distances on out-of-distribution passage retrieval.

Lotus evaluates translation for Mongolian (in traditional script) and Yi.

SDGO is a reinforcement learning framework that leverages a language model's discrimination capabilities as a reward signal to enhance generation safety through iterative self-improvement.
SDGO does not require additional annotated data or external models during training.
SDGO improves model safety compared to prompt-based and training-based baselines while maintaining helpfulness on general benchmarks.
SDGO provides robust performance against out-of-distribution jailbreaking attacks.
SDGO enhances a language model's generation safety with only a small amount of discriminative samples.
GRAID generates geometrically controlled examples for dataset augmentation in harmful content detection using a constrained Large Language Model.
GRAID promotes stylistic diversity in dataset augmentation for harmful content detection through a multi-agentic reflective process.
GRAID uncovers edge cases in dataset augmentation for harmful content detection through a multi-agentic reflective process.
The combination of geometric constraints and multi-agentic reflection in GRAID enables reliable coverage of the input space for harmful content detection.
The combination of geometric constraints and multi-agentic reflection in GRAID enables nuanced exploration of harmful content.
GraDaSE constructs a graph of datasets using provenance-based and topic-based relationships.
GraDaSE jointly encodes structural and textual information of datasets for ranking.
GraDaSE outperforms strong baselines for dataset search with examples.
DataFinder-E evaluates dataset search with examples.
Artificially generated images for stance detection adhere to textual relevance, target consistency, and stance consistency.
An evaluation framework selects the optimal artificially generated image for stance detection from candidate images.
A multimodal stance detection model uses both textual content and artificially generated images to identify author stance.

Instruction-tuned large language models achieve Word Sense Disambiguation performance on par with specialized Word Sense Disambiguation systems and demonstrate greater robustness across domains and difficulty levels.
Large language models can explain the meaning of words in context in generative settings.
Free-form explanation yields higher performance than definition generation and example generation for explaining word meanings in context.
Math problems used to evaluate compositional generalization are generated by combining two original problems with a logical connection.

A simple combination of two math problems with a logical connection without changing the mathematical essence significantly reduces large language models' performance, revealing a limitation in their generalization ability.
An automated pipeline annotates datasets for compositional math problems.
Fitting transformer-based model embeddings into a stochastic process yields ordered latent representations from unordered model outputs.
BBScoreV2 is a likelihood-based evaluation metric for long text sequences.
Stochastic latent space induces a clustered-to-temporal ordered mapping of language model representations in high-dimensional space.
The clustered-to-temporal ordered mapping of language model representations aligns with intrinsic properties of natural language.
Large Reasoning Models can implicitly self-distill their own reasoning to solve hard problems with near System-1-style efficiency while retaining superior performance.
Large Language Models lack deep internal modes and collapse when forced to rely on their own reasoning rather than imitating external traces.
A two-stage curriculum distillation framework builds a robust internal problem-solving student model and then teaches the student model to externalize latent knowledge as explicit reasoning.
Two-stage curriculum distillation outperforms single-stage baselines on challenging mathematical benchmarks.
Six categorization schemes for task decomposition are identified.
Select-Then-Decompose dynamically selects the most suitable decomposition approach based on task characteristics and enhances result reliability through verification.
Select-Then-Decompose lies on the Pareto frontier for task decomposition, achieving an optimal balance between performance and cost.
Jigsaw-Puzzles evaluates general spatial reasoning capabilities in vision-language models using real-world images with high spatial complexity and minimal reliance on domain-specific knowledge.
Vision-language models achieve lower accuracy than humans on spatial reasoning tasks.
Vision-language models achieve lower accuracy on order generation tasks than on other spatial reasoning tasks.
Three lightweight adaptations to the utility function in Minimum Bayes Risk decoding increase sensitivity to structural variability in the outcome space.
A dataset captures three types of latent structure in language model generations: dialogue act, emotion, and response structure (sentence, paragraph, or list).
Two metrics evaluate the structural optimality of Minimum Bayes Risk decoding.
Common similarity-based utility functions for Minimum Bayes Risk decoding fall short in structural optimality.
Three lightweight adaptations to the utility function in Minimum Bayes Risk decoding improve structural optimality.
Increased structural sensitivity in Minimum Bayes Risk decoding improves generation quality on instruction-following benchmarks.
NOVER is a reinforcement learning framework for incentive training that requires only standard supervised fine-tuning data and does not require an external verifier.
NOVER enables incentive training across a wide range of text-to-text tasks.
NOVER outperforms models of the same size that are distilled from large reasoning models.
NOVER enables inverse incentive training.
ToneCraft generates Cantonese lyrics that ensure tonal and melodic harmony.
ToneCraft enables large language models to generate lyrics with a fixed character count while aligning with tonal and melodic structures.
ToneCraft uses an algorithm that combines character-level control, melodic guidance, and a task-specific loss to achieve tonal harmony without compromising generation flexibility and quality.
ToneCraft leverages pure lyric datasets for training, eliminating the need for aligned data.
ToneCraft achieves better alignment of lyrics with melodic contours than existing methods.
In RAG systems, state-of-the-art retrieval pipelines systematically retrieve highly distracting passages in the top ranks.
In real-world RAG systems, LLM positional bias has marginal impact because both relevant and distracting passages are penalized by position.
In RAG systems, strategies that rearrange passages based on LLM positional preferences do not perform better than random shuffling.
A simulation of structured AI-AI debates over relationship-based moral dilemmas uses a 6-dimensional persona space (age, gender, country, social class, ideology, and personality).
Personas affect initial moral stances and debate outcomes in AI-AI debates over moral dilemmas, with political ideology and personality traits having the strongest influence.
In AI-AI debates over moral dilemmas, persuasive success varies by persona traits, and liberal and open personalities achieve higher consensus.
During AI-AI debates over moral dilemmas, logit-based confidence grows while emotional and credibility-based appeals diminish, leading to more tempered argumentation over time.
AllSides-2024 is a dataset of 2024 news articles labeled with left- or right-leaning stances.
LLMs cite left-leaning sources more frequently than traditional retrieval systems for politically sensitive queries.
The bias in LLM-generated citations arises from a preference for left-leaning media outlets rather than for left-oriented content.
LLMs struggle to infer political bias from news content alone but can almost perfectly recognize the political orientation of media outlets based on their names.
AGSER performs zero-shot hallucination detection in large language models.
AGSER categorizes input queries into attentive and non-attentive queries using attention contributions.
AGSER computes consistency scores between generated responses and the original answer by processing attentive and non-attentive queries separately.
AGSER uses the difference between consistency scores for attentive and non-attentive queries as a hallucination estimator.
AGSER reduces computational complexity for zero-shot hallucination detection.
REACT is a unified two-phase framework for precise and controllable knowledge editing in large language models.

REACT computes a directional belief shift vector for each instance by extracting latent factual representations with tailored stimuli and applying Principal Component Analysis with a learnable linear transformation.
REACT applies controllable perturbations to hidden states using a belief shift vector with a magnitude scalar, gated by a pre-trained classifier that permits edits only when contextually necessary.
REACT reduces overfitting in knowledge editing.
REACT preserves balanced basic editing performance (reliability, locality, and generality) under diverse editing scenarios.
Causal Faithfulness measures explanation faithfulness by quantifying the consistency of causal attributions between explanations and model outputs.
Alignment-tuned models produce more faithful and plausible explanations.
A multi-task learning approach trains models jointly on text summarization, text simplification, and Easy-to-Read generation.
MTL-RAG enables in-context learning for Easy-to-Read text generation.
MTL-LoRA is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method for Easy-to-Read text generation.
ETR-fr is a high-quality dataset for Easy-to-Read text generation.
MTL-LoRA achieves better in-domain performance for Easy-to-Read text generation than other strategies.
MTL-RAG achieves better out-of-domain generalization for Easy-to-Read text generation than other strategies.
TP-RAG is the first benchmark for retrieval-augmented, spatiotemporal-aware travel planning.

Integrating reference trajectories improves spatial efficiency and point-of-interest rationality of travel plans but faces challenges in universality and robustness due to conflicting references and noisy data.
EvoRAG synergizes diverse retrieved trajectories with large language models' intrinsic reasoning.
EvoRAG improves spatiotemporal compliance and reduces commonsense violation in travel planning.
A BERT-based retriever fine-tuned with structure-aware supervision selects semantically relevant and structurally aligned exemplars for in-context learning.
A model-agnostic plug-in module amplifies syntactically meaningful information in retriever hidden representations with minimal overhead.
A two-stage exemplar selection strategy using structural alignment outperforms existing baselines on multiple semantic parsing benchmarks with various large language models.
THYME approaches table retrieval from a field-aware hybrid matching perspective.
Table cells favor finer-grained matching over broader matching due to their fragmented and detailed nature, unlike table titles.
THYME outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in table retrieval.
LILaC uses a layered component graph that represents multimodal information at coarse and fine granularity to facilitate efficient and precise reasoning.
LILaC uses an edge-based late-interaction subgraph retrieval method that first identifies coarse-grained nodes for candidate generation and then performs fine-grained reasoning via late interaction.
LILaC achieves state-of-the-art retrieval performance on five benchmarks without additional fine-tuning.
Socratic-MCTS injects subquestion-subanswer pairs into vision-language models' output streams.
Socratic-MCTS induces long reasoning traces in non-reasoning vision-language models without additional training or supervision.

Framing reasoning as a search process with subquestions as latent decisions helps non-reasoning vision-language models connect the dots between fragmented knowledge and produce extended reasoning traces.
A signalling game-based emergent communication environment generates emergent languages with similarity to human language.
XferBench quantifies the statistical similarity of emergent language to human language by measuring its suitability for deep transfer learning.
Entropy has predictive power on the transfer learning performance of emergent language.
Specific hyperparameters produce emergent languages with better transfer learning performance to human language.
Different distortion types in multimodal misinformation share common reasoning capabilities and require task-specific skills.
TRUST-VL is a unified and explainable vision-language model for general multimodal misinformation detection.
The Question-Aware Visual Amplifier module extracts task-specific visual features.
TRUST-Instruct is a large-scale instruction dataset containing structured reasoning chains aligned with human fact-checking workflows.
TRUST-VL generalizes to zero-shot scenarios for multimodal misinformation detection.
Most advanced Multimodal Large Reasoning Models exhibit prevalent safety degradation.
Multimodal Large Reasoning Models show significant safety degradation on jailbreak robustness benchmarks but less pronounced degradation on safety-awareness benchmarks.
A long thought process enhances safety performance in some scenarios for Multimodal Large Reasoning Models.
Leveraging the intrinsic reasoning capabilities of Multimodal Large Reasoning Models to detect unsafe intent is a potential approach for addressing safety issues.
A multimodal tuning dataset incorporates a safety-oriented thought process.
Fine-tuning Multimodal Large Reasoning Models with a multimodal tuning dataset incorporating a safety-oriented thought process enhances safety on jailbreak robustness and safety-awareness benchmarks.
Selective merging of individually trained source models allows cross-prompt essay scoring adaptation without access to source datasets.
Mixing task vectors (parameter updates from fine-tuning) via a weighted sum simulates selective joint-training.
Prior-encoded Information Maximization (PIM) is an unsupervised objective that promotes score discriminability by leveraging priors pre-computed from source datasets.
Selective merging of individually trained source models consistently outperforms joint-training on all sources.
Selective merging of individually trained source models maintains superior robustness compared to other merging methods.
Selective merging of individually trained source models excels under severe distribution shifts where recent leading cross-prompt methods struggle.
Essays can be modeled as graphs for automated essay scoring.
GAT-AES represents each essay trait as a trait node in a graph and connects each pair of trait nodes with directed edges.
GAT-AES uses a convolutional operator to update node representations by allowing neighboring nodes to influence each other.
GAT-AES models multi-hop dependencies among essay traits.
GAT-AES outperforms alternative graph neural networks and approaches using trait-attention mechanisms for modeling trait dependencies in essay scoring.
Divide-and-Conquer Incremental Search (DCIS) determines better scaling factors for RoPE in large language models.
DCIS reduces performance degradation at extended context lengths.
DCIS enables models to fine-tune on short contexts and generalize to long contexts, reducing fine-tuning costs.
Scaling factors identified by DCIS enable large language models to handle extended context lengths without fine-tuning.
DCIS achieves higher search efficiency for scaling factors than other methods.
ToM-SSI evaluates Theory of Mind capabilities in environments rich with social interactions and spatial dynamics.
ToM-SSI is a multimodal benchmark.
ToM-SSI includes group interactions of up to four agents that communicate and move in situated environments.
ToM-SSI enables the study of mixed cooperative-obstructive settings.
ToM-SSI enables the study of parallel reasoning about multiple agents' mental states.
Current foundation models exhibit severely limited Theory of Mind performance in tasks involving mixed cooperative-obstructive settings and parallel reasoning about multiple agents' mental states.
PRISM uses structured schemas for token-efficient long-range reasoning with short-context LLMs.
PRISM produces concise outputs for long-range reasoning tasks.
PRISM leverages key-value caches to reduce computational costs in long-range reasoning.
PRISM operates at tiny context lengths without increasing computational costs or reducing output quality for long-range reasoning tasks.
PRISM generalizes to new long-range reasoning tasks by generating schemas from task descriptions.
TyleR is a type-less yet type-aware approach for subgraph-based inductive link prediction that leverages pre-trained language models for semantic enrichment.
TyleR outperforms state-of-the-art baselines for inductive link prediction in scenarios with scarce type annotations and sparse graph connectivity.
A defense framework uses the semantic understanding capability of large language models to prevent unauthorized real-time extraction and redistribution of web-based intellectual property.

A defense framework that prevents unauthorized real-time extraction and redistribution of web-based intellectual property achieves higher defense success rates across multiple large language models than configuration-based restrictions.
A large-scale evaluation dataset of routes from metropolises is created for geospatial route cognition.
PathBuilder converts natural language instructions into navigation routes and vice versa.
A new evaluation framework and metrics assess the route reversal capabilities of large language models.
Large language models exhibit limited ability to reverse routes: reverse routes typically do not return to the starting point and are not similar to the optimal route.
Large language models exhibit low robustness in route generation and high confidence in incorrect answers.
Causal Tree Extraction generates a causal tree from a medical case report with the primary disease as the root.
J-Casemap is a Japanese case report dataset for Causal Tree Extraction.
A generation-based method extracts causal trees from medical case reports.
Evaluation metrics for Causal Tree Extraction reflect clinician preferences.
J-Casemap improves performance on medical question answering tasks.
Token-level likelihood features inspired by surprisal and the Uniform Information Density hypothesis capture localized uncertainty in language model input comprehension.
Input-only pre-generation error prediction uses token-level likelihood features inspired by surprisal and the Uniform Information Density hypothesis.
Span-localized features improve pre-generation error prediction for larger language models, while global patterns improve pre-generation error prediction for smaller language models.
Input-only pre-generation error prediction requires no access to language model outputs or hidden activations.
SAFENUDGE combines Controlled Text Generation and nudging to safeguard large language models from jailbreak attacks.
SAFENUDGE reduces successful jailbreak attempts by guiding the large language model towards a safe response during a jailbreak attack.
SAFENUDGE adds minimal latency to inference and has a negligible impact on the semantic fluency of outputs.
SAFENUDGE supports tunable safety-performance trade-offs, allowing practitioners to set their own tolerance for balancing safety and restrictions to normal model behavior.
QRHead enhances retrieval from long context in language models.
QRHead is identified by aggregating attention scores with respect to the input query using real-world task examples.
QRRetriever uses the accumulated attention mass of QRHead as retrieval scores.
QRRetriever improves multi-hop reasoning performance over full context and strong dense retrievers.
QRRetriever achieves strong zero-shot re-ranking performance, outperforming other LLM-based re-rankers.
Query-context attention scoring and task selection are crucial for identifying QRHead with strong downstream utility.
Large Reasoning Model reasoning traces are analyzed using Schoenfeld's Episode Theory.

The first publicly available benchmark for fine-grained analysis of machine reasoning includes a large annotated corpus of model-generated math problem solutions with seven cognitive labels and detailed annotation guidebooks.
Large Reasoning Models exhibit transition dynamics between cognitive states during reasoning.
ImplexConv is a large-scale long-term dataset for studying implicit reasoning in personalized dialogues.
TaciTree structures conversation history into multiple levels of summarization.
TaciTree enables a level-based retrieval process that refines search by progressively selecting relevant details.
TaciTree improves the ability of large language models to reason over long-term conversations with implicit contextual dependencies.
Fine-tuning via open weights or closed fine-tuning APIs can destroy safeguards in helpful-only models.
Jailbreak-tuning enables models to generate detailed, high-quality responses to arbitrary harmful requests.
Backdoors can increase both the stealth and the severity of attacks.
Stronger jailbreak prompts are more effective for fine-tuning attacks and link attacks and potentially defenses in the input and weight spaces.
Current and more recent models appear to be becoming increasingly vulnerable to fine-tuning attacks.
Selective use of checklists tends to improve evaluation performance in pairwise comparison tasks but shows less consistent benefits in direct scoring tasks.
Checklist items with low correlation to human evaluation scores often reflect human-written criteria, indicating potential inconsistencies in human evaluation.
PatentScore decomposes patent claim elements hierarchically.
PatentScore uses validation patterns grounded in legal and technical standards.
PatentScore scores patent claims across structural, semantic, and legal dimensions.
PatentScore achieves higher correlation with expert annotations than widely used NLG metrics for evaluating LLM-generated patent claims.
Expectation Preference Optimization (EPO) uses pairs of sample groups for preference learning.
Expectation Preference Optimization (EPO) produces more reliable preference estimations than pairwise Direct Preference Optimization.
Expectation Preference Optimization (EPO) achieves higher zero-shot accuracy than baseline approaches on reasoning benchmarks.
AQuilt constructs instruction-tuning data for specialized domains from unlabeled data.
AQuilt incorporates logic and self-inspection to encourage reasoning processes and self-inspection.
AQuilt uses customizable task instructions for high-quality data generation for any task.
AQuilt achieves performance comparable to DeepSeek-V3 at significantly reduced production cost.
AQuilt-generated data exhibits higher relevance to downstream tasks.
The human-LLM collaborative annotation framework enables cost-efficient stereotype dataset construction.
EspanStereo is a Spanish-language stereotype dataset spanning multiple Spanish-speaking countries across Europe and Latin America.
EspanStereo captures culturally specific biases absent from English-centric resources.
Spanish-supporting large language models exhibit significant variation in stereotypical behavior across countries.
The human-LLM collaborative annotation framework is adaptable to other languages and regions for multilingual stereotype benchmark construction.
ConTEB evaluates retrieval models on their ability to leverage document-wide context.
State-of-the-art embedding models struggle in retrieval scenarios requiring document-wide context.
InSeNT combined with late chunking pooling enhances contextual representation learning while preserving computational efficiency.
InSeNT combined with late chunking pooling improves retrieval quality on ConTEB without sacrificing base model performance.
Chunks embedded with InSeNT combined with late chunking pooling are more robust to suboptimal chunking strategies and larger retrieval corpus sizes.
Language models continue to compute intermediate reasoning outputs even when retrieving memorized noisy labels.
Intervening reasoning adversely affects the memorization of label noise in language models.

Memorization of label noise in language models operates through distributed encoding by aggregating various inputs and intermediate results rather than building a look-up mechanism from inputs to noisy labels.
In four-digit addition tasks, memorization of label noise in language models occurs via outlier heuristics by slightly shifting existing neuron activation patterns to fit noisy labels.
Memorization and generalization in Large Language Models are associated with distinct neuron subsets.
Neuron-level specialization for memorization and generalization is consistent across different Large Language Models.
Inference-time interventions on specific neurons can steer Large Language Models toward memorization or generalization.
The neuron-behavior associations for memorization and generalization are generalizable patterns rather than dataset-specific artifacts.
Length-normalized uncertainty quantification methods for large language models still exhibit output length bias.
UNCERTAINTY-LINE regresses uncertainty scores on output length and uses the residuals as corrected, length-invariant estimates for uncertainty quantification in large language models.
UNCERTAINTY-LINE is a post-hoc, model-agnostic procedure applicable to multiple uncertainty quantification measures.

UNCERTAINTY-LINE produces uncertainty estimates that are less affected by output length than those from length-normalized methods for large language models on machine translation, summarization, and question-answering tasks.
CIKT uses an Analyst component to generate dynamic, explainable user profiles from student historical responses.
CIKT uses a Predictor component that utilizes the generated user profiles to forecast future student performance.
CIKT iteratively refines the Analyst component based on the predictive accuracy of the Predictor component and subsequently retrains the Predictor component using the enhanced user profiles.
CIKT improves prediction accuracy for knowledge tracing.
CIKT improves scalability for knowledge tracing.
MCIP addresses missing safety mechanisms in MCP.
A fine-grained taxonomy captures diverse unsafe behaviors in MCP scenarios.
Benchmark and training data support evaluating and improving large language models' capabilities to identify safety risks in MCP interactions.
Large language models exhibit vulnerabilities in MCP interactions.
Entity-centric Multimodal Preference Optimization (EMPO) achieves enhanced modality alignment compared to existing human preference alignment methods.
High-quality multimodal preference data is automatically constructed from open-source instruction datasets across image, instruction, and response aspects.
Entity-centric Multimodal Preference Optimization (EMPO) reduces hallucination rates on multimodal hallucination benchmarks.
VoiceCraft-X unifies multilingual speech editing and zero-shot text-to-speech synthesis across 11 languages.
VoiceCraft-X uses the Qwen3 large language model for phoneme-free cross-lingual text processing.

VoiceCraft-X uses a novel token reordering mechanism with time-aligned text and speech tokens to handle multilingual speech editing and zero-shot text-to-speech synthesis as a single sequence generation problem.
VoiceCraft-X achieves robust performance in diverse linguistic settings with limited per-language data.
Graph-based confidence estimation methods are training-free and tailored to reasoning tasks.
Graph-based confidence estimation methods model reasoning paths as directed graphs.
Graph-based confidence estimation methods estimate confidence by exploiting graph properties including centrality, path convergence, and path weighting.
Graph-based confidence estimation methods improve confidence estimation for reasoning tasks.
Graph-based confidence estimation methods enhance performance on downstream reasoning tasks.
ToolSafety contains direct harm samples, indirect harm samples, and multi-step samples.
ToolSafety supports multi-step safety through synthesized trajectories.
ToolSafety uses realistic, context-aware sample generation.
Models fine-tuned on ToolSafety maintain safety in multi-step and indirect harm scenarios.
Superficial alignment risks are less severe in non-multi-step settings than in multi-step settings.
An LLM-assisted pipeline for routing U.S. Environmental Protection Agency citizen tips filters out a large portion of irrelevant tips.
An LLM-assisted pipeline for routing U.S. Environmental Protection Agency citizen tips improves routing accuracy compared to the current system.
StepER uses step-wise supervision to align with evolving information and reasoning demands across stages.
StepER incorporates difficulty-aware training to progressively optimize learning by prioritizing suitable steps.
StepER works across various frameworks of multi-step retrieval-augmented language models, including those based on reasoning paths or question decomposition.
StepER enables a smaller model to achieve performance comparable to a much larger teacher model on multi-hop QA tasks.
Compositional entity extraction for online job advertisements represents requirements as complex tree-like structures that connect atomic entities via typed relations.
GOJA is a manually annotated dataset of German job advertisements that captures roles, tools, experience levels, attitudes, and their functional context.
The GOJA dataset has strong inter-annotator agreement.
Transformer models can learn the complex tree-like structure of requirements in online job advertisements.
Compositional entity extraction of AI-related requirements in job advertisements provides analytical value for labor market research.
Collaborative Beam Search uses multiple large language models to generate diverse candidate steps by exploring a broader search space.

Collaborative Beam Search verifies candidate steps using a perplexity-based collective consensus among multiple large language models, eliminating the need for an external verifier or complex prompts.

Collaborative Beam Search reassigns generation budget among multiple large language models using a dynamic quota allocation strategy based on past performance to balance candidate diversity and quality.
Collaborative Beam Search achieves higher accuracy than single-model scaling and multi-model ensemble baselines on arithmetic, logical, and commonsense reasoning tasks.
Sequence-driven structural causal models (SD-SCMs) specify causal models with user-defined structure and language-model-defined mechanisms.
Sequence-driven structural causal models enable sampling from observational, interventional, and counterfactual distributions according to a desired causal structure.
A benchmark for causal inference generates individual-level counterfactual data to test treatment effect estimation.
A benchmark for causal inference that generates individual-level counterfactual data shows that causal methods outperform non-causal methods in treatment effect estimation.
A benchmark for causal inference that generates individual-level counterfactual data reveals that state-of-the-art causal inference methods struggle with individualized effect estimation.
TrinityX uses a Mixture of Calibrated Experts (MoCaE) within the Transformer architecture with calibrated, task-adaptive routing for Helpfulness, Harmlessness, and Honesty alignment.
TrinityX outperforms strong baselines on alignment benchmarks for Helpfulness, Harmlessness, and Honesty.

Calibrated routing is critical for achieving high alignment performance in TrinityX.
TrinityX generalizes across diverse large language model backbones.
Statistical affinity in a RoBERTa model robustly distinguishes semantically distinct constructions that are superficially similar.
Statistical affinity in a RoBERTa model robustly distinguishes schematic constructions with slots for abstract word classes.
Statistical affinity alone is insufficient to identify all constructions from text.
Statistical affinity is an important but partial signal for language learners.
Concise-SAE identifies and edits instruction-relevant neurons using natural language instructions in a training-free manner.
FreeInstruct evaluates instruction following in narrative-rich settings.
Concise-SAE achieves state-of-the-art instruction adherence across varied tasks without compromising generation quality.
In Qwen-based models, attention modules in the earliest layers contribute more to factual recall than MLP modules.
Architectural variations within the autoregressive Transformer family can lead to fundamentally different mechanisms of factual recall.
Online Opinion Mining Benchmark (OOMB) provides (entity, feature, opinion) tuples and an opinion-centric insight for each content instance.
Online Opinion Mining Benchmark (OOMB) enables the evaluation of both extractive and abstractive capabilities of models for opinion mining.
Large language models face challenges in certain aspects of opinion mining but exhibit adaptability in others in realistic online scenarios.
Vanilla LLMs can only handle graph partitioning on extremely small-scale graphs.
Coarsening reduces graph complexity for large-scale graph partitioning with LLMs.
Reasoning captures global and local patterns to generate a coarse partition for large-scale graph partitioning with LLMs.
Refining ensures topological consistency by projecting coarse-grained partitioning results back to the original graph structure for large-scale graph partitioning with LLMs.
A three-phase pipeline enables LLMs to perform graph partitioning across varying graph scales.

Temporal Expert Averaging creates expert models with functional diversity and parameter similarity by fine-tuning a domain-agnostic base model on individual temporal domains with constrained weight changes.
Temporal Expert Averaging sets adaptive averaging coefficients based on the projected proximity of expert models to future domains in a principal component subspace of temporal weight trajectories.
Temporal Expert Averaging outperforms prior Temporal Domain Generalization methods while being more computationally efficient.
SimulatorArena is a benchmark of annotated human–LLM conversations for math tutoring and document creation.
SimulatorArena evaluates user simulators based on message similarity to human behavior and alignment of assistant ratings with human judgments.
Simulators conditioned on user profiles that capture background and message styles align closely with human judgments for math tutoring and document creation tasks.
Simulators conditioned on user profiles that capture background and message styles provide a practical, scalable alternative to human evaluation for math tutoring and document creation tasks.
Grade School Math with Distracting Context (GSM-DC) is a synthetic benchmark for evaluating Large Language Models' reasoning robustness against systematically controlled irrelevant context.
Grade School Math with Distracting Context (GSM-DC) constructs symbolic reasoning graphs with precise distractor injections.
Large Language Models are significantly sensitive to irrelevant context, affecting reasoning path selection and arithmetic accuracy.
Training Large Language Models with strong distractors improves reasoning robustness in in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios.
Stepwise tree search guided by a process reward model enhances reasoning robustness of Large Language Models in out-of-distribution conditions.
A self-adversarial approach generates low-quality rationales by sampling previous iterations of smaller language models for negative knowledge in rationale distillation.
A contrastive loss distills both positive and negative knowledge into smaller language models.
An online-updating discriminator assesses the qualities of rationales and assigns appropriate weights to optimize training in rationale distillation.
Quality-guided contrastive rationale distillation yields higher-quality rationales than existing distillation techniques in reasoning tasks.
CABLE learns token-specific, context-aware biases for each attention head in transformers.
CABLE achieves lower perplexity than other positional encoding methods when evaluated on longer sequences in GPT-2 Medium.
CABLE improves performance in long-context retrieval tasks when applied to BERT base.
Formulating the model merging process as a linear system preserves output feature directions from individual models.
Formulating the model merging process as a linear system creates merged models that maintain multi-task capabilities.
A threat model focuses on adversarial users exploiting policy-adherent agents for personal benefit.
CRAFT is a multi-agent red-teaming system that uses policy-aware persuasive strategies to undermine policy-adherent agents in customer-service scenarios.
Tau-break assesses the robustness of policy-adherent agents against manipulative user behavior.
Defense strategies for policy-adherent agents provide some protection but are insufficient against adversarial attacks.
Hanfu-Bench includes a cultural visual understanding task that examines temporal-cultural feature recognition through multiple-choice visual question answering on single- or multi-image inputs.
Hanfu-Bench includes a cultural image transcreation task that transforms traditional attire into modern designs by inheriting cultural elements and adapting to modern contexts.

Retrieving noisy and irrelevant information degrades GraphRAG performance.

GraphRAG-Filtering refines retrieved information using a two-stage filtering mechanism.
