GraphRAG-FI improves reasoning performance on knowledge graph QA tasks across multiple backbone models.
Forensic Unlearning Membership Attacks (FUMA) assesses whether unlearning leaves behind detectable artifacts that can be exploited to infer membership in the forget set.
Optimization-based unlearning methods leave detectable artifacts that allow membership inference attacks to identify forget set examples.
Retrieval-based unlearning evaluation benchmarks do not evaluate for privacy risks from membership inference attacks.
Infini-gram mini makes petabyte-level text corpora searchable.
Infini-gram mini simultaneously indexes and compresses text.
Infini-gram mini improves indexing speed and reduces memory usage compared to existing FM-index implementations.
Several core language model evaluation benchmarks are heavily contaminated in Internet text crawls.
A benchmark contamination bulletin provides contamination rates for core and community-contributed language model evaluation benchmarks in Internet text.
DIPPER is a training-free framework that transforms a single large language model into an effective inference-time ensemble.
DIPPER uses an optimized and diverse set of prompts in parallel to elicit varied reasoning paths.
A DIPPER ensemble of three Qwen2-MATH-1.5B instances outperforms Qwen2-MATH-7B on the MATH benchmark.
Aggregative Question Answering requires models to reason over large-scale conversation logs to answer aggregational queries.
WildChat-AQA is a benchmark of aggregative questions derived from real-world chatbot conversations.
Existing methods for aggregative question answering struggle to reason effectively over large-scale conversation logs.
Existing methods for aggregative question answering incur prohibitive computational costs when processing large-scale conversation logs.
Knowledge Composition Sampling expands the diversity of generated multi-hop questions by sampling varied knowledge compositions within a given context.
Knowledge Composition Sampling models knowledge composition selection as a sentence-level conditional prediction task.
Knowledge Composition Sampling uses a probabilistic contrastive loss to predict the next most relevant piece of knowledge.
Knowledge Composition Sampling employs a stochastic decoding strategy to balance accuracy and diversity during inference.

An extended procedural mistake detection task requires generating visual self-dialog rationales to inform decisions.
A benchmark dataset for procedural mistake detection uses individual video frames.
Two automated metrics for the coherence of generated rationales use natural language inference.

Forward KL divergence and Reverse KL divergence have complementary roles, with Forward KL divergence boosting underestimated tokens and Reverse KL divergence suppressing overestimated tokens.

A top-k setup evaluates semantic-change discovery without requiring complete annotations.

The SemEval-EN benchmark has extended annotations.

JQL curates diverse and high-quality multilingual data at scale.
JQL reduces computational demands for multilingual data curation.
JQL distills large language models' annotation capabilities into lightweight annotators based on pretrained multilingual embeddings.
Lightweight annotators based on pretrained multilingual embeddings exhibit robust multilingual and cross-lingual performance for languages and scripts unseen during training.
JQL outperforms existing heuristic filtering methods.
JQL increases data retention rates.
GenLink uses a generation-based learning process for schema linking in text-to-SQL to capture implicit schema relationships.
GenLink integrates multiple small language models to ensure robust cross-domain adaptability for text-to-SQL schema linking.
Pragmatic Inference Chain integrates findings from cognitive science and linguistics.
Pragmatic Inference Chain improves the identification of implicit toxic language by large language models compared to baseline prompting methods.
Pragmatic Inference Chain facilitates large language models to produce more explicit and coherent reasoning processes.
DocAgent is a multi-agent framework for multi-modal long-context document understanding that imitates human reading practice.
DocAgent extracts a structured, tree-formatted outline from documents to help agents identify relevant sections efficiently.
DocAgent uses an interactive reading interface that enables agents to query and retrieve various types of content dynamically.
DocAgent includes a reviewer agent that cross-checks responses using complementary sources and maintains a task-agnostic memory bank to facilitate knowledge sharing across tasks.
DocAgent bridges the gap to human-level performance on long-context document understanding benchmarks while maintaining a short context length.
Diagram-Driven Course Questions Generation (DDCQG) generates course-relevant questions about specific diagram elements.
DiagramQG is a dataset of diagrams and course-relevant questions.
Diagram-Driven Course Questions Generation requires domain-specific knowledge across courses.
Diagram-Driven Course Questions Generation has a long-tail distribution in course coverage.
Diagrams for course question generation have high information density.
The Hierarchical Knowledge Integration framework (HKI-DDCQG) identifies relevant diagram patches using trainable CLIP.
The Hierarchical Knowledge Integration framework (HKI-DDCQG) extracts knowledge using frozen vision-language models.
The Hierarchical Knowledge Integration framework (HKI-DDCQG) generates questions using trainable T5.
The Hierarchical Knowledge Integration framework (HKI-DDCQG) outperforms existing models on the DiagramQG dataset and generalizes to natural image datasets.
SMART reconceptualizes sycophancy as a reasoning optimization problem rather than an output alignment issue.
SMART uses Uncertainty-Aware Adaptive Monte Carlo Tree Search that dynamically adjusts exploration based on state-level uncertainty.
SMART uses progress-based reinforcement learning to distill improved reasoning patterns into model adaptation.
SMART reduces sycophancy in large language models while maintaining performance on out-of-distribution inputs.
Large language models often have shallow understanding of puns and lack nuanced grasp typical of human interpretation.
Subtle changes in puns are sufficient to mislead large language models.
Comprehensive and nuanced pun detection benchmarks evaluate model performance on pun detection.
Human evaluation assesses the performance of recent large language models on pun detection.
Collab-Overcooked is an LLM-based Multi-Agent System benchmark built on the Overcooked-AI game with more applicable and challenging tasks in interactive environments.
Collab-Overcooked provides a multi-agent framework that supports diverse tasks and objectives and encourages collaboration through natural language communication.
Collab-Overcooked introduces process-oriented evaluation metrics to assess the fine-grained collaboration capabilities of LLM agents.
LLMs exhibit strong ability in goal interpretation but have significant shortcomings in active collaboration and continuous adaptation.
Several pruning methods are adapted for state-space models.
State-space model-based large language models are robust to pruning with some methods, including WANDA, but degrade quickly with other pruning methods.
LapEigvals uses the top-k eigenvalues of the Laplacian matrix derived from attention maps as input for hallucination detection probes.
LapEigvals achieves state-of-the-art hallucination detection performance among attention-based methods.
LapEigvals demonstrates robustness and generalisation.
MAKAR uses a Knowledge Enhancement Agent that leverages a Multimodal Large Language Model as an implicit knowledge base to enhance ambiguous image-text content using internal knowledge.
MAKAR uses an Entity Correction Agent that retrieves and summarizes web content to correct entities using internal and external knowledge for samples with low-confidence entity boundaries and types.
MAKAR uses an Entity Reasoning Grounding Agent that applies multi-step Chain-of-Thought reasoning to perform entity grounding.
MAKAR achieves state-of-the-art results on Grounded Multimodal Named Entity Recognition benchmarks.
LATTE offloads perception to vision specialists to focus solely on reasoning over high-quality perceptual information.
The multi-modal reasoning traces dataset is synthesized and filtered from perceptual outputs of vision specialists.
LATTE outperforms baselines on benchmarks covering both perception and reasoning abilities.
The effectiveness of multi-modal reasoning traces depends on the data sources, formats, and quality of thoughts.
Specific regimes exist in finite samples where LLM bias or limited expert labels significantly affect parameter estimates from Design-based Supervised Learning and Prediction-Powered Inference.
Design-based Supervised Learning and Prediction-Powered Inference achieve low bias with large datasets.
Design-based Supervised Learning often outperforms Prediction-Powered Inference on bias reduction and empirical efficiency across multiple tasks.
Design-based Supervised Learning has less consistent performance across datasets than Prediction-Powered Inference.
A bias-variance tradeoff exists between Design-based Supervised Learning and Prediction-Powered Inference.
SAFEL evaluates rejection of unsafe commands via the Command Refusal Test.

SAFEL evaluates generation of safe and executable plans via the Plan Safety Test, which decomposes the task into goal interpretation, transition modeling, and action sequencing for fine-grained diagnosis of safety failures.
EMBODYGUARD is a PDDL-grounded benchmark containing LLM-generated scenarios for overtly malicious and contextually hazardous instructions.
Current LLMs often reject clearly unsafe commands but struggle to anticipate and mitigate subtle, situational risks in embodied decision making.
A novel loss maximizes the summed marginal likelihood of multiple positive samples per query.
LLM-generated annotations enhance out-of-domain retrieval performance compared to models trained solely on human annotations or downstream QA metrics.
LLM-generated annotations improve RAG outcomes compared to models trained solely on human annotations or downstream QA metrics.
Combining LLM annotations with 20% of human labels achieves performance comparable to full human annotations.
Interlocutor awareness is the capacity to identify and adapt to the identity and characteristics of a dialogue partner.
Large language models reliably identify same-family peers and prominent model families such as GPT and Claude.
Interlocutor awareness enhances multi-LLM collaboration through prompt adaptation.
Interlocutor awareness introduces new alignment and safety vulnerabilities, including reward-hacking behaviors and increased jailbreak susceptibility.
Fine-tuning datasets often contain samples with safety-degrading features that are not easily identifiable on the surface.
Layer-Aware Representation Filtering uses representations from safety-sensitive layers to detect data samples with safety-degrading features in post-training datasets.
Removing data samples with safety-degrading features mitigates safety alignment degradation during fine-tuning.
SEGALE extends automatic machine translation evaluation to long documents.
SEGALE treats documents as continuous text and applies sentence segmentation and alignment methods.
SEGALE outperforms existing long-form document evaluation schemes.
SEGALE produces evaluation results comparable to those using groundtruth sentence alignments.
Many open-weight large language models fail to effectively translate book-length documents at their reported maximum context lengths.
BIRD is a fully encoded dataset for bronze inscription restoration and dating, grounded in standard scholarly transcriptions and chronological labels.
An allograph-aware masked language modeling framework integrates domain- and task-adaptive pretraining with a Glyph Net that links graphemes and allographs.
The Glyph Net improves bronze inscription restoration.
Glyph-biased sampling improves bronze inscription dating.
Assay2Mol retrieves existing assay records for targets similar to a new target and uses in-context learning with large language models to generate candidate compounds with the retrieved assay data.
Assay2Mol outperforms recent machine learning approaches for generating candidate ligand compounds for target protein structures and generates more synthesizable candidate compounds.
TALON answers questions over long tables using a multi-agent framework.
TALON's planning agent iteratively invokes a tool agent to access and manipulate tabular data based on intermediate feedback, progressively collecting necessary information for answer generation.
TALON's critic agent ensures accuracy and efficiency in tool usage and planning.
Two new benchmarks for long-table question answering are derived from WikiTableQuestion and BIRD-SQL and contain tables ranging from 50 to over 10,000 rows.
TALON sets a new state-of-the-art in long-table question answering across multiple language models.
CS-TaxoBench is a benchmark of 460 taxonomies extracted from human-written survey papers.
TaxoAlign is a three-phase topic-based instruction-guided method for scholarly taxonomy generation.
An automated evaluation framework measures structural alignment and semantic coherence between automatically generated taxonomies and human-created taxonomies.
LCES formulates zero-shot automated essay scoring as a pairwise comparison task.
LCES uses large language models to judge which of two essays is better.
LCES employs RankNet to transform large language model preferences into scalar scores for scalability.
LCES outperforms conventional zero-shot automated essay scoring methods in accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.
LCES is robust across different large language model backbones.
ReConnect transforms indirectly relevant documents into a direct explanation to answer questions.
ReConnect extracts relevant knowledge from various retrieved document subsets and aggregates them into a direct explanation to answer questions.
ReConnect outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on in-domain and out-of-domain commonsense reasoning benchmarks.
Both Multi-Head Attention and Feed-Forward Network independently benefit from long-context supervised fine-tuning in large language models.
Long-context supervised fine-tuning promotes contextual knowledge, while short-context supervised fine-tuning favors parametric knowledge in large language models.
Exclusive reliance on long-context supervised fine-tuning is suboptimal for large language models.
Hybrid training mitigates the knowledge preference bias in supervised fine-tuning of large language models.
LASER scores ASR outputs using LLM in-context learning on prompts with detailed examples.
LASER scores for Hindi using Gemini 2.5 Pro correlate highly with human annotations.
LASER prompts with Hindi examples are effective for error analysis in Marathi, Kannada, and Malayalam.
A smaller LLM finetuned on word-pair examples from reference and ASR predictions predicts the type of penalty for ASR errors.
Most performance improvements in mathematical reasoning for small-scale language models via supervised fine-tuning arise from format alignment rather than genuine reasoning enhancement.

Supervised fine-tuning effectiveness for mathematical reasoning in small-scale language models is strongly influenced by the alignment between the base model’s inductive biases and the teacher model’s output distribution, rather than the teacher’s raw strength.

Scaling up training data for mathematical reasoning via supervised fine-tuning in small-scale language models offers diminishing returns and does not fundamentally alter the mathematical reasoning behavior of small-scale language models.
Partial Adaptation scales down the strength of instruction-tuning.
Reducing instruction-tuning strength improves in-context few-shot learning performance on classic natural language tasks but reduces instruction following ability.

A large-scale dataset annotates third-person honorific pronoun and verb usage in Hindi and Bengali Wikipedia articles with socio-demographic attributes including gender, age group, fame, and cultural origin.
Honorifics are more prevalent in Bengali than in Hindi.
Non-honorifics dominate when referring to infamous, juvenile, and culturally exotic entities.
Men are more frequently addressed with honorifics than women in both Hindi and Bengali, and this disparity is more prominent in Hindi.
Large language models diverge from Wikipedia usage in honorific selection across tasks, languages, and socio-demographic attributes.
Large Vision-Language Models mistakenly perceive text inputs without visual evidence as part of the image, causing erroneous responses.
A subset of Feed-Forward Network neurons, called Visual Absence-aware neurons, signal the visual absence of tokens through a distinctive activation pattern.
A detection module classifies whether an input token is visually grounded by leveraging activation patterns of Visual Absence-aware neurons.
A method refines model outputs by reinterpreting question prompts or replacing detected absent tokens during generation.
Speculative Streaming integrates speculative draft generation directly within the target model using multi-stream attention.
Speculative Streaming generates speculative tokens non-autoregressively with interdependencies between tokens.
Speculative Streaming requires fewer additional parameters and FLOPs than prior single-model speculative decoding approaches.
Speculative Streaming includes a lossless mode that preserves the output of any pre-trained model.
Speculative Streaming includes a shared mode that optimizes speedup and downstream performance.
Machine-translated documents from a high-quality English corpus can significantly contribute to the pretraining quality of multilingual language models.
TransWebEdu is a multilingual corpus created by machine-translating FineWeb-Edu into nine languages.
TransWebLLM is a multilingual language model pretrained from scratch on TransWebEdu.
TransWebLLM matches or outperforms similar-sized multilingual language models on non-English understanding and reasoning tasks with significantly less training data.
Continued pretraining with a small amount of domain-specific data achieves state-of-the-art results for understanding and commonsense reasoning in Arabic, Indonesian, Swahili, and Welsh.
DEER uses a label-guided, token-based retriever for example selection that prioritizes tokens most informative for entity recognition.
DEER identifies error-prone tokens using label statistics and prompts the LLM to make targeted corrections on them for entity recognition.
DEER outperforms existing in-context learning methods and approaches the performance of supervised fine-tuning for entity recognition.
DEER is effective for recognizing both seen and unseen entities.
DEER is robust in low-resource settings for entity recognition.
TurBLiMP is the first Turkish benchmark of linguistic minimal pairs for evaluating the linguistic abilities of language models.
TurBLiMP focuses on word order flexibility and subordination through morphological processes in Turkish.
Cutting-edge large language models struggle with grammatical phenomena that are not challenging for humans.
Cutting-edge large language models exhibit different sensitivities to word order and morphological complexity compared to humans.
Hybrid authorship graphs model humans, LLMs, and texts in a provenance network.
Epistemic doppelgängers are LLM-generated texts that are indistinguishable from human-authored texts.
Proof-of-interaction authorship verification is a mechanism for restoring confidence in language.
Large-scale text generation can induce semantic drift.
Large-scale text generation can erode accountability.
Large-scale text generation can obfuscate intent and authorship.
HydraOpt is a model merging technique that capitalizes on the inherent similarities between the matrices of low-rank adapters.
HydraOpt allows navigating the trade-off between storage size and performance in adapter merging.
HydraOpt reduces storage size compared to storing all adapters while achieving competitive performance.
HydraOpt outperforms existing merging techniques in performance at equivalent or slightly worse storage efficiency.
In multi-label classification, the initial probability distribution for the first label in large language models does not reflect the eventual final output in terms of relative order.
In multi-label classification, large language models suppress all but one label at each generation step.

In multi-label classification, increasing the scale of large language models leads to lower entropy and higher single-label confidence in token distributions while improving the internal relative ranking of the labels.
In multi-label classification, supervised finetuning and reinforcement learning amplify the tendency of large language models to suppress all but one label at each generation step.
The task of distribution alignment for multi-label classification aligns large language model-derived label distributions with empirical distributions from annotator responses in subjective tasks.

In multi-label classification with large language models, a method that takes the maximum probability over all label generation distributions improves distribution alignment and classification performance without additional computation.
Representation redundancy caused by neuron collapse in the parameter space is the underlying reason for reward hacking in Direct Preference Optimization.
Weights-Rotated Preference Optimization implicitly constrains the output layer logits using the KL divergence inherited from Direct Preference Optimization.
Weights-Rotated Preference Optimization explicitly constrains intermediate hidden states by fine-tuning on a multi-granularity orthogonal matrix.
Weights-Rotated Preference Optimization alleviates the reward hacking problem in Direct Preference Optimization.
BiasUnlearn uses dual-pathway unlearning mechanisms to coordinate stereotype forgetting with anti-stereotype retention for targeted debiasing.
BiasUnlearn prevents bias polarity reversal using an adversarial forget set and dynamic dataset swapping.
BiasUnlearn mitigates bias in language models more effectively than existing methods without degrading language modeling capabilities.
BiasUnlearn's debiasing weights are transferable across model variants.
Bias representations in language models become entrenched during pre-training and persist through fine-tuning phases.
Anecdoctoring automatically generates adversarial prompts across languages and cultures.
Anecdoctoring clusters misinformation claims into broader narratives.
Anecdoctoring characterizes claim clusters with knowledge graphs.
Anecdoctoring uses knowledge graphs of claim clusters to augment an attacker LLM.
Anecdoctoring achieves higher attack success rates than few-shot prompting.
Anecdoctoring offers interpretability benefits over few-shot prompting.
BridgeX-ICL is a method for zero-shot cross-lingual in-context learning for low-resource languages.
Language overlap neurons are defined using neuron probe data from MUSE bilingual dictionaries to ensure full activation.
An HSIC-based metric quantifies LLMs' internal linguistic spectrum based on overlapping neurons to guide optimal bridge selection.
Dynamic argument planning in argumentative writing assesses viewpoint divergence and progressively reveals the hierarchical argument outline using a depth-then-breadth paradigm.
Iterative defender-attacker interactions in argumentative writing simulate human debate.
Incorporating Bitzer’s rhetorical situation theory in argumentative writing enables flexible selection of appropriate rhetorical techniques.
A debate-driven rhetorical framework improves logical depth, argumentative diversity, and rhetorical persuasiveness in argumentative essay generation.
CIE finetunes language models to expect a control vector interpolated between a low token embedding and a high token embedding to control response-length.
CIE exerts response-length control more reliably than in-context learning methods or fine-tuning methods that use discrete control signals.
Information density measures based on matrix verbs' subcategorization probability capture substantial idiosyncratic lexical variation.
Information density estimates derived from contextual word embeddings account for additional variance in complementizer usage patterns.
Framing-divergent Event Coreference (FrECo) identifies pairs of event mentions that refer to the same underlying occurrence but differ in framing across documents.
The FrECo corpus is a high-agreement and diverse dataset of event mentions that refer to the same underlying occurrence but differ in framing across documents.
A bootstrapped mining pipeline iteratively expands the training set with high-confidence pairs of event mentions that refer to the same underlying occurrence but differ in framing across documents.
Orthogonal finetuning (OFT) has a computational bottleneck due to weight-centric implementation relying on matrix-matrix multiplications with cubic complexity.
OFTv2 is an input-centric reformulation of orthogonal finetuning that uses matrix-vector multiplications, achieving quadratic computational complexity.
The Cayley–Neumann parameterization approximates the matrix inversion in the Cayley transform using a truncated Neumann series.
OFTv2 achieves faster training and lower GPU memory usage without compromising model performance.
OFTv2 supports finetuning quantized foundation models and outperforms QLoRA in training stability, efficiency, and memory usage.
ViClaim is a multilingual multilabel dataset of video transcripts in English, German, and Spanish covering six topics with sentences labeled as fact-check-worthy, fact-non-check-worthy, or opinion.
An annotation tool supports labeling video transcript sentences as fact-check-worthy, fact-non-check-worthy, or opinion.
Multilingual language models exhibit a performance gap between cross-validation and unseen topics in claim detection for video transcripts.
Branch-Train-Stitch (BTS) is an efficient and flexible training algorithm that combines independently trained large language model experts into a single generalist model.
Lightweight stitch layers are inserted between frozen experts and the seed language model and trained on a small datamix of expert domains to combine experts into a generalist model.

Stitch layers enable the seed language model to integrate representations from any number of experts during the forward pass, allowing generalization to new domains while the seed model remains frozen.
BTS does not alter the constituent language models, enabling a modular approach where experts can be easily removed and new experts can be added with minimal training.
BTS achieves higher generalist performance than alternative model merging approaches on downstream tasks while retaining the specialized capabilities of each expert.
SwarmAgentic fully automates agentic system generation, optimization, and collaboration.
SwarmAgentic constructs agents from scratch.
SwarmAgentic jointly refines agent functionality and coordination via language-driven exploration.
SwarmAgentic maintains a population of candidate systems and evolves them via feedback-guided updates inspired by Particle Swarm Optimization.
SwarmAgentic outperforms baseline methods on the TravelPlanner benchmark for structurally unconstrained tasks.
A focus-level evaluation framework for LLM reviews operationalizes focus as a normalized distribution of attention across predefined facets in paper reviews.
An automatic focus-level evaluation pipeline assesses LLM-generated paper reviews using target facets (problem, method, experiment) and aspect facets (validity, clarity, novelty).
Off-the-shelf large language models, when generating critical assessments in paper reviews, exhibit a biased focus towards technical validity and overlook novelty assessment.
ViMUL-Bench evaluates Video LMMs across 14 languages: Arabic, Bengali, Chinese, English, French, German, Hindi, Japanese, Russian, Sinhala, Spanish, Swedish, Tamil, and Urdu.
ViMUL-Bench tests video LMMs across 15 categories including eight culturally diverse categories such as lifestyles, festivals, foods, rituals, local landmarks, and prominent cultural personalities.
ViMUL-Bench comprises open-ended (short and long-form) and multiple-choice questions for short, medium, and long videos, with samples manually verified by native language speakers.
ViMUL provides a better tradeoff between high-resource and low-resource languages for video understanding.
SMART aligns simulated students to instructed ability levels to predict the difficulty of open-ended items.

SMART forms preference pairs based on response likelihood under a ground-truth item response theory model and applies direct preference optimization to align simulated students to instructed ability levels.

SMART generates simulated student responses, evaluates them with a large language model-based scoring model, and fits the resulting data to an item response theory model to obtain item difficulty estimates.
SMART provides more accurate item difficulty predictions than other methods by leveraging improved ability alignment.
SATBench evaluates the logical reasoning capabilities of large language models through logical puzzles derived from SAT problems.
SATBench translates SAT formulas into logical puzzles using large language models.
SATBench adjusts puzzle difficulty by varying the number of clauses in the SAT formula.
SATBench validates puzzles using LLM-based and solver-based consistency checks and human validation on a subset.
Large language models achieve accuracy close to random baseline on hard UNSAT problems in SATBench.
Large language models exhibit systematic failures including satisfiability bias, context inconsistency, and condition omission in search-based logical reasoning.
M-Spoiler simulates agent interactions within a multi-agent system to generate adversarial samples.
M-Spoiler uses a stubborn agent to optimize adversarial samples by simulating potential stubborn responses from agents in the target system.
Knowledge of an individual agent in multi-agent systems can be used to manipulate collective decisions.
M-Spoiler is more potent than baseline attack methods against defense mechanisms in multi-agent systems.

Collaborative Rational Speech Act (CRSA) uses an information-theoretic gain function adapted from rate-distortion theory to model collaborative multi-turn dialog where both agents have private information and produce utterances conditioned on the dialog.
Collaborative Rational Speech Act (CRSA) yields more consistent, interpretable, and collaborative behavior than existing baselines in referential games and medical doctor-patient dialogs.
RD-MCSA integrates classification rationales with adaptively selected demonstrations for multi-class sentiment analysis under limited supervision.
A tailored balanced coreset algorithm selects a representative, class-balanced subset of annotated samples for generating semantically grounded classification rationales.
A similarity-based mechanism powered by a multi-kernel Gaussian process selects demonstrations to pair with classification rationales.
RD-MCSA outperforms supervised baselines and standard In-Context Learning methods on multi-class sentiment analysis across five benchmark datasets.
Core Parameter Isolation Fine-Tuning identifies core parameter regions for each task by quantifying parameter update magnitudes during independent fine-tuning.
Core Parameter Isolation Fine-Tuning groups tasks with similar core parameter regions based on region overlap to form clusters for joint modeling.

Core Parameter Isolation Fine-Tuning transplants core parameters from individually fine-tuned models into a unified backbone and integrates non-core parameters via Spherical Linear Interpolation to mitigate destructive interference.
Core Parameter Isolation Fine-Tuning employs a lightweight, pipelined supervised fine-tuning phase that freezes core regions from prior tasks to prevent catastrophic forgetting.
Core Parameter Isolation Fine-Tuning alleviates task interference and catastrophic forgetting in multi-task fine-tuning.
Fully personalized hate speech detection models increase user welfare but produce predictions that violate legal hate speech boundaries, especially for users who tolerate highly hateful content.
Overriding predictions from personalized models with those from a boundary classifier enforces legal boundaries in personalized hate speech detection.
Overriding predictions from personalized models with those from a boundary classifier reduces legal violations while minimally affecting user welfare.
LLaMP is a hierarchical multi-agent framework that emulates the materials science research workflow.
LLaMP includes a high-level supervisor agent that decomposes user requests into sub-tasks and coordinates with specialized assistant agents.
LLaMP includes specialized assistant agents that handle domain-specific tasks including retrieving and processing data from the Materials Project and conducting simulations.
A novel metric combines uncertainty and confidence estimates to evaluate the self-consistency of responses.
LLaMP achieves superior performance in material property retrieval, crystal structure editing, and annealing molecular dynamics simulations.
Sparse autoencoders identify toxicity-related directions in the residual stream of large language models to enable targeted activation steering for detoxification.
Steering aggressiveness levels exhibit trade-offs between toxicity reduction and language fluency in large language models.
Stronger steering strengths reduce toxicity more than competitive baselines but can degrade language fluency on GPT-2 Small at higher aggressiveness levels.
Standard NLP benchmark scores remain stable after activation steering, indicating preserved model knowledge and general abilities.
Feature-splitting in wider sparse autoencoders hampers safety interventions for large language model detoxification.
SPaRC evaluates spatial and rule-based reasoning through 2D grid pathfinding puzzles requiring step-by-step planning with arithmetic and geometric rules.
Humans achieve near-perfect accuracy on SPaRC puzzles while the best reasoning models perform poorly, especially on hard puzzles.
Models frequently generate invalid paths on SPaRC puzzles.
Models make errors in navigation and spatial logic as revealed by reasoning tokens on SPaRC puzzles.
Models fail to scale test-time compute with puzzle difficulty on SPaRC.
Multiple solution attempts improve model accuracy on SPaRC puzzles.
Orthogonality of features across residual vector quantization layers hinders effective language model training.
Descending semantic richness in tokens from deeper residual vector quantization layers exacerbates exposure bias during autoregressive decoding.
Siren employs multiple isolated transformers with causal conditioning and anti-causal alignment via reinforcement learning for text-to-audio generation.
Siren outperforms existing language model-based and diffusion-based text-to-audio generation systems.
MUSE identifies and aggregates well-calibrated subsets of large language models using an information-theoretic approach with Jensen-Shannon Divergence for multi-LLM uncertainty quantification.
MUSE improves calibration and predictive performance on binary prediction tasks compared to single-model and naive ensemble baselines.
MUSE provides guided signals for chain-of-thought distillation to fine-tune large language models for calibration.
EMO maps tokens one-to-one via Minimum Edit Distance in Cross-Tokenizer Knowledge Distillation.
EMO distills intra-model relational knowledge by aligning attention matrix patterns using Centered Kernel Alignment on the top-m most important tokens.

EMO aligns final hidden states via Optimal Transport with Importance-Scored Mass Assignment, emphasizing semantically important token representations using importance scores derived from attention weights.
EMO outperforms existing Cross-Tokenizer Knowledge Distillation baselines on embedding-reliant tasks.
A comprehensive taxonomy of Multilingual Knowledge Editing methods covers parameter-based, memory-based, fine-tuning, and hypernetwork approaches.
Multilingual Knowledge Editing faces persistent challenges including cross-lingual propagation, language anisotropy, and limited evaluation for low-resource and culturally specific languages.
False positive solutions in mathematical problem solving for language models persist across different models, datasets, and decoding methods.
Sampling-based inference time scaling methods do not alleviate false positive solutions in mathematical problem solving for language models.
The pass@N evaluation metric is more susceptible to false positives in mathematical problem solving, indicating a lower scaling ceiling than automatic evaluations suggest.

TREPAT generates initial rephrasings for adversarial attacks on detectors of low-credibility content using large language models with prompts inspired by meaning-preserving NLP tasks such as text simplification and style transfer.
TREPAT decomposes modifications into small changes and applies them via beam search until the victim classifier changes its decision.
TREPAT achieves superior performance in constrained scenarios with limited query budgets for long input texts such as news articles.
CODI compresses natural language Chain-of-Thought into continuous space.
CODI distills reasoning ability from language into continuous space by aligning hidden states of a designated token.
CODI is the first implicit Chain-of-Thought approach to match the performance of explicit Chain-of-Thought on GSM8k at the GPT-2 scale.
CODI generalizes to complex datasets.
Large language models can reason effectively in a latent continuous space.
ReDepress is a clinically validated social media dataset for depression relapse detection, comprising 204 Reddit users annotated by mental health professionals.
A cognitive framework for depression relapse detection incorporates attention bias, interpretation bias, memory bias, and rumination into annotation and modeling.
Cognitive markers significantly differentiate depression relapse and non-relapse groups in social media data.
Cognitive theories of depression are validated in social media data.
DSG-MCTS dynamically integrates multiple reasoning strategies, including abductive and analogical reasoning, to expand the reasoning space.
DSG-MCTS enhances reasoning efficiency with a dynamic strategy selection mechanism that adapts to the task context.
DSG-MCTS outperforms state-of-the-art methods on challenging reasoning benchmarks in accuracy and efficiency.
Re-Align constructs a dual-preference dataset for aligning Vision Language Models by leveraging image retrieval to incorporate both textual and visual preference signals.
rDPO extends direct preference optimization for aligning Vision Language Models by incorporating an additional visual preference objective during fine-tuning.
Re-Align mitigates hallucinations in Vision Language Models more effectively than prior alignment methods.
Re-Align improves performance on general visual question-answering tasks.
Re-Align maintains robustness and scalability across diverse Vision Language Model sizes and architectures.
FinCap defines the core capabilities required for the finance domain.
FinRec jointly optimizes continual pre-training and instruction-following and distills preference data using process signals from a generative reward model.
FinTrain provides curated training datasets for financial domain adaptation.
FinEval evaluates financial domain adaptation capabilities defined by FinCap.
Each stage of domain-adaptive post-training for finance contributes to distinct model capabilities.
Cognitive Combination Synthesis (CCS) synthesizes data efficiently and scalably.
Cognitive Combination Synthesis (CCS) recombines and interconnects heterogeneous data from diverse sources to mimic human cognitive behaviors.
Cognitive Combination Synthesis (CCS) enhances advanced reasoning capabilities in large language models.
Mapping-based combination learning improves data utilization efficiency.
Cognitive Combination Synthesis (CCS) achieves strong scalability in synthetic data generation by enhancing data diversity, accuracy, and complexity.
Two approaches for Arabic lemmatization frame lemmatization as classification into a Lemma-POS-Gloss tagset using machine translation and semantic clustering.
A new Arabic lemmatization test set covers diverse genres and is standardized to be consistent with existing datasets.
Classification and clustering for lemmatization yield more robust and interpretable outputs than character-level sequence-to-sequence models.
Character-level sequence-to-sequence models for lemmatization are limited to predicting lemmas and do not predict the full Lemma-POS-Gloss tagset.
Character-level sequence-to-sequence models for lemmatization are prone to hallucinating implausible forms.
LaMP-QA evaluates personalized long-form answer generation.
LaMP-QA covers questions from Arts & Entertainment, Lifestyle & Personal Development, and Society & Culture categories.
Incorporating personalized context improves performance in personalized question answering.
Large language models sometimes struggle to generate self-generated counterfactual explanations (SCEs).
When large language models struggle to generate self-generated counterfactual explanations (SCEs), the models' predictions often do not agree with the models' own counterfactual reasoning.
T2 dynamically adapts reasoning depth based on question complexity for contextual question answering.
T2 achieves higher accuracy than baseline methods on contextual question answering benchmarks.
T2 reduces computational overhead for contextual question answering.
Document Information Gain (DIG) quantifies the contribution of retrieved documents to correct answer generation.
Document Information Gain (DIG) measures a document's value by the difference in LLM generation confidence with and without the document.
InfoGain-RAG trains a specialized reranker using Document Information Gain scores to rank retrieved documents.
InfoGain-RAG filters out irrelevant documents and selects the most valuable ones for answer generation.
InfoGain-RAG outperforms existing approaches in single and multiple retrieval paradigms.
Ambiguous content in preference pairs during Direct Preference Optimization training may introduce ambiguities that degrade performance.
Ambiguity Awareness Optimization automatically re-weights ambiguous content to reduce ambiguities by calculating semantic similarity from preference pairs.

Ambiguity Awareness Optimization consistently surpasses state-of-the-art approaches in performance without markedly increasing response length across multiple model scales and widely adopted benchmark datasets.
Spotlight produces concise, engaging narratives by highlighting the most compelling aspects of a document.
New datasets are curated for benchmarking spotlight generation.
Spotlight generation is implemented by fine-tuning a large language model on benchmark data followed by alignment via Direct Preference Optimization.
Spotlight generation identifies key elements with precision.
Spotlight generation enhances readability.
Spotlight generation boosts the engagement value of the original document.
LLM-based data augmentation for retrieval exhibits diminishing returns beyond a certain scale of augmentation, even when using diverse augmentation strategies.
Using smaller LLMs for data augmentation achieves performance competitive with using larger LLMs for data augmentation in retrieval tasks.
LLM-based data augmentation provides the most benefit to retrieval models that are not well pre-trained.
Route Sparse Autoencoder integrates a routing mechanism with a shared sparse autoencoder to extract features from multiple layers of large language models.
Route Sparse Autoencoder dynamically assigns weights to activations from different layers of large language models.
Route Sparse Autoencoder incurs minimal parameter overhead.
Route Sparse Autoencoder extracts a greater number of features than baseline sparse autoencoders under the same sparsity constraint.
Route Sparse Autoencoder achieves higher interpretability scores than baseline sparse autoencoders under the same sparsity constraint.
Pace is a dataset of high-distress episodes constructed from first-person narratives and structured around Psychological First Aid (PFA).
Pacer provides both empathetic and directive support for panic attack counseling.
PanicEval is a multi-dimensional evaluation framework for general counseling quality and crisis-specific strategies in panic scenarios.
Pacer outperforms strong baselines in counselor-side metrics and client affect improvement for panic attack counseling.
Pacer is consistently preferred over general, CBT-based, and GPT-4-powered models in human evaluations of panic attack counseling.
VggCaps is a multi-modal dataset that pairs audio with corresponding video and uses large language models to generate rich, descriptive captions.
VggCaps captions have greater length, lexical diversity, and human-rated quality than existing benchmarks.
Multi2Cap learns audio-visual representations through an AV-grounding module during pre-training.
Multi2Cap reconstructs visual semantics using audio alone at inference.
Multi2Cap enables visually grounded captioning in audio-only scenarios.
Training an ultra-long prefix in a stylized setting has a convergence guarantee under the Neural Tangent Kernel framework.
An algorithm that introduces and fine-tunes a few extra trainable parameters per transformer layer approximates prefix attention to a guaranteed polynomial-small error.

An algorithm for prefix learning that uses few extra trainable parameters per transformer layer achieves performance that is superior or competitive to full parameter fine-tuning, P-Tuning V2, and LoRA on vision, natural language, and math tasks.
Features dependent on continuous flow of language show the highest divergence between human and AI texts in the body segment.
The body segment is the most informative segment for detecting AI-generated text.
Human texts exhibit greater stylistic variation across introduction, body, and conclusion segments than AI texts.
CBDT decoding estimates the expected utility using examples of domain data.
CBDT decoding generates higher-quality texts than maximum a posteriori (MAP) decoding.

The combination of minimum Bayes risk (MBR) decoding and case-based decision-theoretic (CBDT) decoding outperforms minimum Bayes risk (MBR) decoding in multiple domain-specific translation tasks and image captioning tasks.
CLEANMOL formulates SMILES parsing into a suite of clean and deterministic tasks, including subgraph matching and global graph matching, to promote graph-level molecular comprehension.
A molecular pretraining dataset with adaptive difficulty scoring is constructed for SMILES parsing.
VideoEraser erases undesirable concepts in text-to-video diffusion models without additional training.
VideoEraser prevents text-to-video diffusion models from generating videos with undesirable concepts even when explicitly prompted with those concepts.
VideoEraser uses Selective Prompt Embedding Adjustment for concept erasure in text-to-video diffusion models.
VideoEraser uses Adversarial-Resilient Noise Guidance for concept erasure in text-to-video diffusion models.
VideoEraser outperforms prior methods in efficacy, integrity, fidelity, robustness, and generalizability for concept erasure in text-to-video diffusion models.
An oracle classification task on generative model outputs enables randomized smoothing.
Under bounded oracle classifier error rate, the robustness radius in randomized smoothing depends on the number of samples.
Improved scaling laws relate the certified radius and accuracy to the number of samples in randomized smoothing.
Randomized smoothing maintains minimal loss with fewer samples under weaker assumptions.
Robustness certification is well-defined for state-of-the-art vision-language models.
Robustness certification is computationally feasible for state-of-the-art vision-language models.
Embedding-only Editing (EmbEdit) edits implicit assumptions and priors in text-to-image models without affecting unrelated objects or degrading overall performance.
Embedding-only Editing (EmbEdit) fine-tunes only the word token embedding of the target object to edit implicit assumptions and priors.
Embedding-only Editing (EmbEdit) applies to any text-to-image model with a text encoder.
Embedding-only Editing (EmbEdit) allows easy reversal of edits by restoring the original word token embedding layers.
Embedding-only Editing (EmbEdit) outperforms previous methods in multiple text-to-image models, tasks, and editing scenarios including single and sequential multiple edits.
Distillation from frontier model reasoning traces enables inference-time scaling for table reasoning tasks.
Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards enables inference-time scaling for table reasoning tasks.
Table-R1-Zero, a 7B-parameter model, matches or exceeds the performance of larger models on table reasoning tasks.
Table-R1-Zero generalizes to out-of-domain table reasoning datasets.
Essential table reasoning skills emerge during reinforcement learning training on table reasoning tasks.
SPE attention is equivariant to semantic-preserving permutation of code.
A directed layered graph represents code structure using symmetry relationships.
A symmetry mask is derived from a directed layered graph of code structure.
IPIGuard models the task execution process of LLM agents as a traversal over a planned Tool Dependency Graph (TDG).
IPIGuard decouples action planning from interaction with external data.
IPIGuard reduces unintended tool invocations triggered by injected instructions.
IPIGuard enhances robustness against Indirect Prompt Injection attacks.
Unstructured pruning for Mamba state-space models achieves high parameter reduction with minimal performance loss.
Unstructured pruning for Mamba state-space models using both weight and gradient importance preserves critical parameters.
A gradual pruning schedule maintains model stability for Mamba state-space models.
A global pruning strategy optimizes parameter allocation across Mamba state-space models.
Unstructured pruning for Mamba state-space models reduces inference time and memory usage.
Mamba state-space models are robust to pruning.
Common diversity metrics and reward models for preference optimization systematically bias language models toward shorter outputs, limiting expressiveness.
Diverse-NS is a length-controlled data selection strategy that improves response diversity while maintaining length parity.
Diverse-NS generates and filters preference data that balances diversity, quality, and length to enable effective training with a limited number of preference pairs.
Diverse-NS enhances lexical and semantic diversity in LLaMA-3.1-8B and Olmo-2 family models.
Diverse-NS improves diversity without significant loss in response quality on Divergent Associations, Persona Generation, Alternate Uses, and Creative Writing tasks.

Koel-TTS uses a reward system for preference alignment that ranks speech generation outputs using automatic metrics derived from speech recognition and speaker verification models to encourage matching input text and speaker identity.
Classifier-free guidance in Koel-TTS allows fine-grained control over the influence of conditioning inputs during inference by interpolating conditional and unconditional logits.
Applying classifier-free guidance to a preference-aligned text-to-speech model improves transcription accuracy and speaker similarity.
Koel-TTS achieves state-of-the-art results in zero-shot multilingual text-to-speech for intelligibility, speaker similarity, and naturalness with less training data than prior models.
A graph neural network separates temporal dynamics from static handshape configurations in sign language processing.
A graph neural network combines anatomically informed graph structures with contrastive learning for handshape recognition.
The first benchmark evaluates structured handshape recognition in signing sequences.
Language-to-Space Programming (LaSP) uses LLM-generated codes to analyze 3D spatial relations among objects for training-free 3D visual grounding.
Language-to-Space Programming (LaSP) automatically evaluates and optimizes the generated codes for training-free 3D visual grounding.
Language-to-Space Programming (LaSP) provides a balanced trade-off between performance and efficiency for training-free 3D visual grounding.
Persona prompting has three intended effects: performance advantage of expert personas, robustness to irrelevant persona attributes, and fidelity to persona attributes.
Expert personas in language models usually lead to positive or non-significant performance changes.
Language models are highly sensitive to irrelevant persona details, leading to significant performance drops.
Higher education, specialization, and domain-relatedness in persona attributes can boost performance but their effects are often inconsistent or negligible across tasks.
Mitigation strategies for robustness in persona prompting only work for the largest, most capable language models.
ToM constructs a DocTree through hierarchical semantic parsing of long documents.
ToM performs bottom-up aggregation on the DocTree.
ToM generates rationales at child nodes during the Map step of the Tree MapReduce approach.
ToM aggregates rationales across sibling nodes to resolve conflicts or reach consensus at parent nodes during the Reduce step of the Tree MapReduce approach.
ToM outperforms divide-and-conquer frameworks and retrieval-augmented generation methods in logical coherence and long-context reasoning.
PAKTON is a fully open-source, end-to-end, multi-agent framework with plug-and-play capabilities for question answering in long legal agreements.
PAKTON handles contract analysis through collaborative agent workflows.
PAKTON uses a novel retrieval-augmented generation component for contract analysis.
PAKTON enables automated legal document review that is accessible to non-experts, adaptable, and privacy-preserving.
PAKTON outperforms general-purpose and pretrained models in predictive accuracy, retrieval performance, explainability, completeness, and grounded justifications.
ProxyReward Dataset synthesis uses simple prompts to enable a language model to automatically create training data without extensive labeled data or significant manual effort.
ProxyReward Signal provides a targeted evaluation of information comprehensiveness and accuracy for specific questions.
ProxyReward surpasses GPT-4-Turbo on open-ended long text generation tasks.
ProxyReward surpasses the LLM-as-a-Judge approach on open-ended long text generation tasks.

An LLM-based collaborative annotation framework refines trigger annotations from distant supervision via collaboration among multiple LLMs and a voting process and then carries out argument annotation.
EEMT is the largest event extraction dataset to date.
LLM-PEE recalls candidate event types and splits them into multiple partitions for event extraction.
The EEMT dataset has high event diversity.
LLM-PEE has strong generalization capabilities.
AdaRewriter uses test-time adaptation for conversational query reformulation via an outcome-supervised reward model.
AdaRewriter trains a lightweight reward model with contrastive ranking loss.
AdaRewriter selects the most promising reformulation during inference.
AdaRewriter operates effectively in black-box systems, including commercial LLM APIs.
AdaRewriter outperforms existing methods for conversational query reformulation in most settings.
MS-RAG creates chunk-level, relation-level, and entity-level indexes using dense vectors and knowledge graphs.
MS-RAG uses a mix recall algorithm for retrieval that does not rely on large language models for entity extraction.
MS-RAG includes a multi-semantic rerank module to purify retrieval results.
MS-RAG achieves faster inference speed in question-answering tasks than prior graph-based retrieval-augmented generation methods.
The output probability of text-generation models can be underconfident.
Lower model output probability in text-generation does not necessarily indicate lower output quality.
BoostedProb boosts model confidence during text-generation when multiple viable output options exist.
BoostedProb's quality estimates correlate better with ground-truth quality than raw model probability in different settings without increasing complexity.
BoostedProb comes close to or outperforms supervised and ensemble-based quality estimation approaches in certain settings.

All quality feedback types except error highlights improve decision accuracy and appropriate reliance relative to no feedback for monolingual users deciding whether to share machine translation outputs.

Implicit feedback yields greater gains than explicit feedback in decision accuracy, appropriate reliance, and user perceptions for monolingual users deciding whether to share machine translation outputs.

Question-answer tables receive the highest ratings for helpfulness and trust and the lowest mental burden among the evaluated quality feedback types for monolingual users deciding whether to share machine translation outputs.
NormGenesis generates and annotates socially grounded dialogues in English, Chinese, and Korean.
Violation-to-Resolution (V2R) models the progression of conversations following norm violations through recognition and socially appropriate repair.
Exemplar-based iterative refinement aligns with linguistic, emotional, and sociocultural expectations before full dialogue generation begins.
NormGenesis provides a dataset of multi-turn dialogues annotated at the turn level for norm adherence, speaker intent, and emotional response.
NormGenesis produces dialogues that outperform existing datasets in refinement quality, dialogue naturalness, and generalization performance.
Models trained on Violation-to-Resolution-augmented data exhibit improved pragmatic competence in ethically sensitive contexts.
STIM attributes each token in a reasoning chain to one of multiple memorization sources (local, mid-range, or long-range) based on statistical co-occurrence with the token in the pretraining corpus.
Large Language Models rely more on memorization in complex or long-tail Chain-of-Thought reasoning cases.
Local memorization is often the dominant driver of errors in Chain-of-Thought reasoning of Large Language Models.
Memorization scores from STIM predict wrong tokens in incorrect reasoning steps.
Increasing the temperature parameter during inference reduces AI-text detection accuracy.
TempParaphraser simulates high-temperature sampling effects through multiple normal-temperature generations.
TempParaphraser reduces AI-text detector accuracy.
TempParaphraser preserves text quality.
Training on TempParaphraser-augmented data improves AI-text detector robustness.
WebMMU unifies multilingual website visual question answering, code editing involving HTML/CSS/JavaScript, and mockup-to-code generation using expert-annotated real-world web data.
Multimodal large language models perform well on basic information extraction in multilingual web tasks.

Multimodal large language models struggle with complex multi-step reasoning and precise element grounding, code editing that preserves functionality, and design-to-code generation that maintains hierarchy and supports multilingual content in multilingual web tasks.
SSA-MTE is a large-scale human-annotated machine translation evaluation dataset for 13 African language pairs in the News domain.
SSA-COMET is a reference-based machine translation evaluation metric.
SSA-COMET-QE is a reference-free machine translation evaluation metric.
SSA-COMET and SSA-COMET-QE outperform AfriCOMET in machine translation evaluation for low-resource African languages.
SSA-COMET and SSA-COMET-QE are competitive with Gemini 2.5 Pro in machine translation evaluation for low-resource African languages.

A single d-dimensional steering vector per layer trained with reinforcement learning while freezing base weights matches the accuracy of fully RL-tuned reasoning models across multiple base models and mathematical-reasoning benchmarks.
Millions of adapter weights are unnecessary for high-level chain-of-thought reasoning.
A minimal trainable footprint reduces optimizer memory and inter-GPU communication, lowering the overall cost of fine-tuning.
Steering vectors amplify coherent token directions, providing clearer insight into model internal computations.
Def_Wiki and Def_ArXiv are resources for autoformalization that collect mathematical definitions from Wikipedia and arXiv papers, respectively.
Mathematical definitions are more challenging for LLM autoformalization than existing benchmarks.
LLMs struggle with self-correction in autoformalization of mathematical definitions.
Structured refinement methods improve self-correction capabilities in LLM autoformalization.
Definition grounding strategies reduce undefined errors in LLM autoformalization.
UltraIF decomposes real-world user prompts into simpler queries, constraints, and corresponding evaluation questions for the constraints.
UltraIF trains an UltraComposer to compose constraint-associated prompts with evaluation questions.
UltraComposer synthesizes complicated instructions and filters responses using evaluation questions.

UltraIF aligns a base large language model to match the performance of its instruct-tuned version on instruction-following benchmarks without using benchmark-specific information, using an 8B model for response generation and evaluation.
UltraIF improves an instruct-tuned large language model via self-alignment.
LensGNN uses large language models as an ensembler for multiple graph neural networks.
LensGNN aligns multiple graph neural networks by mapping their representations into the same space.
LensGNN uses LoRA fine-tuning to align the space between graph neural networks and large language models, injecting graph tokens and textual information into large language models.

LensGNN ensembles multiple graph neural networks and leverages the strengths of large language models to achieve a deeper understanding of textual semantic information and graph structural information.
BanMiMe is the first comprehensive Bangla misogynistic meme dataset of culturally grounded samples with misogyny labels, humor categories, metaphor localization, and human-written explanations.
Vision-language models show varying performance in misogyny detection and explanation generation for Bangla memes under zero-shot and prompt-based settings.
Chain-of-Thought fine-tuning enhances model accuracy and the generation of meaningful explanations for misogyny detection in Bangla memes.
Intent-based chart generation from documents generates charts adhering to user-specified intents and grounded on documents in a zero-shot setting.
An unsupervised two-staged method for intent-based chart generation from documents extracts relevant information by decomposing the intent and iteratively validating and refining the data.
A heuristic-guided module selects an appropriate chart type for intent-based chart generation from documents.
An attribution-based metric assesses chart data accuracy using a structured textual representation of charts.
A dataset for intent-based chart generation from documents comprises tuples of intents, documents, and charts from finance and scientific domains.
LOADS selects optimal label sets for zero-shot in-context learning with large language models.
Lexical choice of labels impacts model performance in zero-shot in-context learning.
Lexical choice of labels impacts model sensitivity to label order in zero-shot in-context learning.
Optimal label words activate fewer outlier neurons in large language models' feed-forward networks in zero-shot in-context learning.
LOADS measures neuron activation distribution using kurtosis for label selection in zero-shot in-context learning with a single forward pass and without gradient propagation or labelled data.
LOADS-selected label words improve zero-shot in-context learning across multiple classification tasks, datasets, models, and languages.
Retrieval-Augmented Voting (RAV) retrieves similar visual-tactile data for given visual and tactile inputs without training.
Retrieval-Augmented Voting (RAV) generates tactile descriptions through a voting mechanism without training.
Retrieval-Augmented Voting (RAV) achieves performance comparable to large-scale cross-modal models without training.
Retrieval-Augmented Voting (RAV) performance improves with data of higher annotation accuracy and diversity without additional computational cost.
MERMAID is a multi-agent framework that integrates a multi-perspective self-reflection module, an emotion-guided visual augmentation module, and a cross-modal verification module.
MERMAID enables agents to interact across modalities and reinforce subtle emotional semantics.
MERMAID exhibits greater robustness in emotionally diverse scenarios.
Child-directed language does not consistently improve syntax learning in language models and is usually outperformed by adult-directed text.
FIT-CLAMS uses a frequency-controlled design to enable balanced comparisons of syntactic ability across training corpora.
Frequency effects must be controlled when evaluating syntactic ability in language models.
A computational approach characterizes changes in a protagonist's views and behavior and the interplay between them along the narrative timeline.
In Holocaust survivor testimonies, a constant disposition toward religious belief is common.
In Holocaust survivor testimonies, an oscillating structure for religious practice is common.

CR4-NarrEmote is a large-scale open-vocabulary dataset of narrative emotions annotated via citizen science from passages of long-form fiction and non-fiction across multiple decades, genres, and Anglophone cultural contexts.
CR4-NarrEmote provides mappings from narrative emotion annotations to dimensional (Valence-Arousal-Dominance) and categorical (NRC Emotion) frameworks.
Citizen science annotations in CR4-NarrEmote show substantial alignment with expert-generated labels as measured by lexical, categorical, and semantic agreement.
Med-VRAgent uses Visual Guidance, Self-Reward, and Monte Carlo Tree Search for medical visual reasoning.
Med-VRAgent collects trajectories that are used to fine-tune vision-language models with the proximal policy optimization objective.
Med-VRAgent outperforms existing approaches on multiple medical visual question answering benchmarks.
Overly short reasoning in chain-of-thought augmented Large Language Models degrades performance on simple mathematical problems.
Reasoning length in chain-of-thought augmented Large Language Models is governed by a linear direction in the representation space.
ThinkEdit is an interpretable weight-editing approach that mitigates overly short reasoning in chain-of-thought augmented Large Language Models.
A small subset of attention heads predominantly drives short reasoning behavior in chain-of-thought augmented Large Language Models.
ThinkEdit improves accuracy on mathematical reasoning tasks.

A benchmark framework evaluates robustness of Large Audio-Language Models against audio injection attacks across four attack scenarios: Audio Interference Attack, Instruction Following Attack, Context Injection Attack, and Judgment Hijacking Attack.

Five leading Large Audio-Language Models exhibit significant performance disparities in robustness against audio injection attacks, with no single model demonstrating consistent robustness across all attack types.
Attack effectiveness against audio injection is significantly influenced by the position of malicious content, especially when injected at the beginning of a sequence.

A negative correlation exists between a model's instruction-following capability and its robustness against audio injection attacks: models that strictly adhere to instructions are more susceptible, while safety-aligned models exhibit greater resistance.
Debate, Train, Evolve (DTE) is a ground truth-free training framework that uses multi-agent debate traces to evolve a single language model.
Reflect-Critique-Refine is a prompting strategy that instructs agents to critique and refine their reasoning to improve debate quality.
Debate, Train, Evolve (DTE) demonstrates strong cross-domain generalization.
Debate, Train, Evolve (DTE) captures general reasoning capabilities.
AlignX aligns multilingual representations in multilingual large language models using multilingual semantic alignment and language feature integration.
AlignX stimulates multilingual capability in multilingual large language models via multilingual instruction fine-tuning.
AlignX enhances multilingual large language models' multilingual general generation capability and cross-lingual generation capability.
AlignX brings multilingual representations closer together and improves cross-lingual alignment in multilingual large language models.
Soft rank follows a power law with Feed-Forward Network width in large language models.
Hard rank grows sublinearly with Feed-Forward Network width and has high variance in large language models.
At larger widths, much of the latent space in Feed-Forward Networks is under-utilized.
Feed-Forward Network width selection is a principled trade-off between tail capacity and dominant-mode capacity.
AutoSDT searches for diverse sources, selects ecologically valid tasks, and synthesizes accurate task instructions and code solutions for data-driven discovery using LLMs.
AutoSDT-5K is a dataset of coding tasks for data-driven discovery covering multiple scientific disciplines and numerous Python packages.
AutoSDT-5K is the only automatically collected and the largest open dataset for data-driven scientific discovery.
Most tasks in AutoSDT-5K are ecologically valid.
Most code solutions in AutoSDT-5K are functionally correct.
Self-improved language models exhibit enhanced in-domain reasoning accuracy but degraded out-of-domain reasoning capability due to memorization of training data.
During self-improvement, reasoning-critical layers of language models receive fewer updates than less relevant layers.
Iterative Model Merging balances reasoning improvements and generalization by merging the weights of the original and self-improved language models.
Iterative Model Merging mitigates model collapse and improves generalized reasoning capability.
EvoBot refines generation via Direct Preference Optimization guided by a co-adapting detector that improves its ability to distinguish EvoBot from humans.
EvoBot generates content aligned with diverse user profiles.
EvoBot increasingly bypasses the co-adapting detector through human-like expression.
EvoBot more accurately models real-world opinion dynamics and information spread in multi-agent simulations.
The co-adapting detector is more robust for distinguishing EvoBot from humans.
Adaptive retrieval methods for Retrieval-Augmented Generation based on external information do not require a large language model for uncertainty estimation.
LLM-independent adaptive retrieval methods achieve comparable performance to LLM-based adaptive retrieval methods with improved efficiency.
WildDoc evaluates document understanding in natural environments.
WildDoc includes diverse manually captured document images that reflect real-world conditions.
WildDoc uses document sources from established benchmarks to compare document understanding in natural environments with digital or scanned documents.
WildDoc captures each document multiple times under varying conditions to evaluate model robustness.
Multimodal Large Language Models exhibit performance declines and inadequate robustness on WildDoc compared to traditional benchmarks.
A comprehensive survey provides an in-depth overview of LLM-based judgment and assessment.
LLM-as-a-judge is defined from input and output perspectives.
A systematic taxonomy for LLM-as-a-judge has dimensions: what to judge, how to judge, and how to benchmark.
SemCSE learns semantic embeddings of scientific texts in an unsupervised manner.
SemCSE leverages LLM-generated summaries of scientific abstracts to position semantically related summaries closer together in the embedding space.
SemCSE enforces stronger semantic separation in the embedding space.
A benchmark evaluates models' ability to understand and encode the semantic content of scientific texts.
SemCSE achieves state-of-the-art performance on the SciRepEval benchmark among models of its size.
RewardDS fine-tunes a reward proxy model to guide synthetic data generation for privacy-preserving large language model fine-tuning.
Reward Guided Filtering filters synthetic data using reward signals.
Self-Optimizing Refinement refines synthetic data.
The CORRECT-DETECT trade-off is the inability of large language models to balance coreference disambiguation and ambiguity detection in coreference simultaneously with minimal prompting.
Style in personal narratives is formalized as patterns in linguistic choices made by authors when communicating subjective experiences.
Sequential patterns in linguistic choices in personal narratives are linked to psychological observations.
Language models automatically extract linguistic features including processes, participants, and circumstances.
In dream narratives of a war veteran with post-traumatic stress disorder, verbal processes dominate over mental processes.
ScIRIFF covers five core scientific literature understanding capabilities: information extraction, summarization, question answering, claim verification, and classification.
ScIRIFF is the only entirely expert-written, high-quality instruction-following dataset for extracting and synthesizing information from research literature across diverse scientific fields.
ScIRIFF features complex instructions with long input contexts, detailed task descriptions, and structured outputs.
LLMs finetuned on ScIRIFF improve performance on out-of-distribution scientific literature tasks compared to baselines trained only on general-domain instructions.
RTE-GMoE works with any backbone model for relation triplet extraction.
RTE-GMoE models mutual interactions among task-specific experts for entity recognition, relation extraction, and relation triplet extraction.
RTE-GMoE achieves comprehensive and adaptive graph-based mixture-of-experts interactions.
RTE-GMoE enables collaborative knowledge extraction among experts through dynamic information exchange and knowledge sharing.
RTE-GMoE consistently improves relation triplet extraction performance across multiple backbones and benchmarks.
A value-grounded framework evaluates and distills structured moral reasoning in language models.
Prompting with explicit moral structure improves accuracy and coherence in moral reasoning.
First-principles reasoning and a scaffold combining Schwartz’s framework and care ethics yield the strongest gains in moral reasoning.
Supervised distillation transfers moral competence from large language models to small language models without additional inference cost.
Future research ideas generated by Claude-2 and GPT-4 are more aligned with the perspective of research paper authors than those generated by GPT-3.5 and Gemini.
Future research ideas generated by Claude-2 are more diverse than those generated by GPT-4, GPT-3.5, and Gemini 1.0.
DSSP-RAG refines traditional self-attention into a mixed-attention that distinguishes shared and private semantics for controlled knowledge integration.
DSSP-RAG uses an unsupervised hallucination detection method that captures LLMs' intrinsic cognitive uncertainty to ensure external knowledge is introduced only when necessary.
The Energy Quotient (EQ) is defined by attention difference matrices between task-aligned and task-misaligned layers to reduce noise in external knowledge.
MFCIG-CSS constructs a semantic interaction graph and a prosody interaction graph for multimodal dialogue history at the word level.

MFCIG-CSS's semantic interaction graph and prosody interaction graph encode interactions between word-level semantics, prosody, and their influence on subsequent utterances in multimodal dialogue history.
MFCIG-CSS outperforms all baseline models in prosodic expressiveness on the DailyTalk dataset.
FINDER uses a generative retriever to extract relevant facts from unstructured text and tables for financial numerical reasoning.
FINDER uses context-aware Program of Thought prompting with dynamic selection of in-context examples for financial numerical reasoning.
FINDER achieves state-of-the-art performance on the FinQA and ConvFinQA datasets.
RICO refines captions through visual reconstruction.
RICO generates a reference image from a caption using a text-to-image model.
RICO identifies discrepancies between the original image and the reconstructed image using a multimodal large language model to refine the caption.
RICO performs iterative refinement to progressively generate more faithful and comprehensive descriptions.
RICO-Flash generates captions like RICO using direct preference optimization.
RICO improves caption accuracy and completeness.
Half-truth detection identifies claims that are factually correct but misleading due to omitted critical context.
PolitiFact-Hidden is a benchmark of political claims with sentence-level evidence alignment and inferred claim intent annotations.
TRACER identifies omission-based misinformation by aligning evidence, inferring implied intent, and estimating the causal impact of hidden content.
TRACER improves performance on half-truth detection across multiple baselines.
Early incorrect tokens in language model generation are associated with a higher likelihood of subsequent tokens being factually incorrect.
Streaming-VR enables real-time verification and correction of tokens during language model generation using another language model.
Streaming-VR enhances the factual accuracy of language model outputs.
Streaming-VR is more efficient than prior refinement methods.
Understanding-to-Reasoning Transition (URT) fine-tuning gradually shifts a model's focus from interpreting long chain-of-thoughts to generating long chain-of-thoughts.
Understanding-to-Reasoning Transition (URT) fine-tuning incorporates partial reasoning steps in the input context.
Understanding-to-Reasoning Transition (URT) fine-tuning outperforms standard fine-tuning on rigorous reasoning benchmarks.
Understanding-to-Reasoning Transition (URT) fine-tuning maintains robust performance on long-context comprehension tasks.
Active Layer-Contrastive Decoding (ActLCD) actively decides when to apply contrasting layers in large language model generation.

Active Layer-Contrastive Decoding (ActLCD) casts decoding as a sequential decision-making problem and employs a reinforcement learning policy guided by a reward-aware classifier to optimize factuality beyond the token level in large language model generation.
Active Layer-Contrastive Decoding (ActLCD) mitigates hallucinations in diverse large language model generation scenarios more effectively than state-of-the-art methods.
