An alignment-augmented speculative decoding algorithm requires no training.
Alignment sampling leverages the output distribution from the prefilling phase to generate more aligned draft candidates.
Conditional verification uses an adaptive probability threshold to utilize high-quality but non-aligned draft candidates.
The alignment-augmented speculative decoding algorithm improves generation accuracy and inference efficiency on question answering, summarization, and code completion tasks.
For long-context tasks, 8-bit quantization preserves model accuracy while 4-bit quantization causes substantial model accuracy losses.
For long-context tasks, model performance degradation due to quantization is more severe for non-English inputs.
For long-context tasks, the effect of quantization on model performance depends strongly on the quantization method, model, and task.
reWordBench systematically transforms reward model inputs in meaning- or ranking-preserving ways.
State-of-the-art reward models suffer from substantial performance degradation with minor input transformations, sometimes dropping to significantly below-random accuracy.
Explicitly training reward models to assign similar scores to paraphrases improves their robustness to input transformations.
Robust reward models lead to outputs that are preferred over those from standardly trained reward models in alignment tasks.
TACT is a dataset for transition-aware dialogue modeling that incorporates structurally diverse and integrated mode flows.
TACT supports both user- and agent-driven mode switches.
Switch and Recovery are metrics for evaluating an agent's ability to initiate and recover from mode transitions.
Models trained on TACT outperform baselines in intent detection and mode transition handling.
Applying Direct Preference Optimization to TACT-trained models improves performance in joint mode-intent accuracy and human evaluation win rate against GPT-4o.
SALF implements an adversarial training paradigm using agent symbolic learning optimization instead of numerical updates.

SALF uses a generation agent that crafts deceptive narratives and a detection agent that uses structured debates to identify logical and factual flaws, with iterative refinement through adversarial interactions.
Agent symbolic learning defines learnable weights by agent prompts and simulates back-propagation and gradient descent using natural language representations of weights, loss, and gradients.
SALF generates sophisticated fake news that degrades state-of-the-art detection performance in multilingual settings.
SALF refines detectors to improve detection of refined fake news.
A 'channel sentence' is formulated based on channel correlation with the channel represented as a 'word'.
CP-GPT is a generative pre-trained language model for channel prediction.
Two pre-training tasks are designed based on wireless channel characteristics to enhance understanding of communications channels.
A comprehensive benchmark is proposed for evaluating channel prediction models across multiple dimensions.
CP-GPT learns various channel characteristics.
Refining text generation via Direct Preference Optimization produces dialogue summaries and item recommendation information rich in information crucial for effective recommendations.
Refining text generation via Direct Preference Optimization fosters more natural and realistic conversational recommendation processes.
DES-MoE uses an adaptive router that balances pre-trained knowledge retention and task-specific updates via distillation.
DES-MoE uses real-time expert-domain correlation mapping to isolate domain-specific gradients.
DES-MoE employs a three-phase adaptive fine-tuning schedule that progressively freezes non-specialized parameters.
DES-MoE achieves single-domain expert specialization fine-tuning performance with a single unified model.
DES-MoE reduces catastrophic forgetting when scaling to multiple domains compared to full fine-tuning.
RAG+ explicitly incorporates application-aware reasoning into the Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline.
RAG+ constructs a dual corpus of knowledge and aligned application examples, with the application examples created manually or automatically, and jointly retrieves both during inference.
RAG+ enables large language models to apply retrieved knowledge within structured, goal-oriented reasoning processes.
RAG+ consistently outperforms standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation variants across mathematical, law, and medical domains on multiple large language models with greater gains in complex scenarios.

SciEvent is a multi-domain benchmark of scientific abstracts annotated with a unified event extraction schema, providing manual annotations of event segments, triggers, and fine-grained arguments for structured and context-aware understanding of scientific content.

A multi-stage event extraction pipeline for scientific information extraction segments scientific abstracts into the core scientific activities of Background, Method, Result, and Conclusion and extracts event triggers and arguments.
Event extraction models and large language models struggle in scientific event extraction compared to human annotators, particularly in sociology and humanities domains.
LinguaLens analyzes linguistic mechanisms of large language models using Sparse Auto-Encoders.
LinguaLens provides a large-scale counterfactual dataset of linguistic features for mechanism analysis.
Large language models possess intrinsic representations of linguistic knowledge.
Linguistic knowledge in large language models follows distribution patterns across layers and languages.
Linguistic representations enable control of model outputs.
SGToxicGuard is a dataset and evaluation framework for benchmarking LLM safety in Singapore's low-resource languages, including Singlish, Chinese, Malay, and Tamil.
SGToxicGuard uses a red-teaming approach to probe LLM vulnerabilities in conversation, question-answering, and content composition scenarios.
Safety guardrails of state-of-the-art multilingual LLMs exhibit critical gaps for Singapore's low-resource languages.
MobiZO is a resource-efficient fine-tuning framework for large language models designed for edge devices.
MobiZO uses a parallelized randomized gradient estimator that employs both outer-loop and inner-loop parallelism to eliminate sequential forward passes.
MobiZO uses a specialized Multi-Perturbed LoRA module that enables efficient realization of both inner and outer loop parallelism.
MobiZO integrates seamlessly with ExecuTorch for on-device training without requiring modifications to the runtime.
MobiZO achieves runtime speedups and memory savings while improving fine-tuning accuracy.

OHoRA decomposes pre-trained weight matrices of large language models into orthogonal basis vectors via QR decomposition and splits them into two low-redundancy high-rank components to suppress task-irrelevant information.

OHoRA performs dynamic rank-elevated recomposition through Kronecker product to generate expansive task-tailored representation spaces for precise large language model adaptation and enhanced generalization.
OHoRA outperforms low-rank adaptation methods such as LoRA and its variants while achieving performance comparable to full fine-tuning with a minimal number of trainable parameters.
Disentangled Information Bottleneck separates robust features from non-robust features using a disentangled two-line framework.
Disentangled Information Bottleneck uses a discriminator network to approximate the minimum mutual information of two feature lines for disentangling robust and non-robust features.
Disentangled Information Bottleneck achieves higher accuracy than six baseline methods in adversarial text defense.
SAND enables LLM agents to explicitly deliberate over candidate actions before committing to one.
SAND uses self-consistency action sampling with the base model to synthesize step-wise action deliberation thoughts.
SAND uses execution-guided action critique with the base model to synthesize step-wise action deliberation thoughts.
SAND uses an iterative training procedure that finetunes the LLM agent on deliberation trajectories.
SAND outperforms state-of-the-art agent tuning approaches on interactive agent tasks.
Optimized prompts primarily consist of punctuation and noun tokens that are more rare in the training data.
Optimized prompts are clearly distinguishable from natural language counterparts based on sparse subsets of the model's activations.
Optimized prompts follow a similar path in how their representations form through the network across various families of instruction-tuned models.
KLAAD implicitly aligns attention distributions between stereotypical and anti-stereotypical sentence pairs without directly modifying model weights.

KLAAD uses a composite training objective combining Cross-Entropy, KL divergence, and Triplet losses to guide consistent attention across biased and unbiased contexts while preserving fluency and coherence.
KLAAD improves bias mitigation on BBQ and BOLD benchmarks with minimal impact on language modeling quality.
Attention-level alignment offers a principled solution for mitigating bias in generative language models.
Selecting a question subset based on pre-tested results reduces costs in large language model evaluation.
Question subset selection for large language model evaluation is formulated as an optimization task solved by optimal random sampling and simulated annealing algorithms.
Optimization-based question subset selection is more reliable than clustering-based methods for large language model evaluation.
Syntactic features extracted via probing fail to predict outcomes of targeted syntax evaluations across English linguistic phenomena.
TreeRare uses syntax trees to guide information retrieval and reasoning for knowledge-intensive question answering.
TreeRare traverses the syntax tree in a bottom-up fashion, generating subcomponent-based queries and retrieving relevant passages at each node to resolve localized uncertainty.
TreeRare synthesizes retrieved passages into concise, context-aware evidence for each subcomponent of the question.
TreeRare aggregates evidence across the syntax tree to form the final answer.
TreeRare improves question answering performance on ambiguous or multi-hop reasoning tasks.

Task-aware Contrastive Mixture of Experts minimizes the distance between representations of the same expert and maximizes the distance between representations of different experts to efficiently learn representations of different task samples.
Graph-Centric Dialogue Structuring represents dialogue reply structure.
Non-opinion utterances detection is applied for quadruple extraction in multi-turn conversations.

Task-aware Contrastive Mixture of Experts achieves higher accuracy and computational efficiency than existing parameter-efficient fine-tuning techniques for quadruple extraction in multi-turn conversations.
Cacheback is training-free.
Cacheback is model-agnostic.
Cacheback generates draft sequences using only Least Recently Used cache tables of token n-grams.
Cacheback achieves state-of-the-art performance in speculative decoding among comparable methods.
Cacheback shows potential for fast adaptation to new domains.
TableRAG unifies textual understanding and complex manipulations over tabular data.
TableRAG iteratively performs context-sensitive query decomposition, text retrieval, SQL programming and execution, and compositional intermediate answer generation.
HeteQA evaluates multi-hop heterogeneous reasoning capabilities.
TableRAG sets a new state-of-the-art for heterogeneous document question answering on public datasets and HeteQA.
LiteASR compresses automatic speech recognition encoders with low-rank approximation to reduce inference costs while maintaining transcription accuracy.
LiteASR applies principal component analysis to a small calibration dataset to approximate linear transformations with low-rank matrix multiplications.
LiteASR optimizes self-attention to operate in reduced dimensionality.
LiteASR establishes a new Pareto frontier of accuracy and efficiency for automatic speech recognition.
Features beyond model size and training token count improve the prediction of downstream performance in language models.
Language model training exhibits a trade-off between language and code tasks at a specific proportion of code data.
Web data has a negative impact on the truthfulness of language models.
Personality embeddings can be learned from single sentences of text.
A triplet personality trend comparison dataset enables learning personality embeddings from single sentences.
Single-sentence personality embeddings can be transferred to various downstream tasks.
Personality representation learning at the single-sentence level removes constraints on data collection.
Single-sentence personality embeddings improve performance in personality detection, personality retrieval, and emotion translation prediction.
WildScore evaluates MLLMs' ability to interpret real-world music scores and answer complex musicological queries.
A systematic taxonomy of high-level and fine-grained musicological ontologies enables comprehensive evaluation of symbolic music reasoning.
Multiple-choice question answering enables controlled and scalable assessment of MLLMs' symbolic music understanding.
State-of-the-art MLLMs exhibit both persistent challenges and promising directions in symbolic music reasoning.
Pre-existing stance detection datasets are automatically annotated with dialect or vernacular attributes and text complexity attributes.
Large Language Models incorrectly associate pro-marijuana views with low text complexity in zero-shot stance detection tasks.
Large Language Models incorrectly associate African American dialect with opposition to Donald Trump in zero-shot stance detection tasks.
JailExpert achieves a formal representation of the structure of past jailbreak attack experiences.
JailExpert groups past jailbreak attack experiences based on semantic drift.
JailExpert supports dynamic updating of the jailbreak attack experience pool.
JailExpert improves attack success rate for jailbreak attacks.
JailExpert improves attack efficiency for jailbreak attacks.
The output preference of Multimodal Large Language Models can be arbitrarily manipulated by carefully optimized images.
Preference Hijacking (Phi) manipulates Multimodal Large Language Model response preferences using a preference hijacked image.
Preference Hijacking works at inference time without requiring model modifications.
A universal hijacking perturbation can be embedded into different images to hijack Multimodal Large Language Model responses toward any attacker-specified preferences.
Preference hijacking attacks often generate contextually relevant yet biased responses that are neither overtly harmful nor unethical, making them difficult to detect.

RAGvis constructs a knowledge graph of Exploratory Data Analysis notebooks enriched with structured operation semantics extracted by a Large Language Model guided by an empirically-developed Exploratory Data Analysis operations taxonomy.

RAGvis generates executable Python code for new datasets by retrieving and aligning relevant operations from the knowledge graph, refining them with Large Language Model reasoning, and employing a self-correcting agent.
RAGvis improves code executability, semantic accuracy, and visual quality of generated Exploratory Data Analysis operations.
RAGvis achieves lower token usage compared to Large Language Model-only baselines.
RAGvis enables smaller, open-source Large Language Models to match the performance of proprietary models in automated Exploratory Data Analysis.
VERITAS combines structured vision priors with images, questions, and answers.
VERITAS statistically fuses critique rationales and scores from multiple large multimodal models into a consensus score that serves as ground truth.
VERITAS refines answers by generating candidate answers from multiple large multimodal models based on critiques and selecting the highest-scoring candidate.
Models fine-tuned with data processed by VERITAS outperform models fine-tuned with raw data on multimodal benchmarks for text-rich and fine-grained reasoning tasks.
A lightweight critic model matches state-of-the-art large multimodal models in capability while being more efficient.
Mirror is a synthetic dataset that pairs client statements with corresponding facial images for rolling with resistance.
Vision language models trained on Mirror can analyze facial cues, infer emotions, and generate empathetic responses to manage client resistance.
Mirror enhances the AI therapist's ability to handle client resistance compared to text-based cognitive behavioral therapy approaches.
Mirror fosters therapeutic alliance in the presence of client resistance.
Direct Judgement Preference Optimization trains foundational judge models at large data scales.
Direct Judgement Preference Optimization uses preference pairs for generating meaningful critiques, making accurate judgements, and understanding what comprises good and bad responses.
Direct Judgement Preference Optimization produces 8B-parameter judge models that outperform GPT-4o in pairwise benchmarks.
Direct Judgement Preference Optimization produces judge models that generate factual and actionable critiques.
Direct Judgement Preference Optimization produces judge models that serve as strong foundational judges for continued finetuning.
Large Reasoning Models generate a significant portion of output tokens as useless self-repetitions (word salad) that exhaust the decoding budget without adding value.
The hidden states of tokens trailing each reasoning chunk in Large Reasoning Models exhibit patterns that enable on-the-fly detection of word salad behavior using a single linear classifier.
WordSaladChopper (WSC) is a lightweight, turnkey component for Large Reasoning Models that is minimally invasive to the reasoning trajectory.
WordSaladChopper (WSC) yields length savings with minimal quality loss.
A corpus contains spontaneous conversations between pairs of human discourse participants engaged in a collaborative object-matching task.

Current Large Vision Language Models do not show consistent performance improvement when overhearing more conversations from the same discourse participants repeating the same collaborative object-matching task for multiple rounds.
WebAgent-R1 is an end-to-end multi-turn reinforcement learning framework for training web agents.
WebAgent-R1 learns from online interactions with web environments by asynchronously generating diverse trajectories guided by binary rewards for task success.
WebAgent-R1 outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods and strong proprietary models on the WebArena-Lite benchmark.
Behavior cloning as a warm-up training stage is important for training web agents.
A semi-supervised segmentation model segments Spanish into morphological units.
Gold-standard datasets guide and evaluate Spanish morphological segmentation.
Morphology-aware tokenization improves language modeling performance for Spanish.
AutoRegEmbed encodes text into embedding vectors that capture global semantics through information compression.

AutoRegEmbed aligns text embeddings with positive sample embeddings by leveraging the conditional distribution of embeddings and reducing the likelihood of generating negative samples from text embeddings.
AutoRegEmbed outperforms traditional contrastive learning approaches in dense text encoding.
AutoRegEmbed achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art models in dense text encoding with the same amount of training data.
COPE provides fine-grained panic emotion annotations through human-AI collaboration for accurate labeling and mitigation of linguistic variation biases.
PsychoAgent models risk perception and cognitive differences in panic emotion generation using cross-domain heterogeneous data grounded in psychological mechanisms.
PsychoAgent uses an LLM-based role-playing agent with dedicated prompts to simulate individual psychological chains for panic prediction interpretability.
LLMs fix typos with local contexts when typo neurons are activated in only early layers or only late layers.
Typo neurons in middle layers are central to typo-fixing with global contexts.
Typo heads fix typos by considering the entire context without focusing on specific tokens.
Typo neurons and typo heads function for both typo-fixing and understanding general contexts.
RLAE reformulates LLM ensemble as a Markov Decision Process.
RLAE dynamically adjusts ensemble weights by considering input context and intermediate generation states.
RLAE trains the reinforcement learning agent using rewards that directly correspond to the quality of final outputs.
RLAE outperforms existing ensemble methods on a diverse set of tasks.
RLAE generalizes across different tasks without requiring retraining.
RLAE achieves lower time latency than existing ensemble methods.

An optimal transport formulation calibrates pseudo-labeling to constrain majority classes by transporting the unlabeled sample distribution to the class distribution in semi-supervised text classification.
A memory bank collects high-confidence pseudo-labeled data and true labeled data for class distribution estimation in semi-supervised text classification.
Question-Focus discourse structuring framework models argumentative flow by anchoring each argumentative unit to a guiding question and a set of attentional foci.
An argument reconstruction task uses discourse structure to guide evidence retrieval and argument generation.
A dataset of authoritative Chinese think-tank articles is constructed for argument reconstruction.
Claim Coverage measures the proportion of original claims preserved or similarly expressed in argument reconstructions.
Evidence Coverage assesses the completeness of retrieved supporting evidences in argument reconstructions.
Question-Focus discourse structuring framework achieves higher claim coverage than strong baselines.
Question-Focus discourse structuring framework achieves higher evidence coverage than strong baselines.
AutoCT combines the reasoning capabilities of large language models with the explainability of classical machine learning.
AutoCT autonomously generates, evaluates, and refines tabular features based on public information without human input.
AutoCT uses Monte Carlo Tree Search to iteratively optimize predictive performance.
AutoCT achieves state-of-the-art performance on clinical trial prediction tasks within a limited number of self-refinement iterations.
Dialect-SQL uses Object Relational Mapping (ORM) code as an intermediate language to bridge the dialect gap in Text-to-SQL.
A bootstrapping approach synthesizes ORM code by iteratively integrating verified ORM code into a demonstration pool to provide in-context examples for ORM code generation.
Dialect-SQL outperforms direct SQL generation methods in dialect adaptability for Text-to-SQL.
GPT2-style language models require training data that grows exponentially with the number of hops to learn implicit multi-hop reasoning.
GPT2-style language models require a number of transformer layers that grows linearly with the number of hops to learn implicit multi-hop reasoning.
Curriculum learning mitigates the exponential training data requirement for implicit multi-hop reasoning in GPT2-style language models without eliminating it.
Persona-Environment Behavioral Alignment (PEBA) formulates behavioral alignment as a distribution matching problem grounded in Lewin's behavior equation.

PersonaEvolve (PEvo) is an LLM-based optimization algorithm that iteratively refines agent personas to implicitly align collective behaviors with realistic expert benchmarks in a specified environmental context.
PersonaEvolve reduces distributional divergence in behavioral alignment compared to no steering and explicit instruction baselines.
PersonaEvolve-refined personas generalize to novel, related simulation scenarios.
ATTUN enhances resilience to noise by refining attention mechanisms.
ATTUN identifies the most relevant sections of input data by modifying attention weights.
ATTUN improves the detection of relevant context in fact-checking.
ATTUN improves fact-checking accuracy.
Misleading ChartQA evaluates multimodal large language models on misleading chart reasoning.

Misleading ChartQA contains curated examples of multiple misleader types and chart types with standardized chart code, CSV data, multiple-choice questions, and labeled explanations, validated by expert human review and multimodal large language model checks.
A region-aware reasoning pipeline enhances multimodal large language model accuracy on misleading chart reasoning.
MuCAL is a multilingual KG/Text alignment model that achieves robust cross-modal retrieval across multiple languages and difficulty levels.
MuCAL creates preference data for KG-to-Text generation by ranking candidate texts from multiple large language models.
Direct Preference Optimization aligns KG-to-Text generation outputs with graph semantics without reward modelling.
MuCAL provides a better signal for Direct Preference Optimization than existing metrics in KG-to-English Text generation.
Preference-driven KG-to-English Text generation using MuCAL shows better generalisation on out-of-domain datasets than standard instruction tuning.
Large language models are sensitive to the same contextual effects in the application of feminist and trans-inclusive language reforms for English as humans.
AccessEval evaluates language models for disability bias across six real-world domains and nine disability types using paired neutral and disability-aware queries.
Responses to disability-aware queries have higher factual error than responses to neutral queries.
Responses to disability-aware queries have more negative sentiment than responses to neutral queries.
Responses to disability-aware queries show increased stereotyping compared to neutral queries.
Language model response disparities for disability-aware queries disproportionately affect disabilities affecting hearing, speech, and mobility.
HealthCards is a diverse, high-quality healthcare knowledge flashcard dataset sourced from credible medical resources.
Text-to-image models fine-tuned with the HealthCards dataset generate specialized health flashcards.
HICode inductively generates labels from analysis data and hierarchically clusters the labels in two stages to surface emergent themes.
HICode aligns with human-constructed themes.
HICode shows robust performance in automated and human evaluations.
U.S. opioid crisis litigation documents reveal aggressive marketing strategies by pharmaceutical companies.

TactfulToM evaluates large language models' ability to understand white lies in real-life conversations and reason about prosocial motivations for sparing others' feelings and maintaining social harmony.

TactfulToM is generated through a multi-stage human-in-the-loop pipeline that uses large language models to expand manually designed seed stories into conversations to maintain information asymmetry necessary for authentic white lies.
State-of-the-art large language models perform below humans on TactfulToM, revealing shortcomings in their ability to fully comprehend Theory of Mind reasoning for understanding white lies.
Automatic Dictionary Selection (ADS) automatically selects dictionaries to enhance translation.
SLoW does not require access to training data for frequency estimation.
SLoW does not require additional tuning on Large Language Models.
SLoW saves token usage in dictionary-based translation.
SLoW achieves better translation performance than the full dictionary baseline for many languages.
Two conversation agents generate discourse with assigned OCEAN personality traits (each trait set to High or Low).
Multiple judge agents infer the originally assigned personality traits to explore prediction consistency, inter-model agreement, and alignment with the assigned personality.
LLMs can be guided toward personality-driven dialogue, but their ability to maintain personality traits varies significantly depending on the combination of models and discourse settings.
MultiVox evaluates voice assistants' integration of spoken and visual cues including paralinguistic speech features.
MultiVox includes human-annotated and recorded speech dialogues with diverse paralinguistic features and visual cues including images and videos.
Current open-source voice assistant models consistently struggle to produce contextually grounded responses when integrating spoken and visual cues including paralinguistic speech features.
TTT-Bench evaluates basic strategic, spatial, and logical reasoning abilities in large reasoning models through a suite of four two-player Tic-Tac-Toe-style games.
A programmatic approach generates verifiable two-player game problems for TTT-Bench.
Large reasoning models that excel at hard math problems frequently fail at the Tic-Tac-Toe-style reasoning games in TTT-Bench.
Large reasoning models perform worse on TTT-Bench than on MATH 500 and AIME 2024.
Larger large reasoning models achieve higher performance with shorter reasoning traces on TTT-Bench.
Large reasoning models struggle on long-term strategic reasoning situations in simple and new TTT-Bench tasks.
MolBridge augments molecule-description pairs with alignment signals derived from molecular substructures and chemical phrases.
MolBridge employs substructure-aware contrastive learning.
MolBridge employs a self-refinement mechanism that filters out noisy alignment signals.
MolBridge captures fine-grained correspondences between molecules and text.
MolBridge outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on molecular benchmarks.
The table-to-report task transforms table information into reports for industrial applications.
T2R-bench is a bilingual benchmark for the table-to-report task comprising real-world industrial tables from multiple industry domains and table types.
An evaluation criteria fairly measures the quality of report generation for the table-to-report task.
Large language models have room for improvement on the T2R-bench benchmark.
Large language model response lengths follow Weibull-type generalized extreme value distributions with heavier tails under stochastic generation.
A GEV-generalized Pareto hybrid model improves tail fit for large language model response length distributions while maintaining architectural generalizability.
Prompt anchoring effects exhibit reduced dispersion but increased outliers under randomization in large language model responses.
Higher temperatures amplify large language model response length variability while preserving extreme-value mechanisms across model architectures.
The GEV-generalized Pareto hybrid model's threshold selection method enables precise verbosity control in production systems regardless of large language model choice.
DiplomacyAgent systematically assesses how large language models balance interests against ethical principles in international events.
Large language models exhibit a strong bias towards interests when addressing international events, leading to unsafe decisions that violate ethical and moral principles.
Language models use attention to propagate subject information and MLPs to promote answers during knowledge recall for one-to-many factual queries.
Language models use attention to suppress previous answer tokens and MLPs to amplify the suppression signal during the suppression phase for one-to-many factual queries.
Token Lens decodes aggregated attention updates from specified tokens to analyze how language model components use different tokens.
A knockout method analyzes changes in MLP outputs after removing attention to specified tokens to analyze how language model components use different tokens.
Outcome Reward Models (ORMs) assess the correctness of deductive reasoning outcomes.
An echo generation technique extracts training data for outcome reward models by leveraging large language models' reflection of incorrect assumptions in prompts.
Outcome Reward Models trained on Chain-of-Thought and echo-augmented data improve logical reasoning performance on multiple benchmarks.
PSET uses homophones and synonyms to evaluate phonetic embeddings.
Phoneme2Vec outperforms articulatory embeddings and XPhoneBERT in phonetic embedding evaluation.
Gemini 2.5 Flash outperforms GPT-4o, Llama 3.1-8B, OLMo-7B, and OLMo 2-7B in phonetic embedding evaluation.
HELENE integrates annealed A-GNB gradients with diagonal Hessian estimation and layer-wise clipping as a second-order pre-conditioner.
HELENE provably accelerates and stabilizes convergence by reducing dependence on total parameter space and scaling with the largest layer dimension.
HELENE converges faster than MeZO while improving accuracy.
HELENE supports both full and parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
HELENE outperforms state-of-the-art optimizers in fine-tuning large language models.
In standard counterfactual generation tasks, large language models produce self-generated counterfactual explanations that are valid but not minimal.
In minimal counterfactual generation tasks, large language models produce edits that are too small to change the prediction.
The trade-off between validity and minimality in self-generated counterfactual explanations is consistent across multiple large language models, datasets, and evaluation settings.
EnAnchored-X2X is a synthetic data generation framework that leverages English-to-x capabilities for non-English translation.
Omnidirectional datasets for many-to-many translation are generated by extending English parallel corpora.
An English-referenced quality evaluation proxy assesses non-English translation data quality.
EnAnchored-X2X improves non-English translation across multiple language directions and also enhances English-to-x translation.
Strategic exploitation of English-centric strengths bootstraps comprehensive multilingual translation capabilities in large language models.
FlightGPT uses Supervised Fine-Tuning with high-quality demonstrations to improve initialization and structured reasoning in UAV Vision-and-Language Navigation.

FlightGPT uses Group Relative Policy Optimization with a composite reward (goal accuracy, reasoning quality, format compliance) to enhance generalization and adaptability in UAV Vision-and-Language Navigation.
FlightGPT uses a Chain-of-Thought-based reasoning mechanism to improve decision interpretability in UAV Vision-and-Language Navigation.
FlightGPT demonstrates strong generalization in unseen environments for UAV Vision-and-Language Navigation on the CityNav dataset.
MicroEdit disentangles knowledge representations in large language models by leveraging a sparse autoencoder.
MicroEdit activates only a minimal set of necessary neurons for precise parameter updates.
MicroEdit mitigates interference and preserves unrelated knowledge during lifelong model editing.
MicroEdit robustly handles lifelong knowledge editing in question answering and hallucination settings.
PriME uses evolutionary algorithms to directly optimize task-specific utility for personalized language models.
PriME integrates privacy preservation into the optimization objective to minimize privacy risks for data-sharing users.
PriME consistently outperforms existing baselines in task performance on the LaMP benchmark.
PriME achieves a superior privacy-utility trade-off compared to prior state-of-the-art methods.
PriME has enhanced robustness to membership inference attacks.
Aspect-Oriented Summarization generates LLM summaries of long documents with different aspect-oriented prompts that contain different information signals.
Aspect-Oriented Summarization measures differences in information signals between LLM summaries generated with different aspect-oriented prompts.
Aspect-Oriented Summarization integrates signals from multiple LLM summaries generated with different aspect-oriented prompts for supervised training of transformer models.
CoCoA resolves knowledge conflicts at the token level in large language models using entropy gap, contextual peakedness, and generalized divergence between parametric and contextual distributions.
CoCoA maintains strong performance in low conflict settings.
CoCoA achieves state-of-the-art performance on question answering, summarization, and long-form question answering benchmarks.
CoCoA demonstrates superior sensitivity to conflict variations.
Plutus-ben evaluates language models on Greek financial numeric/textual named entity recognition, question answering, extractive summarization, abstractive summarization, and topic classification.
Plutus-8B is a financial language model fine-tuned on Greek-specific financial data.
Four new expert-annotated Greek financial datasets enable Greek financial language model evaluation.
Greek financial natural language processing exhibits persistent challenges due to linguistic complexity, domain terminology, and financial reasoning gaps.
Cross-lingual transfer is limited for Greek financial natural language processing tasks.
The PersonaConflicts Corpus is a dataset of naturalistic simulated dialogues for conflict scenarios between friends, family members, and romantic partners.
The polarity of relationship backstories shifts human perception of communication breakdowns and impressions of social partners.
Large language models struggle to meaningfully leverage relationship backstories in the detection of communication breakdowns.
Large language models consistently overestimate how positively messages make listeners feel.

WangchanThaiInstruct is a human-authored Thai dataset for evaluation and instruction tuning, covering four professional domains and seven task types, created through a multi-stage quality control process with annotators, domain experts, and AI researchers.
A zero-shot evaluation using WangchanThaiInstruct reveals performance gaps on culturally and professionally specific tasks.
An instruction tuning study using WangchanThaiInstruct isolates the effect of native supervision on model performance.
Models fine-tuned on WangchanThaiInstruct outperform models fine-tuned on translated data in both in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks.
LGEA is an LLM-guided multi-modal entity alignment framework that prioritizes noise reduction before fusion.
LGEA uses large language models for fine-grained visual filtering to remove irrelevant images at the semantic level.
LGEA uses large language models for contextual summarization of attribute information to enhance entity semantics.
LGEA achieves state-of-the-art performance in robust multi-modal alignment on multiple benchmarks.
Multimodal LLMs with knowledge augmentation infer implicit acoustic styles from open-domain instructions.
Diffusion-based generators finely adjust speech details using style factors as control conditions.
Feedback from verification of decoding state consistency, mel-spectrogram consistency, and instruction style consistency optimizes the generator.
An autoencoder trained on parallel TED talk scripts constructs a well-aligned cross-lingual latent space.
Debiasing multilingual large language models in a learned cross-lingual latent space improves overall debiasing performance and cross-lingual transferability.
ResFormer captures long-term contextual dependencies in linear time using a reservoir computing network with a nonlinear readout.
ResFormer models short-term dependencies within sentences using a conventional Transformer architecture with fixed-length inputs.
ResFormer outperforms DeepSeek-Qwen and ModernBERT on the EmoryNLP, MultiWOZ, MELD, and IEMOCAP datasets.
ResFormer exhibits reduced memory consumption.
one-shot CFT generates critique data from a single math problem by using diverse small models to produce candidate solutions and frontier models to generate critiques of these solutions.
one-shot CFT achieves higher reasoning accuracy than one-shot RLVR while requiring less computational resources.
one-shot CFT is robust to the choice of the single math problem used for critique generation.
Existing systems struggle to copy and properly cite unstructured evidence, which tends to be lost in the middle.
The Summaries with Unstructured Evidence Text dataset (SUnsET) is a synthetic dataset for unstructured evidence summarization generated with a novel pipeline.
Training on the Summaries with Unstructured Evidence Text dataset produces evidence that is more relevant and factually consistent than baselines with no fine-tuning and fixed granularity evidence.

Training on the Summaries with Unstructured Evidence Text dataset allows evidence extraction from more diverse locations in the context than baselines with no fine-tuning and fixed granularity evidence.
Training on the Summaries with Unstructured Evidence Text dataset produces summaries that are more relevant and consistent than baselines with no fine-tuning and fixed granularity evidence.
Unified graph-based reasoning analysis enables fine-grained modeling and quantitative analysis of LRM reasoning dynamics.
Unified graph-based reasoning analysis clusters verbose Chain-of-Thought outputs into semantically coherent reasoning steps.
Unified graph-based reasoning analysis constructs directed reasoning graphs to capture contextual and logical dependencies among reasoning steps.
Exploration density, branching, and convergence ratios in reasoning graphs strongly correlate with LRM performance.

DroidCollection is the most extensive open data suite for training and evaluating machine-generated code detectors, including fully AI-generated code, human-AI co-authored code, and adversarial examples crafted to evade detection.
DroidDetect is a suite of encoder-only detectors for machine-generated code detection trained with a multi-task objective.
Existing machine-generated code detectors fail to generalise to diverse coding domains and programming languages outside their narrow training data.
Most machine-generated code detectors are easily compromised by humanising the output distributions using superficial prompting and alignment approaches.
Training on a small number of adversarial examples resolves the vulnerability of machine-generated code detectors to humanising output distributions.
Metric learning and uncertainty-based resampling enhance detector training on noisy distributions.
MoCE performs expert group routing based on sequence-level features.
MoCE activates the top-k experts within the group at the token level.
MoCE encourages expert group specialization.
MoCE consistently outperforms strong baselines on multiple benchmarks.
MoCE enhances generalization capabilities.
Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is the critical training stage that leads to language mixing in Chinese-English bilingual reasoning models.
Language mixing enhances reasoning in Chinese-English bilingual large language models.
A lightweight probe predicts whether a language switch would benefit or harm reasoning in Chinese-English bilingual reasoning models.
Guiding decoding with a lightweight probe that predicts the effect of language switches on reasoning enhances reasoning accuracy in Chinese-English bilingual models.
Language mixing in bilingual large language models is a strategic reasoning behavior.
Confidence-guided Refinement Reasoning (C2R) handles question-answering tasks across text, image, and video domains.
Confidence-guided Refinement Reasoning (C2R) curates a subset of sub-questions and their answers to explore diverse reasoning paths.
Confidence-guided Refinement Reasoning (C2R) selects the most reliable final answer by comparing confidence scores of answer candidates.
Confidence-guided Refinement Reasoning (C2R) relies solely on confidence scores derived from the question-answering model.
The quantity and quality of sub-questions and their answers impact the robustness and reliability of reasoning in question-answering.
AmpleHate identifies explicit targets using a pretrained Named Entity Recognition model and implicit targets via [CLS] tokens.
AmpleHate computes attention-based relationships between explicit targets, implicit targets, and sentence context.
AmpleHate injects relational vectors of target-context relationships into the final sentence representation.
AmpleHate outperforms contrastive learning baselines in implicit hate detection.
AmpleHate produces attention patterns that align with human judgement.
A noise-resistant data construction pipeline uses the in-context learning capabilities of MLLMs to automatically filter and caption web-sourced images.
WebPerson is a large-scale dataset of high-quality person-centric image-text pairs.
GA-DMS adaptively masks noisy textual tokens using gradient-attention similarity scores for cross-modal alignment.
GA-DMS uses masked token prediction objectives that compel the model to predict informative text tokens, enhancing fine-grained semantic representation learning.
GA-DMS achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple text-based person retrieval benchmarks.
The perception bottleneck and token overload are key challenges in extending image-based vision-language models to the video domain.
D-CoDe is a training-free framework for extending image-pretrained vision-language models to video.
Dynamic compression alleviates the perception bottleneck by adaptively selecting representative frames and aggregating spatial tokens in a content-aware manner.
Question decomposition mitigates token overload by reformulating queries into sub-questions that guide the model to focus on distinct aspects of the video.
Audio-Reasoner performs deep reasoning on audio.
CoTA is a high-quality, large-scale, multi-task dataset for audio reasoning.
Structured chain-of-thought training is essential for advancing audio reasoning.
Med-PRM verifies each reasoning step against established medical knowledge bases using retrieval-augmented generation.
Med-PRM achieves state-of-the-art performance on medical QA benchmarks and diagnostic tasks.
Med-PRM integrates in a plug-and-play fashion with policy models.
Med-PRM enables small-scale models to achieve state-of-the-art accuracy on MedQA.
MuLA-F uses singular value decomposition to disentangle language-specific knowledge in LoRA blocks.
MuLA-F uses a local orthogonal tuning strategy.
MuLA-F reduces multilingual knowledge conflicts in federated social media content anomaly detection.
MuLA-F reduces the number of communication rounds in federated social media content anomaly detection.
A taxonomy categorizes Machine Translation errors in the healthcare domain for a publicly available MT system.
An evaluation dataset for Machine Translation in healthcare for Amharic and Tigrinya is derived from pre-existing medical datasets.

Machine Translation errors in healthcare most commonly occur when the source sentence includes medical terminology and procedure descriptions, synonyms, figurative language, and word order differences.
Pre-translation interventions are not effective in reducing clinical risk when the base translation model performs poorly.
Glider uses a semantic global router that leverages LLMs' semantic reasoning to generate task-specific instructions from the input query for expert selection across all layers.
Glider uses a learned local router that facilitates token-level routing decisions within each module.
Glider retrieves the correct expert for held-in tasks.
Glider demonstrates compositional capabilities for held-out tasks.
Balcony freezes the pretrained large language model and inserts additional transformer layers at selected exit points, enabling depth-based dynamic inference with minimal performance degradation.
Balcony trains the additional layers using a self-distillation loss that aligns sub-model outputs with the full model outputs.
Balcony requires fewer training tokens and tunable parameters than prior dynamic inference methods.
Balcony outperforms state-of-the-art dynamic inference methods and compression techniques on multiple models and benchmarks.
CogDual is a role-playing language agent that uses a cognize-then-respond reasoning paradigm.
CogDual jointly models external situational awareness and internal self-awareness.
CogDual generates responses with improved character consistency and contextual alignment.
CogDual uses reinforcement learning with two general-purpose reward schemes designed for open-domain text generation.
CogDual outperforms existing baselines on the CoSER, Cross-MR, and LifeChoice benchmarks.
CogDual generalizes across diverse role-playing tasks.
LiTEx is a linguistically-informed taxonomy for categorizing free-text explanations in English.
A subset of the e-SNLI dataset is annotated using LiTEx.
LiTEx is reliable.
LiTEx aligns with Natural Language Inference labels, highlighted spans, and free-text explanations.
Conditioning explanation generation on LiTEx produces explanations that are linguistically closer to human explanations than conditioning on labels or highlighted spans alone.
VELA evaluates long image captions using an LLM-Hybrid-as-a-Judge framework.
LongCap-Arena evaluates metrics for long image captions with human judgments on Descriptiveness, Relevance, and Fluency.
VELA outperforms existing automatic metrics for long image caption evaluation on LongCap-Arena.
VELA achieves superhuman performance on LongCap-Arena.
A multimodal event extraction strategy uses a multi-level redundant feature selection mechanism.
A multimodal event extraction strategy leverages knowledge editing techniques to enhance event understanding in multimodal large language models.
A multimodal event extraction strategy requires no additional parameter optimization work.
A multimodal event extraction strategy that employs multi-level redundant feature selection and knowledge editing outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on the M2E2 benchmark.
MrGuard uses reasoning to classify multilingual prompts for safety.
Synthetic multilingual data generation for safety classification includes culturally and linguistically nuanced variants.
Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is a curriculum-based framework for multilingual safety classification.
MrGuard consistently outperforms recent baselines in multilingual safety classification across in-domain and out-of-domain languages.
MrGuard preserves safety judgments under multilingual variations including code-switching and low-resource language distractors.
MrGuard generates explanations for safety judgments that help understand language-specific risks and ambiguities in multilingual content.
For hierarchical text classification, HYDRA treats each level in the hierarchy as a separate classification task with its own label space.
For hierarchical text classification, HYDRA uses parameter sharing and level-specific parameterization to incorporate hierarchical awareness without architectural complexity.
For hierarchical text classification, HYDRA consistently outperforms flat models.
For hierarchical text classification, HYDRA matches or exceeds the performance of complex state-of-the-art methods.
In hierarchical text classification, complex components such as graph encoders, label semantics, and autoregressive decoders are often unnecessary.
Inverse confidence weighting selects examples across tasks for label-efficient supervised finetuning of LLMs.
Inverse confidence weighting is easier to implement and less computationally intensive than more complex sampling procedures for label-efficient supervised finetuning of LLMs.
Inverse confidence weighting achieves better accuracy than training on the complete dataset in label-efficient supervised finetuning of LLMs.
Inverse confidence weighting reduces annotation costs while maintaining or improving performance compared to the best existing methods in label-efficient supervised finetuning of LLMs.
CoPriva evaluates adherence of large language models to contextual non-disclosure policies in question answering using direct and indirect attacks.
Many large language models violate user-defined policies and leak sensitive information in question answering.
Violations of user-defined policies by large language models are particularly severe against indirect attacks in question answering.
Large language models struggle to incorporate policy constraints during generation in question answering.
Large language models exhibit a partial ability to revise outputs when explicitly prompted in question answering.
Waste-Bench evaluates the robustness and accuracy of VLLMs on waste classification tasks in cluttered real-world environments with complex scenes and deformed objects.
Guard models are vulnerable to jailbreak attacks due to representation shifts in the embedding layer that cascade through the Transformer stack.
Gamma-Guard uses lightweight residual adapters inserted after the embeddings and at sparse intervals in the model.
Gamma-Guard adapters are initialized with zero-scaled gates to retain original behavior and then fine-tuned adversarially to denoise embeddings and refocus attention.
Gamma-Guard achieves high accuracy on jailbreak attack examples with minimal degradation in accuracy on clean examples.
Gamma-Guard's robustness improvements persist across different layer placements and model sizes.

QSPEC decouples efficiency from quality by integrating low-precision joint quantization for fast drafting and high-precision weight-only quantization for accurate verification via speculative decoding.
QSPEC reuses weights and KV cache across stages to enable near-zero-cost switching without retraining or auxiliary models.
QSPEC achieves speedup without quality degradation compared to high-precision baselines.
QSPEC outperforms state-of-the-art speculative decoding methods in batched settings.
QSPEC supports plug-and-play deployment.
QSPEC generalizes across model scales, quantization methods, and workloads.
ArgCMV is a key point extraction dataset for long-context online discussions from actual human debates.
ArgCMV exhibits higher complexity than ArgKP21 for key point extraction.
Existing key point extraction methods do not adapt well to ArgCMV.
ArgCMV is curated using state-of-the-art large language models.
The MUCAR multilingual dataset contains visual contexts that uniquely resolve ambiguous textual expressions.
The MUCAR dual-ambiguity dataset contains pairs of ambiguous images and ambiguous textual contexts that produce a single clear interpretation through mutual disambiguation.
Evaluations of multimodal models on MUCAR reveal substantial performance gaps compared to human-level performance.
Within a hierarchical text classification task, different prompting strategies yield substantially different results from large language models.
Across different hierarchical text classification tasks, the relative effectiveness of a prompting strategy varies significantly.
HiEPS integrates the results of diverse hierarchical text classification prompting strategies for zero-shot hierarchical text classification.
The path-valid voting mechanism selects a valid hierarchical text classification result with the highest path frequency score.
HiEPS outperforms single prompting strategies for zero-shot hierarchical text classification.
Hateful Word in Context Classification (HateWiC) determines whether a word is used hatefully in a specific context.
A dataset of approximately 4000 Word-in-Context instances for classifying hateful word usage in context, each labeled by three annotators.
Hatefulness of a word in context does not always derive from the sense definition alone.
Including dictionary definitions improves prediction of hateful word senses, except when hateful connotations vary.
Including annotator demographics mitigates performance drop in subjective hate prediction.
DICE uses small language models to refine large language model outputs via chain-of-thought correction.
DICE decouples reasoning and output specification adherence by first generating natural language responses with large language models and then refining them with small language models.
DICE constructs structured chain-of-thought adaptation datasets using a two-stage method.
DICE applies a dual-tuning strategy to fine-tune small language models for generating structured outputs in an analyze-then-answer pattern.
DICE achieves state-of-the-art performance in format accuracy and content correctness for structured reasoning tasks.
CSDS provides role-oriented summaries to capture different speakers' viewpoints in Chinese customer service dialogues.
CSDS structures summaries at the topic level to represent the topic flow of Chinese customer service dialogues.
Existing summarization methods generate redundant and incoherent summaries when evaluated on CSDS.
Existing summarization methods have lower performance on role-oriented summaries and topic structures when evaluated on CSDS.
A systematic evaluation method assesses multilingual capabilities of large language models using qualitative and quantitative approaches.
A novel prompt back-translation method evaluates cross-language generalization in large language models.
Large language models effectively transfer learned knowledge across different languages in tasks where the correct output is independent of the input language.
Large language models struggle to provide accurate results in tasks where the correct output depends on the input language.
FFLM enables zero-shot faithfulness evaluation for text summarization using a moderately-sized foundation language model.
FFLM measures faithfulness by combining probability changes that occur when prefixing text consistent with the output.
FFLM achieves competitive or superior performance to ChatGPT on inconsistency detection and faithfulness rating with a substantially smaller model size.
Language-Guided Temporal Token Pruning (LGTTP) leverages temporal cues from queries to adaptively prune video tokens while preserving contextual continuity and reducing computational overhead.
Language-Guided Temporal Token Pruning (LGTTP) retains higher token density in temporally relevant segments compared to uniform pruning and keyframe selection.
Language-Guided Temporal Token Pruning (LGTTP) is model-agnostic and integrates with TimeChat and LLaVA-Video.
Language-Guided Temporal Token Pruning (LGTTP) reduces computation while preserving task performance.
Language-Guided Temporal Token Pruning (LGTTP) excels on queries with explicit temporal markers.
Language-Guided Temporal Token Pruning (LGTTP) remains effective across general video understanding tasks.
Database-Augmented Query representation (DAQu) augments the original query with query-related metadata across multiple tables.
Database-Augmented Query representation (DAQu) uses a graph-based set-encoding strategy to encode query-related metadata by considering hierarchies of features in the database without order.
Translating images to make them culturally relevant is a task in machine translation.
Three pipelines comprising state-of-the-art generative models perform image translation for cultural relevance.

An evaluation dataset for image translation for cultural relevance has two parts: a concept part with 600 cross-culturally coherent images focusing on a single concept per image and an application part with 100 images curated from real-world applications.
The failure of image-editing models to translate images for cultural relevance is improved by leveraging LLMs and retrievers in the loop.
Current pipelines for image translation for cultural relevance fail for some countries in real-world applications.
Value-aware objective incorporates value vectors in attention approximation.
In language modeling, optimal attention approximation incorporating value vectors outperforms optimal attention approximation ignoring value vectors.
For less skewed kernel functions, value vectors have a greater impact on sparse attention approximation quality.
Logic-Induced-Knowledge-Search (LINK) generates factually-correct long-tail inferential statements.
Logic-Induced-Knowledge-Search (LINK) uses variable-wise prompting grounded on symbolic rules to seek low-confidence statements while ensuring factual correctness.
Logic-Induced-Long-Tail (LINT) is a large-scale long-tail inferential knowledge dataset spanning four domains.
State-of-the-art large language models exhibit a significant performance drop on long-tail inferential knowledge data compared to head distribution data.
Smaller large language models exhibit greater performance degradation on long-tail inferential knowledge data than state-of-the-art models.
DDOT performs flexible-length and flexible-position text infilling without requiring ground-truth positional data.
DDOT jointly denoises token values and token positions using a sample-level Optimal Transport coupling.
DDOT's sample-level Optimal Transport coupling preserves relative token ordering while dynamically adjusting the positions and length of infilled segments.
DDOT achieves performance on par with state-of-the-art non-autoregressive models on text infilling tasks.
DDOT improves training efficiency and flexibility for text infilling.
An automated system for inferring research framings of NLP artefacts extracts key elements (means, ends, stakeholders).
An automated system for inferring research framings of NLP artefacts links extracted elements through interpretable rules and contextual reasoning.
A new dataset for research framing in hate speech detection is annotated.
Automated fact-checking research shows a rise in underspecified research goals.
Automated fact-checking research shows increased emphasis on scientific exploration over application.
Automated fact-checking research shows a shift toward supporting human fact-checkers rather than pursuing full automation.
MMEdit is a benchmark for evaluating the editing of multimodal Large Language Models using a suite of innovative metrics.

Previous model editing baselines can edit multimodal Large Language Models to some extent but with barely satisfactory effect, indicating the potential difficulty of editing multimodal Large Language Models.
WikiSQL-TS and WikiTable-TS evaluate table question answering under topic shift with train-dev-test splits in five distinct topic groups based on WikiSQL and WikiTable-Questions.
Table question answering models exhibit significant performance degradation on unseen topics.
T3QA uses topic-specific vocabulary injection into BERT for topic adaptation in table question answering.
T3QA uses a text-to-text transformer generator based pipeline to generate topic-specific training data for table question answering.
T3QA uses a logical form re-ranker for logical form selection in table question answering under topic shift.
TEMA maps token embeddings from a richly pre-trained language model to a poorly trained language model to create an enriched language model.
TEMA reduces perplexity compared to the original monolingual language model.
TEMA achieves state-of-the-art results on the most semantic downstream tasks.
TEMA is competitive with or outperforms multilingual and extended models for low-resource language problems.
NMP-QEM encodes the answer set of each mini-query as a mixture of Gaussian distributions with multiple means and covariance parameters.
The mixture of Gaussian distributions representation in NMP-QEM approximates any random distribution arbitrarily well in real knowledge graphs.
NMP-QEM defines neural-based logical operators for projection, intersection, and negation that operate on mixture of Gaussian distributions.
NMP-QEM outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets for answering first-order logical queries on knowledge graphs.
CME reduces model confidence for non-inductive feature attributions.
CME improves model calibration in both in-domain and out-of-domain settings.
CME combined with temperature scaling further improves model calibration.
Model explanations can help calibrate posterior probability estimates.
Selective Preference Optimization (SePO) is a selective alignment strategy for efficient key token selection without requiring strong, fine-grained supervision signals.

Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) is feasible as a token-level reward function estimator for any existing alignment dataset, enabling cost-efficient token selection with small-scale models and training data.
Selective Preference Optimization (SePO) outperforms competitive baseline methods by optimizing on a small fraction of key tokens and reducing training costs.
Selective Preference Optimization (SePO) enables weak oracle models to supervise strong policy models for weak-to-strong generalization.
Selective Preference Optimization (SePO) selects useful supervision signals from out-of-distribution data, alleviating the over-optimization problem.

Multi-Turn Text Generation generates rich pseudo-labels through simulated dialogues with multimodal large language models, producing fine-grained and diverse visual descriptions without manual supervision.
Multi-Turn Text Interaction refines user queries at inference time through dynamic, dialogue-based reasoning to interpret and resolve vague, incomplete, or ambiguous descriptions.
CASPER retrieves related exemplars from a retrieval index for an input query, augments them to the query, and applies a generative seq2seq model to produce a parse.
CASPER enables parser behavior to be manipulated by changing the retrieval index or the augmented query construction.
CASPER parses queries in new domains, adapts predictions to specified patterns, and adapts to new semantic schemas without model retraining.
Unsupervised and supervised processing methods for incorporating pre-trained language model embeddings improve knowledge graph completion performance.
Supervised embedding extraction methods extract more informative entity representations for knowledge graph completion.
A knowledge graph completion model adapted from pre-trained language models outperforms recent neural models.
Language models converge on similar abstract analyses for English filler-gap dependency constructions.

Frequency, filler type, and surrounding context are factors in English filler-gap dependency constructions that were previously overlooked in linguistic theory and could motivate changes to standard linguistic theory.
RuCoLA is a Russian corpus of linguistic acceptability built under the binary linguistic acceptability approach.
RuCoLA consists of in-domain sentences from linguistic publications and out-of-domain sentences produced by generative models.
RuCoLA's out-of-domain set facilitates the use of linguistic acceptability for improving language generation.
Widely used language models fall behind humans in linguistic acceptability classification for morphological and semantic errors.
AutoTrial enables controllable generation of clinical eligibility criteria under instructions via a hybrid of discrete and neural prompting.
AutoTrial incorporates knowledge scalably via in-context learning.
AutoTrial uses explicit reasoning chains to provide rationales for the generated clinical eligibility criteria.
AutoTrial generates clinical eligibility criteria texts that are fluent, coherent, and accurately capture relevant clinical concepts.
AutoTrial outperforms GPT-3.5 in human evaluations for clinical eligibility criteria generation despite having a smaller parameter size.
Paired by the Teacher (PbT) synthesizes input-output pairs from unpaired data without human labels or parallel data.
Paired by the Teacher (PbT) compresses unpaired examples into intermediate representations using a teacher LLM.
Paired by the Teacher (PbT) reconstructs inputs from intermediate representations using a student model.
Paired by the Teacher (PbT) achieves performance close to human-annotated pairs.
Paired by the Teacher (PbT) reduces annotation cost compared to direct synthesis.
QAaP reframes question answering as programming for solving time-sensitive questions.
QAaP uses large language models to represent diversely expressed text as well-structured code and select the best matching answer from multiple candidates through programming.
QAaP improves performance on time-sensitive question answering over strong baselines.
RepoCoder performs repository-level code completion using an iterative retrieval-generation pipeline that incorporates a similarity-based retriever and a pre-trained code language model.
RepoCoder generates code at various levels of granularity for repository-level code completion.
RepoBench is a benchmark for repository-level code completion that covers line, API invocation, and function body completion scenarios with real-world repositories.
RepoCoder consistently outperforms the In-File completion baseline and the vanilla retrieval-augmented code completion approach in repository-level code completion.
Balancing the contribution of all parameters improves model generalization performance.
Intra-distillation balances parameter sensitivity by adding a loss term to the training objective.
An adaptive learning method controls the strength of the intra-distillation loss to accelerate convergence.
Training gains decay as synthetic data increases for tool-use data synthesis in large language models.
Training gains decay in tool-use data synthesis for large language models manifests as fragment deficiency (parameter errors) in responses.
iTool uses path exploration of Monte Carlo Tree Search to enhance the diversity of response for synthetic tool-use data.
iTool constructs fine-grained preference pairs to pinpoint model deficiency in tool-use responses.
iTool uses preference optimization algorithms to improve model deficiency in tool-use responses.
Turn-level active learning selects dialogue turns for annotation in dialogue state tracking.
Turn-level active learning achieves comparable dialogue state tracking performance to traditional training with less annotated data.
In-Context Adversarial Game (ICAG) defends against jailbreaks without fine-tuning.
In-Context Adversarial Game (ICAG) conducts an adversarial game using agent learning to dynamically extend knowledge for defending against jailbreaks.
In-Context Adversarial Game (ICAG) strengthens defenses against newly generated jailbreak prompts through continuous improvement.
In-Context Adversarial Game (ICAG) reduces jailbreak success rates across various attack scenarios.
In-Context Adversarial Game (ICAG) transfers to other large language models.
PE-RL improves overall Neutral Point of View quality in answers to sensitive topics relative to strong baselines.
PE-RL increases the presence of supportive details in answers to sensitive topics.
PE-RL reduces oversimplification in answers to sensitive topics.
PE-RL exhibits effective out-of-topic generalization for Neutral Point of View generation in answers to sensitive topics.
SHQ-NPOV is a dataset for evaluating Neutral Point of View in answers to sensitive topics.
A methodology for creating datasets for Neutral Point of View generation in answers to sensitive topics involves iterative human peer-critique and annotator training.
Current state-of-the-art approaches for same side stance classification fail to generalize within and across topics and domains under adversarial sampling.
Current state-of-the-art approaches for same side stance classification require domain knowledge and semantic inference beyond domain-independent linguistic similarity features.

Specialized image search models trained on in-domain datasets and generalized representation models pre-trained on massive image and text data exhibit severe gender bias for gender-neutral natural language queries.
An in-processing fair sampling method addresses gender imbalance in training image search models.
A post-processing feature clipping method based on mutual information debiases multimodal representations of pre-trained image search models.
HyperRank jointly models global and local context information to estimate the importance of candidate keyphrases in hyperbolic space.
HyperRank significantly outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised keyphrase extraction models.

Confidence-Enhanced Adaptation performs frame-level adaptation for ASR in wild acoustic test settings using a confidence-aware weight scheme to avoid filtering out essential information in high-entropy frames.

Confidence-Enhanced Adaptation applies consistency regularization during test-time optimization for ASR in wild acoustic test settings to leverage the inherent short-term consistency of speech signals.
Confidence-Enhanced Adaptation outperforms existing baselines in wild acoustic test settings including Gaussian noise, environmental sounds, accent variations, and sung speech.

In-context learning exhibits a positional bias (DPP bias) where varying the positions of demonstrations, system prompt, and user message in the input causes drastic drifts in predictions and accuracy.
ACCURACY-CHANGE quantifies net gains in accuracy from changes in demonstration positions during in-context learning.
PREDICTION-CHANGE quantifies output volatility from changes in demonstration positions during in-context learning.
Placing demonstrations at the start of the prompt yields the most stable and accurate outputs in in-context learning.
Smaller language models are more affected by the positional bias of in-context learning than larger language models.
The power-law scaling framework applies to Mixture of Experts (MoE) models.
Mixture of Experts (MoE) models achieve lower testing losses than dense models when trained with the same compute budget.
LLM-Adapters integrates various adapters into large language models for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of different tasks.
Adapter types, placement locations, and hyperparameters influence the performance of adapter-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods.

Adapter-based parameter-efficient fine-tuning in smaller-scale large language models with few extra trainable parameters yields comparable or superior performance to larger models in zero-shot inference on simple mathematical reasoning tasks.

Adaptive Proposal Generation Network (APGN) adaptively generates segment proposals for temporal sentence localization in videos by performing foreground-background classification and regression on foreground frames.
Adaptive Proposal Generation Network (APGN) enhances the semantics of generated segment proposals using a proposal consolidation module.
Adaptive Proposal Generation Network (APGN) outperforms state-of-the-art methods for temporal sentence localization in videos.
Extract-Define-Canonicalize (EDC) constructs knowledge graphs through open information extraction, schema definition, and post-hoc canonicalization.
Extract-Define-Canonicalize (EDC) automatically constructs a schema and applies self-canonicalization when no pre-defined schema is available.
Extract-Define-Canonicalize (EDC) includes a trained component that retrieves schema elements relevant to the input text in a retrieval-augmented generation-like manner.
Extract-Define-Canonicalize (EDC) extracts high-quality triplets without parameter tuning.
Extract-Define-Canonicalize (EDC) handles larger schemas than prior methods for knowledge graph construction.
Adversarial Masking and Styled Filling (AMSF) trains a mask predictor by adversarial training without manual configuration.
Adversarial Masking and Styled Filling (AMSF) uses an entropy maximization loss in the word filling module to guarantee diversity of transferred texts.
Adversarial Masking and Styled Filling (AMSF) uses a consistency regularization loss in the word filling module to guarantee semantic consistency of transferred texts.
FOCUS initializes the embedding matrix for a new tokenizer by representing newly added tokens as combinations of tokens in the overlap of the source and target vocabularies.
FOCUS selects overlapping tokens based on semantic similarity in an auxiliary static token embedding space.

FOCUS outperforms random initialization and prior methods on language modeling and downstream tasks including natural language inference, question answering, and named entity recognition when XLM-R is used as the source model.
DEER represents entity relationships as free-text relation descriptions.
DEER extracts relation descriptions using a self-supervised learning method that analyzes dependency patterns.
DEER generates relation descriptions using a transformer-based model.
DEER is an open and informative knowledge graph built without human annotation.

CHENGYU-BENCH benchmarks Chinese idiom understanding and use with human-verified examples through three tasks: classifying the sentiment of Chinese idioms, detecting incorrect usage of Chinese idioms in context, and filling blanks in passages with Chinese idioms without options.

Large language models achieve higher accuracy on classifying the sentiment of Chinese idioms than on detecting incorrect usage of Chinese idioms in context and filling blanks in passages with Chinese idioms without options.
Large language models' errors in Chinese idiom understanding tasks often stem from fundamental misunderstandings of Chinese idiom meanings.
Rainier generates contextually relevant knowledge for commonsense questions.
Rainier imitates knowledge generated by GPT-3 for commonsense question answering.
Rainier uses reinforcement learning with rewards based on increased question answering performance to generate knowledge.
Rainier demonstrates performance gains on commonsense benchmarks including those unseen during training.
Models orders of magnitude smaller than GPT-3 without direct supervision on knowledge generation can produce commonsense knowledge of higher quality than GPT-3.
The graph extension formulation improves performance on scene graph modification tasks over the graph generation approach.
An autoregressive sequence labelling model is the first solution for the graph extension problem in conditional graph modification.
A novel biomedical dataset for conditional graph modification has larger linguistic variability and more complex graphs than existing scene graph modification datasets.

The state-of-the-art graph generation approach fails to generalize on biomedical graph modification tasks with high linguistic variability and complex graphs, while an autoregressive sequence labelling model produces meaningful predictions.
Sequence Span Rewriting is a self-supervised task for sequence-to-sequence pre-training.
Sequence Span Rewriting refines machine-generated imperfect text spans into ground truth text.
Sequence Span Rewriting provides fine-grained and informative supervision for sequence-to-sequence pre-training.
Sequence Span Rewriting is consistent with downstream sentence rewriting tasks including text summarization, question generation, grammatical error correction, and paraphrase generation.
Mixture-of-Partitions partitions a large knowledge graph into smaller sub-graphs.
Mixture-of-Partitions infuses knowledge from sub-graphs into BERT models using lightweight adapters.
Mixture-of-Partitions fine-tunes sub-graph adapters and the underlying BERT through a mixture layer to leverage overall factual knowledge for a target task.
Mixture-of-Partitions achieves state-of-the-art performance on five biomedical datasets.
The interpretation test set evaluates simultaneous machine translation on interpretation data.
Simultaneous machine translation models exhibit a performance difference between evaluation on translation data and interpretation data.
The translation-to-interpretation style transfer method converts offline translations into interpretation-style data.
