The translation-to-interpretation style transfer method improves simultaneous machine translation performance on interpretation data.
The performance gap between translation data and interpretation data evaluation persists after applying the translation-to-interpretation style transfer method.
Simple surface heuristics solve the verb-subject agreement task, indicating a possible flaw in using it to assess neural networks' syntactic ability.
Transformers capture a non-trivial amount of grammatical structure in long-range French object-verb agreement, unlike LSTMs.
Five simple pretraining objectives based on token-level classification tasks serve as replacements for masked language modeling.
Simple pretraining objectives based on token-level classification tasks achieve comparable or better performance than masked language modeling on GLUE and SQUAD when using a BERT-BASE architecture.
A smaller model pretrained with one of the simple pretraining objectives achieves minimal performance drop on GLUE.
Deep co-training performs unsupervised conversation disentanglement.
Deep co-training uses a message-pair classifier to retrieve local relations between messages.
Deep co-training learns session assignments via reinforcement learning by maximizing rewards from message-pair classification.
Deep co-training achieves competitive performance in conversation disentanglement compared to supervised methods.
Disentangled conversations improve performance on multi-party response selection.
Masked hierarchical attention enhances slot interrelation in dialogue state tracking through hierarchical ontology integration.
Combining generation and extraction methods solves out-of-vocabulary problems in state value decoding for dialogue state tracking.
Combining generation and extraction methods for state value decoding in dialogue state tracking improves robustness to out-of-vocabulary problems compared to other methods.

A single multi-task transformer model answers open-domain questions from any text collection by iteratively retrieving supporting facts, reranking them, and predicting answers for varying numbers of retrieval steps.
BeerQA evaluates open-domain questions requiring one, two, or three retrieval steps with a unified Wikipedia corpus.
A BERT-based model identifies health advice in research publications by classifying sentences as strong advice, weak advice, or no advice.
A corpus of sentences from PubMed structured abstracts is annotated for health advice with labels strong advice, weak advice, and no advice.
A model trained on structured abstracts identifies health advice in unstructured abstracts and discussion sections.
Stance detection from social media data achieves high recall for both Pro and Anti classifications but variable precision relative to self-reported stance in public opinion surveys.

Temporal inconsistencies, differences in constructs, and measurement errors from survey respondents and annotators lead to the disconnect between stance expressed in social media data and self-reported stance in public opinion surveys.
A framework for assessing the limitations of stance detection models.
Contrastive Active Learning selects contrastive examples, which are data points that are similar in the model feature space and yet the model outputs maximally different predictive likelihoods.
Contrastive Active Learning performs consistently as well as or better than the best baseline across all tasks and on both in-domain and out-of-domain data.
Contrastive Active Learning achieves a better trade-off between uncertainty and diversity than other acquisition strategies.
Dual latent manifolds enable typography analysis and font reconstruction.
Dual latent manifolds use two inference networks to infer separate latent variables for character and font from sets of glyphs that share a character type or belong to the same font.
Dual latent manifolds enable font reconstruction for a larger number of character types.
Dual latent manifolds generalize to characters not observed during training.
Dual latent manifolds use a loss function that measures likelihood using an adaptive distribution in a projected space to generate natural images without a discriminator.
TransferNet supports both label and text relations in a unified framework for multi-hop question answering.
TransferNet jumps across entities at multiple steps.
At each step, TransferNet attends to different parts of the question.
At each step, TransferNet computes activated scores for relations.
At each step, TransferNet transfers the previous entity scores along activated relations in a differentiable way.
TransferNet has transparent and interpretable intermediate results.
Sequential Randomized Smoothing uses speech enhancement and ROVER voting for adversarial robustness in speech recognition.
Sequential Randomized Smoothing is robust to inaudible adversarial attacks and is only vulnerable to attacks with high distortion.

Enforcing consistency between predictions on observed examples and examples with randomly substituted words often yields comparable or greater benefits than complex data augmentation techniques in natural language processing.
Unsupervised Data Augmentation improves performance for natural language processing tasks without unlabeled data.
Neural Attention-Aware Hierarchical Topic Model jointly reconstructs sentence and document word counts using combinations of bag-of-words topical embeddings and pre-trained semantic embeddings.
Neural Attention-Aware Hierarchical Topic Model transforms pre-trained semantic embeddings into a common latent topical space to align their semantics with bag-of-words topical embeddings.

Neural Attention-Aware Hierarchical Topic Model uses hierarchical KL divergence to leverage document embeddings to regularize sentence embeddings, paying more attention to semantically relevant sentences.
Neural Attention-Aware Hierarchical Topic Model lowers sentence and document reconstruction errors.
Neural Attention-Aware Hierarchical Topic Model discovers more coherent topics from real-world datasets.
COVR is a test-bed for visually-grounded compositional generalization with real images.
COVR uses an almost fully automatic procedure to generate question-answer pairs and context images.
COVR focuses on questions requiring complex reasoning, including quantification and aggregation.
COVR facilitates the creation of compositional splits for zero- or few-shot generalization to new concepts and compositions.
State-of-the-art pre-trained language-and-vision models struggle to compositionally generalize on COVR compositional splits.
PASTE is a tagging-free solution for Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction.
PASTE uses a Pointer Network-based decoding framework that generates an entire opinion triplet at each time step, making it end-to-end.
PASTE's decoder captures interactions between aspects and opinions by considering their entire detected spans when predicting sentiment.
PASTE extracts multiple aspect sentiment triplets, including those with overlapping aspect or opinion spans, from a single review sentence.
A task assesses pre-trained language models on open book and closed book question answering with college-level textbooks.
T5 fine-tuned with BoolQ achieves random-level performance on college-level textbook question answering.
Adding college-level textbooks to pre-training yields only minor improvement in closed book question answering.
Open-book question answering yields better performance than closed book question answering for college-level textbooks.
Pre-trained language models may not have understood college-level textbooks in closed book question answering.
SciClaim is a dataset of scientific claims from Social and Behavior Science (SBS), PubMed, and CORD-19 papers.
SciClaim uses a graph annotation schema that includes coarse-grained entity spans as nodes, relations as edges, and fine-grained attributes modifying entities and their relations.
SciClaim captures causal, comparative, predictive, statistical, and proportional associations over experimental variables along with their qualifications, subtypes, and evidence.
An extension of transformer-based joint entity and relation extraction infers the graph annotation schema for scientific claims.
ECONET equips pre-trained language models with targeted knowledge about event temporal relations.
ECONET uses a self-supervised objective to recover masked-out event and temporal indicators.
ECONET uses a self-supervised objective to discriminate sentences from corrupted counterparts where event or temporal indicators are replaced.
ECONET achieves state-of-the-art or on-par performance on multiple event temporal reasoning tasks.
MATE uses multi-view attention to attend to rows and columns in tables.
MATE scales linearly in speed and memory.
MATE handles very long documents containing tables.
MATE has a more appropriate inductive bias for tabular data.
MATE sets a new state-of-the-art on three table reasoning datasets.
Iterative Alignment Network (IA-Net) iteratively aligns inter- and intra-modal features for temporal sentence grounding.
Iterative Alignment Network (IA-Net) pads multi-modal features with learnable parameters to alleviate the nowhere-to-attend problem for non-matched frame-word pairs.
Iterative Alignment Network (IA-Net) enhances the basic co-attention mechanism in a parallel manner for alignment in temporal sentence grounding.
Iterative Alignment Network (IA-Net) calibrates misaligned attention after each reasoning step.
Iterative Alignment Network (IA-Net) outperforms state-of-the-art methods on three temporal sentence grounding benchmarks.
Information extraction for semi-structured document images is formulated as a sequence generation task.
A single end-to-end information extraction system for semi-structured document images achieves competent performance in real, large-scale production.
STaCK predicts the relative order of sentences by modeling global information with graph neural networks and temporal commonsense knowledge.
STaCK accumulates temporal evidence using knowledge of 'past' and 'future' and formulates sentence ordering as a constrained edge classification problem.
ConvSearch is an end-to-end conversational search system that deeply combines the dialog system with search.
ConvSearch retrieves products using text profiles, which is more robust against imperfect product schema and knowledge than using product attributes alone.
Utterance transfer generates dialogue utterances by using existing dialog from other domains and leveraging search behavior data from e-commerce retailers.
Utterance transfer generates a new conversational search dataset for online shopping.
Utterance transfer improves the availability of training dialogue data without crowd-sourcing.
ConvSearch outperforms the best tested baseline in conversational search for online shopping.
Question answering evaluation is characterized by two paradigms: one that values creating more intelligent systems and another that values building systems that appeal to users.
Subtle but significant distinctions between the two question answering evaluation paradigms exert an outsize influence on question answering research.
IndoNLG evaluates natural language generation for Indonesian, Javanese, and Sundanese on summarization, question answering, chit-chat, and machine translation tasks.
Indo4B-Plus is a clean pretraining corpus for Indonesian, Sundanese, and Javanese.
IndoBART and IndoGPT are natural language generation models pretrained on Indonesian, Sundanese, and Javanese.
IndoBART and IndoGPT achieve competitive performance on natural language generation tasks for Indonesian, Javanese, and Sundanese while using fewer parameters than a larger multilingual model.
Pretraining on closely related, localized languages enables more efficient learning and faster inference for very low-resource languages such as Javanese and Sundanese.
Prompt tuning conditions frozen language models for specific downstream tasks using soft prompts learned through backpropagation.
Prompt tuning outperforms GPT-3's few-shot learning.
Prompt tuning matches the performance of model tuning when language models exceed billions of parameters.
Prompt tuning improves robustness to domain transfer for frozen language models.
Prompt tuning enables efficient prompt ensembling for frozen language models.
The transductive learning approach for unsupervised text style transfer uses a retrieval-based context-aware style representation.
The transductive learning approach for unsupervised text style transfer uses an attentional encoder-decoder with a retriever framework.
The transductive learning approach for unsupervised text style transfer involves top-K relevant sentences in the target style during style transfer.
The transductive learning approach for unsupervised text style transfer uses both sparse (BM25) and dense (MIPS) retrieval functions.
The transductive learning approach for unsupervised text style transfer uses two objective functions to facilitate joint learning.
Computational expenses for intermediate task selection methods are quantified.
Embedding based methods for identifying beneficial intermediate tasks outperform few-shot fine-tuning approaches.
RankNAS uses pairwise ranking for performance ranking in neural architecture search.
RankNAS enables efficient neural architecture search with fewer training examples.
An architecture selection method prunes the search space to concentrate on more promising candidates.
RankNAS designs high-performance architectures for machine translation and language modeling.
RankNAS is orders of magnitude faster than state-of-the-art neural architecture search systems for machine translation and language modeling.
Parameterizing module arguments reduces the number of modules in neural module networks without loss in performance.
Contextualization enhances the capacity of neural module networks to exploit visiolinguistic associations.
CC-Ref+ is a contrast set for evaluating contextualization in visual referring expression grounding.
Contextualized neural module networks outperform prior models on multiple visual reasoning benchmarks including CLEVR-Ref+, CLOSURE, NLVR2, and CC-Ref+.
Different task-oriented dialogue tasks prefer different further pre-training tasks.
Most further pre-training tasks improve only certain task-oriented dialogue tasks rather than all.
Further pre-training tasks that model specific information benefit task-oriented dialogue tasks.
The mutual-learning scenario collaboratively trains machine translation and speech translation models as peers, not in a teacher-student relationship.
The mutual-learning scenario improves end-to-end speech translation performance more effectively than the teacher-student paradigm.
The mutual-learning scenario improves machine translation performance.
Models in the mutual-learning scenario effectively utilize auxiliary information from peer models.
ToxiChat is a crowd-annotated dataset of Reddit threads and model responses labeled with offensive language and stance.
Human responses show higher agreement with toxic comments than with safe comments in Reddit conversations.
Neural dialogue models show higher agreement with offensive comments than with safe comments.
Controllable text generation methods reduce agreement with offensive comments by neural dialogue models.
Controllable text generation methods reduce the generation of offensive replies by neural dialogue models.
HetFormer uses multi-granularity sparse attentions for long-text extractive summarization.
HetFormer models different types of semantic nodes in raw text as a heterogeneous graph and learns the heterogeneous relationships among these nodes.
HetFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance on long-text single- and multi-document extractive summarization tasks.
HetFormer uses less memory and fewer parameters for long-text extractive summarization.
CATE detects metaphors using semi-supervised learning.
CATE employs a contrastive objective to promote increased distance between literal and metaphorical senses of target words based on linguistic theories.
CATE uses a strategy to collect large-scale candidate instances for metaphor detection from a general corpus.
Phrase-BERT produces phrase embeddings using a contrastive fine-tuning objective.
A diverse phrasal paraphrase dataset supports phrase embedding training.
A large-scale dataset of phrases in context from the Books3 corpus supports phrase embedding training.
Phrase-BERT outperforms baselines on phrase-level similarity tasks.
Phrase-BERT exhibits increased lexical diversity between nearest neighbors in the embedding space.
A phrase-based neural topic model interprets topics as mixtures of words and phrases using Phrase-BERT embeddings and a simple autoencoder.
A phrase-based neural topic model produces more coherent and meaningful topics than baseline topic models.
A joint aspect-based sentiment analysis model leverages both encoder sharing and task-specific differences between subtasks.
A dual-encoder design for joint aspect-based sentiment analysis uses one encoder for candidate aspect-opinion pair classification and another encoder for sequence labeling.
Reference-Centric Models ground referents from partner utterances using a structured reference resolver.
Reference-Centric Models condition on referents from partner utterances using a recurrent memory.
Reference-Centric Models use a pragmatic generation procedure to produce references that the partner can resolve.
Reference-Centric Models outperform the state of the art on the OneCommon spatial grounding dialogue task in self-play and human evaluations.
StreamHover annotates and summarizes livestream transcripts.
A benchmark dataset for livestream transcript summarization contains both extractive and abstractive summaries and is larger than existing annotated corpora.
A neural extractive summarization model uses a vector-quantized variational autoencoder to learn latent vector representations of spoken utterances and identify salient utterances to form summaries.
A neural extractive summarization model using vector-quantized variational autoencoder generalizes better than strong baselines for livestream transcript summarization.

A global feature-oriented model for relational triple extraction refines table features through an iterative generate-mine-integrate process that incorporates global associations of relations and token pairs.
A global feature-oriented model for relational triple extraction achieves state-of-the-art performance on three benchmark datasets.
HittER jointly learns entity-relation composition and relational contextualization from the neighborhood of a source entity.
HittER uses a bottom Transformer block to extract features of each entity-relation pair in the local neighborhood of a source entity.
HittER uses a top Transformer block to aggregate relational information from the outputs of the bottom block.
HittER uses a masked entity prediction task to balance information from the relational context and the source entity.
HittER achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple link prediction datasets.
SEFL eliminates the need for trusted entities in federated learning for NLP.
SEFL achieves model accuracy comparable to or better than existing federated learning frameworks for NLP.
SEFL is resilient to client dropouts in federated learning for NLP.

A 2-stage approach generates training data for acceptability classifiers by first creating synthetic data using a combination of existing and new model-based approaches and then filtering and sorting the data with a validation framework.
A 2-stage approach for generating training data for acceptability classifiers adapts to a wide range of data representations and uses only the data the NLG models are trained on.

A 2-stage approach for generating training data for acceptability classifiers is independent of the underlying NLG model architecture and generates realistic samples close to the distribution of NLG model-generated responses.
Acceptability classifiers built using data resembling generation model outputs and a validation framework outperform existing techniques.
Acceptability classifiers for NLG responses built without human references are suitable for runtime deployment.
Techniques for building acceptability classifiers work in few-shot settings via self-training.
Cross-modal input ablation evaluates cross-modal integration in vision-and-language models by measuring prediction performance on the other modality.
Vision-and-language models exhibit stronger influence of visual inputs on text prediction than of text inputs on visual prediction.
Connect-the-Dots aligns definition sentences that express the same meaning from different word sense inventories to collect lexical knowledge.
Connect-the-Dots identifies semantic equivalence between a target word in context and one of its definition sentences using aligned word sense inventories.
Connect-the-Dots improves Word Sense Disambiguation predictions for both frequent and rare word senses.
Connect-the-Dots better captures word meanings in context as measured by the Word-in-Context Task.
A base Non-AutoRegressive model for unsupervised text style transfer uses a training scheme adapted from AutoRegressive models.
Knowledge distillation, contrastive learning, and iterative decoding narrow the performance gap between Non-AutoRegressive and AutoRegressive decoding for unsupervised text style transfer.
Synthetic textual features serve as Rosch-style semantic features for the large-scale detection of Basic-level Categories in English and Mandarin.
Basic-level Category detection using synthetic textual features achieves higher accuracy for English and Mandarin than the state of the art.
Basic-level Category detection using synthetic textual features identifies fewer synsets in English WordNet as Basic-level Categories than existing methods with higher precision.
Rosch’s cue validity algorithm is useful for Basic-level Category detection.
A new indicator for Basic-level Categories models that Basic-level Category features tend to be Basic-level Categories themselves.
A classification task predicts the probability of a hypothesis given facts and soft rules.
The first benchmark dataset evaluates models on predicting hypothesis probabilities given facts and soft rules.
A revised loss function trains pre-trained language models to predict precise probabilities given facts and soft rules.
Fine-tuned models generalize to logical rules not seen during training.
Logical notions expressed by soft rules are transferred to fine-tuned models.
Word-level coreference resolution considers coreference links between individual words and then reconstructs word spans.
Word-level coreference resolution reduces the complexity of coreference modeling to O(n^2).
Word-level coreference resolution allows considering all potential mentions without pruning.
RoBERTa outperforms SpanBERT in word-level coreference resolution.
Word-level coreference resolution performs competitively with recent coreference resolution systems on the OntoNotes benchmark.
Models pretrained on more data encode more syntactic knowledge.
Models pretrained on more data achieve better performance on part-of-speech tagging, dependency parsing, and paraphrase identification.
Models pretrained on more data do not improve performance on every syntactic phenomenon.
Models pretrained on more data incur higher financial and environmental costs.
An efficient mind-map generation network converts documents into graphs via sequence-to-graph.
A graph refinement module adjusts relation graphs using reinforcement learning.
Mind-maps generated by sequence-to-graph and reinforced graph refinement better reveal the underlying semantic structures of documents.
Specific token representations in BERT, which are often ignored in standard classification setups, enable BERT to detect syntactic and semantic abnormalities.
Specific token representations in BERT, which are often ignored in standard classification setups, enable BERT to distinctively separate grammatical number and tense subspaces.
Matched phrase-region pairs are extracted from pre-trained vision-and-language embeddings without fine-tuning.
Four fine-tuning objectives trained with image-caption data without supervised grounding signals outperform baseline models in weakly-supervised and supervised phrase grounding settings.
Embeddings optimized for phrase grounding achieve better phrase grounding without sacrificing representation generality on other downstream tasks.
Decision-focused summarization summarizes relevant information for a decision.
DecSum selects representative sentences that lead to similar model decisions as the full text while accounting for textual non-redundancy.
A testbed for decision-focused summarization requires summarizing the first ten reviews of a restaurant to predict its future Yelp rating.
DecSum achieves higher decision faithfulness and representativeness than text-only summarization methods and model-based explanation methods.
DecSum is the only method that enables humans to predict which restaurant will be better rated in the future above random chance.
Gradient Imitation Reinforcement Learning encourages pseudo label data to imitate the gradient descent direction on labeled data.
Gradient Imitation Reinforcement Learning bootstraps optimization capability through trial and error.
GradLRE handles low-resource relation extraction without unlabeled data by generating data via contextualized augmentation.
Six popular language models are probed for semantic relations.
Hummingbird contains human annotations of representative words for politeness, sentiment, offensiveness, and five emotion types.
BERT identifies content words irrelevant to the target style as important for style prediction, whereas humans do not consider these words as representative of the style.
Human-identified and machine-identified words for style prediction share significant overlap for some styles.
MULTI-EURLEX is a multilingual dataset for topic classification of European Union legal documents in 23 languages with multiple EUROVOC labels.
Temporal concept drift in legal document classification makes chronological data splits more important than random splits.

Fine-tuning multilingually pretrained models for legal document classification on a single source language causes catastrophic forgetting of multilingual knowledge, leading to poor zero-shot cross-lingual transfer.

Adaptation strategies (partial fine-tuning, adapters, BITFIT, LNFIT) retain multilingual knowledge during fine-tuning for legal document classification, improving zero-shot cross-lingual transfer, but their effectiveness depends on the pretrained model and the size of the label set.
An end-to-end neural model generates diverse mathematical word problems in real-world scenarios from a commonsense knowledge graph and equations.
A method learns representations of symbolic equations and commonsense knowledge from edge-enhanced Levi graphs.
A self-planning module automatically fuses equation and commonsense knowledge information during mathematical word problem generation.
A mathematical word problem generation model outperforms state-of-the-art models in automatic and human evaluations.

Vision guided generative pre-trained language models incorporate visual information for multimodal abstractive summarization using attention-based add-on layers without degrading text generation ability.
Vision guided generative pre-trained language models achieve state-of-the-art results for multimodal abstractive summarization on the How2 dataset.
The vision guidance method accounts for most of the performance improvement in multimodal abstractive summarization on the How2 dataset.
Child-Tuning updates a child network of large pretrained models by masking out the gradients of the non-child network during the backward process.
Child-Tuning consistently outperforms vanilla fine-tuning on the GLUE benchmark.
Child-Tuning outperforms prior fine-tuning techniques on the GLUE benchmark.
Child-Tuning obtains better generalization performance on domain transfer and task transfer.
Task augmentation synthesizes a large amount of data for auxiliary-task fine-tuning from target-task unlabeled texts.
STraTA uses self-training to fine-tune the strong base model created by task augmentation on a broad distribution of pseudo-labeled data.
STraTA improves sample efficiency across multiple few-shot benchmarks.
Task augmentation and self-training are complementary and independently effective.
Most open-ended text generation papers fail to report crucial details about their Amazon Mechanical Turk tasks, hindering reproducibility.
Amazon Mechanical Turk workers fail to distinguish between model-generated text and human-generated references even with strict qualification filters, unlike English teachers.
Presenting model-generated text alongside human-generated references improves the quality of Amazon Mechanical Turk worker judgments by enabling better calibration.
Interviews with English teachers reveal challenges in the evaluation of model-generated text.
ExplaGraphs is a generative and structured commonsense-reasoning task that requires predicting the stance of an argument toward a belief and generating a commonsense-augmented explanation graph.
ExplaGraphs provides a dataset of explanation graphs for stance prediction.
The Create-Verify-And-Refine framework collects explanation graphs through multiple rounds of verification and refinement.
Many explanation graphs in ExplaGraphs contain external commonsense nodes with diverse structures and reasoning depths.
The multi-level evaluation framework checks the structural and semantic correctness of generated explanation graphs and their match with ground-truth graphs.
Pseudoword vectors and masked prediction enable investigation of how regions around individual points in contextualized vector spaces correspond to word senses.
BERT-space exhibits regions that correspond to distinct word senses.
BERT-space occasionally exhibits sense voids between regions that do not correspond to any intelligible sense.
BeliefBank records and may modify raw pretrained language model answers.
A weighted MaxSAT solver revises beliefs that significantly clash with other beliefs.
A feedback component issues future queries to pretrained language models using known beliefs as context.
The reasoning and feedback mechanisms result in more consistent beliefs.
The reasoning and feedback mechanisms improve answer accuracy and consistency over time.
CBR-KBQA is a neuro-symbolic case-based reasoning approach for question answering over large knowledge bases.
CBR-KBQA uses a nonparametric memory to store cases of questions and logical forms.
CBR-KBQA uses a parametric model to generate logical forms for new questions by retrieving relevant cases.
CBR-KBQA outperforms the state of the art on the CWQ dataset.
CBR-KBQA can incorporate a few new human-labeled examples without further training to generate logical forms for unseen knowledge base entities and relations.
Rewriting the target side of full-sentence corpora generates pseudo-references for simultaneous translation with fewer unnecessary long-distance reorderings.
Generated pseudo-references improve simultaneous translation performance.
A Transformer-based recurrent architecture encodes constituency parse trees for story visualization.
Commonsense information augments structured input for story visualization.
Bounding boxes and dense captioning incorporate visual structure to provide feedback about characters and objects in generated images within a dual learning setup for story visualization.
Off-the-shelf dense-captioning models trained on Visual Genome improve spatial structure of images in a different target domain without fine-tuning.
Intra-story contrastive loss between words and image sub-regions improves visual quality in story visualization.
Predominantly female and non-white names are less frequent in the training corpora of BERT, GPT-2, T5, and XLNet.
Representations of infrequent names in BERT, GPT-2, T5, and XLNet have higher self-similarity across contexts.
Representations of infrequent names in BERT, GPT-2, T5, and XLNet have lower similarity to their initial token representations.
In BERT, lower-frequency minority group names are associated with higher unpleasantness.
Representations of infrequent names in BERT, GPT-2, T5, and XLNet are overfit to a lower number of observed contexts.
Graph segmentation and alignment are treated as latent variables and induced during end-to-end training for AMR parsing.
A differentiable relaxation is introduced for graph segmentation and alignment to enable end-to-end differentiable optimization in AMR parsing.
Inducing graph segmentation during training yields gains over greedy segmentation heuristics for AMR parsing.
Inducing graph segmentation during training approaches the performance of hand-crafted segmentation rules for AMR parsing.
Schema-driven prompting provides task-aware history encoding for dialogue state tracking of both categorical and non-categorical slots.
Schema-driven prompting augmented with schema descriptions incorporates in-domain knowledge for dialogue state tracking.
Schema-driven prompting with schema descriptions achieves state-of-the-art performance on MultiWOZ 2.2 and competitive performance on MultiWOZ 2.1 and M2M for dialogue state tracking.
End-to-end training of entity resolution and question answering for knowledge graphs requires only question text and answer entities.
A single model performs entity resolution and question answering for knowledge graphs without requiring an external entity resolution component during inference.
End-to-end training of entity resolution and question answering for knowledge graphs is fully differentiable.
End-to-end training of entity resolution and question answering for knowledge graphs achieves performance close to models that use hand-annotated entities.
JPR jointly ranks passages for multi-answer retrieval.
JPR uses an autoregressive reranker to model the joint probability of retrieved passages by selecting a sequence of passages.
JPR achieves better answer coverage for multi-answer retrieval than prior approaches.
JPR enables the use of larger answer generation models in question answering by reducing the number of passages to consider.

Multi-task learning with transformer encoders underperforms single-task learning on part-of-speech tagging, named entity recognition, dependency parsing, constituency parsing, and semantic role labeling.
A set of attention heads in transformer encoders are claimed by most tasks during multi-task learning, causing interference when fine-tuning for individual task objectives.
Some attention heads naturally talented for many tasks cannot be jointly trained to create adequate embeddings for all of those tasks.
Parameter-free probes justify the Stem Cell Hypothesis.

Parameter-free probes demonstrate how attention heads transform across part-of-speech tagging, named entity recognition, dependency parsing, constituency parsing, and semantic role labeling during multi-task learning.
BCNH uses list-wise training to utilize hypernyms and synonyms for biomedical concept normalization.
BCNH applies a norm constraint on the representation of hypernym-hyponym entity pairs.
BCNH outperforms the previous state-of-the-art model for biomedical concept normalization on the NCBI dataset.
QuestEval uses question answering to determine if a summary contains all relevant information from the source document.
QuestEval does not require a ground-truth reference.
QuestEval improves correlation with human judgments for consistency, coherence, fluency, and relevance.
Sparse Phased Transformer uses a sampling function to generate a sparse attention matrix and compress long sequences to shorter sequences of hidden states.
Sparse Phased Transformer concurrently captures interactions between hidden states of different modalities at every layer.
Layer-wise parameter sharing shares parameters between Cross Attention Blocks.
Factorized Co-Attention shares parameters between Cross Attention Blocks.
Sparse Phased Transformer achieves comparable or superior performance on sentiment analysis tasks with reduced model size.
Sparse Phased Transformer improves sample efficiency for sentiment analysis tasks.
Definition generation with appropriate specificity uses the Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer.
A re-ranking mechanism models specificity in definitions.
Combining the Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer and a re-ranking mechanism in definition generation addresses the over/under-specificity problems.
Pretrained language models generate datasets of labeled text pairs from scratch for sentence embedding training.

Training sentence embedding models on generated datasets of labeled text pairs avoids the need for labeled data, finetuning the pretrained language model, and modifications to the pretraining objective.

Generating datasets of labeled text pairs with pretrained language models and finetuning smaller and more efficient sentence embedding models on them outperforms strong baselines on semantic textual similarity tasks.

In out-of-distribution contexts, neural language models interpolate between a predictive distribution consistent with the last word observed and a predictive distribution consistent with the global structure of the input, with predictions approximated by a log-linear combination of the two.
In some languages and in out-of-distribution contexts, noise applied to input tokens encourages generalization consistent with the global structure of the input in neural language models.
In some languages and in out-of-distribution contexts, noise in history representations encourages generalization consistent with the last word observed in neural language models.

Log-linear models with a particular feature correlation structure exhibit interpolation between a predictive distribution consistent with the last word observed and a predictive distribution consistent with the global structure of the input.

Crossmodal-3600 is an evaluation dataset for massively multilingual image captioning comprising 3600 images from regions where the 36 languages are spoken, with human-generated reference captions in consistent style across languages and without direct translation artifacts.
Automatic metrics for massively multilingual image captioning achieve superior correlation with human evaluations when using Crossmodal-3600 as golden references.
Paraphrasing is a principled data augmentation scheme for unsupervised consistency training in named entity recognition.
Conditional Random Field is converted into a multi-label classification module for unsupervised consistency training in named entity recognition.
Consistency on entity appearance is encouraged between original and paraphrased sequences in named entity recognition.
Unsupervised Paraphrasing Consistency Training improves named entity recognition performance in low-resource settings.
Character-level and word-level perturbation methods simulate realistic scenarios of input noise and distribution shifts.
Language models are sensitive to input perturbations and experience performance decreases from small input changes.
Current benchmarks do not reflect model robustness well.

DANLI follows natural language instructions by proactively applying reasoning and planning based on neural and symbolic representations acquired from past experience in natural language and egocentric vision.
DANLI outperforms reactive baselines on the TEACh benchmark.
DANLI provides transparency and explainability for agent behaviors through its reasoning and planning processes and modular framework.
Synthetic (review, summary) pairs in opinion summarization are enriched with aspect controllers.
Aspect controllers are induced by a multi-instance learning model that predicts document aspects at multiple granularity levels.
Aspect-specific opinion summaries are generated by modifying aspect controllers.
SimQA evaluates simultaneous machine translation by translating source language questions word by word and answering as soon as possible.
SimQA jointly measures the speed and accuracy of simultaneous machine translation models in question translation.
Visual Spatial Description produces descriptions focusing on the spatial perspective between two objects in an image.
A manually annotated dataset is created for the Visual Spatial Description task.
Benchmark encoder-decoder models for Visual Spatial Description use VL-BART and VL-T5 as backbones.
Visual Spatial Description models integrate Visual Spatial Relationship Classification using pipeline and end-to-end architectures.
The joint end-to-end architecture is the better choice for integrating Visual Spatial Relationship Classification with Visual Spatial Description.
UNKs Everywhere uses data-efficient methods to adapt pretrained multilingual models to low-resource languages written in unseen scripts.
UNKs Everywhere uses matrix factorization to leverage latent knowledge about multiple languages in the pretrained model's embedding matrix.
UNKs Everywhere improves learning of a new dedicated embedding matrix for the target language by leveraging lexically overlapping tokens shared between mBERT and the target language vocabulary.
UNKs Everywhere improves cross-lingual transfer performance for languages with unseen scripts.
UNKs Everywhere improves cross-lingual transfer performance for low-resource languages written in scripts covered by the pretrained model.
CovidET is a dataset of English Reddit posts related to COVID-19 containing manual annotations of perceived emotions and abstractive summaries of emotion triggers.
Baseline models jointly detect emotions and summarize emotion triggers.
CovidET presents new challenges for emotion-specific summarization.
CovidET presents new challenges for multi-emotion detection in long social media posts.
Wilson-RC produces biased samples for dependency tree sampling with replacement.
Two algorithms produce unbiased samples for dependency tree sampling with replacement.
Two algorithms (incremental and parallel) achieve O(kn^3) runtime for sampling k dependency trees without replacement.
CorrKG uses optimal transport for soft correction to align salient information and rectify semantic bias between source documents and predicted keyphrases.
CorrKG uses frequency-based filtering-and-sorting for hard correction to reduce bias between predicted and ground truth keyphrases and generate accurate and sufficient keyphrases.
Candidate Soups fully uses the valuable information in different candidate translations through model uncertainty.
Candidate Soups improves the translation quality of various non-autoregressive translation models.
Candidate Soups achieves better translation quality than autoregressive translation models while maintaining the faster inference speed of non-autoregressive translation models.
EMMR detects key missing modalities by checking semantic consistency in multimodal sentiment analysis.
EMMR integrates multiple encoder-decoder approaches for sentiment prediction when an inconsistency due to a key missing modality is detected.
Data selection strategies for language model pretraining select unlabeled target domain data for entity extraction without requiring external supervision.
Language models pretrained using the data selection strategies achieve better named entity recognition performance than those using externally supervised data selection strategies.
Language models pretrained using the data selection strategies achieve better named entity recognition performance than those pretrained on all available unlabeled target domain data.
RAPO learns a personalized mapping function for each word in bilingual lexicon induction.
RAPO simultaneously leverages the unique characteristics of individual words and cross-language isomorphism in bilingual lexicon induction.
RAPO achieves superior performance on bilingual lexicon induction for both rich-resource and low-resource languages.
Segment act extends dialog act from the utterance level to the segment level.
A large-scale dataset for segment act is crowdsourced.
FlowEval is the first consensus-based dialogue evaluation framework that utilizes segment act flows.
FlowEval provides a reference-free approach for dialog evaluation by finding pseudo-references.
The new Hebrew UD treebank covers diverse topics from Hebrew Wikipedia.
The annotation quality of the new Hebrew UD treebank is evaluated.
Cross-domain parsing experiments are conducted in Hebrew for the first time.
State-of-the-art results are achieved on Hebrew UD NLP tasks.
The updated UD Hebrew Treebank matches the annotation scheme of the new corpus.
Kernelization of the linear concept-removal objective guards against concept recovery by certain nonlinear adversaries.
The division between linear and nonlinear concept encoding is overly simplistic.
For binary gender neutralization, no single kernel space exclusively contains all concept-related information.
Protecting against all nonlinear adversaries at once is challenging for concept erasure.
NLProofS generates relevant proof steps by conditioning on the hypothesis.
NLProofS uses an independent verifier to check the validity of proof steps to prevent hallucination.
NLProofS searches for proofs by maximizing a global proof score judged by the verifier.
NLProofS achieves state-of-the-art performance on EntailmentBank and RuleTaker.
A fine-tuned T5-based text-to-text transformer struggles with large regular languages in instruction learning.
Instruction learning tasks requiring tracking longer contexts of prior steps are difficult.
Hard RegSet is a challenging instruction learning dataset.
Hard RegSet provides a controlled environment for studying instruction learning.
SciHTC is a dataset for hierarchical multi-label text classification of scientific papers containing 186,160 papers and 1,234 categories from the ACM CCS tree.
Strong baselines exist for hierarchical multi-label text classification on the SciHTC dataset.
A multi-task learning approach for hierarchical multi-label topic classification uses keyword labeling as an auxiliary task.
The SciHTC dataset is challenging for hierarchical multi-label text classification.
Fine-tuned Language Models can be continual learners.
Continual Learning emerges from self-supervision pre-training.
Continual-T0 learns new diverse language generation tasks while maintaining performance on previous tasks.
Continual-T0 combines instructions in novel ways, demonstrating instruction compositionality.
SimSeek-sym generates follow-up questions based on a predetermined answer.
SimSeek-asym generates a question and then finds its corresponding answer within the conversational context.
SimSeek-asym conversations receive favorable reviews in human evaluation.
Wiki-SimSeek is a large-scale resource of synthetic conversational question answering pairs built from Wikipedia documents.
Wiki-SimSeek enables state-of-the-art performance on the QuAC benchmark for conversational question answering.
Reference-free metrics achieve better performance than reference-based metrics for paraphrase evaluation.
Most commonly used metrics for paraphrase evaluation do not align well with human annotation.
ParaScore possesses the merits of reference-based and reference-free metrics for paraphrase evaluation.
ParaScore explicitly models lexical divergence for paraphrase evaluation.
The necessity of position information for masked language modeling increases with the amount of masking.
Masked language models without position encodings cannot reconstruct position information from co-occurrences alone in masked language modeling.
There is a direct relationship between the amount of masking in masked language modeling and the ability of Transformers to capture order-sensitive aspects of language using position encoding.
MUSIED benchmarks event detection on Chinese informal texts from user reviews, text conversations, and phone conversations in a food service e-commerce platform.
MUSIED exhibits textual informality and multi-domain heterogeneity.
The textual informality and multi-domain heterogeneity of MUSIED pose unique challenges for event detection.
Twist decoding is a text generation algorithm that benefits from diverse models at inference time.
Twist decoding does not require shared vocabulary, tokenization, or generation order.

Twist decoding substantially outperforms individual models in machine translation and scientific paper summarization across various scenarios, including when domain-specific and general-purpose models are available.
Twist decoding consistently outperforms the reranking heuristic that rescores output candidates from one model using another model.
A linguistic probing eye movement paradigm extracts eye movement features based on the close relationship between linguistic features and early and late human reading behavior processes.
A weighting strategy integrates sentiment scores from affective commonsense knowledge into eye movement features to form sentiment-eye movement weights.
SEMGraph models intricate sentiment relationships in the context.
SEMGraph directly generalizes to sentiment analysis datasets without eye movement signals.
UniNL detects out-of-domain intents in task-oriented dialogue systems.
KNCL is an objective for representation learning for out-of-domain intent detection.
A KNN-based scoring function detects out-of-domain intents.
UniNL aligns representation learning with the scoring function for out-of-domain intent detection.
Source word quality scoring in neural translation is based on comparing how well each source word is explained by the target sentence versus the source language model.
Source word quality scoring in neural translation does not require training a new model.
Source word quality scoring achieves higher accuracy than state-of-the-art methods on multiple language pairs.
Source word quality scoring is faster than state-of-the-art methods on multiple language pairs.
A dataset contains examples of word omissions and mistranslations for a new language pair in neural translation.

The lack of mutual exclusivity bias, defined as the constraint that one target sequence can only be mapped to one source sequence, is a contributing factor to poor systematic generalization in standard sequence-to-sequence models.
The tendency of standard sequence-to-sequence models to memorize whole examples rather than separating structures from contents is a contributing factor to poor systematic generalization.
Mutual Exclusivity Training prevents sequence-to-sequence models from producing seen generations when facing novel examples via an unlikelihood-based loss.
prim2primX data augmentation automatically diversifies the arguments of every syntactic function to prevent memorization and provide a compositional inductive bias without exposing test-set data.
Combining Mutual Exclusivity Training and prim2primX data augmentation improves systematic generalization on the SCAN and COGS datasets.
PRO-CS combines language and task knowledge for code-switched tasks using instance-based prompt composition.
PRO-CS outperforms prompt-tuning across all datasets and matches or outperforms fine-tuning for code-switched tasks while using a very small fraction of the parameters.
PRO-CS achieves performance comparable to fine-tuned models in low-resource cross-lingual and cross-task settings for code-switched tasks.
ALCQA uses a question rewriting model to align natural language questions with individual actions in complex knowledge base question answering.
ALCQA utilizes a pretrained language model to implicitly align natural language questions with knowledge graph artifacts in complex knowledge base question answering.
ALCQA retrieves top-k similar question-answer pairs through question-to-question alignment during inference for complex knowledge base question answering.
ALCQA uses a reward-guided action sequence selection strategy to select the action sequence from candidate action sequences in complex knowledge base question answering.
Encoding language models with trivial graphs (e.g., balanced trees) achieves competitive or better performance on GLUE benchmark tasks in fully-supervised and few-shot settings.
The performance gains from linguistic knowledge fusion are not significantly attributed to explicit linguistic priors but to feature interactions brought by fusion layers.
Trajectory features enhance video-language alignment in VideoQA.
A heterogeneous graph architecture aligns trajectory-level and frame-level visual features with language features in VideoQA.
VideoQA models depend on language priors and neglect visual-language interactions.
Two training augmentation strategies strengthen cross-modal correspondence in VideoQA models.
SEASON utilizes the allocation of salience expectation to guide abstractive summarization.
SEASON adapts to articles with varying abstractiveness.
News article sentences exhibit a natural salience split.
Mixture of short-channel distillers (MSD) uses a multi-channel distillation framework to aggregate multiple distillers as a mixture for sufficient information transfer.
Mixture of short-channel distillers (MSD) uses an unsupervised parallel domain adaptation method to shorten channels between teacher and student models and preserve domain-invariant features.
Mixture of short-channel distillers (MSD) achieves state-of-the-art performance on zero-shot cross-lingual named entity recognition.
Mixture of short-channel distillers (MSD) shows generalization and compatibility across languages and fields.
Combining translation for training data generation and translation for inference data generation synergizes results on multilingual sentence classification tasks.

Translation artifacts stylized by translators are the main factor for the performance gain achieved by combining translation for training data generation and inference data generation in multilingual sentence classification tasks.
MUSC is a cross-lingual fine-tuning algorithm that uses SupCon and MixUp jointly.
PRINCE replaces some previous ground-truth tokens that constitute the entity in the prefix with masks when predicting the next token within an entity.
PRINCE achieves state-of-the-art results on knowledge-driven data-to-text generation tasks.
Varifocal generates questions based on different focal points within a claim, including spans of the claim and its metadata such as source and date.
Varifocal outperforms previous methods on automatic evaluation metrics for fact-checking question generation.
Varifocal generates more relevant and informative questions for fact-checking as shown by manual evaluation.
Focal points can generate sets of clarification questions for product descriptions.
RLJP splits legal judgment prediction into two steps: generating rationales from case facts and predicting judgment from case facts and the generated rationales.
RLJP provides interactivity and interpretability for legal judgment prediction.
A unifying pipeline for constituency parsing as tagging consists of linearization, learning, and decoding steps.
Classic shift-reduce parsing can be reduced to tetratagging under the assumptions of right-corner transformation in linearization and factored scoring in learning.
The linearization of the derivation tree and its alignment with the input sequence is the most critical factor for accurate parsing-as-tagging methods.
A joint learning framework for restaurant survival prediction and explanation uses multi-modal data of user-restaurant interactions and users' textual reviews.
A graph neural network captures high-order user-restaurant interactions for restaurant survival prediction and explanation.
A co-attention mechanism captures informative signals from noisy textual reviews for restaurant survival prediction and explanation.
A joint learning framework for restaurant survival prediction and explanation improves prediction and explanation over state-of-the-art techniques.
Re-ranking passages with a zero-shot question generation model computes the probability of the input question conditioned on a retrieved passage.
Re-ranking passages with a zero-shot question generation model applies on top of any retrieval method including neural and keyword-based methods.
Re-ranking passages with a zero-shot question generation model does not require domain- or task-specific training.
Re-ranking passages with a zero-shot question generation model improves strong unsupervised retrieval models and strong supervised retrieval models in top-20 passage retrieval accuracy.
Re-ranking passages with a zero-shot question generation model, when added to existing models, achieves state-of-the-art results on full open-domain question answering.
A conceptual framework for Natural Language Generation guides system design to follow efficient and effective production strategies for achieving complex communicative goals.
A conceptual framework for Natural Language Generation characterises efficiency as the parsimonious regulation of production and comprehension costs.
A conceptual framework for Natural Language Generation characterises effectiveness as measured with respect to task-oriented and contextually grounded communicative goals.
A conceptual framework for Natural Language Generation provides concrete suggestions for estimating goals, costs, and utility via modern statistical methods.
A conceptual framework for Natural Language Generation is applied to visually grounded referential games and abstractive text summarisation.
DRLK extracts dynamic hierarchical features in the question answering context.
DRLK performs inter-layer and intra-layer interactions between the question answering context and knowledge graphs on each iteration.
DRLK achieves state-of-the-art performance on two benchmark datasets for medical question answering and commonsense reasoning.
Natural language patches correct model behavior using declarative statements that either override predictions or supply missing information.
Natural language patch applicability is determined separately from the integration of natural language patch information.
A small amount of synthetic data enables models to effectively use natural language patches on real data.

A small set of natural language patches achieves performance improvements on sentiment analysis and relation extraction that would otherwise require finetuning on a substantial number of labeled examples.
Fast-R2D2 uses a top-down unsupervised parser as a model-guided pruning method.
Fast-R2D2 enables parallel encoding during inference.
Fast-R2D2 casts parsing as a split point scoring task by scoring all split points for a sentence and recursively splitting spans using the highest-scoring split point.
Fast-R2D2 uses the reverse order of splits from the top-down unsupervised parser as the pruning order in the encoder.
Fast-R2D2 optimizes the unsupervised parser by minimizing the Kullback-Leibler distance between tree probabilities from the parser and the R2D2 model.
A Predictive State Simulator generates a predicted dialogue state from the ground-truth previous dialogue state during training.
A Slot Detector identifies slots with incorrect values in the predicted dialogue state and slots requiring updates in the current turn.
A State Generator uses the names of slots with incorrect values in the predicted dialogue state or requiring updates in the current turn as a prompt to generate the current dialogue state.
Correctable-DST achieves state-of-the-art joint goal accuracy on MultiWOZ datasets.
Chunk-based kNN-MT retrieves chunks of tokens from the datastore instead of single tokens.
Chunk-based kNN-MT uses strategies to incorporate retrieved chunks into the generation process.
Chunk-based kNN-MT uses strategies to select the steps for searching neighbors in the datastore.
Chunk-based kNN-MT achieves significant speed-ups with a small loss in translation quality in static and on-the-fly domain adaptation.
A zero-shot learning paradigm is format agnostic and applies to text classification, commonsense reasoning, coreference resolution, and sentiment analysis.
A zero-shot learning paradigm converts language tasks into multiple-choice problems.
A zero-shot learning paradigm achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple natural language understanding benchmarks.
A zero-shot learning paradigm produces models with fewer parameters than existing state-of-the-art models.
SPICED is the first paraphrase dataset of scientific findings annotated for degree of information change.
SPICED contains pairs of scientific findings extracted from news stories, social media discussions, and original papers.
SPICED is a challenging dataset for identifying information change in scientific paraphrases.
Models trained on SPICED improve downstream performance on evidence retrieval for fact checking of real-world scientific claims.
Models trained on SPICED reveal large-scale trends in how faithfully people and organizations communicate new scientific findings.
Different previously proposed notions of human label variation are reconciled.
A repository provides access to publicly available datasets with unaggregated labels.
Human label variation is synthesized across data, modeling, and evaluation stages of the machine learning pipeline.
Lookahead heuristics, inspired by the A* search algorithm, estimate future user feedback after using particular support strategies to select strategies that lead to the best long-term effects.
MultiESC captures users’ subtle emotional expressions and understands their emotion causes.
MultiESC outperforms competitive baselines in dialogue generation and strategy planning.
DiDE distills knowledge from a fusion-encoder teacher model into a dual-encoder student model for vision-language understanding tasks.
DiDE aligns the cross-modal attention distributions of the dual-encoder student model with those of the fusion-encoder teacher model.
DiDE achieves performance comparable to fusion-encoder models with significantly faster inference.
Cross-modal attention distillation is crucial for DiDE's performance on vision-language understanding tasks.
Referee trains sentence summarization models without gold summaries.
Referee allows direct control of compression ratio in sentence summarization.
Referee purifies distilled examples using length, fidelity, and Information Bottleneck filters.
Referee uses iterative distillation where student models become teacher models in subsequent iterations.
Referee produces summarization models with sharper controllability of compression ratios without compromising quality.
ScienceWorld tests agents' scientific reasoning abilities in an interactive text environment at the level of an elementary school science curriculum.
Current models cannot reason about or explain learned science concepts in novel contexts.
Agents trained interactively in a grounded environment outperform agents trained statically on expert demonstrations for scientific reasoning.
AELGCN selectively attends to relevant structured information from dependency trees for named entity recognition.
AELGCN updates node representations using edge labels and edge representations using node information in dependency trees.
BiLSTM-AELGCN-CRF integrates AELGCN with BiLSTM-CRF for named entity recognition.
BiLSTM-AELGCN-CRF better exploits structured information from dependency trees to recognize long entities.
The metric distillation rule distills knowledge from a generation metric to a ranker.
Knowledge from a distilled ranker is transferred to a retriever.
A generation model using the distilled ranker achieves state-of-the-art performance on the CommonGen benchmark.
A generation model using the distilled retriever surpasses previous state-of-the-art performance on the CommonGen benchmark.
STGN mitigates the effect of inherent label noise by introducing tailor-made benign noise for each sample.
STGN precisely and stably discriminates correct samples from incorrect samples using multiple principles and applies different intensities of perturbation to them.
STGN has properties beneficial for model generalization.
STGN boosts existing robust training methods.
Prefix-merging is a prefix-based pretraining strategy for few-shot query-focused summarization.
Prefix-merging integrates task knowledge from text summarization and question answering into a merged prefix for query-focused summarization.
Prefix-merging outperforms fine-tuning with few trainable parameters for query-focused summarization.
Different prefix designs influence the performance of prefix-merging for query-focused summarization.
A visualized explanation illustrates how prefix-merging works for query-focused summarization.
Peer-Contrastive Learning (PCL) constructs diverse contrastive positives and negatives at the group level for unsupervised sentence embeddings.
Peer-Contrastive Learning (PCL) performs peer-positive contrast and peer-network cooperation to provide an inherent anti-bias ability when learning from diverse augmentations.
Peer-Contrastive Learning (PCL) outperforms existing methods for unsupervised sentence embeddings on semantic textual similarity benchmarks.
MetaTKG regards Temporal Knowledge Graph prediction as many temporal meta-tasks.
MetaTKG uses a Temporal Meta-learner to learn evolutionary meta-knowledge from temporal meta-tasks.
MetaTKG includes a Gating Integration module that adaptively establishes temporal correlations between meta-tasks.
SUMC-Solver unifies the diverse structures of mathematical expressions in math word problems using an M-tree.
M-tree codes store the path information from the root to leaf nodes and the leaf node information for mathematical expressions.
A Sequence-to-Code model generates M-tree codes for mathematical expressions from math word problem inputs.
SUMC-Solver performs better than state-of-the-art models under low-resource conditions for math word problem solving.
Curriculum-based prompt learning gradually increases the degree of prompt perturbation for abstractive dialogue summarization.
Self-training incorporates unlabeled dialogue to reduce dependency on labeled data in abstractive dialogue summarization.
Curriculum-based prompt learning with self-training achieves state-of-the-art results on the AMI and ICSI dialogue summarization datasets.
Human evaluations show superior summary generation quality for curriculum-based prompt learning with self-training.
FEDEVOCAB protects the privacy of vocabularies in federated learning for NLP tasks.
FEDEVOCAB keeps the local embedding layer on the participant's device and detaches local embedding parameters from federated aggregation.
FEDEVOCAB uses an adaptive updating technique to improve local model performance in cross-device federated learning with private vocabulary mappings.
FEDEVOCAB achieves model performance comparable to existing methods.
FEDEVOCAB provides better privacy-preserving capacity than existing methods.
Some template orders for aspect sentiment quad prediction lead to better generative model performance.
Template-order data augmentation for aspect sentiment quad prediction uses multiple template orders selected by minimal entropy.
Template-order data augmentation improves aspect sentiment quad prediction in low-resource settings.
A hardness-guided domain adaptation framework leverages domain hardness information to improve model adaptability for biomedical named entity recognition in low-resource scenarios.
A hardness-guided domain adaptation framework outperforms the MetaNER model for biomedical named entity recognition in low-resource scenarios.
Parameter-efficient tuning produces a classification head that yields stable performance gains on GLUE and SuperGLUE tasks.
Conditional Autoencoder (ConAE) compresses high-dimensional embeddings for dense retrieval.
Conditional Autoencoder (ConAE) achieves ranking performance comparable to the teacher model after compressing embeddings for dense retrieval.
Conditional Autoencoder (ConAE) reduces index storage and retrieval latency in dense retrieval systems.
Conditional Autoencoder (ConAE) alleviates redundancy in embeddings for dense retrieval using only one linear layer.
TRIME uses a training objective that directly takes in-batch examples as accessible memory.
TRIME includes methods for memory construction and data batching to adapt to local, long-term, and external memory at testing time.
TRIME reduces perplexity in language modeling by leveraging a large memory set from the training corpus.
TRIME adds negligible computational overhead compared to standard language model training.
TRIME is compatible with different neural architectures.
CRAB evaluates causal understanding of events in real-world narratives.
CRAB contains fine-grained, contextual causality annotations for pairs of real-world events describing newsworthy event timelines.
Most large language models perform poorly on causal reasoning for real-world event narratives.
Large language models perform worse on causal reasoning for events derived from complex causal structures than for events derived from simple linear causal chains.
ICE formulates event causality extraction as a template-based conditional generation problem.
ICE captures implicit intra- and inter-event interactions by incorporating ground truth event types and arguments.
ICE uses a knowledge distillation mechanism to handle the unavailability of ground truth event types and arguments during testing.
CEOT strengthens semantic interactions of cause-effect event types and arguments through event-level alignment.
MetricEval formalizes the source of measurement error in NLG evaluation metrics.
MetricEval offers statistical tools for evaluating NLG evaluation metrics based on empirical data.
MetricEval enables quantification of the uncertainty of NLG evaluation metrics for better result interpretation.
Human evaluation for summarization has a conflated validity structure.
LLM-based metrics for summarization have reliability issues.
GEMFormer collects the most certain global elements from the entire document into a global memory and then combines the global memory with local context for multi-hop question answering.
Memory-augmented input with the most certain global elements improves multi-hop question answering performance.
The global explicit memory contains information from supporting facts required for the correct answer in multi-hop question answering.
Almost all existing models, including large language models, capture semantic correlations within utterance embeddings but fail to determine specific causal relationships in conversation.
Incorporating i.i.d. noise terms constructs a structural causal model for causal discrimination in conversation.
Cogn frameworks handle unstructured conversation data.
An autoencoder architecture regards unobservable noise as learnable implicit causes.
A synthetic dataset for the Affective Reasoning in Conversation task includes i.i.d. noise.
Query-probe disagreement in language models consists of three classes: confabulation, deception, and heterogeneity.
The superiority of probing over querying for truthfulness evaluation is due to better calibration on uncertain answers rather than higher accuracy on high-confidence answers in many cases.
In some cases, queries and probes for truthfulness evaluation perform better on different subsets of inputs.
When queries and probes for truthfulness evaluation perform better on different input subsets, ensembling them improves accuracy.
CorefPrompt transforms event coreference resolution into a cloze-style masked language model task.
CorefPrompt enables simultaneous event modeling and coreference discrimination within a single template with a fully shared context.
CorefPrompt introduces event-type compatibility and argument compatibility as auxiliary prompt tasks to demonstrate the reasoning process of event coreference resolution.
Selective labeling for document extraction models simplifies the labeling task by requiring only 'yes/no' labels for candidate extractions predicted by a model trained on partially labeled documents.
Selective labeling for document extraction models uses a custom active learning strategy to select the model's most uncertain predictions for labeling.
Selective labeling for document extraction models reduces the cost of acquiring labeled data without significant loss in accuracy.
Systematic word meta-sense extension (SWORME) tests language models' ability to extend word meaning to denote new meta-senses that bear regular semantic relations with existing senses.
Language models prefer incremental lexical semantic change toward conceptually similar meta-senses such as logical metonymy.
Language models are worse at predicting highly non-literal meaning extensions such as metaphors.
An analogy-based method of word meaning extension improves language model systematicity in making both gradual and radical types of meta-sense extension.
A pseudo-labeling framework for unsupervised Chinese word segmentation integrates the segmentation signal from the unsupervised segmental language model to the pre-trained BERT classifier.

A pseudo-labeling framework integrating the segmentation signal from the unsupervised segmental language model to the pre-trained BERT classifier achieves state-of-the-art performance on eight unsupervised Chinese word segmentation tasks and reduces training time compared to previous approaches.
A manually corrected syllogistic reasoning dataset for legal judgment analysis covers multiple criminal elements, charges, and legal articles.
A set of large language models serves as benchmarks for legal judgment analysis.
Large language models are analyzed in depth regarding their capacity for legal judgment analysis.
NAIL captures most of the performance gains of a Transformer cross-attention model in document reranking using a lexicalized scoring function.
NAIL combined with a BM25 retriever matches the quality of a state-of-the-art dual encoder retriever in document retrieval without requiring an accelerator for query encoding.
NAIL is compatible with encoder-decoder and decoder-only large language models including T5, GPT-3, and PaLM.
NAIL constructs document representations without requiring neural processing of queries.
A framework for testing category-level perceptual grounding in multi-modal language models uses separate neural networks to generate and interpret descriptions of visual categories.
Communicative success, measured by zero-shot classification performance of an interpretation model, is an indicator of perceptual grounding.
Communicative success exposes performance issues in generation models that are not captured by traditional intrinsic NLG evaluation metrics.
Performance issues in generation models exposed by communicative success stem from a failure to ground language in vision at the category level.
Re3Dial constructs billion-scale long-turn dialogues by reorganizing short-turn dialogues.
Re3Dial uses a session retriever trained with contrastive learning to capture semantic and discourse relations in multi-turn dialogues.
Re3Dial employs a diversity sampling strategy to penalize repetitive or generic sessions.
Re3Dial improves dialogue models' ability to utilize long-range context and generate more sensible and informative responses.
Re3Dial constructs a Chinese dialogue corpus with increased average turn length.
Reader handles policy learning that requires reasoning over language-described rules and goals under environmental uncertainty.
Reader generalizes better to test episodes and requires fewer samples than model-free state-of-the-art agents on eight variants of the Read To Fight Monsters environment.
Reader produces inspectable agent plans.
CodeT5+ is an open family of encoder-decoder large language models for code that supports flexible combination of component modules for a wide range of code tasks.
CodeT5+ employs a mixture of pretraining objectives including span denoising, contrastive learning, text-code matching, and causal language modeling on unimodal and bimodal multilingual code corpora.
CodeT5+ is initialized with frozen off-the-shelf large language models without training from scratch for efficient scaling.
CodeT5+ is instruction-tuned to align with natural language instructions.
CodeT5+ achieves state-of-the-art performance on various code-related tasks.
TTE depth ranks texts in a corpus by centrality in transformer-based text embedding space.
The rank sum test determines significant differences between two corpora in transformer-based text embedding distributions.
Using TTE depth for in-context learning prompt selection improves performance over statistical baseline approaches in text classification tasks.
Synthetic data augmentation processes cause a distributional shift away from human-generated text in transformer-based text embedding distributions.
MediaHG balances content and contextual features in social media headline generation.
MediaHG uses a sample module to generate headline candidates for various document views.
MediaHG uses contrastive learning and auxiliary multi-task to choose the best domain-suitable headline based on disentangled budgets.
MediaHG uses separated processing to enable flexible adaptation for headline generation tasks with special domain features.
MediaHG improves headline generation for social media platforms.
Existing grammatical error correction quality estimation models are not good enough at differentiating good corrections from bad ones.
GRECO better estimates the quality of corrected sentences.
Model-agnostic, model-agnostic with voting bias, and model-dependent methods utilize quality estimation models to combine outputs from multiple grammatical error correction systems.
The combined grammatical error correction system outperforms the state of the art on multiple test sets.
Quantifying repetition within a set of sampled model outputs is the most reliable calibration approach for ambiguous question answering.
Quantifying repetition within a set of sampled model outputs remains the most reliable calibration approach across different types of uncertainty, model scales, and instruction tuning conditions.
Sampling-based confidence scores yield more dramatic improvements on ambiguous questions than on relatively unambiguous questions.
Simple recurrent neural networks are equivalent to a subclass of probabilistic finite-state automata.
A recurrent neural network requires Ω(N |Σ|) neurons to represent an arbitrary deterministic finite-state language model with N states over an alphabet Σ.
CoAnnotating allocates annotation work between humans and Large Language Models for unstructured texts at scale.
CoAnnotating uses uncertainty to estimate Large Language Models' annotation capability.
CoAnnotating achieves higher annotation performance than random allocation.
Length vulnerability in contrastive learning-based models is a significant and overlooked research gap.
Elongating a document intensifies the high intra-document similarity in contrastive learning.
Isotropy in contrastive learning is highly dependent on the length range of text in training.
LA(SER)3 is a length-agnostic self-reference framework for semantically robust sentence representation learning.
LA(SER)3 achieves state-of-the-art unsupervised performance on information retrieval benchmarks.
SelfCheckGPT detects sentence-level hallucinations in responses of black-box generative large language models without an external database.
