SelfCheckGPT ranks passages by factuality in responses of black-box generative large language models without an external database.
SelfCheckGPT uses the divergence of stochastically sampled responses to detect hallucinations in black-box generative large language model responses.
SelfCheckGPT outperforms grey-box methods in sentence-level hallucination detection for black-box generative large language models.
SelfCheckGPT outperforms grey-box methods in passage-level factuality assessment for black-box generative large language models.
CONNER systematically and automatically evaluates knowledge generated by large language models from six perspectives: Factuality, Relevance, Coherence, Informativeness, Helpfulness, and Validity.
Lower factuality of knowledge generated by large language models does not significantly hinder downstream tasks.
Relevance and coherence of knowledge generated by large language models are more important than small factual mistakes for downstream tasks.
Prompt Engineering and Knowledge Selection strategies improve knowledge-intensive tasks.
Tuning-free Rule Accumulation guides large language models to improve performance by learning from previous mistakes.
Tuning-free Rule Accumulation accumulates rules from incorrect cases as data arrives sequentially.
Tuning-free Rule Accumulation utilizes accumulated rules to avoid similar mistakes on subsequent inputs.
Tuning-free Rule Accumulation accumulates rules that remain independent of primary prompts and complement prompt design strategies.
Formal grammars can describe the output space for a wide range of structured NLP tasks.
Grammar-constrained decoding serves as a unified framework for structured NLP tasks.
Input-dependent grammars allow the grammar to depend on the input, enabling the generation of different output structures for different inputs.
Grammar-constrained language models outperform unconstrained language models and task-specific finetuned models on information extraction, entity disambiguation, and constituency parsing.
A dataset of five types of visual illusions enables the examination of visual illusions in Vision-Language Models.
Four tasks enable the examination of visual illusions in Vision-Language Models.
Vision-Language Models generally exhibit low alignment with human perception of visual illusions.
Larger Vision-Language Models exhibit higher alignment with human perception of visual illusions.
Larger Vision-Language Models are more susceptible to visual illusions.

CP-BCS generates complete natural language summaries for stripped binary functions by learning execution behavior and logic semantics from a bidirectional instruction-level control flow graph and pseudo code incorporating expert knowledge.
CP-BCS improves the efficiency of reverse engineering.
Independent Component Analysis extracts independent semantic components from pre-trained model embeddings by leveraging anisotropic information remaining after Principal Component Analysis whitening.
Each embedding of words or images is expressed as a composition of a few intrinsic interpretable axes.
Intrinsic interpretable semantic axes remain consistent across languages, algorithms, and modalities.
Word Embedding Association Test is applied to 24 languages for language model bias analysis.
Word Embedding Association Test data is enhanced with culturally relevant information for each language.
Language model bias is analyzed for new dimensions including toxicity and ableism.
Regional bias in language models is analyzed for six Indian languages.
An extensive comparison of embedding methods demonstrates that social biases and new bias dimensions in language models are significant.
Language model APIs overcharge users and provide poorer results for many supported languages.
Speakers of languages that are overcharged by language model APIs tend to come from regions where the APIs are less affordable.

A new method for scaling multilingual vocabularies de-emphasizes token sharing between languages with little lexical overlap and assigns vocabulary capacity to achieve sufficient coverage for each language.
XLM-V's vocabulary produces tokenizations that are more semantically meaningful and shorter than XLM-R's.
XLM-V is a multilingual language model with a one million token vocabulary.
XLM-V outperforms XLM-R on natural language inference, question answering, and named entity recognition tasks.
MADNet maximizes addressee deduction expectation in heterogeneous graph neural networks for multi-party conversation generation.
Four additional types of latent edges complete a fully-connected conversation graph for multi-party conversations with missing addressee labels.
An Expectation-Maximization-based method iteratively generates silver addressee labels and optimizes the quality of generated responses for multi-party conversation generation.
MADNet outperforms baseline models for multi-party conversation generation when addressee labels are partially missing.
SynCSE-partial produces positive and negative annotations for unlabeled sentences.
SynCSE-scratch generates sentences and their annotations from scratch.
SynCSE-scratch is the first contrastive learning method to learn sentence embeddings from scratch without manually collected data samples.
SynCSE-partial and SynCSE-scratch outperform unsupervised baselines on sentence similarity and reranking tasks.
SynCSE-partial achieves performance comparable to supervised models on sentence similarity and reranking tasks.
Noise-Aware Semantic Adjustment (NSA) extracts clear semantics from noisy speech for speech-referring video object segmentation.
Semantic Jitter Suppression (SJS) enables speech-referring video object segmentation models to tolerate noisy queries.
Speech-referring video object segmentation with semantic space alignment between speech and text outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on the AVOS benchmarks.
Verbalizing long videos generates natural language descriptions that are used for zero-shot video understanding.
Verbalizing long videos for zero-shot video understanding outperforms supervised baselines.
The persuasion strategy identification dataset is the first benchmark for story understanding.
Auxiliary tasks for grammatical error correction exploit the alignment between original and corrected sentences.
The order of datasets and individual instances during training affects grammatical error correction performance.
State-of-the-art grammatical error correction performance is achieved with a model that has fewer parameters than T5-XXL.

EVA is a multimodal machine translation dataset for Japanese-English and Chinese-English subtitles from movies and TV episodes that includes a video-helpful evaluation set where subtitles are ambiguous and videos are guaranteed to be helpful for disambiguation.
SAFA is a multimodal machine translation model that uses Frame attention loss and Ambiguity augmentation to fully utilize videos for disambiguation of ambiguous subtitles.
ByteSized32 is a corpus of reasoning-focused text games expressed in Python code.
GPT-4 can use ByteSized32 games as templates for single-shot in-context learning to produce runnable games on unseen topics.
Self-reflection on program errors increases the runnability of generated text games.
A suite of automated metrics assesses game fidelity, technical validity, adherence to task specifications, and winnability.
The automated metrics for game evaluation show high agreement with expert human ratings.
Each utterance emotion dynamics metric studied varies by self-disclosed mental health diagnosis.
Average valence in tweets is higher in the control group than in users with ADHD, MDD, and PTSD.
Valence variability in tweets is lower in the control group than in users with ADHD, depression, bipolar disorder, MDD, PTSD, and OCD but not PPD.
Rise and recovery rates of valence in tweets differ significantly from the control group.
New quantitative measures of absolute and relative equivalence in multilingual task-oriented dialogue system performance capture disparities across languages and within individual languages.
Performance disparities in multilingual task-oriented dialogue systems depend on the nature of the task, the pretrained language model, the target language, and the amount of annotated data.
Multilingual task-oriented dialogue systems exhibit adaptation and intrinsic biases, causing diminished task performance for Arabic and Turkish even when trained on fully parallel data.
Zero-shot drug-drug interaction prediction handles drug interactions involving new drugs.
TextDDI uses a language model-based predictor for drug-drug interaction prediction.
TextDDI uses a reinforcement learning-based information selector that selects concise and pertinent text for drug-drug interaction prediction.
TextDDI selects semantically relevant text for drug-drug interaction prediction.
CoMPosT characterizes LLM simulations using four dimensions: Context, Model, Persona, and Topic.
Caricature in LLM simulations is defined by two criteria: individuation and exaggeration.
GPT-4 simulations of political groups, marginalized groups, and general and uncontroversial topics are highly susceptible to caricature.
ChatGPT-created paraphrases for intent classification are more diverse than crowdsourced paraphrases.
Intent classification models trained on ChatGPT-created paraphrases are at least as robust as models trained on crowdsourced paraphrases.
A reference-free, uncertainty-based hallucination detection method for LLMs focuses on the most informative and important keywords in the given text.
A reference-free, uncertainty-based hallucination detection method for LLMs focuses on unreliable tokens in historical context that may lead to cascading hallucinations.
A reference-free, uncertainty-based hallucination detection method for LLMs focuses on token properties including token type and token frequency.
A reference-free, uncertainty-based hallucination detection method for LLMs achieves state-of-the-art performance in hallucination detection.
A reference-free, uncertainty-based hallucination detection method for LLMs eliminates the need for external knowledge and multiple response samples.
Conceptual structure in human cognition is robust to differences in culture, language, and method of estimation.
Conceptual structure estimates from large language models cohere less across different tasks than conceptual structure estimates from humans.
InterFair uses sensitive information fairly with natural language feedback for debiasing.
InterFair decreases bias in explanations while maintaining prediction accuracy via user interaction with test examples.
InterFair disentangles bias and predictive information from the input via human feedback, leading to simultaneous bias mitigation and improved task performance.
LDST is an LLM-driven Dialogue State Tracking framework based on smaller, open-source foundation models.
LDST uses a novel domain-slot instruction tuning method for Dialogue State Tracking.
LDST achieves performance comparable to ChatGPT in Dialogue State Tracking.
LDST outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods in zero-shot and few-shot Dialogue State Tracking.
Fine-tuning fact-checking models for dialogue results in poor performance on stand-alone fact-checking.
Retrieval adaptation enables fact-checking models trained on stand-alone claims to accurately process dialogue claims.
Transforming conversational inputs enables fact-checking models trained on stand-alone claims to accurately process dialogue claims.

A fact-checking model using retrieval adaptation and transforming conversational inputs is competitive with state-of-the-art dialogue fact-checking models while maintaining performance on stand-alone claims.
The Somali Information Retrieval Corpus is an annotated collection of Somali documents from online news sites and poetry books.
A Somali language information retrieval system uses pseudo-relevance feedback query expansion.
Plans for embodied task completion are more interpretable than environment actions.
Plans for embodied task completion are obtainable via large language model prompting.
Multimodal transformer models outperform language-only models for embodied plan prediction but fall short of oracle plans.
A method synthetically generates embodied dialogues for training plan prediction.
Using synthetic embodied dialogue data, multimodal transformer models outperform language-only models trained on human-human data in zero-shot embodied plan prediction.
For scaling generative retrieval to millions of passages, using synthetic queries as document representations during indexing is centrally important.
For scaling generative retrieval to millions of passages, existing architecture modifications are ineffective when accounting for compute cost.
For scaling generative retrieval to millions of passages, naively scaling model parameters has limits for retrieval performance.
Generative retrieval is competitive with state-of-the-art dual encoders on small corpora.
Scaling generative retrieval to millions of passages remains an unsolved challenge.
A systematic method for testing large language model robustness in multi-hop reasoning tasks employs domain-agnostic perturbations.
Large language models exhibit higher sensitivity to synonym replacement perturbations.
Increasing the proportion of perturbed exemplars in prompts improves the robustness of few-shot prompting methods for large language models.
GPT-4 outperforms prior methods for evaluating information veracity in multiple settings and languages.
GPT-4 and RoBERTa-large exhibit differences in failure modes for evaluating information veracity.
Techniques for handling uncertainty in evaluating information veracity detect impossible examples.
LIAR-New is a dataset of paired English and French misinformation data with Possibility labels indicating if there is sufficient context for veracity evaluation.
The Fine-to-Coarse mapping matrix explicitly leverages the hierarchical relationship where fine-grained entity types are subcategories of coarse-grained entity types.
An inconsistency filtering method removes coarse-grained entities that are inconsistent with fine-grained entity types to avoid performance degradation.
A fine-grained NER approach using a Fine-to-Coarse mapping matrix and inconsistency filtering outperforms K-shot learning and supervised learning methods in low-resource settings.
A dataset in 5 languages assesses whether answers in cross-lingual question answering are attributable to retrieved passages.
A substantial portion of answers in cross-lingual question answering systems is not attributable to retrieved passages even when the system can attend to the retrieved text.
Natural Language Inference models detect whether answers are attributable to retrieved passages in cross-lingual question answering.
PaLM 2 fine-tuned on a small amount of attribution data detects whether answers are attributable to retrieved passages in cross-lingual question answering.
A dataset of e-textbooks in math, science, social science, and business domains with an associated image bank is created.
A text-image matching task for retrieving and assigning web images to textbooks is framed as a matching optimization problem.
Images automatically retrieved and assigned to textbooks are rated nearly as high as original textbook images in crowd-sourced evaluations.
The formulation of the text-image matching optimization problem influences the quality of image assignments to textbooks.
DaP makes read/write decisions for any translation model based on the potential divergence in translation distributions from future information.
DaP extends a frozen wait-k model with lightweight parameters.
DaP is memory and computation efficient.
DaP provides an improved trade-off between translation accuracy and latency and outperforms strong baselines.
IC3 generates a single caption that captures high-level details from several annotator viewpoints.
IC3 captions are rated as helpful as or better than state-of-the-art models by humans in the majority of cases.
IC3 captions outperform single human-generated reference captions in automated recall systems.
TrojanSQL is a backdoor-based SQL injection framework for text-to-SQL systems.
TrojanSQL misleads state-of-the-art text-to-SQL parsers into producing SQL statements that invalidate user queries or compromise sensitive database information.
TrojanSQL includes boolean-based injection and union-based injection attacks that use different triggers to compromise text-to-SQL parsers.
Medium-sized fine-tuned text-to-SQL models and LLM-based text-to-SQL parsers that use prompting techniques are vulnerable to TrojanSQL attacks.
Outlier detection identifies text about people with demographic attributes distant from the norm in toxicity detection datasets.
Model performance is worse for demographic outliers than for non-outliers across toxicity types.
Model performance is worse for text outliers than for non-outliers.
Text and demographic outliers are particularly susceptible to errors in classifying severe toxicity and identity attacks.
Outlier-based identification of harmed populations surfaces greater harms for a larger, more intersectional group than traditional demographic breakdowns.
AutoPM learns a preference model for LLMs via automatic preference data generation.
In-Breadth Data Generation elicits pairwise preference data from LLMs following the helpful-honest-harmless (HHH) criteria.
In-Depth Data Generation enriches a preference dataset with responses spanning a wide quality range.
AutoPM enables LLMs to learn human preferences and align with human values using HHH-guided preference data.
AutoPM provides a general and scalable way to improve LLM performance.
Adversarial passages for dense retrieval systems are generated by perturbing discrete tokens to maximize similarity with training queries.
Adversarial passages cause dense retrieval systems to retrieve them for queries not used to generate the adversarial passages.
Adversarial passages generalize directly to out-of-domain queries and corpora.
All state-of-the-art dense retrievers can be successfully attacked by injecting a small number of adversarial passages.

Gradient-based gradual pruning identifies an optimal sub-network for each language pair in multilingual neural machine translation by using gradient-based information as the pruning criterion and gradually increasing the pruning ratio according to a schedule.
Gradient-based gradual pruning enables partial parameter sharing across language pairs to reduce parameter interference while preserving unique parameters for language-specific information.
Gradient-based gradual pruning improves multilingual neural machine translation performance on IWSLT and WMT datasets.
DMath offers diversity in problem types and lexical usage patterns.
DMath problems are available in English and Korean.
DMath problems include expression trees and Python code as intermediate solutions.
GPT-4 does not achieve perfect accuracy on DMath.
DREAM combines sets of Argument Mining systems using ensemble methods for the four tasks in the Argument Mining pipeline.
DREAM uses recombination of different Argument Mining elements throughout the pipeline.
DREAM achieves higher Argument Mining accuracy than previous best single systems.
Human and machine moderators extensively disagree on offensive speech in social web political discourse.
Human and large-language-model classifiers cannot predict other human raters' responses to offensive speech based on political leanings.
A noise audit combines machine and human responses to measure disagreement in offensive speech moderation.
The vicarious offense dataset captures vicarious offense in offensive speech moderation.
Moderation outcomes vary widely across different machine moderators.
Political leanings combined with sensitive issues affect both first-person and vicarious offense in offensive speech moderation.
Clustering argument sentences, generating abstractive cluster labels, and classifying labels into argumentation frames generates two-level indicative summaries for long discussions.
Two-level indicative summaries for long discussions serve as a convenient navigation tool to explore long discussions.
Discussion Explorer is a visual interface for exploring long discussions.
The dual-channel span generation method generates span candidates for aspect sentiment triplet extraction using syntactic relations and part-of-speech characteristics of aspect and opinion terms.
The dual-channel span generation method learns feature representations for aspect sentiment triplet extraction from syntactic and part-of-speech correlation among terms.
The dual-channel span generation method achieves superior performance on ASTE, ATE, and OTE tasks.
Automatic two-stage metrics for reference-based summarization evaluation extract basic information units from one text sequence and check the extracted units in another sequence.
Automatic two-stage metrics for reference-based summarization evaluation provide high interpretability at both the fine-grained unit level and summary level.
Automatic one-stage metrics for reference-based summarization evaluation achieve a balance between efficiency and interpretability.
NEG-1500-SIMP is a negation dataset created by extending existing benchmarks using GPT-3.
ROLE-1500 is a role reversal dataset created by extending existing benchmarks using GPT-3.
NEG-1500-SIMP-TEMP is a negation dataset created by template-based generation.
Model performance decreases on larger negation and role reversal datasets compared to smaller benchmarks.
BERT and ALBERT exhibit high negation sensitivity, indicating that prior studies on negation with small test sets may have produced skewed findings.
GPT-3 has low accuracy on role reversal examples it generated.
A weight-based, model-agnostic and corpus-agnostic approach maps individual neurons in decoder-only Transformer Language Models to multiple coherent topics.
Disentangling coherent topics from GPT-2 models is feasible using the Wikipedia corpus.
A mathematical formalism for surface form competition quantifies and bounds the impact of surface form competition.
Including given answer choices in the prompt and using in-context learning with one example eliminates the impact of surface form competition in most cases.
Normalization and prompting methods for reducing surface form competition can be ineffective or detrimental to task performance for some language models.
XoT selects the most suitable prompting method for each question.
XoT checks the validity of generated answers using external executors.
XoT dynamically switches among different prompting methods based on feedback from external executors.
XoT is orthogonal to improvements on single reasoning methods.
XoT generalizes to logical reasoning tasks.
Free-text human feedback in commonly used dialog datasets exhibits error types, user response types, and relations between them.
Taxonomies for free-text human feedback in dialogs categorize feedback by error types and user response types.
AutoCompressors compress long contexts into summary vectors that serve as soft prompts for language models.
Summary vectors are trained with an unsupervised objective that processes long documents in segments and uses summary vectors from previous segments for language modeling.
AutoCompressors improve language modeling perplexity by utilizing long contexts.
Summary vectors increase task accuracy and reduce inference costs in in-context learning relative to plain-text demonstrations.

Contrastive learning with adversarial examples (ASR outputs paired with human transcripts) optimizes machine translation network parameters to bring representations of noisy inputs closer to clean inputs in the semantic space.
Curriculum learning stabilizes machine translation training by alternating machine translation log-likelihood loss and contrastive losses.
Contrastive learning with adversarial examples and curriculum learning improves speech translation quality for noisy ASR outputs while maintaining translation quality on clean text.
Verbalized confidences emitted as output tokens are better-calibrated than the conditional probabilities of RLHF-LMs.
Qualitative causal structure is represented as a heterogeneous graph for scientific claim verification.
An attention-based graph neural network model enables causal reasoning across causally-potent factors for scientific claim verification.
A model using only qualitative causal structure achieves comparable performance to PLM-based models for scientific claim verification.
A model incorporating semantic features outperforms state-of-the-art approaches for scientific claim verification.
Cross-lingual alignment prompting aligns representations across different languages.
Task-specific solver prompting generates the final chain of thoughts and results for the reasoning task.
Cross-lingual self-consistent prompting (CLSP) ensembles different reasoning paths across languages.
Cross-lingual prompting (CLP) and cross-lingual self-consistent prompting (CLSP) achieve state-of-the-art performance in zero-shot chain-of-thought reasoning across languages.
Paraphrase-based prefix-style prompts synthesize authentic LLM-generated content using GPT-3.5-turbo.
Perturbation-based prefix-style prompts synthesize deceptive LLM-generated content using GPT-3.5-turbo.
Zero-shot in-context semantic reasoning with cloze-style prompts using GPT-3.5-turbo discerns genuine from deceptive posts and news articles.
Disinformation detection with GPT-3.5-turbo in zero-shot setting maintains performance on out-of-distribution datasets.
An explicit schema representation captures habitual knowledge about events that an agent often participates in, including typical goals, sub-events, preconditions, and postconditions.
A dialogue generation approach conditions a large language model using retrieved schemas to generate persona-based responses.
A method bootstraps schema creation by generating generic passages from simple facts and inducing schemas from the generated passages.
CiteBench unifies multiple diverse datasets for citation text generation.
CiteBench enables standardized evaluation of citation text generation models across task designs and domains.
Shared embeddings between the classifier and the generative model enable the generative model to leverage gradients from the classifier for generating perturbed tokens.
Adversarial training that combines adversarial regularization and perturbed token detection provides token-level supervision and improves sample utilization efficiency.
Generative adversarial training with perturbed token detection enhances model robustness on the AdvGLUE benchmark.
SMoP uses short soft prompts to achieve efficient training and inference while maintaining the performance gains of longer soft prompts.
SMoP employs a gating mechanism to train multiple short soft prompts specialized for different subsets of the data.
SMoP outperforms baseline methods in prompt tuning.
HaluEval evaluates large language models in recognizing hallucinations using generated and human-annotated samples.
A ChatGPT-based two-step framework (sampling-then-filtering) generates hallucinated samples.
ChatGPT generates hallucinated content in specific topics by fabricating unverifiable information.
Existing large language models face challenges in recognizing hallucinations.
Providing external knowledge or adding reasoning steps improves hallucination recognition in large language models.
The Chinese Essay Discourse Coherence Corpus (CEDCC) is a multi-task dataset for assessing discourse coherence in Chinese essays.
CEDCC integrates coherence grading, topical continuity, and discourse relations for assessing discourse coherence in Chinese essays.
Baselines for coherence grading, topical continuity, and discourse relations in Chinese essays are established.
Coherence impacts discourse relation recognition and automated essay scoring in Chinese essays.
MAUD is an expert-annotated reading comprehension dataset for merger agreement understanding based on the American Bar Association’s 2021 Public Target Deal Points Study.
MAUD is the only expert-annotated merger agreement dataset.
MAUD serves as a benchmark for the legal profession and the NLP community.
Transformer-based models perform above random on most merger agreement understanding questions on MAUD.
Transformer-based models have room for improvement on a large subset of merger agreement understanding questions on MAUD.
Model-based rationales show high agreement between contrastive and non-contrastive explanation settings.
Human annotations show high agreement between contrastive and non-contrastive explanation settings.
Model-based explanations align equally well with human rationales in contrastive and non-contrastive settings.
Humans do not necessarily explain in a contrastive manner.
Human rationale annotations for a subset of the BIOS dataset cover contrastive and non-contrastive explanation settings.
DiVERT learns an interpretable representation of errors behind distractors in math multiple-choice questions.
DiVERT outperforms state-of-the-art approaches for distractor generation in math multiple-choice questions.
DiVERT produces error labels for distractors in math multiple-choice questions that are of comparable quality to human-authored error labels.
A two-stream architecture for mental disease detection learns shared clues of multiple mental diseases while capturing the specificity of each disease.
A two-stream architecture for mental disease detection simultaneously processes text and symptom features to combine both modalities and provide knowledge-based explainability.
A two-stream architecture for mental disease detection improves detection performance for relatively rare mental diseases.
The Vietnamese Multi-Dialect dataset captures the diversity of 63 provincial dialects of Vietnamese.
Baseline models for dialect identification and speech recognition are provided for the Vietnamese Multi-Dialect dataset.
Geographical factors influence Vietnamese dialects.
Current approaches face constraints in multi-dialect speech recognition.
Explainable Personality Recognition reveals the reasoning process as supporting evidence for personality traits.
Chain-of-Personality-Evidence (CoPE) performs reasoning from specific contexts to short-term personality states to long-term personality traits.
PersonalityEvd is an explainable personality recognition dataset constructed from dialogues.
Explainable personality recognition tasks require models to recognize personality state and trait labels along with supporting evidence.
Explainable personality recognition is challenging for large language models.
SUPER evaluates large language models on setting up and executing tasks from research repositories.
SUPER includes 45 end-to-end problems with expert-annotated solutions, 152 sub-problems focusing on specific challenges such as configuring a trainer, and 602 automatically generated problems.
SUPER uses evaluation measures that assess task success and progress with gold solutions or approximations.
State-of-the-art large language models struggle to solve the problems in SUPER.
ASETF transforms continuous adversarial suffix embeddings into coherent and understandable text.
ASETF reduces computation time for generating adversarial suffixes.
ASETF automatically generates multiple adversarial samples.
ASETF achieves a higher attack success rate than existing techniques.
ASETF enhances the textual fluency of adversarial prompts.
ASETF generates transferable adversarial suffixes that successfully attack multiple large language models, including black-box large language models.
Wug InDirect Evidence Test (WIDET) evaluates language models' grammatical knowledge induction from indirect evidence.

Language models do not induce grammatical knowledge from indirect evidence in certain language phenomena after repeated exposure to training instances that are structurally identical to evaluation instances but use different lexical items.
MiniCheck is a small model that checks facts in LLM outputs against grounding documents with GPT-4-level performance.
Synthetic training data for fact-checking is generated by a structured procedure using GPT-4 to create realistic yet challenging instances of factual errors.
Models trained on synthetic fact-checking data check individual facts and recognize information synthesis across sentences.
LLM-AggreFact is a benchmark that unifies datasets for evaluating fact-checking and grounding of LLM generations.
GDTB is an open-access, multi-genre benchmark for English shallow discourse parsing in the PDTB framework based on the UD English GUM corpus.
GDTB is compatible with the PDTB framework.
Cross-domain relation classification exhibits substantial out-of-domain degradation between the Wall Street Journal corpus and GDTB.
Joint training on the Wall Street Journal corpus and GDTB alleviates out-of-domain degradation in cross-domain relation classification.
RepMatch quantifies the similarity between subsets of training instances by comparing the knowledge encoded in models trained on them.
RepMatch enables similarity comparisons across arbitrary subsets of instances, including dataset-to-dataset and instance-to-dataset analyses.
RepMatch identifies subsets of a dataset that yield better model performance than random subsets of the same size.
RepMatch uncovers heuristics underlying the construction of challenge datasets.
VHASR uses vision as hotwords in multimodal speech recognition.
VHASR utilizes a dual-stream architecture that produces text from two streams separately and then combines the outputs.
VHASR outperforms unimodal ASR systems.
VHASR achieves state-of-the-art performance among image-based multimodal ASR systems.
The invocation of intra-repository functions and third-party APIs significantly influences the quality of function annotations generated by Large Language Models.
An annotation method enhances context for code retrieval by incorporating intra-repository function content and third-party API information.
A sorting method addresses multi-level function call relationships in code repositories by integrating with Large Language Models.
The Query4Code dataset is validated for code retrieval through model training and human evaluation.
Cost analysis confirms the scalability of the annotation method for code retrieval.
TempoFormer is the first task-agnostic transformer-based and temporally-aware model for dynamic representation learning.
TempoFormer is jointly trained on inter and intra context dynamics.
TempoFormer introduces a novel temporal variation of rotary positional embeddings.
TempoFormer can be used as the temporal representation foundation of other models or applied to different transformer-based architectures.
TempoFormer achieves state-of-the-art results on three different real-time change detection tasks.
BlockWorld-Repairs is a dataset of multi-modal Third Position Repair sequences in an instruction-following manipulation task with referential ambiguity.
Vision and Language Models underperform compared to humans in processing Third Position Repairs and recovering from miscommunication.
Specialised losses targeting relevant tokens during fine-tuning improve Vision and Language Models' performance and generalisation to new scenarios in processing Third Position Repairs.

The LLM feature-based framework for dialogue constructiveness assessment uses dataset-independent and interpretable linguistic features that are extractable by prompting a large language model and by simple heuristics.
LLM feature-based models for dialogue constructiveness assessment achieve performance at least as good as standard feature-based models and neural models.
LLM feature-based models for dialogue constructiveness assessment learn more robust prediction rules and avoid relying on superficial shortcuts that trouble neural models.
Embedding Space Maps (ESMs) are light-weight neural networks that approximate the effect of fine-tuning a language model.
A study evaluates NLP task transferability and task selection at the largest scale.
Embedding Space Maps (ESMs) reduce execution time and disk space usage in task selection while retaining high selection performance.
IRIS performs black-box jailbreaking by using a single model as both the attacker and target.
IRIS iteratively refines adversarial prompts through self-explanation to ensure that even well-aligned large language models obey adversarial instructions.
IRIS rates and enhances the output given the refined prompt to increase its harmfulness.
IRIS achieves near-perfect jailbreak success rates on multiple large language models with few queries.
IRIS outperforms prior approaches in automatic, black-box, and interpretable jailbreaking while requiring fewer queries.

CGVST identifies task-specific neurons in large language models by concentrating on the most significant tokens during task processing, which eliminates redundant tokens and minimizes interference from non-essential neurons.
CGVST accurately locates task-specific neurons in large language models.
SynthesizRR uses retrieval augmentation to vary the content seeding the large language model during dataset synthesis for classification tasks.
SynthesizRR improves lexical diversity in synthesized classification datasets compared to 32-shot prompting and prior approaches.
SynthesizRR improves semantic diversity in synthesized classification datasets compared to 32-shot prompting and prior approaches.
SynthesizRR improves similarity to human-written text in synthesized classification datasets compared to 32-shot prompting and prior approaches.
SynthesizRR improves distillation performance for classification tasks compared to 32-shot prompting and prior approaches.
PairDistill uses pairwise reranking to make fine-grained distinctions between similarly relevant documents during dense retrieval model training.
PairDistill achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple dense retrieval benchmarks.
RICHES interleaves retrieval with sequence generation in a single decoding pass.
RICHES retrieves documents by directly decoding their contents from the corpus.
RICHES adapts to diverse new tasks via prompting alone.
RICHES works with any Instruction-tuned model without additional training.
GraphBridge bridges local and global perspectives in text-attributed graphs by leveraging contextual textual information to enhance fine-grained understanding.
A graph-aware token reduction module improves efficiency and scalability in text-attributed graph representation learning.
GraphBridge achieves state-of-the-art performance on text-attributed graph representation learning tasks.
LM2 includes a decomposer module that identifies key concepts necessary to solve a problem and generates step-by-step subquestions according to the problem's reasoning requirement.
LM2 includes a solver model that generates solutions to subproblems.
LM2 includes a verifier module that checks the solutions generated by the solver model.
LM2 constructs the reasoning context using subproblems and solutions based on feedback from the verifier.
LM2 trains its models to coordinate using policy learning.
LM2 outperforms existing methods on in-domain and out-of-domain reasoning problems.
An evaluation methodology for reading comprehension tasks uses semantic frame annotation to characterize linguistic complexity.
Two linguistic complexity factors are good predictors of model failure in French reading comprehension, while others are less predictive.
Fine-grained linguistically-motivated automatic evaluation of reading comprehension tasks is possible and helps understand models' abilities to handle specific linguistic characteristics.
Current state-of-the-art models fail to handle some linguistic characteristics in reading comprehension, indicating that handling them requires more than increasing model size.
Removing spurious correlations between protected groups and all image attributes mitigates societal bias in image-text datasets.
Data filtering mitigates inpainting biases in image-text datasets.
Societal bias is reduced without performance compromise in multi-label image classification and image captioning tasks.
Text-based hate speech examples improve the classification accuracy for vision-language hate speech.
Text-based demonstrations outperform vision-language demonstrations in few-shot learning for hate speech detection.
A first-order bit-level CKY algorithm encodes lexicon and syntax in a unified binary representation space.
Grammar induction training under the contrastive hashing framework uses unsupervised learning.
A novel loss function imposes stronger yet balanced alignment signals for grammar induction.
Larger multilingual LLMs exhibit high-quality linear alignments between corresponding concepts in different languages.
Multilingual LLMs exhibit better generalization for languages with similar typology.
Multilingual LLMs exhibit better generalization for abstract concepts.
Prompt-based embeddings exhibit better alignment than word embeddings in multilingual LLMs.
Prompt-based embeddings exhibit less linear projections than word embeddings in multilingual LLMs.
Pre-trained language models exhibit a window of maximal alignment to human cognitive development during training, regardless of model size.
Before the window of maximal alignment, training endows pre-trained language models with the requisite structure for rapid learning from experience.
After the window of maximal alignment, training of pre-trained language models reduces loss but does not increase alignment with human cognition.
LLM-generated topic lists have significant overlap with human-generated topic lists but may miss document-specific topics.
LLM suggestions improve task completion speed but introduce anchoring bias that may reduce the depth and nuance of analysis.
CoBB performs sequence-to-sequence mapping from imperfect reasonings to correct reasonings for question answering in black-box large language models.
CoBB selects representative training pairs by minimizing statistical divergence between the sampled subset and the entire collection using a genetic algorithm.
CoBB trains the adaptation model by contrasting the likelihoods of correct and incorrect reasonings.
CoBB improves reasoning accuracy on question answering benchmarks relative to best-performing adaptation baselines.
MetaGPT formalizes model merging as a multi-task learning framework to minimize the average loss difference between the merged model and each individual task model.
MetaGPT leverages the local linearity of large language models and the orthogonality of task vectors to separate the data term and the scaling coefficients term.
MetaGPT is data-agnostic for merging large language models.
MetaGPT bypasses the heavy search process for merging large language models.
MetaGPT achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple tasks in model merging for large language models.
ToolBH assesses hallucinations in tool-augmented large language models through a multi-level diagnostic process including solvability detection, solution planning, and missing-tool analysis.
ToolBH evaluates hallucinations in tool-augmented large language models across three breadth scenarios: missing necessary tools, potential tools, and limited functionality tools.
On the ToolBH benchmark, larger model parameters do not guarantee better performance in tool-augmented large language models; training data and response strategies are also important factors.
On the ToolBH benchmark, assessing task solvability is the primary reason for model errors in tool-augmented large language models.
On the ToolBH benchmark, open-weight models suffer performance drops with verbose replies in tool-augmented large language models, while proprietary models excel with longer reasoning.
Memorization status is inter-correlated with model size, continuation size, and context size.
Transition dynamics occur between sentences of different memorization scores.
Generating memorized sequences causes a sudden drop in input token frequency, and generating unmemorized sequences causes a sudden increase (boundary effect).
Sentences with different memorization scores form clusters in the embedding space.
Generating memorized and unmemorized sequences results in an inverse boundary effect in the entropy of probability distributions.
The predictability of memorization is related to model size and continuation length.
A Transformer model trained on large language model hidden states can predict unmemorized tokens.
XC-Translate is the first large-scale, manually-created benchmark for machine translation that focuses on text containing culturally-nuanced entity names.
KG-MT integrates information from a multilingual knowledge graph into a neural machine translation model in an end-to-end manner using a dense retrieval mechanism.
Current machine translation systems and large language models struggle to translate texts containing entity names.
KG-MT outperforms state-of-the-art machine translation approaches.
No single programming language consistently delivers optimal performance across all tasks and models in Program of Thoughts.
MultiPoT is a task and model agnostic approach that harnesses strength and diversity of multiple programming languages for Program of Thoughts.
MultiPoT achieves comparable or superior performance to the best monolingual Program of Thoughts approach in almost all tasks across all models.
Structure Guided Prompt converts unstructured text into a graph using large language models.
Structure Guided Prompt instructs large language models to navigate the graph using task-specific strategies to formulate responses.
Structure Guided Prompt enhances the multi-step reasoning capabilities of large language models for a broader spectrum of natural language scenarios.
Retrieved Sequence Augmentation (RSA) enhances protein representation learning without the need for sequence alignment and cumbersome preprocessing.
RSA achieves better performance than MSA Transformer in structural prediction and property prediction tasks.
RSA is significantly faster than MSA Transformer.
RSA demonstrates enhanced transferability for predicting de novo proteins.
MIRAGE is a plug-and-play approach that uses model internals for faithful answer attribution in retrieval-augmented generation applications.
MIRAGE detects context-sensitive answer tokens and pairs them with retrieved documents that contribute to their prediction using saliency methods.
MIRAGE shows high agreement with human answer attribution on a multilingual extractive question answering dataset.
MIRAGE achieves citation quality and efficiency comparable to self-citation in open-ended question answering while allowing finer-grained control of attribution parameters.
MT-Ladder is trained on pseudo-refinement triplets obtained from existing LLMs without additional human cost.
MT-Ladder uses a hierarchical fine-tuning strategy with an easy-to-hard schema during training.
MT-Ladder-2B elevates raw translations to the level of top-tier open-source models.
MT-Ladder-7B enhances translation performance to be on par with GPT-4.
MASIVE is a dataset of Reddit posts in English and Spanish that includes a large number of unique affective states in both languages.
Affective state identification is framed as a masked span prediction task for language generation models.
Smaller finetuned multilingual models outperform larger language models on the affective state identification task when evaluated on region-specific Spanish affective states.
Pretraining on MASIVE improves model performance on existing emotion benchmarks.
Native speaker-written data is essential for good performance on the affective state identification task.
Structural-anchor-based compression compresses spreadsheets.
Inverse index translation compresses spreadsheets.
Data-format-aware aggregation compresses spreadsheets.
SheetEncoder improves performance in spreadsheet table detection compared to vanilla serialization in GPT4's in-context learning setting.
Fine-tuned large language models with SheetEncoder outperform existing models on spreadsheet data.
Entity matching is formulated as a conditional generation task rather than binary classification.
LLM reasoning is distilled into smaller entity matching models using natural language explanations.
Distilling LLM reasoning via natural language explanations achieves strong out-of-domain generalization for entity matching.
Natural language explanations are important for performance and model robustness in entity matching.
Retrospex combines LLM action likelihood with action values from an RL Critic trained on past experiences via offline retrospection.
Retrospex uses a dynamic action rescoring mechanism that increases the importance of experience-based values for tasks requiring more environmental interaction.
PrExMe evaluates a large-scale set of prompt templates for open-source LLM-based metrics on machine translation and summarization.
Some open-source LLMs used as metrics exhibit idiosyncratic preferences, favoring textual labels for grading generated texts while others favor numeric scores.
Seemingly innocuous changes to the requested output format for LLM-based metrics strongly affect prompt stability and model rankings.
RECIPE converts knowledge statements into short and informative continuous prompts that are prefixed to the LLM's input query embedding to refine responses based on the knowledge.
RECIPE integrates a Knowledge Sentinel that calculates a dynamic threshold to determine if the retrieval repository contains relevant knowledge.
The retriever and prompt encoder in RECIPE are jointly trained to achieve reliability, generality, and locality in knowledge editing.
RECIPE maintains the overall performance of large language models during lifelong knowledge editing.
RECIPE has fast editing and inference speed during lifelong knowledge editing.

Language models trained with moderately accurate reward models exhibit superior performance to those trained with highly accurate reward models on relevance, factuality, and completeness tasks as measured by the QA-FEEDBACK dataset.
A human-annotated dataset provides coherent summaries and natural language user feedback for five publicly available datasets to improve coherence in extractive summarization.
Supervised fine-tuning with natural language human feedback aligns Large Language Models to generate coherent extractive summaries.
Over 10,000 monolingual and multilingual language models for over 250 languages, including multiple under-studied language families in NLP, are pre-trained.
Adding multilingual data in moderation improves low-resource language modeling performance.

The improvement in low-resource language modeling performance from adding multilingual data depends on the syntactic similarity of the added languages, with vocabulary overlap having marginal additional effects.
High-resource languages consistently perform worse in multilingual pre-training scenarios.
Adding multilingual data hurts language modeling performance for both low-resource and high-resource languages when dataset sizes are large, due to limited model capacity.
ANGST is a benchmark for depression-anxiety comorbidity classification from social media posts.
Large language models have limited performance in multi-class comorbid classification for depression and anxiety from social media posts.
Rusty-DAWG enables arbitrary-length n-gram search over a corpus in constant time with respect to corpus size.
For n-grams of length greater than 4, language model-generated text is less novel than human-written text.
For n-grams of length 4 or smaller, language model-generated text is more novel than human-written text.
Larger language models decrease the novelty of generated text.
More constrained decoding strategies decrease the novelty of generated text.
Language models complete n-grams with lower loss when the n-grams are more frequent in the training data.
The spectrum of relative likelihood provides useful features for human-model text detection.
A detection procedure for human-model text detection uses supervised and heuristic-based classification methods.
Subtle differences between human and model languages have theoretical roots in psycholinguistics.
C3PA is the first open regulation-aware dataset of expert-annotated privacy policies for CCPA.
C3PA contains expert-labeled privacy policy text segments for CCPA-specific disclosure mandates.
C3PA is uniquely suited for aiding automated audits of compliance with CCPA disclosure mandates.
Adaptive Immune-based Sound-Shape Code (ISSC) generates adversarial Chinese text via character substitution.
Sound-Shape code generates natural Chinese character substitutions by integrating multiple Chinese features.
Adaptive immune algorithm determines the replacement order in adversarial Chinese text attacks to reduce population duplication and improve search ability.
In in-context learning, the cost of demonstration configuration correlates with task performance.
A two-dimensional evaluation paradigm for in-context learning evaluates methods based on both task performance and demonstration configuration cost.
An optimized demonstration for in-context learning on one language model generalizes to language models of different sizes.
A plugin strategy for in-context learning methods improves the trade-off between task performance and demonstration configuration cost.
Dual-oriented Disentangled Network with Counterfactual Intervention (DuoDN) disentangles modality-specific information from multimodal semantic information for multimodal intent detection.
Dual-oriented Disentangled Network with Counterfactual Intervention (DuoDN) applies causal inference by injecting confounders to understand causal effects for multimodal intent detection.
Language models can be fine-tuned to implicitly optimize a linear regressor that predicts humans' reading times of in-context linguistic units using the language model's surprisal estimates.
Fine-tuning language models to implicitly optimize a linear regressor for predicting humans' reading times of words using surprisal estimates improves their psychometric predictive power.
A language model's psychometric predictive power is inversely related to its performance on downstream NLP tasks and its perplexity on held-out test data.

Manipulating a language model's alignment to psychometric data induces an inverse relationship between psychometric predictive power and a model's performance on downstream NLP tasks as well as its perplexity on held-out test data.
Cluster-Norm minimizes the impact of salient but unrelated features in activation space by clustering and normalizing activations of contrast pairs prior to unsupervised probing.
Cluster-Norm improves the accuracy of unsupervised probes in identifying intended knowledge amidst distractions.
Matryoshka-Adaptor reduces embedding dimensionality without compromising performance.
Matryoshka-Adaptor integrates with any LLM architecture, including black-box APIs.
Matryoshka-Adaptor operates in unsupervised and supervised learning settings.
GPT-4 outperforms other large language models in detecting solidarity frames towards women and migrants in German parliamentary debates and approaches human annotation quality.
Solidarity with migrants outweighs anti-solidarity in German parliamentary debates.
Frequencies and types of solidarity towards migrants shift over time in German parliamentary debates.

Group-based notions of (anti-)solidarity are replaced by compassionate solidarity, focusing on the vulnerability of migrant groups, and exchange-based anti-solidarity, focusing on the lack of economic contribution, in German parliamentary debates.
Powerful large language models, when carefully prompted, serve as cost-effective alternatives to human annotation for hard social scientific tasks.
Reverse reward engineering composes interpretable features into a white-box reward function for language model alignment.
Emulating the gold reward in language model alignment requires generating relevant and sufficiently long responses for open-ended questions and consistent responses for closed-ended questions.
A white-box reward function composed from interpretable features serves as a strong baseline for language model alignment without requiring human feedback datasets or reward model training.
SecCoder generates secure code using in-context learning and a safe demonstration.
SecCoder uses a dense retriever to select the most helpful demonstration for improving code security.
SecCoder demonstrates superior generalizability on unseen test cases for secure code generation.
SecCoder demonstrates better robustness against model attacks for secure code generation.
SN-Caption-test-align is a benchmark for soccer game commentary generation with manually annotated timestamps for 49 matches.
A multi-modal temporal alignment pipeline automatically corrects and filters soccer game commentary datasets at scale.
MatchTime is a soccer game commentary dataset for training that has been automatically corrected and filtered.
MatchVoice is an automatic soccer game commentary generation model trained on the MatchTime dataset.
Better video-text alignment in soccer game commentary datasets leads to performance improvements in commentary generation.
SVM and neural supertaggers achieve higher accuracy than the baseline for HPSG supertagging.
SVM and neural supertaggers increase parsing speed and parser accuracy for HPSG parsing.
A supertagger-based parser can be more accurate than an existing integrated tagger for HPSG parsing.
HPSG parsing results can differ depending on the evaluation dataset, even for in-domain data.
English Resource Grammar treebanks are in a Huggingface token classification format.
Understanding ambiguous political discourse requires social context grounding in real-world entities, actions, and attitudes.
Datasets for ambiguous political discourse require understanding social context.
Structured models explicitly modeling social context outperform larger language models on social context understanding tasks.
Structured models explicitly modeling social context lag behind human performance on social context understanding tasks.
QuBE enhances LLM agents' focus on task-relevant contexts in partially observable environments by constructing a belief state via question answering.
QuBE mitigates reasoning derailment in LLM agents in partially observed environments.
All large language models tested except GPT-4 show significant variations in responses to culturally sensitive and non-sensitive prompts on culturally sensitive and neutral datasets.
Culturally-conditioned prompting is not robust for eliciting cultural bias in large language models that are not stable with respect to arbitrary prompting cues.
Some datasets intended to be culturally neutral contain a non-trivial fraction of culturally sensitive questions or tasks.
GottBERT is the first German single-language RoBERTa model pre-trained exclusively on the German portion of the OSCAR dataset.
Filtering the OSCAR corpus does not significantly affect the performance of German RoBERTa models.
GottBERT base models achieve the best performance among base models on the majority of evaluated downstream tasks.
The Language Confusion Benchmark (LCB) evaluates language confusion in LLMs across 15 typologically diverse languages using existing and newly-created English and multilingual prompts.
Llama Instruct and Mistral models exhibit high degrees of language confusion in monolingual and cross-lingual generation tasks.
The strongest LLMs exhibit language confusion in monolingual and cross-lingual generation tasks.
Base and English-centric instruct models are more prone to language confusion than other models.
Complex prompts and high sampling temperatures aggravate language confusion in LLMs.
Few-shot prompting, multilingual supervised fine-tuning, and preference tuning partially mitigate language confusion in LLMs.
World to Code (W2C) organizes multi-modal data generation output into Python code format.
World to Code (W2C) uses vision-language models to extract cross-modal information via different prompts.
World to Code (W2C) filters generated outputs using a consistency filtering strategy.
World to Code (W2C) improves visual question answering and visual grounding benchmarks across different vision-language models.
Code parsing ability in vision-language models provides better cross-modal equivalence than detail caption ability.
GRASP replaces redundant layers with a minimal set of adaptive singular parameters for efficient model compression.
GRASP uses gradient-based attribution on a small calibration dataset to adaptively identify and retain critical singular components.
GRASP consistently outperforms existing compression methods in maintaining performance under high compression ratios.
AMR is harnessed for a range of downstream applications.
Specific techniques incorporate AMR into downstream applications.
Self-refine Instruction-tuning elicits Small Language Models to self-improve their reasoning abilities.
Self-refine Instruction-tuning transfers reasoning abilities from Large Language Models to Small Language Models via Instruction-tuning on synthetic demonstrations.

Self-refine Instruction-tuning uses Direct Preference Optimization to refine reasoning paths by automatically sampling generated responses and providing rewards based on ground truths from Large Language Models.
Self-refine Instruction-tuning outperforms standard Instruction-tuning on commonsense and math reasoning tasks in both in-domain and out-of-domain scenarios.
Self-refine Instruction-tuning aligns the reasoning abilities of Small and Large Language Models on commonsense and math reasoning tasks.
LLM-generated persona-based responses are distinguishable from neutral baseline responses.
LLM-generated responses for directly affected personas exhibit higher exaggeration, amplifying identity-specific portrayals.
LLM-generated responses for different personas frame the same historical event in conflicting ways in military, political, and morally charged contexts.
LLM-generated direct personas adopt more passive constructions in institutional contexts but shift to active framing when emotional immediacy is foregrounded.
Subtle asymmetries in tone, stance, and emphasis in LLM-generated historical narratives systematically distort how history is told and remembered.
SciPrompt automatically retrieves scientific topic-related terms for verbalizer augmentation in low-resource scientific text classification.
SciPrompt selects semantically correlated and domain-specific label terms from scientific literature for verbalizer augmentation.
SciPrompt uses correlation scores as additional weights in a verbalization strategy to enhance language model prediction performance during tuning.

SciPrompt outperforms state-of-the-art prompt-based fine-tuning methods for scientific text classification in few-shot and zero-shot settings, particularly for fine-grained and emerging scientific topics.
Targeted deviations from merge lists in BPE inference cause significant degradation in language model performance.
Non-targeted merge-list free BPE inference algorithms cause minimal impact on downstream language model performance.

Backdoors planted with training intensity more aggressive or more conservative than default are harder to detect than default backdoors using trigger inversion or meta classifier based detection methods.
CARE is a multilingual resource of culturally specific questions and responses with human judgments.
A modest amount of high-quality native preferences improves cultural awareness in language models.

Models with stronger initial cultural performance benefit more from alignment with cultural preferences, resulting in performance gaps among models from different regions due to varying access to culturally relevant data.
Semantic similarity of speaker turns quantifies responsivity.
Large language models quantify responsivity by identifying the relation between two speaker turns.
The large language model-based approach for quantifying responsivity outperforms the semantic similarity approach.
Large language model-based responsivity quantification characterizes whether a response is substantive.
Conversation-level derived metrics support meaningful characterizations and differentiations of conversations.
Greedy Attention Logit Interpolation reuses pretrained positional intervals greedily for length extrapolation in large language models.
Greedy Attention Logit Interpolation interpolates attention logits to eliminate outliers for length extrapolation in large language models.
Greedy Attention Logit Interpolation achieves stable performance across diverse long-context tasks without input-length-specific tuning.
Large language models interpret positional intervals unevenly.
Restricting attention logit interpolation to narrower ranges improves performance on short-context tasks.
Probability distribution collapse is the underlying cause of the expressiveness bottleneck in unsupervised neural grammar induction.
Collapse-relaxing neural parameterization mitigates probability distribution collapse in unsupervised neural grammar induction.
Collapse-relaxing neural parameterization improves parsing performance across multiple languages in unsupervised neural grammar induction.
Collapse-relaxing neural parameterization enables more compact grammars across multiple languages in unsupervised neural grammar induction.
Poisoning the demonstration context manipulates the behavior of large language models without fine-tuning.
ICLAttack is a backdoor attack method for in-context learning in large language models.
ICLAttack uses poisoning of demonstration examples to make large language models behave in alignment with predefined intentions.
ICLAttack uses poisoning of demonstration prompts to make large language models behave in alignment with predefined intentions.
ICLAttack uses correctly labeled poisoned examples to enhance natural stealth.
The local-prediction deficit measures the information loss from relying solely on the local window in large language models using Jensen-Shannon divergence.
Integration windows of large language models and humans are strongly modulated by sentence boundaries.
Predictions in large language models and humans primarily rely on words within the same sentence or clause.
The information loss from relying solely on the local window in large language models follows a power-law decay as the window length increases and drops sharply at the sentence boundary.
The boundary effect on information integration in large language models is primarily attributed to linguistic structural markers such as punctuation, rather than implicit syntactic or semantic cues.
TEXAES is a dataset for textual aesthetics in large language model responses.
TAPO is a fine-tuning method for textual aesthetics based on direct preference optimization.
TAPO leverages textual aesthetics without compromising content correctness.
An evaluation method measures textual aesthetics using text analysis.
An evaluation method measures textual aesthetics using image analysis.
Textual aesthetics data and the TAPO fine-tuning method improve aesthetic scores and enhance performance on general language model evaluation benchmarks.
RAED retrieves external knowledge to improve factual grounding in entity descriptions.
RAED disambiguates entities without fixed entity inventories.
RAED reduces hallucinations in generated entity descriptions.
RAED outperforms systems requiring predefined candidate sets on Entity Disambiguation.
RAED-generated entity descriptions improve performance in Emerging Entity Linking.
R-TOFU augments existing unlearning tasks with realistic chain-of-thought annotations.
R-TOFU provides step-wise metrics that expose residual knowledge invisible to answer-level checks.
Conventional answer-only unlearning objectives leave substantial forget traces in reasoning.
Reasoned IDK preserves coherent yet inconclusive reasoning and achieves a stronger balance between forgetting efficacy and model utility than earlier refusal styles.
Decoding variants such as ZeroThink and LessThink can reveal forgotten content even after unlearning.
Hallucinations in vision-language models are attributed to a dynamic subset of attention heads in each layer.
SPIN selectively suppresses attention heads that exhibit low attention to image tokens, retaining the top-k attention heads for each text query token.
SPIN operates in a task-agnostic manner without significant compute or latency overhead during inference.
SPIN reduces hallucination scores while maintaining F1 and improves throughput compared to existing methods.
SWAN interleaves layers without positional encodings and sliding-window attention layers with rotary positional encodings.
SWAN applies a dynamic scaling mechanism for attention scores during inference.
SWAN achieves strong length extrapolation without requiring additional long-context training.
SWAN is more computationally efficient than the standard Transformer architecture, resulting in lower training cost and higher inference throughput.
Existing pre-trained decoder-only models can be adapted to the SWAN architecture with minimal continued training to enable extended contexts.
Multimodal large language models exhibit response uncertainty when presented with a single deceptive cue.

A two-stage evaluation pipeline quantifies vulnerability to misleading information by first eliciting the original response on unperturbed inputs and then injecting explicit false-answer hints and implicit contextual contradictions to compute the misleading rate.

The Multimodal Uncertainty Benchmark (MUB) is a collection of image–question pairs stratified into low, medium, and high difficulty levels based on the proportion of multimodal large language models that are misled.
Multimodal large language models exhibit high response uncertainty when presented with explicit false-answer hints and implicit contextual contradictions.
Fine-tuning open-source multimodal large language models on a compact mixed-instruction dataset reduces the misleading rate for explicit and implicit misleading cues.
RCScore quantifies how instruction formulation affects model responses.
RCScore reveals performance variations in model responses that are undetected by conventional metrics.
Instruction style can shift model accuracy.
Cross-Response Similarity measures stylistic self-consistency using RCScore metrics.
Cross-Response Similarity correlates strongly with task accuracy, suggesting that consistency is a valuable proxy for model reliability.
Primus provides open-source datasets for cybersecurity LLM pretraining, instruction fine-tuning, and reasoning distillation with cybersecurity-specific self-reflection data.
Continued pre-training on Primus improves performance on public cybersecurity benchmarks.
Reasoning distillation with Primus improves performance on the CISSP exam.
Vision-language-action models suffer from spurious forgetting, where robot training overwrites crucial visual-text alignments.
Vision-language-action models suffer from task interference, where competing control and understanding tasks degrade performance when trained jointly.
ChatVLA uses Phased Alignment Training to incrementally integrate multimodal data after initial control mastery.
ChatVLA uses a Mixture-of-Experts architecture to minimize task interference.
ChatVLA surpasses state-of-the-art vision-language-action methods on multimodal understanding benchmarks.
ChatVLA achieves superior performance on real-world robot manipulation tasks compared to existing vision-language-action methods.
Bias in large language model outputs is deviation from a desired distribution, which may be an equal or real-world distribution depending on application goals.
A weighted adaptive loss based fine-tuning method aligns large language model gender-profession output distributions with desired distributions while preserving language modeling capability.
Masked language models exhibit bias under both equal and real-world desired distributions.
Autoregressive large language models exhibit no bias under equality desired distributions but exhibit notable bias under real-world desired distributions.
A weighted adaptive loss based fine-tuning method achieves significant mitigation of bias under real-world desired distributions in both masked and autoregressive large language models.
MovieCORE probes deeper cognitive understanding of movie content using questions that engage System-2 thinking and are specific to the video material.
An agentic brainstorming approach uses multiple large language models as thought agents to generate and refine high-quality question-answer pairs.
A set of cognitive tests evaluates video question answering dataset quality by assessing depth, thought-provocation potential, and syntactic complexity.
A comprehensive evaluation scheme assesses video question answering model performance on deeper cognitive tasks.
Agentic Choice Enhancement (ACE) improves video-language model reasoning capabilities post-training.
NormXLogit assesses the significance of individual input tokens.
NormXLogit operates based on the input and output representations associated with each token.
The norm of word embeddings can be utilized as a measure of token importance.
A token's importance is related to how predictive its representation is of the language model's final output.
NormXLogit outperforms existing gradient-based methods in faithfulness.
NormXLogit offers competitive performance compared to leading architecture-specific techniques.
MemeSafetyBench pairs real meme images with harmful and benign instructions.
Vision-language models are more vulnerable to meme-based harmful prompts than to synthetic or typographic images.
Memes increase harmful responses and decrease refusals compared to text-only inputs.
Multi-turn interactions partially mitigate vulnerability to memes, but vulnerability remains elevated.
NL-DEBUGGING employs natural language as an intermediate representation for code debugging.
NL-DEBUGGING enables a broader modification space for code debugging through direct refinement guided by execution feedback.

MMDiag is a large-scale multi-turn multimodal dialogue dataset generated through collaboratively designed rules and GPT assistance, featuring complex dialogues with contextual dependencies that require models to track, ground, and recall information across multiple turns and disparate visual regions.

DiagNote uses a dual-module architecture that explicitly separates reasoning, performed via step-by-step Chain-of-Thought, from grounding, performed via predicting bounding box annotations, and has the modules interact iteratively to dynamically refine understanding.
DiagNote demonstrates advantages in grounding and joint processing and reasoning of vision and language information compared to existing multimodal large language models.
Memory-QA is a task for answering recall questions about visual content from previously stored multimodal memories.
Pensieve integrates memory-specific augmentation, time- and location-aware multi-signal retrieval, and multi-memory QA fine-tuning for the Memory-QA task.
A multimodal benchmark for the Memory-QA task illustrates real challenges in answering recall questions from multimodal memories.
Chain-of-thought prompting and supervised fine-tuning alone fail to generalise reliably in few-shot open-set classification with anonymised class semantics for small language models.
Wasserstein GFN is a novel amortised Generative Flow Network framework that uses latent trajectories to approximate the Bayesian posterior.
Wasserstein GFN enables small language models to achieve performance comparable to much larger models in few-shot open-set classification in complex datasets.
JOLT-SQL jointly optimizes schema linking and SQL generation via a unified loss in a single-stage supervised fine-tuning framework.
JOLT-SQL uses discriminative schema linking enhanced by local bidirectional attention.
JOLT-SQL uses a confusion-aware noisy schema sampling strategy with selective attention to improve robustness under noisy schema conditions.
JOLT-SQL achieves state-of-the-art execution accuracy for Text-to-SQL among comparable-size open-source models.
JOLT-SQL improves training and inference efficiency for Text-to-SQL.
UNCLE is a benchmark for evaluating uncertainty expression in long- and short-form question answering.
UNCLE directly links short- and long-form question answering through aligned questions and gold-standard answers.
New metrics assess models' capabilities to selectively express uncertainty.
Current models fail to convey uncertainty appropriately in long-form generation.
Training-based methods yield greater gains than prompt-based methods for improving uncertainty expression in long-form generation.
Short- and long-form uncertainty expression have alignment gaps.
HVGuard is the first reasoning-based hateful video detection framework that utilizes multimodal large language models.
HVGuard integrates Chain-of-Thought reasoning to enhance multimodal interaction modeling and implicit hate interpretation.
HVGuard uses a Mixture-of-Experts network for efficient multimodal fusion and final decision-making.
HVGuard is modular and extensible, allowing flexible integration of different multimodal large language models and encoders.
HVGuard outperforms existing advanced hateful video detection tools on public datasets for English and Chinese.
Verbal uncertainty in large language models is governed by a single linear feature in the representation space.
The linear feature governing verbal uncertainty has only moderate correlation with the semantic uncertainty of large language models.
The mismatch between semantic and verbal uncertainty is a better predictor of hallucinations than semantic uncertainty alone.
Intervening on verbal uncertainty at inference time reduces confident hallucinations on short-form answers.
COCOA continually revises a constitution based on observed model behaviors and trains models to comply with the evolving principles.
COCOA uses the learned constitution to guide reinforcement learning for model alignment with updated normative signals.
COCOA improves safety for a 7B model without human annotations, achieving performance comparable to much larger state-of-the-art models.
VerIF combines rule-based code verification with LLM-based verification from a large reasoning model.
VerInstruct is a high-quality instruction-following dataset with verification signals.
Models trained with VerIF achieve state-of-the-art performance on instruction-following benchmarks among models of comparable size.
Models trained with VerIF generalize to unseen constraints in instruction following.
Models trained with VerIF retain their general capabilities.
Downstream task performance correlates with the adherence of token distributions to power-law behavior.
Aligning token distributions with Zipfian scaling improves model efficiency and effectiveness.
Models achieve peak performance when token distributions adhere to Zipf’s law.
Zipfian alignment is a robust and generalizable criterion for vocabulary size selection.
The translationese-index (T-index) provides a graded measure of translationese using likelihood ratios from two contrastively fine-tuned language models.
The translationese-index (T-index) generalizes to unseen genres, authors, and language pairs.
