The translationese-index (T-index) aligns with human judgments of translationese when computed from language models fine-tuned on a small amount of synthetic translation data.
The translationese-index (T-index) has low correlation with existing machine translation quality estimation metrics such as BLEU and COMET.
SOCIAL SCAFFOLDS facilitates generalization across social reasoning tasks by generating rationales that make social cues explicit.
SOCIAL SCAFFOLDS generates task-agnostic rationales that capture the perspectives of the speaker, the listener, and the general world-view.
Providing SOCIAL SCAFFOLDS rationales as augmentations aids task performance for supervised fine-tuning and in-context learning.
Providing all three SOCIAL SCAFFOLDS rationale types improves cross-task performance and inferred speaker intent.
SOCIAL SCAFFOLDS rationales complement the input text and are used effectively by models.
PLLuM-Align is the first Polish preference dataset for large language model alignment.
PLLuM-Align is created entirely through human annotation to reflect Polish language and cultural nuances.
PLLuM-Align includes response rating, ranking, and multi-turn dialog data.
FinRAGBench-V integrates multimodal data in financial RAG.
FinRAGBench-V provides visual citation for traceability in financial RAG.
FinRAGBench-V includes a bilingual retrieval corpus and a human-annotated question-answering dataset covering heterogeneous financial data types.
RGenCite integrates visual citation with generation in Retrieval-Augmented Generation.
An automatic citation evaluation method assesses the visual citation capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models.
FinRAGBench-V is challenging for financial multimodal RAG systems.
ChartGaze is an eye-tracking dataset capturing human gaze patterns during chart question answering tasks.
Large Vision-Language Models often diverge from human gaze in chart question answering, reducing interpretability and accuracy.
Gaze-guided attention refinement aligns image-text attention with human fixations in chart question answering.
Gaze-guided attention refinement improves answer accuracy for chart question answering.

GANGRL-LLM is a semi-supervised framework that integrates Generative Adversarial Networks and Large Language Models to enhance malicious code generation and SQL Injection detection in few-sample learning scenarios.
GANGRL-LLM employs a GAN-based discriminator that improves malicious pattern recognition through adversarial learning with generated samples and limited real samples.
GANGRL-LLM employs an LLM-based generator that refines malicious code synthesis quality using reward signals from the discriminator.
GRADA is a graph-based reranking framework for defending against adversarial document attacks in Retrieval Augmented Generation systems.
GRADA reduces the success rate of adversarial document attacks while maintaining accuracy in Retrieval Augmented Generation systems.
The first benchmark for post-hoc calibration of LLM-based text-to-SQL parsing is established.
Platt scaling improves calibration over raw model output probabilities for text-to-SQL parsing.
Sub-clause frequency (SCF) scores provide granular signals of correctness for text-to-SQL calibration by leveraging the structured nature of SQL queries.
Multivariate Platt scaling (MPS) extends Platt scaling to combine sub-clause frequency scores for text-to-SQL calibration.
Combining multivariate Platt scaling (MPS) and sub-clause frequency (SCF) scores improves calibration and error detection for text-to-SQL parsing over traditional Platt scaling.

Fully Probabilistic Evaluation (FPE) provides a probabilistic evaluation of Large Language Model Unlearning by querying unlearned models with semantically similar inputs and multiple sampling attempts.
Input Distribution Sampling selects high-quality inputs for Large Language Model Unlearning evaluation to enable a stricter measure of information leakage risks.

Contrastive Embedding Loss (CEL) distances latent representations of unlearned samples from adaptively clustered contrast samples and aligns them with random vectors in Large Language Model Unlearning.
Fully Probabilistic Evaluation (FPE) uncovers more unlearned information leakage risks than prior evaluation methods.
Contrastive Embedding Loss (CEL) improves unlearning effectiveness and robustness while retaining high model utility.
A syntax-aware retrieval-augmented mechanism uses the syntactic structure of symbolic expressions to perform context-aware token retrieval from a pre-constructed datastore during inference.
An entropy-based confidence network dynamically adjusts the fusion strength between neural and retrieved components by estimating predictive uncertainty.
Retrieval augmentation enhances the generalization performance of neural symbolic regression models.
Iterative Multilingual Spectral Attribute Erasure identifies and mitigates joint bias subspaces across multiple languages through iterative SVD-based truncation.
Iterative Multilingual Spectral Attribute Erasure mitigates bias in zero-shot settings by using linguistically similar languages when target language data is unavailable.
Iterative Multilingual Spectral Attribute Erasure maintains model utility during debiasing.
Graphs formalize meaning and Synchronous Hyperedge Replacement Grammar (SHRG) formalizes the syntax–semantics interface.
Computationally-sound induction algorithms acquire statistical grammar knowledge.

A controlled paradigm simulates child language acquisition by inducing interpretable Synchronous Hyperedge Replacement Grammars (SHRGs) from semantic graphs and evaluating them by their ability to generate utterances.
Unsupervised statistical learning performs as well as a supervised oracle in grammar induction when a proper symbolic grammar is assumed.
An evaluation methodology for unlearning inspects concept vectors of unlearned concepts using vocabulary projections.
ConceptVectors is a benchmark of hundreds of concrete concepts and their concept vectors in two open-source large language models.
Existing unlearning methods minimally alter the concept vectors of unlearned concepts and primarily suppress them during inference.
Direct ablation of the concept vectors of unlearned concepts removes the associated knowledge and reduces susceptibility to adversarial recovery of that knowledge.
A curated dataset of African traditional herbal medicine evaluates cultural bias in medical LLMs.
Multiple-choice questions and fill-in-the-blank completions evaluate treatment preferences and responsiveness to cultural context in medical LLMs.
Cultural Bias Score (CBS) quantifies outcome preferences in medical LLMs.
Cultural Bias Attribution (CBA) quantifies prompt influences in medical LLMs.
Prompt adaptation can reduce inherent cultural bias and enhance cultural alignment in medical LLMs.
Medical LLMs vary in their responsiveness to contextual guidance.
Medical LLMs persistently default to allopathic (Western) treatments in zero-shot scenarios.

Abductive and deductive logical inference pipelines excel in visual and symbolic modalities and on harder tasks, while inductive logical inference pipelines are competitive in textual modality and on easier problems.

Task format significantly influences the relative advantage between inductive and abductive-deductive logical inference, with inductive logical inference outperforming abductive-deductive logical inference in free-text rule-execution.
The comparative dynamics of inductive and abductive-deductive logical inference generalize to broader in-context learning.
Advanced abductive-deductive logical inference strategies, such as hypothesis selection and iterative refinement, substantially scale large language model reasoning.
An Approximate Distance Join algorithm refines LLM-generated pseudo query graphs by flexibly aligning them with the knowledge graph's structure.
A compact schema graph enables efficient Approximate Distance Join, reducing overhead and improving retrieval accuracy.
SAFE outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and efficiency for knowledge graph querying.
PIIvot is a lightweight framework for PII anonymization that leverages data context to simplify PII detection.
QATD_2k is the largest open-source real-world tutoring dataset for educational dialogue.
EcoLoRA uses a round-robin segment sharing scheme that uploads only a complementary LoRA segment per round to reduce network bandwidth in federated fine-tuning of large language models.
EcoLoRA uses adaptive sparsification methods tailored to LoRA’s training dynamics in federated fine-tuning of large language models.
EcoLoRA reduces communication overhead without compromising performance in federated fine-tuning of large language models.
For tasks with multiple parallel reasoning steps, parallel decoding within one sequence decodes multiple tokens per forward pass using a tree-like attention mask without additional memory usage.
Parallel decoding within one sequence achieves decoding speedup while maintaining answer quality.
RETAIL supports decision-making for implicit travel planning queries.
RETAIL covers explicit travel planning queries with and without revision needs.
RETAIL enables environmental awareness to ensure travel plan feasibility in real-world scenarios.
RETAIL incorporates detailed point-of-interest information for all-in-one travel plans.
TGMA is a topic-guided multi-agent framework for real-world travel planning.
The strongest existing models achieve very low pass rates on real-world travel planning tasks.
TGMA achieves higher pass rates on real-world travel planning tasks than the strongest existing models.
Different Pre-trained Multilingual Language Models are biased towards specific types of sentence pairs that are treated as noise in Neural Machine Translation.
Debiasing heuristics remove parallel sentences that are treated as noise in Neural Machine Translation from web-mined parallel corpora.

Neural Machine Translation models trained on web-mined parallel corpora curated using debiasing heuristics achieve better translation performance and reduced disparity across Pre-trained Multilingual Language Models.
Toxic neurons account for a limited portion of DPO's toxicity reduction across multiple language models.
DPO induces distributed activation shifts across all MLP neurons to reduce toxicity.
DPO's toxicity reduction is attributed to four neuron groups: two reducing toxicity and two promoting anti-toxicity, whose combined effects replicate DPO across models.
An activation editing method mimics DPO through distributed shifts along a toxicity representation.
An activation editing method reduces toxicity more effectively than DPO while preserving perplexity without requiring weight updates.
SAFE-SQL generates self-augmented Text-to-SQL examples by prompting a large language model.
SAFE-SQL filters self-augmented examples through three relevance assessments to construct high-quality in-context learning examples.
SAFE-SQL achieves higher execution accuracy than previous zero-shot and few-shot Text-to-SQL frameworks.
SAFE-SQL provides performance gains in extra hard and unseen scenarios where conventional methods often fail.
The Integral Transformer denoises attention by integrating signals sampled from the logit distribution.
The Integral Transformer preserves the contributions of special tokens critical for model performance.
The Integral Transformer outperforms vanilla, Cog, and Differential attention variants on rigorous knowledge and reasoning benchmarks.
The Integral Transformer balances attention distributions and reduces rank collapse in upper Transformer layers.
ECC is the first dialogue dataset where conversations and emotion-cause labels are automatically generated synergistically during creation.
EC-DD automatically generates conversations for the ECC dataset using knowledge and large language models.
CAER is a causality-aware empathetic response model trained on the ECC dataset.
ECC achieves comparable or superior performance compared to artificially constructed empathy dialogue datasets.
XNationQA evaluates the cultural literacy of multilingual language models through questions on geography, culture, and history of multiple countries in multiple languages.
Novel transference metrics measure knowledge transfer across languages in multilingual language models.
Multilingual language models often demonstrate greater knowledge of cultural information in English than in the dominant language of the respective culture.
Better performance in Western languages by multilingual language models does not necessarily indicate greater knowledge about Western countries.
Multilingual language models have a very limited ability to transfer knowledge across languages, especially open-source models.
TASO reduces redundancy in LoRA by leveraging importance information from pretrained model weights.
TASO estimates parameter importance on downstream tasks and identifies task-specific core regions based on the distribution of importance scores.
TASO uses the location information of task-specific core regions to determine the sparse structure of LoRA modules, enabling redundancy removal before fine-tuning.
TASO achieves better fine-tuning performance than standard LoRA with fewer trainable parameters.
Symbolically-Guided Monte Carlo Process Supervision synthesizes high-quality symbolic reasoning trajectories with stepwise pseudo-labels at scale via Monte Carlo estimation.
A Process Reward Model trained on synthesized symbolic reasoning trajectories selects more symbolic trajectories.
Symbolically-Guided Monte Carlo Process Supervision employs symbolic reasoning trajectories with Direct Preference Optimization and Supervised Fine-Tuning.
Symbolically-Guided Monte Carlo Process Supervision improves logical reasoning on FOLIO and LogicAsker benchmarks for frontier and open-weight models.
Fine-tuning on symbolic reasoning trajectories synthesized via Monte Carlo estimation enhances out-of-domain generalizability on claim verification data.
HD-PiSSA assigns different principal components of the pre-trained weights to each GPU for fine-tuning.
HD-PiSSA aggregates delta updates from orthogonal adapters across devices collectively for fine-tuning.
HD-PiSSA achieves higher effective updated ranks than data-parallel LoRA or PiSSA when fine-tuning on multiple GPUs with the same per-device adapter rank.
HD-PiSSA outperforms LoRA and PiSSA on mathematics, code, and multi-task learning tasks.
Hypernetwork Model Alignment (Hyma) selects optimal uni-modal models and trains connector modules for multi-modal foundation models.
Hypernetwork Model Alignment (Hyma) uses a hypernetwork to generate connector modules for multiple combinations of uni-modal models.
Hypernetwork Model Alignment (Hyma) reduces the computational cost of searching for the best uni-modal model pair while matching the multi-modal benchmark performance of grid search.
RA-RAG estimates source reliability by cross-checking information across multiple sources.
RA-RAG retrieves documents from the top-k reliable and relevant sources.
RA-RAG aggregates information using weighted majority voting.
RA-RAG scales efficiently as the number of sources increases.
RA-RAG outperforms baseline methods in scenarios with heterogeneous source reliability.
RA-RAG estimates the reliability of real-world sources.
Grammar Pruning is a zero-shot framework for task-oriented semantic parsing that requires only the domain's full API schema values.
Grammar Pruning uses a rule-based entity extractor to dynamically prune the context-free grammar, limiting generation to only plausible intents, slots, and values at each step.
Grammar Pruning reduces hallucinations and slashes decoding time for task-oriented semantic parsing.
Grammar Pruning with small language models achieves high execution accuracy in task-oriented semantic parsing, rivaling State-of-the-Art cloud-based solutions.
Grammar Pruning with small language models sustains lower end-to-end latency than existing methods for task-oriented semantic parsing.
DASA is an adaptive Transformer for Chinese short text matching that uses data augmentation and semantic awareness to handle word ambiguity.
DASA uses a Graph Attention Transformer Encoder that takes two word lattice graphs as input and integrates sense information from N-HowNet to moderate word ambiguity.
DASA uses an LLM to generate similar sentences for optimal text representation.
DASA achieves better results than previous state-of-the-art methods on the MNS, LCQMC, AFQMC, and BQ datasets.
GraphAgent is an automated agent pipeline that addresses explicit and implicit graph-enhanced semantic dependencies for predictive and generative tasks.
GraphAgent includes a Graph Generator Agent that creates knowledge graphs for semantic dependencies.
GraphAgent includes a Task Planning Agent that interprets user queries and formulates tasks via self-planning.
GraphAgent includes a Task Execution Agent that automates task execution with tool matching.
GraphAgent combines language and graph language models to reveal complex relational and semantic patterns.
EuroGEST evaluates gender-stereotypical reasoning in large language models across English and 29 European languages.
EuroGEST has high accuracy in translations and gender labels across languages.
Multilingual language models exhibit the strongest gender stereotypes that women are beautiful, empathetic, and neat and men are leaders, strong, tough, and professional.
Larger multilingual language models encode gender stereotypes more strongly.
Instruction finetuned multilingual language models exhibit gender stereotypes.
Morables is a human-verified benchmark for assessing abstract moral reasoning in large language models using fables and short stories from historical literature.
Morables uses multiple-choice questions targeting moral inference with carefully crafted distractors to challenge models to go beyond shallow, extractive question answering.
Morables includes adversarial variants designed to surface large language model vulnerabilities and shortcuts due to data contamination.
Large language models remain susceptible to adversarial manipulation and rely on superficial patterns rather than true moral reasoning in moral inference tasks.
Large language models exhibit significant self-contradiction in moral reasoning tasks depending on the framing of the moral choice.
Reasoning-enhanced models fail to improve moral reasoning performance, indicating that scale, not reasoning ability, is the primary driver of performance in moral reasoning tasks.
TruthfulnessEval assesses the truthfulness of quantized LLMs on logical reasoning, common sense, and imitative falsehoods.
Quantized LLMs have internally truthful representations.
Deceptive prompts override truth-consistent behavior in quantized LLMs, whereas honest and neutral prompts maintain truth-consistent behavior.
Slide2Code is a benchmark for generating slides from reference images with difficulty-tiered samples.
The Slide Complexity Metric tiers slide generation samples by difficulty.
SlideCoder generates editable slides from reference images with layout awareness and retrieval augmentation.
The Color Gradient-based Segmentation algorithm segments slides using color gradients.
The Hierarchical Retrieval-Augmented Generation method decomposes complex slide generation tasks and enhances code generation.
SlideMaster is a 7B open-source model fine-tuned for slide generation using improved reverse-engineered data.
Distillation is detrimental to the performance of high-order numerical Transformer models.
Iterative Implicit Euler Transformer (IIET) simplifies high-order numerical methods for Transformers using an iterative implicit Euler approach.
IIET facilitates model compression compared to PCformer.
IIET improves average accuracy over vanilla Transformers and PCformer.
Iteration Influence-Aware Distillation (IIAD) balances the performance-efficiency trade-off using a flexible threshold.
E-IIET reduces inference overhead while retaining high task accuracy.
The most efficient IIET variant improves average performance over vanilla Transformer without sacrificing speed.
Instruction following models can be trained to adhere to length constraints specified in instructions at inference time.
Models trained to adhere to length constraints outperform standard instruction following models in evaluations designed to test length instruction following.
MoVa provides 16 labeled datasets and benchmarking results for classifying human morals and values from four theoretical frameworks.
The all@once classification strategy is a lightweight LLM prompting strategy that scores all related concepts simultaneously for classifying human morals and values.
The all@once classification strategy outperforms fine-tuned models for classifying human morals and values across multiple domains and frameworks.
MoVa includes a new application that helps evaluate psychological surveys for human morals and values classification.
COCO-Tree augments vision language model outputs with neurosymbolic concept trees learned from large language models.
COCO-Tree uses a beam search-inspired reasoning process that provides a rationale for vision language model predictions.
COCO-Tree improves compositional generalization over baselines.
InferAct, grounded in Theory-of-Mind, uses the belief reasoning ability of LLMs to preemptively detect misaligned actions in LLM agents.
InferAct alerts users for timely correction of misaligned actions, preventing adverse outcomes.
InferAct improves misaligned action detection compared to baselines.
InferAct improves agent alignment through misalignment correction.
SememeDef is a Sememe Prediction dataset for learning sememe knowledge.
HN7 and CWN5 are Chinese datasets for Link Prediction evaluation.
SememeLP leverages sememe knowledge for Link Prediction.
SememeLP improves Link Prediction performance for lexico-semantic knowledge graphs in both English and Chinese.
Sememic components benefit Link Prediction for lexico-semantic knowledge graphs.
DMDTEval constructs a translation test set with multi-domain ambiguous word annotation.
DMDTEval curates a diverse set of disambiguation prompt strategies.
DMDTEval designs precise disambiguation metrics.
TombRaider exploits the ability of large language models to store, retrieve, and use historical knowledge for jailbreaking.
TombRaider uses an inspector agent to extract relevant historical information and an attacker agent to generate adversarial prompts to bypass safety filters.
TombRaider outperforms state-of-the-art jailbreak techniques in attack success rates on bare models and against defence mechanisms.
Existing large language model safeguards have critical vulnerabilities.
LLM safety research exhibits a significant and growing language gap, with even high-resource non-English languages receiving minimal attention.
Non-English languages are rarely studied as a standalone language in LLM safety research.
LLM safety research for the English language exhibits poor language documentation practice.
LLMs can infer core persona traits.
Subtle shifts in emotionality and extraversion occur in persona traits during dialogue generation, influencing dialogue dynamics.
Applying persona traits modifies the distribution of emotional support strategies, enhancing the relevance and empathetic quality of responses.
Malfunction amplification attacks cause agents to execute repetitive or irrelevant actions.
Malfunction amplification attacks induce high failure rates in multiple scenarios.
Malfunction amplification attacks compromise implemented and deployable agents in multi-agent scenarios.
Self-examination methods defend against malfunction amplification attacks.
Malfunction amplification attacks are more difficult to detect than previous overtly harmful attacks.
FraCom represents conversation history as a series of predicates plus arguments to preserve key information useful for memory.
FraCom composes propositional graphs for conversation history based on connections between shared arguments.
FraCom retrieves relevant propositions from the graph based on arguments in the current query.
FraCom enhances response generation for large language models in long-term conversation.
